  color varchar [note: 'Observed color of the sample.']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text notes regarding regulatory or quality compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the sample record was first created in the system.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the sample is considered expired for testing or use.']
  gmo_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the sample contains genetically modified organisms.']
  hazard_status varchar [note: 'Safety classification of the sample.']
  last_tested_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent testing performed on the sample.']
  moisture_content_percent double [note: 'Percentage of moisture present in the sample.']
  odor varchar [note: 'Observed odor characteristics of the sample.']
  organic_certified boolean [note: 'True if the sample is certified organic, otherwise false.']
  ph_value double [note: 'Measured pH level of the sample.']
  quality_score double [note: 'Numerical score representing overall quality assessment of the sample.']
  regulatory_status varchar [note: 'Compliance status with relevant food safety regulations.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments about the sample.']
  sample_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the sample across systems.']
  sample_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or label for the sample.']
  sample_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the sample based on its processing stage.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the sample.']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'Physical location (e.g., warehouse, freezer) where the sample is stored.']
  storage_temperature_c double [note: 'Temperature in degrees Celsius at which the sample is stored.']
  taste_profile varchar [note: 'Descriptive notes on the taste of the sample.']
  test_equipment_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the equipment used for the analysis.']
  test_method varchar [note: 'Standard method or protocol used for testing the sample.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the sample record.']
  volume_ml double [note: 'Volume of the sample in milliliters.']
  weight_g double [note: 'Weight of the sample in grams.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for sample. Referenced by sample_id.'
}

Table manufacturing.production_order {
  production_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the production order. Primary key for this entity.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Required for Order‑to‑Production traceability; production orders originate from customer sales orders, so linking to account enables order fulfillment reporting.']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Brand performance report requires linking each production order to its brand for tracking volume and cost per brand.']
  carbon_footprint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.carbon_footprint. Business justification: Regulatory GHG reporting requires linking each production order to its calculated carbon footprint for traceability.']
  co_packer_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Co‑packer supplier must be recorded for FDA/HACCP traceability and allergen control.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Required for Manufacturing Cost Allocation Report to allocate production costs to the correct cost center; cost_center string is denormalized and replaced.']
  demand_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.demand_plan. Business justification: Production orders are created from demand plans; linking enables S&OP reconciliation and demand‑supply variance analysis.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee ID or badge number of the production operator or supervisor who posted the confirmation transaction.']
  formulation_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.formulation_version. Business justification: Manufacturing must reference the exact R&D formulation version approved for the SKU to satisfy compliance and quality audits.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.plant. Business justification: Linking production_order to plant centralizes plant metadata and removes the redundant plant_code string.']
  product_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.product_registration. Business justification: Production order release requires verified product registration to ensure compliance with FDA/USDA regulations.']
  production_shift_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.production_shift. Business justification: Required for assigning each production order to a specific shift, enabling shift‑based labor planning and OTIF performance reporting.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Supports Profitability Analysis per production order, linking manufacturing output to finance profit_center for reporting.']
  promotion_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trade.promotion_event. Business justification: Production Planning uses Promotion Events to drive order quantities; linking enables the Production Planning Report to allocate capacity to specific promotions.']
  purchase_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_contract. Business justification: Production runs are executed under specific supplier contracts; linking enables contract compliance reporting.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: OTIF and cost tracking require linking each production order to the originating purchase order.']
  quality_haccp_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.haccp_plan. Business justification: HACCP plan execution is tracked per production order for compliance reporting and audit traceability.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: Regulatory & cost reporting requires each production order to be linked to its originating R&D project for traceability and budgeting.']
  sales_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.order. Business justification: Order fulfillment process requires linking each production order to the originating sales order for traceability and scheduling.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the finished goods SKU or semi-finished intermediate being produced in this order.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.work_center. Business justification: A production order is executed on a specific work center; linking to work_center provides hierarchy and eliminates the free‑text production_line column.']
  actual_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when production execution was completed and final confirmation posted. Captured from MES or CO11N transaction.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when production execution began on the shop floor. Captured from MES or manual confirmation.']
  batch_size decimal(15,3) [note: 'Standard or planned batch size for this production run. Represents the nominal production lot size for the SKU on the assigned line.']
  bom_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the BOM used for this production order. Ensures traceability of ingredient and component specifications used in production.']
  confirmation_number varchar [note: 'SAP PP confirmation document number generated by CO11N transaction. Links to detailed confirmation records including time, quantity, and goods movements.']
  confirmation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the production confirmation was posted in SAP PP via CO11N transaction.']
  confirmation_type varchar [note: 'Type of production confirmation: partial (interim confirmation during production run) or final (completion confirmation closing the order).']
  confirmed_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Actual quantity confirmed as produced via CO11N confirmation transaction. Represents good output quantity posted to inventory.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the production order record was first created in SAP S/4HANA PP system. Represents the beginning of the order lifecycle.']
  fefo_lot_consumption_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether raw material lot consumption for this order followed FEFO inventory management rules (consuming lots with nearest expiration first). True if FEFO was enforced, False otherwise. Critical for perishable food and beverage ingredients.']
  fifo_lot_consumption_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether raw material lot consumption for this order followed FIFO inventory management rules. True if FIFO was enforced, False otherwise.']
  gmp_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this production order was executed in full compliance with GMP requirements. True if all GMP critical control points were met and documented, False if any deviations occurred.']
  goods_movement_document varchar [note: 'SAP MM material document number for the goods receipt posting (MIGO 101 movement type) that moved confirmed quantity into finished goods inventory.']
  haccp_ccp_log_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier to the HACCP CCP monitoring log for this production order. Links to detailed records of critical control point measurements (temperature, pH, time, etc.) taken during production.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the production order record was last updated in the source system. Tracks the most recent change to order header data, status, or confirmations.']
  mes_work_order_reference varchar [note: 'Work order identifier from Aveva/Wonderware MES system. Provides linkage between SAP PP production order and detailed MES execution records including real-time process data, equipment performance, and operator actions.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'SAP PP production order number - the externally-known business identifier for this manufacturing execution record. Typically 10-12 alphanumeric characters.']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the production order: created (order created but not released to shop floor), released (released for execution), in_progress (active production), confirmed (production completed and confirmed via CO11N), technically_complete (all confirmations and goods movements posted), closed (order financially settled and closed), cancelled (order cancelled before completion). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: created|released|in_progress|confirmed|technically_complete|closed|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the production order: standard (regular production run), rework (reprocessing of non-conforming product), trial (R&D or NPD pilot batch), co-pack (third-party manufacturing arrangement), toll (toll manufacturing arrangement where customer provides materials).']
  planned_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Target quantity to be produced as specified in the production order header. Measured in base unit of measure for the SKU.']
  rework_disposition varchar [note: 'Final disposition of reworked material: reprocessed (successfully reworked to original spec), downgraded (reworked to lower-grade product), scrapped (could not be salvaged), returned_to_vendor (raw material defect). Applicable only when order_type is rework.']
  rework_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of product requiring rework or reprocessing. This quantity is typically moved to a rework order for corrective processing.']
  rework_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the reason for rework when order_type is rework. Examples: quality defect, packaging error, labeling error, contamination, off-spec product. Null for non-rework orders.']
  routing_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the production routing or recipe used for this order. Defines the sequence of operations and process parameters.']
  scheduled_end_date date [note: 'Planned completion date for production order execution as determined by production scheduling or SAP IBP.']
  scheduled_start_date date [note: 'Planned start date for production order execution as determined by production scheduling or SAP IBP.']
  scrap_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of product scrapped or discarded during production due to quality defects, process failures, or other non-conformances. Measured in base unit of measure.']
  shift varchar [note: 'Shift during which the production order was executed: day, evening, night, or weekend shift.']
  toll_manufacturer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the toll manufacturing partner when order_type is toll. In toll arrangements, the customer provides raw materials and the toll manufacturer provides processing services. Null for non-toll orders.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Base unit of measure for all quantities on this order (e.g., KG for kilograms, EA for each, LB for pounds, CS for cases). ISO standard unit codes.']
  yield_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Actual yield percentage calculated as (confirmed_quantity / planned_quantity) * 100. Measures production efficiency and material utilization.']
  yield_variance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Variance between planned and actual yield, calculated as (actual_yield - planned_yield) / planned_yield * 100. Positive values indicate over-yield, negative values indicate under-yield.']

  Note: 'Core manufacturing execution record representing a discrete production run, work order, or rework order issued against a finished goods SKU or semi-finished intermediate — serving as the complete lifecycle record from creation through confirmation and closure. Captures order type (standard, rework, trial), planned vs. actual quantities, scheduled start/end times, actual start/end times, production line assignment, shift, batch size, yield, scrap, rework quantities, order status (created, released, in-progress, confirmed, technically complete, closed), SAP PP production order number, MES work order reference, BOM version used, routing version, cost center, plant code, FIFO/FEFO lot consumption flags, rework reason code and originating order reference (if rework type), rework yield and disposition (if rework), confirmation number, confirmed quantity, yield variance percentage, scrap quantity confirmed, rework quantity confirmed, confirmation type (partial, final), goods movement document number, confirming operator ID, and confirmation datetime. Primary transactional anchor for manufacturing execution in SAP S/4HANA PP module. Rework orders are modeled as production orders with type=rework per SAP PP standard. Confirmations (CO11N) are lifecycle events on this entity — each production order carries its own confirmation records including partial and final confirmations, goods movements, and yield variance calculations.'
}

Table manufacturing.batch_record {
  batch_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the electronic batch record. Primary key for the batch record entity. Serves as the system-generated surrogate key for tracking manufacturing batch execution history.']
  co_packer_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the co-packer supplier who manufactured this batch. Populated only when co_packer_flag is True. Links to supplier master data for contract terms and quality agreements.']
  formulation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the approved formulation (recipe) used for this batch. Links to the bill of materials and ingredient specifications.']
  formulation_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.formulation_version. Business justification: Batch traceability to the specific R&D formulation version used is required for product recall, shelf‑life, and regulatory documentation.']
  inbound_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.inbound_shipment. Business justification: Batch traceability to the inbound shipment is required for recall, quality investigation, and regulatory reporting.']
  inspection_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_lot. Business justification: Inspection results are recorded per batch; linking enables release decisions and traceability of quality inspections.']
  primary_batch_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary production operator responsible for executing this batch. Used for accountability, training verification, and performance tracking.']
  production_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production order that authorized this batch manufacturing run. Links the batch record to the planning and scheduling system.']
  promotion_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trade.promotion_event. Business justification: Batch records must be tied to the Promotion Event they fulfill for traceability, ROI analysis, and regulatory reporting of promotional batches.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Batch traceability to the source PO is required for recalls and cost allocation.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the finished goods SKU being manufactured in this batch. Identifies the specific product variant produced.']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing plant or facility where this batch was produced. Critical for multi-site traceability and regulatory compliance.']
  tertiary_batch_qa_reviewer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the quality assurance professional who reviewed and approved this batch for release. Required for batch disposition decision and regulatory compliance.']
  waste_generation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.waste_generation. Business justification: Waste generation reports are produced per batch; linking batch_record to waste_record enables accurate waste accounting.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production line or work center where this batch was manufactured. Identifies the physical manufacturing resource.']
  actual_yield_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The actual quantity of finished goods produced and approved from this batch. May differ from batch size due to process losses, quality rejections, or overproduction.']
  allergen_statement varchar [note: 'The allergen declaration for this batch as it appears on the product label. Lists all major allergens present (milk, eggs, fish, shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, sesame) and may-contain statements for cross-contact allergens. Critical for consumer safety and regulatory compliance.']
  batch_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, observations, or special instructions related to this batch. May include process notes, quality observations, equipment issues, or other relevant information not captured in structured fields.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'The unique alphanumeric identifier assigned to a specific production batch. Also known as lot number. This is the externally-known business identifier used for traceability, recall management, and regulatory compliance. Must be unique across all production facilities and time periods.']
  batch_size_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The planned or target quantity of finished goods to be produced in this batch. Represents the theoretical yield based on the formulation and production order.']
  batch_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the batch record. Tracks the batch through manufacturing execution, quality approval, and release stages. In-process indicates active manufacturing; pending QA indicates awaiting quality approval; released indicates approved for distribution; rejected indicates failed quality checks; quarantine indicates isolated pending investigation; on hold indicates regulatory or quality hold.']
  best_before_date date [note: 'The date until which the product is expected to retain optimal quality characteristics. May differ from expiry date. Used for quality assurance and consumer guidance. Common in food and beverage products where safety extends beyond optimal quality period.']
  co_packer_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this batch was manufactured by a third-party co-packer (contract manufacturer) rather than in-house. True indicates co-packer production; False indicates internal manufacturing. Critical for supply chain visibility and quality accountability.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this batch record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data governance and compliance.']
  deviation_count varchar [note: 'The total number of manufacturing deviations, exceptions, or non-conformances recorded during batch execution. Includes process parameter excursions, equipment failures, and procedural deviations. Zero indicates perfect execution. Used for quality trending and CAPA analysis.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date after which the product should not be consumed or used. Calculated based on manufacturing date plus shelf life days. Critical for FEFO inventory management and regulatory compliance.']
  gluten_free_claim_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this batch carries a gluten-free claim on the label. True indicates gluten-free claim present; False indicates no claim. Must meet FDA gluten-free standard of less than 20 ppm gluten. Requires testing and validation.']
  gmp_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this batch was manufactured in full compliance with Good Manufacturing Practice requirements. True indicates all GMP controls were met; False indicates GMP deviations occurred. Critical for regulatory audit trail.']
  haccp_signoff_status varchar [note: 'Status of HACCP critical control point verification and approval for this batch. Indicates whether all critical control points were monitored and within acceptable limits. Pending indicates awaiting review; approved indicates all CCPs passed; rejected indicates CCP failure; not applicable for products not requiring HACCP.']
  halal_certification_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this batch is certified halal per Islamic dietary law. True indicates halal certified; False indicates not certified. Requires halal certificate and compliance documentation.']
  kosher_certification_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this batch is certified kosher by a recognized kosher certification agency. True indicates kosher certified; False indicates not certified. Requires kosher certificate and supervision records.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this batch record was last updated. Audit trail field for tracking changes and data lineage.']
  manufacturing_date date [note: 'The calendar date of batch production. This is the date printed on product labels and used for regulatory compliance. Typically the date manufacturing started or the date the batch was completed, per company policy.']
  manufacturing_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when batch manufacturing execution was completed. Marks the completion of all processing steps and final packaging. Used for cycle time analysis and OEE calculation.']
  manufacturing_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when batch manufacturing execution began. Marks the start of raw material dispensing and processing. Critical for shelf life calculation and FIFO/FEFO inventory management.']
  non_gmo_verified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this batch is verified as non-genetically modified organism (non-GMO) by a third-party certification program. True indicates non-GMO verified; False indicates not verified or contains GMO ingredients.']
  organic_certification_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this batch is certified organic per USDA NOP or equivalent organic standards. True indicates certified organic; False indicates conventional. Requires organic certificate and audit trail.']
  packaging_configuration varchar [note: 'Description of the primary and secondary packaging used for this batch. Includes package type, size, and material. Examples: 12oz PET bottle 24-pack, 500g stand-up pouch, 1L Tetra Pak carton. Links to packaging specification.']
  parent_batch_number varchar [note: 'The batch number of the original batch from which reworked material was sourced. Populated only when rework_flag is True. Maintains traceability chain for reworked batches.']
  qa_approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the quality assurance reviewer approved this batch for release to distribution. Marks the transition from pending QA to released status.']
  regulatory_hold_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this batch is under regulatory hold pending investigation or approval from regulatory authorities (FDA, USDA, etc.). True indicates hold in place; False indicates no regulatory restriction. Prevents distribution until cleared.']
  regulatory_hold_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason for regulatory hold. Populated only when regulatory_hold_flag is True. Documents the specific regulatory concern, investigation, or pending approval.']
  rework_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this batch includes reworked material from a previous batch. True indicates rework present; False indicates all new material. Rework must be documented per GMP requirements.']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'The number of days from manufacturing date to expiry date. Defines the product stability period. Used to calculate expiry and best before dates. Derived from product specification and stability studies.']
  sterilization_method varchar [note: 'The thermal or non-thermal sterilization process applied to this batch. Pasteurization indicates low-temperature short-time treatment; UHT indicates ultra-high temperature; retort indicates in-container sterilization; aseptic indicates sterile processing and packaging; none indicates no sterilization (e.g., dry goods); other for alternative methods. Critical for food safety and shelf life.']
  toll_manufacturing_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this batch was produced under a toll manufacturing arrangement where the company provides raw materials and the toll manufacturer provides processing services. True indicates toll manufacturing; False indicates standard production. Distinct from co-packing where the manufacturer may source materials.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for batch size and yield quantities. Common values include KG (kilograms), LB (pounds), L (liters), GAL (gallons), EA (each), CS (cases). Must align with SKU base unit of measure.']

  Note: 'Electronic batch record (EBR) capturing the complete manufacturing history of a single production batch from raw material dispensing through finished goods release. Includes batch number (lot number), batch size, unit of measure, manufacturing date, expiry date, best-before date, shelf life days, batch status (in-process, pending QA, released, rejected, quarantine), GMP compliance flag, HACCP sign-off status, deviation count, operator IDs, line supervisor sign-off, co-packer flag, toll manufacturing flag, and regulatory hold indicator. Serves as the legal manufacturing record per FDA 21 CFR Part 211 and GMP requirements. Sourced from Aveva/Wonderware MES Batch Management module.'
}

Table manufacturing.work_center {
  work_center_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the work center. Primary key.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.asset. Business justification: Work centers are major machines; linking to maintenance asset enables preventive maintenance scheduling and compliance reporting.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the financial cost center to which production costs and overhead for this work center are allocated.']
  environmental_instrument_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.environmental_instrument. Business justification: Renewable Energy Certificates are allocated to specific equipment/work centers for compliance and reporting.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing plant where this work center is located.']
  production_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_line. Business justification: Work center is part of a production line; adding production_line_id links work_center to its line and provides needed connectivity.']
  responsible_supervisor_employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee']
  scada_node_id varchar [note: 'SCADA system node identifier for real-time monitoring and control of this work center\'s automation equipment.']
  active_from_date date [note: 'Date when this work center became operational and available for production scheduling.']
  active_to_date date [note: 'Date when this work center was decommissioned or taken out of service. Null for currently active work centers.']
  allergen_capable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this work center is capable of processing products containing major food allergens (milk, eggs, fish, shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans). Critical for allergen control and production scheduling.']
  allergen_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of specific allergen types that this work center is approved to handle (e.g., milk, peanuts, tree nuts, soy). Empty if allergen_capable_flag is false. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: milk|eggs|fish|shellfish|tree_nuts|peanuts|wheat|soybeans|sesame|sulfites — promote to reference product]']
  available_hours_per_day decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total available production hours per day for this work center based on shift pattern and planned maintenance windows.']
  capacity_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the rated capacity (e.g., cases per hour, bottles per minute, pounds per hour). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cases|bottles|cans|pounds|kilograms|liters|gallons|pallets — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  changeover_time_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total time in minutes required to switch from one product or SKU to another, including teardown, cleaning, setup, and validation. Key metric for production efficiency and scheduling.']
  cip_cycle_time_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard duration in minutes for a complete CIP cleaning cycle on this work center. Null if CIP is not required.']
  cip_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this work center requires automated clean-in-place procedures between production runs. Critical for liquid processing, dairy, and beverage lines.']
  co_packing_approved_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this work center is approved for co-packing or toll manufacturing operations for third-party brands.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this work center record was first created in the system.']
  gmp_classification varchar [note: 'GMP hygiene classification level of the work center determining the sanitation, personnel, and environmental control requirements.']
  haccp_ccp_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this work center contains one or more HACCP critical control points requiring continuous monitoring and documentation.']
  halal_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this work center is certified for halal food production by a recognized halal certification body.']
  kosher_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this work center is certified for kosher food production by a recognized kosher certification agency.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent preventive maintenance or major service performed on this work center.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this work center record was last updated in the system.']
  line_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the work center by its primary production process type. Determines the type of operations and products that can be manufactured on this line. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mixing|filling|packaging|pasteurization|fermentation|extrusion|co-packing|blending|bottling|canning — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  maximum_run_size decimal(15,3) [note: 'Maximum production quantity that can be manufactured on this work center in a single run due to equipment, storage, or process constraints.']
  mes_asset_tag varchar [note: 'Unique asset identifier used by the MES system (Aveva/Wonderware) to track real-time production data, OEE metrics, and equipment performance for this work center.']
  minimum_run_size decimal(15,3) [note: 'Minimum production quantity that can be economically manufactured on this work center in a single run. Used to enforce MOQ constraints in production scheduling.']
  next_scheduled_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date of the next planned preventive maintenance shutdown for this work center. Used for production scheduling and capacity planning.']
  oee_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target OEE percentage for this work center representing the product of availability, performance, and quality rates. Industry benchmark for world-class manufacturing is 85%.']
  organic_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this work center is certified for organic food production under USDA NOP or equivalent organic standards.']
  production_area varchar [note: 'Physical production area or zone within the plant where the work center is located (e.g., Mixing Room, Filling Hall, Packaging Wing, Cold Storage).']
  rated_capacity_units_per_hour decimal(15,3) [note: 'Maximum theoretical production capacity of the work center expressed in units per hour under optimal operating conditions. Used for production planning and scheduling.']
  run_size_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for minimum and maximum run sizes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cases|bottles|cans|pounds|kilograms|liters|gallons|batches — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  setup_time_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard time in minutes required to prepare the work center for a production run, including equipment configuration, material staging, and pre-operational checks.']
  shift_pattern varchar [note: 'Standard operating shift pattern for this work center determining its available production hours per day.']
  standard_cycle_time_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard time in minutes required to produce one unit or batch on this work center under normal operating conditions. Used for production scheduling and costing.']
  teardown_time_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard time in minutes required to clean, sanitize, and restore the work center after a production run. Critical for GMP compliance and allergen control.']
  work_center_category varchar [note: 'High-level categorization of the work center by its functional role in the manufacturing process.']
  work_center_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the work center used in production planning and scheduling systems. Typically alphanumeric code assigned by manufacturing operations.']
  work_center_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the work center including its purpose, capabilities, and operational characteristics.']
  work_center_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the work center for human identification and reporting purposes.']
  work_center_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the work center indicating its availability for production scheduling and execution.']

  Note: 'Master data for all production work centers, lines, and cells within manufacturing plants. Captures work center code, description, plant, production area, line type (mixing, filling, packaging, pasteurization, fermentation, extrusion, co-packing), rated capacity (units/hour), standard cycle time, setup time, teardown time, OEE target, minimum run size, maximum run size, allergen capability flags, kosher/halal certification flag, clean-in-place (CIP) required flag, work center status (active, maintenance, decommissioned), and MES asset tag. SSOT for production line master data used in scheduling and OEE reporting. Sourced from SAP PP work center master and Aveva/Wonderware MES.'
}

Table manufacturing.production_schedule {
  production_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the production schedule record. Primary key.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Needed for Production Planning report; schedules are built around specific customer orders, linking to account provides accurate capacity planning per customer.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for a production campaign (a series of related production runs for similar products to minimize changeovers). Used in campaign-based manufacturing strategies.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production planner or planning team responsible for creating or maintaining this schedule entry.']
  product_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.product_registration. Business justification: Scheduling must verify product registration status to avoid producing non‑compliant SKUs.']
  production_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production order that this schedule entry is planning for. Links to the production order master data.']
  promotion_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trade.promotion_event. Business justification: Production schedules are aligned with Promotion Events to ensure capacity meets promotional demand; required for the Promotion Capacity Planning report.']
  quality_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.specification. Business justification: Schedules reference the approved product specification to ensure correct parameters and compliance during execution.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) that is scheduled to be produced in this schedule entry.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the co-packing or toll manufacturing partner if this production is outsourced. Null if production is internal.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the work center (production line, machine, or manufacturing cell) where this production is scheduled to occur.']
  allergen_changeover_required boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether allergen-related line clearance and deep cleaning is required before this production run due to allergen matrix considerations.']
  batch_size_target decimal(18,3) [note: 'Target batch size for this production run, expressed in the same UOM as planned quantity. Used for batch manufacturing and Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) compliance.']
  capacity_utilization_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Planned utilization percentage of the work center\'s available capacity for this schedule entry. Used for capacity planning and bottleneck analysis.']
  changeover_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Allocated time in minutes for changeover activities (cleaning, setup, line clearance) before this production run. Critical for sequencing optimization and minimizing downtime.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this production schedule record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  crew_size_required varchar [note: 'Number of operators or crew members required to execute this production schedule entry. Used for labor capacity planning.']
  execution_completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the actual production execution for this schedule entry was completed and confirmed by the MES. Used for schedule vs. actual performance analysis.']
  forecast_demand_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The forecasted demand quantity that this schedule entry is intended to fulfill. Used for demand-driven planning and forecast accuracy tracking.']
  frozen_zone_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this schedule entry falls within the frozen planning horizon where changes are restricted to maintain schedule stability.']
  haccp_ccp_applicable boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this production schedule entry involves HACCP Critical Control Points that require monitoring and documentation per food safety regulations.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this production schedule record was last updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  last_replanning_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this schedule entry was last revised or replanned. Used for tracking schedule stability and replanning frequency.']
  lot_number_planned varchar [note: 'Pre-assigned lot number for traceability purposes. Enables First In First Out (FIFO) and First Expired First Out (FEFO) inventory consumption tracking and lot genealogy.']
  material_availability_status varchar [note: 'Status indicating whether all required raw materials and ingredients are available for this scheduled production. Confirmed indicates full availability; partial indicates some materials available; pending indicates materials not yet checked; shortage indicates insufficient inventory.']
  otif_target_date date [note: 'The target delivery date for On Time In Full (OTIF) performance measurement. Used to track schedule adherence and customer service level.']
  planned_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The quantity of the SKU planned to be produced in this schedule entry, expressed in the base unit of measure for the SKU.']
  planned_quantity_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the planned quantity (e.g., cases, kilograms, liters, pallets). Aligns with SKU base UOM.']
  planning_horizon varchar [note: 'The planning time bucket or horizon this schedule belongs to (e.g., weekly, daily, shift-level). Indicates the granularity of the planning cycle.']
  priority_code varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to this schedule entry. Critical indicates customer-committed or regulatory deadline; high indicates tight inventory; normal indicates standard planning; low indicates buffer or make-to-stock.']
  quality_inspection_required boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this production run requires in-process or post-production quality inspection per Quality Management (QM) protocols.']
  released_to_mes_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this schedule entry was released to the Manufacturing Execution System (MES) for execution. Marks the transition from planning to execution.']
  replanning_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason for the most recent replanning event (e.g., demand change, material shortage, equipment breakdown, customer request). Used for root cause analysis of schedule instability.']
  safety_stock_target decimal(18,3) [note: 'Target safety stock level that this production schedule entry is intended to maintain or replenish. Used for inventory optimization.']
  schedule_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments from the planner regarding special considerations, constraints, or instructions for this schedule entry.']
  schedule_source varchar [note: 'Origin system or method that generated this schedule entry. MRP indicates Material Requirements Planning; manual indicates planner override; IBP indicates Integrated Business Planning system; demand_sensing indicates real-time demand signal; optimization indicates algorithmic sequencing.']
  schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the production schedule entry. Draft indicates preliminary planning; firmed indicates committed plan; released indicates sent to MES for execution; executed indicates production completed; cancelled indicates schedule entry voided.']
  schedule_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the production schedule. Allows tracking of schedule revisions and replanning iterations (e.g., V1, V2, V2.1).']
  scheduled_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total planned production duration in minutes, including run time but excluding changeover time. Used for Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) baseline calculations.']
  scheduled_end_datetime timestamp [note: 'The planned date and time when production for this schedule entry is expected to be completed. Used for capacity planning and On Time In Full (OTIF) tracking.']
  scheduled_start_datetime timestamp [note: 'The planned date and time when production for this schedule entry is expected to begin. Used for capacity planning and sequencing.']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'The order position of this schedule entry within the work center\'s production sequence for the planning period. Used for sequencing optimization and changeover minimization.']
  setup_instructions varchar [note: 'Special setup or configuration instructions for the work center to execute this schedule entry. May reference standard operating procedures (SOPs) or work instructions.']
  shift_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the production shift during which this schedule entry is planned (e.g., Day, Night, Swing). Used for labor planning and shift performance analysis.']

  Note: 'Forward-looking production schedule records generated by the planning system (SAP IBP / JDA Blue Yonder) and executed via MES. Captures schedule version, planning horizon, scheduled production order reference, work center assignment, SKU, planned quantity, scheduled start datetime, scheduled end datetime, schedule status (draft, firmed, released, executed, cancelled), schedule source (MRP, manual, IBP), changeover time allocated, sequence number on line, and last replanning timestamp. Enables sequencing optimization, changeover minimization, and OTIF performance tracking.'
}

Table manufacturing.oee_event {
  oee_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the OEE event record. Primary key for the OEE event entity.']
  equipment_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.equipment_master. Business justification: OEE events are tied to a specific equipment asset; linking to equipment_master gives full asset details and eliminates the loosely typed equipment_tag.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing plant or facility where this OEE event occurred.']
  primary_oee_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production operator who was responsible for the work center when the event occurred.']
  production_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production order that was active when this OEE event occurred.']
  production_shift_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production shift during which this OEE event occurred.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product (SKU) that was being manufactured when this OEE event occurred.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the work center (production line or equipment) where this OEE event occurred.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'The production batch or lot number that was being processed when the OEE event occurred, enabling full traceability per FIFO/FEFO requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this OEE event record was first created in the MES system, representing when the event was initially captured.']
  duration_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total duration of the OEE loss event in minutes, calculated as the difference between event_end_timestamp and event_start_timestamp.']
  event_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the OEE loss event ended and normal production resumed, captured from the MES system.']
  event_source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the source system that captured and reported this OEE event (e.g., Aveva Wonderware MES, SCADA system, manual entry).']
  event_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the OEE loss event began, captured from the MES system in real-time.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the OEE event from occurrence through resolution. Tracks the event from initial detection through maintenance response to final closure.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the OEE loss event type. Determines which OEE component (availability, performance, quality) is impacted.']
  fault_code varchar [note: 'Technical fault code or alarm code captured from the equipment control system at the time of the event, used for detailed troubleshooting and maintenance analysis.']
  gmp_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this OEE event triggered a GMP compliance review or documentation requirement. True if GMP review is required, False otherwise.']
  haccp_ccp_impacted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this OEE event impacted a HACCP Critical Control Point, requiring food safety review and documentation. True if CCP was impacted, False otherwise.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this OEE event record was last updated, tracking changes to root cause, resolution actions, or status updates.']
  loss_category varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the loss event was planned (scheduled maintenance, changeover) or unplanned (breakdown, quality issue, material shortage).']
  loss_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized reason code from MES that categorizes the root cause of the loss event (e.g., mechanical failure, electrical fault, material shortage, utility failure, operator error, quality hold, sanitation hold).']
  maintenance_work_order_number varchar [note: 'The work order number from the maintenance management system if a maintenance work order was raised in response to this event. Null if no work order was created.']
  maintenance_work_order_raised_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a maintenance work order was created in response to this OEE event. True if a work order was raised, False otherwise.']
  oee_component_impacted varchar [note: 'Identifies which of the three OEE components (availability, performance, quality) is primarily impacted by this loss event.']
  operator_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by the production operator providing additional context, observations, or details about the OEE event.']
  production_loss_units decimal(12,2) [note: 'Quantity of production units lost or not produced due to this OEE event, measured in the unit of measure of the product being manufactured.']
  resolution_action varchar [note: 'Description of the corrective action taken to resolve the OEE event and restore normal production, entered by operator or maintenance personnel.']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Operator-entered or maintenance technician-entered description of the root cause of the OEE loss event, captured for continuous improvement analysis.']
  stoppage_category varchar [note: 'Detailed classification of downtime events into categories such as mechanical failure, electrical fault, material shortage, utility failure, operator error, quality hold, or sanitation hold. Applicable only when event_type is downtime.']

  Note: 'Granular OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) event records captured from Aveva/Wonderware MES Performance Analytics module, encompassing all availability, performance, and quality loss events including unplanned line stoppages with full lifecycle tracking from occurrence through maintenance response to resolution. Each record represents a discrete loss event on a work center during a production run. Captures event type (downtime, speed loss, minor stoppage, quality reject, planned maintenance, changeover, mechanical failure, electrical fault, material shortage, utility failure, operator error, quality hold, sanitation hold), event start/end timestamp, duration minutes, loss category (planned/unplanned), loss reason code, fault code, equipment tag, stoppage category for downtime events, maintenance work order raised flag, maintenance work order number, production loss units, affected production order, work center, shift, operator-entered root cause, resolution action, operator-entered notes, and OEE component impacted (availability, performance, quality). This is the single source of truth for ALL production line downtime and loss events — both brief speed losses and extended stoppages requiring maintenance intervention. Foundation for OEE calculation, reliability KPIs, MTBF/MTTR analysis, and continuous improvement programs.'
}

Table manufacturing.changeover {
  changeover_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the changeover event. Primary key for the changeover data product.']
  changeover_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the QA (Quality Assurance) operator who signed off on the changeover verification.']
  changeover_production_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the production order that was running before the changeover event.']
  changeover_production_order_to_production_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the production order that will run after the changeover event.']
  changeover_sanitation_crew_lead_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sanitation crew lead responsible for executing the changeover and cleaning procedures.']
  changeover_sku_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) that was being produced before the changeover.']
  changeover_sku_to_sku_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) that will be produced after the changeover.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant where the changeover occurred.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the work center or production line where the changeover occurred.']
  actual_duration_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual duration of the changeover event in minutes from start to completion.']
  actual_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual end date and time when the changeover event was completed in format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual start date and time when the changeover event began in format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  allergen_clearance_verified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether allergen clearance was verified and approved. True if verified, False if not applicable or not verified.']
  allergen_from varchar [note: 'Allergen present in the SKU being produced before the changeover. Used for allergen management and clearance verification.']
  allergen_to varchar [note: 'Allergen present in the SKU being produced after the changeover. Used for allergen management and clearance verification.']
  atp_rlu_value varchar [note: 'Numeric RLU (Relative Light Units) value from the ATP swab test. Lower values indicate cleaner surfaces.']
  atp_swab_test_result varchar [note: 'Result of the ATP (Adenosine Triphosphate) swab test used to detect organic contamination on surfaces.']
  cfu_count varchar [note: 'CFU (Colony Forming Units) count from the microbiological swab test. Indicates the level of bacterial contamination.']
  chemical_agent_used varchar [note: 'Name or code of the chemical cleaning agent used during the sanitation process.']
  chemical_concentration_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Concentration level of the chemical agent used, expressed as a percentage.']
  cip_cycle_completed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the CIP (Clean-in-Place) cycle was completed successfully. True if completed, False if not applicable or incomplete.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the changeover record was first created in the system in format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  deviation_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Deviation from planned duration in minutes. Positive values indicate the changeover took longer than planned; negative values indicate it was faster.']
  deviation_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes explaining any deviations, issues, or special circumstances encountered during the changeover.']
  deviation_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the reason for any deviation from the planned changeover duration or procedure.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the changeover event.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Type of changeover event. CIP = Clean-in-Place, COP = Clean-out-of-Place. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: flavor_to_flavor|allergen_to_allergen|allergen_to_non_allergen|non_allergen_to_allergen|format_change|cip|cop|dry_clean|full_sanitation|allergen_clean|scheduled_sanitation|unscheduled_sanitation — promote to reference product]']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the changeover record was last modified in the system in format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  line_clearance_checklist_completed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the line clearance checklist was completed. True if completed, False if incomplete.']
  microbiological_swab_result varchar [note: 'Result of the microbiological swab test to detect bacterial contamination.']
  overall_pass_fail_status varchar [note: 'Overall pass or fail status of the changeover event based on all verification tests and procedures.']
  planned_duration_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Planned duration of the changeover event in minutes based on standard operating procedures.']
  production_clearance_granted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether production clearance was granted to resume operations after the changeover. True if granted, False if pending or denied.']
  rinse_verification_result varchar [note: 'Result of the rinse verification test to ensure all chemical residues have been removed.']
  sanitation_procedure_code varchar [note: 'Standard operating procedure code for the sanitation method used during the changeover.']
  sanitation_type varchar [note: 'Type of sanitation procedure performed during the changeover event. None indicates no sanitation was required.']
  schedule_reference_code varchar [note: 'Reference code linking the changeover event to the production schedule or planning system.']
  scheduled_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned end date and time for the changeover event in format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  scheduled_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned start date and time for the changeover event in format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']

  Note: 'Records each line changeover, sanitation, and cleaning event between or during production runs on a work center — serving as the single source of truth for all line clearance activities including both product changeovers and standalone sanitation events. Captures event type (flavor-to-flavor changeover, allergen-to-allergen, allergen-to-non-allergen, format change, CIP — Clean-in-Place, COP — Clean-out-of-Place, dry clean, full sanitation, allergen clean, scheduled sanitation, unscheduled sanitation), from-SKU, to-SKU, work center, plant, planned vs. actual duration, start/end timestamps, production order from/to, event status (planned, in-progress, completed, verified), CIP cycle completed flag, allergen clearance verified flag, sanitation procedure code, chemical agents used, concentration levels, rinse verification result, ATP swab test result, microbiological swab result (CFU count), pass/fail status, QA sign-off operator, sanitation crew lead, production clearance granted flag, deviation from standard time, and schedule reference. Critical for scheduling optimization, allergen management, GMP/HACCP/FSMA compliance, and line clearance verification.'
}

Table manufacturing.haccp_ccp_log {
  haccp_ccp_log_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the HACCP critical control point monitoring log entry. Primary key for the log record.']
  equipment_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific equipment or instrument used to perform the CCP monitoring (e.g., thermometer serial number, metal detector unit ID). Critical for calibration traceability.']
  primary_haccp_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the operator or quality technician who performed and verified the CCP monitoring. Establishes accountability and traceability for compliance audits.']
  production_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production order under which this CCP monitoring occurred. Links the CCP log to the manufacturing execution context.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the work center or production line where the CCP monitoring took place. Enables location-specific analysis of control point performance.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'The production batch identifier for which this CCP monitoring was performed. Critical for lot traceability and recall management.']
  ccp_identifier varchar [note: 'The unique code or identifier for the critical control point as defined in the HACCP plan (e.g., CCP-1, CCP-2-PASTEURIZE).']
  ccp_name varchar [note: 'The descriptive name of the critical control point being monitored (e.g., Pasteurization Temperature, Metal Detection, pH Check, Brix Measurement, Allergen Verification).']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, observations, or context related to the CCP monitoring event. May include operator observations, environmental conditions, or other relevant details.']
  corrective_action_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether a corrective action is required based on the CCP monitoring result (True = corrective action needed, False = no action needed).']
  corrective_action_taken varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the corrective action(s) taken in response to a CCP deviation. Documents the specific steps taken to bring the process back into control and disposition of affected product.']
  critical_limit_lower decimal(18,4) [note: 'The minimum acceptable value for this CCP as defined in the HACCP plan. Values below this threshold indicate a deviation requiring corrective action.']
  critical_limit_upper decimal(18,4) [note: 'The maximum acceptable value for this CCP as defined in the HACCP plan. Values above this threshold indicate a deviation requiring corrective action.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'The origin of the CCP monitoring data (mes_automated = automatically captured from Aveva/Wonderware MES, manual_entry = operator-entered, laboratory_system = from LIMS, sensor_network = from IoT sensors).']
  deviation_severity varchar [note: 'Classification of the severity of the CCP deviation if one occurred (minor = slight deviation with low risk, major = significant deviation requiring investigation, critical = severe deviation with immediate food safety risk).']
  hazard_type varchar [note: 'The category of food safety hazard that this CCP is designed to control (biological, chemical, physical, or allergen).']
  in_limit_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the measured value falls within the defined critical limits (True = within limits, False = out of specification).']
  measured_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The actual measured or observed value at the critical control point (e.g., temperature reading, pH level, Brix degree, metal detector sensitivity test result).']
  monitoring_frequency varchar [note: 'The required frequency of monitoring for this CCP as defined in the HACCP plan (e.g., continuous, every 15 minutes, every batch, hourly).']
  monitoring_method varchar [note: 'The method or procedure used to monitor this CCP (e.g., calibrated thermometer, pH meter, metal detector test, visual inspection, laboratory analysis).']
  monitoring_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the CCP measurement or observation was recorded. Critical for establishing real-time compliance and traceability.']
  product_disposition varchar [note: 'The final disposition decision for product affected by this CCP monitoring event (released = approved for distribution, rework = reprocessed to meet specifications, reprocess = returned to earlier production step, destroyed = disposed of, quarantine = held pending investigation).']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this CCP log record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data integrity and compliance.']
  record_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this CCP log record was last modified. Critical for audit trail and data integrity compliance.']
  regulatory_hold_triggered_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this CCP deviation triggered a regulatory hold on the batch, preventing release until investigation and disposition are complete (True = hold triggered, False = no hold).']
  review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the supervisor or quality manager completed their review of this CCP log entry.']
  supervisor_review_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this CCP log entry requires supervisor or quality manager review due to deviation or other exceptional circumstances (True = review required, False = no review needed).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit in which the measured value is expressed (e.g., Celsius, Fahrenheit, pH units, Brix degrees, ppm, CFU/g).']
  verification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the CCP monitoring record was verified and signed off by the responsible operator or quality personnel.']

  Note: 'Real-time and operator-entered log of HACCP (Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points) critical control point (CCP) monitoring records for each production batch. Captures CCP identifier, CCP name (e.g., pasteurization temperature, metal detection, pH check, Brix measurement), production order, batch number, work center, monitoring timestamp, measured value, unit of measure, critical limit lower bound, critical limit upper bound, in-limit flag, corrective action required flag, corrective action taken description, verified by operator ID, and regulatory hold triggered flag. Mandatory for FDA FSMA compliance and GFSI certification. Sourced from Aveva/Wonderware MES and manual entry. SSOT boundary: This entity owns real-time CCP monitoring readings and corrective actions during production execution. The quality domain owns the HACCP plan definition, hazard analysis, and CCP validation/verification programs.'
}

Table manufacturing.gmp_event {
  gmp_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the GMP compliance event record. Primary key.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing plant where the GMP event occurred. Supports multi-site compliance tracking and regulatory reporting.']
  primary_gmp_reported_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who reported or documented the GMP event. Supports accountability and recognition of quality culture.']
  production_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production order during which the GMP event occurred. Links the event to the specific manufacturing run.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product being manufactured when the GMP event occurred. Enables product-specific compliance tracking and risk assessment.']
  tertiary_gmp_closed_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee or quality manager who approved closure of the GMP event. Null if event is not yet closed.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the work center or production line where the GMP event was observed. Enables analysis of event patterns by production area.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'The batch or lot number of the product being manufactured when the GMP event occurred. Critical for traceability and potential recall scenarios.']
  capa_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number linking this GMP event to a formal CAPA record in the quality management system. Null if no CAPA was initiated.']
  ccp_number varchar [note: 'The specific HACCP Critical Control Point number if this event relates to a CCP. Null if the event does not involve a CCP.']
  closure_datetime timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the GMP event was formally closed after all corrective actions were verified as effective. Null if event is not yet closed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this GMP event record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  event_datetime timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the GMP event was observed or occurred on the production floor. This is the actual event timestamp, distinct from when it was recorded in the system.']
  event_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the GMP event, typically formatted as GMP-YYYYMMDD-NNNN for external reference and audit trail.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the GMP event. Open indicates newly reported, under investigation indicates active root cause analysis, pending approval indicates awaiting management review, closed indicates investigation complete and actions implemented, cancelled indicates event was determined invalid or duplicate.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the GMP event indicating the nature of the compliance issue or action. Deviation indicates departure from approved procedures, observation is a potential issue noted, non-conformance is a failure to meet requirements, corrective action addresses existing problems, preventive action prevents potential problems, and near miss is an event that could have resulted in a deviation.']
  external_audit_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier linking this GMP event to an external audit finding from FDA, USDA, GFSI auditor, or customer audit. Null if not related to an external audit.']
  fda_notification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this GMP event requires specific notification to the FDA under food safety regulations. True if FDA notification is required, false otherwise.']
  financial_impact_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated or actual financial impact of the GMP event, including costs of rework, scrap, investigation, and potential regulatory penalties. Measured in the company\'s reporting currency.']
  gmp_event_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the GMP event, including what was observed, the circumstances, and any immediate context. This field captures the full story of the event for investigation and audit purposes.']
  haccp_ccp_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this GMP event occurred at or relates to a HACCP Critical Control Point. True if the event involves a CCP, false otherwise. Critical for food safety risk assessment.']
  immediate_action_taken varchar [note: 'Description of the immediate containment or corrective actions taken at the time the GMP event was discovered. Documents the initial response to prevent further impact.']
  impact_assessment varchar [note: 'Detailed assessment of the potential or actual impact of the GMP event on product quality, food safety, regulatory compliance, and business operations. Documents the risk evaluation performed.']
  investigation_completion_datetime timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the investigation of the GMP event was completed and root cause determined. Null if investigation is still in progress.']
  investigation_start_datetime timestamp [note: 'The date and time when formal investigation of the GMP event began. Used to track investigation cycle time and compliance with internal SLAs.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this GMP event record was last updated in the data platform. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  previous_event_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the event number of a previous related GMP event if this is a recurrence. Null if this is a first occurrence or not linked to a prior event.']
  product_disposition varchar [note: 'Final disposition decision for the product batch affected by the GMP event. Released indicates product approved for distribution, rework indicates product requires reprocessing, regrade indicates product downgraded to alternate use, quarantine indicates product held pending further evaluation, destroy indicates product must be disposed of, under evaluation indicates disposition not yet determined.']
  quantity_affected decimal(18,3) [note: 'The quantity of product affected by the GMP event, measured in the product\'s base unit of measure. Used to assess the scale of impact and potential financial exposure.']
  quantity_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the quantity affected field. Common units include kg (kilograms), lb (pounds), l (liters), gal (gallons), ea (each), case, or pallet. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: kg|lb|l|gal|ea|case|pallet — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  recurrence_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this GMP event is a recurrence of a previously documented event with the same or similar root cause. True if recurrence, false if first occurrence. Used to identify systemic issues.']
  regulatory_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this GMP event meets the criteria for mandatory reporting to regulatory authorities such as FDA or USDA. True if reportable, false otherwise.']
  reported_datetime timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the GMP event was formally reported and recorded in the quality management system. May differ from event_datetime if there was a delay in reporting.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the root cause of the GMP event. Used for trend analysis and identification of systemic issues requiring corrective action. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: equipment_failure|human_error|material_defect|process_deviation|environmental_condition|documentation_error|training_gap|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the GMP event. Critical indicates potential for serious harm to consumers or significant regulatory impact, major indicates substantial deviation from GMP requirements, and minor indicates low-impact deviation with minimal risk.']
  usda_notification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this GMP event requires specific notification to the USDA, typically for meat, poultry, or egg product manufacturing. True if USDA notification is required, false otherwise.']
  veeva_vault_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the master document record in Veeva Vault QMS where the complete GMP event investigation file, attachments, and audit trail are stored.']

  Note: 'Records of GMP (Good Manufacturing Practice) compliance events, observations, and deviations captured during production operations. Captures event type (deviation, observation, non-conformance, corrective action, preventive action), severity level (critical, major, minor), event datetime, production order, batch number, work center, plant, description, root cause category, immediate action taken, CAPA reference number, regulatory reportable flag, FDA/USDA notification required flag, event status (open, under investigation, closed), and closure datetime. Supports GMP audit readiness and regulatory inspection response. Managed in Veeva Vault QMS. SSOT boundary: This entity owns production-floor GMP events and deviations observed during manufacturing execution. The quality domain owns GMP audit programs, audit findings, supplier GMP assessments, and CAPA lifecycle management.'
}

Table manufacturing.lot_consumption {
  lot_consumption_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the lot consumption transaction record. Primary key for the lot consumption entity.']
  batch_record_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the batch record documenting the production run. Links lot consumption to the specific batch manufacturing execution record for GMP compliance and traceability.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Needed for Material Consumption Costing and variance analysis; cost_center_code is a denormalized code that should reference finance.cost_center.']
  ingredient_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ingredient.lot. Business justification: REQUIRED: Enables lot‑level traceability for recalls and FDA FSMA reporting by linking each consumption record to the specific ingredient lot used.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.plant. Business justification: Lot consumption records occur at a plant; linking to plant provides authoritative plant data and removes the free‑text plant_code.']
  po_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.po_line. Business justification: Linking consumption to a specific PO line enables variance analysis and accurate spend tracking.']
  production_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production order against which this lot consumption occurred. Links consumption to the manufacturing order that triggered the material usage.']
  promotion_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trade.promotion_event. Business justification: Material consumption is allocated to specific promotions for accurate cost‑of‑goods‑sold and promotional spend reconciliation.']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: REQUIRED: Links each consumption event to the exact inventory lot consumed, supporting lot traceability, regulatory reporting, and OTIF calculations.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Identifying the supplier of each consumed lot supports quality audits and allergen compliance.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the work center or production line where the material was consumed. Links consumption to the specific manufacturing resource.']
  allergen_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether the consumed material contains or may contain allergens as defined by FDA and FALCPA regulations. Used for allergen control and cross-contamination prevention.']
  backflush_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this consumption was automatically backflushed based on production confirmation (True) or manually posted (False). Backflushing automates material consumption based on BOM and production output.']
  bom_quantity decimal(18,6) [note: 'The planned or standard quantity of this material specified in the Bill of Materials for the production order. Used to calculate consumption variance.']
  confirmation_status varchar [note: 'The current status of the consumption transaction in the system workflow: confirmed (posted to inventory), pending (awaiting approval), rejected (not accepted), or reversed (cancelled after posting).']
  consumption_quantity decimal(18,6) [note: 'The quantity of material consumed from the specified lot during production. Recorded in the unit of measure specified in the unit_of_measure field.']
  consumption_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the material lot was consumed in the production process. Critical for FIFO/FEFO compliance and production timeline tracking.']
  consumption_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the consumption event indicating the purpose or nature of material usage: standard production, rework/reprocessing, trial run, quality sample, or waste/scrap.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'The user ID or username of the person who created this consumption record. Used for accountability and audit purposes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this lot consumption record was first created in the system. Part of the audit trail for data governance and compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the cost values are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  fifo_fefo_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number indicating the order in which lots were consumed according to FIFO or FEFO inventory management rules. Lower numbers indicate earlier consumption priority.']
