  gmo_status varchar [note: 'Classification of the consumed material regarding genetically modified organism content: GMO (contains GMO), non-GMO (verified non-GMO), or unknown (not tested/declared).']
  halal_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether the consumed lot is certified halal according to Islamic dietary law. Required for halal product manufacturing and labeling.']
  kosher_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether the consumed lot is certified kosher by a recognized kosher certification agency. Required for kosher product manufacturing and labeling.']
  lot_expiry_date date [note: 'The expiration or best-before date of the consumed lot. Used to enforce FEFO consumption rules and ensure materials are used before expiration.']
  material_document_number varchar [note: 'The unique document number generated by the ERP system for this goods movement transaction. Used for audit trail and transaction traceability.']
  material_document_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year in which the material document was posted. Combined with material_document_number forms a unique key in the source system.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'The user ID or username of the person who last modified this consumption record. Used for accountability and change tracking.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this lot consumption record was last modified. Tracks the most recent change for audit and data quality purposes.']
  movement_type varchar [note: 'The SAP movement type code that classifies the inventory transaction (e.g., 261 for goods issue to production order, 262 for reversal). Determines how the consumption affects inventory and financial accounts.']
  organic_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether the consumed lot is certified organic according to USDA NOP or equivalent organic certification standards. Critical for organic product integrity and labeling compliance.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The date on which the consumption transaction was posted to the financial and inventory ledgers. May differ from consumption_timestamp for backdated postings.']
  reservation_number varchar [note: 'The material reservation document number that allocated this lot for consumption against the production order. Links consumption to the planned material requirement.']
  supplier_lot_number varchar [note: 'The original lot or batch number assigned by the supplier for the consumed material. Critical for traceability back to the raw material source and supplier quality investigations.']
  total_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total cost of the consumed quantity, calculated as consumption_quantity * unit_cost. Represents the material cost component of the production order.']
  unit_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'The standard or actual cost per unit of measure for the consumed material at the time of consumption. Used for production cost calculation and COGS determination.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit in which the consumption quantity is measured (e.g., KG, LB, EA, L, GAL). Must align with the material master unit of measure.']
  variance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The consumption variance expressed as a percentage of the BOM quantity. Calculated as (variance_quantity / bom_quantity) * 100.']
  variance_quantity decimal(18,6) [note: 'The difference between actual consumption quantity and the BOM planned quantity. Positive values indicate over-consumption; negative values indicate under-consumption.']
  warehouse_location_code varchar [note: 'The storage location or warehouse from which the material lot was withdrawn for consumption. Links to the warehouse management system location master.']

  Note: 'Transactional records of raw material, ingredient, and packaging component lot consumption against production orders, implementing FIFO/FEFO inventory management. Captures production order, batch record, consumed material code, consumed lot number, consumption quantity, unit of measure, consumption timestamp, FIFO/FEFO sequence number, expiry date of consumed lot, warehouse storage location, consumption type (standard, rework, trial), variance from BOM quantity, and confirmation status. Provides full lot traceability from raw material to finished goods batch. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM goods movement and MES batch management.'
}

Table manufacturing.copacking_order {
  copacking_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the co-packing or toll manufacturing order. Primary key for this entity.']
  bill_of_materials_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific BOM record used for this co-packing order.']
  copacker_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the third-party co-manufacturer (co-packer) supplier who will execute the production. Links to procurement supplier master.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.plant. Business justification: Co‑packing orders occur at a plant; linking to plant centralizes location data and removes redundant facility name/location fields.']
  promotion_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trade.promotion_event. Business justification: Co‑packing orders are often executed to supply promotional SKUs; linking enables Promo fulfillment tracking and cost allocation.']
  purchase_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the master co-packing or toll manufacturing contract governing this order.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the purchase order record in the procurement system for this co-packing service.']
  sales_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.order. Business justification: Co‑packing operations are triggered by specific sales orders; linking enables order‑to‑batch traceability and accurate delivery scheduling.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product master record for the SKU being co-packed.']
  actual_production_date date [note: 'The actual date when production was completed by the co-packer.']
  actual_quantity_produced decimal(15,3) [note: 'The actual quantity of finished goods units produced by the co-packer upon order completion.']
  allergen_control_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether allergen control procedures at the co-packer facility have been verified for this order. True if verified, False if not verified.']
  bom_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the BOM (Bill of Materials) supplied to the co-packer for production execution.']
  copacker_batch_number varchar [note: 'The batch or lot number assigned by the co-packer to the production run.']
  copacking_order_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique order number for the co-packing arrangement, used for external communication with co-packer and internal tracking.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who created this co-packing order record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this co-packing order record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the landed cost (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The calculated expiration date for the co-packed product batch based on production date and shelf life.']
  fsma_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the co-packer facility is compliant with FSMA (Food Safety Modernization Act) requirements. True if compliant, False if non-compliant.']
  gfsi_scheme varchar [note: 'The specific GFSI-recognized certification scheme held by the co-packer (e.g., SQF, BRC, FSSC 22000, IFS).']
  gmp_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent GMP audit conducted at the co-packer facility.']
  gmp_audit_status varchar [note: 'Status of the co-packer facility\'s GMP (Good Manufacturing Practice) audit: current (audit valid), expired (audit needs renewal), pending (audit scheduled), not-audited (no audit on file).']
  goods_receipt_date date [note: 'The date when the co-packed finished goods were received into our inventory.']
  goods_receipt_number varchar [note: 'The goods receipt document number generated when the co-packed finished goods are received into inventory.']
  haccp_ccp_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the co-packer has documented compliance with HACCP (Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points) critical control points for this production run. True if compliant, False if non-compliant or not verified.']
  internal_batch_number varchar [note: 'Our internal batch or lot number assigned to the co-packed production for traceability and inventory management.']
  landed_cost_per_unit decimal(15,4) [note: 'The total landed cost per unit for the co-packed product, including manufacturing cost, raw materials (if supplied by us), logistics, and any other charges. Used for COGS (Cost of Goods Sold) calculation.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who last modified this co-packing order record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this co-packing order record was last updated.']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the co-packing order: planned (order created), in-production (manufacturing underway), received (goods received), quality-hold (pending quality clearance), closed (order completed), cancelled (order terminated).']
  planned_production_date date [note: 'The scheduled date when production is planned to start or be completed at the co-packer facility.']
  planned_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'The planned quantity of finished goods units to be produced by the co-packer as per the order.']
  production_line varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific production line at the co-packer facility used for this order.']
  quality_hold_reason varchar [note: 'Description of the reason for quality hold if applicable (e.g., failed inspection, documentation incomplete, sensory deviation).']
  quality_hold_status boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the co-packed goods are currently on quality hold pending inspection and release. True if on hold, False if released or not applicable.']
  raw_materials_supplied_by varchar [note: 'Indicates who supplies the raw materials for production: us (we supply), copacker (co-packer supplies), or mixed (both parties supply components).']
  receiving_warehouse_code varchar [note: 'Code of the warehouse or distribution center where the co-packed goods are received.']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'The shelf life in days assigned to the co-packed product batch from production date.']
  sqf_certification_level varchar [note: 'The level of SQF certification held by the co-packer (Level 1: Food Safety Fundamentals, Level 2: Food Safety Plans, Level 3: Comprehensive Food Safety and Quality).']
  sqf_certification_status varchar [note: 'Status of the co-packer\'s SQF (Safe Quality Food) or GFSI (Global Food Safety Initiative) certification: certified (valid certification), expired (needs renewal), pending (application in progress), not-certified (no certification).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the planned and actual quantities (e.g., cases, units, pallets, kg). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cases|units|pallets|kg|lbs|liters|gallons — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Master and transactional record for co-packing and toll manufacturing arrangements where production is outsourced to a third-party co-manufacturer (co-packer). Captures co-packing order number, co-packer supplier ID, co-packer facility name and location, SKU being co-packed, planned quantity, actual quantity produced, planned production date, actual production date, BOM version supplied, raw materials supplied by (us vs. co-packer), quality hold status, co-packer batch number, our internal batch number assigned, GMP audit status of co-packer, SQF/GFSI certification status, order status (planned, in-production, received, quality-hold, closed), and landed cost per unit. Links to procurement and quality domains.'
}

Table manufacturing.manufacturing_production_bom {
  manufacturing_production_bom_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the production BOM record. Primary key for the production BOM entity.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.plant. Business justification: Links BOM to the plant where it is valid, supporting plant‑specific formulation control and compliance with local regulations.']
  primary_manufacturing_employee_id varchar [note: 'The user ID of the quality or production manager who approved this BOM for production use.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Ensures each production BOM version is tied to the SKU it produces, critical for traceability in batch release and regulatory reporting.']
  allergen_declaration_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this BOM contains any of the major allergens requiring declaration per FDA and FSMA regulations.']
  alternative_bom_group varchar [note: 'Groups alternative BOMs for the same SKU (e.g., seasonal formulations, regional variants, equipment-specific recipes). Null if no alternatives exist.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date when this BOM received final approval for production execution.']
  approved_for_production_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this BOM has passed all quality, regulatory, and operational reviews and is authorized for commercial production.']
  base_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'The standard batch size for which component quantities are defined. Typically expressed in production units (e.g., 1000 kg, 500 cases).']
  base_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the base quantity. Must align with the SKU\'s primary production UOM. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: KG|LB|L|GAL|EA|CS|CASE — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  batch_record_template_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the master batch record template used for GMP documentation when executing this BOM.']
  bom_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the BOM. Only active BOMs are available for production order creation.']
  bom_usage_type varchar [note: 'The primary purpose of this BOM. Production BOMs are executable; costing BOMs are used for standard cost calculation; engineering BOMs are R&D prototypes.']
  bom_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for this BOM configuration. Increments with each formulation change or process improvement.']
  changeover_time_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'The expected time in minutes required to changeover equipment from the previous product to this BOM. Critical for production scheduling optimization.']
  clean_label_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this BOM meets clean label criteria (no artificial ingredients, preservatives, or additives) for marketing and consumer transparency.']
  co_packing_arrangement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this BOM is executed under a co-packing or toll manufacturing arrangement with a third-party manufacturer (3PL).']
  cost_estimate_version varchar [note: 'Links to the standard cost estimate version used for COGS calculation based on this BOM\'s component costs.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this BOM record was first created in the system.']
  gmp_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this BOM has been validated for GMP compliance including proper sequencing, critical control points, and documentation requirements.']
  haccp_critical_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this BOM includes HACCP critical control points requiring enhanced monitoring and documentation during production.']
  inventory_consumption_method varchar [note: 'The method used to consume component inventory during production. FIFO (First In First Out) or FEFO (First Expired First Out) are standard for food and beverage to ensure freshness and minimize waste.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this BOM record was last modified. Tracks all changes for audit and compliance purposes.']
  last_reviewed_date date [note: 'The date when this BOM was last reviewed for accuracy, regulatory compliance, and cost optimization. BOMs should be reviewed annually or upon ingredient changes.']
  lot_traceability_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether full lot-level traceability is required for all components per FSMA traceability rules or customer requirements.']
  plm_formulation_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the R&D formulation record in the PLM system from which this production BOM was derived. Maintains traceability from innovation to manufacturing.']
  production_line_assignment varchar [note: 'The specific production line or work center where this BOM is typically executed. Supports line-specific BOMs for equipment constraints.']
  regulatory_approval_number varchar [note: 'The FDA, USDA, or other regulatory body approval or registration number for this BOM formulation, if required for the product category.']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'The guaranteed shelf life in days for the finished product produced from this BOM, from production date to expiration.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this BOM data originated (e.g., SAP PP, Oracle ERP, Aveva MES).']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier for this BOM in the source system. Enables traceability back to the operational system of record.']
  standard_cycle_time_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'The expected production cycle time in minutes to produce one base quantity batch. Used for capacity planning and OEE calculation.']
  storage_conditions_required varchar [note: 'The required storage conditions for the finished product (e.g., ambient, refrigerated 2-8°C, frozen below -18°C). Critical for warehouse management and distribution planning.']
  valid_from_date date [note: 'The date from which this BOM version becomes effective for production scheduling and execution.']
  valid_to_date date [note: 'The date after which this BOM version is no longer valid for new production orders. Null indicates open-ended validity.']
  yield_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Expected yield percentage accounting for normal production losses, evaporation, and waste. Used for accurate material requirements planning (MRP).']

  Note: 'Manufacturing Bill of Materials (BOM) master data defining the ingredient and packaging component composition for each SKU at a specific plant and BOM version — including both header-level BOM metadata and all component line items as a single integrated entity. Header captures SKU, plant, BOM version, valid-from/to dates, batch size basis, yield percentage, BOM status (active, superseded, trial, obsolete), approved-for-production flag, allergen declaration flag, clean label compliance flag, and last reviewed date. Component lines capture line number, material code, material description, component type (raw material, packaging, semi-finished, by-product), quantity per batch, unit of measure, scrap percentage, issue method (backflush, manual), allergen flag, critical ingredient flag, approved substitutes list, storage conditions required, lead time days, and phantom item flag. This is the single source of truth for all production-executable BOM data (header + all component lines), distinct from the R&D/PLM formulation BOM owned by the product domain. Sourced from SAP PP BOM master (CS01/CS02). Enables precise MRP explosion, lot traceability at the component level, and allergen cross-contact analysis.'
}

Table manufacturing.routing {
  routing_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the production routing master record.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing plant where this routing is executed.']
  routing_approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who approved this routing for production use.']
  routing_created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who created this routing record.']
  routing_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production planner or scheduler responsible for maintaining this routing and resolving production issues.']
  routing_modified_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who last modified this routing record.']
  routing_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the third-party co-packing supplier if this routing represents externally manufactured production. Null for internal production.']
  routing_toll_manufacturer_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the toll manufacturing supplier if this routing represents production where the company provides raw materials and the supplier provides processing. Null for internal or co-packed production.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the finished good SKU that this routing produces.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary production line or work center group where this routing is executed. Used for capacity planning and scheduling.']
  allergen_handling_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this routing requires special allergen handling procedures, such as line changeover cleaning or dedicated equipment, to prevent cross-contact.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'The current approval state of this routing. Approved routings are released for production use; pending routings await engineering or quality approval; rejected routings require rework; expired routings have passed their review date.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this routing was approved for production use.']
  base_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'The standard production lot size for which the routing times are defined. Actual production order times are scaled proportionally from this base.']
  base_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the base quantity (e.g., KG, LB, EA, CS). Aligns with the SKU primary UOM.']
  changeover_time_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'The standard time required to change over the production line from the previous SKU to this routing, including cleaning, setup, and first-article inspection.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this routing record was first created in the system.']
  gmp_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this routing has been validated for GMP compliance. True means all operations meet GMP documentation, training, and control requirements.']
  haccp_critical_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this routing contains one or more HACCP critical control points (CCPs) that require monitoring and documentation per the food safety plan.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'The date when this routing was last reviewed and validated by engineering or production management.']
  lot_size_from decimal(15,3) [note: 'The minimum production lot size for which this routing is valid. Supports lot-size-dependent routing selection.']
  lot_size_to decimal(15,3) [note: 'The maximum production lot size for which this routing is valid. Null indicates no upper limit.']
  maximum_batch_size decimal(15,3) [note: 'The largest production quantity that can be produced in a single batch using this routing, constrained by equipment capacity or process limits.']
  minimum_batch_size decimal(15,3) [note: 'The smallest production quantity that can be economically or technically produced using this routing, expressed in the base UOM.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this routing record was last modified.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next periodic review of this routing to ensure continued accuracy and compliance.']
  operation_count varchar [note: 'The total number of sequential operations (work center steps) defined in this routing.']
  organic_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this routing is certified for organic production under USDA NOP or equivalent standards, requiring segregation and documentation.']
  rework_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether non-conforming product from this routing can be reworked through a defined rework operation sequence.']
  routing_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the routing, typically summarizing the production method or key process characteristics (e.g., \'High-speed aseptic filling line\', \'Manual batch mixing and packaging\').']
  routing_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the routing. The externally-known unique code used to reference this routing in production planning and scheduling systems.']
  routing_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the routing. Active routings are available for production order creation; draft and under-review routings are in development; inactive and obsolete routings are no longer used.']
  routing_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the routing. Standard routings are the default production method; alternative routings provide flexibility for capacity or material constraints; trial routings support NPD; co-packing and toll manufacturing routings reflect external production arrangements.']
  scrap_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The expected percentage of material loss or waste across all operations in this routing, used for material planning and costing.']
  total_standard_production_time_minutes decimal(12,2) [note: 'The sum of all operation standard times (setup, machine, labor) across the routing, expressed in minutes. Used for capacity planning and production scheduling.']
  usage varchar [note: 'The business purpose for which this routing is maintained. Production routings drive MES execution; costing routings feed standard cost calculations; maintenance and quality inspection routings support non-production work orders.']
  valid_from_date date [note: 'The date from which this routing version becomes effective for production planning and scheduling.']
  valid_to_date date [note: 'The date until which this routing version remains effective. Null indicates open-ended validity.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the routing. Allows multiple versions of the same routing to coexist for different time periods or production scenarios.']

  Note: 'Production routing master data defining the sequence of manufacturing operations (work center steps) required to produce a SKU. Captures routing number, routing version, SKU, plant, valid-from/to dates, routing status, total standard production time, and the ordered list of operations with their work center assignments, standard times (setup, machine, labor), and operation descriptions (e.g., mixing, pasteurization, filling, capping, labeling, palletizing). Sourced from SAP PP routing master. Distinct from the BOM (what goes in) — the routing defines how it is made and in what sequence.'
}

Table manufacturing.process_parameter {
  process_parameter_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the process parameter reading record.']
  capa_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the corrective action record initiated in response to this out-of-spec parameter reading, if applicable.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production operator or technician who was responsible for the process at the time of reading, or who manually entered the value. Required for GMP audit trail.']
  equipment_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific production equipment, vessel, or machine from which this parameter was measured.']
  formulation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production recipe or formulation being executed at the time of this parameter reading.']
  production_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production order during which this parameter was measured.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the finished good or intermediate product being manufactured when this parameter was captured, enabling product-specific process analysis.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the work center or production line where the parameter was captured.']
  alarm_triggered_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether this parameter reading triggered an alarm or alert in the MES system due to exceeding control limits or deviation thresholds.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing batch or lot number associated with this parameter reading, enabling full traceability per FIFO/FEFO and GMP requirements.']
  calibration_due_date date [note: 'Next scheduled calibration date for the sensor or instrument at the time of reading, in format yyyy-MM-dd. Ensures measurement validity per GMP and ISO 9001 requirements.']
  ccp_number varchar [note: 'HACCP plan reference number for this Critical Control Point, if applicable (e.g., \'CCP-1\', \'CCP-2A\'). Used for regulatory audit traceability.']
  corrective_action_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether this out-of-spec reading requires formal corrective action documentation per HACCP or quality management procedures.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this process parameter record was first created in the data platform, in format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Distinct from reading_timestamp which captures the actual measurement time.']
  data_quality_flag varchar [note: 'Assessment of the reliability of this parameter reading: valid (normal operation), suspect (potential sensor issue), invalid (known bad data), or estimated (interpolated due to sensor failure).']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the parameter reading: automated_sensor (captured directly from MES historian) or manual_entry (operator-entered value).']
  deviation_percentage decimal(8,4) [note: 'Calculated percentage deviation of the measured value from the target value, enabling quick assessment of process variation and capability.']
  engineering_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the parameter value (e.g., Celsius, Fahrenheit, PSI, bar, pH units, degrees Brix, liters/min, RPM, Nm, percent RH, CFU/ml, percent ABV).']
  haccp_ccp_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether this parameter is designated as a HACCP Critical Control Point requiring enhanced monitoring and documentation per food safety regulations.']
  in_spec_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether the measured value falls within the acceptable control limits (LCL to UCL). False indicates an out-of-spec event requiring investigation.']
  lower_control_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'Minimum acceptable value for this parameter; readings below this threshold indicate an out-of-specification condition requiring corrective action per SPC and HACCP protocols.']
  measured_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'The actual numeric value recorded for this process parameter at the reading timestamp.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this process parameter record was last updated in the data platform, in format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Required for 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail compliance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operator or quality technician comments regarding this parameter reading, including context for deviations, corrective actions taken, or special circumstances.']
  parameter_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the parameter type: temperature, pressure, pH (acidity measure), Brix (sugar content), flow rate, speed, torque, humidity, CFU (Colony Forming Units) count, or ABV (Alcohol by Volume). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: temperature|pressure|ph|brix|flow_rate|speed|torque|humidity|cfu_count|abv — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  parameter_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the process parameter being measured (e.g., \'Pasteurization Tank 3 Temperature\', \'Filling Line 2 pH Probe\').']
  parameter_tag_name varchar [note: 'Unique tag identifier from the MES historian system (Aveva/Wonderware) representing the specific sensor or control point.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Manufacturing facility or plant identifier where this parameter was measured, enabling multi-site process comparison and benchmarking.']
  process_phase varchar [note: 'Stage or phase of the production process during which this parameter was measured (e.g., \'mixing\', \'pasteurization\', \'cooling\', \'filling\', \'packaging\'). Enables phase-specific process capability analysis.']
  quality_status varchar [note: 'Quality disposition of the batch or production run based on this parameter reading: approved (within spec), rejected (out of spec, product quarantined), under_review (pending quality review), or conditional (approved with restrictions).']
  reading_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the parameter measurement was captured from the sensor or manually entered, in format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  regulatory_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether this parameter reading must be included in regulatory submissions or audit documentation per FDA, USDA, or other governing body requirements.']
  sample_number varchar [note: 'Laboratory or in-process sample identifier if this parameter reading was associated with a physical sample taken for testing or verification.']
  sensor_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the physical sensor or instrument that captured the measurement, enabling calibration traceability and maintenance tracking.']
  shift_code varchar [note: 'Production shift identifier during which this parameter was captured (e.g., \'A\', \'B\', \'C\', \'Day\', \'Night\'). Enables shift-based performance analysis and OEE tracking.']
  target_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'The intended or setpoint value for this parameter as defined in the production recipe or process specification.']
  upper_control_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'Maximum acceptable value for this parameter; readings above this threshold indicate an out-of-specification condition requiring corrective action per SPC and HACCP protocols.']

  Note: 'Time-series process parameter readings captured from Aveva/Wonderware MES historian for critical in-process control points during production. Captures parameter tag name, parameter description, work center, production order, batch number, reading timestamp, measured value, engineering unit, target value, lower control limit, upper control limit, in-spec flag, parameter category (temperature, pressure, pH, Brix, flow rate, speed, torque, humidity, CFU count, ABV), and data source (automated sensor, manual entry). Enables process capability analysis, SPC (Statistical Process Control), and regulatory traceability for parameters like pasteurization temperature and pH.'
}

Table manufacturing.plant {
  plant_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the manufacturing plant, co-packing facility, or production site. Primary key for the plant master data product.']
  establishment_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.establishment_registration. Business justification: Each plant must be linked to its regulatory establishment registration for audit and reporting.']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Links plant manager to employee record for compliance, audit trails, and hierarchical reporting in plant‑level dashboards.']
  network_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.network_node. Business justification: Logistics planning maps each plant to a distribution network node for transportation scheduling and OTIF performance reporting.']
  price_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_zone. Business justification: REGIONAL PRICING: Plant location determines applicable price zone for pricing rules; price zone assignment is required for price list selection and compliance reporting.']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for the plant location including street number and name.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for additional location details such as building number, suite, or floor.']
  capacity_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the daily capacity metric (e.g., cases, pallets, kilograms, pounds, liters, gallons) aligned with the primary output of the facility.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality where the plant is located.']
  cold_storage_capacity_pallets varchar [note: 'Refrigerated and frozen storage capacity at the plant site measured in standard pallet positions for temperature-sensitive products and ingredients.']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date when the plant was first commissioned and began commercial production operations.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code indicating the country where the plant is located (e.g., USA, MEX, CAN, DEU, CHN).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this plant master data record was first created in the system.']
  daily_capacity_units decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum daily production capacity of the plant measured in standard production units per day. Used for capacity planning, demand allocation, and OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) calculations.']
  decommissioning_date date [note: 'Date when the plant was permanently closed and ceased all production operations. Null for active facilities.']
  erp_plant_code varchar [note: 'Plant code or site identifier used in the primary ERP system (SAP S/4HANA, Oracle Cloud ERP) for transactional processing, inventory management, and financial reporting. May differ from the business plant_code for legacy integration reasons.']
  fda_registration_number varchar [note: 'FDA facility registration number required for food manufacturing facilities under FSMA (Food Safety Modernization Act) regulations. Mandatory for US-based facilities and importers.']
  gfsi_certification_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the current GFSI certification requiring renewal to maintain compliance and customer approval status.']
  gfsi_scheme varchar [note: 'The GFSI-benchmarked food safety certification scheme held by the plant. SQF (Safe Quality Food), BRC (British Retail Consortium), FSSC 22000 (Food Safety System Certification), IFS (International Featured Standards), or none if not certified.']
  gmp_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the plant holds current GMP (Good Manufacturing Practice) certification required for food and beverage manufacturing quality standards.']
  halal_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the plant holds halal certification from a recognized Islamic authority enabling production of halal-certified products.']
  kosher_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the plant holds kosher certification from a recognized rabbinical authority enabling production of kosher-certified products.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this plant master data record was most recently updated or modified.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the plant location in decimal degrees format for mapping and logistics optimization.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the plant location in decimal degrees format for mapping and logistics optimization.']
  mes_site_code varchar [note: 'Site or facility identifier used in the Manufacturing Execution System (Aveva/Wonderware MES) for production scheduling, batch management, and OEE tracking.']
  number_of_production_lines varchar [note: 'Total count of active production lines or manufacturing lines within the plant used for production scheduling and line allocation.']
  operating_days_per_week varchar [note: 'Standard number of operating days per week for the plant (typically 5, 6, or 7) used for production planning and capacity calculations.']
  operating_hours_per_day decimal(4,1) [note: 'Standard operating hours per day for the plant used for capacity planning and OEE calculations. Typically 8, 16, or 24 hours depending on shift structure.']
  organic_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the plant holds organic certification (USDA Organic, EU Organic, or equivalent) enabling production of certified organic products.']
  organic_certifying_body varchar [note: 'Name of the accredited organic certification body that issued the organic certification (e.g., QAI, CCOF, Oregon Tilth, Ecocert).']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the plant used across operational systems. Typically a 4-10 character alphanumeric code used in ERP systems (SAP plant code, Oracle site code).']
  plant_name varchar [note: 'Official business name of the manufacturing plant or production facility.']
  plant_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the plant in its lifecycle. Active indicates full production operations, idle indicates temporarily not producing but maintained, decommissioned indicates permanently closed, under construction indicates new facility being built, and temporarily closed indicates short-term shutdown for maintenance or seasonal operations.']
  plant_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the facility type indicating ownership and operational model. Owned manufacturing indicates company-operated production facilities, co-packer indicates third-party contract manufacturing, toll manufacturer indicates facilities where the company provides ingredients and the manufacturer provides processing, pilot plant indicates small-scale testing facilities, R&D facility indicates research and development sites, and distribution center indicates warehousing and logistics hubs.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the plant address used for mail delivery and logistics routing.']
  primary_product_category varchar [note: 'Primary product category or product line manufactured at this plant (e.g., beverages, snacks, dairy, bakery, confectionery) used for portfolio planning and specialization tracking.']
  region varchar [note: 'Business-defined geographic region or market area for reporting and planning purposes (e.g., North America, EMEA, APAC, Latin America).']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or regional administrative division where the plant is located.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the plant location (e.g., America/New_York, Europe/London, Asia/Shanghai) used for production scheduling and shift management.']
  usda_establishment_number varchar [note: 'USDA establishment number assigned to facilities that process meat, poultry, or egg products under USDA inspection. Required for facilities producing USDA-regulated products.']
  warehouse_capacity_pallets varchar [note: 'Total warehouse storage capacity at the plant site measured in standard pallet positions for finished goods and raw material inventory management.']

  Note: 'Master data for all manufacturing plants, co-packing facilities, and production sites operated or contracted by the business. Captures plant code, plant name, plant type (owned manufacturing, co-packer, toll manufacturer, pilot plant, R&D facility), address, country, region, plant manager, plant capacity (units/day), number of production lines, GMP certification status, GFSI scheme (SQF, BRC, FSSC 22000), certification expiry date, FDA registration number, USDA establishment number, kosher certification flag, halal certification flag, organic certification flag, plant status (active, idle, decommissioned), and ERP plant code. SSOT for manufacturing facility master data.'
}

Table manufacturing.equipment_master {
  equipment_master_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the production equipment asset record. Primary key for the equipment master data product.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.asset. Business justification: Unifies equipment master with maintenance asset for shared calibration, inspection, and reliability data.']
  maintenance_responsible_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Assigns a primary maintenance owner to each equipment, supporting preventive‑maintenance scheduling and CAPA accountability.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant where this equipment is physically located and operated.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the work center or production area within the plant where this equipment is assigned for production scheduling and capacity planning.']
  acquisition_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Original purchase price or total capitalized cost of acquiring and installing the equipment, used for asset valuation and depreciation calculations.']
  acquisition_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the acquisition cost (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  allergen_handling_capability varchar [note: 'Description of allergen types this equipment is qualified to handle, or designation as allergen-free equipment. Critical for allergen control and production scheduling to prevent cross-contamination.']
  asset_owner_department varchar [note: 'Department or business unit responsible for the ownership, budgeting, and strategic decisions related to this equipment asset.']
  calibration_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the most recent calibration certificate documenting the equipment\'s measurement accuracy and compliance with specifications.']
  calibration_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between required calibration activities as defined by regulatory requirements, manufacturer specifications, or internal quality standards.']
  capacity_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the rated capacity value, indicating how production throughput is quantified for this equipment.']
  cip_capable_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether this equipment is designed for automated clean-in-place sanitation without disassembly, enabling efficient changeover and sanitation cycles.']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date when the equipment was validated, qualified, and approved for production use following installation and testing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this equipment master record was first created in the system, supporting audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  criticality_level varchar [note: 'Business criticality classification of the equipment based on its impact to production continuity, safety, and quality. Critical equipment requires highest priority maintenance and monitoring.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'Date when the equipment was permanently removed from service and decommissioned, marking the end of its operational lifecycle.']
  decommission_reason varchar [note: 'Business justification or reason for decommissioning the equipment (e.g., end of life, obsolescence, facility closure, technology upgrade).']
  energy_consumption_rating_kwh decimal(12,3) [note: 'Rated or average energy consumption of the equipment per operating hour, used for sustainability tracking and operational cost analysis.']
  equipment_category varchar [note: 'High-level grouping of equipment by operational category for reporting and analysis purposes.']
  equipment_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name of the equipment asset (e.g., \'Line 3 Filling Machine\', \'Pasteurizer Unit A\').']
  equipment_tag_number varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric identifier assigned to the equipment asset for operational tracking and maintenance purposes. This is the externally-known business identifier used in MES systems, work orders, and maintenance schedules.']
  equipment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the equipment by its primary production function within the manufacturing process. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: filling_machine|mixer|blender|pasteurizer|extruder|packaging_line|labeler|metal_detector|checkweigher|cip_system|oven|cooler|conveyor|palletizer|wrapper|sealer — 16 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  food_contact_surface_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether this equipment has surfaces that come into direct contact with food products, requiring specific sanitation and material compliance standards.']
  gmp_critical_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether this equipment is classified as GMP-critical, requiring enhanced documentation, validation, and change control procedures.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date when the equipment was physically installed at the manufacturing facility.']
  last_calibration_date date [note: 'Date when the equipment was last calibrated or verified for measurement accuracy, applicable to equipment requiring calibration per GMP or quality standards.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this equipment master record was last updated, supporting change tracking and audit trail requirements.']
  manufacturer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the original equipment manufacturer (OEM) who produced the equipment asset.']
  mes_asset_tag varchar [note: 'Unique identifier used by the MES system (Aveva/Wonderware) to track this equipment in real-time production monitoring, batch execution, and performance analytics.']
  model_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer\'s model designation for the equipment, used for parts ordering and technical documentation reference.']
  next_calibration_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next required calibration or verification activity to maintain equipment qualification status.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, special handling instructions, or contextual information about the equipment that does not fit structured fields.']
  oee_baseline_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target OEE percentage established as the performance baseline for this equipment, used for monitoring and continuous improvement initiatives. OEE measures availability, performance, and quality.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the equipment indicating its availability for production use.']
  preventive_maintenance_schedule_code varchar [note: 'Reference code linking this equipment to its preventive maintenance plan schedule defining frequency and tasks for routine maintenance activities.']
  rated_capacity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Maximum production capacity of the equipment as specified by the manufacturer or validated during commissioning, expressed in the unit of measure appropriate to the equipment type.']
  safety_certification_code varchar [note: 'Reference code or identifier for safety certifications held by this equipment (e.g., UL, CE, CSA), documenting compliance with electrical and mechanical safety standards.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number assigned by the manufacturer to this specific equipment unit for warranty and service tracking.']
  technical_specification_document_code varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the technical specification document containing detailed engineering specifications, operating parameters, and maintenance procedures for this equipment.']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the manufacturer\'s warranty coverage for this equipment expires, after which repairs and parts are no longer covered under warranty.']

  Note: 'Master data for all production equipment assets within manufacturing plants — filling machines, mixers, blenders, pasteurizers, extruders, packaging lines, labelers, metal detectors, checkweighers, and CIP systems. Captures equipment tag number, equipment name, equipment type, manufacturer, model, serial number, plant, work center assignment, installation date, commissioning date, rated capacity, current status (operational, under maintenance, decommissioned), preventive maintenance schedule reference, last calibration date, next calibration due date, calibration certificate number, OEE baseline target, and MES asset tag. SSOT for production equipment master data used in OEE, maintenance, and calibration tracking.'
}

Table manufacturing.production_line {
  production_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for production_line']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee managing the line.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the facility location where the line resides.']
  parent_production_line_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on production_line (parent_production_line_id)']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant containing the line.']
  capacity_per_hour decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum production capacity per hour (units per hour).']
  changeover_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Average time required to change over the line between products.']
  control_system varchar [note: 'Type of control system governing the line.']
  downtime_minutes varchar [note: 'Accumulated downtime minutes for the reporting period.']
  energy_consumption_kwh decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total energy used by the line in kilowatt‑hours.']
  gmp_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates if the line meets Good Manufacturing Practice requirements.']
  haccp_status varchar [note: 'Current HACCP compliance status for the line.']
  is_automated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the line is fully automated.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent safety inspection.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date when the line was last maintained.']
  last_quality_check_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent quality inspection.']
  line_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code assigned to the line.']
  line_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the line\'s purpose and characteristics.']
  line_end_date date [note: 'Date the line was retired or removed from service.']
  line_group varchar [note: 'Logical group or family the line belongs to (e.g., primary, secondary).']
  line_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the production line.']
  line_operating_mode varchar [note: 'Mode in which the line operates.']
  line_speed_m_per_min decimal(8,2) [note: 'Physical speed of the line in meters per minute.']
  line_start_date date [note: 'Date the production line was first put into service.']
  line_type varchar [note: 'Functional type of the line (e.g., bottling, mixing).']
  maintenance_status varchar [note: 'Status of the line\'s maintenance cycle.']
  next_maintenance_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next maintenance activity.']
  oee_actual decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured OEE percentage for the line.']
  oee_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'Planned OEE percentage for the line.']
  quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aggregated quality score for the line (0‑100).']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the production line record was first created.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the production line record.']
  safety_incident_count varchar [note: 'Number of safety incidents recorded for the line.']
  safety_status varchar [note: 'Current safety condition of the line.']
  setup_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Average time to set up the line for a production run.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the line.']
  uptime_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of time the line was operational.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for production_line. Referenced by production_line_id.'
}

Table quality.haccp_plan {
  haccp_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for haccp_plan']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Regulatory safety reports are organized per brand; associating each HACCP plan with its brand supports brand‑level safety documentation and audit trails.']
  establishment_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.establishment_registration. Business justification: HACCP plans are required per registered facility; linking enables compliance reporting and audit traceability.']
  responsible_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: HACCP plan must be owned and approved by a Food Safety Manager (employee) per regulatory compliance process.']
  acceptance_criteria varchar [note: 'Pre‑defined criteria that must be met for validation to be successful.']
  allergen_changeover_validation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether allergen changeover cleaning validation has been performed.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the HACCP plan.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the plan was approved.']
  approver_name varchar [note: 'Name of the food‑safety professional who approved the plan.']
  cleaning_validation_method varchar [note: 'Methodology used for cleaning validation (e.g., CIP, visual inspection).']
  confidence_level decimal(5,2) [note: 'Statistical confidence (percentage) associated with validation results.']
  doc_control_number varchar [note: 'Control number used for document management and version control.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the HACCP plan expires or is superseded (nullable for open‑ended plans).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the HACCP plan becomes effective.']
  equipment_qualification_study varchar [note: 'Reference to the equipment qualification (IQ/OQ/PQ) study linked to the plan.']
  execution_end_date date [note: 'Date when validation activities were completed.']
  execution_start_date date [note: 'Date when validation activities commenced.']
  haccp_plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the HACCP plan.']
  hazard_categories varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of hazard categories addressed by the plan.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information or comments.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the validation activity.']
  plan_number varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier assigned to the HACCP plan (e.g., HACCP-2024-001).']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the plan (e.g., product, facility, process).']
  protocol_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the validation protocol document.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the HACCP plan record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the HACCP plan record.']
  regulatory_basis varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework(s) that drive the HACCP plan (e.g., FDA FSMA Preventive Controls).']
  revalidation_interval_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of days between required re‑validations.']
  revalidation_trigger varchar [note: 'Condition that initiates a re‑validation (e.g., product change, equipment upgrade).']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the plan\'s scope (products, processes, facilities covered).']
  test_results_summary varchar [note: 'High‑level summary of test outcomes and observations.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last updated the HACCP plan record.']
  validation_scope varchar [note: 'Specific processes, equipment, or areas covered by the validation.']
  validation_type varchar [note: 'Category of validation associated with the plan.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the HACCP plan (e.g., v1.0, v2.1).']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the HACCP plan record.']

  Note: 'Master record for Hazard Analysis and Critical Control Points (HACCP) plans per production facility and product category, including all associated process validation, cleaning validation, and equipment qualification studies. Captures plan version, scope, hazard categories (biological, chemical, physical, radiological), regulatory basis (FDA FSMA Preventive Controls, USDA FSIS HACCP, Codex Alimentarius), approval status, effective dates, and responsible food safety team. Includes validation records: validation type (process/thermal, cleaning/CIP, equipment IQ/OQ/PQ, aseptic), scope, protocol reference, execution dates, acceptance criteria, test results summary, statistical confidence level, outcome (validated/failed), revalidation trigger conditions, and approval authority. Covers allergen changeover cleaning validation. SSOT for HACCP program governance, preventive control validation, process/cleaning/equipment qualification, and food safety plan management across all manufacturing sites.'
}

Table quality.critical_control_point {
  critical_control_point_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the critical control point record.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sensor that records the CCP measurement.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operator responsible for monitoring this CCP.']
  equipment_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of equipment used to enforce or monitor the CCP.']
  establishment_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.establishment_registration. Business justification: CCPs are defined for each registered plant; the FK supports regulatory mapping of critical limits to the establishment.']
  haccp_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent HACCP plan that includes this CCP.']
  production_line_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the production line where the CCP is applied.']
  audit_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the most recent audit of this CCP.']
  audit_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the latest audit was performed.']
  ccp_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the CCP, often used in documentation and labeling.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of the CCP with regulatory requirements.']
  control_point_category varchar [note: 'Broad category of the control point measurement.']
  corrective_action_procedure varchar [note: 'Standard operating procedure to follow when a deviation occurs.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the CCP record was created.']
  critical_control_point_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the CCP, e.g., "Cooking Temperature Check".']
  critical_control_point_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the CCP.']
  critical_limit_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure associated with the critical limit value.']
  critical_limit_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'Numeric threshold that must not be exceeded (or must be met) for the hazard.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system for the CCP definition (e.g., MES, manual entry).']
  deviation_trigger varchar [note: 'Condition that triggers a deviation (e.g., "temperature > 75°C").']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the CCP becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the CCP is retired or superseded (nullable).']
  escalation_path varchar [note: 'Defined escalation steps and contacts when a deviation occurs.']
  hazard_type varchar [note: 'Category of hazard the CCP is designed to control.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the CCP is considered a critical control point (true) or a monitoring point (false).']
  last_measured_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'Numeric value recorded in the most recent observation.']
  last_measurement_status varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent observation (pass, fail, or out of range).']
  last_observed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent monitoring observation for this CCP.']
  measurement_type varchar [note: 'Specific type of measurement captured at the CCP.']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the measurement type.']
  monitoring_frequency_minutes varchar [note: 'How often the CCP must be monitored, expressed in minutes.']
  monitoring_method varchar [note: 'Method used to monitor the CCP (e.g., sensor, manual check, lab test).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for any supplemental information about the CCP.']
  process_step varchar [note: 'Specific manufacturing step or operation where the CCP is applied.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to applicable regulatory requirement (e.g., FSMA, GFSI).']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk classification associated with the CCP.']
  sampling_frequency_minutes varchar [note: 'How often sampling is performed, expressed in minutes.']
  sampling_method varchar [note: 'Method used to obtain the sample for measurement (e.g., random, systematic).']
  sampling_size varchar [note: 'Number of units or volume taken for each sample.']
  tolerance_lower decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum acceptable value below the critical limit before a deviation is flagged.']
  tolerance_upper decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum acceptable value above the critical limit before a deviation is flagged.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the CCP record.']
  verification_activity varchar [note: 'Periodic verification activity to ensure the CCP remains effective.']

  Note: 'Master entity defining each Critical Control Point (CCP) within a HACCP plan, plus all associated real-time monitoring observations recorded during production. Records the CCP number, process step, hazard type, critical limits (temperature, pH, Brix, water activity, time, metal detector sensitivity), monitoring method, monitoring frequency, corrective action procedure, verification activity, and responsible operator. Monitoring records capture each observation: batch/lot, timestamp, measured value, critical limit comparison, pass/fail result, operator ID, equipment/sensor ID, and deviation trigger with escalation path. Linked to the parent HACCP plan and specific production line. SSOT for CCP definition and all CCP monitoring data. Supports real-time food safety control and FSMA regulatory traceability.'
}

Table quality.inspection_lot {
  inspection_lot_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the inspection lot record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who performed the inspection.']
  equipment_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the equipment or instrument used during inspection.']
  facility_inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.facility_inspection. Business justification: Lot inspection results are part of a facility inspection report; linking enables traceability to inspection findings.']
  order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.order. Business justification: Required for Order Fulfillment Inspection Report: each inspection lot must be linked to the sales order it satisfies for traceability and compliance.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Required for the Lot Release Inspection Report to tie each inspection lot to the specific SKU for batch approval.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: Failed inspection lots trigger corrective work orders; linking provides traceability from lot to maintenance action.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing batch identifier associated with the inspected material.']
  control_chart_type varchar [note: 'Type of statistical control chart used for SPC analysis.']
  cp_value decimal(10,4) [note: 'Statistical Cp value indicating process capability for the inspected characteristic.']
  cpk_value decimal(10,4) [note: 'Statistical Cpk value indicating process capability centered within specification limits.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the inspection lot record was first created in the system.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the inspected lot expires or reaches end of shelf life.']
  fda_approval_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of FDA approval or notification linked to the inspected product.']
  gfs_i_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lot meets GFSI certification requirements.']
  humidity_recorded decimal(8,2) [note: 'Relative humidity measured during inspection, expressed as a percentage.']
  inspection_date date [note: 'Calendar date when the inspection was performed.']
  inspection_method varchar [note: 'Method used to conduct the inspection (e.g., visual, laboratory, sensor, taste, microbial).']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the inspection lot.']
  inspection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp of the inspection event.']
  inspection_type varchar [note: 'Type of quality inspection performed (e.g., incoming, in‑process, final release).']
  lot_creation_date date [note: 'Date when the material lot was originally created.']
  lot_disposition varchar [note: 'Final disposition of the lot after inspection.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the inspection lot by the manufacturing system.']
  material_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the material or SKU being inspected.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text comments or observations recorded by the inspector.']
  overall_result varchar [note: 'Aggregated outcome of the inspection lot.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Code of the production plant where the inspection took place.']
  quantity_inspected decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total quantity of material inspected, expressed in the unit of measure.']
  regulatory_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Compliance status with applicable regulatory requirements (e.g., FDA, GFSI).']
  sample_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of material represented by the sample.']
  sample_size varchar [note: 'Number of units sampled for inspection.']
  sample_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the sampled quantity.']
  sampling_plan varchar [note: 'Reference to the sampling plan applied for this inspection.']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Calculated shelf life of the lot in days.']
  specification_version varchar [note: 'Version of the quality specification applied to this inspection.']
  stock_posting_status varchar [note: 'Result of the stock posting operation after inspection.']
  temperature_recorded decimal(8,2) [note: 'Temperature measured during inspection, expressed in degrees Celsius.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the inspected quantity (e.g., kilograms, liters).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the inspection lot record.']
  usage_decision varchar [note: 'Decision on whether the inspected material can be used, rejected, or held.']

  Note: 'SAP QM-aligned entity representing quality inspection lots, their characteristic-level results, and statistical evaluations. Captures lot number, inspection type (incoming, in-process, final release), material/SKU, batch, plant, quantity, usage decision (accept/reject/hold), and stock posting outcome. Includes individual characteristic results: measured values (moisture, fat, pH, microbial count, organoleptic score), specification limits, valuation, inspection method, equipment used, and inspector. Supports statistical process control (SPC) with control charts, Cp/Cpk calculations, lot disposition decisions, and full quality inspection traceability across the supply chain. SSOT for all quality inspection events and measurements.'
}

Table quality.non_conformance {
  non_conformance_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the non-conformance record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Non‑conformance source tracking needs the customer account that reported the issue; this supports the Non‑Conformance Root‑Cause Analysis process.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Cost accounting of non‑conformance impacts requires allocating expense to a cost center for internal reporting.']
  critical_control_point_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Critical Control Point deviation linked to the event.']
  inspection_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_lot. Business justification: Link non_conformance to the inspection lot where the issue was discovered; enables proper relational traceability and removes redundant free‑text column.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing facility where the batch was produced.']
  product_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.product_registration. Business justification: Reportable NCRs can trigger changes to product registration status; the FK supports regulatory update workflows.']
  production_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_order. Business justification: Root‑cause investigations must reference the exact production order that generated the defect for corrective action.']
  production_shift_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the production shift during which the batch was manufactured.']
  promotion_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trade.promotion_event. Business justification: Non‑Conformance Tracking during Promotional Periods links NC records to promotions to identify period‑specific issues.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: NCRs generated during R&D testing are tracked against the originating R&D project for corrective action planning.']
  reporting_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: NCRs are logged by the employee who discovers the issue; required for root‑cause analysis and audit traceability.']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.shipment. Business justification: Non‑conformance events discovered during shipment need to be tied to that shipment for root‑cause analysis and corrective action.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Non‑conformance incidents are tracked per SKU in the Non‑Conformance Management Process and regulatory reporting.']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Root‑cause analysis and recall actions require linking each NCR to the specific stock position affected.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the external or internal party associated with the source (e.g., supplier, customer, internal department).']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: Each non‑conformance generates a maintenance work order to remediate equipment; linking ties root‑cause to corrective work.']
  batch_expiration_date date [note: 'Calculated expiration date for the batch based on shelf‑life.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing batch or lot number linked to the non-conformance.']
  batch_production_date date [note: 'Date on which the batch was manufactured.']
  batch_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units produced in the batch linked to the non-conformance.']
  complaint_category varchar [note: 'Category of the consumer or customer complaint associated with the non-conformance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: safety|quality|labeling|packaging|taste|odor|appearance — promote to reference product]']
  complaint_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the complaint was received.']
  complaint_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the issue reported by the customer or internal stakeholder.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Target date for completion of the corrective action plan.']
  corrective_action_plan varchar [note: 'Description of the corrective actions defined to address the root cause.']
  corrective_action_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the corrective action implementation.']
  cost_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the cost impact amount.']
  cost_impact_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated financial impact of the non-conformance (e.g., rework, scrap, recall costs).']
  defect_type varchar [note: 'Primary classification of the defect causing the non-conformance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: contamination|label_error|packaging|taste|appearance|foreign_body|microbial|chemical|physical|texture — promote to reference product]']
  disposition_decision varchar [note: 'Final action taken for the affected product.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the non-conformance was first observed or recorded.']
  hold_duration_days varchar [note: 'Planned duration of the hold in days.']
  hold_location varchar [note: 'Warehouse or storage location where the product is held.']
  hold_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the hold action.']
  hold_type varchar [note: 'Type of containment action applied to the affected product.']
  investigation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the root cause investigation.']
  ncr_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the non-conformance event, used for tracking and reporting.']
  product_line varchar [note: 'Higher‑level product family or line to which the affected SKU belongs.']
  quality_metric_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the quality metric value.']
  quality_metric_value decimal(10,4) [note: 'Measured value of the primary quality metric (e.g., CFU count, pH).']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the non-conformance record was created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the non-conformance record.']
  regulatory_report_type varchar [note: 'Specific regulatory reporting mechanism applicable to the event.']
  regulatory_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the event must be reported to a regulatory authority.']
  resolution_status varchar [note: 'Overall outcome status of the non-conformance handling process.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the root cause. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: process|material|equipment|human_error|supplier|design|environment|unknown — promote to reference product]']
  root_cause_detail varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the identified root cause.']
  severity_classification varchar [note: 'Risk severity assigned to the non-conformance based on impact and likelihood.']
  severity_score varchar [note: 'Numeric severity rating (1‑5) where higher values indicate greater risk.']
  source_type varchar [note: 'Origin of the non-conformance event, indicating how it was detected.']

  Note: 'Master record for all quality non-conformance events (NCRs), customer/consumer complaints, and product hold/containment actions. Captures NCR number, source (in-process, supplier, customer complaint, consumer hotline, audit finding, CCP deviation), affected SKU/batch/lot, defect type, complaint details (category, channel — call center, retailer, social media, DTC — description, investigation status, resolution), severity classification, regulatory reportability (FDA MedWatch, USDA, Reportable Food Registry), hold/containment details (hold type, warehouse location, segregation status, hold duration, disposition authority), disposition decision (release, rework, scrap, return to supplier, hold, destroy), root cause category, and originating inspection lot or CCP deviation. SSOT for all quality failures, deviations, complaints, containment holds, and disposition actions across the enterprise.'
}

Table quality.capa {
  capa_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the Corrective and Preventive Action record.']
  customer_complaint_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer complaint that initiated this CAPA, if applicable.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal owner responsible for the CAPA.']
