  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: CAPA corrective‑action expenses are charged to a GL account to track corrective cost effectiveness.']
  non_conformance_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the non‑conformance that triggered this CAPA, if applicable.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: CAPA records need to reference the R&D project they remediate, enabling project‑level effectiveness reporting.']
  related_audit_finding_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the audit finding linked to this CAPA, if applicable.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: CAPA implementation often requires maintenance tasks; linking associates corrective actions with specific work orders.']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the CAPA actions were actually completed.']
  attached_document_count varchar [note: 'Number of supporting documents attached to the CAPA record.']
  capa_number varchar [note: 'Business-visible CAPA reference number assigned by quality management (e.g., CAPA-2023-001).']
  capa_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the CAPA.']
  capa_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the record is a corrective action or a preventive action.']
  ccn_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the critical control point (CCP) related to the CAPA.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date when the CAPA record was formally closed.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments or notes related to the CAPA.']
  corrective_action_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the corrective action to be implemented.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the CAPA record was first created in the system.']
  department varchar [note: 'Business department responsible for the CAPA.']
  effectiveness_verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify that the CAPA was effective.']
  effectiveness_verification_result varchar [note: 'Result of the effectiveness verification.']
  is_closed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the CAPA record is closed (true) or still open (false).']
  is_effective boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the CAPA was determined to be effective (true) or not (false).']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent review of the CAPA effectiveness and status.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing lot number linked to the CAPA.']
  owner_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the employee owning the CAPA.']
  preventive_action_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the preventive action to avoid recurrence.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority for execution of the CAPA actions.']
  product_code varchar [note: 'Code of the product associated with the CAPA, if applicable.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework or standard that the CAPA addresses (e.g., FDA, GFSI, ISO 22000).']
  risk_assessment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score representing the risk level associated with the issue, used for prioritization.']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the identified root cause.']
  root_cause_method varchar [note: 'Methodology used to determine the root cause (e.g., 5-Why, Fishbone).']
  severity varchar [note: 'Severity level assigned to the issue addressed by the CAPA.']
  target_completion_date date [note: 'Planned date by which the CAPA actions should be completed.']
  title varchar [note: 'Brief descriptive title summarizing the corrective or preventive action.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the CAPA record.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date when the effectiveness verification was performed.']
  verification_responsible varchar [note: 'Name of the person who performed the effectiveness verification.']

  Note: 'Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) master record linked to non-conformances, audit findings, customer complaints, or CCP deviations. Captures CAPA number, type (corrective/preventive), root cause analysis method (5-Why, Fishbone), root cause description, corrective actions defined, preventive actions defined, responsible owner, target completion date, actual completion date, effectiveness verification method, and closure status. Managed within Veeva Vault QMS.'
}

Table quality.micro_test_result {
  micro_test_result_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the microbiological test result record.']
  analyst_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Microbiological test results must be attributed to the lab analyst for compliance reporting and corrective action.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.asset. Business justification: Micro‑test results are generated on lab equipment that is a maintained asset; linking supports asset maintenance planning.']
  equipment_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the equipment used for the analysis.']
  lab_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the laboratory that performed the test.']
  sample_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the physical sample that was tested.']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.shipment. Business justification: Microbial test results for a batch are required for each shipment to meet regulatory compliance and quality release.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Microbial test results must be linked to SKU for the Microbial Testing Compliance Report required by FDA.']
  accreditation_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the laboratory is accredited for the test method.']
  analysis_date date [note: 'Date the laboratory completed the analysis.']
  analyst_name varchar [note: 'Name of the laboratory analyst who performed the test.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing batch identifier for the sample.']
  chain_of_custody varchar [note: 'Record of custody transfers for the sample from collection to analysis.']
  collection_date date [note: 'Date the sample was collected from the source.']
  collection_location varchar [note: 'Facility, line, or site where the sample was obtained.']
  collection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the sample was taken, including time zone.']
  control_sample_type varchar [note: 'Type of control sample used (positive, negative, or blank).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the test result record was first created in the system.']
  detection_limit decimal(12,2) [note: 'Analytical detection limit for the test method.']
  detection_limit_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the detection limit.']
  incubation_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Temperature at which the sample was incubated.']
  incubation_time_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Duration of incubation for the test.']
  is_control_sample boolean [note: 'True if the sample is a quality control (positive/negative) sample.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot or batch number assigned to the material at production.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations recorded by the analyst.']
  pass_fail varchar [note: 'Overall pass/fail determination based on result criteria.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the test result meets applicable regulatory criteria (e.g., FSMA limits).']
  result_interpretation varchar [note: 'Narrative interpretation of the test outcome by the analyst.']
  result_reported_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the test result was officially reported to the requesting party.']
  result_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the result value.']
  result_value decimal(12,2) [note: 'Quantitative test outcome (e.g., CFU count).']
  sample_condition varchar [note: 'Physical condition of the sample at collection.']
  sample_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the sample (e.g., supplier, in‑house production, field collection).']
  sample_type varchar [note: 'Category of the sample (e.g., raw material, work‑in‑process, finished goods, environmental swab, water).']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Calculated shelf‑life in days based on the microbiological result.']
  target_organism varchar [note: 'Microbial organism or indicator targeted by the test.']
  test_method varchar [note: 'Standard analytical method used for the microbiological test.']
  test_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the test.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the test result record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for microbiological testing including sample tracking and laboratory results. Covers sample registration (source, SKU/material, lot/batch, collection point, chain of custody, lab assignment, test panel requested) and test results (test method per AOAC/ISO/USDA FSIS, target organism — Salmonella, Listeria, E. coli O157:H7, TPC, Yeast & Mold — CFU count or presence/absence, detection limit, incubation conditions, lab accreditation, pass/fail). Applies to raw materials, WIP, finished goods, environmental swabs, and water samples. Critical for FSMA Preventive Controls and HACCP verification. SSOT for all microbiological sample-to-result tracking.'
}

Table quality.sensory_panel {
  sensory_panel_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the sensory evaluation panel.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the panel leader/coordinator.']
  primary_sensory_plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing or testing facility where the panel took place.']
  rd_sensory_panel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.rd_sensory_panel. Business justification: Cross‑domain sensory panels require traceability from quality panel to the originating R&D sensory panel for consistency checks.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Sensory panel data is tied to SKU for the Sensory Evaluation Report used in product development decisions.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the panel data was approved for analysis.']
  attribute_list varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of sensory attributes evaluated (e.g., appearance, aroma, flavor).']
  audit_status varchar [note: 'Internal audit outcome for the panel.']
  average_overall_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Mean overall sensory score across panelists.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing batch number of the product sample.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance status of the panel data.']
  confidence_interval decimal(5,2) [note: 'Confidence interval percentage for overall scores.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the panel record was first created.']
  data_collection_method varchar [note: 'Method used to capture panelist scores.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Shelf‑life expiration date of the product sample.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot number associated with the product sample.']
  manufacturing_date date [note: 'Date the product batch was manufactured.']
  median_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Median overall sensory score.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form observations or comments.']
  overall_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the panel.']
  panel_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the panel was conducted.']
  panel_end_time timestamp [note: 'Exact end time of the sensory evaluation session.']
  panel_leader_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the panel leader.']
  panel_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or title for the sensory panel.']
  panel_start_time timestamp [note: 'Exact start time of the sensory evaluation session.']
  panel_type varchar [note: 'Type of sensory test performed.']
  panelist_count varchar [note: 'Number of panelists who participated.']
  panelist_data_source varchar [note: 'Origin of panelist participants.']
  product_gtin varchar [note: 'GTIN of the evaluated product.']
  product_upc varchar [note: 'UPC barcode of the evaluated product.']
  protocol_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the sensory evaluation protocol used.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Regulatory authority governing the panel\'s compliance.']
  scoring_scale varchar [note: 'Scale used for scoring (e.g., 1‑9 hedonic).']
  sensory_degradation_trend varchar [note: 'Observed trend in sensory quality over shelf life.']
  shelf_life_day varchar [note: 'Number of days from manufacturing to expiration for the sampled product.']
  standard_deviation decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard deviation of overall scores.']
  statistical_method varchar [note: 'Statistical analysis method applied to panel results.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the panel record.']

  Note: 'Master record for sensory evaluation panels including panel configuration and all individual panelist scoring data. Captures panel type (descriptive, hedonic, discrimination, triangle test, consumer), product/SKU evaluated, panel date, panelist roster, evaluation protocol, sensory attributes assessed (appearance, aroma, flavor, texture, mouthfeel, aftertaste, overall acceptability), scoring scale, and panel leader. Includes panelist-level scores: attribute name, score value, hedonic scale, confidence interval, and qualitative comments. Enables statistical analysis of organoleptic quality, shelf life sensory degradation, NPD sensory benchmarking, and gold standard maintenance.'
}

Table quality.shelf_life_study {
  shelf_life_study_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the shelf life study record.']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Shelf‑life studies underpin brand marketing of product freshness; associating studies with brand enables brand‑level shelf‑life claim validation.']
  evaluator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Shelf‑life studies are conducted and signed off by a quality scientist (employee) to meet shelf‑life specification requirements.']
  formulation_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.formulation_version. Business justification: Shelf‑life studies are performed on specific formulation versions; linking provides data for product release decisions.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Shelf‑life study expenses are posted to a GL account for product lifecycle cost analysis.']
  product_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.product_registration. Business justification: Shelf‑life study results are required for product registration dossiers; linking ensures compliance documentation.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Shelf‑life study results are linked to SKU for the Shelf‑Life Validation Study supporting label shelf‑life claims.']
  spec_quality_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.specification. Business justification: Shelf life studies evaluate the stability of a product as defined by its specification.']
  assay_method varchar [note: 'Analytical method used for each measurement (e.g., pH meter, HPLC, sensory panel).']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the production batch used for the study.']
  compliance_standard varchar [note: 'Regulatory or certification framework governing the study (e.g., FDA, GFSI).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the shelf life study record was first created in the system.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Originating operational system (e.g., SAP QM, Veeva Vault QMS) that supplied the study data.']
  degradation_model varchar [note: 'Mathematical model selected to describe product degradation over time.']
  evaluation_schedule varchar [note: 'Narrative schedule describing planned evaluation days (e.g., "Day 0, 30, 60, 90").']
  evaluator_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person or lab responsible for conducting the evaluations.']
  evaluator_role varchar [note: 'Job role or function of the evaluator (e.g., Quality Engineer, Microbiologist).']
  gtin varchar [note: 'Global identifier for the product, may be 14‑digit GTIN‑14.']
  humidity_acceleration_factor decimal(6,3) [note: 'Factor used to model the effect of humidity on product degradation.']
  lab_location varchar [note: 'Physical location or facility where the study measurements are performed.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot identifier for traceability of raw material or finished product.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form observations or comments captured during the study.']
  packaging_format varchar [note: 'Description of the packaging type (e.g., bottle, pouch, can).']
  q10_value decimal(6,3) [note: 'Temperature coefficient indicating rate increase per 10 °C rise, used for accelerated studies.']
  shelf_life_study_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the study purpose, design, and scope.']
  shelf_life_study_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the study.']
  storage_humidity_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Relative humidity level maintained during the study.']
  storage_light_exposure varchar [note: 'Light exposure condition for the product during storage.']
  storage_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Controlled storage temperature in degrees Celsius for the study.']
  study_end_date date [note: 'Calendar date when the shelf life study concluded or was terminated.']
  study_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name given to the shelf life study for easy identification.']
  study_start_date date [note: 'Calendar date when the shelf life study commenced.']
  study_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the study is real‑time, accelerated, or based on the Arrhenius model.']
  target_shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Targeted shelf life duration in days that the study aims to validate.']
  temperature_acceleration_factor decimal(6,3) [note: 'Factor applied to adjust real‑time data to accelerated temperature conditions.']
  upc varchar [note: '12‑digit barcode number associated with the product.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the study record.']

  Note: 'Master record for shelf life and accelerated shelf life testing (ASLT) studies including all time-point measurements and degradation trend analysis. Captures study type (real-time, accelerated, Arrhenius model), SKU, storage conditions (temperature, humidity, light exposure), packaging format, target shelf life, and evaluation schedule. Includes time-point results: evaluation day, quality parameter measured (pH, Brix, water activity, microbial count, sensory score, color delta-E, viscosity), measured value, specification limit, pass/fail status, and evaluator. Enables Q10 modeling, degradation kinetics, label date coding decisions, and FEFO inventory management.'
}

Table quality.food_safety_audit {
  food_safety_audit_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the food safety audit record.']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Food safety audit results are reported per brand for compliance dashboards and consumer trust communications.']
  center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.distribution_center. Business justification: Food‑safety audits are performed on distribution centers; linking audit to the DC records audit scope and results.']
  establishment_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.establishment_registration. Business justification: Food safety audits are conducted on registered establishments; the FK ties audit outcomes to the registration record.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Food‑safety audit fees are allocated to a GL account to reflect audit expense in financial reporting.']
  lead_auditor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Each food safety audit is led by a designated auditor (employee); required for audit reports and corrective actions.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the production or distribution facility being audited.']
  audit_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the audit record was first entered into the system.']
  audit_end_date date [note: 'Date when the audit fieldwork concluded.']
  audit_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the audit (e.g., internal audit reference).']
  audit_start_date date [note: 'Date when the audit fieldwork began.']
  audit_title varchar [note: 'Human‑readable title or name of the audit.']
  audit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the audit (internal, external, or regulatory).']
  audit_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the audit record.']
  auditing_body varchar [note: 'Organization that performed the audit (e.g., SGS, Bureau Veritas).']
  certificate_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the certification expires.']
  certificate_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the issued certification document.']
  certification_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the audit regarding certification status.']
  certification_scheme varchar [note: 'Food safety certification scheme applied to the audit (e.g., SQF, BRC).']
  closure_status varchar [note: 'Overall status of corrective action closure for the audit.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Target date for completion of required corrective actions.']
  corrective_action_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any corrective actions are required.']
  document_library_path varchar [note: 'File system or DMS path to the audit’s supporting documents.']
  document_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the audit documentation.']
  finding_count varchar [note: 'Total number of findings recorded for the audit.']
  food_safety_audit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the audit.']
  lead_auditor_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary auditor responsible for the audit.']
  major_non_conformance_count varchar [note: 'Count of major non‑conformances identified.']
  minor_non_conformance_count varchar [note: 'Count of minor non‑conformances identified.']
  next_audit_due_date date [note: 'Planned date for the subsequent audit cycle.']
  observation_count varchar [note: 'Number of observations (non‑conformances) recorded.']
  opportunity_for_improvement_count varchar [note: 'Number of OFI (Opportunity for Improvement) findings.']
  overall_grade varchar [note: 'Letter grade assigned to the audit based on performance.']
  overall_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score representing overall audit performance (e.g., 0‑100).']
  owner_department varchar [note: 'Business department responsible for the audit record.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall risk rating derived from audit findings.']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the audit scope (sites, processes, products).']
  training_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if personnel training is required as a result of audit findings.']

  Note: 'Master record for food safety and quality system audits (SQF, BRC, FSSC 22000, ISO 22000, GFSI-benchmarked schemes, internal audits, regulatory inspections), all findings, and the QMS controlled document library. Captures audit ID, type, certification scheme, auditing body, facility, date range, lead auditor, scope, overall grade, certification outcome, certificate number/expiry, and next audit due date. Includes finding-level detail: finding type (major NC, minor NC, observation, OFI), clause reference, description, risk level, required corrective action, response due date, and closure status. Manages QMS controlled documents: document ID, type (SOP, work instruction, form, policy, specification, HACCP plan, validation protocol), title, version, effective/expiry dates, owner, approval workflow, training requirement flag, and applicable sites. SSOT for audit management, certification status, GFSI scheme compliance, and QMS document governance per ISO 9001 Clause 7.5.'
}

Table quality.specification {
  specification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for specification']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Specifications must be approved by a qualified employee (e.g., Quality Manager) to ensure regulatory compliance.']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Brand managers need direct access to product specifications for compliance checks and marketing claim substantiation; linking specification to brand enables brand‑level spec dashboards.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Product specifications must reference SKU to ensure each SKU meets its defined quality specification.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name of the person or system that approved the specification.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the specification was approved.']
  audit_status varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent compliance audit for this specification.']
  brix_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target sugar content expressed as degrees Brix.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative reason for the most recent revision.']
  color varchar [note: 'Standardized color designation for the product. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: red|green|blue|yellow|white|black|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  compliance_standard varchar [note: 'Food safety or quality standard to which the specification adheres.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the specification record was first created.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the specification becomes effective.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the specification expires, if applicable.']
  fat_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable fat content as a percentage of total weight.']
  height_cm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Target height dimension in centimeters.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the specification is considered critical for product safety or regulatory compliance.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent audit performed on the specification.']
  length_cm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Target length dimension in centimeters.']
  listeria_cfu_g decimal(10,0) [note: 'Maximum allowable Listeria monocytogenes colony forming units per gram.']
  moisture_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable moisture content as a percentage of total weight.']
  organoleptic_score varchar [note: 'Numeric score (1‑10) representing sensory evaluation results.']
  packaging_type varchar [note: 'Standard packaging format for the product.']
  ph decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target pH (acidity) of the product.']
  protein_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum required protein content as a percentage of total weight.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential revision number for change tracking.']
  salmonella_cfu_g decimal(10,0) [note: 'Maximum allowable Salmonella colony forming units per gram.']
  sensory_panel_method varchar [note: 'Methodology used for organoleptic testing.']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Maximum allowable shelf life in days under specified storage conditions.']
  sodium_mg_per_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable sodium concentration in milligrams per kilogram.']
  spec_type varchar [note: 'Category of the specification (finished product, raw material, packaging, work‑in‑process).']
  specification_code varchar [note: 'Business-visible code uniquely identifying the specification within the organization.']
  specification_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the specification purpose and scope.']
  specification_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the specification.']
  specification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the specification.']
  storage_condition varchar [note: 'Recommended storage condition for the product.']
  tpc_cfu_g decimal(10,0) [note: 'Maximum total aerobic plate count per gram.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the specification record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the specification, following semantic versioning.']
  weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Target weight of the product or component in kilograms.']
  width_cm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Target width dimension in centimeters.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system that created the specification record.']

  Note: 'Master record defining quality specifications for finished goods, raw materials, packaging, and WIP. Managed via TraceGains and SAP QM. Captures specification ID, material/SKU, specification type (finished product, raw material, packaging), version, effective date, physical parameters (weight, dimensions, color), chemical parameters (pH, Brix, moisture, fat, protein, sodium), microbiological limits (TPC, Salmonella, Listeria), organoleptic standards, and approval status. SSOT for quality acceptance criteria.'
}

Table quality.allergen_matrix {
  allergen_matrix_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for allergen_matrix']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Allergen declarations are brand‑specific for labeling and marketing claims; linking matrix to brand supports clean‑label and allergen claim compliance.']
  formulation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the formulation record that defines the ingredient blend for the SKU.']
  production_line_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing line where the product is produced.']
  spec_quality_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.specification. Business justification: Allergen matrix defines allergen profile for SKUs; linking to specification centralizes product quality attributes.']
  allergen_celery varchar [note: 'Indicates presence of celery or celery extracts.']
  allergen_control_measures varchar [note: 'Textual description of controls (e.g., dedicated equipment, cleaning procedures) used to mitigate allergen risk.']
  allergen_egg varchar [note: 'Indicates presence of egg proteins.']
  allergen_fish varchar [note: 'Indicates presence of finned fish allergens.']
  allergen_lupin varchar [note: 'Indicates presence of lupin protein.']
  allergen_matrix_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the allergen matrix record.']
  allergen_milk varchar [note: 'Indicates presence of milk proteins.']
  allergen_mollusk varchar [note: 'Indicates presence of mollusk allergens (e.g., clams, mussels).']
  allergen_mustard varchar [note: 'Indicates presence of mustard seeds or derivatives.']
  allergen_peanut varchar [note: 'Indicates if peanuts are present, may be present, or absent in the product.']
  allergen_sesame varchar [note: 'Indicates presence of sesame seeds or oil.']
  allergen_shellfish varchar [note: 'Indicates presence of crustacean or mollusk shellfish allergens.']
  allergen_soy varchar [note: 'Indicates presence of soy proteins.']
  allergen_sulfites varchar [note: 'Indicates presence of sulfite additives above regulatory thresholds.']
  allergen_tree_nut varchar [note: 'Status of tree nut allergens (e.g., almond, walnut) in the product.']
  allergen_wheat varchar [note: 'Indicates presence of wheat gluten.']
  cleaning_validation_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent cleaning validation was performed.']
  cleaning_validation_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes from the cleaning validation activity.']
  cleaning_validation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of cleaning validation for allergen control.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the allergen matrix record was first created.']
  cross_contact_risk varchar [note: 'Assessed risk of allergen cross‑contact during manufacturing.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the allergen matrix is considered valid for labeling and production.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the allergen matrix is no longer valid; null if indefinite.']
  gtin varchar [note: '14‑digit global identifier for the product.']
  label_declaration_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the allergen must be declared on the consumer label.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the allergen matrix.']
  matrix_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the matrix follows the standard regulatory template or a custom internal version.']
  risk_assessment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (0‑100) summarizing overall allergen risk for the SKU.']
  sku varchar [note: 'Unique SKU code that identifies the finished product.']
  upc varchar [note: '12‑digit barcode used for retail scanning and inventory.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version of the allergen matrix for change management.']

  Note: 'Master record defining the allergen profile for each SKU, formulation, and production line. Captures the 14 major allergens (FDA Big 9 + EU 14) present, may-contain declarations, cross-contact risk assessment, allergen control measures in place, cleaning validation status, and label declaration requirements. Critical for regulatory compliance (FDA FALCPA, EU 1169/2011), consumer safety, and co-packing/toll manufacturing risk management.'
}

Table quality.customer_complaint {
  customer_complaint_id varchar [pk, note: 'System generated unique identifier for each complaint record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Required for Complaint Management Report: each complaint must be tied to the originating customer account for liability and service tracking.']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Complaints are analyzed per brand to drive brand reputation metrics and corrective actions; a brand FK provides the necessary aggregation.']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the complainant (if known) linking to the customer master.']
  handling_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Customer complaints are assigned to a responsible employee for investigation and resolution, tracked in complaint handling reports.']
  order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.order. Business justification: Customer Complaint Traceability: linking complaint to the original sales order enables root‑cause analysis, warranty handling, and service recovery.']
  promotion_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trade.promotion_event. Business justification: Promotion Impact & Complaint Correlation report links complaints to specific promotion events to assess promotion‑driven quality issues.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Customer complaints are linked to SKU for the Complaint Resolution Process and regulatory reporting.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing batch or lot number associated with the complained product.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date when the complaint was formally closed.']
  complainant_address varchar [note: 'Mailing address of the complainant, used for regulatory follow‑up if required.']
  complainant_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address of the complainant for follow‑up communication.']
  complainant_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the individual or organization submitting the complaint.']
  complainant_phone varchar [note: 'Contact phone number of the complainant.']
  complainant_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the complainant is a consumer, trade partner, or internal stakeholder.']
  complaint_category varchar [note: 'Standard classification of the complaint type.']
  complaint_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the complaint, used for tracking and communication.']
  complaint_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the complaint was received or logged.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the complaint record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the financial impact amount.']
  customer_complaint_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative provided by the complainant describing the issue.']
  customer_complaint_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the complaint.']
  expected_resolution_date date [note: 'Target date for resolving the complaint as set during investigation.']
  financial_impact_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary impact associated with the complaint (e.g., refunds, warranty costs).']
  investigation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the internal investigation into the complaint.']
  product_upc varchar [note: 'Universal Product Code of the product linked to the complaint.']
  regulatory_agency varchar [note: 'Regulatory body to which the complaint is reportable, if any.']
  regulatory_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the complaint must be reported to a regulatory agency (e.g., FDA MedWatch, USDA).']
  resolution_action varchar [note: 'Action taken to resolve the complaint (e.g., product recall, reformulation, packaging change).']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Identified root cause of the issue after investigation.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Severity level assigned to the complaint based on impact and risk.']
  source_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the complaint was received.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the complaint record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for consumer and trade customer quality complaints received through all channels (call center, retailer, social media, DTC). Captures complaint ID, complaint source, product/SKU, batch/lot number, complaint category (foreign material, off-flavor, packaging defect, illness, labeling error), complaint description, severity, regulatory reportability flag (FDA MedWatch, USDA), investigation status, root cause, resolution action, and closure date. Supports FSMA reportable food registry obligations.'
}

Table quality.product_recall {
  product_recall_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each product recall event.']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Recall investigations and communications are executed at the brand level; linking recalls to brand enables brand‑centric recall reporting and KPI tracking.']
  center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.distribution_center. Business justification: Recalls are executed per distribution center; linking recall to DC supports regulatory reporting and execution tracking.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Recall financial loss is posted to a specific GL account for regulatory and management reporting.']
  initiating_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Recall initiation is performed by a designated employee (e.g., Recall Coordinator) to satisfy FDA/USDA recall procedures.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Recall records need a direct SKU link for the Recall Management Process to identify affected products.']
  spec_quality_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.specification. Business justification: Product recall events are associated with a specific product specification.']
  affected_lot_codes varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of lot or batch codes subject to the recall.']
  affected_sku_codes varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of SKU identifiers impacted by the recall.']
  audit_report_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal audit report linked to the recall.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall compliance status of the recall with regulatory requirements.']
  corrective_action_plan varchar [note: 'Planned actions to prevent recurrence of the issue.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the recall record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three‑letter currency code for monetary loss amount.']
  distribution_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic scope of the recall distribution.']
  fda_notification_date date [note: 'Date the FDA was notified of the recall.']
  initiating_party_name varchar [note: 'Name of the party (e.g., manufacturer, regulator) that triggered the recall.']
  is_voluntary boolean [note: 'True if the recall was initiated voluntarily by the company.']
  monetary_loss_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated financial loss associated with the recall, excluding recovered inventory.']
  press_release_issued boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a public press release was issued for the recall.']
  product_recovery_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units successfully recovered from the market.']
  quantity_affected varchar [note: 'Total number of units (e.g., cases, bottles) affected by the recall.']
  recall_classification varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the recall purpose.']
  recall_closure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the recall was officially closed.']
  recall_initiation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the recall was formally initiated.']
  recall_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the recall by the company or regulator.']
  recall_reason varchar [note: 'Primary cause prompting the recall.']
  recall_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the recall event.']
  recall_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the recall according to FDA definitions or voluntary withdrawal.']
  retailer_notification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of retailer notifications regarding the recall.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Assessed risk level of the recall based on severity and exposure.']
  root_cause_analysis varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the root cause investigation findings.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the recall record.']
  usda_notification_date date [note: 'Date the USDA was notified of the recall (if applicable).']

  Note: 'Master record for product recall and withdrawal events. Captures recall ID, recall type (Class I, II, III per FDA / voluntary withdrawal), triggering event (micro failure, allergen mislabel, foreign material, regulatory action), affected SKUs, affected lot/date codes, quantity affected, distribution scope, recall initiation date, FDA/USDA notification date, press release issued flag, retailer notification status, product recovery quantity, and recall closure date. SSOT for recall management and regulatory reporting.'
}

Table quality.qms_document {
  qms_document_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each QMS document record.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: QMS document management costs are posted to a GL account for expense tracking and compliance audit.']
  owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: QMS documents have an owner employee responsible for maintenance and training, required for document control audits.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: QMS documents (e.g., SOPs) linked to specific R&D projects support compliance and knowledge management.']
  spec_quality_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.specification. Business justification: QMS documents often pertain to a specific product specification.']
  applicable_sites varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of plant or site codes where the document applies.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the document was approved.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the approval workflow.']
  approver varchar [note: 'Name of the individual who approved the document.']
  checksum varchar [note: 'Hash value (e.g., SHA‑256) for file integrity verification.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data classification of the document per corporate policy.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was first created in the system.']
  document_category varchar [note: 'High‑level business domain the document belongs to.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the document (e.g., SOP-001, HACCP-2023).']
  document_subtype varchar [note: 'Optional finer classification of the document (e.g., SOP – Production, SOP – Packaging).']
  document_type varchar [note: 'Category of the QMS document. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Validation Protocol — promote to reference product]']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the document becomes effective for the organization.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the document is no longer valid; may be null for indefinite documents.']
  file_format varchar [note: 'Electronic file format of the document.']
  file_path varchar [note: 'Storage location or URL of the electronic document.']
  file_size_bytes varchar [note: 'Size of the electronic file in bytes.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the record.']
  owner varchar [note: 'Name of the person or function responsible for the document.']
  owner_department varchar [note: 'Organizational department that owns the document.']
  qms_document_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the document.']
  regulatory_compliance varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework(s) the document satisfies.']
  review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic review of the document.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential revision count for the document.']
  title varchar [note: 'Human‑readable title of the QMS document.']
  training_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether required training has been completed.']
  training_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether personnel must be trained on this document.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier following the company\'s versioning scheme (e.g., 1.0, 2.1.3).']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the record.']

  Note: 'Master record for quality management system (QMS) controlled documents managed in Veeva Vault. Captures document ID, document type (SOP, work instruction, form, policy, specification, HACCP plan, validation protocol), title, version number, effective date, expiry/review date, document owner, approval workflow status, training requirement flag, and applicable sites. SSOT for QMS document control and version management per ISO 9001 Clause 7.5 and GFSI scheme requirements.'
}

Table quality.supplier_quality_assessment {
  supplier_quality_assessment_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each supplier quality assessment record.']
  assessor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Supplier assessments are performed by a qualified employee (assessor) whose identity is needed for audit trails.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Supplier assessment costs are allocated to a cost center for budgeting and performance measurement.']
  spec_quality_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.specification. Business justification: Supplier quality assessments are performed against product specifications.']
  supplier_esg_score_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.supplier_esg_score. Business justification: Supplier Quality Assessment incorporates ESG scores; the assessment workflow uses the supplier_esg_score to evaluate environmental and social performance alongside quality metrics.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the supplier being assessed.']
  approved_supplier_list_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the supplier on the company\'s Approved Supplier List.']
  assessment_date date [note: 'Date the assessment was performed or officially recorded.']
  assessment_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments and observations recorded during the assessment.']
  assessment_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the assessment for tracking and communication with suppliers.']
  assessment_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the assessment indicating overall compliance.']
  assessment_type varchar [note: 'Method used to conduct the supplier quality assessment.']
  assessor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the quality professional who performed the assessment.']
  audit_report_reference varchar [note: 'Document identifier for the detailed audit report linked to this assessment.']
  brc_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier holds a valid BRC certification.']
  brix_value decimal(4,2) [note: 'Sugar content measurement of the sample expressed in degrees Brix.']
  cap_action_implemented boolean [note: 'Indicates whether identified corrective and preventive actions have been completed.']
  certification_status varchar [note: 'Current certification status of the supplier under relevant schemes.']
  cfu_count varchar [note: 'Microbiological count (CFU) observed in sampled material.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier meets all required compliance criteria.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Deadline for implementing any required corrective actions.']
  corrective_action_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the assessment identified issues that need corrective action.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the assessment record was first created in the system.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Source system where the assessment data originated.']
  document_control_number varchar [note: 'Reference number for the quality management document linked to this assessment.']
  food_safety_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (0‑100) reflecting the supplier\'s food‑safety performance.']
  fssc_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier holds a valid FSSC 22000 certification.']
  last_reviewed_by varchar [note: 'Name of the quality manager who performed the final review of the assessment.']
  last_reviewed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the final review was completed.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing lot identifier associated with the sampled material.']
  material_category varchar [note: 'Category of the material or ingredient supplied (e.g., raw ingredient, packaging, finished good).']
  non_conformance_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct non‑conformances identified during the assessment.']
  overall_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Weighted aggregate of food safety, quality, and compliance scores.']
  ph_value decimal(4,2) [note: 'Measured acidity/alkalinity of the sample.']
  quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (0‑100) reflecting overall product quality performance of the supplier.']
  reassessment_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the supplier must be re‑assessed to maintain approval.']
  regulated_product_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the assessed material is subject to specific regulatory controls.']
  regulatory_basis varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework(s) governing the assessment criteria.']
  risk_tier varchar [note: 'Risk classification assigned to the supplier based on assessment outcomes.']
  sample_size varchar [note: 'Number of samples taken for laboratory testing.']
  sampling_method varchar [note: 'Method used to select samples for testing during the assessment.']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Estimated shelf life of the material in days based on assessment.']
  sqf_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier holds a valid SQF certification.']
  supplier_quality_assessment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the assessment.']
  supplier_rating varchar [note: 'Overall rating assigned to the supplier based on assessment results.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the assessment record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for supplier quality evaluations and approved supplier assessments conducted by the quality team. Captures assessment ID, supplier, material/ingredient category, assessment type (questionnaire, on-site audit, desk review, third-party certificate review), assessment date, SQF/BRC/FSSC 22000 certification status, food safety score, quality score, approved supplier list (ASL) status, risk tier, and re-assessment due date. Supports TraceGains supplier compliance management.'
}

Table quality.process_validation {
  process_validation_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the process validation record.']
  approval_authority_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Process validation results require sign‑off by an approval authority employee to satisfy GMP and regulatory validation records.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Process validation activities generate costs that must be recorded in a GL account for compliance budgeting.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'FK to manufacturing.plant']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product to which the validation applies.']
  spec_quality_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.specification. Business justification: Process validation studies validate manufacturing processes for a product defined by its specification.']
  acceptance_criteria varchar [note: 'Pre‑defined criteria that must be met for validation to be accepted.']
  allergen_changeover_validation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the validation includes allergen changeover verification.']
  approval_authority varchar [note: 'Name or role of the individual/committee approving the validation.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the validation was formally approved.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch identifier when validation is scoped to a specific production batch.']
  cleaning_validation_method varchar [note: 'Method used for cleaning validation (e.g., visual, swab, chemical, microbiological).']
  confidence_level decimal(5,2) [note: 'Statistical confidence (percentage) associated with the validation results.']
  doc_control_number varchar [note: 'Reference number linking to the controlled QMS document.']
  equipment_qualification_study varchar [note: 'Reference to the equipment qualification study associated with this validation.']
  execution_end_date date [note: 'Date when validation activities were completed.']
  execution_start_date date [note: 'Date when validation activities commenced.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent review of the validation record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations related to the validation.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the validation: validated, failed, or conditionally validated.']
  process_validation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the validation record.']
  protocol_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the detailed validation protocol document.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the validation record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the validation record.']
  regulatory_basis varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework or standard that drives the validation requirements.']
  revalidation_interval_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of days between required revalidations.']
  revalidation_trigger varchar [note: 'Condition or event that initiates a revalidation (e.g., product change, equipment upgrade).']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk classification associated with the validation.']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Narrative describing the scope (product, line, equipment) covered by the validation.']
  test_results_summary varchar [note: 'Concise summary of test outcomes and observations.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who last modified the validation record.']
  validation_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code assigned to the validation (e.g., PV‑2023‑001).']
  validation_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the validation study.']
  validation_scope varchar [note: 'High‑level scope indicator (product, line, equipment).']
  validation_type varchar [note: 'Category of validation performed: process, cleaning/CIP, equipment, thermal, or aseptic.']
  validation_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the validation protocol or study.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who initially created the validation record.']

  Note: 'Master record for process validation, cleaning validation, and equipment qualification studies at manufacturing facilities. Captures validation type (process, cleaning/CIP, equipment, thermal process, aseptic), scope (product, line, equipment), protocol reference, execution dates, acceptance criteria, test results summary, statistical confidence level, outcome (validated/failed), revalidation trigger conditions, and approval authority. Includes cleaning validation for allergen changeover verification. Critical for GMP compliance, HACCP verification, and GFSI scheme requirements (SQF 2.5, BRC 6.1).'
}

Table quality.environmental_monitoring {
  environmental_monitoring_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each environmental monitoring sample record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Environmental monitoring expenses are tracked against a cost center for compliance cost reporting.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the quality analyst who performed or reviewed the test.']
  lab_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the laboratory where the sample was analyzed.']
  collection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the environmental sample was collected at the facility.']
  corrective_action_triggered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the test result initiated a corrective action according to the EMP SOP.']
  detection_limit decimal(10,2) [note: 'Lowest concentration of the target that the method can reliably detect, expressed in the same units as result_quantity.']
  humidity_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Relative humidity at the time of sample collection, expressed as a percentage.']
  location_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the exact location within the zone where the sample was collected.']
  method_used varchar [note: 'Analytical method employed for the test (e.g., PCR, culture, ATP luminometer).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text field for any additional observations, comments, or deviations noted by the analyst.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the environmental monitoring record was first entered into the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the environmental monitoring record.']
  result_flag varchar [note: 'Qualitative outcome of the test indicating presence (positive) or absence (negative) of the target organism.']
  result_quantity decimal(12,4) [note: 'Numeric result of the test (e.g., CFU per cm²) when a quantitative measurement is applicable.']
  sample_type varchar [note: 'Physical form of the sample collected (e.g., swab, air, surface rinse, water).']
  temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Ambient temperature at the time of sample collection, recorded in degrees Celsius.']
  test_target varchar [note: 'Microbiological or hygiene target analyzed for the sample (e.g., Listeria spp., Listeria monocytogenes, ATP, Aerobic Plate Count).']
  trend_status varchar [note: 'Trend classification based on historical results for the same zone and target.']
  zone varchar [note: 'Designated zone of the facility where the sample was taken (Zone 1 food contact, Zone 2 near food contact, Zone 3 non‑food contact, Zone 4 remote).']

  Note: 'Transactional record for environmental monitoring program (EMP) samples collected across production facilities. Captures sample ID, sample zone (Zone 1 food contact, Zone 2 near food contact, Zone 3 non-food contact, Zone 4 remote), sample location, collection date, test target (Listeria spp., Listeria monocytogenes, ATP, APC), result (positive/negative, CFU/cm²), corrective action triggered flag, and trend status. Supports FSMA Preventive Controls environmental monitoring requirements.'
}

Table quality.hold_record {
  hold_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the hold record.']
  center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.distribution_center. Business justification: Inventory holds are managed at specific distribution centers; linking hold_record to the DC enables inventory visibility and recall coordination.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Holding inventory incurs cost; linking to a cost center enables accurate inventory holding cost accounting.']
  order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.order. Business justification: Order Hold Management: holds are often placed on specific sales orders pending quality investigation; linking provides order status and financial impact visibility.']
  primary_hold_initiated_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'System identifier of the person who created the hold.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Quality hold records must reference SKU to manage inventory holds per specific product SKU.']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Hold management process needs to tie a hold record to the exact stock position being quarantined for traceability and release decisions.']
  attached_document_count varchar [note: 'Number of supporting documents (e.g., lab reports, audit findings) attached to the hold record.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing batch number associated with the held material.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current regulatory compliance status of the held material.']
  disposition_decision varchar [note: 'Final action taken on the held material (release, rework, destroy, return to supplier, or quarantine).']
  disposition_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for the chosen disposition action.']
  disposition_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the disposition decision was executed.']
  hold_creation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the hold record was first created in the system.']
  hold_expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the hold is scheduled to be automatically released if no disposition occurs.']
  hold_initiated_by varchar [note: 'Name of the person who created the hold record.']
  hold_initiated_department varchar [note: 'Business department that originated the hold request.']
  hold_initiation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the hold was placed.']
  hold_last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the hold record.']
  hold_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable identifier assigned to the hold transaction.']
  hold_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text description explaining why the hold was initiated.']
  hold_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the hold (e.g., active, released, rework, destroyed, pending, closed).']
  hold_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the hold reason (quality, microbiological, allergen, regulatory, or customer complaint).']
  is_fefo_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the hold respects First‑Expired‑First‑Out inventory rules.']
  is_fifo_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the hold respects First‑In‑First‑Out inventory rules.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot identifier for the batch of material placed on hold.']
  material_upc varchar [note: 'Universal Product Code of the material, 12‑digit numeric identifier.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑text comments or observations related to the hold.']
  quality_manager_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the quality manager who authorized the hold.']
  quantity_held decimal(15,3) [note: 'Amount of material placed on hold, expressed in the selected unit of measure.']
  regulatory_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number from FDA, USDA, or other regulator linked to the hold.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the held quantity (e.g., kilogram, gram, pound, unit, case, liter).']
  warehouse_location_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse or storage location where the hold is physically applied.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for product holds (quality, micro, allergen, regulatory, customer complaint) placed on finished goods, WIP, or raw materials pending quality disposition. Captures hold type, affected material/SKU, lot/batch, quantity, hold initiation date, reason, warehouse location, disposition decision (release, rework, destroy, return to supplier), disposition date, and authorizing quality manager. Integrates with warehouse management for physical segregation and supports FIFO/FEFO inventory management, lot traceability, and regulatory containment requirements.'
}

Table quality.certificate_of_analysis {
  certificate_of_analysis_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for certificate_of_analysis']
  analyst_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: COA must record the analyst employee who performed the test for traceability and compliance reporting.']
  product_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.product_registration. Business justification: COA documents are part of the product registration compliance package; linking enables regulatory review of test results.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: COA documents are tied to SKU for regulatory compliance and traceability of material specifications.']
  spec_quality_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.specification. Business justification: Certificate of Analysis records test results for a lot; linking to specification ties the CoA to the product specification.']
  alcohol_by_volume decimal(5,2) [note: 'Alcohol content expressed as a percentage of volume.']
  allergen_presence varchar [note: 'Indicates whether declared allergens were detected.']
  analyst_name varchar [note: 'Name of the laboratory analyst who performed the test.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the CoA was formally approved.']
  approver_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person who approved the CoA.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch identifier associated with the lot.']
  brix decimal(5,2) [note: 'Sugar content expressed in degrees Brix.']
  certificate_of_analysis_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the CoA record.']
  coa_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable identifier assigned to the CoA by the issuing entity.']
  coa_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the CoA source: internal lab, supplier‑provided, or third‑party laboratory.']
  compliance_standard varchar [note: 'Regulatory or industry standard referenced (e.g., FDA, GFSI, ISO 22000).']
  digital_document_uri varchar [note: 'Link to the digital copy of the CoA document.']
  document_control_number varchar [note: 'Control number used for document management and versioning.']
  document_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the CoA document.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the CoA is no longer valid.']
  fda_approval_number varchar [note: 'FDA approval identifier associated with the product, if applicable.']
  gfs_i_certified boolean [note: 'True if the product is certified under a GFSI scheme.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date the CoA was formally issued.']
  issuing_entity varchar [note: 'Entity that performed the testing and issued the CoA.']
  lab_location varchar [note: 'Physical location of the laboratory where testing occurred.']
  lot_disposition varchar [note: 'Final disposition of the lot after CoA evaluation.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot identifier for the batch of material tested.']
  material_upc varchar [note: 'UPC code of the material covered by the CoA.']
  microbial_cfu_count varchar [note: 'Colony‑forming unit count from microbiological testing.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations related to the CoA.']
  overall_conformance varchar [note: 'Overall pass/fail result of the CoA against its specifications.']
  ph decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured pH of the product.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Code of the manufacturing plant where the material was produced.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the CoA record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the CoA record.']
  regulatory_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the CoA meets applicable regulatory requirements.']
  result_status varchar [note: 'Pass/fail/out‑of‑spec status for the primary test.']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Declared shelf life of the product in days.']
  specification_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the specification document or standard used for testing.']
  storage_humidity_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Recommended storage humidity level.']
  storage_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Recommended storage temperature for the product.']
  test_date date [note: 'Date on which the analytical testing was performed.']
  test_method varchar [note: 'Standard method or protocol used for the primary test.']
  test_result_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the primary test result (e.g., mg/kg).']
  test_result_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Numeric result of the primary analytical test.']

  Note: 'Master record for Certificates of Analysis (CoA) issued for finished goods lots and received from ingredient/raw material suppliers. Captures CoA ID, material/SKU, lot/batch number, issuing entity (internal lab, supplier, third-party lab), issue date, test parameters and results (physical, chemical, microbiological), specification reference, overall conformance status, and digital document reference. Supports customer CoA requests, supplier incoming quality control, and regulatory traceability.'
}

Table quality.audit_finding {
  audit_finding_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for audit_finding']

  audit_type varchar [note: 'Origin of the audit that generated the finding.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date the finding was formally closed.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes added by auditors or owners.']
  corrective_action_description varchar [note: 'Planned steps to remediate the finding.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Target date for completing corrective actions.']
  corrective_action_owner varchar [note: 'Individual or team responsible for executing corrective actions.']
  corrective_action_status varchar [note: 'Current progress state of the corrective action.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the finding record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative of the issue identified during the audit.']
  detection_date date [note: 'Date the non‑conformance was first observed.']
  finding_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable identifier assigned by the audit team to track the finding.']
  finding_source varchar [note: 'Method or channel through which the finding was identified.']
  follow_up_date date [note: 'Planned date for post‑closure verification.']
  follow_up_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether additional monitoring is needed after closure.']
  is_repeat_finding boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the same issue has been recorded previously.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Plant, warehouse, or site where the finding was observed.']
  non_conformance_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the type of deviation.']
  product_code varchar [note: 'SKU or internal code of the product affected.']
  regulatory_standard varchar [note: 'Applicable food‑safety standard (e.g., SQF, BRC, FSSC 22000).']
  reported_by_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person who reported the finding.']
  responsible_party varchar [note: 'Team, department, or individual accountable for corrective action.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric risk rating derived from severity and likelihood.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category describing the underlying cause.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Risk severity assigned to the finding based on impact and likelihood.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the finding.']
  title varchar [note: 'Brief title summarizing the audit finding.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the finding record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for audit_finding. Referenced by related_audit_finding_id.'
}

Table quality.laboratory {
  laboratory_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for laboratory']
  parent_laboratory_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on laboratory (parent_laboratory_id)']
  accreditation_body varchar [note: 'Organization that provided the laboratory accreditation.']
  accreditation_date date [note: 'Date when the laboratory received its accreditation.']
  accreditation_status varchar [note: 'Current accreditation status of the laboratory.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the laboratory.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information (e.g., suite or building).']
  capacity_per_day varchar [note: 'Maximum number of samples the laboratory can process per day.']
  certification_expiry date [note: 'Expiration date of the laboratory\'s current certification.']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the laboratory is situated.']
  code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the laboratory in external systems.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall compliance standing of the laboratory.']
  country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the laboratory.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the laboratory record was first created.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the laboratory\'s purpose and capabilities.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the laboratory became operational or the record became effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the laboratory ceased operations or the record is no longer effective (nullable).']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for laboratory communications.']
  emergency_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person to contact in an emergency.']
  emergency_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number for emergency contact related to the laboratory.']
  equipment_count varchar [note: 'Total number of major testing instruments in the laboratory.']
  humidity_control_range varchar [note: 'Allowed humidity range for controlled environments (e.g., "30-50% RH").']
  inspection_score varchar [note: 'Numeric score (0‑100) resulting from the latest inspection.']
  is_haccp_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the laboratory complies with HACCP requirements.']
  lab_area_sqm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total floor area of the laboratory in square meters.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent regulatory or internal inspection.']
  location_name varchar [note: 'Name of the site or campus where the laboratory is located.']
  manager_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Direct phone number of the laboratory manager.']
  manager_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the person responsible for laboratory operations.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the laboratory.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional remarks or comments about the laboratory.']
  operating_hours varchar [note: 'Standard daily operating hours (e.g., "08:00-17:00").']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact telephone number for the laboratory.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal/ZIP code for the laboratory address.']
  primary_testing_method varchar [note: 'Main analytical technique employed by the laboratory (e.g., HPLC, GC‑MS).']
  safety_incident_count varchar [note: 'Number of recorded safety incidents in the laboratory over the past year.']
  shift_pattern varchar [note: 'Typical work shift pattern for laboratory staff.']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province of the laboratory location.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the laboratory.']
  temperature_control_range varchar [note: 'Allowed temperature range for controlled environments (e.g., "2-8C").']
  type varchar [note: 'Category of testing performed by the laboratory.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the laboratory record.']
  waste_disposal_method varchar [note: 'Method used for disposing laboratory waste (e.g., incineration, autoclave).']

  Note: 'Master reference table for laboratory. Referenced by lab_id.'
}

Table quality.sample {
  analyst_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the analyst who performed the test.']
  capa_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the corrective action record linked to a non‑conformance.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee or contractor who performed the sample collection.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product that the sample represents.']
  analysis_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the laboratory analysis was completed.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing batch number associated with the sampled product.']
  collection_location varchar [note: 'Facility, plant, or site code where the sample was taken.']
  collection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the sample was physically collected.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑text field for additional observations or notes about the sample.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the sample record was first created in the system.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Calculated expiry date of the sampled product based on shelf‑life.']
  humidity_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Relative humidity measured at the collection point.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot identifier used for traceability of the sampled material.']
  non_conformance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the sample result triggered a non‑conformance event.']
  result_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the result value (e.g., "CFU/g", "pH").']
  result_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Numeric value produced by the test (e.g., CFU count, pH).']
  sample_code varchar [note: 'External code or barcode assigned to the sample for tracking across labs and processes.']
  sample_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or description of the sample (e.g., "Batch 12 – Chocolate Bar").']
  sample_type varchar [note: 'Category of the sample such as raw material, finished product, in‑process, or environmental swab.']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the product is expected to remain fit for consumption.']
