  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the sample within the quality workflow.']
  storage_condition varchar [note: 'Required storage condition for the sample after collection.']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'Physical location (e.g., freezer ID, shelf) where the sample is stored.']
  temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Temperature of the sample at the time of collection, expressed in degrees Celsius.']
  test_method varchar [note: 'Analytical method or protocol used to evaluate the sample (e.g., AOAC, ISO 22000).']
  test_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the primary quality test performed on the sample.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the sample record.']
  volume_ml decimal(10,2) [note: 'Measured volume of the sample in milliliters.']
  weight_grams decimal(10,2) [note: 'Measured weight of the sample in grams.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for sample. Referenced by sample_id.'
}

Table supply.demand_plan {
  demand_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each demand plan record at the SKU/DC/time-period granularity. Primary key for the demand_plan data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer or channel account for which the demand plan is generated, when the plan is customer-specific (e.g., key account demand plan). Nullable for aggregate/market-level plans.']
  center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Distribution Center (DC) or warehouse location for which the demand plan is generated. Supports network-level demand disaggregation across the DC network and 3PL partners.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Name or user identifier of the business stakeholder (e.g., Demand Planning Manager, VP Supply Chain) who approved the consensus forecast during the S&OP cycle. Supports governance and audit trail for plan approval workflows.']
  plan_version_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific planning version or scenario under which this demand plan record was generated (e.g., baseline, consensus, adjusted). Supports multi-version S&OP cycle management.']
  product_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.product_registration. Business justification: Demand planning incorporates product registration status to avoid forecasting for items pending regulatory approval.']
  promotion_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trade.promotion_plan. Business justification: Demand Planning incorporates promotion plans to adjust forecasted demand; linking enables the Demand Impact Forecast report.']
  sales_opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.opportunity. Business justification: Demand forecasting uses qualified sales opportunities as drivers; the Opportunity‑Based Forecasting report requires this link.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) for which the demand plan is generated. The SKU is the lowest-level product identifier used in demand planning and inventory management.']
  sop_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Sales and Operations Planning (S&OP) / Integrated Business Planning (IBP) cycle in which this demand plan record was generated or last updated. Enables traceability of demand plan versions to specific planning cycles.']
  adjusted_forecast_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Demand forecast quantity after planner-applied manual adjustments, overrides, or market intelligence inputs on top of the statistical baseline. Reflects demand planner judgment before consensus alignment.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the demand plan record was formally approved during the S&OP consensus cycle. Supports audit trail, SLA compliance tracking, and planning cycle performance measurement.']
  baseline_forecast_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Underlying demand forecast quantity representing the expected sell-through volume excluding any promotional uplift, new product launch effects, or extraordinary events. Used as the reference point for uplift/downlift factor calculation.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Trade channel for which this demand plan is generated (e.g., retail, foodservice, direct-to-consumer, e-commerce). Supports channel-level demand disaggregation and channel-specific supply planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: retail|foodservice|direct_to_consumer|ecommerce|club|convenience|drug|mass — promote to reference product]']
  consensus_forecast_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Final agreed demand volume (in selling units) resulting from the cross-functional S&OP consensus process, incorporating inputs from Sales, Marketing, Finance, and Supply Chain. This is the primary input to supply planning and inventory optimization.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this demand plan record was first created in the system. Serves as the audit creation timestamp for data lineage and Silver Layer ingestion tracking.']
  customer_order_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Confirmed customer purchase order quantity for the SKU/DC/period, sourced from SAP S/4HANA SD or EDI order feeds. Represents committed demand and is used to override or supplement statistical forecasts in the near-term horizon.']
  demand_class varchar [note: 'Classification of the demand type for supply planning purposes. \'regular\' = baseline replenishment demand; \'promotional\' = trade promotion-driven demand; \'new_product\' = NPD launch demand; \'phase_out\' = end-of-life demand; \'safety_stock\' = buffer stock demand.']
  demand_sensing_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Near-term demand signal quantity derived from high-frequency data inputs including Point of Sale (POS) data, customer orders, and syndicated data (Nielsen/IRI). Used for daily/weekly supply execution and short-horizon replenishment decisions.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period (month or quarter number) within the fiscal year to which this demand plan record belongs. Supports period-over-period demand trend analysis and S&OP cycle alignment.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which this demand plan record belongs, as defined by the company\'s financial calendar. Used for annual budgeting, S&OP alignment, and year-over-year demand comparison.']
  forecast_algorithm varchar [note: 'Statistical or machine learning algorithm used to generate the baseline statistical forecast for this SKU/period. Used for model governance, accuracy benchmarking, and algorithm selection optimization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: exponential_smoothing|arima|moving_average|machine_learning|croston|holt_winters|causal — promote to reference product]']
  forecast_confidence_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Statistical confidence level (expressed as a percentage, 0–100) associated with the demand forecast, indicating the reliability of the forecast model output. Used to flag low-confidence forecasts for planner review and safety stock buffer sizing.']
  forecast_error_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Historical Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) of the forecasting model for this SKU/DC combination, expressed as a percentage. Used to assess forecast model accuracy, trigger model recalibration, and size safety stock buffers.']
  forecast_lower_bound_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Lower confidence interval bound for the demand forecast quantity, representing the low-end demand scenario. Used for inventory optimization, downside risk assessment, and supply-demand balancing.']
  forecast_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure in which all forecast quantity fields are expressed (e.g., CS = cases, EA = each, LB = pounds, KG = kilograms). Aligns with the selling unit of measure defined in the SAP S/4HANA material master. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CS|EA|LB|KG|OZ|GAL|L|MT — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  forecast_upper_bound_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Upper confidence interval bound for the demand forecast quantity, representing the high-end demand scenario. Used for safety stock calculation, capacity planning, and supply risk management.']
  is_frozen boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the demand plan record is frozen (locked) for supply execution and cannot be modified by planners. Frozen plans are used as firm inputs to production scheduling and procurement within the planning fence horizon.']
  is_new_product boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this demand plan record is for a New Product Development (NPD) / new SKU launch, where no historical sales data exists for statistical forecasting. Triggers use of analogous product forecasting or market-based estimation methods.']
  plan_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the demand plan record, formatted as DP-YYYY-MM-NNNNNN. Used for cross-system traceability between SAP IBP, JDA/Blue Yonder, and ERP systems.']
  plan_period_end_date date [note: 'The last calendar date of the planning time bucket to which this demand plan record applies. Together with plan_period_start_date, defines the demand planning horizon window.']
  plan_period_start_date date [note: 'The first calendar date of the planning time bucket (day, week, or month) to which this demand plan record applies. Used to align demand signals with supply planning and inventory replenishment cycles.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the demand plan record within the S&OP consensus cycle. \'statistical\' = system-generated baseline; \'adjusted\' = planner-modified; \'consensus\' = cross-functional agreed; \'approved\' = executive-signed-off; \'frozen\' = locked for supply execution. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|statistical|adjusted|consensus|approved|frozen|superseded — promote to reference product]']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the demand plan by its generation method and purpose. \'statistical\' = algorithm-generated baseline; \'consensus\' = S&OP agreed volume; \'customer_order\' = customer-committed orders; \'demand_sensing\' = near-term POS/syndicated signal; \'promotional\' = uplift-adjusted plan. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: statistical|consensus|customer_order|demand_sensing|promotional — promote to reference product]']
  planner_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text commentary entered by the demand planner to document the rationale for manual forecast adjustments, market intelligence inputs, or exceptions. Supports audit trail and knowledge transfer within the S&OP process.']
  planning_horizon varchar [note: 'Time granularity of the demand plan bucket. \'daily\' and \'weekly\' are used for near-term demand sensing and supply execution; \'monthly\' is used for S&OP and IBP cycles; \'quarterly\' for long-range planning.']
  pos_sales_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Actual Point of Sale (POS) sell-through quantity for the SKU during the plan period, sourced from retailer POS data feeds. Used as a demand sensing input signal to update near-term forecasts and detect demand shifts.']
  promotional_uplift_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Incremental demand volume (in selling units) attributed to planned trade promotions, marketing campaigns, or temporary price reductions above the baseline forecast. Sourced from Trade Promotion Management (TPM) systems and applied as an uplift factor.']
  seasonality_index decimal(7,4) [note: 'Seasonal index factor for the SKU/period combination, representing the ratio of period demand to average annual demand. Values >1.0 indicate above-average seasonal demand; <1.0 indicate below-average. Used in statistical forecasting model decomposition.']
  sensing_horizon_days varchar [note: 'Number of days into the future covered by the demand sensing signal for this plan record. Typically 1–14 days for near-term POS-driven demand sensing. Distinguishes short-horizon sensing from longer-horizon statistical forecasts.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system from which this demand plan record was generated or ingested. Used for data lineage tracking and cross-system reconciliation in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  statistical_forecast_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'System-generated baseline demand forecast quantity (in selling units) produced by the statistical forecasting algorithm (e.g., exponential smoothing, ARIMA, machine learning) in SAP IBP or JDA/Blue Yonder, prior to any manual adjustment or consensus override.']
  syndicated_market_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Market-level demand quantity derived from syndicated data sources (Nielsen/IRI market tracking or SPINS natural channel data) for the SKU/period. Used to calibrate statistical forecasts against total market consumption trends.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this demand plan record was last modified, including planner adjustments, consensus overrides, or system recalculations. Used for change tracking and incremental Silver Layer processing.']
  uplift_factor decimal(7,4) [note: 'Multiplicative factor applied to the baseline forecast to account for demand uplift (>1.0) or downlift (<1.0) from events such as trade promotions, new product launches, distribution gains/losses, or market disruptions. A value of 1.0 indicates no adjustment.']
  uplift_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason code explaining the cause of the uplift or downlift factor applied to the baseline forecast. Used for demand decomposition analysis and post-event forecast accuracy review. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promotion|new_product_launch|distribution_gain|distribution_loss|seasonality|event|competitor_action|price_change|none — promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Statistical and consensus-based demand forecast at SKU/DC/time-period granularity, serving as the primary input to supply planning and inventory optimization. Captures baseline statistical forecast, adjusted forecast, consensus volume, demand sensing signals (POS data, syndicated data, customer orders), uplift/downlift factors, confidence intervals, and sensing horizon. Supports both monthly S&OP planning horizons and daily/weekly demand signal refresh for near-term supply execution. Generated through demand sensing and S&OP consensus cycles.'
}

Table supply.plan {
  plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the supply plan record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for this entity.']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supply chain manager or S&OP lead who approved this supply plan version. Required for IBP governance and approval workflow audit trail.']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Brand‑level supply planning aligns production, inventory, and distribution with the brand’s marketing objectives and sales targets.']
  center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Distribution Center (DC) or 3PL node in the supply network for which this plan defines planned receipts and shipments.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Cost allocation of a production plan to an internal cost center for monthly financial reporting and variance analysis.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Posting of production costs from the supply plan to the appropriate GL account for accounting.']
  ibp_scenario_id varchar [note: 'The scenario identifier from SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning) under which this supply plan was generated. Enables traceability back to the source IBP planning run and scenario.']
  plan_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supply chain planner or IBP user responsible for authoring and owning this supply plan. Supports planner attribution and audit trail.']
  plan_version_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific version snapshot of this supply plan, enabling cycle-over-cycle comparison and plan-vs-plan variance analysis.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing plant or production facility associated with this supply plan record. Used for SKU/plant-level planning granularity.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Profitability analysis of each supply plan by profit center to support P&L statements.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: Supply Plan aligns production with a specific New Product Development project; planners need the project ID to sync supply with product launch roadmap.']
  sales_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.sales_contract. Business justification: Supply planning must honor contracted volumes; linking supply_plan to sales_contract enables the Contract‑Based Production Planning process.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) for which this supply plan is generated. Drives replenishment signals and production scheduling at the SKU level.']
  allocation_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether supply allocation decisions are required for this SKU/DC due to constrained supply. When true, the supply plan drives allocation logic in the order management and trade management systems.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the supply plan was formally approved by the designated S&OP approver. Null if the plan has not yet been approved. Part of IBP governance audit trail.']
  closing_inventory_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Projected on-hand inventory quantity for the SKU at the end of the planning horizon after accounting for planned production, receipts, and shipments. Indicates supply-demand balance position.']
  co_packing_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the planned production quantity for this SKU involves co-packing or toll manufacturing arrangements with a third-party contract manufacturer, affecting supply lead times and MOQ constraints.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this supply plan record was first captured in the data platform. Used for audit trail and IBP governance.']
  demand_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total planned demand quantity for the SKU over the planning horizon as consumed from the demand plan. Represents the demand signal used for supply-demand balancing in IBP.']
  demand_sensing_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the demand quantity in this supply plan was derived from SAP IBP Demand Sensing (short-horizon statistical signal) rather than the standard statistical forecast. Affects plan accuracy benchmarking.']
  horizon_end_date date [note: 'The last date of the planning horizon covered by this supply plan. Defines the end boundary for planned production, receipts, and shipment quantities.']
  horizon_start_date date [note: 'The first date of the planning horizon covered by this supply plan. Defines the start boundary for planned production, receipts, and shipment quantities.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Total supply lead time in calendar days for the SKU from production order release to DC receipt. Used in replenishment signal timing and safety stock calculations.']
  lot_size decimal(18,4) [note: 'Standard production lot size for the SKU at the plant, used to round planned production quantities to feasible manufacturing batch sizes in the constrained supply plan.']
  moq decimal(18,4) [note: 'Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) constraint applied to this SKU in the supply plan. Ensures production and procurement orders meet supplier or manufacturing minimum thresholds.']
  network_tier varchar [note: 'Identifies the tier of the supply network node (plant, primary DC, secondary DC, 3PL, cross-dock) for which this supply plan record is generated. Supports network design analytics.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or commentary entered by the planner or approver describing key assumptions, constraints, or exceptions relevant to this supply plan version. Supports IBP governance documentation.']
  opening_inventory_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'On-hand inventory quantity for the SKU at the start of the planning horizon. Used as the baseline for supply-demand balance position calculations.']
  plan_date timestamp [note: 'The real-world business event timestamp when this supply plan version was generated or published in the IBP system. Distinct from the audit record creation timestamp.']
  plan_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the supply plan, used in IBP, ERP, and cross-functional S&OP communications. Follows the SP-{alphanumeric} naming convention.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the supply plan within the IBP approval workflow. Drives downstream replenishment signal release and production scheduling actions.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the supply plan indicating whether it reflects capacity-constrained production, unconstrained demand, consensus S&OP output, baseline reference, or simulation scenario.']
  planned_production_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total planned production quantity for the SKU at the plant over the planning horizon, expressed in the SKU\'s base unit of measure. Key volume metric driving MES scheduling and capacity allocation.']
  planned_receipts_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total planned inbound receipts quantity for the SKU at the DC over the planning horizon, including transfers from plants and external supplier deliveries. Key volume metric for supply-demand balancing.']
  planned_shipments_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total planned outbound shipments quantity for the SKU from the DC over the planning horizon. Drives replenishment signals and allocation decisions when supply is constrained.']
  planning_cycle varchar [note: 'Identifies the IBP/S&OP planning cycle (e.g., monthly-2024-06) to which this supply plan belongs. Enables cycle-over-cycle comparison and governance tracking.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text reason provided by the approver when a supply plan is rejected in the IBP approval workflow. Null when plan_status is not \'rejected\'. Supports governance audit trail.']
  replenishment_signal_released boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the replenishment signal derived from this supply plan has been released to the ERP or WMS for execution. Tracks plan-to-execution handoff status.']
  rotation_policy varchar [note: 'Inventory rotation policy applied to this SKU in the supply plan. FIFO (First In First Out) and FEFO (First Expired First Out) are standard in food and beverage to minimize waste and ensure food safety compliance.']
  safety_stock_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Minimum buffer inventory quantity for the SKU at the DC/plant, set to protect against demand variability and supply disruptions. Drives replenishment trigger signals in IBP.']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Remaining shelf life in days for the SKU as applied in this supply plan. Governs FEFO (First Expired First Out) rotation policy and constrains planned shipment sequencing.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this supply plan record originated. Supports data lineage and cross-system reconciliation in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  supply_constraint_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this supply plan record reflects a constrained supply scenario where planned production or receipts are insufficient to fully satisfy planned demand. Triggers allocation decision workflows.']
  supply_demand_balance_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Net supply-demand balance position for the SKU over the planning horizon (planned supply minus planned demand). Positive values indicate surplus; negative values indicate a supply gap requiring allocation decisions.']
  uom varchar [note: 'Base unit of measure for all quantity fields in this supply plan record (e.g., CS=Case, EA=Each, KG=Kilogram). Aligns with the SKU\'s base UOM in SAP S/4HANA. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CS|EA|KG|LB|L|GAL|MT|OZ — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this supply plan record was last modified in the data platform. Tracks revisions and supports audit trail for IBP governance.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential integer version number of this supply plan snapshot within a planning cycle. Increments with each revision, enabling plan-vs-plan variance analysis.']

  Note: 'Constrained supply plan defining planned production quantities, planned receipts, planned shipments, and supply-demand balance positions at SKU/plant/DC level across the planning horizon. Supports full version management with plan snapshots for cycle-over-cycle comparison, version numbering, creation timestamps, planning cycle references, planner attribution, approval workflows, and plan-vs-plan variance analysis. Drives replenishment signals, production scheduling, and allocation decisions when supply is constrained. Each plan instance captures approval status, key volume metrics (total planned production, total planned receipts), and maintains audit trail for IBP governance.'
}

Table supply.replenishment_order {
  replenishment_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the replenishment order record in the Silver layer lakehouse. Primary key. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — represents a discrete supply chain planning event with a full lifecycle (planned → firmed → released → received).']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Replenishment Order Execution Report ties each order to the retailer account that generated demand, enabling OTIF performance tracking and cost allocation.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Promotional replenishment orders are created from active campaigns to ensure inventory availability for campaign‑driven sales.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the logistics carrier or 3PL partner assigned to transport this replenishment order. Used for carrier performance tracking, freight cost allocation, and SLA compliance monitoring.']
  center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.distribution_center. Business justification: Replenishment Order Execution process requires identifying the target DC to restock; linking ensures accurate inventory allocation and DC‑level reporting.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Assign order processing and procurement costs to a cost center for internal budgeting.']
  demand_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the demand plan or forecast that drove the generation of this replenishment order. Links supply execution back to the demand sensing or S&OP cycle, enabling supply-demand balancing analysis and forecast accuracy measurement.']
  destination_location_network_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the receiving plant, Distribution Center (DC), or 3PL facility where inventory is to be delivered. Drives inbound shipment creation, receiving dock scheduling, and inventory position updates at the destination.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Record the financial impact of replenishment orders in the appropriate GL account.']
  inbound_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the inbound shipment or Advanced Shipping Notice (ASN) created from this replenishment order upon release. Links the supply order to physical logistics execution for tracking, dock scheduling, and OTIF measurement.']
  primary_replenishment_network_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating plant, Distribution Center (DC), or 3PL facility from which inventory will be shipped or transferred. Used for network design analysis, inbound logistics planning, and supply-demand balancing across the DC network.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Replenishment orders are converted to external purchase orders; linking tracks the conversion and order lifecycle.']
  raw_material_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the finished good, raw material, or packaging material being replenished. Links to the material master for product specifications, shelf life parameters, and FIFO/FEFO rotation rules applicable to this order.']
  sales_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.order. Business justification: Order‑driven replenishment: each replenishment order is generated to satisfy a specific sales order, required for the Replenishment Execution Report.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: REQUIRED: Replenishment order processing and regulatory traceability need a direct FK to the SKU; experts expect sku_id on order for cost, shelf‑life, and compliance reporting.']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Order fulfillment process tracks which inventory stock position is being replenished, enabling OTIF and inventory aging analysis.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier or vendor party fulfilling this replenishment order for external procurement types (purchase requisition, purchase order). For internal stock transfers, this references the supplying plant or DC as a party. Maps to PARTY_REFERENCE category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'The date on which goods were physically received at the destination location (goods receipt posting date). Used to calculate On Time In Full (OTIF) performance, lead time actuals, and delivery variance against requested and confirmed dates.']
  asn_number varchar [note: 'The Advanced Shipping Notice (ASN) number transmitted by the supplier or source location confirming shipment of this replenishment order. Enables proactive receiving dock scheduling, WMS pre-receipt processing, and shipment tracking. Transmitted via EDI 856.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'The production batch or lot number associated with the material being replenished. Enables batch-level traceability for food safety recall management, FEFO rotation enforcement, and quality hold management. Populated when the replenishment order is linked to a specific production batch.']
  confirmed_delivery_date date [note: 'The delivery date confirmed by the supplier or supplying location. May differ from requested_delivery_date. Used to calculate delivery date variance, assess OTIF risk, and trigger escalation workflows when confirmation is later than requested.']
  confirmed_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The quantity confirmed by the supplier or supplying location as available and committed for fulfillment. May differ from ordered_quantity due to partial availability, capacity constraints, or MOQ adjustments. Used for supply-demand gap analysis and OTIF measurement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this replenishment order record was first captured in the data platform. Distinct from order_date (business event time). Used for data lineage, audit trail, and SLA monitoring of data freshness. Maps to RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the unit_cost and total_order_value are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency procurement and financial consolidation across global operations.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The expiration or best-before date of the batch being replenished. Used to enforce FEFO rotation policy, calculate shelf_life_days_remaining, and ensure compliance with customer minimum remaining shelf life requirements. Critical for food safety and waste reduction.']
  ibp_plan_version varchar [note: 'The SAP Integrated Business Planning (IBP) plan version or S&OP cycle version under which this replenishment order was generated (e.g., \'BASELINE_2024Q3\', \'CONSENSUS_V2\'). Enables comparison of supply proposals across planning scenarios and IBP consensus cycles.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'The planned lead time in calendar days from order release to expected goods receipt at the destination location. Derived from the purchasing info record or supply planning parameters. Used for replenishment timing calculations, safety stock parameter setting, and supply planning optimization.']
  moq_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ordered_quantity meets or exceeds the Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) requirement for this material-supplier combination. True = compliant; False = below MOQ threshold, requiring buyer review or rounding up before release.']
  moq_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) applicable to this replenishment order as defined in the purchasing info record or supply planning parameters. Ensures ordered_quantity meets supplier or logistics minimum thresholds. Critical for procurement compliance and cost optimization.']
  mrp_controller varchar [note: 'The MRP controller code or planner identifier responsible for managing the replenishment of this material at the source plant. Used for workload assignment, exception management, and accountability tracking in supply planning operations.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, buyer comments, exception explanations, or special handling instructions associated with this replenishment order. Used by supply planners and procurement teams to document non-standard situations, escalations, or supplier communications.']
  order_date date [note: 'The business date on which the replenishment order was created or generated by the MRP/supply planning system. Represents the principal real-world event time for this transaction. Used for aging analysis, lead time calculation, and OTIF measurement baseline. Maps to BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the replenishment order as assigned by the ERP or supply planning system (e.g., SAP purchase order number, stock transport order number, or MRP planned order number). Used for cross-system traceability and communication with suppliers and logistics partners. Maps to BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the replenishment order. Tracks progression from MRP-generated planned order through firming, release to warehouse/supplier, in-transit movement, and final goods receipt. Cancelled indicates the order was voided before fulfillment. Maps to LIFECYCLE_STATUS category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the replenishment order by its procurement mechanism. Stock Transfer Order (STO) moves inventory between internal plants or DCs. Purchase Requisition is a request to procure from an external supplier. Planned Order is an MRP-generated supply proposal. Purchase Order is a confirmed external procurement. Intercompany Transfer moves goods between legal entities. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: stock_transfer_order|purchase_requisition|planned_order|purchase_order|intercompany_transfer — promote to reference product]']
  ordered_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The quantity of material requested in the replenishment order, expressed in the base unit of measure (e.g., cases, pallets, kg). This is the gross supply proposal quantity generated by MRP or supply planning before any MOQ rounding or adjustment.']
  planned_goods_issue_date date [note: 'The planned date on which goods are to be issued from the source location (outbound shipment departure). Used for outbound logistics scheduling, warehouse pick/pack planning, and lead time calculation between goods issue and goods receipt.']
  planning_run_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the MRP or supply planning run that generated this replenishment order. Enables traceability of supply proposals back to the specific planning cycle, demand signal, and parameter set used. Supports audit of planning decisions and scenario comparison.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the replenishment order to govern fulfillment sequencing when supply is constrained. Critical = immediate action required (e.g., stockout risk); High = elevated urgency; Medium = standard planning cycle; Low = opportunistic or forward-buy.']
  quality_hold boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this replenishment order or the associated batch is subject to a quality hold that prevents goods receipt or putaway at the destination. True = quality hold active; False = no hold. Integrates with QM module and HACCP/GMP compliance processes.']
  received_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The actual quantity of material received at the destination location upon goods receipt. Used to calculate fill rate, identify short shipments, and trigger OTIF performance measurement against the confirmed quantity.']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'The date by which the replenishment order is required to arrive at the destination location to meet demand or safety stock targets. Drives supplier scheduling, transportation booking, and OTIF measurement. Set by supply planning based on demand signals and inventory parameters.']
  rotation_policy varchar [note: 'The inventory rotation policy applicable to this replenishment order. FIFO (First In First Out) = oldest stock consumed first; FEFO (First Expired First Out) = stock with earliest expiry date consumed first (critical for perishable food and beverage products); LIFO (Last In First Out) = rarely used in food industry.']
  safety_stock_trigger boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this replenishment order was triggered by a safety stock breach (projected inventory falling below safety stock level) rather than a standard demand-driven replenishment signal. True = safety stock replenishment; False = demand-driven or scheduled replenishment.']
  shelf_life_days_remaining varchar [note: 'The number of days of remaining shelf life on the material at the time of order creation, based on the production date and expiry date of the batch being replenished. Critical for FEFO compliance, quality assurance, and ensuring product arrives at destination with sufficient remaining shelf life to meet customer requirements.']
  total_order_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total monetary value of the replenishment order calculated as ordered_quantity multiplied by unit_cost. Used for procurement spend analysis, inventory investment tracking, and financial planning. Expressed in the order currency. Classified as confidential.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'The mode of transportation used to move the replenishment order from source to destination location. Drives freight cost estimation, lead time calculation, and carbon footprint reporting for sustainability compliance.']
  unit_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'The standard or purchase cost per unit of measure for the material in this replenishment order. Used to calculate total order value, Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) impact, and inventory valuation. Classified as confidential as it reflects procurement pricing strategy.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The base unit of measure in which ordered, confirmed, and received quantities are expressed (e.g., CS=Cases, EA=Each, KG=Kilograms, LB=Pounds, PAL=Pallets, L=Liters, GAL=Gallons). Aligns with the material master UOM configuration in SAP. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CS|EA|KG|LB|PAL|L|GAL — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this replenishment order record was last modified in the data platform. Tracks changes to order quantity, status, dates, or routing. Used for incremental data processing and audit compliance. Maps to RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.']

  Note: 'Planned or released replenishment order generated by MRP or supply planning to move finished goods or raw materials between plants, DCs, and 3PL locations. Captures order type (stock transfer, purchase requisition, planned order), source and destination locations, requested quantity, MOQ compliance, priority, and order status lifecycle (planned → firmed → released → received). Foundation for inbound shipment creation and OTIF measurement.'
}

Table supply.safety_stock_policy {
  safety_stock_policy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the safety stock policy record. Primary key for the safety_stock_policy data product in the supply domain.']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Safety‑stock policies are often set per brand to mitigate demand spikes generated by brand‑specific marketing promotions.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Track safety‑stock holding costs against the cost center responsible for inventory.']
  network_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the DC (Distribution Center) or warehouse location where this safety stock policy applies. Supports multi-node DC network and 3PL partner locations.']
  primary_safety_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supply planner or demand planner responsible for owning and maintaining this safety stock policy. Supports accountability in the IBP/S&OP cycle and manual override workflows.']
  product_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.product_registration. Business justification: Safety‑stock calculations must respect product‑specific regulatory limits (e.g., maximum allowable stock for allergen‑controlled items).']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) governed by this safety stock policy. Links to the product master to identify the specific finished good or semi-finished item.']
  spec_quality_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.specification. Business justification: Safety‑stock calculations depend on product specs (shelf‑life, storage) defined in SPECIFICATION; required for inventory planning reports.']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Safety‑stock policy execution needs the specific stock position to apply reorder points and safety‑stock quantities per location.']
  abc_classification varchar [note: 'ABC classification of the SKU based on demand volume or revenue contribution. \'A\' items are high-value/high-velocity requiring tighter safety stock management; \'C\' items are low-velocity with broader buffers. Used to prioritize inventory management effort.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date on which this safety stock policy was formally approved by the designated supply chain authority. Part of the S&OP governance and change management audit trail.']
  calculation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to derive the safety stock quantity. \'statistical\' uses demand variability and service level targets; \'days_of_supply\' uses a fixed days-of-supply multiplier; \'manual\' is planner-set; \'min_max\' uses min/max replenishment bands; \'demand_driven\' uses demand sensing signals from SAP IBP.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the business reason for the most recent change to this safety stock policy (e.g., \'Seasonal uplift for Q4 holiday demand\', \'Supplier lead time increase\', \'Post-launch NPD recalibration\'). Required for audit and S&OP governance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this safety stock policy record was first created in the system. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.']
  demand_horizon_days varchar [note: 'Number of forward-looking days of demand used in the safety stock calculation. Defines the planning horizon over which demand variability is measured for statistical safety stock methods.']
  demand_variability_factor decimal(10,6) [note: 'Standard deviation or coefficient of variation of demand used in statistical safety stock calculations. Derived from historical demand sensing data in SAP IBP. Higher values result in larger safety stock buffers.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this safety stock policy becomes binding and governs replenishment decisions for the SKU-location combination. Aligns with IBP/S&OP planning cycle activation.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which this safety stock policy ceases to be effective. Null indicates an open-ended policy with no planned expiration. Used to manage seasonal or promotional policy windows.']
  ibp_policy_code varchar [note: 'Source system identifier for this safety stock policy record in SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning). Used for cross-system traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver layer and the SAP IBP operational system.']
  is_new_product boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this safety stock policy applies to a new product introduction (NPD/NPI). New products typically use manual or days-of-supply methods due to absence of historical demand data. Triggers enhanced monitoring during launch phase.']
  is_promotional boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this policy includes a promotional uplift buffer to support planned trade promotions (TPM/TPO events). When True, additional safety stock is pre-positioned at the DC ahead of promotional periods.']
  is_seasonal boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this safety stock policy incorporates seasonal demand adjustments. When True, the safety stock quantity and days of supply parameters are adjusted based on seasonal demand patterns from the demand planning system.']
  jda_policy_code varchar [note: 'Source system identifier for this safety stock policy record in JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning. Populated when JDA is the system of record for replenishment planning at the DC network level.']
  last_reviewed_date date [note: 'Date on which this safety stock policy was last reviewed by the supply planning team. Supports periodic review cadence governance within the IBP/S&OP cycle. Policies not reviewed within the review cycle may be flagged for recalibration.']
  lead_time_days decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average replenishment lead time in days from order placement to receipt at the DC location. Used in reorder point and statistical safety stock calculations. Sourced from supplier SLA agreements.']
  lead_time_variability_days decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard deviation of replenishment lead time in days. Used in statistical safety stock calculations to account for supply uncertainty. Higher variability increases the required safety stock buffer.']
  max_stock_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Maximum allowable on-hand inventory quantity for the SKU at the location. Defines the upper bound of the min/max replenishment band. Replenishment orders are sized to bring inventory up to this level.']
  min_stock_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Minimum allowable on-hand inventory quantity for the SKU at the location. Defines the lower bound of the min/max replenishment band. Replenishment is triggered when inventory falls to or below this level.']
  moq decimal(18,4) [note: 'Minimum Order Quantity constraint applied to replenishment orders for this SKU-location policy. Sourced from supplier agreements or manufacturing run minimums. Replenishment orders cannot be placed below this quantity.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic review of this safety stock policy. Drives the supply planning review calendar and ensures policies remain calibrated to current demand and supply conditions.']
  planning_segment varchar [note: 'Supply chain planning segment classification for the SKU-location combination. Determines the applicable replenishment and safety stock strategy. \'make_to_stock\' is most common for CPG/food and beverage finished goods.']
  policy_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this safety stock policy record across systems (e.g., SAP IBP, JDA/Blue Yonder). Used for cross-system reconciliation and EDI references.']
  policy_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name describing the safety stock policy, typically referencing the SKU, location, or planning segment it governs (e.g., \'RTD Beverage - DC East - Statistical SS\').']
  policy_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the safety stock policy. \'active\' means the policy is live and governing replenishment; \'draft\' means under configuration; \'pending_review\' means awaiting S&OP approval; \'suspended\' means temporarily disabled.']
  policy_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the safety stock policy record, incremented each time the policy parameters are updated. Supports policy change history tracking and audit trail requirements within the supply planning governance framework.']
  remaining_shelf_life_min_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum acceptable remaining shelf life percentage at time of receipt or shipment from the DC. Ensures safety stock inventory meets customer and retailer freshness requirements. Expressed as a percentage of total shelf life (e.g., 66.67 means at least 2/3 of shelf life must remain).']
  reorder_point_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Inventory level at which a replenishment order is triggered for the SKU at the specified location. Calculated as safety stock plus expected demand during replenishment lead time.']
  replenishment_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Standard replenishment order quantity triggered when the reorder point is reached. May be constrained by MOQ (Minimum Order Quantity) or pallet/layer rounding rules from the supplier or DC network.']
  review_cycle_days varchar [note: 'Frequency in days at which inventory position is reviewed and replenishment decisions are made. Relevant for periodic review systems. Impacts the safety stock calculation for periodic review models.']
  rotation_policy varchar [note: 'Inventory rotation rule applied at the DC location for this SKU. FIFO (First In First Out) is standard for ambient products; FEFO (First Expired First Out) is mandatory for perishable, chilled, and frozen food and beverage products to minimize waste and ensure food safety compliance.']
  safety_days_of_supply decimal(10,2) [note: 'Safety stock expressed as number of days of forward demand coverage. Used when calculation_method is \'days_of_supply\'. Provides a demand-relative buffer measure aligned with FIFO/FEFO rotation policies.']
  safety_factor decimal(10,6) [note: 'Statistical safety factor (z-score) corresponding to the target service level. Applied to demand and lead time variability to compute the safety stock quantity in statistical calculation methods. Derived from the normal distribution at the specified service level target.']
  safety_stock_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'The calculated or manually set safety stock quantity (in base unit of measure) that must be maintained as a buffer to protect against demand variability and supply uncertainty. Principal quantitative fact of this policy record.']
  service_level_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target service level (cycle service level or fill rate) expressed as a percentage (e.g., 98.5) used in statistical safety stock calculations. Drives the safety factor (z-score) applied to demand variability. Directly impacts OTIF performance.']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Total shelf life of the SKU in days from manufacture date. Used in FEFO rotation policy enforcement and safety stock sizing to ensure buffer inventory does not expire before consumption. Critical for perishable food and beverage products.']
  uom_code varchar [note: 'Base unit of measure in which safety stock quantities, reorder points, and replenishment quantities are expressed (e.g., CS for case, EA for each, KG for kilogram). Aligns with SAP S/4HANA material master UOM.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this safety stock policy record was most recently modified. Used to detect changes during incremental Silver layer processing and to support S&OP change management audit trails.']
  xyz_classification varchar [note: 'XYZ classification of the SKU based on demand variability. \'X\' items have stable, predictable demand; \'Y\' items have moderate variability; \'Z\' items have highly erratic demand. Combined with ABC classification to set differentiated safety stock policies.']

  Note: 'Inventory buffer policy defining safety stock levels and reorder parameters per SKU per location across the DC network. Captures safety stock quantity, safety days of supply, reorder point, maximum stock level, min/max replenishment bands, and calculation method (statistical, manual, days-of-supply). Governs when replenishment is triggered and how much buffer inventory is maintained to protect against demand variability and supply uncertainty.'
}

Table supply.inbound_shipment {
  inbound_shipment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the inbound shipment record in the lakehouse Silver layer. Primary key. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — represents a discrete inbound logistics event with a full lifecycle from ASN through goods receipt and putaway.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the transportation carrier or 3PL partner responsible for the inbound shipment leg. Used for carrier performance tracking, OTIF (On Time In Full) measurement, and freight cost allocation.']
  certificate_of_analysis_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.certificate_of_analysis. Business justification: Each inbound shipment requires a linked COA to satisfy FDA/GFSI audit and product release decisions.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Allocate inbound logistics expenses to the responsible cost center for expense tracking.']
  establishment_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.establishment_registration. Business justification: Inbound shipments must be tied to the registered facility of the supplier for FDA/USDA import compliance tracking.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Post freight and handling charges of inbound shipments to the designated GL account.']
  inspection_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_lot. Business justification: INBOUND_SHIPMENT quality inspection results must be linked to INSPECTION_LOT for compliance release and traceability reports.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the receiving plant or Distribution Center (DC) in the supply network where the inbound shipment is destined. Used for network design analytics and inventory update triggering.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Advanced Shipping Notice (ASN) must be tied to the originating purchase order for traceability and compliance reporting.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material or Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) being received. Links to the product/material master for ingredient, packaging, or finished goods identification. Supports RESOURCE_REFERENCE for downstream line-level analytics.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier, co-packer, or inter-company transfer source party in the master data. Supports PARTY_REFERENCE category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role. Used for carrier performance tracking and supplier compliance reporting via TraceGains.']
  actual_arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the carrier physically arrived at the receiving dock or gate. BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category. Used for OTIF (On Time In Full) calculation, carrier performance KPIs, and dock turnaround time analysis.']
  asn_date date [note: 'Date the Advance Ship Notice (ASN) was transmitted by the supplier. BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category (day-level precision for planning). Used for lead time analysis and supplier compliance measurement against EDI 856 transmission SLAs.']
  asn_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the Advance Ship Notice (ASN) transmitted by the supplier or co-packer prior to physical shipment. Maps to SAP MM inbound delivery document number (VL31N/DESADV). Serves as the primary cross-reference between supplier systems and internal logistics. BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category.']
  batch_lot_number varchar [note: 'Supplier-assigned or internally-generated batch or lot number assigned to the received material at goods receipt. Critical for FIFO/FEFO rotation policy enforcement, allergen matrix traceability, HACCP compliance, and FSMA lot-level traceability requirements.']
  certificate_of_analysis_ref varchar [note: 'Reference number or document identifier for the supplier-provided Certificate of Analysis (CoA) accompanying the shipment. Validates that the received material meets specification. Stored in Veeva Vault or TraceGains for regulatory audit readiness.']
  container_number varchar [note: 'ISO 6346 shipping container identification number for ocean or intermodal freight. Used for port customs clearance, container tracking, and import compliance documentation.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country where the material was manufactured or processed. Required for customs declarations, USDA country-of-origin labeling (COOL) compliance, and import duty calculation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the inbound shipment record was first created in the source system (SAP MM or EDI inbound processing). RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and SLA compliance tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the invoice_value (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency AP processing and financial reporting. MONETARY_TRIPLET component.']
  delivery_note_number varchar [note: 'Supplier-issued delivery note or packing slip number accompanying the physical shipment. Used for goods receipt posting in SAP MM (MIGO) and for dispute resolution with suppliers.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration or best-before date of the received batch/lot. Drives FEFO (First Expired First Out) inventory rotation, shelf life compliance, and quality hold decisions. Mandatory for food safety regulatory compliance under FDA and FSMA.']
  freight_terms varchar [note: 'Freight payment terms indicating who bears the transportation cost for the inbound shipment. Prepaid means supplier pays; collect means Food Beverage pays at destination. Used for freight cost allocation and AP processing.']
  goods_receipt_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the goods receipt was formally posted in SAP MM (MIGO transaction), triggering inventory update and accounts payable (AP) posting. Critical for FIFO/FEFO lot assignment and financial period close.']
  gross_weight_kg decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total gross weight of the inbound shipment in kilograms, including packaging and pallets. Used for freight cost calculation, dock capacity planning, and customs documentation. QUANTITATIVE_RESULT category.']
  incoterms_code varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) code defining the transfer of risk and responsibility between supplier and Food Beverage for the shipment. Determines freight cost ownership and insurance liability. Used in AP invoice validation and landed cost calculation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Supplier-issued commercial invoice number associated with the inbound shipment. Used for three-way match (PO, GR, Invoice) in accounts payable (AP) processing and financial period close.']
  invoice_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Commercial invoice value of the inbound shipment in the transaction currency. Used for AP three-way match, landed cost calculation, and COGS (Cost of Goods Sold) valuation. MONETARY_TRIPLET component (gross amount).']
  is_hazmat boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the inbound shipment contains hazardous materials (e.g., cleaning chemicals, food-grade solvents) requiring special handling, labeling, and regulatory compliance under DOT/IATA regulations.']
  is_temperature_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the inbound shipment requires temperature-controlled (cold chain) transportation and storage conditions (e.g., refrigerated or frozen). Triggers cold chain compliance checks and specialized putaway routing in WMS.']
  manufacture_date date [note: 'Date the material was manufactured by the supplier or co-packer. Used in conjunction with expiry_date for FEFO (First Expired First Out) rotation policy enforcement and shelf life management.']
  otif_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the inbound shipment was delivered On Time and In Full (OTIF) against the scheduled_arrival_date and quantity_shipped. True = OTIF achieved; False = OTIF missed. Used for supplier and carrier performance scorecards.']
  pallet_count varchar [note: 'Number of pallets included in the inbound shipment. Used for dock scheduling, labor resource planning, and warehouse space allocation at the receiving DC or plant.']
  pro_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-assigned Progressive (PRO) number or Bill of Lading (BOL) number used to track the shipment in transit. Enables real-time inbound visibility and carrier performance tracking. Used for freight claims and dispute resolution.']
  putaway_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the received goods were physically placed into the designated storage location within the plant or DC. Marks completion of the inbound logistics lifecycle. Used for dock-to-stock cycle time measurement.']
  quality_inspection_status varchar [note: 'Status of the quality inspection lot associated with the goods receipt in SAP QM. Indicates whether the received batch has passed, failed, or is pending quality inspection before release to unrestricted stock. Supports HACCP, SQF, and GFSI compliance.']
  quantity_received decimal(18,4) [note: 'Actual quantity of the material physically received and posted in SAP MM goods receipt (MIGO). May differ from quantity_shipped due to transit damage, short shipment, or over-delivery. Triggers inventory update upon posting.']
  quantity_shipped decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total quantity of the material dispatched by the supplier as declared in the ASN. Expressed in the base unit of measure (UoM) of the material master. Used as the baseline for receipt variance calculation and supplier compliance.']
  quantity_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure (UoM) for quantity_shipped and quantity_received fields (e.g., KG, LB, CS, EA, L, GAL). Aligns with SAP MM material master base unit of measure. Required for accurate inventory valuation and supply planning.']
  quantity_variance decimal(18,4) [note: 'Difference between quantity_shipped and quantity_received (shipped minus received). Positive value indicates short delivery; negative value indicates over-delivery. Used for receipt-vs-order reconciliation, supplier dispute management, and AP invoice matching.']
  receipt_variance_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining the cause of any quantity variance between quantity_shipped and quantity_received. Used for supplier dispute management, root cause analysis, and AP invoice reconciliation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: short_shipment|over_delivery|transit_damage|quality_rejection|counting_error|no_variance — promote to reference product if additional codes required]']
  scheduled_arrival_date date [note: 'Expected date of physical arrival at the receiving plant or DC as communicated in the ASN or purchase order. Used for inbound dock scheduling, labor planning, and OTIF (On Time In Full) baseline measurement.']
  shipment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the inbound shipment. Tracks progression from in-transit through physical receipt and putaway. LIFECYCLE_STATUS category. Values: in_transit (ASN issued, goods en route), arrived (carrier confirmed delivery at gate), receiving (goods receipt in progress), received (goods receipt posted in SAP MM), putaway (goods stored in final storage location), cancelled (shipment cancelled prior to receipt).']
  shipment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the inbound shipment origin type. Distinguishes between direct supplier deliveries, co-packing receipts, inter-company stock transfers, 3PL-managed transfers, and toll manufacturing returns. Supports supply chain segmentation and cost allocation.']
  storage_location_code varchar [note: 'SAP storage location code within the receiving plant or DC where the goods are physically putaway after receipt. Supports FIFO/FEFO rotation policy enforcement and WMS integration.']
  temperature_range varchar [note: 'Required temperature range classification for the inbound shipment during transport and storage. Ambient (15-25°C), Chilled (0-8°C), Frozen (-18°C or below), Ultra-Frozen (-40°C or below). Used for cold chain compliance and carrier selection.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Mode of transportation used for the inbound shipment leg. Used for freight cost benchmarking, carbon footprint/sustainability reporting, and carrier performance segmentation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the inbound shipment record in the source system. RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category. Supports change data capture (CDC) in the lakehouse Silver layer and audit trail requirements.']

  Note: 'Inbound logistics record capturing the full lifecycle of materials moving from suppliers, co-packers, or inter-company transfers to plants and DCs — from advance ship notice (ASN) through physical goods receipt and putaway. Captures carrier, expected and actual arrival dates, quantity shipped, quantity received, receipt posting date, batch/lot assignment (FIFO/FEFO), purchase order reference, delivery note, storage location, valuation details, and receipt variances. Triggers inventory updates upon confirmed receipt. Supports inbound visibility, carrier performance tracking, receipt-vs-order reconciliation, and accounts payable posting.'
}

Table supply.demand_balance {
  demand_balance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each point-in-time supply-demand balance record produced during an integrated business planning (IBP) balancing run. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — represents a discrete planning event with a clear lifecycle at SKU/location/week granularity.']
  center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Distribution Center (DC) or planning location node at which the supply-demand balance is evaluated. Supports multi-node network design and 3PL partner visibility.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Report supply‑demand balance metrics per cost center for internal performance dashboards.']
  plan_version_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific planning version (e.g., baseline, consensus, simulation) under which this balance record was generated. Enables version comparison and what-if scenario analysis.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) for which the supply-demand balance is calculated. The SKU is the primary product planning unit in the food and beverage supply chain.']
  sop_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Sales and Operations Planning (S&OP) / Integrated Business Planning (IBP) cycle during which this balance record was produced. Links the balance snapshot to the formal monthly S&OP governance process.']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Supply‑Demand Balance report requires linking each balance record to the actual stock position to compute days‑of‑supply and safety‑stock calculations.']
  balance_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this supply-demand balance record, formatted as SDB-YYYY-WW-NNNNNN. Used by supply planners to reference specific balance snapshots in workbench communications and exception resolution workflows.']
  balance_run_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the IBP/S&OP planning run that generated this supply-demand balance record. Links the balance snapshot to its originating planning execution cycle.']
  balance_run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The principal real-world event timestamp recording when the IBP/S&OP balancing run that produced this record was executed. Used to sequence snapshots and identify the most current balance position.']
  balance_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the supply-demand balance record. \'open\' indicates an active unresolved balance; \'shortage\' and \'excess\' indicate exception states requiring planner action; \'balanced\' indicates supply meets demand within tolerance; \'exception\' indicates a system-flagged anomaly; \'closed\' indicates the planning period has passed.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Sales channel for which the demand component of this balance record applies. Enables channel-specific supply-demand balancing, particularly for Direct Store Delivery (DSD) versus warehouse-distributed channels in food and beverage.']
  constrained_supply_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total projected supply quantity available for the SKU at the planning location for the planning bucket after applying capacity, material, and logistics constraints. Represents the supply plan output from SAP IBP or JDA supply planning.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording when this supply-demand balance record was first persisted in the Silver layer lakehouse. Supports data lineage, SLA tracking, and audit trail requirements.']
  days_of_supply decimal(10,2) [note: 'Number of days the projected available balance quantity can cover forward demand at the current demand rate. A key supply health metric used to identify shortage risk and excess inventory positions. Calculated as PAB divided by average daily demand.']
  exception_priority varchar [note: 'Planner-assigned or system-calculated priority level for resolving the supply-demand imbalance. Used to sequence workbench exception resolution activities. \'critical\' indicates immediate action required; \'none\' indicates no exception.']
  exception_resolution_status varchar [note: 'Current resolution status of any supply-demand exception associated with this balance record. Tracks the planner workflow from initial exception identification through resolution or escalation.']
  fill_rate_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Projected fill rate percentage for the planning bucket, calculated as constrained supply divided by unconstrained demand. A key On Time In Full (OTIF) performance indicator used to assess service level risk and customer order fulfillment capability.']
  firm_orders_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of confirmed, firm customer orders or distribution orders already committed for the SKU at the planning location within the planning bucket. Represents the hard demand component within the planning time fence.']
  is_excess_exception boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this supply-demand balance record has been flagged as an excess inventory exception, meaning projected available balance exceeds the maximum inventory target. Drives disposition and markdown decisions.']
  is_frozen_period boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the planning bucket falls within the frozen planning horizon, during which supply plan changes are restricted. Frozen periods reflect firm manufacturing schedules and committed supplier orders that cannot be altered without significant cost.']
  is_shortage_exception boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this supply-demand balance record has been flagged as a shortage exception, meaning projected available balance falls below the safety stock threshold. Drives exception-based workbench prioritization for supply planners.']
  moq decimal(18,4) [note: 'Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) constraint applicable to the SKU at the planning location. Replenishment signals must meet or exceed this threshold. Sourced from supplier agreements or manufacturing run parameters.']
  opening_inventory_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Projected on-hand inventory quantity at the start of the planning bucket for the SKU at the planning location. Serves as the beginning balance for the supply-demand calculation.']
  planned_receipts_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total quantity of planned inbound supply receipts (purchase orders, production orders, transfer orders) scheduled to arrive at the planning location during the planning bucket. Feeds into the constrained supply calculation.']
  planner_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by the supply planner documenting exception resolution actions, assumptions, escalation rationale, or override justifications for this supply-demand balance record. Supports audit trail and knowledge transfer.']
  planning_bucket_end_date date [note: 'The last calendar date of the weekly planning bucket for which this supply-demand balance record applies. Together with planning_bucket_start_date defines the exact planning week.']
  planning_bucket_start_date date [note: 'The first calendar date of the weekly planning bucket (time fence) for which this supply-demand balance record applies. Defines the start of the SKU/location/week granularity period.']
  planning_horizon_weeks varchar [note: 'Number of weeks forward from the balance run date that this supply-demand balance record covers within the planning horizon. Contextualizes the balance record within the short-term (frozen), mid-term, or long-term planning horizon.']
  projected_available_balance_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Projected Available Balance (PAB) quantity at the end of the planning bucket, calculated as opening inventory plus constrained supply minus unconstrained demand. The primary output metric used by supply planners to assess inventory health and identify imbalances.']
  qty_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure applied to all quantity fields in this supply-demand balance record (e.g., CS=Cases, EA=Each, LB=Pounds, KG=Kilograms). Ensures consistent interpretation of supply and demand quantities across SKUs. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CS|EA|LB|KG|OZ|GAL|L|MT — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  remaining_shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Estimated remaining shelf life in days for the projected on-hand inventory at the planning location as of the planning bucket start date. Used to identify at-risk inventory that may expire before it can be consumed, supporting FEFO-based exception management.']
  reorder_point_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Inventory level at which a replenishment order should be triggered for the SKU at the planning location. Used in conjunction with safety stock and lead time parameters to drive replenishment signals.']
  replenishment_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity recommended by the planning system to be replenished (via production, procurement, or transfer) to resolve the supply-demand gap. Subject to Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) and lot-size constraints.']
  replenishment_signal varchar [note: 'Type of replenishment signal generated by the planning system to address a supply shortfall for this SKU/location/week. Indicates whether the system recommends a planned production order, purchase requisition, stock transfer order, or manual planner intervention.']
  rotation_policy varchar [note: 'Inventory rotation policy applied to this SKU at the planning location. FIFO (First In First Out) and FEFO (First Expired First Out) are standard food and beverage policies to minimize waste and ensure product freshness compliance. Drives warehouse management and replenishment sequencing.']
  safety_stock_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Target safety stock quantity for the SKU at the planning location for the planning bucket, as defined in the inventory optimization parameters. Used to evaluate whether the projected available balance meets the minimum buffer requirement.']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Total shelf life in days for the SKU as defined in the product specification. Used in FEFO rotation policy calculations and to assess whether projected available balance will expire before consumption, triggering waste risk flags.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this supply-demand balance record originated. Enables data lineage tracing and supports multi-system integration scenarios where both SAP IBP and JDA/Blue Yonder may contribute planning data.']
  supply_demand_gap_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Net quantity difference between constrained supply and unconstrained demand for the planning bucket. A positive value indicates excess supply; a negative value indicates a shortage. The primary exception-driving metric for supply planner workbench prioritization.']
  supply_plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the supply strategy for this SKU at the planning location. Determines how supply is sourced: make-to-stock (MTS) from own manufacturing, make-to-order (MTO), co-packing (Co-Pack), toll manufacturing, or buy-resell. Influences replenishment lead times and MOQ parameters.']
  target_service_level_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Target customer service level percentage (Service Level Agreement) for the SKU at the planning location, used as the benchmark against which fill_rate_pct is evaluated. Drives safety stock parameter calculations in SAP IBP inventory optimization.']
  unconstrained_demand_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total projected demand quantity for the SKU at the planning location for the planning bucket, before applying any supply constraints. Represents the market-facing demand signal from the consensus forecast or demand sensing output in SAP IBP.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording when this supply-demand balance record was last modified, such as after a planner override, exception resolution, or re-balancing run. Supports change tracking and audit compliance.']

  Note: 'Point-in-time supply-demand balance record produced during integrated planning balancing runs. Captures projected inventory position, unconstrained demand, constrained supply, projected available balance (PAB), days of supply, and exception flags (shortage, excess) at SKU/location/week granularity. Used by supply planners to identify and resolve imbalances, prioritize exception resolution, and drive daily workbench decisions.'
}

Table supply.ibp_scenario {
  ibp_scenario_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the IBP planning scenario or simulation version. Primary key for this entity. Entity role: MASTER_RESOURCE — represents a planning scenario artifact with its own identity and lifecycle within the SAP IBP S&OP process.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supply chain planner or analyst who created or owns this scenario within the IBP planning process.']
  sop_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the S&OP/IBP planning cycle this scenario belongs to, linking the scenario to its parent consensus planning cycle.']
  source_scenario_ibp_scenario_id varchar [note: 'Native scenario identifier from the originating system of record (e.g., SAP IBP scenario key or JDA planning version ID). Enables reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and the upstream planning system.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which this scenario was formally approved within the S&OP/IBP governance process. Enables tracking of approval cycle times and scenario readiness for consensus planning meetings.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or user ID of the supply chain leader or S&OP process owner who approved this scenario for use in consensus planning. Provides audit trail for scenario governance.']
