  co_packing_included_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether co-packing (contract packaging) capacity is included in the supply assumptions for this scenario. True = co-packing capacity is modeled as an available supply source, relevant for NPD launches and capacity overflow scenarios.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this scenario record was first created in the system. Provides audit trail for scenario lifecycle management and S&OP cycle governance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code used for all monetary values in this scenario (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Ensures consistent financial comparisons across multi-currency global F&B operations.']
  dc_count varchar [note: 'Number of Distribution Centers (DCs) included in this scenario\'s network design. Applicable primarily to network_design scenario type. Enables comparison of network configurations with different DC footprints.']
  dc_network_throughput_capacity decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total assumed throughput capacity of the DC network in this scenario, expressed in dc_network_throughput_uom. Used in network design scenarios to evaluate whether the proposed DC configuration can handle projected demand volumes.']
  dc_network_throughput_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure used to express DC network throughput assumptions in this scenario (e.g., cases, pallets, lbs). Standardizes capacity comparisons across network design scenario variants.']
  demand_assumption varchar [note: 'Source or method used to derive the demand input for this scenario. Determines whether the scenario uses the statistical baseline, consensus-adjusted forecast, customer committed orders, external market intelligence, or a custom demand override.']
  demand_uplift_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Percentage adjustment applied to the base demand forecast for this scenario. Positive values represent upside demand assumptions (e.g., +15% for promotional event); negative values represent downside risk (e.g., -10% for demand softening). Used in upside/downside scenario modeling.']
  freight_cost_model varchar [note: 'Freight cost modeling approach applied in this scenario for inbound and outbound logistics cost estimation. Actual_rates uses current carrier contracts; spot_market uses prevailing spot rates; blended uses a weighted mix; custom uses scenario-specific rate assumptions.']
  inventory_policy varchar [note: 'Inventory rotation policy applied within this scenario for perishable and shelf-life-sensitive F&B products. FIFO (First In First Out) and FEFO (First Expired First Out) are standard in food manufacturing to minimize waste and ensure food safety compliance.']
  is_consensus_plan boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this scenario has been designated as the official consensus plan for the S&OP cycle. Only one scenario per S&OP cycle should be flagged True. Drives downstream supply execution and financial commitment.']
  is_locked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this scenario is locked from further modification. True = scenario is frozen for audit and comparison purposes; no further edits are permitted. Supports version-controlled planning governance.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this scenario record was most recently updated. Enables change detection, incremental data loading in the Databricks lakehouse silver layer, and audit trail for scenario revisions.']
  moq_override_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this scenario overrides standard MOQ (Minimum Order Quantity) constraints for procurement and production orders. True = MOQ constraints relaxed for scenario modeling purposes (e.g., testing unconstrained supply response).']
  network_design_locations varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or descriptive text of DC locations included in this network design scenario (e.g., \'Chicago IL, Dallas TX, Atlanta GA\'). Captures the geographic footprint assumption for network_design scenario type. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promote to reference product if structured location data is required]']
  oee_assumption_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Assumed OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) percentage for manufacturing lines within this scenario. Drives production capacity calculations and throughput assumptions. Lower OEE assumptions model downside capacity risk; higher values model optimized production performance.']
  planner_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by the supply chain planner documenting key assumptions, data quality caveats, or analytical observations for this scenario. Supports S&OP meeting preparation and scenario documentation.']
  planning_bucket varchar [note: 'Time granularity of the planning periods within this scenario. Weekly buckets are used for near-term tactical planning; monthly for mid-range S&OP; quarterly for strategic horizon.']
  planning_horizon_end_date date [note: 'Last date of the planning horizon covered by this scenario. Together with planning_horizon_start_date, defines the full temporal scope of the scenario.']
  planning_horizon_months varchar [note: 'Total number of months covered by the planning horizon for this scenario. Typical IBP horizons range from 3 months (tactical) to 36 months (strategic network design).']
  planning_horizon_start_date date [note: 'First date of the planning horizon covered by this scenario. Defines the temporal scope of the supply-demand balancing analysis.']
  replenishment_method varchar [note: 'Replenishment signal generation method applied in this scenario. MRP (Material Requirements Planning) is driven by BOM explosion; kanban uses pull signals; reorder_point triggers at stock threshold; vendor_managed inventory (VMI) delegates to supplier; consignment uses supplier-owned stock.']
  safety_stock_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate safety stock parameters within this scenario. Statistical methods use demand variability and lead time; fixed_days uses a target days-of-supply; min_max uses floor/ceiling thresholds; dynamic adjusts based on demand sensing signals.']
  scenario_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the scenario\'s assumptions, business context, and analytical purpose. Captures the rationale for the what-if analysis or planning hypothesis being tested.']
  scenario_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the planning scenario (e.g., \'FY25 Q2 Baseline\', \'Hurricane Season Downside\', \'DC Network Consolidation v2\'). Used in S&OP presentations and IBP planning workbooks.']
  scenario_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the scenario, used in S&OP meeting decks, planning reports, and cross-functional communications. Format: SCN-{YYYY}-{NNNNNN}.']
  scenario_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the IBP scenario within the S&OP approval workflow. Draft indicates work-in-progress; in_review is under cross-functional review; approved is consensus-accepted; rejected was not accepted; archived is retired from active planning.']
  scenario_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the planning scenario by its analytical purpose within the IBP/S&OP process. Baseline represents the consensus plan; upside/downside represent demand or supply risk bounds; what-if tests specific assumptions; network_design evaluates DC network configuration changes.']
  scenario_version varchar [note: 'Numeric version counter for the scenario, incremented each time the scenario is revised or re-run. Enables version-controlled what-if analysis and comparison of scenario iterations within the same S&OP cycle.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this scenario was originated or imported. Enables data lineage tracking across the supply chain planning technology stack.']
  supply_constraint_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this scenario incorporates supply-side constraints such as raw material availability, manufacturing capacity limits, or MOQ restrictions. True = constrained supply plan; False = unconstrained demand-driven plan.']
  target_service_level_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target fill rate or case fill rate (%) assumed in this scenario for customer service level commitments. Drives safety stock calculations and replenishment signal generation. Typically expressed as OTIF (On Time In Full) or fill rate targets per SLA agreements.']
  three_pl_included_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether 3PL (Third-Party Logistics) partners are included in the distribution network assumptions for this scenario. True = 3PL capacity is modeled alongside owned DC network.']
  toll_manufacturing_included_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether toll manufacturing capacity is included in the supply assumptions for this scenario. True = toll manufacturing is modeled as an available production source, relevant for capacity expansion and cost-comparison scenarios.']
  total_landed_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated total landed cost for the supply network under this scenario, including manufacturing COGS, inbound freight, warehousing, and outbound distribution costs. Key financial output metric for network design and strategic scenario comparison. Expressed in scenario currency.']

  Note: 'Planning scenario or simulation version used during S&OP/IBP cycles for tactical and strategic what-if analysis. Captures scenario name, version, planning horizon, scenario type (baseline, upside, downside, what-if, network design), approval status, creating planner, and type-specific parameters. For network design scenarios: includes DC count, locations, throughput assumptions, freight cost model, service level targets, and total landed cost. Enables version-controlled what-if analysis, consensus planning, and strategic network optimization across the F&B supply chain.'
}

Table supply.sop_cycle {
  sop_cycle_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the S&OP/Integrated Business Planning (IBP) cycle record. Primary key. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — represents a discrete, recurring cross-functional planning event with a defined lifecycle (draft → in-progress → closed).']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the individual accountable for facilitating and driving the S&OP/IBP cycle to completion. Typically the Supply Chain Planning Director or S&OP Process Manager. Serves as the primary point of contact for cross-functional escalations.']
  attendee_count varchar [note: 'Total number of cross-functional participants who attended the S&OP/IBP cycle meetings (demand review, supply review, pre-S&OP, and executive review combined). Used as a process maturity indicator and governance compliance metric.']
  capacity_utilization_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aggregate manufacturing capacity utilization percentage (0–100) for the planning period as assessed during the supply review of this S&OP cycle. Derived from the constrained supply plan vs. available production capacity. Values above 85% typically trigger capacity escalation discussions.']
  consensus_decision_summary varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative summarizing the key cross-functional decisions, trade-offs, and commitments agreed upon during this S&OP/IBP cycle. Captures the rationale for scenario selection, supply gap resolutions, and any deviations from the financial operating plan.']
  consensus_demand_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the consensus demand volume approved in this S&OP cycle (e.g., CS = cases, EA = each, KG = kilograms). Ensures consistent interpretation of volume figures across demand, supply, and finance teams. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CS|EA|KG|LB|L|GAL|MT — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  consensus_demand_volume decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total consensus demand volume (in standard cases or agreed unit of measure) approved by cross-functional teams during this S&OP cycle. Represents the agreed-upon demand signal used to drive supply planning, procurement, and manufacturing scheduling for the planning period.']
  consensus_revenue_target decimal(18,2) [note: 'Financial revenue target (in reporting currency) aligned and approved during this S&OP/IBP cycle. Represents the commercial commitment reconciled between the demand plan and the financial operating plan. Used for EBITDA tracking and financial close alignment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this S&OP cycle record was first created in the system. Audit trail field supporting data lineage, compliance reporting, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary values in this S&OP cycle record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Ensures consistent financial reporting across multi-currency global operations.']
  cycle_close_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this S&OP/IBP cycle was formally closed and the approved consensus plan was locked for execution. Marks the end of the active planning window. Used for cycle duration KPI measurement and audit compliance.']
  cycle_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional commentary, risk flags, assumptions, or contextual notes recorded by the S&OP cycle owner or cross-functional team. May include references to external market events, supply disruptions, regulatory changes, or strategic initiatives impacting the planning cycle.']
  cycle_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the S&OP/IBP cycle, formatted as SOP-YYYY-MM-NNN (e.g., SOP-2024-10-001). Used in cross-functional communications, meeting agendas, and audit trails to uniquely reference a planning cycle. Maps to SAP IBP Planning Cycle ID.']
  cycle_open_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this S&OP/IBP cycle was formally opened and made active in the planning system. Serves as the principal business event timestamp marking the start of the cycle\'s active planning window. Used for SLA tracking and cycle duration analytics.']
  cycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the S&OP/IBP cycle. Drives workflow routing, access controls, and downstream planning system locks. Draft: cycle initiated but not yet active. In Progress: milestone reviews underway. Pending Approval: awaiting executive sign-off. Approved: consensus plan ratified. Closed: cycle archived. Cancelled: cycle voided.']
  cycle_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the planning cycle cadence and scope. Determines which cross-functional teams participate and which decisions are in scope. Monthly S&OP covers full supply-demand balancing; weekly demand review focuses on near-term demand sensing; executive IBP covers strategic financial reconciliation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: monthly_sop|weekly_demand_review|executive_ibp|supply_review|pre_sop|demand_review — promote to reference product]']
  demand_plan_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the demand plan (from SAP IBP or JDA/Blue Yonder) that was reviewed and approved during this S&OP cycle. Enables traceability between the S&OP cycle record and the specific demand plan version used as input to supply planning and financial reconciliation.']
  demand_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled or actual date of the Demand Review milestone within this S&OP cycle. During the demand review, commercial and demand planning teams align on the unconstrained consensus demand forecast, incorporating statistical forecasts, market intelligence, and promotional uplifts.']
  executive_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled or actual date of the Executive S&OP or IBP review meeting where senior leadership approves the consensus plan, resolves escalated supply-demand gaps, and aligns on financial commitments and strategic trade-offs.']
  financial_reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Status of the financial reconciliation between the consensus volume plan and the financial operating plan (budget/latest estimate) at the close of this S&OP cycle. Aligned: volume plan supports financial targets. Gap Identified: volume shortfall vs. financial plan. Escalated: gap referred to CFO/leadership. Resolved: gap closed via scenario or plan adjustment.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period (month number 1–13 depending on calendar) within the fiscal year to which this S&OP cycle belongs. Enables alignment of planning cycle outcomes with period-close financial reporting and EBITDA reconciliation.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which this S&OP cycle belongs, as defined in the corporate financial calendar (e.g., 2024). Used for financial reconciliation, budget alignment, and year-over-year planning comparisons.']
  inventory_target_days decimal(10,1) [note: 'Target days of supply (DOS) for finished goods inventory approved during this S&OP cycle. Reflects the inventory optimization policy balancing service level targets, working capital constraints, and shelf life (FEFO) requirements for the planning period.']
  is_locked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this S&OP cycle\'s consensus plan has been locked for execution (True), preventing further modifications to the approved demand and supply plan. Locking triggers downstream replenishment signals, procurement purchase orders, and manufacturing schedules.']
  new_product_items_count varchar [note: 'Number of new product development (NPD) SKUs included in the demand and supply plans reviewed during this S&OP cycle. Tracks the volume of innovation pipeline items requiring dedicated supply chain readiness assessment and launch planning.']
  open_action_item_count varchar [note: 'Number of open action items carried forward from this S&OP cycle at the time of cycle closure. Tracks cross-functional accountability and process discipline. A high count may indicate unresolved supply-demand gaps or escalation items requiring follow-up in the next cycle.']
  planning_horizon_months varchar [note: 'Number of months covered by the planning horizon reviewed in this S&OP cycle. Typically 18–24 months for strategic IBP cycles and 3–6 months for operational S&OP cycles in the Food and Beverage industry. Drives the time fence for supply-demand balancing decisions.']
  planning_period_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date of the planning horizon governed by this S&OP cycle. Together with planning_period_start_date, defines the full time fence for supply-demand balancing decisions made in this cycle.']
  planning_period_start_date date [note: 'First calendar date of the planning horizon governed by this S&OP cycle. Defines the start boundary for demand, supply, inventory, and financial plans reviewed and approved within this cycle. Typically the first day of the planning month or week.']
  pre_sop_date date [note: 'Scheduled or actual date of the Pre-S&OP (reconciliation) meeting where supply, demand, and finance teams resolve gaps between the demand plan and constrained supply plan before escalating unresolved issues to the executive S&OP review.']
  promotional_volume_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total consensus demand volume attributable to trade promotions and promotional uplifts in the planning period reviewed during this S&OP cycle. High promotional volume percentages increase demand variability and require additional safety stock and supply flexibility.']
  revenue_gap_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary gap (in reporting currency) between the consensus revenue plan approved in this S&OP cycle and the financial operating plan (budget or latest estimate). Positive values indicate revenue shortfall vs. financial plan. Key input for executive escalation and corrective action planning.']
  safety_stock_review_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether safety stock parameters were formally reviewed and updated during this S&OP cycle (True) or carried forward unchanged from the prior cycle (False). Safety stock reviews are triggered by significant changes in demand variability, lead times, or service level targets.']
  scenario_name varchar [note: 'Name or label of the supply-demand scenario approved by the executive team during this S&OP cycle (e.g., \'Base Case\', \'Upside Demand\', \'Supply Constrained\'). Identifies which planning scenario was selected as the consensus plan for execution.']
  scenario_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the approved planning scenario for this S&OP cycle. Base Case: most likely outcome. Upside: optimistic demand/supply scenario. Downside: pessimistic scenario. Supply Constrained: demand exceeds available supply. Risk Adjusted: incorporates identified supply chain risk mitigations.']
  service_level_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target customer service level (fill rate or OTIF percentage) approved for the planning period during this S&OP cycle. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 98.50 = 98.5%). Drives safety stock parameters and replenishment signal calibration across the DC network.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this S&OP cycle record was sourced or initiated. SAP_IBP: SAP Integrated Business Planning. JDA_BLUE_YONDER: JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning. ORACLE_ERP: Oracle Cloud ERP. MANUAL: manually entered outside a planning system.']
  supply_gap_volume decimal(18,3) [note: 'Volumetric gap (in consensus demand UOM) between the unconstrained consensus demand plan and the constrained supply plan identified during this S&OP cycle. Positive values indicate supply shortfall; negative values indicate supply surplus. Key input for executive escalation and scenario planning.']
  supply_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled or actual date of the Supply Review milestone within this S&OP cycle. Supply planning teams assess capacity constraints, raw material availability, MOQ compliance, and FIFO/FEFO inventory rotation to produce a constrained supply plan.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this S&OP cycle record was most recently modified. Supports incremental data loading in the Databricks Silver layer and audit trail requirements for planning governance.']

  Note: 'Sales and Operations Planning (S&OP) / Integrated Business Planning (IBP) cycle record governing the cadence of cross-functional supply-demand alignment. Captures planning period, cycle type (monthly S&OP, weekly demand review, executive IBP), cycle status, key milestone dates (demand review, supply review, pre-S&OP, executive review), consensus decisions, attendees, action items, and financial reconciliation outcomes. Drives the rhythm of planning activities, scenario approvals, and cross-functional accountability across demand, supply, finance, and commercial teams in the F&B organization.'
}

Table supply.network_node {
  network_node_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each physical node in the supply chain network. Primary key for the network_node master record.']
  center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.distribution_center. Business justification: Logistics network mapping ties nodes that are physical DCs to the DC master for routing, capacity planning, and compliance reporting.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the supply chain node facility. Used for carrier routing, ASN generation, and regulatory compliance reporting (FDA FSMA facility registration).']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address detail (suite, building, dock number) for the supply chain node. Supports precise carrier delivery instructions and ASN routing.']
  city varchar [note: 'City in which the supply chain node is located. Used in network design, lane definition, and geographic reporting.']
  co_packing_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this node has co-packing or toll manufacturing capability — performing secondary packaging, kitting, or assembly operations on behalf of the brand. Relevant for NPD and promotional pack configurations.']
  cold_storage_capacity_pallets varchar [note: 'Number of pallet positions dedicated to temperature-controlled (chilled or frozen) storage. Used for dairy, beverage, and perishable product inventory planning and FEFO compliance.']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date on which the supply chain node became operationally active. Used in asset lifecycle management, network design history, and regulatory compliance tracking.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code where the node is located (e.g., \'USA\', \'CAN\', \'GBR\'). Drives regulatory compliance framework selection (FDA, EFSA, USDA) and customs/trade documentation.']
  cross_dock_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this node supports cross-docking operations — transferring inbound shipments directly to outbound without storage. Reduces handling costs and lead times for high-velocity SKUs.']
  customs_bonded_warehouse boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this node is a licensed customs bonded warehouse, allowing storage of imported goods before customs duties are paid. Relevant for international supply chain nodes and import/export operations.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'Date on which the supply chain node was or is planned to be decommissioned. Nullable for active nodes. Used in network design planning, lease management, and IBP scenario modeling.']
  dock_door_count varchar [note: 'Number of loading/unloading dock doors at the facility. Constrains inbound and outbound throughput scheduling and carrier appointment management.']
  dsd_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this node supports Direct Store Delivery (DSD) operations — direct shipment from the node to retail store locations bypassing intermediate DCs. Drives route planning and trade promotion execution.']
  erp_plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA or Oracle ERP plant/organization code corresponding to this network node. Enables cross-system reconciliation between the supply chain network model and ERP master data.']
  fda_facility_registration_number varchar [note: 'FDA-assigned facility registration number for food manufacturing and storage facilities as required under FSMA Section 102. Mandatory for US-based manufacturing plants and distribution centers handling food products.']
  floor_area_sqft decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total usable floor area of the node facility in square feet. Supports capacity planning, lease management, and facility benchmarking.']
  food_safety_certification varchar [note: 'Primary GFSI-recognized food safety certification held by the node (e.g., SQF, BRC, FSSC 22000, IFS). Mandatory for supplier qualification and co-packer onboarding under TraceGains supplier compliance management.']
  gmp_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the node holds a current Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) certification. Required for manufacturing and co-packing nodes under FDA, USDA, and GFSI compliance frameworks.']
  ibp_location_code varchar [note: 'SAP Integrated Business Planning (IBP) location identifier for this node. Enables direct linkage between the network node master and IBP demand sensing, inventory optimization, and S&OP planning models.']
  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Decimal latitude coordinate of the node facility. Used in network optimization models, transportation lane distance calculations, and geographic visualization in IBP.']
  lease_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the facility lease expires for leased or 3PL-operated nodes. Drives network design risk assessment, contract renewal planning, and financial forecasting in Oracle Cloud Financials.']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Decimal longitude coordinate of the node facility. Used in network optimization models, transportation lane distance calculations, and geographic visualization in IBP.']
  moq_pallets varchar [note: 'Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) in pallet units for inbound replenishment orders to this node. Enforced during replenishment signal generation in SAP IBP and JDA to avoid sub-optimal shipments.']
  n3pl_provider_name varchar [note: 'Name of the Third-Party Logistics (3PL) provider operating this node, if applicable. Used in SLA governance, contract management, and performance scorecarding.']
  network_tier varchar [note: 'Hierarchical tier of the node within the distribution network (e.g., national DC feeds regional DCs, which feed forward DCs). Determines replenishment flow, safety stock echelon, and FIFO/FEFO rotation policy assignment.']
  node_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the supply chain node across systems (e.g., SAP plant code, DC code, 3PL site code). Used in EDI transactions, ASN documents, and IBP network models.']
  node_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the supply chain node (e.g., \'Chicago Regional DC\', \'Atlanta Co-Pack Facility\'). Used in reporting, planograms, and network design documentation.']
  node_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the supply chain node. Controls whether the node is eligible for demand planning, replenishment signal generation, and network design optimization in SAP IBP and JDA.']
  node_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the supply chain node by its primary operational function. Drives network design rules, safety stock policy assignment, and lane definitions in IBP. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: manufacturing_plant|distribution_center|3pl_warehouse|co_packer|cross_dock|forward_dc — promote to reference product]']
  operating_hours_end varchar [note: 'Standard daily operating end time (HH:MM, 24-hour format) for the node. Used in carrier appointment scheduling, outbound dispatch windows, and transportation management.']
  operating_hours_start varchar [note: 'Standard daily operating start time (HH:MM, 24-hour format) for the node. Used in carrier appointment scheduling, inbound receiving windows, and transportation management.']
  operating_model varchar [note: 'Describes the ownership and operational arrangement for the node — whether the facility is company-owned, leased, operated by a 3PL partner, or used for toll manufacturing or co-packing. Drives cost allocation (COGS), SLA governance, and contract management.']
  otif_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'On Time In Full (OTIF) delivery performance target percentage for shipments originating from this node. Used in SLA governance, carrier scorecards, and supply chain KPI reporting.']
  pallet_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of pallet positions available at the node for storage. Principal capacity metric used in inventory optimization, safety stock policy assignment, and network design in SAP IBP.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the supply chain node. Used for carrier zone determination, freight cost calculation, and geographic clustering in network design.']
  replenishment_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Standard number of days required to replenish inventory at this node from its upstream supply source. Used in safety stock calculations, demand sensing, and IBP supply-demand balancing.']
  rotation_policy varchar [note: 'Inventory rotation method enforced at this node — First In First Out (FIFO), First Expired First Out (FEFO), or Last In First Out (LIFO). Drives WMS pick sequencing and shelf life compliance for food safety.']
  safety_stock_policy_code varchar [note: 'Code referencing the safety stock calculation policy assigned to this node (e.g., days-of-supply, statistical, min-max). Drives inventory optimization parameters in SAP IBP and JDA demand/supply planning.']
  service_level_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target fill rate or case fill rate percentage (e.g., 98.5) for outbound order fulfillment from this node. Drives safety stock policy and OTIF performance measurement.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-2 state or province code where the node is located (e.g., \'IL\', \'CA\', \'ON\'). Used for tax jurisdiction, regulatory compliance, and transportation lane modeling.']
  temperature_zone varchar [note: 'Temperature-controlled storage capability of the node (ambient, chilled, frozen, or multi-temp). Critical for food safety compliance (HACCP, FSMA), shelf life management, and FEFO rotation policy assignment.']
  throughput_rate_pallets_per_day varchar [note: 'Maximum number of pallets the node can receive, process, and ship per day. Used in capacity planning, OTIF modeling, and supply-demand balancing in JDA and SAP IBP.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the node location (e.g., \'America/Chicago\', \'Europe/London\'). Used for scheduling inbound/outbound appointments, production shifts, and EDI transaction timestamps.']

  Note: 'Master record for each physical node in the supply chain network — manufacturing plants, distribution centers, 3PL warehouses, co-packer facilities, and cross-dock points. Captures node type, geographic coordinates, capacity (pallet positions, throughput rate), operating model (owned, leased, 3PL-operated), service level parameters, and network tier (national DC, regional DC, forward DC). Foundation entity for network design, lane definition, capacity planning, and safety stock policy assignment.'
}

Table supply.lane {
  lane_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the transportation lane record. Primary key for the lane entity.']
  carbon_footprint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.carbon_footprint. Business justification: Transport emissions report requires each lane to be linked to its carbon footprint record for regulatory GHG reporting.']
  destination_node_network_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the destination network node (e.g., Distribution Center (DC), customer distribution center, retail store, or 3PL facility) to which shipments are delivered on this lane.']
  lane_backup_carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the secondary or backup carrier assigned to this lane, activated when the primary carrier is unavailable or capacity-constrained. Supports supply chain resilience and continuity planning.']
  lane_carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary carrier assigned to this transportation lane for standard replenishment and distribution movements.']
  lane_network_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the origin network node (e.g., manufacturing plant, Distribution Center (DC), supplier facility, or 3PL warehouse) from which shipments depart on this lane.']
  co2_emission_factor decimal(10,4) [note: 'Estimated CO2 equivalent emissions per unit distance (kg CO2e per mile) for this lane based on freight mode and carrier fleet profile. Supports sustainability reporting and EPA environmental compliance.']
  contract_effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the carrier contract and associated freight rates for this lane become effective. Used for rate validity checks during shipment planning.']
  contract_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the carrier contract and associated freight rates for this lane expire. Triggers carrier bid management and contract renewal workflows.']
  contract_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier of the freight contract or carrier rate agreement governing this lane. Links to the carrier contract management system for rate validation and audit.']
  cross_border_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this lane crosses an international border, triggering customs documentation, import/export compliance, and regulatory reporting requirements (e.g., FDA Prior Notice for food imports).']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to all freight cost fields on this lane (e.g., USD, EUR, CAD). Supports multi-currency network operations.']
  delivery_days varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of days of the week on which deliveries are accepted at the destination node for this lane (e.g., \'Tue,Thu,Sat\'). Used for appointment scheduling and receiving window management.']
  destination_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the destination node\'s country (e.g., USA, CAN, MEX). Required for cross-border compliance, import duties, and regulatory reporting.']
  distance_miles decimal(10,2) [note: 'Calculated or contracted road/rail distance between origin and destination nodes in miles. Used for freight cost estimation, carrier rate negotiation, and carbon emissions modeling.']
  dsd_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this lane is used for Direct Store Delivery (DSD), where product is shipped directly from a manufacturing plant or DC to a retail store, bypassing the customer\'s distribution center.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this transportation lane becomes operationally active and available for replenishment routing and shipment planning.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which this transportation lane is scheduled to become inactive or is retired from the network. Nullable for open-ended lanes.']
  freight_cost_per_load decimal(14,2) [note: 'Contracted flat-rate freight cost per full truckload (TL) or equivalent load unit on this lane. Applicable primarily for TL and dedicated lanes where per-load pricing is negotiated.']
  freight_cost_per_mile decimal(10,4) [note: 'Contracted or benchmark freight cost per mile for this lane, used in carrier bid management, rate benchmarking, and transportation cost modeling.']
  freight_cost_per_unit decimal(12,4) [note: 'Standard contracted freight cost per unit (case or pallet) shipped on this lane, expressed in the operating currency. Used for Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) allocation, transportation cost modeling, and network optimization.']
  freight_mode varchar [note: 'Transportation mode used on this lane. TL = Truckload, LTL = Less-Than-Truckload, intermodal = combined rail/truck, parcel = small package carrier, rail = dedicated rail, ocean = sea freight. Drives freight cost modeling and carrier selection. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: TL|LTL|intermodal|parcel|rail|ocean|air — promote to reference product]']
  freight_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure used for freight cost and capacity calculations on this lane (e.g., pallet, case, lb, kg, cwt). Aligns with carrier contract terms and SAP S/4HANA MM configuration.']
  fuel_surcharge_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Current fuel surcharge percentage applied on top of the base freight rate for this lane, expressed as a percentage. Updated periodically per carrier contract terms and fuel index benchmarks.']
  hazmat_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this lane is certified and approved for the transport of hazardous materials (e.g., cleaning chemicals, CO2 cartridges). Drives carrier certification requirements and regulatory compliance checks.']
  lane_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the lane, used in carrier bid management, freight contracts, and Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) communications. Unique across the network.']
  lane_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name for the lane, typically combining origin and destination node names (e.g., \'Chicago DC to Atlanta DC\'). Used in reporting and network design tools.']
  lane_status varchar [note: 'Current operational lifecycle status of the lane. \'active\' lanes are available for replenishment routing; \'inactive\' lanes are retired; \'pending\' lanes are under carrier negotiation; \'suspended\' lanes are temporarily halted.']
  lane_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the lane\'s operational role. \'primary\' is the standard routing; \'backup\' is an alternate route activated when primary is unavailable; \'spot\' is used for ad-hoc shipments; \'dedicated\' is a contracted exclusive lane.']
  last_reviewed_date date [note: 'Date on which this lane record was last reviewed and validated by the transportation or supply chain planning team. Supports lane master data governance and periodic network design reviews.']
  lead_time_days decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total replenishment lead time in days for this lane, including order processing, pick/pack, and transit time. Distinct from transit_time_days as it captures the full end-to-end cycle used in demand planning and inventory optimization.']
  max_load_capacity decimal(12,3) [note: 'Maximum shipment capacity per load on this lane, expressed in the freight unit of measure (e.g., pallets, cases, or weight). Used for load planning and carrier capacity management.']
  min_order_qty decimal(12,3) [note: 'Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) required to activate a shipment on this lane, expressed in the freight unit of measure. Enforced during replenishment signal generation in SAP IBP.']
  network_region varchar [note: 'Geographic or operational region classification for this lane (e.g., \'Northeast\', \'Southeast\', \'Midwest\', \'West Coast\', \'International\'). Used for network design segmentation and regional distribution analytics.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, special handling instructions, or exceptions relevant to this lane (e.g., seasonal restrictions, bridge weight limits, port congestion advisories).']
  origin_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the origin node\'s country (e.g., USA, CAN, MEX). Required for cross-border compliance, customs documentation, and trade lane analytics.']
  otif_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Contracted On Time In Full (OTIF) performance target percentage for this lane, used to measure carrier compliance and supply chain reliability. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 98.50).']
  pickup_days varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of days of the week on which carrier pickups are available at the origin node for this lane (e.g., \'Mon,Wed,Fri\'). Used for replenishment scheduling and order cut-off management.']
  service_level varchar [note: 'Contracted service level for this lane defining delivery speed and reliability commitment (e.g., standard, expedited, overnight, economy). Linked to On Time In Full (OTIF) and Service Level Agreement (SLA) performance targets.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this lane record originates (e.g., SAP Transportation Management, JDA/Blue Yonder TMS, Oracle SCM, or manually maintained). Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  temperature_zone varchar [note: 'Temperature-controlled handling requirement for this lane, critical for food safety compliance. \'ambient\' = dry goods; \'chilled\' = refrigerated (2-8°C); \'frozen\' = frozen (-18°C or below); \'controlled\' = specific temperature range per product specification. Aligns with FSMA and HACCP requirements.']
  third_party_logistics_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this lane is operated by a Third-Party Logistics (3PL) provider rather than a company-owned or dedicated carrier. Drives 3PL performance management and cost allocation.']
  transit_time_days decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard transit time in calendar days from origin departure to destination receipt for this lane. Used in replenishment lead time calculation, safety stock parameterization, and SAP IBP supply planning.']
  transit_variability_days decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard deviation or average variability in transit time in days for this lane, derived from historical shipment performance. Used in safety stock calculation and inventory optimization in SAP IBP.']

  Note: 'Defined transportation lane between two network nodes (origin-destination pair) used for supply chain routing, freight cost estimation, and replenishment lead time calculation. Captures origin node, destination node, transit time, freight mode (TL, LTL, intermodal, parcel), carrier assignments, lane distance, standard freight cost per unit, and active/inactive status. Supports network optimization, carrier bid management, and transportation cost modeling.'
}

Table supply.planning_exception {
  planning_exception_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for planning_exception']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Exception Management process records which customer account\'s demand caused a planning exception, supporting root‑cause analysis and service level reporting.']
  center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Distribution Center or planning location node where the supply-demand imbalance or constraint violation was detected.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Capture the cost impact of planning exceptions in the associated cost center for exception reporting.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supply planner responsible for reviewing and resolving this exception. Drives daily planner workbench assignment and exception-based management workflows.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific SKU affected by this supply planning exception. Identifies the finished good, semi-finished, or raw material involved in the imbalance.']
  sop_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the IBP/S&OP planning cycle run during which this exception was generated. Links the exception to the specific planning iteration for traceability.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Sales or distribution channel associated with the demand driving this exception. Retail: traditional grocery/mass/club. Foodservice: restaurants and institutional. DTC: direct-to-consumer. Ecommerce: online retail. Export: international distribution.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this exception record was first written to the Silver layer data product. Supports audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  demand_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total demand quantity (in planning UOM) for the affected SKU at the affected location within the exception time bucket, as calculated by the IBP or MRP run. Represents the demand side of the supply-demand imbalance.']
  detection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the supply planning exception was first detected and raised by the MRP or IBP balancing run. Represents the principal business event time for this record.']
  due_date date [note: 'Target date by which the planner must resolve or escalate the exception to prevent downstream supply chain impact. Drives SLA compliance tracking on the planner workbench.']
  escalation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason this exception was escalated beyond the standard planner workbench. Populated only when is_escalated is True. Supports root cause analysis and S&OP review.']
  exception_bucket_end_date date [note: 'End date of the planning time bucket in which the supply-demand imbalance or constraint violation occurs. Together with start date defines the affected planning window.']
  exception_bucket_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the planning time bucket (week, period, or day) in which the supply-demand imbalance or constraint violation occurs. Used for time-phased exception analysis.']
  exception_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible business identifier for the supply planning exception, used in planner workbench, exception reports, and cross-system communication (e.g., ERP, IBP). Format: EXC-YYYYMMDD-XXXXXX.']
  exception_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the supply planning exception within the planner workbench workflow. Open: newly raised, awaiting review. In_review: planner actively working resolution. Resolved: corrective action applied. Escalated: elevated to senior planner or S&OP team. Closed: no further action required.']
  exception_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the supply planning exception category. Shortage: supply insufficient to meet demand. Excess: supply exceeds demand beyond safety stock. Late delivery: inbound supply arrives after required date. MOQ violation: order quantity below Minimum Order Quantity. Shelf life risk: inventory at risk of expiry before consumption. Allocation breach: customer or channel allocation limit exceeded.']
  fill_rate_impact_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated percentage point reduction in order fill rate attributable to this exception if unresolved. Expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 5.25 = 5.25% fill rate reduction). Supports customer service level impact quantification.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period (P01–P13) in which the exception time bucket falls, aligned to the company\'s fiscal calendar. Enables exception volume and severity reporting by financial period for S&OP and IBP cycle reviews.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year in which the exception time bucket falls. Used for year-over-year exception trend analysis and annual S&OP performance reporting.']
  imbalance_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Net quantity gap between supply and demand (supply_qty minus demand_qty) for the affected SKU and location in the exception bucket. Negative value indicates shortage; positive value indicates excess. Core metric for exception prioritization.']
  is_auto_resolved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the exception was automatically resolved by the planning system without planner intervention (True) or required manual planner action (False). Supports automation rate KPI tracking and continuous improvement.']
  is_escalated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this exception has been escalated to a senior supply planner, S&OP team, or management for resolution (True) or remains at standard planner level (False).']
  moq_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Minimum Order Quantity constraint applicable to the affected SKU from the relevant supplier or manufacturing plant. Populated when exception_type is moq_violation to quantify the constraint breach.']
  otif_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this exception is projected to negatively impact On Time In Full (OTIF) delivery performance to customers if not resolved (True). Used to prioritize exceptions with direct customer service level consequences.']
  planner_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by the supply planner documenting analysis, context, interim actions, or rationale for resolution decisions. Supports knowledge transfer and audit trail.']
  planning_horizon varchar [note: 'Planning horizon bucket in which the exception falls. Short_term: 0–4 weeks (operational). Mid_term: 5–13 weeks (tactical). Long_term: 14+ weeks (strategic). Determines urgency and escalation path within the S&OP process.']
  planning_run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the MRP or IBP planning run that generated this exception. Enables traceability of exceptions to specific planning engine executions and supports run-over-run comparison.']
  planning_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure in which demand_qty, supply_qty, and imbalance_qty are expressed for this exception (e.g., CS=Case, EA=Each, KG=Kilogram, LB=Pound, L=Litre, GAL=Gallon, MT=Metric Ton, PAL=Pallet). Aligns with SAP IBP planning UOM configuration. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CS|EA|KG|LB|L|GAL|MT|PAL — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  recommended_action varchar [note: 'System-generated or planner-entered recommended corrective action to resolve the exception (e.g., \'Expedite PO #12345\', \'Reallocate 500 CS from DC-West\', \'Reduce production run by 200 MT\', \'Approve substitution to SKU-98765\'). Drives exception-based management workflows.']
  resolution_action_taken varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the actual corrective action applied by the planner to resolve the exception. Captured at resolution time for audit trail and continuous improvement analysis.']
  resolution_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the resolution method applied to close the exception. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: expedite|reallocation|substitution|demand_reduction|production_increase|accepted_risk|cancelled — promote to reference product]']
  resolved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the exception was marked as resolved or closed by the planner. Used to calculate resolution cycle time and SLA adherence metrics.']
  rotation_policy varchar [note: 'Inventory rotation policy applicable to the affected SKU at the affected DC. FIFO (First In First Out): oldest stock consumed first. FEFO (First Expired First Out): stock closest to expiry consumed first — standard for perishable food and beverage products. LIFO (Last In First Out): rarely used in food and beverage.']
  safety_stock_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Configured safety stock quantity for the affected SKU at the affected DC at the time the exception was raised. Used to assess whether the imbalance breaches the safety stock buffer and to calibrate exception severity.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Business impact severity level assigned to the exception, used to prioritize planner workbench queue. Critical: immediate risk to customer service or food safety. High: significant supply disruption within the planning horizon. Medium: manageable imbalance requiring near-term action. Low: informational, minor deviation.']
  shelf_life_remaining_days varchar [note: 'Number of days of remaining shelf life for the at-risk inventory lot at the time the exception was raised. Populated when exception_type is shelf_life_risk. Supports FEFO rotation policy enforcement and food safety compliance.']
  shelf_life_threshold_days varchar [note: 'Minimum acceptable remaining shelf life (in days) configured for the SKU at the DC, below which a shelf_life_risk exception is triggered. Defined per SKU-DC combination in the planning system.']
  source_exception_ref varchar [note: 'Native exception identifier or alert number from the originating system of record (e.g., SAP IBP alert ID, JDA exception number). Enables cross-system traceability and deduplication.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record that generated or originated this supply planning exception. Supports data lineage and cross-system reconciliation in the Silver layer.']
  supply_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total available supply quantity (in planning UOM) for the affected SKU at the affected location within the exception time bucket, including on-hand inventory, open purchase orders, and planned production orders.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this exception record, including status changes, planner notes, or resolution actions. Supports change tracking and audit compliance.']

  Note: 'Supply planning exception raised when a supply-demand imbalance, constraint violation, or planning rule breach is detected during MRP or IBP balancing runs. Captures exception type (shortage, excess, late delivery, MOQ violation, shelf life risk, allocation breach), severity, affected SKU/location/time bucket, recommended resolution action, and resolution status. Drives daily planner workbench prioritization and exception-based management workflows.'
}

Table supply.capacity_constraint {
  capacity_constraint_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each capacity constraint record in the supply planning system. Primary key for the capacity_constraint data product. Entity role: MASTER_RESOURCE — represents a managed planning resource (capacity bucket) with its own identity and lifecycle.']
  center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Distribution Center (DC) or warehouse node to which this capacity constraint applies. Null when the constraint is associated with a manufacturing plant rather than a DC. Links to the DC master in the supply network.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Allocate capacity constraint impacts (e.g., overtime) to the relevant cost center for cost control.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Name or user identifier of the supply planner, capacity manager, or S&OP analyst who last reviewed and validated this capacity constraint record. Provides accountability and audit trail for planning decisions.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing plant or production facility to which this capacity constraint applies. Null when the constraint is associated with a DC or logistics node rather than a production plant. Links to the plant master in SAP S/4HANA.']
  production_line_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific production line or work center within a plant to which this capacity constraint applies. Populated only when resource_type is \'production_line\'. Links to the work center or production line master in SAP S/4HANA PP or Aveva MES.']
  sop_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the S&OP/IBP planning cycle during which this capacity constraint was defined or last reviewed. Links to the S&OP cycle master to enable traceability of capacity decisions across monthly planning cycles.']
  transportation_lane_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the transportation lane (origin-destination pair) to which this capacity constraint applies. Populated only when resource_type is \'transportation_lane\'. Links to the lane master in JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management.']
  available_capacity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total available capacity for the resource within the defined capacity bucket period, expressed in the unit of measure specified by capacity_uom (e.g., cases per day, pallet positions, labor hours, truck loads). Represents the theoretical maximum throughput or storage before any utilization. Serves as the MEASUREMENT_OR_VALUE category for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.']
  bottleneck_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this resource is identified as a bottleneck in the current planning cycle (True = bottleneck, False = not a bottleneck). Set by the planning engine (SAP IBP or JDA) when utilization_pct exceeds utilization_ceiling_pct. Triggers network rebalancing, co-packing evaluation, or 3PL overflow decisions.']
  bucket_end_date date [note: 'The last calendar date of the capacity bucket period to which this constraint record applies. Combined with bucket_start_date, defines the exact planning window for available and utilized capacity values.']
  bucket_start_date date [note: 'The first calendar date of the capacity bucket period to which this constraint record applies. Combined with bucket_end_date, defines the exact planning window for available and utilized capacity values.']
  capacity_bucket varchar [note: 'Time granularity of the capacity constraint record. Defines the planning horizon bucket over which available and utilized capacity are measured. \'daily\' is used for short-term finite scheduling; \'weekly\' for S&OP/IBP mid-term planning; \'monthly\' and \'quarterly\' for strategic network design.']
  capacity_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure in which available_capacity and utilized_capacity are expressed. Varies by resource_type: production lines typically use cases or kg per day; DC storage uses pallet positions; labor uses hours; transportation uses truck loads or pallets. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cases|pallets|labor_hours|truck_loads|kg|liters|units — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  changeover_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Hours allocated within the capacity bucket for product changeovers (SKU switches) on the production line, including allergen cleaning, flavor changeovers, and packaging format changes. Reduces effective throughput capacity. Tracked separately from planned_downtime_hours for root cause analysis.']
  constraint_category varchar [note: 'Business category classifying the root cause or nature of the capacity constraint. \'throughput\' relates to production or logistics flow rate; \'storage\' to physical space; \'labor\' to workforce availability; \'equipment\' to machinery or vehicle availability; \'regulatory\' to compliance-driven limits (e.g., FDA GMP, FSMA); \'seasonal\' to demand-driven temporary constraints.']
  constraint_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the capacity constraint record, used in SAP IBP and JDA/Blue Yonder planning runs to reference this constraint. Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.']
  constraint_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name for the capacity constraint (e.g., \'Line 3 Daily Throughput Cap\', \'DC Chicago Pallet Positions\'). Serves as the IDENTITY_LABEL category for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.']
  constraint_source varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this capacity constraint record originated. Enables data lineage tracking and reconciliation across planning systems. \'sap_ibp\' = SAP Integrated Business Planning; \'jda_blue_yonder\' = JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning; \'aveva_mes\' = Aveva/Wonderware MES; \'manual\' = planner-entered; \'erp\' = SAP S/4HANA or Oracle Cloud ERP.']
  constraint_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the capacity constraint record. \'active\' means the constraint is live and enforced in planning runs; \'inactive\' means it has been retired or superseded; \'pending\' means it is defined but not yet effective; \'suspended\' means temporarily bypassed. Serves as the LIFECYCLE_STATUS category for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.']
  constraint_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the capacity constraint is a hard or soft limit in supply planning. \'hard\' constraints cannot be violated by the planning engine (e.g., physical line speed, dock door count); \'soft\' constraints can be overridden with a penalty cost or planner approval (e.g., preferred utilization ceiling, labor overtime threshold).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this capacity constraint record was first created in the system. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.']
  dock_doors_available varchar [note: 'Number of dock doors available at the DC for inbound and/or outbound operations within the capacity bucket. DC-specific metric used to plan ASN (Advanced Shipping Notice) scheduling, carrier appointments, and DSD (Direct Store Delivery) operations. Null when resource_type is not \'dock_door\' or \'dc_throughput\'.']
  dock_doors_utilized varchar [note: 'Number of dock doors actually scheduled or utilized within the capacity bucket period. Used alongside dock_doors_available to compute dock door utilization rate and identify scheduling bottlenecks at the DC.']
  effective_capacity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Net usable capacity after applying OEE, planned downtime, changeover time, and other efficiency factors to the theoretical available_capacity. Represents the realistic throughput ceiling used in constrained supply planning runs. Expressed in capacity_uom.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which this capacity constraint record is no longer active and is excluded from planning runs. Null for open-ended constraints. Used to manage time-bound constraints such as seasonal capacity limits or temporary equipment outages.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this capacity constraint record becomes effective and is enforced in planning runs. Enables forward-looking constraint management and scenario planning for known future capacity changes (e.g., planned line shutdowns, DC expansions).']
  labor_hours_available decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total labor hours available for production or DC operations within the capacity bucket, accounting for scheduled shifts, headcount, and planned absences. Used in labor capacity planning and S&OP cycles. Null when resource_type is not \'labor\'.']
  labor_hours_utilized decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual or planned labor hours consumed within the capacity bucket period. Used to assess workforce loading against available labor hours and identify overtime or underutilization scenarios.']
  last_reviewed_date date [note: 'Date on which this capacity constraint record was last reviewed and validated by a supply planner or capacity manager. Used to ensure constraint parameters remain current and aligned with operational reality across S&OP cycles.']
  oee_target_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Target OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) percentage for the production line or equipment resource within this capacity bucket. OEE = Availability × Performance × Quality. Used to set realistic effective capacity expectations in finite scheduling and SAP IBP planning runs. Applicable only when resource_type is \'production_line\'.']
  otif_risk_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this capacity constraint poses a risk to OTIF (On Time In Full) delivery performance (True = OTIF risk present). Set when the constraint is projected to cause order fulfillment shortfalls within the bucket period. Used to prioritize constraint resolution and escalation.']
  overflow_action varchar [note: 'Recommended or approved action to resolve a capacity overload when bottleneck_flag is True. \'co_pack\' triggers co-packing or toll manufacturing evaluation; \'3pl_overflow\' routes excess volume to a 3PL partner; \'demand_defer\' defers lower-priority demand; \'interplant_transfer\' reallocates production to another plant; \'none\' means no action required.']
  pallet_positions varchar [note: 'Total number of pallet storage positions available at the DC or warehouse for this capacity bucket. DC-specific metric used in warehouse capacity management and network design. Null when resource_type is not \'storage\' or \'dc_throughput\'.']
  planned_downtime_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Hours of scheduled downtime within the capacity bucket for maintenance, sanitation (GMP/HACCP compliance), changeovers, or regulatory inspections. Reduces effective_capacity from available_capacity. Critical for food and beverage manufacturing where sanitation windows are mandated by GMP and FSMA.']
  planner_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for supply planners to document context, assumptions, or exceptions related to this capacity constraint (e.g., \'Line 3 down for allergen deep clean week of 2024-03-11\', \'DC Chicago dock expansion in progress\'). Supports S&OP cycle documentation and audit trail.']
  planning_version varchar [note: 'Identifier for the SAP IBP or JDA planning version or scenario to which this capacity constraint record belongs (e.g., \'BASELINE\', \'SCENARIO_A\', \'CONSENSUS\'). Enables multi-scenario capacity analysis and S&OP cycle management.']
  regulatory_hold_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this capacity constraint is driven by a regulatory hold or compliance requirement (True = regulatory-driven constraint). Examples include FDA FSMA inspection holds, USDA audit shutdowns, or GMP sanitation mandates that reduce available production capacity.']
  resource_type varchar [note: 'Categorizes the type of supply chain resource to which this capacity constraint applies. Drives how the constraint is interpreted in finite capacity planning: production_line (manufacturing throughput), dc_throughput (Distribution Center inbound/outbound volume), transportation_lane (carrier/lane capacity), dock_door (DC dock door availability), labor (workforce hours), storage (pallet positions or cubic volume). Serves as the CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE category for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.']
  safety_stock_impact decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of safety stock that cannot be replenished due to this capacity constraint within the bucket period, expressed in capacity_uom. Used in inventory optimization to quantify the risk exposure created by constrained capacity and inform safety stock parameter adjustments in SAP IBP.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this capacity constraint record was last modified. Used to detect changes between planning cycles and support incremental Silver layer processing and audit trail requirements.']
  utilization_ceiling_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Maximum acceptable utilization percentage for this resource before the constraint is flagged as a bottleneck or overload. Typically set below 100% to preserve buffer capacity (e.g., 85% for production lines to allow for changeovers and maintenance, 90% for DC throughput). Used in SAP IBP and JDA planning to trigger alerts and rebalancing.']
  utilization_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Ratio of utilized_capacity to available_capacity expressed as a percentage (0–100). Calculated field stored for planning visibility: utilization_pct = (utilized_capacity / available_capacity) * 100. Used to identify bottleneck resources and trigger network rebalancing decisions. Values above the utilization_ceiling_pct indicate an overload condition.']
  utilized_capacity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Actual or planned capacity consumed within the bucket period, expressed in the same unit of measure as available_capacity (capacity_uom). Used to compute utilization rate and identify bottlenecks. In constrained planning runs, this reflects the sum of all planned orders or shipments assigned to this resource.']

  Note: 'Production and logistics capacity constraint record used in constrained supply planning and DC capacity management. Captures resource type (production line, DC throughput, transportation lane, dock doors), capacity bucket (daily, weekly, monthly), available vs. utilized capacity, constraint type (hard/soft), and DC-specific metrics (pallet positions, labor hours, dock door utilization). Enables finite capacity planning, bottleneck identification, and network rebalancing decisions.'
}

Table supply.inbound_receipt {
  inbound_receipt_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for inbound_receipt']
  inbound_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the inbound logistics shipment or Advanced Shipping Notice (ASN) associated with this goods receipt. Enables end-to-end supply chain traceability from supplier dispatch to DC receipt.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'SAP plant code (WERKS) identifying the manufacturing facility or distribution center where the goods were physically received. Drives inventory posting and valuation area.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Internal identifier for the material or finished good received (MATNR). Links to the material master for product specifications, allergen data, shelf life parameters, and storage requirements.']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'SAP storage location code (LGORT) within the plant where received goods are physically placed. Determines bin assignment in WMS and supports FIFO/FEFO rotation policy enforcement.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Internal identifier for the supplier/vendor (LIFNR) from whom the materials were received. Enables supplier performance tracking, OTIF measurement, and accounts payable matching. PARTY_REFERENCE category.']
  asn_number varchar [note: 'Supplier-transmitted Advanced Shipping Notice number received via EDI (856 transaction set) prior to physical delivery. Used to pre-receive inventory, schedule dock appointments, and validate shipment contents against PO.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Supplier or internally assigned batch/lot number (CHARG) for the received material. Critical for food safety traceability, FEFO rotation, allergen management, and recall execution per FSMA 204 requirements.']
  bill_of_lading_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-issued bill of lading number for the inbound shipment. Serves as the legal contract of carriage and is required for freight claims, customs clearance, and 3PL reconciliation.']
  certificate_of_analysis_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the supplier-provided Certificate of Analysis accompanying the shipment. Documents quality test results (microbiological, chemical, physical) for the received batch. Required for GFSI, SQF, and HACCP compliance.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating where the received material was manufactured or grown. Required for customs declarations, USDA country-of-origin labeling (COOL), and import compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the goods receipt record was first created in the source system (SAP CPUDT + CPUTM). Used for audit trail and data lineage. RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the valuation and receipt value amounts (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency operations across global DC network.']
  delivery_note_number varchar [note: 'Supplier-provided delivery note or packing slip number (LIEFN) accompanying the inbound shipment. Used for three-way match (PO, GR, invoice) and ASN reconciliation.']
  document_date date [note: 'The date on the physical delivery document or packing slip (BLDAT) as provided by the supplier. May differ from posting_date when receipts are backdated or processed with delay.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration or best-before date of the received batch. Governs FEFO inventory rotation, shelf life compliance, and eligibility for distribution. Mandatory for perishable food and beverage products per FDA and EFSA regulations.']
  goods_receipt_note varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by the receiving clerk at time of goods receipt. Captures exceptions, damage observations, temperature deviations, or other relevant receiving conditions not captured in structured fields.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'INCOTERMS code from the purchase order defining the point of title transfer and risk for the inbound shipment (e.g., FOB, CIF, DDP, EXW). Determines when inventory ownership transfers to Food Beverage.']
  is_consignment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the received goods are consignment stock (supplier-owned inventory held at Food Beverage\'s facility). True = consignment; False = standard purchased stock. Affects accounts payable timing and inventory ownership.']
  is_return_delivery boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this goods receipt represents a return delivery from a customer or a reversal of a prior outbound delivery being returned to stock. True = return; False = standard inbound receipt.']
  manufacture_date date [note: 'Date on which the received batch was manufactured by the supplier or co-packer. Used for shelf life calculation, FEFO rotation policy, and food safety compliance documentation.']
  material_document_number varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA MM material document number (MBLNR) generated upon goods receipt posting. Serves as the externally-known business identifier for this receipt transaction, referenced on supplier correspondence and internal audit trails.']
  material_document_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year (MJAHR) associated with the SAP material document number. Required in combination with material_document_number to uniquely identify the document in SAP S/4HANA.']
  movement_type varchar [note: 'SAP MM movement type code (BWART) classifying the nature of the goods movement (e.g., 101 = GR for PO, 103 = GR into blocked stock, 105 = GR from blocked stock, 501 = receipt without PO). Drives inventory account determination and stock type posting.']
  po_ordered_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Original quantity ordered on the purchase order line item. Compared against received_qty to calculate receipt variance and identify short-shipments or over-deliveries for supplier performance management.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The accounting posting date (BUDAT) on which the goods receipt is recorded in SAP, triggering inventory update and accounts payable accrual. Determines the fiscal period for financial reporting. BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category.']
  purchase_order_line_item varchar [note: 'Line item number (EBELP) within the purchase order that this goods receipt references. Identifies the specific material, quantity, and price agreed upon in the PO.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'SAP purchase order number (EBELN) against which this goods receipt is posted. Links the physical receipt to the procurement commitment and triggers accounts payable processing.']
  quality_inspection_lot varchar [note: 'SAP QM inspection lot number (PRUEFLOS) created automatically upon goods receipt for materials subject to quality inspection. Links the receipt to the quality inspection results and usage decision in Veeva Vault or SAP QM.']
  quality_usage_decision varchar [note: 'Outcome of the quality inspection usage decision for the received batch. \'accepted\' = released to unrestricted stock; \'rejected\' = returned to vendor or destroyed; \'conditional_release\' = released with deviations noted; \'pending\' = inspection in progress; \'waived\' = skip-lot or pre-approved supplier.']
  receipt_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the goods receipt transaction. \'posted\' = inventory updated; \'reversed\' = full reversal posted; \'blocked\' = held pending quality or compliance review; \'in_quality_inspection\' = under QM usage decision; \'partially_reversed\' = partial quantity reversed; \'cancelled\' = voided before posting. LIFECYCLE_STATUS category. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: posted|reversed|blocked|in_quality_inspection|partially_reversed|cancelled — promote to reference product]']
  receipt_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the goods were physically received and scanned at the dock door or receiving bay. Used for OTIF calculation, lead time analysis, and dock scheduling.']
  received_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Actual quantity of material physically received and posted to inventory (MENGE). Expressed in the base unit of measure. Used for inventory reconciliation, fill rate calculation, and OTIF measurement.']
  receiving_plant_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the receiving location type. Distinguishes between manufacturing plants, distribution centers (DC), co-packer facilities, 3PL warehouses, and cross-dock points. Drives putaway rules and inventory ownership.']
  remaining_shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Number of days remaining between the receipt posting date and the expiration date at time of receipt. Used to enforce minimum remaining shelf life (MRSL) thresholds for customer acceptance and distribution eligibility.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operational system of record from which this goods receipt record was ingested into the Silver Layer. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and multi-system integration auditing.']
  stock_type varchar [note: 'SAP stock type (INSMK) into which the received quantity is posted. \'unrestricted\' = available for use/sale; \'quality_inspection\' = under QM hold; \'blocked\' = not available; \'restricted\' = batch-restricted; \'returns\' = returned goods stock. Governs FIFO/FEFO rotation eligibility.']
  supplier_lot_number varchar [note: 'Supplier\'s own lot or batch identification number as printed on the shipping label or certificate of analysis. Distinct from the internal batch_number assigned in SAP. Required for supplier traceability and recall coordination.']
  total_receipt_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total inventory value posted for this receipt (valuation_price × received_qty) in local currency. Drives the accounts payable accrual entry and inventory balance update in SAP FI/CO.']
  uom varchar [note: 'Base unit of measure (MEINS) in which the received quantity is expressed (e.g., KG, L, EA, CS, PAL). Aligns with the material master base UOM for inventory valuation and planning.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this goods receipt record in the source system. Supports change detection in the Silver Layer ETL pipeline and audit compliance. RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category.']
  valuation_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Unit price at which the received material is valuated in inventory (DMBTR/MENGE). Used for inventory valuation, COGS calculation, and accounts payable accrual. Reflects moving average price or standard price per material master. MONETARY_TRIPLET category.']

  Note: 'Goods receipt transaction recorded in SAP S/4HANA MM when inbound materials or finished goods are physically received at a plant or DC. Captures purchase order reference, delivery note, material, batch/lot number, quantity received, unit of measure, storage location, valuation price, and posting date. Triggers inventory update and accounts payable posting.'
}

Table supply.otif_performance {
