  otif_performance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each OTIF performance record in the supply chain service measurement dataset. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — represents a discrete supply chain service measurement event with a clear lifecycle.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the retail or foodservice customer account for whom this OTIF performance record is tracked. Supports retailer scorecard compliance and customer service reporting.']
  center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Distribution Center (DC) from which the shipment originated. Supports DC-level OTIF performance benchmarking and network design analysis.']
  sales_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating sales order against which this OTIF performance is measured. Used to trace service compliance back to the customer order.']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the shipment record associated with this OTIF performance measurement. Links to the outbound delivery or shipment entity in the supply chain execution system.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) included in this OTIF performance measurement. Enables SKU-level service compliance analysis.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'The date the shipment was physically received and confirmed at the customer or retailer destination. Used to calculate on-time compliance by comparing against the requested or confirmed delivery date.']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the transportation carrier or 3PL (Third-Party Logistics) provider responsible for delivering this shipment. Used for carrier performance scorecarding and OTIF root cause attribution.']
  carrier_scac varchar [note: 'Standard Carrier Alpha Code (SCAC) uniquely identifying the transportation carrier per NMFTA standards. Used for EDI transactions, freight audit, and carrier performance benchmarking.']
  case_fill_rate decimal(7,4) [note: 'Ratio of cases shipped to cases ordered, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.9750 = 97.50%). Measures the degree to which the order was fulfilled at the case level. Key metric for supply chain customer service performance.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Sales channel through which the order was placed and fulfilled (e.g., retail, foodservice, DSD=Direct Store Delivery, ecommerce, club). Enables channel-specific OTIF performance benchmarking.']
  confirmed_delivery_date date [note: 'The delivery date confirmed by the supplier or carrier at time of order acceptance. May differ from the requested delivery date and serves as the committed delivery window for OTIF measurement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this OTIF performance record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and Silver Layer ingestion tracking per lakehouse governance standards.']
  days_early_late varchar [note: 'Signed integer representing the number of days the shipment arrived relative to the required delivery date. Negative values indicate early delivery; positive values indicate late delivery; zero indicates on-time delivery. Used for trend analysis and carrier performance management.']
  delivered_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total quantity of the SKU confirmed as received by the customer or retailer, which may differ from shipped quantity due to transit damage, short receipt, or carrier discrepancies.']
  delivery_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known outbound delivery document number from the ERP or WMS system (e.g., SAP delivery number). Serves as the primary business identifier for this OTIF performance record and is used in retailer dispute resolution and carrier communications.']
  is_backorder boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether any portion of the order was placed on backorder due to insufficient inventory at time of shipment. True = backorder exists; False = no backorder.']
  is_in_full boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the shipment met the in-full delivery threshold as defined by the retailer or customer OTIF policy (typically 100% or a defined fill rate threshold). True = in-full; False = short shipment.']
  is_on_time boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the shipment was delivered on or before the required delivery date per the retailer or customer OTIF policy. True = on-time delivery; False = late delivery.']
  is_otif boolean [note: 'Boolean composite flag indicating whether the shipment satisfied BOTH the on-time AND in-full criteria simultaneously. True = OTIF compliant; False = OTIF non-compliant. Primary KPI for retailer scorecard reporting.']
  is_penalty_assessed boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether a retailer OTIF fine or chargeback has been formally assessed and invoiced against this shipment. True = penalty assessed; False = no penalty assessed or still under review.']
  is_substitution boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether an alternative SKU was substituted for the originally ordered SKU due to supply shortage or product unavailability. True = substitution occurred; False = original SKU shipped.']
  line_fill_rate decimal(7,4) [note: 'Ratio of order lines shipped complete to total order lines, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.9500 = 95.00%). Measures fulfillment completeness at the order line level, distinct from case fill rate.']
  measurement_date date [note: 'The principal business event date on which this OTIF performance record was evaluated or scored. Typically aligned with the actual delivery date or the retailer scorecard reporting date.']
  ordered_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total quantity of the SKU ordered by the customer on the purchase order, expressed in the order unit of measure. Used as the denominator for in-full compliance calculation.']
  otif_policy_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the retailer or customer OTIF compliance policy applied to this performance record. Tracks policy changes over time to ensure accurate historical compliance measurement.']
  otif_score decimal(7,4) [note: 'Composite OTIF compliance score for this shipment record, expressed as a decimal between 0 and 1 (e.g., 0.9800 = 98.00%). Calculated as the product of on-time rate and in-full rate per retailer scorecard methodology. Primary KPI for retailer compliance reporting.']
  penalty_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the retailer penalty exposure amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency financial exposure reporting.']
  penalty_exposure_amt decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated monetary value of retailer OTIF fines or chargebacks associated with this non-compliant shipment, expressed in the transaction currency. Used for financial exposure tracking and trade compliance management.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'Retailer or customer-issued purchase order number associated with this shipment. Used for retailer scorecard reconciliation and OTIF fine dispute resolution.']
  qty_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure used for ordered, shipped, and delivered quantity fields (e.g., CS=Case, EA=Each, LB=Pound, KG=Kilogram, PL=Pallet, TI=Tier). Ensures consistent quantity comparison across the OTIF calculation.']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'The date the customer or retailer originally requested delivery of the order. Used as the baseline for on-time compliance measurement against the OTIF standard.']
  retailer_scorecard_window varchar [note: 'The retailer-defined measurement window or compliance period (e.g., \'Walmart W2024-Q1\', \'Target FY2024-P03\') to which this OTIF performance record is attributed. Aligns internal tracking with external retailer scorecard reporting cycles.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'Categorized root cause classification for OTIF failures. Used for corrective action prioritization and supply chain performance improvement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: supply_shortage|carrier_delay|dc_error|customer_change|forecast_miss|weather_event|system_error|other — promote to reference product if additional values are needed]']
  root_cause_detail varchar [note: 'Free-text description providing additional detail on the root cause of an OTIF failure beyond the root cause category. Captures specific circumstances such as ingredient shortage, carrier equipment failure, or DC picking error for corrective action documentation.']
  ship_date date [note: 'The date the shipment physically departed the Distribution Center (DC) or manufacturing facility. Used to assess carrier transit time and identify DC-side delays contributing to OTIF failures.']
  shipment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the shipment associated with this OTIF performance record. Tracks the workflow state from order release through final delivery confirmation.']
  shipped_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total quantity of the SKU actually shipped to the customer, expressed in the order unit of measure. Used as the numerator for in-full and case fill rate compliance calculations.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operational system of record from which this OTIF performance record was sourced (e.g., SAP S/4HANA SD, Oracle Cloud Supply Chain, JDA/Blue Yonder TMS). Supports data lineage and audit traceability in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Mode of transportation used for this shipment (e.g., TL=Truckload, LTL=Less-than-Truckload, parcel, rail, intermodal, ocean). Influences OTIF transit time expectations and carrier accountability.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this OTIF performance record was last modified in the data platform, such as when root cause classification was updated or penalty status was revised. Supports change tracking and audit compliance.']

  Note: 'Supply chain service performance record measuring On-Time In-Full (OTIF) delivery compliance and order fill rates at shipment, customer, and SKU/DC levels. Captures requested vs. actual delivery dates, ordered vs. delivered quantities, on-time flag, in-full flag, OTIF composite score, case fill rate, line fill rate, root cause category (supply shortage, carrier delay, DC error), retailer penalty exposure (e.g., OTIF fines), and backorder/substitution flags. Primary KPI for supply chain customer service and retailer scorecard compliance.'
}

Table supply.partner_sla {
  partner_sla_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for partner_sla']
  center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Distribution Center (DC) or warehouse node at which this SLA applies. Links the SLA commitment to a specific physical logistics node in the supply network.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent logistics or 3PL master contract under which this SLA is governed. Enables traceability from individual SLA records to the overarching contractual agreement.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the internal Food Beverage supply chain or procurement manager responsible for managing and enforcing this SLA. Used for accountability, escalation routing, and contract renewal ownership.']
  primary_partner_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the third-party logistics (3PL) provider or logistics partner entity that is party to this SLA. Used to link performance commitments to the vendor master.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SLA contract automatically renews at expiry unless formally terminated. When True, a renewal notice period (renewal_notice_days) applies before the expiry date.']
  bonus_structure_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the financial incentive mechanism awarded when the 3PL partner exceeds the KPI target value. Supports performance-based contracting and incentive alignment with logistics partners.']
  bonus_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Numeric value of the bonus awarded per measurement period when the 3PL partner exceeds the KPI target. Interpreted in context of bonus_structure_type. Supports billing reconciliation and incentive payment processing.']
  contract_term_months varchar [note: 'Duration of the SLA contract term expressed in months. Used to calculate expiry dates, trigger renewal workflows, and benchmark contract lengths across the 3PL partner portfolio.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this SLA record was first created in the system of record. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SLA lifecycle tracking from initial contract execution.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which penalty and bonus financial values are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency 3PL contracts across global DC network.']
  dc_node_code varchar [note: 'Business-facing alphanumeric code identifying the specific DC or warehouse node covered by this SLA. Used in operational reporting, ASN processing, and WMS integration to identify the physical location.']
  dispute_resolution_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of calendar days within which performance disputes between Food Beverage and the 3PL partner must be resolved per the SLA terms. Governs billing reconciliation timelines and contract compliance.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which this SLA becomes binding and performance measurement begins. Aligns with the contract commencement date for the 3PL engagement at the specified DC node.']
  escalation_threshold_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Performance level (expressed as a percentage of the KPI target) below which a formal escalation to senior management or contract review is triggered. Distinct from the penalty threshold — represents the point at which relationship management intervention is required.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which this SLA ceases to be binding. Null for open-ended agreements. Used to trigger contract renewal workflows and performance review cadences.']
  food_safety_certification varchar [note: 'Food safety certification standard that the 3PL partner is contractually required to maintain at the DC node. SQF = Safe Quality Food; BRC = British Retail Consortium; FSSC 22000 = Food Safety System Certification; ISO 22000 = Food Safety Management System. Supports GFSI compliance and supplier qualification.']
  governing_law_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the jurisdiction whose laws govern this SLA contract (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU). Required for multi-national 3PL contracts to determine applicable regulatory and legal frameworks.']
  haccp_compliance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the 3PL partner is contractually required to maintain a HACCP plan at the DC node. Applicable for food-grade warehousing and cold chain operations. Supports FDA and USDA regulatory compliance.']
  inventory_accuracy_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Contractually committed inventory record accuracy target expressed as a percentage, measured via cycle counts or physical inventory audits at the DC node. Supports WMS reconciliation and shrinkage management.']
  is_cold_chain boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SLA governs temperature-controlled (cold chain) logistics operations at the DC node. When True, temperature compliance monitoring and FSMA cold chain traceability requirements apply.']
  kpi_target_value decimal(10,4) [note: 'Numeric target threshold that the 3PL partner must achieve for the specified SLA type. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 98.5 for 98.5% OTIF) or absolute rate depending on sla_type. This is the contractually committed performance level.']
  kpi_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the kpi_target_value and any associated threshold values. Ensures unambiguous interpretation of numeric SLA targets across different SLA types (e.g., percent for OTIF, degrees_celsius for temperature compliance).']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent formal compliance or performance audit conducted at the 3PL partner\'s DC node under this SLA. Used to track audit cadence and identify overdue assessments.']
  measurement_frequency varchar [note: 'Cadence at which the 3PL partner\'s performance against this SLA is formally measured and reported. Drives the scheduling of performance scorecards and billing reconciliation cycles.']
  minimum_acceptable_value decimal(10,4) [note: 'Floor threshold below which the 3PL partner\'s performance triggers a penalty clause or escalation. Distinct from the target value — represents the contractual breach boundary rather than the aspirational goal.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next formal SLA performance review between Food Beverage and the 3PL partner. Derived from review_cadence but stored explicitly to support calendar-driven workflow triggers and procurement planning.']
  order_accuracy_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Contractually committed order accuracy target expressed as a percentage of orders fulfilled without errors (wrong item, wrong quantity, wrong destination). Specific to order_accuracy SLA type. Supports fulfillment quality management.']
  otif_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Contractually committed OTIF delivery performance target expressed as a percentage. Specific to OTIF-type SLAs. Captures the percentage of orders that must be delivered on time and in full per measurement period. Distinct from the generic kpi_target_value to enable direct OTIF reporting.']
  penalty_structure_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the financial penalty mechanism applied when the 3PL partner fails to meet the minimum acceptable KPI value. fixed_fee = flat dollar amount; percentage_of_invoice = deduction from billing; credit_note = issued credit; service_credit = applied to future invoices; none = no financial penalty.']
  penalty_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Numeric value of the penalty applied per measurement period when the 3PL partner breaches the minimum acceptable KPI threshold. Interpreted in context of penalty_structure_type (e.g., dollar amount for fixed_fee, percentage for percentage_of_invoice).']
  renewal_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days prior to expiry_date by which either party must provide written notice of intent not to renew. Applicable when auto_renewal_flag is True. Drives contract management alerts and procurement workflows.']
  review_cadence varchar [note: 'Frequency at which formal business reviews of this SLA are conducted between Food Beverage and the 3PL partner. Governs the scheduling of QBRs (Quarterly Business Reviews) and contract renewal discussions.']
  rotation_policy varchar [note: 'Contractually mandated inventory rotation method the 3PL partner must apply at the DC node. FIFO = First In First Out; FEFO = First Expired First Out (preferred for perishable food and beverage products); LIFO = Last In First Out; none = no rotation requirement.']
  sla_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier for this SLA record, used in contract documents, performance reviews, and EDI communications with the 3PL partner. Follows the format SLA-[alphanumeric code].']
  sla_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the SLA record. Active = currently in force; suspended = temporarily paused (e.g., force majeure); expired = past contract end date; pending_renewal = under renegotiation; terminated = ended before natural expiry; draft = not yet executed.']
  sla_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the SLA by the performance dimension it governs. OTIF = On Time In Full delivery; order_accuracy = correctness of order fulfillment; inventory_accuracy = cycle count accuracy; throughput = units processed per period; temperature_compliance = cold chain adherence; damage_rate = product damage incidence.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this SLA record was ingested into the Silver Layer lakehouse. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and audit traceability across the enterprise integration landscape.']
  temperature_max_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable storage or transport temperature in degrees Celsius for cold chain SLA compliance. Applicable when is_cold_chain is True. Exceedances trigger penalty clauses and food safety escalation protocols.']
  temperature_min_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum allowable storage or transport temperature in degrees Celsius for cold chain SLA compliance. Applicable when is_cold_chain is True. Used to define the contractual temperature band for chilled or frozen product handling.']
  throughput_target_units_per_day decimal(12,2) [note: 'Contractually committed daily throughput capacity target in units (cases or eaches) that the 3PL partner must sustain at the DC node. Applicable to throughput-type SLAs. Supports capacity planning and peak season readiness assessments.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this SLA record. Used to detect changes, support incremental data loads, and maintain audit trail integrity in the Silver Layer.']

  Note: 'Third-party logistics (3PL) and logistics partner service level agreement record defining performance commitments for each DC or warehouse node operated by an external partner. Captures provider name, DC node reference, SLA type (OTIF, order accuracy, inventory accuracy, throughput, temperature compliance for cold chain), target KPI value, measurement frequency, penalty/bonus structure, contract term, and review cadence. Governs 3PL performance management, billing reconciliation, and contract renewal decisions. Note: primary key is partner_sla_id.'
}

Table supply.plan_version {
  plan_version_id varchar [pk, note: 'Business-facing identifier for the plan version, used to reference this version from supply_plan and demand_plan records. Enables cross-product linkage during the FK resolution step.']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the individual who approved or rejected this plan version. Required for S&OP governance and audit trail.']
  ibp_scenario_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the SAP IBP scenario (e.g., base case, upside, downside) associated with this plan version. Enables what-if scenario comparison across S&OP iterations.']
  previous_version_plan_version_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the immediately preceding plan version within the same planning cycle. Enables direct plan-vs-plan variance analysis and version lineage tracing for IBP governance.']
  primary_plan_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supply planner who created or owns this plan version. Supports accountability and audit trail for IBP governance.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: Each Supply Plan Version reflects the version of the product roadmap from an R&D project; linking enables version‑controlled supply forecasting.']
  sop_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the S&OP / IBP planning cycle that produced this version. Links the version to the formal monthly or weekly IBP governance cycle.']
  allocation_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether supply allocation across customers or channels is required in this plan version due to constrained supply. Triggers allocation management processes in SAP IBP or S/4HANA.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of this plan version within the S&OP governance process. Draft versions are in progress; pending_review awaits sign-off; approved versions are the active baseline; superseded versions have been replaced by a newer version.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this plan version was formally approved by the designated approver. Null if not yet approved. Critical for S&OP governance audit trail.']
  channel_scope varchar [note: 'Distribution channel scope covered by this plan version (e.g., all channels, retail only, foodservice, direct-to-consumer, export). Supports channel-specific supply planning and trade management alignment.']
  co_packing_included_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether co-packing (contract manufacturing) volumes are included in the planned production and receipts quantities for this version. Relevant for toll manufacturing and co-packer network planning.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year to which this plan version belongs (e.g., P01 through P13 for 13-period calendars). Aligns supply plan versions with financial reporting periods.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year to which this plan version primarily belongs (e.g., FY2025). Used for financial alignment of supply plans with budgeting and EBITDA reporting cycles.']
  forecast_accuracy_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Weighted mean absolute percentage error (WMAPE) or equivalent forecast accuracy metric used as a planning assumption for this version. Informs safety stock and buffer inventory parameters.']
  horizon_end_date date [note: 'The last date of the planning horizon covered by this version. Together with horizon_start_date, defines the full temporal scope of the supply plan.']
  horizon_start_date date [note: 'The first date of the planning horizon covered by this version. Defines the temporal scope of the supply plan snapshot.']
  is_active_version boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is the currently active (latest approved) version of the supply plan for the given planning cycle. Only one version per planning cycle should be active at any time.']
  is_baseline boolean [note: 'Flags this version as the official baseline plan for the planning cycle, used as the reference point for variance analysis and performance measurement against actuals.']
  plan_creation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when this plan version snapshot was created in the system. Serves as the principal business event timestamp for the version record and supports audit trail for IBP governance.']
  plan_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the supply plan (e.g., SP-2024-000123). Used in cross-functional communications, ERP references, and EDI exchanges.']
  plan_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic or organizational scope of this plan version. Indicates whether the version covers the full global network, a regional subset, a national market, the DC network, or a single plant.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the supply plan by planning horizon and purpose. Operational plans cover short-term execution; tactical cover mid-term; strategic cover long-range network design. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: operational|tactical|strategic|contingency|baseline — promote to reference product if additional types emerge]']
  planning_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency cadence of the planning cycle that generated this version (e.g., monthly S&OP, weekly demand sensing refresh). Drives scheduling of IBP governance reviews.']
  publish_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this plan version was published to downstream execution systems (SAP S/4HANA, MES, WMS). Null if not yet published. Supports SLA tracking for plan-to-execution lead time.']
  published_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this plan version has been published and communicated to downstream stakeholders (e.g., manufacturing, procurement, logistics). Published versions trigger execution workflows.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation provided by the approver when rejecting this plan version. Null if the version was not rejected. Supports governance audit trail and continuous improvement of the S&OP process.']
  service_level_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target fill rate or OTIF (On Time In Full) service level percentage used as a planning input for this version (e.g., 98.5%). Drives safety stock calculations and replenishment signal parameters.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this plan version was originated or extracted (e.g., SAP_IBP, JDA_BLUE_YONDER). Supports data lineage and Silver layer provenance tracking.']
  supply_constraint_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this plan version was generated under known supply constraints (e.g., raw material shortage, capacity bottleneck, supplier disruption). Triggers allocation and prioritization workflows.']
  supply_demand_balance_qty decimal(18,3) [note: 'Net supply-demand balance at the version level (total planned supply minus total demand). Positive values indicate surplus; negative values indicate a supply gap requiring resolution.']
  total_demand_qty decimal(18,3) [note: 'Aggregate consensus demand volume across all SKUs and channels captured in this plan version. Enables plan-vs-plan demand variance analysis across S&OP cycles.']
  total_planned_production_qty decimal(18,3) [note: 'Aggregate planned production volume across all SKUs and plants captured in this plan version. Key volume metric for S&OP executive review and capacity utilization analysis.']
  total_planned_receipts_qty decimal(18,3) [note: 'Aggregate planned inbound receipts (from suppliers, co-packers, and toll manufacturers) across all SKUs and DCs captured in this plan version. Supports supply-demand balancing at the version level.']
  total_planned_shipments_qty decimal(18,3) [note: 'Aggregate planned outbound shipment volume across all SKUs and DCs in this plan version. Used for OTIF target-setting and distribution network capacity planning.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this plan version record was last modified. Supports change tracking and data lineage in the Silver layer lakehouse.']
  version_label varchar [note: 'Human-readable label for this version (e.g., \'Baseline\', \'Consensus\', \'Final\', \'Pre-S&OP\', \'Executive Review\'). Used in reporting and S&OP presentations to distinguish versions.']
  version_locked_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this plan version has been locked and is no longer editable. Locked versions serve as immutable reference points for variance analysis and regulatory audit purposes.']
  version_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes from the planner describing key assumptions, constraints, or changes incorporated in this version (e.g., \'Adjusted for Q3 promotional uplift\', \'Reflects raw material shortage at Plant A\').']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential integer version number within a planning cycle (e.g., 1, 2, 3). Increments each time a new snapshot is created for the same planning cycle, enabling plan-vs-plan variance analysis.']
  volume_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure applied to all volume quantity fields in this plan version (e.g., CS for cases, EA for eaches, KG for kilograms). Ensures consistent interpretation of volume metrics across planning systems. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CS|EA|KG|LB|L|GAL|MT — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Versioned snapshot of a supply plan at a specific point in time, enabling comparison across planning cycles and S&OP iterations. Captures version number, plan creation timestamp, planning cycle reference, planner, approval status, and key volume metrics (total planned production, total planned receipts). Supports plan-vs-plan variance analysis and audit trail for IBP governance.'
}

Table supply.allocation_plan {
  allocation_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the allocation plan record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer receiving the allocated quantity.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who approved the allocation plan.']
  promotion_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trade.promotion_plan. Business justification: Allocation plans may reserve inventory for upcoming promotions; linking supports the Promotion‑Driven Allocation report.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the SKU to which the allocation applies.']
  superseded_allocation_plan_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on allocation_plan (superseded_allocation_plan_id)']
  supply_plan_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.plan_version. Business justification: Allocation plan is versioned; linking to the supply plan version provides hierarchy and removes redundant version fields.']
  allocated_quantity decimal(18,2) [note: 'Quantity of the SKU that is allocated to the customer for the plan horizon.']
  allocation_horizon_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the allocation plan covers from its effective start date.']
  allocation_method varchar [note: 'Specific algorithm or rule used to compute the allocation (e.g., fair share, priority).']
  allocation_plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the allocation plan.']
  allocation_quantity_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for allocated and demanded quantities.']
  allocation_reason varchar [note: 'Business driver for the allocation (e.g., shortage, new product launch).']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the allocation plan was approved.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Sales or distribution channel for which the allocation is defined.']
  contractual_commitment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the allocation is driven by a contractual commitment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the allocation plan record was created in the system.']
  demand_class varchar [note: 'Classification of demand intensity for the SKU‑customer pair.']
  demanded_quantity decimal(18,2) [note: 'Customer‑projected demand quantity for the SKU during the allocation horizon.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the allocation plan becomes effective for execution.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the allocation plan expires or is superseded; null for open‑ended plans.']
  forecast_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the demand forecast used for the allocation (e.g., statistical, consensus).']
  is_locked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the plan is locked from further edits.']
  plan_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or rationale for the allocation decisions.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the plan based on the allocation strategy applied.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Numeric priority used when the method is priority‑based; lower numbers indicate higher priority.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter code representing the geographic region or market.']
  replenishment_signal varchar [note: 'Signal indicating if replenishment is triggered by this allocation.']
  revenue_weight decimal(10,4) [note: 'Weight factor based on the customer\'s revenue contribution, used in revenue‑weighted allocations.']
  safety_stock_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether safety stock considerations are applied in the allocation.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that originated the allocation plan record.']
  tier varchar [note: 'Business tier classification of the customer for allocation weighting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the allocation plan record.']

  Note: 'Allocation plan defining how constrained supply is distributed across customers, channels, and regions during shortage or launch situations. Captures allocation method (fair-share, priority-based, revenue-weighted, contractual commitment), allocation horizon, affected SKUs, customer tier assignments, allocated vs. demanded quantities, allocation effective dates, and approval status. Critical during supply disruptions, new product launches, seasonal peaks, and promotional surges in F&B where demand routinely exceeds available supply.'
}

Table distribution.center {
  center_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the distribution center. Primary key for the distribution center master record.']
  price_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_zone. Business justification: Links each distribution center to its pricing zone, supporting regional price‑zone based pricing strategies and zone‑specific cost allocation reports.']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for the distribution center physical location including street number and name.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for additional location details such as building, suite, or unit number. Nullable.']
  ambient_zone_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the distribution center has ambient temperature storage zones for dry goods and shelf-stable products.']
  center_name varchar [note: 'Official business name of the distribution center facility.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality where the distribution center is located.']
  contact_email_address varchar [note: 'Primary contact email address for the distribution center facility for operational communications and coordination.']
  contact_phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the distribution center facility for operational coordination and emergency contact.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the distribution center location (e.g., USA, CAN, MEX).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the distribution center record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage and audit trail.']
  cross_dock_capable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the distribution center has cross-docking capabilities to transfer products directly from inbound to outbound without storage.']
  cubic_capacity decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total cubic footage storage capacity of the distribution center for volumetric capacity planning.']
  dc_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the distribution center used across operational systems and external communications. Typically alphanumeric facility code.']
  direct_store_delivery_hub_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the distribution center serves as a hub for Direct Store Delivery operations to retail locations.']
  dock_door_count varchar [note: 'Total number of loading dock doors available for inbound and outbound shipments. Critical for throughput capacity analysis.']
  ecommerce_fulfillment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the distribution center handles direct-to-consumer e-commerce order fulfillment and last-mile delivery.']
  erp_plant_code varchar [note: 'Plant or location code in the ERP system (SAP S/4HANA) representing this distribution center for financial and supply chain transactions.']
  facility_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the distribution center based on primary storage and handling capability. Ambient for dry goods, refrigerated for chilled products, frozen for sub-zero storage, multi-temperature for mixed zones, cross-dock for flow-through operations, consolidation for order aggregation.']
  frozen_zone_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the distribution center has frozen storage zones for products requiring sub-zero temperature storage.']
  gfsi_certification_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the current GFSI certification. Used for compliance monitoring and audit scheduling.']
  gfsi_certification_scheme varchar [note: 'Specific GFSI-recognized certification scheme held by the facility (e.g., SQF Level 2, BRC Storage and Distribution, FSSC 22000). Nullable if not certified. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SQF|BRC|FSSC_22000|IFS|GLOBALG.A.P.|GRMS|PrimusGFS|CanadaGAP — promote to reference product]']
  gfsi_certification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of GFSI-recognized certification (e.g., SQF, BRC, FSSC 22000) for the distribution center. Critical for food safety compliance and customer requirements.']
  haccp_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the distribution center has HACCP certification for food safety management. Required for facilities handling high-risk food products.']
  inbound_dock_door_count varchar [note: 'Number of dock doors dedicated to receiving inbound shipments from suppliers and manufacturing plants.']
  iso_9001_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the distribution center has ISO 9001 quality management system certification.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the distribution center record was last updated in the system. Used for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the distribution center in decimal degrees for geospatial analysis and route optimization.']
  lease_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the facility lease agreement for leased distribution centers. Nullable for owned facilities. Used for real estate planning and renewal management.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the distribution center in decimal degrees for geospatial analysis and route optimization.']
  operating_hours_weekday varchar [note: 'Standard operating hours for weekdays (Monday-Friday) in format HH:MM-HH:MM (e.g., 06:00-22:00). Used for scheduling and service level planning.']
  operating_hours_weekend varchar [note: 'Standard operating hours for weekends (Saturday-Sunday) in format HH:MM-HH:MM. Nullable if facility does not operate on weekends.']
  operational_start_date date [note: 'Date when the distribution center began operational activities and started processing inventory and shipments.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the distribution center in its lifecycle. Active indicates full operations, inactive for non-operational but maintained, under construction for facilities being built, decommissioned for permanently closed, seasonal for periodic operations, temporarily closed for short-term shutdowns.']
  outbound_dock_door_count varchar [note: 'Number of dock doors dedicated to shipping outbound orders to customers, retail stores, and distribution points.']
  ownership_model varchar [note: 'Ownership and operational control model for the distribution center. Company-owned for internal facilities, third-party logistics (3PL) for outsourced operations, leased for rented facilities with internal operations, co-managed for hybrid arrangements.']
  pallet_position_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of pallet positions available for storage in the distribution center. Key metric for inventory capacity planning.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the distribution center location used for shipping and logistics routing.']
  primary_service_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region or market area primarily served by this distribution center for distribution network planning and territory management.']
  refrigerated_zone_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the distribution center has refrigerated storage zones for chilled products requiring cold chain management.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or primary administrative division where the distribution center is located.']
  storage_square_footage decimal(12,2) [note: 'Square footage dedicated to product storage areas excluding staging, dock, and administrative space.']
  temperature_zone_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct temperature-controlled zones within the distribution center for managing products with different storage requirements.']
  third_party_logistics_provider varchar [note: 'Name of the third-party logistics provider managing the facility if ownership model is 3PL or co-managed. Null for company-owned facilities.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the distribution center location used for scheduling and operational time calculations (e.g., America/New_York, America/Chicago).']
  total_square_footage decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total building square footage of the distribution center facility including all storage, staging, and administrative areas.']
  twenty_four_seven_operation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the distribution center operates 24 hours a day, 7 days a week for continuous fulfillment operations.']
  wms_system_identifier varchar [note: 'Identifier for the Warehouse Management System instance managing this distribution center. Used for system integration and data reconciliation.']

  Note: 'Master record for each Distribution Center (DC) in the F&B network — physical facility attributes including location, type (ambient, refrigerated, frozen, cross-dock), storage capacity (pallet positions, cubic footage), temperature zones, dock door count, WMS system identifier, 3PL-managed flag, FDA facility registration number, GFSI certification status, and operational hours. SSOT for DC identity across the distribution domain.'
}

Table distribution.carrier {
  carrier_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the transportation carrier. Primary key for the carrier master record.']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the transportation carrier organization as registered with regulatory authorities.']
  carrier_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the carrier in the approved carrier network. Controls eligibility for load assignment.']
  carrier_type varchar [note: 'Classification of carrier business model: asset-based (owns equipment), non-asset-based (brokerage), third-party logistics provider, private fleet (company-owned), or Direct Store Delivery fleet.']
  claims_ratio_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of shipments resulting in damage, loss, or shortage claims over the past 12 months. Used for carrier risk assessment.']
  cold_chain_capable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the carrier has temperature-controlled equipment and processes certified for transporting refrigerated and frozen food products.']
  contracted_rate_tier varchar [note: 'Rate tier classification based on contracted pricing agreement. Tier 1 represents preferred pricing, spot market represents non-contracted rates.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the carrier master record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage and audit trail.']
  dispatch_phone varchar [note: '24/7 dispatch phone number for load tendering, driver coordination, and real-time shipment tracking.']
  dot_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier issued by the US Department of Transportation Federal Motor Carrier Safety Administration for interstate commercial carriers.']
  edi_capable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the carrier supports EDI transaction sets for load tendering, status updates, and Advanced Shipping Notice (ASN) processing.']
  edi_protocol varchar [note: 'Technical protocol used for electronic data interchange with the carrier: ANSI X12, EDIFACT, API integration, or none.']
  fleet_size varchar [note: 'Total number of power units (tractors, trucks) operated by the carrier. Used for capacity planning and carrier capability assessment.']
  fsma_certification_date date [note: 'Date when the carrier received FSMA Sanitary Transportation compliance certification. Used to track recertification cycles.']
  fsma_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the carrier meets FSMA Sanitary Transportation of Human and Animal Food requirements including temperature control, equipment sanitation, and training documentation.']
  fsma_expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the carriers FSMA Sanitary Transportation compliance certification. Triggers recertification workflow.']
  headquarters_address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for the carriers corporate headquarters location.']
  headquarters_city varchar [note: 'City where the carriers corporate headquarters is located.']
  headquarters_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the carriers headquarters location (e.g., USA, CAN, MEX).']
  headquarters_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the carriers corporate headquarters location.']
  headquarters_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province code where the carriers corporate headquarters is located.']
  insurance_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Reference number for the carriers liability and cargo insurance certificate on file. Required for carrier qualification.']
  insurance_coverage_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total liability coverage amount in USD provided by the carriers insurance policy. Must meet minimum threshold for carrier approval.']
  insurance_expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the carriers current insurance policy. Carrier status automatically suspended if insurance lapses.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent carrier compliance audit covering safety, food safety, insurance, and operational standards.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the carrier master record was most recently updated. Used for change tracking and data synchronization.']
  mc_number varchar [note: 'Operating authority number issued by FMCSA for for-hire interstate carriers. Required for carriers transporting regulated commodities.']
  next_audit_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next carrier compliance audit. Used to trigger audit planning and carrier notification.']
  onboarding_date date [note: 'Date when the carrier was approved and onboarded into the approved carrier network. Marks the start of the business relationship.']
  otif_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Rolling 12-month percentage of shipments delivered on-time and in-full without damage or shortage. Key performance indicator for carrier evaluation.']
  payment_terms_days varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms in days from invoice date for carrier freight charges (e.g., Net 30, Net 45).']
  performance_tier varchar [note: 'Performance classification based on On-Time In-Full (OTIF) delivery, claims ratio, and service quality metrics. Determines load allocation priority.']
  preferred_carrier_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the carrier is designated as a preferred partner with priority load allocation and strategic relationship status.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary carrier contact for operational communications and load coordination.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary business contact at the carrier organization for operational coordination and issue escalation.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Business phone number of the primary carrier contact for urgent operational issues and load coordination.']
  safety_rating varchar [note: 'Federal Motor Carrier Safety Administration safety rating based on compliance review. Satisfactory rating required for carrier approval.']
  scac_code varchar [note: 'Four-letter unique identifier assigned by the National Motor Freight Traffic Association (NMFTA) for transportation companies. Used in EDI transactions and shipping documentation.']
  service_coverage_region varchar [note: 'Geographic regions where the carrier provides transportation services. Free-text description of coverage area (e.g., US Midwest, Eastern Seaboard, National).']
  service_mode varchar [note: 'Primary mode of transportation service offered by the carrier. Defines equipment type and service level capability. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: truckload|less_than_truckload|parcel|intermodal|refrigerated|dry_van|flatbed|tanker — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  temperature_range_max_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum temperature in Celsius that the carriers refrigerated equipment can maintain. Critical for chilled food distribution.']
  temperature_range_min_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum temperature in Celsius that the carriers refrigerated equipment can maintain. Critical for frozen food distribution.']

  Note: 'Master record for all transportation carriers (TL, LTL, parcel, refrigerated, DSD fleet) used in outbound distribution. Captures SCAC code, DOT/MC number, carrier type, service modes, insurance certificates, FSMA Sanitary Transportation compliance status, cold chain capability flags, EDI capability, contracted rate tier, and performance tier classification. SSOT for carrier identity in the distribution domain.'
}

Table distribution.delivery_route {
  delivery_route_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the delivery route. Primary key for the delivery route entity.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific vehicle (truck, trailer, van) assigned to this route execution. Populated for execution records, may be null for route master templates.']
  center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the distribution center or warehouse that serves as the origin point for this route. All deliveries on this route originate from this DC.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the driver assigned to execute this route instance. Populated for execution records, may be null for route master templates.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: Supports Route Planning for R&D sample kit deliveries, required by the Sample Distribution Schedule.']
  territory_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales or distribution territory to which this route is assigned. Used for territory management and sales alignment.']
  actual_mileage decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual total mileage driven during route execution, captured from vehicle telematics or odometer readings. Used for cost analysis and route optimization.']
  cases_delivered varchar [note: 'Total number of product cases successfully delivered to customers during route execution. Used for fill rate calculation and revenue recognition.']
  cases_loaded varchar [note: 'Total number of product cases loaded onto the vehicle at route start. Used for inventory reconciliation and load planning analysis.']
  cases_refused varchar [note: 'Total number of product cases refused by customers during delivery due to quality issues, order discrepancies, or receiving problems. Subset of cases_returned.']
  cases_returned varchar [note: 'Total number of product cases returned to the distribution center undelivered. Includes unsold inventory from DSD routes and refused deliveries.']
  cash_collected decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total cash payments collected from customers during route execution. Applicable primarily to DSD routes with cash-on-delivery transactions. Used for cash reconciliation.']
  check_collected decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total check payments collected from customers during route execution. Applicable primarily to DSD routes. Used for accounts receivable reconciliation.']
  cold_chain_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this route requires temperature-controlled (refrigerated or frozen) transportation to maintain product quality and food safety compliance. True if any stop requires cold chain.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this route record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this route master definition became active and available for scheduling. Used for route lifecycle management and historical analysis.']
  end_time timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when the driver returned to the distribution center and completed route execution. Used for route duration analysis and driver shift management.']
  estimated_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Planned total duration for route completion in minutes, including drive time, stop time, and loading/unloading. Used for driver scheduling and shift planning.']
  estimated_mileage decimal(10,2) [note: 'Planned total mileage for the route in miles, based on stop sequence and routing optimization. Used for cost estimation and vehicle assignment.']
  execution_date date [note: 'The specific date on which this route instance was executed. For route execution records, this is the primary business event date. For route master records, this field may be null.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when this route master definition is scheduled to be retired or replaced. Null for routes with no planned end date. Used for route lifecycle management.']
  fill_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of ordered cases that were successfully delivered on this route. Calculated as (cases_delivered / cases_loaded) * 100. Key metric for order fulfillment performance.']
  frequency varchar [note: 'Scheduled frequency at which this route is executed. DAILY for every business day, WEEKLY for once per week, BIWEEKLY for every two weeks, MONTHLY for once per month, ON_DEMAND for ad-hoc execution.']
  fuel_consumed_gallons decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total fuel consumed during route execution in gallons. Captured from vehicle telematics or fuel card transactions. Used for cost allocation and sustainability reporting.']
  handheld_device_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the mobile handheld device (scanner, tablet, or mobile computer) used by the driver for proof of delivery, order capture, and route tracking.']
  hazmat_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this route transports hazardous materials requiring special handling, placarding, and driver certification per DOT regulations.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this route record was last updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  otif_performance_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of deliveries on this route that met both on-time and in-full criteria. Key performance indicator for distribution service level. Calculated as (on-time and in-full deliveries / total planned deliveries) * 100.']
  route_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the route, used for operational reference and scheduling. Typically alphanumeric code assigned by transportation management system.']
  route_completion_status varchar [note: 'Overall completion status of the route execution instance. COMPLETED indicates all planned stops were attempted, PARTIAL indicates some stops were skipped, CANCELLED indicates route was aborted, IN_PROGRESS indicates route is currently being executed, NOT_STARTED indicates route has not yet begun.']
  route_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or description of the route, often including geographic or customer references (e.g., \'Downtown Chicago Loop\', \'Northeast Territory A\').']
  route_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the route template. ACTIVE routes are in regular operation, INACTIVE are temporarily not in use, SUSPENDED are on hold pending review, PLANNED are designed but not yet operational, RETIRED are permanently discontinued.']
  route_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the route based on delivery model. DSD (Direct Store Delivery) for direct-to-retail routes, DC_TO_RETAIL for distribution center to retail, FOODSERVICE for restaurant/institutional delivery, DTC for direct-to-consumer, CROSS_DOCK for cross-docking operations, BACKHAUL for return logistics.']
  scheduled_days_of_week varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of days when route is scheduled to run (e.g., \'MON,WED,FRI\'). Applicable for WEEKLY and BIWEEKLY frequency routes.']
  start_time timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when the driver departed the distribution center to begin route execution. Used for OTIF (On Time In Full) performance tracking.']
  stop_count varchar [note: 'Number of planned delivery stops on this route. Used for route complexity assessment and driver workload planning.']
  stops_completed varchar [note: 'Number of delivery stops successfully completed during this route execution. Used to calculate route completion rate and identify service failures.']
  stops_planned varchar [note: 'Number of delivery stops planned for this route execution instance. Used to calculate route completion rate.']
  temperature_range_max_f decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum acceptable temperature in Fahrenheit for cold chain routes. Used for temperature monitoring and compliance verification. Null for non-cold-chain routes.']
  temperature_range_min_f decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum acceptable temperature in Fahrenheit for cold chain routes. Used for temperature monitoring and compliance verification. Null for non-cold-chain routes.']
  vehicle_type_required varchar [note: 'Type of vehicle required to execute this route based on cargo volume, weight, and special handling requirements. Determines vehicle assignment from fleet.']

  Note: 'Master definition and execution record for recurring outbound delivery routes used in DSD (Direct Store Delivery) and DC-to-customer distribution. Master attributes (relatively static, updated infrequently): route code, route type (DSD, DC-to-retail, foodservice, DTC), assigned territory, stop sequence template, estimated mileage, frequency, vehicle type requirement, cold chain requirement, and active status. Execution attributes (one record per route-day execution instance): execution date, driver, vehicle, start/end times, stops planned vs completed, cases loaded/delivered/returned/refused, cash/check collections, handheld device ID, and route completion status. The master portion defines the route template; execution instances capture daily operational performance against that template. SSOT for all route definition and route execution data in the distribution domain, including DSD operational performance. Supports route optimization, driver assignment, territory management, and DSD operational reporting.'
}

Table distribution.fulfillment_order {
  fulfillment_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the fulfillment order record. Primary key for the fulfillment order entity representing a customer order released to the distribution center for picking, packing, and shipping.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account receiving this fulfillment. Identifies the buyer or consignee for the order.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Order attribution to marketing campaigns is required for sales‑to‑campaign performance reporting.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the transportation carrier or third-party logistics (3PL) provider assigned to transport this order. Used for carrier performance tracking and freight cost allocation.']
  center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the distribution center or warehouse facility responsible for fulfilling this order. Identifies the source location for inventory picking and shipping operations.']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Captures the employee who created the order, supporting auditability and accountability in order processing.']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list. Business justification: Required for the Order Pricing Audit report, which tracks which price list version was applied to each fulfillment order for compliance and margin analysis.']
  production_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_order. Business justification: Traceability report links each fulfillment order to the production order that created the shipped product.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Assigns revenue from each fulfillment order to a profit center for profitability analysis and reporting.']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the delivery route to which this fulfillment order has been assigned. Used for Direct Store Delivery (DSD) route optimization and last-mile logistics planning.']
  sales_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating sales order that triggered this fulfillment order. Links the distribution execution back to the commercial transaction.']
  ship_to_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the destination location where goods will be delivered. May represent a retail store, distribution center, foodservice location, or direct-to-consumer address.']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the consolidated shipment that includes this fulfillment order. Multiple orders may be combined into a single shipment for transportation efficiency.']
  wave_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the picking wave or batch to which this fulfillment order has been assigned. Waves group multiple orders for efficient picking and resource optimization.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'The actual date the order was delivered to the customer location. Captured from proof of delivery (POD) or carrier tracking systems. Used for OTIF performance measurement.']
  actual_ship_date date [note: 'The actual date the order shipped from the distribution center. Used for OTIF performance measurement and variance analysis against planned and requested dates.']
  asn_number varchar [note: 'Advanced Shipping Notice document number transmitted to the customer via Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) prior to shipment arrival. Enables customer receiving preparation and inventory planning.']
  asn_sent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time the Advanced Shipping Notice was transmitted to the customer. Used for EDI compliance tracking and customer service level measurement.']
  cold_chain_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this fulfillment order contains temperature-sensitive products requiring cold chain compliance throughout storage and transportation. True indicates refrigerated or frozen handling required; false indicates ambient temperature handling.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time this fulfillment order record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  cross_dock_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this fulfillment order is designated for cross-docking operations, where goods move directly from receiving to shipping without intermediate storage. True indicates cross-dock handling; false indicates standard warehouse flow with putaway and storage.']
  delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time the order was delivered to the customer. Captured from proof of delivery or carrier tracking. Used for OTIF measurement and service level compliance.']
  exception_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the type of exception or disruption encountered during fulfillment execution. Examples include inventory shortage, damaged product, carrier delay, address error, or customer request change. Used for root cause analysis and process improvement.']
  exception_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the exception or disruption encountered during fulfillment. Provides context for resolution actions and root cause investigation.']
  exception_resolution_status varchar [note: 'Current status of exception resolution efforts. Open indicates exception has been identified but not yet addressed; in_progress indicates resolution actions are underway; resolved indicates exception has been successfully resolved; escalated indicates exception requires management intervention.']
  fill_rate_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of the order quantity that was successfully fulfilled and shipped. Calculated as shipped quantity divided by ordered quantity. Used for service level measurement and inventory availability analysis.']
  fulfillment_channel varchar [note: 'The business channel through which this order is being fulfilled. Determines handling requirements, service level expectations, and routing logic. Retail represents traditional grocery and mass merchandiser channels; foodservice represents restaurant and institutional channels; direct-to-consumer (DTC) represents online orders shipped to individual consumers; direct store delivery (DSD) represents orders delivered directly to retail stores bypassing DC consolidation.']
  fulfillment_order_number varchar [note: 'Business-readable unique identifier for the fulfillment order. Used for operational tracking, communication with warehouse staff, and customer service inquiries.']
  fulfillment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the fulfillment order within the distribution execution process. Released indicates order is available for wave assignment; wave_assigned indicates order is assigned to a picking wave; picking indicates order is actively being picked; picked indicates picking is complete; packing indicates order is being packed; packed indicates packing is complete; staged indicates order is staged for loading; loaded indicates order is loaded on carrier vehicle; shipped indicates order has departed the DC; in_transit indicates order is en route to destination; delivered indicates order has been received by customer; exception indicates order has encountered a problem requiring intervention; cancelled indicates order has been cancelled. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: released|wave_assigned|picking|picked|packing|packed|staged|loaded|shipped|in_transit|delivered|exception|cancelled — 13 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  hazmat_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this fulfillment order contains hazardous materials requiring special handling, labeling, and transportation compliance. True indicates hazmat present; false indicates no hazmat.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time this fulfillment order record was last updated. Used for change tracking and data synchronization.']
  order_release_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time the fulfillment order was released from the order management system to the warehouse management system for execution. Marks the start of the fulfillment lifecycle.']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the fulfillment order based on handling and processing requirements. Standard represents normal replenishment orders; rush represents expedited orders requiring priority handling; cross-dock represents orders that bypass storage and move directly from receiving to shipping; drop-ship represents orders shipped directly from supplier to customer; sample represents non-commercial product samples; promotional represents orders tied to marketing campaigns or trade promotions.']
  otif_target_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this fulfillment order is included in On-Time In-Full performance measurement. True indicates order is subject to OTIF service level agreements; false indicates order is excluded from OTIF metrics (e.g., sample orders, internal transfers).']
  picking_complete_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when all picking operations were completed for this fulfillment order. Used for cycle time analysis and throughput measurement.']
  picking_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when picking operations began for this fulfillment order. Used for cycle time analysis and warehouse productivity measurement.']
  planned_ship_date date [note: 'The date the distribution center plans to ship the order based on capacity, inventory availability, and carrier schedules. May differ from requested ship date due to operational constraints.']
  priority_code varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the fulfillment order for warehouse resource allocation and sequencing. Critical represents emergency orders requiring immediate processing; high represents time-sensitive orders with tight delivery windows; normal represents standard priority orders; low represents orders that can be delayed if capacity constraints exist.']
  promised_delivery_date date [note: 'The date committed to the customer for delivery of the order. Used for On-Time In-Full (OTIF) performance measurement and customer service level tracking.']
  requested_ship_date date [note: 'The date the customer or sales order requested the order to ship from the distribution center. Used for planning and scheduling warehouse operations.']
  ship_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time the order departed the distribution center. Used for transit time calculation and carrier performance tracking.']
  total_order_cases varchar [note: 'The total number of cases or cartons in the fulfillment order. Used for warehouse productivity measurement, handling cost allocation, and load planning.']
  total_order_lines varchar [note: 'The total number of distinct line items or SKUs in the fulfillment order. Used for picking complexity assessment and labor planning.']
  total_order_pallets decimal(8,2) [note: 'The total number of pallets in the fulfillment order, including partial pallets expressed as decimal values. Used for transportation planning, dock scheduling, and freight cost calculation.']
  total_order_volume_m3 decimal(12,3) [note: 'The total cubic volume of the fulfillment order in cubic meters. Used for warehouse space planning, vehicle capacity utilization, and freight cost calculation.']
  total_order_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'The total gross weight of the fulfillment order in kilograms, including product and packaging. Used for carrier selection, freight cost calculation, and load planning.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-provided tracking number for shipment visibility and proof of delivery. Used for customer service inquiries and delivery status monitoring.']

  Note: 'Core outbound fulfillment order record representing a customer order released to the DC for picking, packing, and shipping. Captures fulfillment order number, source sales order reference, customer account, ship-to location, requested ship date, promised delivery date, fulfillment channel (retail, foodservice, DTC, DSD), order priority, wave/batch assignment, cross-dock flag, fulfillment status (released, wave-assigned, picking, packed, staged, shipped, delivered, exception), OTIF target flag, total order weight/cube, and any exception/disruption codes with resolution status. SSOT for outbound order execution lifecycle in the distribution domain.'
}

Table distribution.fulfillment_order_line {
  fulfillment_order_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the fulfillment order line item. Primary key for this entity.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse associate who picked this line. Used for labor tracking and picker performance analysis.']
  fulfillment_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent fulfillment order header. Links this line to its parent order document.']
  ingredient_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ingredient.lot. Business justification: Recall Management Process: order lines need the exact ingredient lot to enable precise product recalls.']
  pallet_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the pallet or shipping unit containing this line item. Used for pallet-level tracking and cross-docking operations.']
  price_list_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list_line. Business justification: Captures the exact price list line used for each order line, enabling line‑level margin calculations and price change impact reporting.']
  production_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_order. Business justification: Line‑level traceability ties each order line to the specific production order that produced the SKU.']
  promotion_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trade.promotion_line. Business justification: Each order line may be driven by a specific promotion line; linking enables accurate promotion ROI and claim reconciliation.']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.shipment. Business justification: Partial shipments require linking each line to the shipment it was shipped on; many lines per shipment, each line may appear on multiple shipments, so child side gets FK.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the master product record. Links this line to the product master data for detailed product attributes.']
  allocated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when inventory was allocated to this line. Marks the beginning of the fulfillment process.']
  backorder_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether unfulfilled quantity from this line was placed on backorder for future fulfillment. Used for backorder management and customer service.']
  carton_count varchar [note: 'Number of cartons or cases used to fulfill this line. Used for packaging and freight calculation.']
  catch_weight_actual decimal(15,3) [note: 'Actual weight for catch-weight items where weight varies by unit. Used for variable-weight products like fresh produce, meat, or cheese.']
  catch_weight_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for catch weight. Defines whether weight is expressed in pounds, kilograms, ounces, or grams.']
  confirmed_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of the SKU confirmed for fulfillment and actually picked. May differ from ordered quantity due to inventory availability or allocation rules.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this fulfillment order line record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the line amount. Supports multi-currency operations for international distribution.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Product expiration or best-by date for the lot being fulfilled. Used for FEFO picking logic and shelf life management.']
  fefo_pick_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line was picked using FEFO logic to ensure oldest expiration dates are shipped first. Critical for perishable food and beverage products.']
  gtin varchar [note: 'Global trade item number (UPC/EAN) for the product. Standardized identifier used across the supply chain for product identification.']
  hazmat_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the product is classified as hazardous material requiring special handling and shipping compliance. Relevant for certain cleaning chemicals or alcohol products.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number within the fulfillment order. Determines the ordering and position of this line item in the order.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current fulfillment status of the order line. Tracks the line through the warehouse execution lifecycle from allocation to shipment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|allocated|picked|packed|shipped|cancelled|short_shipped — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  line_total_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total monetary value for this line (quantity × unit price). Used for order value calculation and financial reporting.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this fulfillment order line record was last modified. Used for change tracking and audit purposes.']
  ordered_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of the SKU originally ordered by the customer. Represents the requested amount before any adjustments or shortages.']
  original_sku varchar [note: 'Original SKU that was ordered if a substitution was made. Null if no substitution occurred.']
  packed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the line was packed for shipment. Used for packing efficiency and order completion tracking.']
  pick_zone varchar [note: 'Warehouse zone or area where the product was picked. Used for labor planning and zone-based picking strategies.']
  picked_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the line was physically picked from warehouse inventory. Used for pick productivity and cycle time analysis.']
  production_date date [note: 'Date the product was manufactured or packaged. Used for shelf life calculation and quality tracking.']
  promotion_code varchar [note: 'Trade promotion or discount code applied to this line. Used for TPM tracking and promotional effectiveness analysis.']
  quality_inspection_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line underwent quality inspection before shipment. Used for quality assurance and food safety compliance.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number for serialized inventory items. Used for high-value products requiring individual unit tracking.']
  shipped_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Actual quantity of the SKU shipped to the customer. Final quantity that left the distribution center.']
