  shipped_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the line was shipped from the distribution center. Used for OTIF calculation and ASN generation.']
  short_ship_reason_code varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining why the line was short-shipped or not fully fulfilled. Used for fill rate analysis and root cause investigation.']
  sku varchar [note: 'Internal stock keeping unit code identifying the specific product variant being fulfilled. Used for inventory tracking and warehouse operations.']
  substitution_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a product substitution was made for this line due to stockout or customer approval. Used for substitution rate tracking.']
  temperature_zone varchar [note: 'Required temperature zone for the product. Critical for cold chain compliance and proper storage of perishable food and beverage items.']
  unit_price decimal(15,2) [note: 'Price per unit for this line item. Used for invoice generation and revenue recognition.']
  uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the line quantity. Defines whether quantities are expressed in cases, eaches, pallets, or other packaging units.']
  warehouse_location varchar [note: 'Specific warehouse bin or location from which the product was picked. Used for inventory accuracy and slotting optimization.']

  Note: 'Line-level detail for each SKU within a fulfillment order. Captures line number, SKU/GTIN, ordered quantity (cases/eaches), confirmed quantity, UOM, lot number, expiration date, FEFO pick flag, catch-weight actual, line status, and any short-ship reason code. Enables lot traceability and FEFO compliance at the line level.'
}

Table distribution.shipment {
  shipment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the physical shipment record. Primary key for the shipment entity.']
  batch_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.batch_record. Business justification: Regulatory batch traceability links shipments to the originating batch record for recall management.']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Brand KPI report requires each shipment linked to its primary brand for on‑time‑in‑full and cost analysis.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Campaign fulfillment tracking needs to tie shipments generated by a specific marketing campaign to measure lift and ROI.']
  carbon_footprint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.carbon_footprint. Business justification: Required for logistics GHG reporting per shipment (Scope 3 emissions) used in ESG disclosures and carbon accounting.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the transportation carrier responsible for moving the shipment.']
  center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution center or warehouse from which the shipment originates.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Required for freight cost allocation to internal cost centers for accurate expense reporting per shipment.']
  delivery_route_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the delivery route or multi-stop tour that this shipment is part of, used for route optimization and DSD operations.']
  driver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Needed to assign internal drivers to shipments for safety incident tracking and driver performance dashboards.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Needed to post freight expense of each shipment to the appropriate GL account for financial statements.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.plant. Business justification: Logistics planning requires knowing the originating plant for each shipment.']
  price_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_zone. Business justification: Associates shipments with the pricing zone of origin, needed for freight cost allocation and OTIF compliance reporting by zone.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: Required for New Product Launch Shipment Tracking report linking each shipment to its originating R&D project.']
  retailer_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trade.retailer_agreement. Business justification: Compliance reporting requires each shipment to be linked to the retailer agreement governing delivery windows, fees, and penalties.']
  sales_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.order. Business justification: Required for Shipment‑Order reconciliation report used in OTIF compliance; each shipment must be tied to the originating sales order.']
  ship_to_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the destination location (customer site, retail store, or receiving facility) for the shipment.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Inbound receipt of goods from a supplier to a distribution center is tracked in shipments for inventory, quality, and compliance reporting.']
  actual_delivery_datetime timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the shipment was delivered to the destination location, used for OTIF and SLA compliance tracking.']
  actual_pickup_datetime timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the carrier picked up the shipment from the origin DC, used for OTIF performance tracking.']
  asn_sent_datetime timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the ASN was transmitted to the customer, used for EDI compliance and customer notification tracking.']
  asn_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an EDI 856 Advanced Shipping Notice has been transmitted to the customer or receiving facility.']
  case_count varchar [note: 'Total number of cases or cartons included in the shipment across all pallets.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the shipment record was first created in the WMS or TMS system.']
  cross_dock_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this shipment was processed through a cross-docking operation without long-term storage at the DC.']
  delivery_exception_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating any delivery exception or issue encountered during shipment (e.g., damaged product, refused delivery, address error, weather delay).']
  delivery_exception_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes describing any delivery exceptions, issues, or special circumstances encountered during shipment execution.']
  dsd_route_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this shipment is part of a DSD route where products are delivered directly to retail stores, bypassing retailer DCs.']
  freight_cost_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total freight cost charged by the carrier for this shipment, used for freight spend analysis and accrual.']
  freight_cost_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the freight cost amount.']
  hazmat_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment contains hazardous materials requiring special handling, labeling, and carrier certification.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the shipment record was last updated, used for change tracking and data synchronization.']
  loading_sequence varchar [note: 'Instructions or sequence identifier for how pallets should be loaded into the trailer to optimize delivery route and unloading efficiency.']
  max_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable temperature in Celsius for the shipment to maintain product quality and safety during transit.']
  min_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum allowable temperature in Celsius for the shipment to maintain product quality and safety during transit.']
  otif_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment met OTIF performance criteria (delivered on time and with complete order quantity), a key supply chain KPI.']
  pallet_count varchar [note: 'Total number of pallets or handling units included in the shipment.']
  pod_signature_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person who signed for the delivery at the destination location, captured as part of proof of delivery.']
  pod_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the proof of delivery was captured, typically matching or closely following the actual delivery datetime.']
  proof_of_delivery_received_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether proof of delivery documentation (signature, photo, or electronic confirmation) has been received from the carrier or customer.']
  scheduled_delivery_datetime timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time when the shipment is scheduled to arrive at the destination location.']
  scheduled_pickup_datetime timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time when the carrier is scheduled to pick up the shipment from the origin DC.']
  seal_number varchar [note: 'Security seal number applied to the trailer or container to ensure tamper-evidence and cargo integrity during transit.']
  service_level varchar [note: 'The transportation service level or speed tier selected for this shipment (e.g., standard ground, expedited, next-day).']
  shipment_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the shipment, used for tracking and communication with carriers and customers.']
  shipment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the shipment in the outbound logistics workflow.']
  stop_sequence varchar [note: 'The sequence number of this delivery stop within the overall route, used for route planning and driver instructions.']
  temperature_requirement varchar [note: 'Temperature control requirement for the shipment to maintain cold chain compliance and product quality (ambient, refrigerated, frozen, or controlled).']
  temperature_zone_segregation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment contains products requiring segregation into multiple temperature zones within the same trailer.']
  third_party_logistics_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this shipment was fulfilled by a third-party logistics provider rather than a company-owned distribution center.']
  total_cube_m3 decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total volumetric cube of the shipment in cubic meters, used for load planning and carrier billing.']
  total_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total gross weight of the shipment in kilograms, including product, packaging, and pallets.']
  trailer_number varchar [note: 'The trailer, container, or vehicle identification number assigned to this shipment for physical transport.']
  utilization_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of trailer or container capacity utilized by this shipment, calculated based on weight or cube constraints.']

  Note: 'Physical shipment record consolidating one or more fulfillment orders onto a single carrier move, including load configuration details. Captures shipment number, carrier, service level, trailer/container number, seal number, origin DC, destination, scheduled/actual pickup and delivery datetimes, total weight, total cube, pallet count, utilization percentage, loading sequence, freight cost, temperature requirement (ambient/refrigerated/frozen), temperature zone segregation, and shipment status. Supports outbound logistics execution, carrier performance tracking, freight cost management, and cold chain compliance documentation.'
}

Table distribution.shipment_stop {
  shipment_stop_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the shipment stop record. Primary key.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer or location receiving the delivery or providing the pickup. Links to the customer master for account and contact details.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vehicle (truck, van, trailer) used for this stop. Used for vehicle utilization, maintenance scheduling, and cold chain equipment tracking.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the carrier or 3PL (Third-Party Logistics) provider executing this stop. Used for carrier performance tracking and cost allocation.']
  delivery_route_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the planned route or DSD (Direct Store Delivery) route that this stop belongs to. Used for route optimization and performance analysis.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the driver who executed this stop. Used for driver performance tracking, training needs, and labor cost allocation.']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent shipment or route that this stop belongs to. Links to the shipment header for multi-stop delivery routes.']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Required for dock planning and inventory visibility: each shipment stop must be linked to a defined storage location in inventory.']
  supplier_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier_site. Business justification: Each inbound shipment stop occurs at a supplier site; linking enables logistics planning and carrier‑supplier coordination.']
  actual_arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time the vehicle arrived at the stop location. Captured via driver mobile app, GPS telematics, or manual entry. Used for OTIF calculation.']
  actual_cases_delivered varchar [note: 'Number of cases actually delivered and accepted at this stop. Used for fill rate and OTIF measurement.']
  actual_departure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time the vehicle departed from the stop location after completing delivery or pickup. Used for dwell time and route efficiency analysis.']
  actual_volume_m3 decimal(12,3) [note: 'Actual volume in cubic meters of goods delivered or picked up at this stop. May differ from planned due to refusals or adjustments.']
  actual_weight_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Actual weight in kilograms of goods delivered or picked up at this stop. May differ from planned due to refusals or adjustments.']
  asn_number varchar [note: 'Advanced Shipping Notice number sent to the customer prior to delivery via EDI (Electronic Data Interchange). Used for customer receiving preparation and cross-reference.']
  cases_picked_up varchar [note: 'Number of cases picked up from the customer location (e.g., returns, empties, reverse logistics). Applicable when stop_type is pickup or return.']
  cases_refused varchar [note: 'Number of cases refused by the customer at delivery due to damage, temperature excursion, incorrect product, or other quality issues. Triggers return and credit processes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this shipment stop record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  delivery_instructions varchar [note: 'Special instructions for the driver at this stop (e.g., use rear entrance, call upon arrival, leave at loading dock). Provided by customer or route planner.']
  dwell_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Total time in minutes spent at the stop location, calculated as the difference between actual_arrival_timestamp and actual_departure_timestamp. Used for route efficiency and driver productivity analysis.']
  failure_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes explaining the failure reason in detail. Captured by driver or dispatcher when a stop cannot be completed.']
  failure_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating why the stop failed if stop_status is failed. Used for root cause analysis and service improvement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: customer_closed|access_denied|address_incorrect|product_refused|vehicle_breakdown|weather|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  in_full_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the full planned quantity was delivered (True = actual_cases_delivered equals planned_cases_delivered, False = short delivery). Used for OTIF KPI calculation.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this shipment stop record was last updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  on_time_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the stop was completed on time (True = arrival within scheduled window, False = late or early outside tolerance). Used for OTIF KPI calculation.']
  planned_cases_delivered varchar [note: 'Number of cases scheduled for delivery at this stop according to the route plan. Used for load planning and fill rate calculation.']
  planned_volume_m3 decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total planned volume in cubic meters of goods to be delivered or picked up at this stop. Used for vehicle capacity planning and cube utilization.']
  planned_weight_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total planned weight in kilograms of goods to be delivered or picked up at this stop. Used for vehicle load planning and compliance with weight regulations.']
  pod_photo_captured_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a photo proof of delivery was captured at this stop (True = photo taken, False = no photo). Used for contactless delivery verification.']
  pod_signature_captured_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a proof of delivery signature was captured at this stop (True = signature obtained, False = no signature). Required for delivery confirmation and dispute resolution.']
  pod_signature_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person who signed for the delivery. Captured via driver mobile app or paper manifest. Used for delivery verification.']
  pod_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the proof of delivery was captured. May differ slightly from actual_departure_timestamp if signature is obtained before final vehicle departure.']
  scheduled_arrival_date date [note: 'Planned date for arrival at the stop location. Used for customer communication and route planning.']
  scheduled_arrival_time_end timestamp [note: 'End of the scheduled arrival time window. Defines the latest acceptable arrival time for on-time performance measurement.']
  scheduled_arrival_time_start timestamp [note: 'Beginning of the scheduled arrival time window. Used for customer appointment scheduling and OTIF (On Time In Full) measurement.']
  stop_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the stop, typically a sequential or formatted code used in route planning and driver manifests.']
  stop_sequence varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of this stop within the shipment route. Used for route optimization and execution order (e.g., 1, 2, 3).']
  stop_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the stop: scheduled (planned but not started), in_transit (en route), arrived (vehicle at location), completed (delivery/pickup finished), failed (unable to complete), cancelled (stop removed from route).']
  stop_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the stop activity: delivery (outbound to customer), pickup (inbound from supplier or return), cross-dock (transfer between vehicles), return (product return), or inspection (quality check point).']
  temperature_at_delivery_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Product temperature in Celsius recorded at the time of delivery. Captured via temperature monitoring device or manual thermometer reading. Required for cold chain compliance.']
  temperature_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the delivery maintained required cold chain temperature throughout the stop (True = compliant, False = excursion detected). Critical for food safety and FSMA compliance.']

  Note: 'Individual stop on a multi-stop shipment or DSD route, representing a delivery or pickup event at a specific customer location. Captures stop sequence, stop type (delivery, pickup, cross-dock), customer location, scheduled arrival window, actual arrival time, actual departure time, cases delivered, cases refused, POD signature captured flag, and stop status. Enables route execution tracking and OTIF measurement at the stop level.'
}

Table distribution.proof_of_delivery {
  proof_of_delivery_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the proof of delivery record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer who received the delivery.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the carrier or third-party logistics (3PL) provider that executed the delivery.']
  delivery_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the delivery order fulfilled by this delivery.']
  delivery_route_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the delivery route on which this delivery was executed, used for Direct Store Delivery (DSD) route management.']
  ship_to_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific delivery location (store, warehouse, distribution center) where goods were received.']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent shipment that this proof of delivery confirms.']
  actual_delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the delivery was completed and goods were received, critical for OTIF measurement and invoice matching.']
  asn_number varchar [note: 'Advanced Shipping Notice number associated with this delivery, used for Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) processing and pre-receipt notification.']
  cases_delivered_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Actual number of cases successfully delivered and accepted by the receiver, used for fill rate calculation.']
  cases_ordered_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total number of cases that were originally ordered and scheduled for delivery.']
  cases_refused_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Number of cases refused or rejected by the receiver at the time of delivery.']
  cold_chain_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the delivery met cold chain temperature requirements throughout transit and at delivery, ensuring food safety and quality for perishable products.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the proof of delivery record was first created in the system.']
  delivery_condition_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the overall condition of the delivered goods at the time of receipt.']
  delivery_condition_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes describing the condition of the delivered goods, including any damage, quality concerns, or packaging issues observed at delivery.']
  delivery_photo_captured boolean [note: 'Indicates whether photographic evidence of the delivery was captured via mobile device or ePOD system.']
  delivery_photo_url varchar [note: 'URL or storage path to the delivery photo image file, used for proof of delivery and dispute resolution.']
  delivery_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the delivery in its lifecycle workflow.']
  dispute_raised boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a delivery dispute has been raised by the customer or carrier regarding this proof of delivery.']
  geolocation_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'GPS latitude coordinate captured at the time of delivery, used for delivery verification and route optimization analytics.']
  geolocation_longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'GPS longitude coordinate captured at the time of delivery, used for delivery verification and route optimization analytics.']
  in_full_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the full ordered quantity was delivered without shortages, used for fill rate and in-full delivery KPI measurement.']
  invoice_matched boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the proof of delivery has been successfully matched to the corresponding invoice for three-way matching (PO, receipt, invoice).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the proof of delivery record was last updated or modified.']
  on_time_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the delivery was completed within the scheduled time window, used for on-time delivery KPI measurement.']
  otif_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the delivery met On Time In Full (OTIF) performance criteria: delivered on scheduled date within time window and with full ordered quantity.']
  pod_capture_method varchar [note: 'Method used to capture the proof of delivery: electronic POD device, paper scan, EDI 214 Transportation Carrier Shipment Status Message, mobile application, or no signature required.']
  pod_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the proof of delivery document, used for tracking and dispute resolution.']
  receiver_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual who received and signed for the delivery at the customer location.']
  receiver_signature_captured boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a digital or physical signature was captured from the receiver as proof of receipt.']
  receiver_title varchar [note: 'Job title or role of the individual who received the delivery (e.g., Store Manager, Receiving Clerk, Warehouse Supervisor).']
  refusal_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for refusal or rejection of delivered goods. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: damaged|expired|wrong_product|overage|quality_issue|no_order|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  refusal_reason_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes providing additional detail about the reason for refusal, used for dispute resolution and quality investigation.']
  scheduled_delivery_date date [note: 'Planned date for the delivery, used for On Time In Full (OTIF) measurement and Service Level Agreement (SLA) tracking.']
  scheduled_delivery_time_window_end timestamp [note: 'End of the scheduled delivery time window, used for precise delivery appointment tracking.']
  scheduled_delivery_time_window_start timestamp [note: 'Start of the scheduled delivery time window, used for precise delivery appointment tracking.']
  signature_image_url varchar [note: 'URL or storage path to the digital signature image file captured via electronic Proof of Delivery (ePOD) device.']
  stop_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential stop number on the delivery route, used for route optimization and last-mile logistics tracking.']
  temperature_at_delivery_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Recorded temperature of the delivery at the time of receipt, critical for cold chain compliance and food safety verification for refrigerated and frozen products.']
  temperature_excursion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a temperature excursion (deviation from acceptable range) occurred during transit or at delivery, requiring quality review.']

  Note: 'Electronic or paper Proof of Delivery (POD) record confirming receipt of goods at the customer location. Captures POD number, shipment reference, stop reference, delivery datetime, receiver name, receiver signature (digital), cases received, cases refused, refusal reason, temperature at delivery (cold chain), condition notes, and POD capture method (ePOD, paper scan, EDI 214). Critical for OTIF measurement, dispute resolution, and invoice matching.'
}

Table distribution.advance_ship_notice {
  advance_ship_notice_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the advanced shipping notice record. Primary key.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the transportation carrier responsible for delivering this shipment.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Assigns ASN processing costs to a cost center, enabling accurate cost allocation for inbound logistics.']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Tracks the employee responsible for ASN creation, required for traceability and regulatory audit reports.']
  edi_trading_partner_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the trading partner (customer, retailer, supplier, or co-packer) associated with this ASN. For outbound ASNs, this is the recipient; for inbound ASNs, this is the sender.']
  fulfillment_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fulfillment order that this ASN is fulfilling. Links ASN to the originating customer order or purchase order.']
  ship_from_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the originating facility (distribution center, manufacturing plant, co-packer, or supplier warehouse) from which goods are shipped.']
  ship_to_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the destination facility (customer DC, retail store, foodservice location, or receiving warehouse) where goods will be delivered.']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical shipment record associated with this ASN. Links ASN to warehouse management and transportation execution systems.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: ASN processing uses SKU to validate shipment contents, enabling accurate inventory allocation and regulatory compliance checks.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: ASN originates from the supplying vendor; linking to supplier supports performance tracking and compliance reporting.']
  actual_ship_date date [note: 'Actual date when the shipment departed from the origin facility. Used for OTIF performance measurement.']
  asn_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the ASN transmitted to customers or received from suppliers. Externally-known unique reference number.']
  asn_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the ASN. Tracks progression from creation through transmission, acknowledgment, and final receipt or rejection. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|transmitted|acknowledged|in_transit|received|rejected|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  asn_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the ASN direction and purpose. Outbound ASNs are sent to customers/retailers; inbound ASNs are received from suppliers, co-packers, or contract manufacturers.']
  bill_of_lading_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-issued bill of lading number for the shipment. Legal document serving as receipt and contract of carriage.']
  cold_chain_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether continuous temperature monitoring and cold chain documentation is required for this shipment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this ASN record was first created in the system.']
  edi_acknowledgment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the trading partner acknowledged receipt of the ASN via EDI 997 functional acknowledgment.']
  edi_control_number varchar [note: 'Unique control number assigned to the EDI 856 transaction for tracking and reconciliation purposes.']
  edi_transaction_set varchar [note: 'EDI transaction set identifier. ASNs use the 856 Ship Notice/Manifest transaction set.']
  edi_transmission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the ASN was transmitted via EDI to the trading partner. Used for compliance and SLA tracking.']
  expected_delivery_date date [note: 'Planned date when the shipment will arrive at the destination facility. Critical for DC receiving planning and retailer compliance.']
  expected_delivery_time_window_end timestamp [note: 'End of the delivery appointment window. Late arrivals may result in chargebacks or rejected shipments.']
  expected_delivery_time_window_start timestamp [note: 'Start of the delivery appointment window. Many retailers require precise time-slot delivery for dock scheduling.']
  expected_ship_date date [note: 'Planned date when the shipment will depart from the origin facility.']
  hazmat_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment contains hazardous materials requiring special handling and documentation.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this ASN record was last modified. Used for audit trail and data synchronization.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'Customer or supplier purchase order number that this ASN is fulfilling. Used for cross-reference and compliance validation.']
  ship_to_address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line of the destination location. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  ship_to_city varchar [note: 'City of the destination location. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  ship_to_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code of the destination location.']
  ship_to_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the destination location. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  ship_to_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province code of the destination location. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  temperature_zone varchar [note: 'Required temperature control zone for the shipment. Critical for cold chain compliance and food safety.']
  total_carton_count varchar [note: 'Total number of cartons or cases included in this shipment. Used for receiving verification and inventory accuracy.']
  total_pallet_count varchar [note: 'Total number of pallets included in this shipment. Used for dock space planning and receiving resource allocation.']
  total_volume decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total cubic volume of the shipment. Used for trailer utilization and warehouse space planning.']
  total_weight decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total weight of the shipment including packaging. Used for freight cost validation and carrier capacity planning.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-provided tracking number for real-time shipment visibility and proof of delivery.']
  volume_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for shipment volume. CF=cubic feet, CBM=cubic meters, L=liters.']
  weight_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for shipment weight. LB=pounds, KG=kilograms, TON=US tons, MT=metric tonnes.']

  Note: 'Advanced Shipping Notice (ASN) record transmitted to customers or received from suppliers/co-packers prior to shipment arrival. Captures ASN number, ASN type (outbound to customer, inbound from supplier), trading partner, shipment reference, expected arrival date, SKU/GTIN list, lot numbers, quantities, pallet labels (SSCC), and EDI 856 transaction status. Supports retailer compliance, DC receiving, and FSMA traceability requirements.'
}

Table distribution.wms_transaction {
  wms_transaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the WMS transaction record. Primary key for all warehouse movement events within distribution centers.']
  carton_id varchar [note: 'Carton or case identifier for case-level tracking. Used for serialized shipment tracking and ASN generation.']
  center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution center where this transaction occurred.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Allocates labor and equipment costs captured in WMS transactions to the responsible cost center for internal cost tracking.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse operator or picker who executed this transaction. Used for productivity tracking and accountability.']
  experimental_formula_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.experimental_formula. Business justification: Enables inventory traceability of experimental formula batches during pilot manufacturing.']
  fulfillment_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fulfillment order associated with this transaction. Populated for pick, loading, and wave release transactions.']
  ingredient_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ingredient.lot. Business justification: Warehouse Transaction Traceability: linking WMS moves to ingredient lot supports audit of lot handling.']
  lifecycle_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.lifecycle_assessment. Business justification: Links warehouse transactions to product LCA data to calculate real‑time carbon intensity per move.']
  production_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_order. Business justification: Warehouse transaction reconciliation with production orders ensures inventory accuracy.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: WMS transactions record receipt and movement of inventory against a specific purchase order for traceability and audit.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: WMS transaction reporting requires linking each inventory movement to the master SKU for traceability, costing, and compliance reports.']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Regulatory and audit need: WMS transaction must reference the specific stock position moved to ensure traceability and accurate valuation.']
  asn_number varchar [note: 'Advanced Shipping Notice number for inbound receipt transactions. Links WMS receipts to supplier shipment notifications via EDI.']
  catch_weight decimal(12,3) [note: 'Actual weight for variable-weight products (meat, produce, cheese). Used when product weight varies from standard case weight.']
  catch_weight_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for catch weight: lb (pounds), kg (kilograms), oz (ounces), g (grams).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this record was first created in the data lakehouse. Audit field for data lineage tracking.']
  dock_door varchar [note: 'Dock door number for receipt and loading transactions. Used for yard management and carrier coordination.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Product expiration or best-by date. Used for FEFO (First Expired First Out) picking logic and shelf life management.']
  fefo_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this pick transaction followed FEFO logic (picking the lot with the nearest expiration date first). Critical for food safety and waste reduction.']
  from_location varchar [note: 'Source warehouse location for the transaction. Format typically zone-aisle-bay-level (e.g., A-12-05-C). Null for receipt transactions.']
  gtin varchar [note: 'Global Trade Item Number for the product. Standardized product identifier used across the supply chain (GTIN-8, UPC-12, EAN-13, or GTIN-14 format).']
  hazmat_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the product in this transaction is classified as hazardous material requiring special handling and storage.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this record was last modified in the data lakehouse. Audit field for change tracking.']
  pallet_code varchar [note: 'License plate or pallet identifier for the handling unit. Used for pallet-level tracking and cross-docking operations.']
  po_number varchar [note: 'Purchase order number for receipt transactions. Links warehouse receipts to procurement orders.']
  quality_inspection_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this transaction triggered or required a quality inspection. Used for GMP and HACCP compliance tracking.']
  quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of product moved in this transaction. Positive for receipts and putaways, negative for picks and adjustments out.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Reason code for adjustment and transfer transactions: damage (product damage), expiration (expired product), quality_hold (QA hold), customer_return (returned goods), overage (excess inventory), shortage (missing inventory), cycle_count_variance (count discrepancy), system_correction (ERP sync adjustment). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: damage|expiration|quality_hold|customer_return|overage|shortage|cycle_count_variance|system_correction — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  reference varchar [note: 'Original transaction identifier from the source WMS system. Used for audit trail and reconciliation with operational WMS.']
  storage_zone varchar [note: 'Warehouse zone classification for the transaction location: reserve (long-term storage), pick_face (active picking area), bulk (pallet storage), cross_dock (direct transfer), quarantine (quality hold), staging (outbound prep), returns (customer returns), damaged (defective goods). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: reserve|pick_face|bulk|cross_dock|quarantine|staging|returns|damaged — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  temperature_zone varchar [note: 'Temperature control zone for cold chain compliance: ambient (room temperature), refrigerated (35-45°F), frozen (0°F or below), controlled (specific temperature range). Critical for food safety and quality assurance.']
  to_location varchar [note: 'Destination warehouse location for the transaction. Format typically zone-aisle-bay-level (e.g., B-08-12-A). Null for pick and loading transactions.']
  transaction_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the WMS transaction: pending (queued for execution), in_progress (being executed), completed (successfully finished), cancelled (aborted), error (system failure).']
  transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the warehouse transaction was executed in the WMS. This is the operational event time, distinct from audit timestamps.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Type of warehouse movement transaction: receipt (inbound goods arrival), putaway (placement into storage), pick (order fulfillment selection), replenishment (stock movement to pick face), transfer (location-to-location movement), cycle_count (inventory verification), adjustment (inventory correction), wave_release (batch order release), loading (dock door staging). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: receipt|putaway|pick|replenishment|transfer|cycle_count|adjustment|wave_release|loading — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the transaction quantity: each (individual unit), case (shipping case), pallet (full pallet), layer (pallet layer), inner_pack (multi-pack), display_unit (retail display).']
  wave_code varchar [note: 'Wave or batch identifier for grouped order fulfillment. Links pick transactions to wave planning and release events. Null for non-pick transactions.']
  wms_source_system varchar [note: 'Source WMS system identifier (e.g., Manhattan, Blue Yonder, SAP EWM, HighJump). Used for multi-WMS environments and data lineage.']

  Note: 'Granular WMS (Warehouse Management System) transaction record capturing all warehouse movements within a DC — receipts, putaways, picks, replenishments, transfers, cycle counts, adjustments, wave/batch releases, and dock door loading events. Captures transaction type, transaction datetime, DC, storage location (zone/aisle/bay/level), SKU, lot, quantity, UOM, operator ID, equipment ID, wave/batch ID, dock door reference, and WMS source system reference. Wave/batch release transactions serve as the operational record for wave planning within the DC (wave ID, wave release time, orders in wave, wave completion status). Provides the operational audit trail for all inventory movements, pick operations, and wave execution within distribution facilities. Supports lot traceability, FEFO compliance verification, DC productivity measurement, and wave performance analysis.'
}

Table distribution.cold_chain_event {
  cold_chain_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the cold chain monitoring event record.']
  batch_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.batch_record. Business justification: Cold‑chain compliance monitoring per batch requires linking temperature events to the batch record.']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Temperature compliance reports are often brand‑specific; linking events to brand enables brand‑wise quality monitoring.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the transportation carrier responsible for the cold chain asset during transit.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the quality assurance or food safety personnel who reviewed the cold chain event and made the disposition decision.']
  product_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.product_registration. Business justification: Temperature excursion reports must reference the product registration to satisfy FDA and FSMA traceability requirements.']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the shipment or load associated with this cold chain monitoring event.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Cold‑chain monitoring must associate temperature excursions with the specific SKU to satisfy FSMA/HACCP audit and product recall processes.']
  alert_notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether an automated alert notification was sent to responsible personnel when the excursion was detected (True if sent, False if not sent).']
  alert_recipient_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of email addresses or user IDs that received the temperature excursion alert notification.']
  asset_identifier varchar [note: 'Unique identifier or code for the specific cold chain asset (e.g., trailer number, zone code, reefer unit serial number).']
  asset_type varchar [note: 'Type of cold chain asset being monitored (trailer, distribution center zone, reefer unit, container, warehouse room, or transport vehicle).']
  brix_measurement decimal(5,2) [note: 'Sugar content measurement in degrees Brix if applicable to the product being monitored (primarily for beverages and fruit products).']
  corrective_action_taken varchar [note: 'Description of the corrective action implemented in response to a temperature excursion or monitoring alert.']
  corrective_action_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when corrective action was initiated in response to the temperature excursion.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the cold chain event record was first created in the system.']
  destination_location_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the destination location (retail store, distribution center, or customer site) for the cold chain shipment.']
  disposition_status varchar [note: 'Final disposition decision for the product following temperature excursion review (approved for sale, rejected, quarantined, pending review, or destroyed).']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the temperature monitoring event was recorded in format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  excursion_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total duration in minutes that the temperature remained outside the acceptable range during the excursion event.']
  excursion_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the recorded temperature exceeded the acceptable range (True if excursion occurred, False if within range).']
  excursion_severity varchar [note: 'Classification of the temperature excursion severity based on duration and deviation magnitude (minor, moderate, major, or critical).']
  fsma_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the cold chain event monitoring and response met FSMA compliance requirements (True if compliant, False if non-compliant).']
  gps_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the cold chain asset at the time of the temperature reading.']
  gps_longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the cold chain asset at the time of the temperature reading.']
  haccp_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the cold chain event monitoring and response met HACCP critical control point requirements (True if compliant, False if non-compliant).']
  humidity_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Relative humidity percentage in the cold chain environment if monitored alongside temperature.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing lot or batch number of the product being monitored for traceability purposes.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the cold chain event record was last modified or updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding the cold chain event, excursion circumstances, or corrective actions taken.']
  origin_location_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the origin location (distribution center, manufacturing plant, or warehouse) where the cold chain monitoring began.']
  ph_measurement decimal(4,2) [note: 'Acidity or alkalinity measurement on the pH scale if applicable to the product being monitored (critical for food safety and quality).']
  product_category varchar [note: 'High-level category of food or beverage product being transported or stored in the cold chain asset. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: dairy|beverage|frozen|fresh_produce|meat|seafood|prepared_foods — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  quality_hold_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the product was placed on quality hold due to the temperature excursion (True if on hold, False if released).']
  recorded_temperature_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Actual temperature reading captured by the sensor in degrees Celsius.']
  recorded_temperature_fahrenheit decimal(5,2) [note: 'Actual temperature reading captured by the sensor in degrees Fahrenheit.']
  regulatory_report_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the temperature excursion severity requires reporting to regulatory authorities such as FDA or USDA (True if reporting required, False if not required).']
  regulatory_report_submitted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the regulatory report was submitted to the appropriate governing body if required.']
  review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the cold chain event was reviewed and disposition decision was made.']
  sensor_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the temperature monitoring sensor or device that captured the reading.']
  target_temperature_max_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum acceptable temperature threshold for the cold chain asset in degrees Celsius.']
  target_temperature_max_fahrenheit decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum acceptable temperature threshold for the cold chain asset in degrees Fahrenheit.']
  target_temperature_min_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum acceptable temperature threshold for the cold chain asset in degrees Celsius.']
  target_temperature_min_fahrenheit decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum acceptable temperature threshold for the cold chain asset in degrees Fahrenheit.']

  Note: 'Temperature monitoring event record for cold chain compliance during storage and transit. Captures event datetime, asset type (trailer, DC zone, reefer unit), asset identifier, sensor ID, recorded temperature (°F/°C), target temperature range (min/max), Brix or pH if applicable, excursion flag, excursion duration (minutes), corrective action taken, and FSMA/HACCP compliance status. Critical for food safety regulatory compliance and cold chain SLA management.'
}

Table distribution.freight_invoice {
  freight_invoice_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the freight invoice record. Primary key.']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Cost allocation to brands for freight spend is required for brand profitability and budgeting.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the carrier who provided the transportation service and issued this invoice.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Ensures freight invoice amounts are charged to the correct cost center, supporting cost accounting and budgeting.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'The user ID of the person or system that completed the freight audit for this invoice.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Links each freight invoice to the GL account for proper posting of freight expense in the general ledger.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Freight invoices are billed to the purchase order that initiated the shipment, enabling financial reconciliation and OTIF analysis.']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the shipment record for which this freight invoice was issued.']
  accessorial_charges decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total of all accessorial charges (liftgate, inside delivery, residential delivery, detention, redelivery, etc.).']
  approved_payment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The amount approved for payment after audit and dispute resolution. May differ from total billed amount if short-paid or adjusted.']
  audit_status varchar [note: 'The current status of the freight audit process for this invoice. Indicates whether the invoice has been reviewed, approved, or flagged for dispute.']
  audited_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the freight audit was completed for this invoice.']
  bol_number varchar [note: 'The Bill of Lading number associated with this shipment. Legal document issued by carrier to shipper.']
  contracted_rate_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The expected total amount based on the contracted rate agreement with the carrier for this shipment profile.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this freight invoice record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this invoice (e.g., USD, CAD, MXN).']
  delivery_date date [note: 'The date the shipment was delivered to the destination location.']
  destination_city varchar [note: 'The city to which the shipment was delivered.']
  destination_country_code varchar [note: 'The ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code to which the shipment was delivered (e.g., USA, CAN, MEX).']
  destination_location_code varchar [note: 'The code identifying the shipment destination location (customer DC, retail store, or end customer).']
  destination_postal_code varchar [note: 'The postal code to which the shipment was delivered.']
  destination_state_province varchar [note: 'The state or province to which the shipment was delivered.']
  discount_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Any discount applied to the invoice based on contracted rates or promotional agreements.']
  dispute_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes describing the details of the dispute, including supporting documentation references and resolution actions.']
  dispute_reason_code varchar [note: 'The code identifying the reason for disputing this invoice (e.g., rate discrepancy, duplicate charge, incorrect accessorial, service failure).']
  distance_miles decimal(10,2) [note: 'The distance traveled for this shipment in miles, as calculated by the carrier.']
  fuel_surcharge decimal(15,2) [note: 'The fuel surcharge applied to the shipment based on current fuel prices and carrier fuel surcharge tables.']
  invoice_date date [note: 'The date the carrier issued the freight invoice.']
  invoice_due_date date [note: 'The date by which payment is due to the carrier per the payment terms.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'The carrier-issued invoice number. This is the externally-known unique identifier for this freight invoice.']
  line_haul_charge decimal(15,2) [note: 'The base transportation charge for moving the freight from origin to destination, excluding accessorials and surcharges.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this freight invoice record was last modified.']
  origin_city varchar [note: 'The city from which the shipment originated.']
  origin_country_code varchar [note: 'The ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code from which the shipment originated (e.g., USA, CAN, MEX).']
  origin_location_code varchar [note: 'The code identifying the shipment origin location (DC, plant, or supplier facility).']
  origin_postal_code varchar [note: 'The postal code from which the shipment originated.']
  origin_state_province varchar [note: 'The state or province from which the shipment originated.']
  payment_date date [note: 'The date payment was issued to the carrier for this invoice.']
  payment_reference_number varchar [note: 'The reference number of the payment transaction (check number, wire transfer ID, ACH trace number) used to pay this invoice.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'The current payment status of this freight invoice.']
  payment_terms_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from invoice date until payment is due (e.g., Net 30, Net 45).']
  pro_number varchar [note: 'The carrier-assigned Progressive Rotating Order (PRO) number used to track the shipment. Standard identifier in LTL and truckload freight.']
  rate_variance_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The variance between the billed amount and the contracted rate amount. Positive values indicate overbilling; negative values indicate underbilling.']
  received_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the freight invoice was received by the organization (via EDI, email, or carrier portal).']
  service_level varchar [note: 'The specific service level provided by the carrier (e.g., Standard, Expedited, Next Day, Two Day, White Glove, Temperature Controlled).']
  service_mode varchar [note: 'The mode of transportation service provided (Full Truckload, Less Than Truckload, Parcel, Intermodal, Air, Ocean, Rail, Courier). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FTL|LTL|Parcel|Intermodal|Air|Ocean|Rail|Courier — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  shipment_date date [note: 'The date the shipment was picked up by the carrier from the origin location.']
  total_billed_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The total amount billed by the carrier on this invoice, including line haul, fuel surcharge, accessorials, and any adjustments.']
  weight_actual decimal(15,3) [note: 'The actual weight of the shipment as measured or certified by the carrier.']
  weight_uom varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the shipment weight (pounds, kilograms, tons, metric tons).']

  Note: 'Carrier freight invoice record for transportation services rendered. Captures invoice number, carrier, invoice date, payment due date, shipment references, billed charges (line haul, fuel surcharge, accessorials), contracted rate reference, variance amount (billed vs. contracted), audit status (approved, disputed, short-paid), and payment status. Supports freight audit and payment (FAP) processes and carrier cost management.'
}

Table distribution.otif_record {
  otif_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the OTIF performance measurement record.']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Brand‑level OTIF scorecards need a direct link from OTIF records to the brand of the product shipped.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the carrier responsible for transporting the shipment.']
  center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the distribution center that fulfilled the order and is responsible for the OTIF performance.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account for which OTIF performance is being measured.']
  fulfillment_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fulfillment order that this OTIF record measures performance against.']
  responsible_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Identifies the employee accountable for OTIF compliance, used in OTIF performance and penalty reporting.']
  actual_delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time when the shipment was delivered to the customer location.']
  asn_number varchar [note: 'The ASN number sent to the customer prior to shipment arrival, used for receiving coordination.']
  cases_delivered decimal(12,2) [note: 'The actual number of cases delivered to the customer, used to calculate fill rate.']
  cases_ordered decimal(12,2) [note: 'The total number of cases ordered by the customer in the fulfillment order.']
  chargeback_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number for the retailer chargeback or deduction associated with this OTIF failure.']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this OTIF record was closed after resolution or penalty payment.']
  committed_delivery_window_end timestamp [note: 'The end of the delivery time window committed to the customer, used to determine on-time performance.']
  committed_delivery_window_start timestamp [note: 'The start of the delivery time window committed to the customer, used to determine on-time performance.']
  corrective_action_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the corrective action plan or ticket created to address the root cause and prevent recurrence.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this OTIF record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the penalty amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  delivery_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the delivery destination address.']
  delivery_city varchar [note: 'City of the delivery destination.']
  delivery_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the delivery destination (e.g., USA, CAN, MEX).']
  delivery_destination_type varchar [note: 'The type of destination location for the delivery (e.g., retail store, distribution center, foodservice, direct-to-consumer).']
  delivery_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the delivery destination.']
  delivery_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province of the delivery destination.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this OTIF failure has been escalated to senior management due to severity or repeat occurrence.']
  fill_rate_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of ordered cases that were successfully delivered, calculated as (cases_delivered / cases_ordered) * 100.']
  in_full_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the full ordered quantity was delivered without shortages (True = in-full, False = short).']
  measurement_date date [note: 'The date on which the OTIF performance was measured and recorded.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this OTIF record was last updated with new information (e.g., root cause, corrective action).']
  on_time_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the delivery was completed within the committed time window (True = on-time, False = late).']
  otif_composite_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'The composite OTIF performance score, typically expressed as a percentage (0-100), representing the combined on-time and in-full achievement.']
  penalty_fine_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The monetary penalty or fine amount assessed by the retailer for OTIF non-compliance.']
  proof_of_delivery_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the proof of delivery document or electronic signature confirming receipt.']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the OTIF record (e.g., created, under investigation, resolved, penalty paid, closed). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: created|under_investigation|root_cause_identified|corrective_action_assigned|resolved|penalty_paid|closed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  responsible_party varchar [note: 'The organizational unit or individual responsible for managing this OTIF record and corrective actions.']
  retailer_otif_program varchar [note: 'The specific retailer OTIF compliance program under which this delivery performance is measured (e.g., Walmart OTIF, Target In-Stock, Kroger On-Time Delivery).']
  root_cause_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the root cause of the OTIF miss (e.g., carrier delay, DC late release, inventory shortage, weather event). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: carrier_delay|dc_late_release|inventory_short|weather|traffic|equipment_failure|labor_shortage|system_error|other — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the root cause analysis for the OTIF failure.']
  shipment_reference varchar [note: 'The shipment identifier associated with this OTIF measurement, linking to the physical delivery.']
  temperature_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether cold chain temperature requirements were maintained throughout delivery (True = compliant, False = breach).']

  Note: 'On-Time In-Full (OTIF) performance measurement record capturing delivery performance against customer-committed windows at the shipment or fulfillment order level. Captures measurement date, customer account, retailer OTIF program (Walmart OTIF, Target In-Stock, Kroger OTD, Costco delivery compliance), shipment reference, fulfillment order reference, committed delivery window, actual delivery datetime, on-time flag, in-full flag, OTIF composite score, cases ordered, cases delivered, fill rate percentage, penalty/fine amount, chargeback reference, root cause code for misses (carrier delay, DC late release, inventory short, weather), and corrective action reference. Each record has an independent lifecycle: created at measurement, updated with root cause and corrective action, closed upon resolution or penalty payment. Supports retailer scorecard compliance, chargeback/penalty management, and continuous improvement programs.'
}

Table distribution.returns_receipt {
  returns_receipt_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the returns receipt record. Primary key.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer or account returning the product. Null for internal transfers or unattributed returns.']
  center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution center where the return was received.']
  delivery_route_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the DSD route from which the product was returned. Applicable for DSD return source types.']
  original_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original customer order that included this product. Used for order-to-cash reconciliation.']
  original_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original outbound shipment from which this product is being returned. Used for root cause analysis and OTIF tracking.']
  price_list_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list_line. Business justification: References the original price list line for the returned SKU, allowing accurate refund calculations and return‑impact financial analysis.']
  primary_returns_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the quality inspector who evaluated the returned product.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Internal unique identifier linking to the product master record.']
  cold_chain_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the returned product maintained proper temperature control during transit and storage. False indicates potential quality compromise.']
  condition_code varchar [note: 'The physical and quality condition of the returned product upon receipt. Determines disposition path.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this returns receipt record was first created in the system.']
  credit_memo_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of the credit memo issued to the customer for this return. Links to accounts receivable and financial reconciliation.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the return value amount.']
  disposition_decision varchar [note: 'The final decision on how the returned product will be handled. Drives inventory adjustment and financial accounting.']
  disposition_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the disposition decision was executed (restocked, destroyed, donated, etc.).']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The expiration or best-by date of the returned product. Used to determine disposition and FEFO compliance.']
  gtin varchar [note: 'The 14-digit global trade item number for the returned product. Used for cross-system reconciliation and traceability.']
  inspection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when quality inspection was completed for the returned product.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing lot or batch number of the returned product. Critical for traceability, recall management, and FSMA compliance.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this returns receipt record was last updated.']
  pallet_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the pallet on which the returned product was received. Used for warehouse location tracking.']
  production_date date [note: 'The date the product was manufactured. Supports shelf life calculation and traceability.']
  quality_inspection_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the returned product requires quality inspection before disposition decision is finalized.']
  quantity_returned decimal(15,3) [note: 'The quantity of product returned, expressed in the unit of measure specified.']
  recall_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this return is part of a product recall event. Triggers special handling and regulatory reporting.']
  recall_number varchar [note: 'Official recall event identifier if this return is associated with a product recall. Used for regulatory reporting and traceability.']
  receipt_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the returned product was physically received at the distribution center. Critical for FEFO compliance and shelf life tracking.']
  return_authorization_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known authorization number issued to approve the return. Used for tracking and reconciliation with customer service and sales systems.']
  return_reason_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes providing additional context on the reason for the return. Captured from customer service or driver input.']
  return_source_type varchar [note: 'The type of originating source from which the product was returned.']
  return_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the reason for the return. Drives disposition logic and financial treatment.']
  return_value_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The monetary value of the returned product, used for credit calculation and financial reporting.']
  returns_receipt_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the returns receipt record. Tracks progression from receipt through final disposition.']
  sku varchar [note: 'Internal stock keeping unit code identifying the returned product at the item level.']
  temperature_zone varchar [note: 'The required storage temperature zone for the returned product. Critical for cold chain compliance and food safety.']
  uom varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the returned quantity (Each, Case, Pallet, Pound, Kilogram, Gallon, Liter). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|CS|PL|LB|KG|GAL|LTR — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  warehouse_location varchar [note: 'The specific bin, aisle, or zone location within the DC where the returned product is stored pending disposition.']

  Note: 'Inbound returns receipt record for product returned from customers, retailers, or DSD routes back to a DC. Captures return authorization number, return type (unsold, damaged, expired, recall), customer/route source, receipt datetime, DC, SKU/GTIN, lot number, quantity returned, condition code (saleable, damaged, expired, destroyed), disposition decision (restock, donate, destroy, rework), and credit memo reference. Supports FEFO compliance, recall management, and reverse logistics.'
}

Table distribution.yard_management {
  yard_management_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each yard management event record.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the carrier responsible for the trailer; links to the carrier master data.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Charges yard handling activities to the appropriate cost center for expense control and performance measurement.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the driver operating the trailer; links to driver master data.']
  previous_yard_management_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on yard_management (previous_yard_management_id)']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: Captures yard events (detention, loading) for inbound/outbound pilot trial shipments tied to a specific R&D project.']
  appointment_reference varchar [note: 'External appointment or booking number tied to the trailer\'s scheduled yard time.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the yard management record was first created in the system.']
  detention_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when detention ended; null if still detained.']
  detention_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when detention or demurrage clock started for the trailer.']
  dock_door_assigned varchar [note: 'Identifier of the dock door where the trailer is staged or loaded.']
  driver_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the driver associated with the yard event.']
  event_number varchar [note: 'Business-assigned sequential identifier for the yard event, used for tracking and reference.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the yard event record.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the yard event occurred.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Type of yard activity captured (e.g., trailer check‑in, dock assignment, internal yard move, or check‑out).']
  humidity_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Relative humidity measured in the yard area during the event.']
  is_detained boolean [note: 'True if the trailer is currently under detention or demurrage.']
  load_weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Weight of the cargo loaded onto the trailer, measured in kilograms.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the yard management record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations captured by yard personnel.']
  priority_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the trailer/event is marked as high priority for processing.']
  seal_number varchar [note: 'Security seal identifier applied to the trailer for tamper evidence.']
  temperature_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Recorded ambient temperature of the trailer or yard spot at event time.']
  temperature_controlled_flag boolean [note: 'True if the trailer requires temperature control (refrigerated or frozen).']
  trailer_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier printed on the trailer chassis, used for logistics and compliance.']
  trailer_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the trailer within the yard.']
  unload_weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Weight of the cargo removed from the trailer, measured in kilograms.']
  yard_move_distance_meters varchar [note: 'Distance the trailer was moved within the yard, measured in meters.']
  yard_spot_location varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code of the specific yard spot or parking location assigned to the trailer.']

  Note: 'Yard management transaction record tracking trailer and vehicle movements within DC yard boundaries. Captures yard event type (check-in, dock assignment, yard move, check-out), trailer number, carrier, arrival datetime, dock door assigned, yard spot location, trailer status (loaded inbound, empty, loaded outbound, live unload, drop), seal number verification, detention/demurrage start time, driver check-in/out, appointment reference, priority flag, temperature-controlled trailer flag, and yard event status. Supports yard visibility, dock door utilization optimization, detention cost management, and trailer turn-time KPIs at high-volume F&B distribution centers.'
}

Table distribution.location {
  location_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for location']
  parent_location_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on location (parent_location_id)']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the location.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information (suite, unit, etc.).']
  capacity_sqft decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total usable square‑footage capacity of the facility.']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the location resides.']
  close_date date [note: 'Date the location ceased operations (null if still active).']
  close_time varchar [note: 'Daily closing time in HH:mm (24‑hour) format.']
  cold_chain_compliant boolean [note: 'True if the site meets cold‑chain regulatory requirements.']
  compliance_certifications varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of certifications (e.g., ISO 22000, SQF).']
  country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the location.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the location record was first created.']
  dock_count varchar [note: 'Number of loading docks available at the location.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the location information is considered valid.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the location information is no longer valid (null for open‑ended).']
  is_primary boolean [note: 'True if this location is the primary hub for its business unit.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent regulatory or safety inspection.']
  latitude double [note: 'Geographic latitude in decimal degrees.']
  loading_bays varchar [note: 'Count of dedicated loading bays for outbound shipments.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Business‑assigned alphanumeric code used externally to reference the location.']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Category of the location indicating its operational role.']
  longitude double [note: 'Geographic longitude in decimal degrees.']
  manager_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Contact phone number for the location manager.']
  manager_email varchar [note: 'Email address for the location manager.']
  manager_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person responsible for day‑to‑day operations.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the facility, warehouse, store, or distribution point.']
  next_inspection_due date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next required inspection.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or special instructions for the location.']
  open_date date [note: 'Date the location began operations.']
  open_time varchar [note: 'Daily opening time in HH:mm (24‑hour) format.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal/ZIP code for the location.']
  receiving_bays varchar [note: 'Count of bays used for inbound receipt of goods.']
  region varchar [note: 'Broad region (e.g., North America, EMEA) for reporting aggregation.']
  security_level varchar [note: 'Security classification of the site.']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province of the location.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the location.']
  temperature_control boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the location has temperature‑controlled storage.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time‑zone identifier for the location (e.g., America/New_York).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the location record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for location. Referenced by ship_from_location_id.'
}

Table distribution.wave {
  wave_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for wave']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'FK to distribution.carrier']
  consolidated_wave_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on wave (consolidated_wave_id)']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary transportation route assigned to the wave.']
  actual_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date‑time when wave processing completed.']
  actual_quantity varchar [note: 'Actual number of units processed in the wave.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date‑time when wave processing began.']
  actual_weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total actual weight of items processed in the wave, expressed in kilograms.']
  compliance_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the wave meets required regulatory or quality compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the wave record was first created in the system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the wave is scheduled to end or be retired (nullable for open‑ended waves).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the wave becomes active for execution.']
  is_expedited boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the wave is marked for expedited handling.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments entered by operations staff.']
