  planned_quantity varchar [note: 'Total number of units planned to be processed in the wave.']
  planned_weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total planned weight of all items in the wave, expressed in kilograms.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level of the wave.']
  scheduled_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned end date‑time for wave execution.']
  scheduled_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned start date‑time for wave execution.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the wave.']
  temperature_max_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum temperature requirement for the wave.']
  temperature_min_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum temperature requirement for the wave (e.g., cold‑chain compliance).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the wave record.']
  wave_category varchar [note: 'Higher‑level classification of the wave (e.g., seasonal, promotional, replenishment).']
  wave_code varchar [note: 'External business code or reference number assigned to the wave by the distribution system.']
  wave_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the wave purpose and scope.']
  wave_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the wave used for reporting and operational planning.']
  wave_type varchar [note: 'Category of the wave indicating the primary logistics activity (e.g., pick, pack, load, dispatch).']

  Note: 'Master reference table for wave. Referenced by wave_id.'
}

Table distribution.pallet {
  pallet_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for pallet']
  parent_pallet_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on pallet (parent_pallet_id)']
  barcode varchar [note: 'Scannable barcode representing the pallet\'s external identifier.']
  condition varchar [note: 'Physical condition assessment of the pallet.']
  current_location_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse, dock, or carrier location where the pallet is presently situated.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the pallet should be retired or inspected for compliance.']
  height_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Height dimension of the pallet in centimeters.']
  is_refrigerated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the pallet is designed for temperature‑controlled shipments.']
  label varchar [note: 'Human‑readable label or name assigned to the pallet for easy identification.']
  last_inspection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent inspection performed on the pallet.']
  length_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Length dimension of the pallet in centimeters.']
  manufacture_date date [note: 'Date the pallet was manufactured.']
  material varchar [note: 'Primary material from which the pallet is constructed.']
  max_load_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum weight the pallet can safely carry, in kilograms.']
  next_maintenance_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next preventive maintenance or inspection.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional remarks or observations about the pallet.']
  owner_department varchar [note: 'Internal department responsible for the pallet.']
  pallet_type varchar [note: 'Category of pallet based on construction and purpose.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the pallet within distribution operations.']
  temperature_range_c varchar [note: 'Operational temperature range for refrigerated pallets, expressed as min‑max in Celsius.']
  usage_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the pallet has been used in shipments.']
  weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual weight of the empty pallet in kilograms.']
  width_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Width dimension of the pallet in centimeters.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for pallet. Referenced by pallet_id.'
}

Table distribution.carton {
  carton_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for carton']
  parent_carton_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on carton (parent_carton_id)']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'FK to procurement.supplier']
  carton_code varchar [note: 'Business code or barcode representing the carton type.']
  carton_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the carton based on usage and design.']
  cold_chain_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates if the carton meets cold chain requirements for temperature-sensitive goods.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the carton record was created.']
  description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the carton.']
  dimensions_height_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Physical height of the carton in centimeters.']
  dimensions_length_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Physical length of the carton in centimeters.']
  dimensions_width_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Physical width of the carton in centimeters.']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Result of the latest inspection of the carton.']
  is_reusable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the carton is designed for reuse.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent inspection of the carton.']
  manufacturer varchar [note: 'Name of the entity that manufactures the carton.']
  material varchar [note: 'Primary material composition of the carton.']
  max_load_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum load weight the carton can safely hold.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the carton.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the carton record.']
  volume_cubic_m decimal(12,4) [note: 'Internal volume capacity of the carton in cubic meters.']
  weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Empty weight of the carton in kilograms.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for carton. Referenced by carton_id.'
}

Table sales.order {
  order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the order.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer who placed the order.']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Order approval workflow requires manager employee ID; needed for audit, compliance, and financial reporting.']
  bill_to_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the location for billing purposes.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Orders are attributed to the marketing campaign that generated the demand; linking supports sales‑to‑marketing attribution and spend ROI analysis.']
  center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.distribution_center. Business justification: Order is fulfilled from a distribution center; linking enables direct relationship and removes distribution_center_code.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.plant. Business justification: Order originates from a manufacturing plant; linking provides traceability and eliminates redundant plant_code.']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the price list applied to the order.']
  price_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_zone. Business justification: Orders are priced according to regional price zones; required for regional pricing analysis, tax calculation, and regulatory reporting.']
  promotion_event_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of any promotion applied to the order.']
  retailer_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trade.retailer_agreement. Business justification: Required for Order‑Retailer Agreement compliance reporting; ties each order to the specific retailer agreement governing slotting fees and promotions.']
  revenue_gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Required for General Ledger posting of order revenue; financial statements and tax reporting depend on linking each sales order to its revenue GL account.']
  sales_contract_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales contract governing the order.']
  sales_organization_id varchar [note: 'FK to sales.sales_organization']
  sales_rep_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales representative responsible for the order.']
  ship_to_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the location to which the order will be shipped.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'Date the order was actually delivered.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the order was placed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this order record was first captured.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the order amounts.']
  edi_850_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of the EDI 850 purchase order transaction.']
  erp_sales_doc_number varchar [note: 'Sales document number generated in the ERP system.']
  freight_terms varchar [note: 'Terms governing freight cost responsibility.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'International commercial terms governing shipment responsibilities.']
  is_backorder boolean [note: 'True if any line items are on backorder.']
  is_split_order boolean [note: 'True if the order is split across multiple shipments.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the order by the sales system.']
  order_source varchar [note: 'Origin channel through which the order was entered.']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the order.']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the order based on processing rules.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Payment terms agreed for the order (e.g., Net 30).']
  placed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the order was created in the source system.']
  priority_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the order is marked as high priority.']
  promised_delivery_date date [note: 'Date promised by the company to deliver the order.']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'Date requested by the customer for delivery.']
  revenue_recognition_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the order is eligible for revenue recognition at booking.']
  sales_region varchar [note: 'Geographic sales region associated with the order.']
  shipping_method varchar [note: 'Method used to transport the order to the customer.']
  total_discount_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Aggregate discount amount applied to the order.']
  total_gross_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Sum of line item list prices before discounts and taxes.']
  total_net_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final amount after discounts and taxes.']
  total_quantity varchar [note: 'Sum of ordered quantities across all lines.']
  total_tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total tax calculated for the order.']
  total_volume_cu_m decimal(10,3) [note: 'Aggregate volume of all items in the order (cubic meters).']
  total_weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Aggregate weight of all items in the order (kilograms).']
  trade_allowance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if trade allowances (e.g., discounts, rebates) are applied.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the order record.']

  Note: 'Complete sales order document capturing all commercial sales transactions across retail, foodservice, and DTC channels, including both header-level and line-level detail in a single authoritative record. Header: order number, order date, requested delivery date, order type (standard, rush, blanket, standing), order status, channel, customer account reference, ship-to and bill-to locations, currency, payment terms, incoterms, total order value, EDI 850 reference, and ERP sales document number. Lines: line number, SKU/GTIN, ordered quantity, unit of measure (case, each, pallet), confirmed quantity, shipped quantity, open quantity, list price, net price after trade allowances, discount amount, line status, requested ship date, scheduled ship date, plant/DC assignment, batch number for FEFO compliance, and revenue recognition flag. SSOT for all outbound sales order commitments within the sales domain.'
}

Table sales.invoice {
  invoice_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the invoice record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer billed on this invoice.']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Invoice approval must be signed off by an employee; essential for control‑testing and audit trails.']
  bill_to_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the party responsible for payment.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Invoice posting to the GL is mandatory for revenue recognition and audit trails; linking invoice to GL account enables accurate income statement reporting.']
  original_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original invoice when this record is a credit or debit memo.']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list. Business justification: Required for invoicing audit: invoices must reference the price list used to calculate line amounts for compliance and margin reporting.']
  production_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_order. Business justification: Cost of goods sold and compliance reporting need to associate each invoice with the production order that generated the sold goods.']
  promotion_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trade.promotion_event. Business justification: Needed for financial reconciliation of promotional spend; links each sales invoice to the promotion event that generated the sales.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Invoice‑PO reconciliation report requires linking each invoice to its purchase order for financial matching.']
  ship_to_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the party receiving the goods.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the invoice.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the invoice was approved.']
  billing_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the billing address.']
  billing_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the billing address (optional).']
  billing_city varchar [note: 'City component of the billing address.']
  billing_country varchar [note: 'Three-letter country code of the billing address.']
  billing_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the billing address.']
  billing_state varchar [note: 'State or province component of the billing address.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the contract governing the transaction.']
  cost_center varchar [note: 'Organizational unit responsible for the cost of the invoice.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the invoice record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 code of the transaction currency.']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which payment must be received.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Rate used to convert from transaction currency to reporting currency, if different.']
  freight_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Charges for transportation and logistics associated with the invoice.']
  invoice_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the invoice was issued to the customer.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the invoice (e.g., sequential number).']
  invoice_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the invoice.']
  invoice_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the invoice purpose (standard, credit memo, debit memo, intercompany).']
  is_credit_memo boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the invoice is a credit memo (true) or not (false).']
  line_count varchar [note: 'Number of line items included on the invoice.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method used by the customer to settle the invoice.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the payment for this invoice.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Contractual terms defining when payment is due (e.g., Net 30).']
  posted_to_ledger_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the invoice has been posted to the general ledger.']
  profit_center varchar [note: 'Organizational unit responsible for the profit attribution of the invoice.']
  revenue_category varchar [note: 'Classification of revenue (e.g., gross sales, trade spend, net revenue).']
  subtotal_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of line net amounts before discounts, freight, and tax.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax calculated for the invoice based on applicable tax codes.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the tax jurisdiction and rate applied.']
  tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage rate applied for tax calculation.']
  total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final amount payable after all discounts, freight, and tax.']
  trade_discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount of trade promotions or discounts applied to the invoice.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the invoice record.']

  Note: 'Complete commercial invoice document issued to customers upon shipment or delivery of goods, capturing both header-level and line-level detail in a single authoritative billing record for revenue recognition. Header: invoice number, invoice date, invoice type (standard, credit memo, debit memo, intercompany), customer account, billing address, currency, subtotal, trade discount, freight, tax, total amount, payment terms, EDI 810 reference, and SAP SD billing document number. Lines: line number, SKU/GTIN, billed quantity, unit of measure, list price, invoice unit price, line discount, line net amount, tax code, cost center, profit center, GL account code, and revenue category (gross sales, trade spend, net revenue). SSOT for accounts receivable origination within sales domain; finance domain owns downstream AR aging and collections.'
}

Table sales.sales_contract {
  sales_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the sales contract.']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Contracts are negotiated per brand; linking enables brand‑level contract performance tracking and compliance reporting.']
  center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.distribution_center. Business justification: Links supply contracts to the distribution center fulfilling them, needed for contract performance and capacity planning reports.']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: Needed for Contract Signatory Register, linking each sales contract to the customer contact who authorized the agreement, required for compliance audit.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Cost allocation of contract‑related expenses to a cost center is needed for budgeting, variance analysis, and cost‑to‑serve calculations.']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list. Business justification: Contracts lock in a specific price list governing product pricing; needed for contract compliance, pricing validation, and revenue realization reports.']
  product_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.product_registration. Business justification: Contract compliance checks require linking each sales contract to the product registration to verify the product is authorized for sale.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Contracts are allocated to profit centers for performance tracking and internal profitability reporting; required by monthly P&L consolidation.']
  promotion_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trade.promotion_plan. Business justification: Contracts are often executed under a defined promotion plan; this link supports contract‑level promotion budgeting and performance tracking.']
  purchase_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_contract. Business justification: Contract compliance dashboard aligns sales contracts with upstream purchase contracts to calculate true margin.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: Sales contracts for new product launches reference the originating R&D project to ensure specifications, regulatory compliance, and cost recovery are aligned.']
  retailer_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the retailer party associated with the contract.']
  salesforce_opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating Salesforce CRM opportunity.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Contract management report ties each sales contract to a specific SKU for volume commitments and pricing; SKU ID is essential for accurate contract enforcement.']
  spec_quality_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.specification. Business justification: Contract Specification Compliance: sales contracts reference agreed product specifications; linking ensures compliance monitoring and audit reporting.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract automatically renews at expiration.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Unique contract number assigned by the company.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Category of the contract defining its primary purpose.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the contract record was created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values.']
  edlp_tier varchar [note: 'Pricing tier for EDLP pricing structure.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the contract ends or expires; null for open‑ended contracts.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the contract becomes binding.']
  exclusive_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the contract grants exclusive rights to the retailer.']
  fee_per_store decimal(10,2) [note: 'Slotting fee amount charged per store.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates if the contract is currently active (derived from status).']
  launch_date date [note: 'Date when the product is scheduled to be launched in stores under this contract.']
  min_shelf_duration_days varchar [note: 'Minimum number of days the product must remain on shelf.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Terms governing payment schedule and method.']
  performance_clause varchar [note: 'Text describing velocity‑based performance criteria and penalties.']
  rebate_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage rebate applied based on volume thresholds.']
  rsp_commitment decimal(12,2) [note: 'Committed RSP per unit for the contract.']
  sales_contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the contract.']
  slotting_fee_total decimal(12,2) [note: 'Aggregate fee paid for shelf slotting across all stores.']
  store_count varchar [note: 'Number of retail locations covered by the contract.']
  termination_notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days notice required to terminate the contract.']
  total_committed_volume decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total volume of product units committed under the contract.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the contract record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number of the contract for amendment tracking.']
  volume_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the committed volume.']

  Note: 'Master commercial agreement between Food Beverage and a customer (retailer, foodservice operator, distributor) governing pricing, volume commitments, promotional terms, slotting fees, and service levels. Captures contract number, contract type (volume rebate, pricing agreement, distribution agreement, co-packing agreement, slotting agreement), effective dates, contracted volume, RSP commitments, EDLP pricing tiers, payment terms, exclusivity clauses, auto-renewal flag, and Salesforce CRM opportunity reference. For slotting-type contracts: includes retailer account, SKU/GTIN, store count, fee per store, total commitment, payment schedule, shelf section, launch date, minimum shelf duration, and velocity-based performance clauses. ADR: Slotting agreements are modeled as a contract_type within this product (not as a separate entity) because they are negotiated by sales/category teams during new item sell-in and directly tied to distribution point creation. Trade domain owns broader promotional agreements (TPM/TPO); this product owns the commercial commitment for shelf placement fees. Commission/incentive compensation calculation is owned by the finance/workforce domain; sales provides quota attainment data as input.'
}

Table sales.opportunity {
  opportunity_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the sales opportunity.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Attribution of sales opportunities to specific marketing campaigns for ROI reporting and performance analysis.']
  opportunity_account_id varchar [note: 'Internal identifier of the target customer.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: Opportunity qualification requires linking to the R&D project that defines the new product, enabling launch timeline and cost tracking.']
  sales_rep_id varchar [note: 'Internal employee identifier for the sales representative.']
  target_customer_account_id varchar [note: 'FK to customer.account']
  actual_close_date date [note: 'Date when the opportunity was actually closed (won or lost).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the opportunity record was first created in the system.']
  estimated_annual_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected annual revenue in US dollars if the opportunity is won.']
  estimated_case_volume varchar [note: 'Projected number of cases (units) expected to be sold annually.']
  expected_close_date date [note: 'Projected date when the opportunity is expected to be closed.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form notes captured by the sales team during the opportunity lifecycle.']
  opportunity_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the opportunity, objectives, and key considerations.']
  opportunity_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the opportunity, typically reflecting the target account or SKU.']
  opportunity_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the opportunity, used in communications and reporting.']
  opportunity_type varchar [note: 'Category of the opportunity indicating the business scenario.']
  probability_of_close decimal(5,2) [note: 'Likelihood of winning the opportunity expressed as a percentage.']
  sales_territory varchar [note: 'Geographic or market territory assigned to the opportunity.']
  salesforce_opportunity_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the opportunity in Salesforce CRM.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that created the opportunity record (e.g., Salesforce, manual entry).']
  stage varchar [note: 'Current stage of the sales pipeline for the opportunity.']
  target_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the product would be sold if the opportunity is won.']
  target_distribution_points varchar [note: 'Number of distribution points (e.g., stores) targeted for placement.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the opportunity record.']

  Note: 'Sales pipeline opportunity representing a potential new business win, account expansion, or new SKU distribution placement. Captures opportunity name, opportunity type (new account, new SKU, channel expansion, competitive win-back), stage (prospecting, qualification, proposal, negotiation, closed-won, closed-lost), estimated annual revenue, estimated case volume, target customer, target channel, target distribution points (TDP), probability of close, expected close date, assigned sales rep, sales territory, and Salesforce CRM opportunity ID. Enables sales force automation and pipeline management.'
}

Table sales.rep {
  rep_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for rep']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manager (sales leader) for this rep.']
  target_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.sustainability_target. Business justification: Sales rep performance scorecards include sustainability target achievement metrics.']
  assigned_customer_count varchar [note: 'Number of customer accounts assigned to the rep.']
  certification_status varchar [note: 'Status of any required sales certifications.']
  commission_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Commission percentage applied to sales.']
  compensation_plan varchar [note: 'Compensation structure applied to the rep.']
  compliance_training_completed_date date [note: 'Date when mandatory compliance training was completed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the rep record was created in the system.']
  email varchar [note: 'Primary business email address for the rep.']
  employment_status varchar [note: 'Current employment status of the rep.']
  external_rep_code varchar [note: 'External identifier used by partners or third‑party systems.']
  full_name varchar [note: 'Legal full name of the sales representative.']
  hire_date date [note: 'Date the rep was hired.']
  is_remote_worker boolean [note: 'Indicates if the rep works remotely.']
  language_preference varchar [note: 'Preferred language for communication with the rep.']
  last_performance_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent performance review.']
  next_performance_review_due date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next performance review.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the rep.']
  primary_contact_method varchar [note: 'Preferred primary contact channel for the rep.']
  quota_annual_cases decimal(12,2) [note: 'Target annual case volume quota.']
  quota_annual_revenue decimal(15,2) [note: 'Target annual revenue quota for the rep.']
  record_source varchar [note: 'Source system where the rep record originated.']
  rep_type varchar [note: 'Category of sales role performed by the rep.']
  sales_channel varchar [note: 'Primary sales channel the rep operates in.']
  sales_district varchar [note: 'Specific sales district within the region.']
  sales_force_user_code varchar [note: 'User identifier in Salesforce CRM for the rep.']
  sales_region varchar [note: 'Geographic sales region assigned to the rep.']
  sap_employee_number varchar [note: 'Employee number used in SAP SD module.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date the rep\'s employment ended, if applicable.']
  territory_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the sales territory assigned to the rep.']
  travel_region varchar [note: 'Travel region classification for the rep.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the rep record.']

  Note: 'Sales force master record for field sales representatives, key account managers, and broker/agent personnel responsible for managing customer relationships and driving revenue. Captures rep ID, full name, rep type (field sales, key account manager, national account manager, broker, DSD driver), assigned territory, assigned customer accounts, sales region, sales district, quota (annual revenue and case volume), manager reference, employment status, Salesforce CRM user ID, and SAP SD sales employee number. SSOT for sales force identity within the sales domain.'
}

Table sales.territory {
  territory_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the sales territory.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales manager responsible for the territory.']
  acv_coverage_target decimal(15,2) [note: 'Target ACV value (in USD) the territory aims to cover.']
  assigned_account_count varchar [note: 'Number of retail, foodservice, or DTC accounts assigned to the territory.']
  assigned_channel varchar [note: 'Primary sales channel(s) the territory serves: retail, foodservice, or direct‑to‑consumer.']
  country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code for the territory.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the territory record was first created in the system.']
  district varchar [note: 'Sub‑region within a region used for finer territory granularity.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the territory definition expires or is superseded; null if open‑ended.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the territory definition becomes active.']
  is_exclusive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the territory is exclusive to a single sales channel or partner.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent business review of the territory definition.']
  market_share_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Desired market share percentage for the territory.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the territory for resource allocation.']
  quota_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary sales quota assigned to the territory for the planning period.']
  region varchar [note: 'Higher‑level geographic region that groups multiple territories.']
  sales_target_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Projected sales revenue target for the territory.']
  state_province_list varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of states or provinces included in the territory.']
  tdp_coverage_target varchar [note: 'Target number of distribution points the territory should reach.']
  territory_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the territory in sales and planning systems.']
  territory_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description providing additional context about the territory.']
  territory_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the sales territory.']
  territory_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the territory.']
  territory_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the territory: geographic, account‑based, or channel‑based.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the territory record.']

  Note: 'Geographic and account-based sales territory definition used to assign sales reps, set quotas, and measure performance. Captures territory code, territory name, territory type (geographic, account-based, channel-based), region, district, country, state/province list, assigned channel (retail, foodservice, DTC), ACV (All Commodity Volume) coverage target, TDP (Total Distribution Points) target, number of assigned accounts, effective start and end dates, and territory manager reference. Enables territory planning, quota allocation, and sales performance measurement.'
}

Table sales.quota {
  quota_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the quota record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales manager or executive who approved the quota.']
  sales_rep_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales representative to whom the quota is assigned.']
  territory_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales territory or region for the quota assignment.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date the quota was formally approved.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the quota record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary quotas (e.g., USD, EUR).']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'Fiscal quarter (1‑4) within the fiscal year for the quota.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year to which the quota belongs.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional remarks, comments, or audit notes related to the quota.']
  period_end date [note: 'Last calendar date of the quota period; nullable for open‑ended periods.']
  period_start date [note: 'First calendar date of the quota period (e.g., start of month, quarter, or year).']
  period_type varchar [note: 'Granularity of the quota period (month, quarter, or year).']
  quota_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the purpose or special conditions of the quota.']
  quota_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the quota record.']
  quota_type varchar [note: 'Business dimension the quota targets (e.g., revenue, case volume, new distribution points, total distribution points, ACV, new SKU introductions).']
  region varchar [note: 'High‑level geographic region (e.g., NA, EMEA, APAC) associated with the quota.']
  source varchar [note: 'Origin of the quota allocation (top‑down corporate allocation, bottom‑up rep submission, or manual adjustment).']
  stretch_target_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Optional aggressive target above the primary quota to incentivize over‑performance.']
  target_confidence varchar [note: 'Confidence rating of the target based on forecasting methodology.']
  target_measurement_method varchar [note: 'Method used to derive the target (e.g., forecast, historical average, market share model).']
  target_units varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the target value (e.g., USD, cases, points).']
  target_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Primary numeric target for the quota (e.g., $1,000,000 revenue or 200,000 cases).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the quota record.']

  Note: 'Sales quota assignment record defining revenue, volume, and distribution targets for a sales rep or territory over a defined period. Captures quota period (month, quarter, year), quota type (revenue, case volume, new distribution points, TDP), assigned rep or territory, target value, stretch target value, quota currency, quota status (draft, approved, active), approval date, approver reference, and quota source (top-down allocation, bottom-up submission). Enables sales performance management and incentive compensation calculation.'
}

Table sales.pos_transaction {
  pos_transaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the POS transaction record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Needed for POS Sales Attribution report, attributing each transaction to the retailer account for revenue and performance analysis.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the product (SKU) sold.']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Each POS sale updates the corresponding stock position; linking POS transaction to stock_position enables real‑time inventory balance reconciliation.']
  average_price decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average price per unit for this line (dollar_sales / units_sold).']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the monetary values.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the POS record (retailer feed, syndicated provider, or direct partnership).']
  display_flag boolean [note: 'True if the product was featured on a shelf display at time of sale.']
  dollar_sales decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total sales amount in dollars for the line (units × price).']
  feature_flag boolean [note: 'True if the product was part of a special feature (e.g., end‑cap).']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the line within the receipt.']
  month_of_year varchar [note: 'Month number (1‑12) for the transaction date.']
  pos_terminal_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the point‑of‑sale terminal that captured the scan.']
  price_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the price applied (regular, promotional, discount).']
  product_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category of the product (e.g., Beverage, Snack).']
  product_subcategory varchar [note: 'More specific subcategory within the product category.']
  promotion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the unit was sold under a promotional price.']
  receipt_number varchar [note: 'Identifier linking this line to the parent receipt or transaction header.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was first ingested into the lakehouse.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  retailer_banner varchar [note: 'Name of the retailer banner or chain (e.g., Walmart, Kroger).']
  sales_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the sale was made.']
  scan_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the barcode was scanned by the terminal.']
  store_format varchar [note: 'Classification of the store format.']
  store_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region where the store is located (e.g., Northeast, Midwest).']
  transaction_date date [note: 'Calendar date of the transaction (store local time).']
  transaction_day_of_week varchar [note: 'Day of week on which the transaction occurred. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Mon|Tue|Wed|Thu|Fri|Sat|Sun — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  transaction_reference_number varchar [note: 'Business‑level identifier for the transaction (may be receipt number).']
  transaction_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the transaction line.']
  transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the transaction was recorded.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Nature of the transaction line (sale, return, or inventory adjustment).']
  transaction_week varchar [note: 'Week number (1‑52) derived from transaction_date.']
  units_sold varchar [note: 'Number of units of the product scanned in this line.']
  week_of_year varchar [note: 'ISO week number for the transaction date.']
  year varchar [note: 'Four‑digit year of the transaction.']

  Note: 'Point-of-sale scan data record capturing consumer purchase activity at the retail store level, sourced from retailer POS feeds, syndicated data providers (Nielsen/IRI/Circana/SPINS), and direct retailer data sharing programs (e.g., Walmart Luminate, Kroger 84.51°). Captures store ID, retailer banner, UPC/GTIN, transaction date/week, units sold, dollar sales, average retail price, promoted/display/feature flags, and data source. SSOT for raw/ingested POS scan data within the enterprise; analytics and insights domains consume this data for derived metrics (market share, promotional lift, price elasticity) but do not own the source records. Enables sell-through velocity analysis, promotional lift measurement, share tracking, and category management insights at the store-week-SKU grain.'
}

Table sales.distribution_point {
  distribution_point_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the distribution point record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Needed for Distribution Planning Dashboard, associating each distribution point with the retailer account it serves to allocate inventory.']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Distribution points capture presence of each brand; linking allows brand distribution metrics and market coverage reporting.']
  center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.distribution_center. Business justification: Supports Distribution Point Allocation report linking retailer points to the supplying distribution center for inventory planning.']
  product_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.product_registration. Business justification: Distribution planning uses product registration data to ensure only compliant products are stocked at each point.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Distribution analytics require linking each distribution point record to the exact SKU to track inventory, velocity, and shelf‑life per product.']
  acv_change_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percent change in ACV weighted distribution versus prior period.']
  acv_weighted_distribution_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of ACV represented by outlets where the SKU is active, weighted by sales volume.']
  classification_type varchar [note: 'Category of outlet: retail store, foodservice location, or direct‑to‑consumer channel.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the distribution point record was first created in the system.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the syndicated data snapshot (Nielsen, IRI, or Circana).']
  distribution_point_code varchar [note: 'Business‑assigned code uniquely identifying the distribution point within the organization.']
  distribution_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the SKU at the outlet: authorized (contracted), active (selling), or voided (no longer carried).']
  in_stock_flag boolean [note: 'True if the SKU is currently stocked at the outlet; false otherwise.']
  location_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the outlet or distribution point.']
  market_area varchar [note: 'Geographic market region or sales territory for the outlet.']
  numeric_distribution_change_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percent change in numeric distribution versus prior period.']
  numeric_distribution_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total outlets carrying the SKU, regardless of sales weight.']
  period_over_period_change_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percent change of the primary metric compared to the previous period.']
  period_type varchar [note: 'Aggregation window of the snapshot: weekly, 4‑week, 13‑week, or 52‑week.']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the record itself: active, inactive, or archived.']
  retailer_banner varchar [note: 'Brand or banner under which the outlet operates (e.g., Walmart, Kroger, Starbucks).']
  snapshot_date date [note: 'Date of the syndicated data snapshot (weekly, 4‑week, etc.).']
  tdp_change_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percent change in TDP contribution versus prior period.']
  tdp_contribution_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Share of total distribution points contributed by the outlet for the SKU.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the distribution point record.']
  velocity_units_per_point decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average units sold per distribution point for the SKU over the snapshot period.']
  void_date date [note: 'Date on which the SKU distribution was terminated at the outlet.']

  Note: 'SSOT for SKU-level distribution presence, measurement, and periodic syndicated metric snapshots at retail and foodservice locations. Captures store/outlet ID, retailer banner, SKU/GTIN, distribution status (authorized, active, voided), ACV weighted distribution percentage, TDP (Total Distribution Points) contribution, numeric distribution percentage, velocity (units per point of distribution), in-stock status, void date, and periodic snapshot data by time period (weekly, 4-week, 13-week, 52-week) including snapshot date, market area, data source (Nielsen/IRI/Circana), and period-over-period change metrics. Enables distribution health monitoring, new item launch tracking, void management, TDP target attainment reporting, ACV trend analysis, and syndicated data reconciliation. Subsumes all periodic ACV/TDP snapshot reporting previously modeled separately.'
}

Table sales.planogram {
  planogram_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the planogram record.']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Planograms define shelf layout for a brand; linking supports brand compliance audits and shelf‑share analysis.']
  concept_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.concept. Business justification: Planogram design uses the product concept from R&D to allocate shelf space for upcoming launches, requiring a link to the concept entity.']
  adjacency_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text notes on required adjacency or separation constraints for the SKU.']
  category_captain_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the category is managed by a category captain (true/false).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the planogram record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  days_of_supply varchar [note: 'Projected number of days the SKU will remain on shelf based on current inventory and velocity.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the planogram expires or is superseded (nullable for open‑ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the planogram becomes active in stores.']
  facings varchar [note: 'Count of product facings allocated to the SKU.']
  horizontal_position varchar [note: 'Sequential position from left to right within the shelf section.']
  last_sync_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent extraction from the source system.']
  planogram_category varchar [note: 'Retail category (e.g., Snacks, Beverages) for which the planogram is defined.']
  planogram_code varchar [note: 'External reference code used by retail partners to identify the planogram.']
  planogram_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the planogram used for reporting and search.']
  planogram_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the planogram.']
  planogram_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the planogram is for a standard category, a specific store, or a promotional layout.']
  retailer_banner varchar [note: 'Retail chain or banner to which the planogram applies (e.g., Walmart, Kroger).']
  shelf_depth_units varchar [note: 'Depth of the shelf allocated to the SKU measured in unit count (e.g., cans).']
  shelf_level varchar [note: 'Vertical level on the shelf where the SKU resides.']
  shelf_section varchar [note: 'Logical section of the shelf (e.g., Aisle 3, Endcap) where the SKU is placed.']
  shelf_width_inches decimal(6,2) [note: 'Physical width of the shelf space assigned to the SKU (inches).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that generated the planogram data.']
  store_format varchar [note: 'Store format classification (e.g., Supercenter, Convenience, Club).']
  total_facings varchar [note: 'Sum of all facings across SKUs in the planogram.']
  total_linear_feet decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total linear shelf space allocated in the planogram (feet).']
  total_space_sqft decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total shelf area allocated in square feet, derived from linear feet and shelf depth.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the planogram record.']
  velocity_rank varchar [note: 'Rank of the SKU\'s sales velocity within the category (1 = highest).']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the planogram allowing version control and audit.']

  Note: 'Retail shelf planogram document defining the approved shelf layout, space allocation, and individual SKU placement details for Food Beverage products within a retailer category, captured as a single consolidated record. Header: planogram name, retailer banner, store format, category, version, effective dates, total linear feet, total facings, category captain flag, and status. SKU-level placement detail: shelf section, shelf level (top, middle, bottom, floor), horizontal position, number of facings, shelf depth (units), shelf width (inches), days of supply on shelf, velocity rank within category, and adjacency notes. Source: JDA/Blue Yonder Space Planning. Enables category management, shelf space optimization, space-to-sales analysis, and planogram compliance auditing.'
}

Table sales.call {
  call_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for call']
  campaign_execution_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign_execution. Business justification: Sales calls are executed under a specific campaign execution; linking enables call effectiveness measurement against campaign goals.']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: Required for Sales Call Management Report linking each call to the store contact person (store manager) to track engagement and follow‑up actions.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer account visited during the call.']
  outlet_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific location within the store (e.g., aisle, section).']
  plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the planned call schedule or campaign.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: Sales calls presenting new product concepts record the related R&D project to capture feedback and align field activities with development status.']
  sales_rep_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales representative who performed the call.']
  store_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the retail store or outlet where the call took place.']
  call_category varchar [note: 'High‑level categorization of the call purpose.']
  call_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the sales call occurred (actual event time).']
  call_result varchar [note: 'Outcome classification of the call.']
  call_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the call.']
  call_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the sales call purpose.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Medium through which the call was conducted.']
  competitive_observations varchar [note: 'Notes on competitor activity or product placement observed during the call.']
  competitor_presented varchar [note: 'Identifier of any competitor SKU shown to the customer.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the call record was first created in the system.']
  crm_activity_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the related activity record in Salesforce CRM.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values.']
  display_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Compliance status of promotional displays or fixtures.']
  display_type varchar [note: 'Type of promotional display used (e.g., endcap, shelf, floor stand).']
  duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Length of the call measured in minutes.']
  feedback_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric rating (e.g., 0‑10) provided by the customer or rep after the call.']
  follow_up_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next interaction or action.']
  is_new_item_presented boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a new product was presented during the call.']
  is_promo_sold_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether promotional inventory was sold in during the call.']
  next_steps varchar [note: 'Planned follow‑up actions resulting from the call.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes captured by the sales rep during or after the call.']
  objectives varchar [note: 'Business objectives or goals set for the sales call.']
  orders_taken_discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total discount amount applied to orders taken during the call.']
  orders_taken_gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total gross monetary value of orders taken during the call before discounts.']
  orders_taken_net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net monetary value of orders taken after discounts.']
  outcomes varchar [note: 'Results achieved during the call, including any commitments or insights.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the call for planning purposes.']
  reason varchar [note: 'Specific reason or trigger for the sales call.']
  region varchar [note: 'Geographic region code where the call took place.']
  shelf_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Compliance status of product shelf placement.']
  shelf_space_sqft decimal(10,2) [note: 'Amount of shelf space (in square feet) allocated to the product during the call.']
  skus_presented varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of SKU identifiers shown to the customer.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Source system that originated the call record.']
  territory varchar [note: 'Sales territory associated with the call.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User name of the person who last updated the call record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the call record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User name of the person who created the call record.']

  Note: 'Field sales activity record capturing customer visits, account calls, and sales interactions conducted by sales reps. Captures call date, call type (in-store visit, buyer meeting, category review, new item presentation, promotional sell-in, competitive audit), sales rep reference, customer account, store/outlet location, call objectives, call outcomes, SKUs presented, orders taken during call, competitive observations, shelf compliance findings, display compliance status, next steps, and Salesforce CRM activity reference. Enables sales force effectiveness measurement and call planning.'
}

Table sales.broker {
  broker_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the broker record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Needed for Broker‑Account Assignment report, showing which broker services each customer account for commission calculations.']
  coverage_territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.territory. Business justification: Broker coverage is defined by territory; replacing free‑text field with FK improves consistency and enables territory‑level reporting.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the broker\'s mailing address.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the broker\'s mailing address, if applicable.']
  bank_account_number varchar [note: 'Broker\'s bank account number for commission payouts.']
  bank_routing_number varchar [note: 'Routing number for the broker\'s bank account.']
  broker_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the broker organization or individual.']
  broker_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the broker relationship.']
  broker_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the broker based on the channel or market they serve.']
  city varchar [note: 'City component of the broker\'s address.']
  commission_basis varchar [note: 'Metric on which the commission rate is calculated.']
  commission_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage commission the broker earns on qualifying sales.']
  contract_effective_from date [note: 'Start date when the broker contract becomes binding.']
  contract_effective_until date [note: 'End date of the broker contract; null if open‑ended.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'External reference number for the broker agreement.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the broker contract.']
  country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code of the broker\'s location.']
  covered_channels varchar [note: 'Sales channels (e.g., retail, foodservice, DSD) the broker operates in.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the broker record was first created in the system.']
  last_performance_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent performance evaluation for the broker.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or remarks about the broker.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms agreed with the broker.']
  performance_tier varchar [note: 'Tier assigned to the broker based on sales performance.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'ZIP or postal code for the broker\'s address.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary broker contact.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the main person to contact at the broker.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Telephone number for the primary broker contact.']
  sap_vendor_number varchar [note: 'Vendor number assigned to the broker in SAP S/4HANA.']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province component of the broker\'s address.']
  tax_identifier varchar [note: 'Government‑issued tax identifier for the broker (e.g., EIN).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the broker record.']

  Note: 'Master record for food and beverage sales brokers and manufacturer representatives who sell Food Beverage products on behalf of the company in specific channels or geographies. Captures broker ID, broker company name, broker type (retail broker, foodservice broker, DSD broker, export agent), coverage territory, covered channels, covered customer accounts, commission rate structure, commission basis (gross sales, net sales, cases), contract effective dates, performance tier, primary contact, and SAP vendor number. SSOT for broker identity and commercial terms within the sales domain; customer domain owns the broader customer/partner entity but sales owns the broker-as-selling-agent relationship, commission structures, and performance management. Enables broker management, commission calculation, and channel coverage governance.'
}

Table sales.credit_memo {
  credit_memo_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the credit memo record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer receiving the credit.']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Credit memo issuance is approved by a finance employee; required for regulatory reporting and reconciliation.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Credit memos reverse revenue entries; linking to a GL account ensures proper reversal posting and auditability.']
  order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales order that generated the original invoice.']
  original_credit_memo_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on credit_memo (original_credit_memo_id)']
  original_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original invoice that the credit memo offsets.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Return processing links credit memos to the originating purchase order for inventory and financial adjustments.']
  reversal_credit_memo_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the credit memo that this record reverses.']
  sales_rep_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales rep associated with the original sale.']
  adjustment_type varchar [note: 'Category of financial adjustment represented by the credit memo.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval workflow status of the credit memo.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who approved the credit memo.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the credit memo was approved.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes or comments.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the credit memo record was first created in the system.']
  credit_amount_gross decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total credit amount before taxes and adjustments.']
  credit_memo_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the credit memo (e.g., CM-202300001).']
  credit_memo_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the credit memo.']
  credit_memo_type varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the credit memo.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the credit amounts.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'Identifier used by external partners or EDI for the credit memo.']
  is_manual_entry boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the credit memo was entered manually (true) or generated automatically (false).']
  is_processed boolean [note: 'True when the credit memo has been fully processed in downstream systems.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date the credit memo was issued to the customer.']
  ledger_posted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the credit memo was posted to the ledger.']
  net_credit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Net amount to be credited to the customer after tax.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Payment terms applicable to the credit (e.g., Net 30).']
  posting_to_ledger_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the credit memo has been posted to the general ledger.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standard code indicating why the credit memo was issued.']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description providing details of the credit memo reason.']
  related_order_number varchar [note: 'Business number of the related sales order.']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'True if this credit memo reverses a previous credit.']
  sales_organization varchar [note: 'Organizational unit responsible for the sale.']
  sales_region varchar [note: 'Geographic sales region linked to the original transaction.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of origin for the credit memo record.']
  tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Tax component associated with the credit memo.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the tax regime applied to the credit.']
  tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax rate (percentage) used to calculate tax_amount.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who last updated the record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the credit memo record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who created the record.']

  Note: 'Credit memo document issued to customers for returns, pricing adjustments, quality claims, or billing corrections — captures credit memo number, issue date, reason code, original invoice reference, credit amount, and approval status'
}

Table sales.rebate_agreement {
  rebate_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the rebate agreement record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Rebate costs are charged to a cost center to enable accurate cost tracking and budget variance analysis.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer or account receiving the rebate.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Rebate expense must be posted to a specific GL account for compliance and rebate settlement reporting.']
  purchase_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_contract. Business justification: Rebate calculations depend on the underlying purchase contract with the supplier, requiring a direct link.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: Rebate calculations depend on the R&D project\'s cost basis, so linking rebate agreements to the associated rd_project tracks eligibility and financial impact.']
  renewed_rebate_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on rebate_agreement (renewed_rebate_agreement_id)']
  sales_contract_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the underlying sales contract linked to this rebate agreement.']
  target_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.sustainability_target. Business justification: Rebate agreements are conditioned on meeting specific sustainability targets, requiring linkage for eligibility verification.']
  accrual_method varchar [note: 'Whether rebates are accrued over time or paid cash at settlement.']
  agreement_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the rebate agreement.']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier or code for the rebate agreement.']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the agreement (e.g., volume‑based, growth‑based, or mixed).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the rebate agreement record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values in the agreement.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the rebate agreement becomes binding.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the rebate agreement expires or is terminated (null for open‑ended).']
  eligibility_criteria varchar [note: 'Business rules that determine eligibility (e.g., product mix, channel, promotional constraints).']
  last_settlement_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent rebate settlement.']
  max_rebate_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cap on the total rebate payable under the agreement.']
  min_rebate_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Floor amount for rebate eligibility, if applicable.']
  next_settlement_date date [note: 'Planned date for the upcoming rebate settlement.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information or comments.']
  qualifying_product_category varchar [note: 'Product category or categories that count toward rebate thresholds.']
  rebate_agreement_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the agreement purpose and scope.']
  rebate_agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the agreement.']
  rebate_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of sales value to be rebated once thresholds are met.']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Administrative status of the record for data‑governance purposes.']
  region varchar [note: 'Region or market where the rebate agreement applies (ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 code).']
  sales_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which sales are captured for rebate calculation.']
  settlement_frequency varchar [note: 'How often rebate settlements are processed.']
  settlement_method varchar [note: 'Method used to settle rebate amounts with the customer.']
  threshold_quantity decimal(15,2) [note: 'Quantity threshold that triggers rebate eligibility (e.g., total cases purchased).']
  threshold_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the threshold quantity.']
  tier_levels varchar [note: 'Definition of tier thresholds and corresponding rebate percentages, stored as a structured string.']
  tiered_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement uses tiered rebate structures.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the rebate agreement record.']

  Note: 'Customer rebate agreement master record defining volume-based or growth-based rebate programs offered to retail and foodservice accounts — captures agreement terms, qualifying thresholds, rebate percentages, settlement frequency, and accrual method'
}

Table sales.store {
  store_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for store']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the franchisee entity that owns the store.']
  parent_store_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on store (parent_store_id)']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the store.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information (suite, unit, etc.).']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the store is located.']
  closing_date date [note: 'Date the store ceased operations, if applicable.']
  country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the store location.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the store record was first created in the system.']
  district varchar [note: 'Sub‑region or district within a region.']
  is_franchise boolean [note: 'True if the store operates under a franchise agreement.']
  last_inventory_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent full inventory count.']
  latitude double [note: 'Geographic latitude of the store in decimal degrees.']
  longitude double [note: 'Geographic longitude of the store in decimal degrees.']
  manager_email varchar [note: 'Business email address of the store manager.']
  manager_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the store manager.']
  manager_phone varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the store manager.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the store, often displayed to customers and in internal dashboards.']
  opening_date date [note: 'Date the store first opened for business.']
  opening_hours varchar [note: 'Standard weekly opening hours expressed in free‑text format.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the store address.']
  region varchar [note: 'Higher‑level geographic region grouping stores for reporting.']
  sales_area_sqft decimal(10,2) [note: 'Retail space dedicated to sales activities, excluding back‑office areas.']
  square_footage decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total retail floor area of the store in square feet.']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province of the store location.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the store.']
  status_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation for the current status, e.g., renovation, lease expiry.']
  store_cluster varchar [note: 'Group of stores with similar performance characteristics for analytics.']
  store_format varchar [note: 'Operational format describing service style.']
  store_number varchar [note: 'Business-assigned alphanumeric identifier used in retail systems and reporting.']
  timezone varchar [note: 'IANA time‑zone identifier for the store location.']
  type varchar [note: 'Classification of the store based on ownership and format.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the store record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for store. Referenced by store_id.'
}

Table sales.sales_organization {
  sales_organization_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for sales_organization']
  parent_org_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the immediate parent sales organization in the hierarchy.']
  parent_sales_organization_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on sales_organization (parent_sales_organization_id)']
  address varchar [note: 'Primary mailing address of the sales organization.']
  annual_sales_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total sales revenue generated by the organization in the most recent fiscal year.']
  classification varchar [note: 'Category indicating whether the organization is internal, external, or a partner.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Internal cost‑center identifier linked to the sales organization for budgeting.']
  country varchar [note: 'ISO‑3 country code of the primary country for the sales organization.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the sales organization’s purpose or scope.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the sales organization became operational.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the sales organization ceased operations (null if still active).']
  external_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales organization in an external ERP/CRM system.']
  fiscal_year_start_month varchar [note: 'Numeric month (1‑12) that marks the start of the organization’s fiscal year.']
  legal_entity_name varchar [note: 'Official legal name of the organization as registered.']
  legal_entity_type varchar [note: 'Legal structure of the organization.']
  manager_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person responsible for the sales organization.']
  market_segment varchar [note: 'Target market segment for the sales organization.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the sales organization.']
  number_of_employees varchar [note: 'Total headcount employed by the sales organization.']

  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Main telephone number for the sales organization’s point of contact.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the sales organization record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the sales organization record.']
  region varchar [note: 'Geographic region (e.g., North America, EMEA) where the sales organization operates.']
  reporting_currency varchar [note: 'Currency used for financial reporting of the organization.']
  sales_channel varchar [note: 'Primary channel through which the organization sells (e.g., retail, foodservice).']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the sales organization.']
  tax_number varchar [note: 'Government‑issued tax identifier for the organization.']
  timezone varchar [note: 'IANA timezone identifier for the organization’s primary location.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'System user or process that performed the most recent update.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'System user or process that created the record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for sales_organization. Referenced by sales_org_id.'
}

Table sales.distribution_channel {

  channel_category varchar [note: 'General delivery model of the channel.']

  channel_status_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation for why the channel is in its current status.']
  channel_subtype varchar [note: 'Fine‑grained classification of the channel (e.g., flagship store, marketplace).']
  channel_type varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the channel (e.g., retail, foodservice, direct‑to‑consumer).']
  code varchar [note: 'Compact alphanumeric code representing the channel, used in reporting and integrations.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the channel meets internal and regulatory compliance requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the distribution channel record was first created in the system.']
  data_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (0‑100) representing the overall data quality of the channel record.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the channel\'s purpose, scope, and characteristics.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the channel started operating or was first made available.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the channel is retired or no longer used (null if open‑ended).']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic coverage level of the channel.']
  is_primary boolean [note: 'True if this channel is the primary distribution route for its associated product line.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the channel information was last reviewed for accuracy.']
  market_segment varchar [note: 'Business market segment that the channel primarily serves.']
  name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the distribution channel (e.g., Retail Store, Online Direct).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Supplementary information or comments about the channel.']
  review_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between scheduled reviews of the channel record.']
  sales_channel_group varchar [note: 'Logical grouping name for related channels (e.g., North America Retail).']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the channel.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the channel record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for distribution_channel. Referenced by distribution_channel_id.'
}

Table trade.promotion_plan {
  promotion_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the promotion plan record.']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Brand‑level promotion planning uses this FK to allocate spend and track performance per brand.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who approved the promotion plan.']
  fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trade.fund. Business justification: Promotion plans allocate financial resources from funds; linking promotion_plan to fund enables budget tracking.']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list. Business justification: Promotion planning requires linking each promotion plan to the specific price list used for its promotional pricing, enabling the Promotion Planning Report to trace pricing decisions.']
  primary_promotion_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the retailer account targeted by the promotion.']
  promotion_account_id varchar [note: 'FK to customer.account.account_id — Trade promotions target specific customer accounts. Without this FK, cannot query which promotions are planned for which retailer — core TPM functionality.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: Promotion plans are created for new product launches; linking to rd_project enables ROI analysis per R&D project.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Promotion plans are often co‑funded by suppliers; the Promotion Planning process records the responsible supplier for budgeting and ROI analysis.']
