  target_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.sustainability_target. Business justification: Sustainability Impact Assessment Report links each promotion plan to the ESG target it supports, enabling carbon‑reduction tracking of promotional spend.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval workflow status for the promotion plan.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the promotion plan received final approval.']
  baseline_volume varchar [note: 'Projected sales volume without the promotion (historical baseline).']
  channel varchar [note: 'Primary sales channel targeted by the promotion.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the promotion plan record was created.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Planned end date when the promotion expires.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Planned start date when the promotion becomes active.']
  estimated_roi_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Projected return on investment expressed as a percentage.']
  forecast_created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the analyst who created the forecast.']
  forecast_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the forecast was generated.']
  forecast_method varchar [note: 'Method used to generate the promotion forecast.']
  forecast_version varchar [note: 'Identifier for the version of the forecast model or scenario.']
  funding_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the financial resources allocated to the promotion.']
  incremental_lift_estimate varchar [note: 'Estimated additional units sold due to the promotion.']
  is_exclusive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the promotion is exclusive to the retailer (true) or shared with other retailers (false).']
  net_revenue_impact decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected net revenue change after accounting for trade spend and incremental sales.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or special instructions for the promotion.']
  promotion_code varchar [note: 'External code used by trade marketing to reference the promotion plan.']
  promotion_mechanic varchar [note: 'Mechanic used to deliver the promotion (e.g., discount, rebate, free gift).']
  promotion_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the promotion plan for reporting and UI display.']
  promotion_plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the promotion plan.']
  promotion_type varchar [note: 'Category of promotional event (e.g., temporary price reduction, display, BOGO).']
  promotional_budget_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary fields in the promotion plan.']
  region varchar [note: 'Geographic region or market where the promotion is executed (e.g., NA, EU).']
  sku_ids varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of SKU identifiers participating in the promotion.']
  target_sales_units varchar [note: 'Sales unit target set for the promotion period.']
  target_sales_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary sales target (e.g., USD) for the promotion.']
  total_promoted_volume varchar [note: 'Sum of baseline and incremental lift – total units expected to be sold under the promotion.']
  trade_spend_investment decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary amount allocated to the promotion (discounts, rebates, fees).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the promotion plan.']

  Note: 'Master record for trade promotion plans created by sales/trade marketing teams. Defines the complete promotional event including promotion type (TPR, display, feature ad, BOGO, scan-back), planned start/end dates, target retailer accounts, participating SKUs, funding source, promotional mechanic, approval status, and volume/ROI forecast. Includes planning-stage forecasts: baseline volume, incremental lift estimate, total promoted volume, net revenue impact, trade spend investment, estimated ROI, forecast method (statistical model, historical analogy, sales rep input), and forecast version. Serves as the authoritative header for all downstream execution, accrual, deduction, and settlement tracking. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA SD, TPM modules, and SAP IBP demand sensing.'
}

Table trade.promotion_event {
  promotion_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the promotion event record.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Required for Campaign‑Promotion ROI report linking each promotion event to its originating marketing campaign.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Promotion expense must be posted to a Cost Center for budgeting and P&L reporting; finance cost_center tracks these allocations.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier who created the promotion event record.']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list. Business justification: Promotion events generate price list updates; linking event to the price list allows the Promotion Execution Dashboard to associate price changes with the originating event.']
  promotion_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent promotion plan.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: Promotion events execute the launch of a product defined by an R&D project; linking tracks spend per project.']
  retailer_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the retailer account where the promotion was executed.']
  store_cluster_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the store cluster (geographic grouping) for the promotion execution.']
  actual_end_date date [note: 'Actual end date when the promotion concluded at the retailer.']
  actual_revenue decimal(15,2) [note: 'Actual revenue generated by the promotion.']
  actual_sales_volume varchar [note: 'Actual sales volume achieved during the promotion.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'Actual start date when the promotion began at the retailer.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the promotion event record was approved by audit.']
  audit_status varchar [note: 'Internal audit status of the promotion event record.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Sales channel(s) where the promotion was applied.']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes regarding compliance observations.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Compliance assessment result for the promotion execution.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the promotion event record was created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for monetary amounts.']
  event_number varchar [note: 'Business reference number for the promotion event.']
  execution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the promotion event was actually executed in the field.']
  field_verification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the field verification.']
  is_field_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether field execution was verified by a supervisor.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who last modified the promotion event record.']
  mechanic_type varchar [note: 'Type of promotional mechanic deployed for the event.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes about the promotion event.']
  planned_end_date date [note: 'Planned end date of the promotion as per the promotion plan.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Planned start date of the promotion as per the promotion plan.']
  product_category varchar [note: 'High-level product category targeted by the promotion.']
  promotion_event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the promotion event.']
  promotion_objective varchar [note: 'Primary business objective of the promotion.']
  promotion_type varchar [note: 'Category of promotion type applied.']
  promotional_materials_delivered boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if promotional materials were delivered to the store.']
  promotional_materials_quantity varchar [note: 'Quantity of promotional materials delivered.']
  promotional_materials_type varchar [note: 'Type of promotional materials used for the event.']
  promotional_spend_discount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total discount or chargeback amount applied to the promotional spend.']
  promotional_spend_gross decimal(15,2) [note: 'Gross promotional spend amount before any deductions.']
  promotional_spend_net decimal(15,2) [note: 'Net promotional spend after discounts and chargebacks.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter region code (e.g., USA, CAN) for the promotion execution.']
  sku_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of SKUs included in the promotion.']
  store_count_actual varchar [note: 'Number of stores that actually executed the promotion.']
  store_count_planned varchar [note: 'Number of stores originally scheduled to participate in the promotion.']
  target_revenue decimal(15,2) [note: 'Planned revenue target for the promotion.']
  target_sales_volume varchar [note: 'Planned sales volume target (units) for the promotion.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the promotion event record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the execution of a trade promotion event at a specific retailer account and store cluster. Tracks actual execution dates, participating store count, promotional mechanic deployed (e.g., end-cap display, circular feature, TPR shelf tag), compliance status, and field execution confirmation. Links to the parent promotion plan and captures variance between planned and actual execution. Sourced from Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud and field execution data.'
}

Table trade.promotion_line {
  promotion_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the promotion line item.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the retailer receiving the promotion.']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Brand reporting requires each promotion line (SKU) to be associated with its brand for ACV and TDP calculations.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Each promotion line generates revenue/expense entries; linking to GL Account enables accurate journal posting and audit.']
  nutrition_label_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.nutrition_label. Business justification: Each promotional line must reference the correct nutrition label for the SKU to meet labeling regulations and marketing material compliance.']
  price_list_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list_line. Business justification: Each promotion line adjusts a specific price list line; the link supports the Promotion Line‑to‑Price Traceability report used by pricing analysts.']
  promotion_event_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent promotion plan (header).']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Promotion line records financial impact per SKU; adding SKU FK enables detailed analysis.']
  target_customer_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_segment. Business justification: REQUIRED: Promotion lines are built to target specific customer segments; the segment ID is needed for segment‑based promotion planning reports.']
  allocation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to allocate promotion funding across SKUs.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the promotion line.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the promotion line was approved or rejected.']
  baseline_volume decimal(12,2) [note: 'Expected sales volume without the promotion.']
  bill_back_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Rate used for bill‑back calculations on promotional sales.']
  budget_amount decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total budget allocated for the promotion line.']
  cost_of_goods_sold_per_unit decimal(12,4) [note: 'COGS for the SKU used to calculate margin on promotional sales.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the promotion line record was created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary amounts.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the promotion ends for this line.']
  funding_amount_per_unit decimal(12,4) [note: 'Funding allocated to the retailer per unit sold under the promotion.']
  incremental_volume_target decimal(12,2) [note: 'Targeted additional units to be sold beyond baseline.']
  is_exclusive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the promotion is exclusive to the retailer.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential order of the line within the promotion.']
  margin_per_unit decimal(12,4) [note: 'Profit margin per unit after accounting for promotion funding.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional remarks or instructions.']
  off_invoice_allowance_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Rate of off‑invoice allowance expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.10 = 10%).']
  planned_price decimal(12,4) [note: 'Planned promotional price per unit for the SKU.']
  planned_volume_lift decimal(12,2) [note: 'Projected increase in units sold due to the promotion.']
  promotion_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category of the promotion strategy.']
  promotion_line_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the promotion line.']
  promotion_type varchar [note: 'Mechanism of the promotion applied to the SKU.']
  quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Quantity of units allocated or expected for the promotion line.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Geographic region code for the promotion line.']
  sales_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the promotion is offered.']
  scan_back_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Rate applied to retailer‑scanned sales for reimbursement.']
  sku varchar [note: 'SKU of the product included in the promotion line.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the promotion becomes effective for this line.']
  target_store_type varchar [note: 'Type of store (e.g., supermarket, convenience) targeted by the promotion.']
  total_funding_amount decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total monetary funding for the promotion line (derived from volume and per‑unit funding).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the promotion line record.']

  Note: 'Line-item detail within a trade promotion plan representing a specific SKU or product group included in the promotion. Captures planned promotional price, off-invoice allowance rate, scan-back rate, bill-back rate, planned volume lift, baseline volume, incremental volume target, and funding amount per unit. Enables SKU-level trade spend allocation and ROI measurement. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA SD condition records and TPM line detail.'
}

Table trade.fund {
  fund_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each trade fund record.']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Trade fund allocation is performed per brand; this FK enables fund‑to‑brand reconciliation reports.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Internal party (e.g., trade manager) responsible for the fund.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Trade funds are allocated to specific Profit Centers for performance measurement and internal reporting.']
  target_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.sustainability_target. Business justification: Trade fund allocations are tracked against specific ESG targets for the Trade Fund ESG Allocation Report.']
  accrued_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount accrued based on spend that has been recognized but not yet paid.']
  allocation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to distribute the fund across eligible promotions.']
  allocation_scope varchar [note: 'Level at which the fund is allocated (e.g., retailer, sales territory, brand/category).']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the fund was approved by finance or trade management.']
  classification varchar [note: 'High‑level category of the fund purpose (e.g., marketing, price promotion, slotting).']
  committed_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Sum of amounts already committed to promotion plans.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the fund record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the fund amounts.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the fund becomes effective for spend.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the fund expires or is no longer available (nullable for open‑ended).']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year to which the fund belongs.']
  fund_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the trade fund in external systems and contracts.']
  fund_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description providing context or notes about the fund.']
  fund_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the trade fund for reporting and analysis.']
  fund_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the fund.']
  fund_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the fund is a lump‑sum, accrual‑based, or fixed allowance allocation.']
  paid_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount that has been paid out to retailers or partners.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter code representing the geographic region for the fund allocation.']
  remaining_balance decimal(15,2) [note: 'Remaining budget available for future commitments.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that supplied the fund data (e.g., SAP S/4HANA, Oracle ERP).']
  total_approved_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total budget approved for the fund, expressed in the fund currency.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the fund record.']

  Note: 'Master record for trade spending funds and budgets allocated to retailer accounts, sales territories, or brand/category buckets. Tracks fund type (lump sum, accrual-based, fixed allowance), total approved budget, amount committed to promotion plans, amount accrued, amount paid, and remaining balance. Supports trade spend governance and budget control. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA CO and TPM fund management modules.'
}

Table trade.accrual {
  accrual_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the accrual record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the retailer with which the promotion is executed.']
  promotion_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the promotional agreement that generated this accrual.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Financial accruals must be tied to a specific SKU for GAAP reporting; linking ensures accurate cost allocation per product.']
  accrual_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the accrual, used in reporting and audit trails.']
  accrual_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the accrual record.']
  accrual_type varchar [note: 'Mechanism by which the accrual is calculated (e.g., off‑invoice, bill‑back, scan‑back, lump‑sum).']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the accrual liability recognized for the period.']
  basis varchar [note: 'Basis used to compute the accrual (per case, per unit, or percentage of net sales).']
  basis_quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Quantity (cases, units, etc.) used as the denominator for the accrual calculation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the accrual record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code of the accrual amount.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the accrual becomes effective for accounting purposes.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the accrual liability expires or is no longer valid.']
  gl_account varchar [note: 'General Ledger account to which the accrual is posted.']
  net_sales_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net sales value for the period, used when the accrual basis is a percentage of net sales.']
  period_end date [note: 'Last calendar date of the period for which the accrual is calculated.']
  period_start date [note: 'First calendar date of the period for which the accrual is calculated.']
  posting_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the GL posting transaction generated from this accrual.']
  rate decimal(12,4) [note: 'Rate applied to the accrual basis (e.g., $0.25 per case or 2.5% of net sales).']
  reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text description explaining the business rationale for the accrual.']
  recognition_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the accrual was recognized in the financial system.']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'True if the accrual has been reversed or cancelled; otherwise False.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the accrual record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing trade promotion accruals — the financial liability recognized as products are sold under a promotional agreement. Tracks accrual type (off-invoice, bill-back, scan-back, lump sum), accrual rate, accrual basis (per case, per unit, percentage of net sales), accrual period, accrued amount, reversal status, and GL posting reference. Critical for accurate P&L and COGS reporting. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA FI/CO accrual engine.'
}

Table trade.deduction {
  deduction_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the trade deduction record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the retail customer or distributor that submitted the deduction claim.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user who approved the deduction.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Deduction amounts must be posted to a GL Account to reflect chargebacks and adjustments in the general ledger.']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the invoice against which the deduction is applied.']
  promotion_event_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the promotion linked to the deduction.']
  retailer_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the trade agreement or contract governing the deduction terms.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Chargeback deductions are calculated per SKU; FK enables traceability of deductions to the exact product for audit and rebate reconciliation.']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Chargeback deductions adjust inventory; referencing the exact stock_position ensures accurate inventory accounting and audit trails.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of adjustments (e.g., discounts, fees) applied to the gross amount.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the deduction was approved.']
  audit_trail_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the audit log entry capturing change history for this deduction.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch identifier for the product lot involved in the deduction.']
  chargeback_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage rate applied for chargeback calculations.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes captured by users.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the deduction record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for the deduction amounts.']
  deduction_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category classifying the nature of the deduction.']
  deduction_date date [note: 'Date the deduction was recorded or became effective.']
  deduction_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description providing additional context for the deduction.']
  deduction_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the deduction by the retailer or distributor.']
  deduction_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the deduction.']
  deduction_type varchar [note: 'Category describing why the deduction was recorded.']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the deduction is currently under dispute.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the deduction is disputed.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date the product lot expires, relevant for quality‑related deductions.']
  freight_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Freight cost component related to the deduction.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Original amount claimed before any adjustments or offsets.']
  handling_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Handling fee component associated with the deduction.']
  is_manual_entry boolean [note: 'True if the deduction was entered manually rather than via automated feed.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot number used for traceability of the product.']
  market_segment varchar [note: 'Business segment of the retailer or distributor.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final amount after adjustments, representing the amount to be deducted from payment.']
  processing_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the deduction was processed in the downstream system.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'Purchase order reference associated with the original sale.']
  quantity decimal(12,3) [note: 'Quantity of product units associated with the deduction.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the specific reason for the deduction.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter code of the geographic region where the deduction originated.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating source system that created the deduction record.']
  supporting_document_number varchar [note: 'Reference to external documentation (e.g., claim form) that supports the deduction.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component associated with the deduction, if applicable.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the tax jurisdiction or rule applied.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the quantity field.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the deduction record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for all trade-related deductions taken against invoices by retail customers and distributors — including short payments, promotional claims, chargebacks reconciling contracted vs. list price differences, and unauthorized deductions requiring resolution.'
}

Table trade.slotting_fee {
  slotting_fee_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the slotting fee record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Name of the individual who approved the slotting fee agreement.']
  network_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.network_node. Business justification: Slotting‑fee contracts are negotiated per store location; linking supports the Slotting Fee Allocation report by node.']
  packaging_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.packaging_profile. Business justification: Slotting fees depend on packaging type; linking enables the Slotting Fee Packaging Sustainability Cost Analysis.']
  primary_slotting_account_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the retailer receiving the slotting fee.']
  retailer_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trade.retailer_agreement. Business justification: Slotting fees are negotiated per retailer agreement; linking slotting_fee to retailer_agreement captures this hierarchy.']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Slotting fee contracts specify shelf placement; linking to storage_location enables tracking fees per shelf for compliance and accounting.']
  accrual_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the fee is accrued over the contract period.']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'External contract number assigned by the retailer for the slotting fee agreement.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the slotting fee agreement was approved.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the slotting fee arrangement complies with FDA/USDA/GFSI regulations.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Effective end date of the slotting fee agreement; null if open‑ended.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Effective start date of the slotting fee agreement.']
  contract_term_months varchar [note: 'Duration of the agreement expressed in months.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the slotting fee record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the fee amounts (e.g., USD, EUR).']
  fee_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the slotting fee purpose and terms.']
  fee_per_store decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount charged for each store covered by the agreement.']
  fee_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the slotting fee.']
  fee_tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Tax amount calculated on the slotting fee, if taxable.']
  fee_taxable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the slotting fee is subject to tax.']
  fee_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the slotting fee (e.g., new item introduction, reset, promotional).']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the invoice generated for the slotting fee payment.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'System user who last updated the slotting fee record.']
  net_fee_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Fee amount after tax and any discounts (total_fee_commitment – fee_tax_amount).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑text comments or special conditions.']
  payment_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the slotting fee payment must be received.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method used to remit the slotting fee payment.']
  payment_schedule varchar [note: 'Frequency or trigger for fee payments.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current payment status of the slotting fee.']
  shelf_facing_count varchar [note: 'Number of product facings allocated on the shelf as part of the agreement.']
  shelf_position varchar [note: 'Preferred vertical position on the shelf for the product.']
  sku_list varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of SKUs included in the slotting fee arrangement.']
  slotting_fee_category varchar [note: 'Primary product category for which the slotting fee applies (e.g., Beverages, Snacks).']
  store_count varchar [note: 'Number of retailer locations covered by the slotting fee agreement.']
  total_fee_commitment decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total monetary commitment for the entire contract period (fee_per_store × store_count).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the slotting fee record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'System user who created the slotting fee record.']

  Note: 'Master and transactional record for slotting fees paid to retailers for shelf space allocation, new item placement, or reset participation. Captures retailer account, store count, category, SKU list, fee amount per store, total fee commitment, payment schedule, shelf placement terms (number of facings, shelf position), and contract period. Tracks slotting fee spend as a component of total trade investment. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA SD and retailer agreement documentation.'
}

Table trade.retailer_agreement {
  retailer_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the retailer agreement record.']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: REQUIRED: Retailer agreements must record the primary retailer contact for contract execution and compliance; linking to contact provides the needed reference.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Name of the individual who approved the agreement.']
  initiative_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.sustainability_initiative. Business justification: Retailer agreements often contain sustainability commitments; linking allows the Retailer Sustainability Commitment Dashboard to monitor compliance.']
  price_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_zone. Business justification: Retailer agreements are scoped to geographic price zones; the link enables the Agreement‑Zone Compliance report required by regional pricing governance.']
  primary_retailer_account_id varchar [note: 'FK to customer.account']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: Retailer agreements for new product introductions are tied to the originating R&D project for compliance and performance monitoring.']
  retailer_account_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the retailer party associated with this agreement.']
  agreement_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the retailer agreement for easy reference.']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the agreement (e.g., contract number).']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Category of the agreement defining the primary commercial mechanism.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the agreement was approved.']
  bonus_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary bonus awarded for exceeding performance thresholds.']
  bonus_clause varchar [note: 'Text describing bonuses for exceeding performance thresholds.']
  category_scope varchar [note: 'List of product categories covered by the agreement (comma‑separated).']
  co_op_advertising_allowance decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary allowance for cooperative advertising activities.']
  compliance_measure_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify retailer compliance with agreement terms.']
  creation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the agreement record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for monetary fields.']
  display_compliance_requirement varchar [note: 'Specification of shelf‑display or facings required for the retailer.']
  edlp_price_commitment decimal(12,2) [note: 'Committed price for Everyday Low Price (EDLP) items.']
  edlp_price_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the EDLP price commitment.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date the agreement expires or is terminated (nullable for open‑ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date the agreement becomes binding.']
  funding_commitment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount the retailer commits to fund promotional activities.']
  is_exclusive_agreement boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the retailer is prohibited from similar agreements with competitors.']
  is_multi_year boolean [note: 'True if the agreement spans multiple fiscal years.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the agreement record.']
  mdf_allocation_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Allocated amount for Marketing Development Funds (MDF).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional comments or special conditions.']
  penalty_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary penalty applied when performance thresholds are not met.']
  penalty_clause varchar [note: 'Text describing penalties for non‑performance.']
  performance_measure_period varchar [note: 'Frequency at which performance is evaluated.']
  performance_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum performance metric (e.g., sales %) required to avoid penalties.']
  promotional_event_count varchar [note: 'Number of promotional events scheduled under the agreement.']
  rebate_tier_structure varchar [note: 'JSON‑encoded definition of volume rebate tiers and rates.']
  renewal_option_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement includes an automatic renewal option.']
  retailer_agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the agreement.']
  scan_back_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of sales that must be reported back via scan‑back.']
  scan_back_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the scan‑back rate.']
  sku_scope varchar [note: 'List of specific SKUs covered by the agreement (comma‑separated).']
  termination_notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days required to give notice before terminating the agreement.']
  total_funding_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate monetary amount committed for promotional funding under the agreement.']
  volume_target decimal(12,2) [note: 'Committed sales volume target for the retailer (e.g., cases, units).']
  volume_target_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the volume target.']

  Note: 'Master record for formal trade agreements with retail customers including annual joint business plans (JBPs), volume rebate agreements, promotional calendars, EDLP pricing commitments, and category captain agreements. Captures agreement type, effective dates, retailer account, committed volume targets, promotional event count, funding commitments, performance thresholds, and penalty/bonus clauses. Contains granular line-item terms as child records: each term specifies type (volume rebate tier, promotional funding rate, EDLP price commitment, display compliance requirement, scan-back rate, co-op advertising allowance, MDF allocation), applicable SKU or category scope, threshold conditions, rate or amount, measurement period, and compliance measurement method. Supports multi-term agreement governance, compliance monitoring, and earned income calculation. SSOT for all retailer commercial terms. Sourced from Salesforce CRM and SAP S/4HANA SD contracts.'
}

Table trade.agreement_term {
  agreement_term_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the agreement term record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the retailer party associated with the agreement.']
  category_strategy_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product category scope for the term (if not SKU‑specific).']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: Agreement terms (e.g., slotting fees, rebates) are negotiated per product launch; linking to rd_project aligns terms with the correct project.']
  retailer_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the retailer agreement to which this term belongs.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific SKU to which the term applies (if scoped to a single product).']
  accrual_method varchar [note: 'Method used to accrue the term\'s financial impact.']
  agreement_term_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the term.']
  chargeback_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage rate applied for chargeback calculations.']
  chargeback_type varchar [note: 'Type of chargeback (e.g., scan‑back, sell‑through).']
  compliance_actual decimal(12,2) [note: 'Actual measured value for the compliance metric.']
  compliance_metric varchar [note: 'Metric used to assess compliance (e.g., display facing count).']
  compliance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether compliance monitoring is required for this term.']
  compliance_target decimal(12,2) [note: 'Target value for the compliance metric.']
  contract_term_code varchar [note: 'Identifier linking this term to a higher‑level contract term grouping, if applicable.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the agreement term record was first created.']
  edlp_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Committed Everyday Low Price for the SKU or category.']
  edlp_price_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the EDLP price.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the term becomes binding.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the term expires or is superseded (null for open‑ended).']
  is_exclusive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the term grants exclusive rights or conditions.']
  measurement_period varchar [note: 'Reporting period over which the term is measured.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments or remarks about the term.']
  promotional_funding_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Rate (percentage) of promotional funding provided to the retailer.']
  promotional_funding_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the funding is a percentage of sales or a fixed amount.']
  rate_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount or rate associated with the term (e.g., rebate per unit, funding amount).']
  rate_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the rate amount.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code representing the geographic region of the agreement.']
  slotting_fee_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount charged for slotting the product.']
  slotting_fee_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the slotting fee.']
  term_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description providing details of the term.']
  term_type varchar [note: 'Category of the commercial term (e.g., volume rebate, promotional funding, EDLP price commitment).']
  threshold_quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Quantity threshold that triggers the term (e.g., units sold for a rebate tier).']
  threshold_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the threshold quantity.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the agreement term record.']

  Note: 'Line-item detail of specific commercial terms within a retailer agreement. Each record captures a single term type (volume rebate tier, promotional funding rate, EDLP price commitment, display compliance requirement, scan-back rate, co-op advertising allowance), the applicable SKU or category scope, threshold conditions, rate or amount, and measurement period. Enables granular tracking of multi-term retailer agreements and compliance monitoring.'
}

Table trade.volume_rebate {
  volume_rebate_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the volume rebate record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the retail customer receiving the rebate.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who approved the rebate.']
  retailer_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trade.retailer_agreement. Business justification: Volume rebates are earned under specific retailer agreements; linking volume_rebate to retailer_agreement records this relationship.']
  sales_contract_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the underlying rebate agreement contract.']
  accrual_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the rebate has been accrued in financial statements.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the rebate was approved.']
  batch_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the data load or processing batch.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'External reference number of the rebate agreement.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the rebate amount.']
  is_exclusive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the rebate is exclusive to the customer.']
  measurement_period_end date [note: 'End date of the volume measurement period for the rebate.']
  measurement_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the volume measurement period for the rebate.']
  net_rebate_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Rebate amount after tax adjustments.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or remarks about the rebate.']
  party_name varchar [note: 'Name of the retail customer or account holder.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method used to settle the rebate with the customer.']
  product_category varchar [note: 'Product category covered by the rebate program.']
  purchase_volume decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total volume of product purchased by the customer during the measurement period.']
  rebate_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary amount earned from the rebate before tax.']
  rebate_calculation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the rebate calculation was performed.']
  rebate_program_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the rebate program as defined in the retailer agreement.']
  rebate_program_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the rebate program.']
  rebate_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Rebate rate applied to the qualified volume (e.g., $ per case or percentage).']
  rebate_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the rebate (e.g., volume‑based, growth‑based, mix‑based).']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the rebate record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the rebate record.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter code representing the geographic region applicable to the rebate.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which the rebate was settled.']
  settlement_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the rebate settlement process.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record that originated the rebate data (e.g., SAP S/4HANA).']
  tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Tax component associated with the rebate amount.']
  tax_included_flag boolean [note: 'Specifies if tax is included in the rebate amount.']
  threshold_volume decimal(15,3) [note: 'Purchase volume required to qualify for the tier.']
  tier_level varchar [note: 'Rebate tier achieved based on purchase volume.']
  volume_rebate_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the rebate record.']
  volume_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the purchase volume (e.g., cases, liters).']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who initially created the rebate record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for volume-based rebates earned by retail customers upon achieving purchase volume thresholds defined in retailer agreements. Captures rebate program reference, measurement period, actual volume purchased, threshold tier achieved, rebate rate, earned rebate amount, payment method (credit memo, check, off-invoice), and settlement status. Supports accurate rebate liability accrual and settlement. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA SD rebate processing.'
}

Table trade.category_captain {
  category_captain_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the category captain agreement record.']
  primary_category_account_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the retailer partner stored in Salesforce CRM.']
  promotion_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trade.promotion_plan. Business justification: Category captain agreements are scoped to a promotion plan; linking provides parent context.']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the category captain agreement by the business.']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the agreement; indicates whether Food Beverage acts as a category captain or advisor.']
  agreement_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the agreement document, incremented on each amendment.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the agreement within internal governance.']
  assortment_authority varchar [note: 'Degree of authority to recommend product assortment within the category.']
  audit_trail varchar [note: 'Compact log of key audit events (creation, approval, amendment) for the agreement.']
  category_captain_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the agreement in its lifecycle.']
  category_scope varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of product categories covered by the agreement (e.g., snacks, beverages).']
  competitive_exclusion_terms varchar [note: 'Textual description of competitor products that must be excluded from the assortment.']
  competitive_inclusion_terms varchar [note: 'Textual description of competitor products that may be included in the assortment.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement meets all applicable FDA/USDA/GFSI regulatory requirements.']
  contract_document_url varchar [note: 'Link to the stored contract document in the document management system.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the agreement record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the agreement becomes binding.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the agreement expires; null for open‑ended agreements.']
  kpi_acv_target decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary target for All Commodity Volume generated by the category.']
  kpi_category_growth_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'Desired percentage growth in category sales over the agreement period.']
  kpi_tdp_target varchar [note: 'Target value for Total Distribution Points to be achieved under the agreement.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments or observations related to the agreement.']
  planogram_ownership varchar [note: 'Level of control Food Beverage has over planogram design for the retailer.']
  review_cycle_months varchar [note: 'Number of months between formal agreement performance reviews.']
  shelf_space_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target percentage of shelf space to be allocated to Food Beverage products.']
  signed_date date [note: 'Date on which the agreement was formally signed by both parties.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the agreement record.']

  Note: 'Master record for category captain agreements where Food Beverage serves as the designated category advisor for a retailer. Captures retailer account, category scope, agreement effective dates, planogram ownership rights, assortment recommendation authority, shelf space allocation targets, review cycle frequency, performance KPIs (TDP, ACV, category growth targets), and competitive product inclusion/exclusion terms. Sourced from Salesforce CRM and category management documentation.'
}

Table trade.settlement {
  settlement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for settlement']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the retailer receiving the settlement.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Spend settlements are charged to Cost Centers for expense tracking and variance analysis.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.gl_account']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.profit_center']
  promotion_claim_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trade.promotion_claim. Business justification: Settlements finalize payments for approved promotion claims; required for the Promotion Claim Settlement reconciliation process.']
  accounting_date date [note: 'Date on which the settlement is recorded in the general ledger.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of all adjustments (taxes, fees, discounts) applied to the gross amount.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the settlement request.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the settlement record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code of the settlement amount.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount before any adjustments, taxes, or deductions.']
  is_manual_settlement boolean [note: 'True if the settlement was processed manually; otherwise False.']
  is_tax_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether tax is included in the net settlement amount.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final amount payable after adjustments; the amount actually settled.']
  original_claim_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount originally claimed by the retailer before settlement.']
  payment_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number from the payment instrument (e.g., check number, wire transfer ID).']
  period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period (e.g., 2023Q4) to which the settlement belongs.']
  posted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the settlement entry was posted to the accounting system.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the settlement has been reconciled with the claim.']
  settlement_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description providing context or notes about the settlement.']
  settlement_method varchar [note: 'Method used to deliver the settlement funds.']
  settlement_number varchar [note: 'External settlement reference number assigned by finance for tracking.']
  settlement_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the settlement.']
  settlement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the settlement was executed.']
  settlement_type varchar [note: 'Category of settlement transaction indicating the payment mechanism.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating ERP or financial system that generated the settlement record.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component of the settlement, if applicable.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Accounting tax code used for the settlement.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the settlement record.']
  variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Difference between original claim amount and net settlement amount.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for the financial settlement of trade promotion claims and deduction resolutions — the actual payment or credit issued to close a validated promotional claim, resolve a retailer deduction, or pay a volume rebate. Captures settlement type (credit memo, check, off-invoice deduction, wire transfer), settlement amount, original claim/deduction amount, variance, settlement date, payment reference, GL account, cost center, profit center, reconciliation status, and approval workflow state. Bridges trade promotion execution and accounts receivable/payable. Enables trade spend close-out reporting and period-end reconciliation. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA FI.'
}

Table trade.promotion_claim {
  promotion_claim_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the promotion claim record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the retailer submitting the promotional claim.']
  product_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.product_registration. Business justification: Promotion claim validation requires confirming the claimed SKU is registered; linking claim to product_registration enables compliance checks and audit reporting.']
  promotion_event_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the trade promotion to which this claim relates.']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Promotion claim validation requires matching claimed sales to the specific inventory stock position from which the product was sold.']
  approved_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount approved for reimbursement after validation.']
  claim_adjustment_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary adjustment applied to the claim (e.g., penalties or bonuses).']
  claim_adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for any monetary adjustment to the claim.']
  claim_date date [note: 'Business event date representing the day the claim was generated.']
  claim_line_item_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the promotional activity or SKU(s) covered by the claim.']
  claim_number varchar [note: 'External claim reference number assigned by the retailer or trading partner.']
  claim_original_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Original monetary amount before any adjustments.']
  claim_period_end date [note: 'Last day of the period for which the promotion claim is submitted.']
  claim_period_start date [note: 'First day of the period for which the promotion claim is submitted.']
  claim_processing_status varchar [note: 'Current status of claim processing in the back‑office system.']
  claim_source_system varchar [note: 'System of origin for the claim data (e.g., SAP S/4HANA, EDI, third‑party portal).']
  claim_submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the claim was received in the system.']
  claim_type varchar [note: 'Method used for the claim: scan data, bill‑back, or off‑invoice reconciliation.']
  claimed_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the claim before any adjustments.']
  claimed_volume decimal(12,2) [note: 'Quantity of product units (e.g., liters, cases) claimed for reimbursement.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the monetary amounts.']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the claim is under dispute.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the claim.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the claim record was first created in the data lake.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the claim record.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation why a claim was rejected, if applicable.']
  supporting_document_ref varchar [note: 'Reference (e.g., file name or URL) to documentation that validates the claim.']
  validation_status varchar [note: 'Result of automated or manual validation of the claim.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for retailer-submitted promotional claims requesting reimbursement for executed trade promotions. Captures claim type (scan data, bill-back, off-invoice reconciliation), retailer account, promotion reference, claim period, claimed volume, claimed amount, supporting documentation reference, validation status, approved amount, rejection reason, and dispute flag. Central to the trade deduction management and claim validation workflow. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA SD and EDI claim transactions.'
}

Table trade.spend_budget {
  spend_budget_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for spend_budget']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal owner (e.g., brand manager) responsible for the budget.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Budget amounts are tied to GL Accounts to enable budget vs. actual comparisons in financial reporting.']
  ibp_scenario_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.ibp_scenario. Business justification: Trade‑spend budgets are aligned to IBP scenarios; linking enables the Budget‑to‑Scenario allocation dashboard.']
  promotion_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trade.promotion_plan. Business justification: Spend budgets are created for specific promotion plans; linking enables aggregation of budget data.']
  accrued_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount accrued from executed promotions but not yet paid.']
  allocation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to allocate the budget across sub‑periods or promotion types.']
  allocation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the total budget allocated when the allocation method is percentage‑based.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval workflow state of the budget.']
  brand varchar [note: 'Brand to which the budget applies.']
  budget_code varchar [note: 'External business code or number used to reference the budget in financial and trade systems.']
  budget_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name describing the purpose or scope of the budget.']
  budget_period varchar [note: 'Planning period for the budget (full fiscal year or specific quarter).']
  budget_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the budget by its planning horizon or promotional focus.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Sales channel covered by the budget (e.g., Retail, Foodservice, Direct‑to‑Consumer).']
  co_op_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Allocated budget for manufacturer‑retailer co‑operative advertising spend.']
  committed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of the budget that has been formally committed to approved promotions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the budget record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary amounts in the budget.']
  customer_tier varchar [note: 'Retailer or customer tier classification (e.g., Tier 1, Tier 2).']
  display_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Allocated budget for in‑store display promotional spend.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date on which the budget expires or is no longer valid; null for open‑ended budgets.']
  feature_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Allocated budget for feature (end‑cap) promotional spend.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year to which the budget belongs.']
  fund_type varchar [note: 'Mechanism by which the budget is managed and disbursed.']
  last_revision_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent change to the budget record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Supplementary remarks or comments from budget owners or approvers.']
  paid_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount already paid out against the budget.']
  remaining_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Funds still available for future promotional spend.']
  slotting_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Allocated budget for slotting fee or shelf‑space purchase spend.']
  spend_budget_category varchar [note: 'Product category scope of the budget (e.g., Snacks, Beverages).']
  spend_budget_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the purpose, constraints, or notes for the budget.']
  spend_budget_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the budget record.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date on which the budget becomes effective for spend planning.']
  total_approved_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of all approved funds for the budget across all promotion types.']
  tpr_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Allocated budget for temporary price reduction promotions.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to any field in the budget record.']

  Note: 'Master record for all trade spending funds and budgets — the single source of truth for financial governance of trade investments. Encompasses annual brand/category/channel budgets, retailer account fund allocations, territory-level funds, and promotion-type-specific pools. Captures budget period, scope (brand, category, channel, customer tier), total approved budget, budget by promotion type (TPR, display, feature, slotting, co-op), phasing by period, fund type (lump sum, accrual-based, fixed allowance), committed amount, accrued amount, paid amount, remaining balance, budget owner, approval status, and last revision date. Supports real-time trade spend governance, promotion plan approval guardrails, and budget vs actual variance reporting. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA CO, TPM fund management modules, and Oracle Cloud Financials.'
}

Table trade.route_allowance {
  route_allowance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for route_allowance']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the distributor account receiving the allowance.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.asset. Business justification: DSD route allowance is paid to delivery‑truck assets; linking enables allocation of allowance per vehicle for compliance and cost tracking.']
  compliance_document_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the compliance document governing the allowance.']
  delivery_route_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.delivery_route. Business justification: Route allowances are budgeted per DSD delivery route; linking allows allowance calculations and route‑level financial reporting.']
  dsd_route_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Direct Store Delivery route to which the allowance applies.']
  lane_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.lane. Business justification: DSD route allowances are defined per transportation lane; needed for the Route Allowance Calculation report.']
  promotion_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trade.promotion_plan. Business justification: Route allowances are defined per promotion plan to support DSD routes; linking provides plan context.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product/SKU to which the allowance is tied.']
  allowance_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code assigned to the allowance agreement.']
  allowance_rate decimal(12,4) [note: 'Monetary or percentage rate of the allowance.']
  allowance_rate_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the allowance rate.']
  allowance_type varchar [note: 'Category of the allowance applied to the DSD route.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Distribution channel (e.g., DSD, warehouse‑to‑store).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the allowance record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary allowances.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the allowance expires or is terminated (null for open‑ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the allowance becomes effective.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or remarks about the allowance.']
  region varchar [note: 'Geographic region associated with the DSD route.']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Status of regulatory review for the allowance (e.g., price compliance).']
  route_allowance_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the allowance agreement.']
  settlement_frequency varchar [note: 'How often the allowance is settled with the distributor.']
  store_list varchar [note: 'JSON‑encoded list of store identifiers covered by the allowance.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the allowance record.']

  Note: 'Master and transactional record for allowances and pricing adjustments specific to Direct Store Delivery (DSD) routes. Captures DSD route identifier, distributor account, store list, product/SKU, allowance type (route allowance, fuel surcharge offset, handling allowance), allowance rate, effective date range, and settlement frequency. Manages the unique trade economics of DSD channel execution distinct from warehouse-delivered retail. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA SD DSD module.'
}

Table trade.coop_advertising {
  coop_advertising_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for coop_advertising']
  promotion_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trade.promotion_plan. Business justification: Co‑op advertising programs are a type of promotion; linking ties spend to the underlying promotion plan for ROI analysis.']
  retailer_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the retailer partner receiving co‑op funding.']
  target_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.sustainability_target. Business justification: Co‑op advertising budgets are tied to sustainability initiatives; required for the Co‑op Advertising ESG Alignment Report.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the co‑op advertising claim was approved by Food Beverage.']
  business_event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the co‑op advertising program was officially created or approved.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Primary distribution channel for the advertising (retail stores, online platforms, or both).']
  claim_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the co‑op advertising claim.']
  co_op_program_code varchar [note: 'External code or number used to reference the cooperative advertising program in contracts and reports.']
  coop_advertising_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the objectives and creative details of the co‑op advertising program.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code representing the currency of monetary amounts.']
  food_beverage_funding_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Portion of the total cost funded by Food Beverage.']
  is_exclusive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the co‑op advertising is exclusive to the retailer (true) or shared with other partners (false).']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle stage of the co‑op advertising record.']
  media_vehicle varchar [note: 'Specific media channel or placement used for the advertisement (e.g., TV spot, radio slot, banner URL).']
  media_vehicle_type varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the media vehicle.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Settlement status of the retailer’s portion of the co‑op funding.']
  program_type varchar [note: 'Category of the co‑op advertising activity (e.g., circular feature, digital ad, in‑store radio, loyalty app feature).']
  proof_of_performance_ref varchar [note: 'Document or system reference that validates the advertising run was executed as planned.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this record was first inserted into the data lake.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this record.']
  region varchar [note: 'Geographic region where the advertising is targeted.']
  retailer_funding_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Portion of the total cost funded by the retailer partner.']
  run_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date the co‑op advertising is scheduled to run.']
  run_start_date date [note: 'First calendar date the co‑op advertising is scheduled to run.']
  target_sku_list varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of SKU identifiers that are covered by the co‑op advertising program.']
  total_program_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Aggregate cost of the advertising program before any funding allocations.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the employee who created the co‑op advertising record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for cooperative advertising programs funded jointly by Food Beverage and retail customers. Captures co-op program type (circular feature, digital ad, in-store radio, loyalty app feature), retailer account, media vehicle, run dates, total program cost, Food Beverage funding amount, retailer funding amount, proof-of-performance documentation reference, claim status, and payment status. Tracks co-op advertising as a distinct component of total trade investment. Sourced from Salesforce CRM Marketing Cloud and SAP S/4HANA.'
}

Table trade.promotion_price_assignment {
  promotion_price_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the promotion_price_assignment association']
  price_list_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the price_list_line']
  promotion_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the promotion_event']
  discount_percent decimal [note: 'Discount percentage applied by the promotion to the price list line']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the promotion discount ends for this price list line']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the promotion discount starts for this price list line']

  Note: 'This association captures the operational link between a trade promotion_event and the price_list_line records it affects. It stores the discount percent applied by the promotion and the effective date range of that discount for each SKU.. Existence Justification: A promotion_event can apply to many price_list_line records, each representing a SKU price, and a single price_list_line can be included in multiple promotion events over time. The link between them carries operational data such as the discount percent and the effective dates of the promotion, which are managed by users when creating or editing promotions.'
}

Table trade.store_cluster {
  store_cluster_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for store_cluster']
  parent_store_cluster_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on store_cluster (parent_store_cluster_id)']
  average_sales_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Average sales revenue generated by the cluster over the most recent fiscal period.']
  code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the store cluster in external systems.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the store cluster record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for monetary values associated with the cluster.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed free‑text description of the store cluster.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the store cluster became effective for planning and reporting.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the store cluster ceases to be effective (null if open‑ended).']
  is_flagship boolean [note: 'True if the cluster contains flagship or high‑visibility stores.']
  market_segment varchar [note: 'Target market segment for the cluster (e.g., premium, value, health‑conscious).']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the store cluster.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information about the cluster.']
  region varchar [note: 'Geographic region (e.g., Northeast, Midwest) where the cluster is located.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the store cluster.']
  store_count varchar [note: 'Count of individual retail locations that belong to this cluster.']
  type varchar [note: 'Classification of the cluster based on its purpose or composition.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the store cluster record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for store_cluster. Referenced by store_cluster_id.'
}

Table customer.account {
  account_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the account record.']
  bill_to_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.bill_to_location. Business justification: Account billing address fields are duplicated; linking to bill_to_location centralizes address data and removes redundant columns.']
  credit_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.credit_profile. Business justification: Credit information is detailed in credit_profile; moving credit_rating and payment_terms to credit_profile normalizes credit data.']
  edi_trading_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.edi_trading_partner. Business justification: EDI trading partner configuration belongs to a B2B account; adding FK enables direct association without creating a bidirectional link.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal sales representative managing the account.']
  loyalty_program_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the loyalty program to which the account belongs.']
  preferred_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Preferred Supplier Assignment process links each key account to its primary supplier for contract negotiation and service level management.']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list. Business justification: REQUIRED: Assign a default price list to each customer account for pricing engine calculations; standard practice in F&B account‑level pricing.']
  sales_territory_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales territory responsible for the account.']
  account_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the account (e.g., retailer name, consumer name).']
  account_number varchar [note: 'External business number used to identify the account in contracts and transactions.']
  account_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the account.']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Category that defines the nature of the account holder.']
  annual_revenue_band varchar [note: 'Revenue range band for the account (e.g., <$1M, $1‑5M, $5‑10M, >$10M).']
  channel varchar [note: 'Primary sales channel through which the account purchases products.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the account record was first created.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the account agreement ends or is scheduled to terminate (null for open‑ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the account agreement becomes effective.']
  first_order_date date [note: 'Date of the first recorded purchase from this account.']
  key_account_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the account is designated as a strategic key account.']
  last_order_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent purchase from this account.']
  market_segment varchar [note: 'Segment of the market to which the account belongs (e.g., mass market, premium).']
  nps_score varchar [note: 'Latest Net Promoter Score for the account (0‑10).']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary contact person for the account.']
  primary_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary contact.']
  primary_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the primary contact.']
  shipping_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the shipping address.']
  shipping_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the shipping address (optional).']
  shipping_city varchar [note: 'City component of the shipping address.']
  shipping_country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the shipping address.']
  shipping_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal/ZIP code of the shipping address.']
  shipping_state varchar [note: 'State or province of the shipping address.']
  status_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation for the current status of the account.']
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'True if the account is tax‑exempt under applicable regulations.']
  tax_identifier varchar [note: 'Government‑issued tax identification number for the account.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the account record.']

  Note: 'Master record for all customer accounts across B2B and B2C channels — retailer accounts (grocery, mass, club, convenience), foodservice operators (QSR, casual dining, institutional), distributors, wholesalers, and DTC/e-commerce consumers. Each account has a unique business identity with attributes including account type, channel, status, credit rating, payment terms, assigned sales territory, key account flag, annual revenue band, and first-order date. The single authoritative source for \'who is our customer\' across all commercial systems.'
}

Table customer.contact {
  contact_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the contact.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Required for Account Management: assigns each contact an account manager employee for sales territory planning and performance reporting.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the contact.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information (apartment, suite, etc.).']
  birth_date date [note: 'Date of birth of the contact (used for age-based analytics).']
  city varchar [note: 'City component of the contact\'s address.']
  contact_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the contact.']
  contact_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the contact based on relationship to the company.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code representing the contact\'s country.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the contact record was first created.']
  email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for electronic communication.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Given name of the contact.']
  full_name varchar [note: 'Complete legal name of the individual contact.']
  language_preference varchar [note: 'Preferred language for communications.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Family name of the contact.']
  nps_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'True if the contact is eligible to receive Net Promoter Score surveys.']
  opt_in_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the contact has opted in to marketing communications.']
  opt_out_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the contact has opted out of marketing communications.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for voice contact.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the contact\'s address.']
  preferred_communication_channel varchar [note: 'Channel the contact prefers for outreach.']
  role varchar [note: 'Business role of the contact (e.g., buyer, category manager, accounts payable).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that supplied the contact data.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Original identifier of the contact in the source system.']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province component of the contact\'s address.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'Time zone of the contact for scheduling communications.']
  title varchar [note: 'Professional title or position of the contact within the account.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the contact record.']
