  Note: 'Individual contacts associated with B2B customer accounts — buyers, category managers, accounts payable contacts, foodservice operators, and DTC consumers. Stores full name, title, role, email, phone, preferred communication channel, opt-in/opt-out flags, and NPS survey eligibility. Sourced from Salesforce CRM Contact object.'
}

Table customer.ship_to_location {
  ship_to_location_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each ship-to location record.']
  network_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.network_node. Business justification: Delivery Routing maps each ship‑to location to its serving distribution center (network node), essential for logistics optimization and carrier planning.']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Assigning a ship‑to address to an internal storage location supports dedicated inventory allocation and location‑based availability reporting.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the ship‑to location.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information (suite, unit, etc.).']
  capacity_cubic_meters decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum volume the location can store, expressed in cubic meters.']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the ship‑to location is situated.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address for the primary contact at the location.']
  contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary contact person at the ship‑to location.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number for the primary contact at the location.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the location.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the ship‑to location record was first created.']
  delivery_window_end_time timestamp [note: 'Latest time a delivery may be accepted at this location.']
  delivery_window_start_time timestamp [note: 'Earliest time a delivery may be accepted at this location.']
  dock_requirements varchar [note: 'Specific dock or equipment requirements for receiving shipments (e.g., dock height, trailer type).']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the location becomes valid for shipping.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the location is no longer valid for shipping (null if indefinite).']
  geocode_accuracy_m varchar [note: 'Estimated accuracy of the geocoded coordinates in meters.']
  is_primary boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is the primary ship‑to address for the parent account.']
  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic latitude of the location in decimal degrees.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier used in ERP and logistics systems to reference the location.']
  location_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the ship‑to location (e.g., store name, warehouse name).']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Category of the ship‑to location indicating its role in the supply chain.']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic longitude of the location in decimal degrees.']
  max_weight_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum weight the location can receive per delivery, in kilograms.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the ship‑to location.']
  receiving_hours varchar [note: 'Standard hours during which the location accepts deliveries (e.g., 08:00‑17:00).']
  ship_to_location_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the location record.']
  special_instructions varchar [note: 'Any additional handling or access instructions for deliveries.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province of the ship‑to location.']
  temperature_zone varchar [note: 'Temperature control category required for the location.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the ship‑to location record.']

  Note: 'All ship-to delivery addresses for customer accounts — retail store locations, foodservice establishment addresses, DC receiving docks, and DTC consumer delivery addresses. Captures address, geolocation, delivery window constraints, dock requirements, temperature zone requirements (ambient, chilled, frozen), and active/inactive status. Supports OTIF and DSD routing.'
}

Table customer.bill_to_location {
  bill_to_location_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the bill-to location record.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the billing location.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information (e.g., suite or floor).']
  bill_to_location_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the billing location.']
  billing_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the billing contact.']
  billing_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary contact for billing communications.']
  billing_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the billing contact.']
  city varchar [note: 'City of the billing address.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the billing location.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the bill-to location record was first created.']
  credit_limit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum credit amount approved for the billing location.']
  credit_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the credit limit approval process.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code used for invoicing this location.']
  e_invoicing_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the location accepts electronic invoicing.']
  edi_billing_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the location supports EDI billing transactions.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the billing location becomes active for invoicing.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the billing location ceases to be active (null if open‑ended).']
  is_default_bill_to boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this location is the default billing address for the associated customer account.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the bill-to location record.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Internal code used to reference the billing location within ERP systems.']
  location_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the billing location (e.g., Corporate HQ Billing).']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the billing location indicating its business role.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the billing location.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms applied to invoices for this location.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the billing location.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province component of the billing address.']
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the location is exempt from sales tax.']
  tax_exempt_reason varchar [note: 'Reason or certification supporting tax exemption status.']
  tax_registration_number varchar [note: 'Government‑issued tax identifier (e.g., VAT number, EIN).']
  tax_registration_type varchar [note: 'Type of tax registration number associated with the billing location.']

  Note: 'Bill-to addresses and invoicing entities for customer accounts — corporate billing offices, AP departments, and DTC billing addresses. Stores billing address, invoicing currency, tax registration numbers (VAT/EIN), e-invoicing preferences, and EDI billing capability flags. Distinct from ship-to to support split billing scenarios common in retail and foodservice.'
}

Table customer.account_hierarchy {
  account_hierarchy_id varchar [pk, note: 'System generated unique identifier for each node in the account hierarchy.']
  parent_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the immediate parent account in the hierarchy.']
  account_hierarchy_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the account or its role in the hierarchy.']
  account_hierarchy_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the account hierarchy node.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the hierarchy record was first created in the source system.']
  credit_terms varchar [note: 'Payment terms agreed with the account (e.g., Net 30).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the hierarchy relationship ends (null for open‑ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the hierarchy relationship becomes effective.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Depth of the account within the hierarchy (1 = top level).']
  hierarchy_type varchar [note: 'Specifies the type of hierarchy relationship (e.g., retail banner to store).']
  loyalty_program_flag boolean [note: 'True if the account participates in the company loyalty program.']
  market varchar [note: 'Geographic market classification for the account.']
  nps_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Latest Net Promoter Score for the account, ranging from 0 to 10.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country or region code where the account is primarily located.']
  rollup_acv_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether All Commodity Volume (ACV) should be rolled up to the parent.']
  rollup_tdp_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Total Distribution Points (TDP) should be rolled up to the parent.']
  sales_channel varchar [note: 'Primary sales channel through which the account purchases products.']
  segment varchar [note: 'Business segment classification used for reporting and planning.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system of record for the hierarchy data.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Native identifier of the account in the source system.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the hierarchy record.']

  Note: 'Parent-child hierarchy relationships for customer accounts — retail banner to store, foodservice chain to operator, distributor to sub-distributor, and corporate parent to subsidiary. Captures hierarchy level, parent account reference, hierarchy type (retail, foodservice, distributor), effective dates, and roll-up flags for ACV and TDP reporting. Enables consolidated reporting across account groups.'
}

Table customer.segment {
  segment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the customer segment record.']
  acv_band varchar [note: 'All Commodity Volume band associated with the segment.']
  assignment_method varchar [note: 'Method used to assign accounts to this segment.']
  audience varchar [note: 'Target audience classification of the segment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the segment record was first created in the system.']
  criteria varchar [note: 'Expression or rule text that defines the inclusion criteria for the segment.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the segment definition expires; null if open‑ended.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the segment definition becomes effective.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if this segment is the default assignment for accounts without explicit segment.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the segment definition was last reviewed for relevance.']
  nielsen_iri_alignment varchar [note: 'Code linking the segment to Nielsen or IRI market data taxonomy.']
  owner varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the business owner responsible for the segment.']
  primary_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this segment is the primary segment for an account when multiple assignments exist.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority used to rank segments when multiple apply; lower number = higher priority.']
  segment_code varchar [note: 'Short business code used to reference the segment in systems.']
  segment_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the segment purpose and characteristics.']
  segment_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the segment.']
  segment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the segment.']
  segment_type varchar [note: 'Category defining the basis of the segment (e.g., channel, tier, strategic priority).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the segment record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number of the segment definition, incremented on each change.']

  Note: 'Market segmentation taxonomy and account-to-segment assignment management — segment definitions (channel segment, tier classification, strategic priority, ACV band, Nielsen/IRI alignment) plus temporal assignment records linking accounts to segments with effective dating, assignment method (manual, rule-based, model-driven), primary segment flags, and assignment audit trail. Single product owns both the segment master data and the many-to-many account assignments. Supports trade promotion targeting, pricing strategy differentiation, demand planning segmentation, and account evolution tracking across the F&B customer lifecycle.'
}

Table customer.credit_profile {
  credit_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the credit profile record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the credit profile record was created.']
  credit_hold_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the account is currently on credit hold.']
  credit_hold_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the credit hold was applied.']
  credit_insurance_coverage varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the credit is insured and the coverage level.']
  credit_limit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum amount of credit extended to the customer under this profile.']
  credit_limit_adjustment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount by which the credit limit was increased or decreased.']
  credit_limit_adjustment_date date [note: 'Date when the credit limit was last adjusted.']
  credit_limit_adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for the most recent credit limit change.']
  credit_limit_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the credit limit.']
  credit_limit_override_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Override amount applied to the credit limit.']
  credit_limit_override_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the credit limit has been manually overridden.']
  credit_limit_override_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for manually overriding the credit limit.']
  credit_limit_override_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the credit limit override was applied.']
  credit_limit_override_user varchar [note: 'User identifier who performed the credit limit override.']
  credit_limit_review_frequency varchar [note: 'How often the credit limit is reviewed (e.g., quarterly, annually).']
  credit_limit_review_last_done date [note: 'Date when the most recent credit limit review was performed.']
  credit_limit_review_next_due date [note: 'Planned date for the next credit limit review.']
  credit_limit_review_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes captured during the credit limit review.']
  credit_limit_review_owner varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person or team responsible for the credit review.']
  credit_profile_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the credit profile.']
  credit_profile_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the credit profile based on the level of service or risk exposure.']
  credit_rating varchar [note: 'Internal or external rating indicating creditworthiness of the account.']
  credit_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next formal credit review.']
  credit_score varchar [note: 'Numerical credit score derived from internal or external models.']
  credit_score_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the credit score (e.g., internal model, external agency).']
  credit_status_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation for the current credit status.']
  credit_terms_notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes about the credit terms.']
  credit_utilization_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Current utilization of the credit limit expressed as a percentage.']
  duns_rating varchar [note: 'External credit rating from Dun & Bradstreet.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the credit profile becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the credit profile expires or is terminated; null for open‑ended.']
  insurance_coverage_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum amount covered by the credit insurance policy.']
  insurance_policy_number varchar [note: 'Policy identifier for the credit insurance.']
  insurance_provider varchar [note: 'Name of the insurer providing credit insurance.']
  last_credit_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the most recent credit review was completed.']
  outstanding_balance_threshold decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable outstanding balance before triggering alerts.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment condition associated with the credit profile.']
  profile_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the credit profile, used for external reference.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system of record providing the credit data (e.g., SAP S/4HANA FI).']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the source system record for traceability.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the credit profile record.']

  Note: 'Credit and payment terms profile for B2B customer accounts — credit limit, credit rating, payment terms (Net 30, Net 60, COD), credit hold status, credit review date, outstanding balance threshold, Dun & Bradstreet rating, and credit insurance coverage. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA FI credit management and Oracle Cloud Financials.'
}

Table customer.loyalty_program {
  loyalty_program_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the loyalty program.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal team or business unit responsible for the program.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'Regulatory or policy constraints applicable to the program (e.g., GDPR, CCPA).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the program record was first created in the system.']
  data_classification varchar [note: 'Classification level for the program data per corporate policy.']
  earn_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Number of loyalty points awarded per base monetary unit (e.g., per $1).']
  earn_rate_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for the earn rate.']
  earn_rule_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of how points are earned.']
  earn_rule_type varchar [note: 'Category of activity that triggers point accrual.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the program is terminated or expires; null for open‑ended.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the program becomes legally effective.']
  enrollment_channel varchar [note: 'Primary channel through which members enroll in the program.']
  enrollment_end_date date [note: 'Date after which new enrollments are closed; null if ongoing.']
  enrollment_start_date date [note: 'Date when members may begin enrolling in the program.']
  loyalty_program_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the loyalty program.']
  max_points_balance varchar [note: 'Upper limit on the number of points a member may hold.']
  points_balance_reset_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether points balances reset on a periodic basis (e.g., annually).']
  points_expiry_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after earning that points expire.']
  points_expiry_policy varchar [note: 'Policy governing how point expiry is calculated.']
  program_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to uniquely reference the program in external systems.']
  program_description varchar [note: 'Full textual description of the loyalty program\'s purpose and benefits.']
  program_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the loyalty program as presented to members.']
  program_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the program based on reward mechanics.']
  redeem_rule_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of how points can be redeemed.']
  redeem_rule_type varchar [note: 'Category of redemption option offered to members.']
  redemption_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Monetary value of a single loyalty point when redeemed.']
  tier_1_min_points varchar [note: 'Minimum accumulated points required to attain Tier 1.']
  tier_1_name varchar [note: 'Display name of the first loyalty tier.']
  tier_2_min_points varchar [note: 'Minimum accumulated points required to attain Tier 2.']
  tier_2_name varchar [note: 'Display name of the second loyalty tier.']
  tier_3_min_points varchar [note: 'Minimum accumulated points required to attain Tier 3.']
  tier_3_name varchar [note: 'Display name of the third loyalty tier.']
  tier_4_min_points varchar [note: 'Minimum accumulated points required to attain Tier 4.']
  tier_4_name varchar [note: 'Display name of the fourth loyalty tier.']
  tier_5_min_points varchar [note: 'Minimum accumulated points required to attain Tier 5.']
  tier_5_name varchar [note: 'Display name of the fifth loyalty tier.']
  tier_levels varchar [note: 'Total count of distinct loyalty tiers defined in the program.']
  tier_structure varchar [note: 'Structured definition (e.g., JSON) of tier levels, benefits, and thresholds.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier that performed the latest update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the program record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier that created the program record.']

  Note: 'Complete loyalty lifecycle management for DTC and B2C consumer engagement — program definitions (name, type, earn/redeem rules, tier thresholds, expiry policy), individual consumer enrollments (tier, points balance, lifetime value, enrollment channel, tier qualification dates), and points transaction activity (earn, redeem, adjust, expire events with triggering purchase/referral/promotion context). Single product owns the full loyalty lifecycle from program design through member enrollment to transaction-level audit trail across DTC web, mobile app, and in-store channels. Supports program ROI analysis, tier migration tracking, redemption analytics, and consumer lifetime value calculation.'
}

Table customer.loyalty_enrollment {
  loyalty_enrollment_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each loyalty enrollment record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the consumer who is enrolled in the loyalty program.']
  enrolling_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Captures which employee enrolled the account into the loyalty program, supporting incentive calculations and enrollment audit.']
  loyalty_program_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the loyalty program to which the consumer is enrolled.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the loyalty enrollment record was first created in the system.']
  enrollment_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the consumer enrolled (e.g., website, mobile app, in‑store).']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'Date the consumer officially joined the loyalty program.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date the enrollment expires or is scheduled to terminate, if applicable.']
  lifetime_points_earned varchar [note: 'Total points the consumer has earned over the lifetime of the enrollment.']
  loyalty_enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the loyalty enrollment.']
  next_tier_threshold varchar [note: 'Points required to reach the next tier level.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the enrollment.']
  opt_in_communication_preferences varchar [note: 'Preferred communication channel(s) the consumer has opted into for loyalty messages.']
  points_balance decimal(12,2) [note: 'Current points balance available for redemption.']
  preferred_language varchar [note: 'Consumer\'s preferred language for loyalty communications.']
  program_tier varchar [note: 'Current tier level of the consumer within the loyalty program.']
  redemption_history_summary varchar [note: 'Brief textual summary of the consumer\'s redemption activity (e.g., number of redemptions, recent reward types).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that originated the enrollment record (e.g., Salesforce CRM).']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the record in the source system.']
  tier_qualification_date date [note: 'Date the consumer qualified for the current tier.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the loyalty enrollment record.']

  Note: 'Individual consumer enrollments in loyalty programs — enrollment date, program tier, current points balance, lifetime points earned, redemption history summary, tier qualification date, next tier threshold, opt-in communication preferences, and enrollment channel (DTC web, mobile app, in-store). Links consumers to loyalty programs with their own lifecycle.'
}

Table customer.loyalty_transaction {
  loyalty_transaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each loyalty transaction record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the customer who earned or redeemed the points.']
  loyalty_program_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the loyalty program under which this transaction is recorded.']
  processing_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Records the employee who processed the loyalty points transaction, required for audit trails and employee commission reporting.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the loyalty activity was captured.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the loyalty transaction record was first inserted into the lakehouse.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which earned points are scheduled to expire, if applicable.']
  is_manual_adjustment boolean [note: 'True if the transaction was entered manually by an operator, false otherwise.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional context or comments about the transaction.']
  order_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the order that generated the loyalty points (if applicable).']
  points_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Number of loyalty points associated with this transaction (positive for earn, negative for redeem/adjust).']
  points_balance_after decimal(12,2) [note: 'Customer\'s loyalty points balance immediately after this transaction was applied.']
  points_balance_before decimal(12,2) [note: 'Customer\'s loyalty points balance immediately before this transaction was applied.']
  processing_status varchar [note: 'Result of the backend processing for the transaction.']
  reward_type varchar [note: 'Type of reward associated with a redemption transaction.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that originated the loyalty transaction record.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the transaction in the source system.']
  transaction_reference varchar [note: 'External reference code used in loyalty program communications and reporting.']
  transaction_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the loyalty transaction.']
  transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the loyalty event actually occurred.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the transaction earned, redeemed, adjusted, or expired points.']
  triggering_event varchar [note: 'Business event that caused the points transaction (e.g., a purchase, referral, survey completion, or promotion).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the loyalty transaction record.']

  Note: 'Individual loyalty points earn and redemption events — transaction type (earn, redeem, adjust, expire), points amount, triggering event (purchase, referral, survey, promotion), transaction date, associated order reference, redemption reward type, and processing status. Provides full audit trail of consumer loyalty activity for DTC and e-commerce channels.'
}

Table customer.nps_survey {
  nps_survey_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each NPS survey response record.']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the respondent (contact or account) who answered the survey.']
  respondent_segment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer segment to which the respondent belongs.']
  ship_to_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the location (ship‑to) associated with the respondent.']
  cadence varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the survey is sent (e.g., weekly, monthly).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the NPS response record was first created in the system.']
  dispatch_method varchar [note: 'Method used to deliver the NPS survey to the respondent.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the survey campaign was closed or expired (nullable for open‑ended campaigns).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the survey campaign became active for respondents.']
  follow_up_action varchar [note: 'Planned action to address the respondent\'s feedback.']
  follow_up_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which the follow‑up action should be completed.']
  nps_classification varchar [note: 'Categorization of the NPS score: promoter (9‑10), passive (7‑8), or detractor (0‑6).']
  nps_score varchar [note: 'Numeric Net Promoter Score provided by the respondent (0‑10).']
  respondent_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address of the respondent for communication.']
  respondent_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the individual respondent.']
  respondent_phone varchar [note: 'Contact phone number of the respondent.']
  respondent_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the respondent is a B2B account or a B2C consumer.']
  response_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the respondent submitted the survey.']
  response_date date [note: 'Calendar date of the NPS response (derived from response_timestamp).']
  response_device_type varchar [note: 'Device category used by the respondent to submit the survey.']
  response_ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address captured at the time of survey submission.']
  response_language varchar [note: 'Language in which the respondent completed the survey.']
  response_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the response within the organization (e.g., web portal, mobile app, call center, in‑store kiosk).']
  response_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the respondent submitted the NPS survey.']
  survey_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the NPS survey campaign definition that this response belongs to.']
  survey_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the NPS survey campaign.']
  survey_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the survey response record.']
  survey_type varchar [note: 'Category of the NPS survey (e.g., post‑purchase, periodic, event‑driven).']
  survey_version varchar [note: 'Version label of the survey definition (e.g., v1.0, v2.1).']
  target_channel varchar [note: 'Primary distribution channel for which the survey is intended (e.g., retail, foodservice, direct‑to‑consumer).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the NPS response record.']
  verbatim_comments varchar [note: 'Open‑ended feedback text entered by the respondent.']

  Note: 'Net Promoter Score measurement program and individual response capture — survey campaign definitions (name, type, target channel, cadence, dispatch method) and respondent-level response records (NPS score 0-10, promoter/passive/detractor classification, verbatim comments, response dates, follow-up actions, response channel, associated account or contact). Single product owns both the survey design and all response data. Supports transactional and relational NPS across B2B account health and B2C brand sentiment, closed-loop detractor follow-up, promoter advocacy identification, and NPS trend analysis by segment, channel, and account tier.'
}

Table customer.nps_response {
  nps_response_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the NPS response record.']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the respondent (customer contact) who provided the NPS response.']
  nps_survey_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the NPS survey definition.']
  ship_to_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the location associated with the respondent for this response.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the NPS response record was created in the system.']
  follow_up_action_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a follow‑up action is required for this response.']
  nps_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the NPS score: promoter (9-10), passive (7-8), detractor (0-6).']
  nps_score varchar [note: 'NPS rating provided by the respondent on a scale of 0 to 10.']
  respondent_type varchar [note: 'Indicates if the respondent is a business (B2B) or consumer (B2C) entity.']
  response_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the respondent submitted the NPS response.']
  response_date date [note: 'Date when the respondent submitted the NPS response.']
  response_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the NPS response.']
  response_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the NPS response was recorded.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Source system that captured the NPS response (e.g., Salesforce, SurveyMonkey).']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the response in the source system.']
  survey_dispatch_date date [note: 'Date when the NPS survey was dispatched to the respondent.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the NPS response record.']
  verbatim_comment varchar [note: 'Open‑ended comment provided by the respondent.']

  Note: 'Individual NPS survey responses from customers and consumers — respondent account or contact reference, survey dispatch date, response date, NPS score (0-10), promoter/passive/detractor classification, verbatim comment, response channel, and follow-up action flag. Captures the voice-of-customer signal for brand and account health monitoring.'
}

Table customer.interaction {
  interaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for interaction']
  assigned_rep_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales or service representative assigned to this interaction.']
  case_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the service case linked to this interaction, if any.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system user who logged the interaction.']
  interaction_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the account (if B2B) associated with the interaction.']
  interaction_customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer (B2B or B2C) involved in the interaction.']
  marketing_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketing campaign associated with the interaction, if any.']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales opportunity linked to this interaction, if any.']
  ship_to_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the ship‑to or service location relevant to the interaction.']
  attachment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any attachment (e.g., document, image) is associated with the interaction.']
  audit_trail varchar [note: 'JSON string capturing audit trail of changes for compliance purposes.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Medium through which the interaction occurred.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the interaction required compliance review (e.g., regulatory, privacy).']
  compliance_review_status varchar [note: 'Status of the compliance review for the interaction.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the interaction record was first created in the system.']
  duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Length of the interaction in minutes.']
  escalation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided for escalation, if applicable.']
  follow_up_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the required follow‑up should be completed.']
  follow_up_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a follow‑up action is required after the interaction.']
  interaction_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable reference number for the interaction.']
  interaction_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the interaction record.']
  interaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the interaction took place.']
  interaction_type varchar [note: 'Category of the interaction such as call, email, visit, trade show, complaint, inquiry, or meeting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: call|email|visit|trade_show|complaint|inquiry|meeting — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  is_escalated_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the interaction was escalated to a higher support tier.']
  is_internal_flag boolean [note: 'True if the interaction is internal (e.g., between employees) rather than external customer.']
  is_test_interaction boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the record is a test or training interaction.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639‑1 language code of the interaction.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes captured by the representative.']
  nps_score varchar [note: 'Net Promoter Score captured during the interaction, if collected.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'Result or outcome of the interaction (e.g., resolved, pending, escalated).']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority assigned to the interaction for handling.']
  product_sku varchar [note: 'SKU of the product discussed during the interaction, if applicable.']
  product_upc varchar [note: 'UPC code of the product discussed, if applicable.']
  rating varchar [note: 'Rating (1‑5) given by the customer for the interaction.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to any regulatory case or filing linked to the interaction.']
  response_channel varchar [note: 'Channel used to deliver the response.']
  response_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when a response was sent back to the customer.']
  sentiment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Sentiment score derived from interaction content (range -1.00 to 1.00).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Source system that originated the interaction record (e.g., Salesforce, Service Cloud).']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the record in the source system.']
  subject varchar [note: 'Brief subject or title of the interaction.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the interaction record.']

  Note: 'CRM interaction records capturing all touchpoints between Food Beverage and its customers — interaction type (call, email, visit, trade show, complaint, inquiry), interaction date, channel, subject, outcome, follow-up required flag, associated opportunity or case reference, and assigned rep. Sourced from Salesforce CRM Activity and Task objects. Covers both B2B account management and B2C consumer service interactions.'
}

Table customer.case {
  case_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the case.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the account associated with the case (for B2B customers).']
  assigned_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Needed to track the employee responsible for each customer service case, enabling SLA monitoring and case ownership reporting.']
  capa_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the corrective action record linked to this case.']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer (B2B or B2C) who reported the case.']
  related_case_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of a related case if this case is a follow-up or duplicate.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product involved in the case, if applicable.']
  attached_file_count varchar [note: 'Number of files attached to the case (e.g., photos, documents).']
  case_category varchar [note: 'Broad category of the case for reporting purposes.']
  case_number varchar [note: 'Unique case identifier assigned by the system.']
  case_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the case.']
  case_type varchar [note: 'Category of the case indicating the nature of the issue.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the case was submitted.']
  channel_detail varchar [note: 'Additional details about the channel (e.g., email address, phone number).']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the case was closed or resolved.']
  complaint_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the issue reported by the customer.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the case record was created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for monetary amounts.']
  escalation_level varchar [note: 'Escalation tier reached for the case.']
  impact_severity varchar [note: 'Severity of the impact on the customer or business.']
  is_duplicate boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this case is identified as a duplicate of another case.']
  is_escalated boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the case was escalated to higher support tier.']
  is_financial_impact boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the case has a financial impact (e.g., refund, credit).']
  monetary_amount_refunded decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount actually refunded to the customer.']
  monetary_amount_requested decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount requested by the customer (e.g., refund amount).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional internal notes or comments related to the case.']
  nps_score varchar [note: 'Net Promoter Score captured from the customer after case resolution.']
  opened_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the case was initially opened (business event).']
  origin varchar [note: 'Origin of the case: internal (initiated by staff) or external (customer initiated).']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the case based on impact and urgency.']
  regulatory_report_type varchar [note: 'Type of regulatory report associated with the case.']
  regulatory_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the case is reportable to regulatory bodies (e.g., FDA adverse event).']
  resolution_action varchar [note: 'Action taken to resolve the case (e.g., replacement, refund, corrective action).']
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the resolution action was completed.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'High-level root cause classification determined during investigation.']
  satisfaction_rating varchar [note: 'Customer satisfaction rating (1-5) for the case handling.']
  sku varchar [note: 'Stock Keeping Unit of the product related to the case.']
  sla_actual_hours varchar [note: 'Actual time taken to resolve the case in hours.']
  sla_met_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the case met the SLA target (true) or missed (false).']
  sla_target_hours varchar [note: 'Target resolution time in hours as defined by SLA for this case type.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User ID of the employee who last updated the case record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the case record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID of the employee who created the case record.']

  Note: 'Customer service cases and complaint records tracking issue resolution from open to close — case type (product quality complaint, delivery shortage, billing dispute, recall notification, food safety consumer contact), priority, SLA tracking, root cause categorization, resolution actions, and escalation history. Covers B2B account issues (delivery, billing, quality) and B2C consumer complaints including FDA-reportable adverse events. Supports CAPA linkage for quality-driven cases.'
}

Table customer.segment_assignment {
  segment_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each segment assignment record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer account to which the segment is assigned.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who performed the segment assignment.']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer segment classification.']
  assignment_date date [note: 'Date on which the segment assignment was initially made.']
  assignment_method varchar [note: 'Mechanism used to assign the segment: manual, rule‑based, or model‑driven.']
  assignment_reason varchar [note: 'Business rationale or trigger for assigning the segment (e.g., new product launch, market shift).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the segment assignment record was first created in the system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date the segment assignment ceases to be active; null if open‑ended.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'First date the segment assignment becomes active for the account.']
  is_current boolean [note: 'True if the assignment is currently effective (today falls between start and end dates).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for any additional information about the assignment.']
  primary_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this segment is the primary classification for the account at the effective date.']
  segment_assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the segment assignment record.']
  segment_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority of the segment; lower numbers indicate higher priority.']
  segment_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the segment definition used at assignment time.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that originated the segment assignment record.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Original identifier of the record in the source system.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the segment assignment record.']

  Note: 'Association table linking customer accounts to their active segment classifications with effective dating — segment assignment date, assigned-by user, assignment method (manual, rule-based, model-driven), effective start and end dates, and primary segment flag. Supports temporal segmentation changes as accounts evolve across channels and tiers.'
}

Table customer.preference {
  preference_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for preference']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the consumer to whom this preference belongs.']
  age_group varchar [note: 'Age segment of the consumer.']
  alcohol_preference varchar [note: 'Consumer\'s preferred alcohol content level.']
  allergen_free_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the consumer avoids all allergens.']
  caffeine_preference varchar [note: 'Consumer\'s preferred caffeine level.']
  communication_opt_in_email boolean [note: 'True if the consumer has opted in to receive email communications.']
  communication_opt_in_push boolean [note: 'True if the consumer has opted in to receive push notifications.']
  communication_opt_in_sms boolean [note: 'True if the consumer has opted in to receive SMS communications.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the preference record was first created.']
  data_consent_ccpa boolean [note: 'Indicates CCPA consent status for personal data processing.']
  data_consent_gdpr boolean [note: 'Indicates GDPR consent status for personal data processing.']
  dietary_gluten_free_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the consumer prefers gluten‑free products.']
  dietary_halal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the consumer prefers halal‑certified products.']
  dietary_kosher_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the consumer prefers kosher‑certified products.']
  dietary_vegan_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the consumer prefers vegan products.']
  gender varchar [note: 'Self‑identified gender of the consumer.']
  health_condition_flag boolean [note: 'True if the consumer has health‑related dietary restrictions (e.g., diabetic).']
  income_bracket varchar [note: 'Income level category of the consumer.']
  language_preference varchar [note: 'Preferred language for communications.']
  last_preference_update_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent preference change.']
  loyalty_program_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates if the consumer has opted into loyalty programs.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes about the consumer\'s preferences.']
  nps_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates if the consumer consents to receive NPS surveys.']
  organic_preference_flag boolean [note: 'True if the consumer prefers organically certified products.']
  preference_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the preference record.']
  preferred_flavor_profile varchar [note: 'Flavor profile the consumer favors (e.g., sweet, salty, spicy). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sweet|salty|spicy|sour|umami|bitter|other — promote to reference product]']
  preferred_pack_size varchar [note: 'Typical pack size the consumer prefers when purchasing.']
  preferred_product_category varchar [note: 'Primary product category the consumer prefers (e.g., snacks, beverages, dairy). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: snacks|beverages|dairy|convenience|organic|health|other — promote to reference product]']
  preferred_purchase_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the consumer most often purchases.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that supplied the preference data.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the record in the source system.']
  sugar_content_preference varchar [note: 'Preferred sugar content level in products.']
  sustainable_packaging_preference_flag boolean [note: 'True if the consumer prefers sustainable/eco‑friendly packaging.']
  timezone_preference varchar [note: 'Preferred timezone of the consumer.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the preference record.']

  Note: 'DTC and B2C consumer dietary preferences, product affinities, and communication preferences — dietary flags (gluten-free, vegan, halal, kosher, allergen-free), preferred product categories, flavor preferences, pack size preferences, preferred purchase channel, communication opt-ins (email, SMS, push), and data consent flags (GDPR, CCPA). Supports personalization and clean label consumer targeting.'
}

Table customer.edi_trading_partner {
  edi_trading_partner_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the EDI trading partner configuration.']
  communication_endpoint varchar [note: 'URL or network address of the partner\'s EDI gateway.']
  communication_protocol varchar [note: 'Network protocol used to exchange EDI messages with the partner.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'Regulatory or industry compliance constraints applicable to the partner (e.g., FDA, GFSI).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the trading partner record was first created.']
  edi_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the technical EDI contact.']
  edi_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the technical contact responsible for EDI integration.']
  edi_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the technical EDI contact.']
  edi_standard varchar [note: 'Standard used for EDI messages with this partner.']
  edi_trading_partner_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the trading partner configuration.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the EDI partnership ends or is scheduled to be retired.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the EDI partnership becomes active.']
  is_test_environment boolean [note: 'True if the partner configuration is for testing; false for production.']
  last_onboarded_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the partner was most recently onboarded or re‑validated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes regarding the partner, special handling, or exceptions.']
  onboarding_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the partner\'s EDI onboarding process.']
  partner_code varchar [note: 'Business‑assigned code used to reference the partner in internal systems.']

  partner_type varchar [note: 'Category of the partner based on its role in the supply chain.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary contact for EDI communications.']
  primary_contact_method varchar [note: 'Preferred communication channel for the primary contact.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the main person to contact at the trading partner organization.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the primary contact for EDI communications.']
  supported_transaction_sets varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of EDI transaction sets (e.g., 850,855,856,810) supported for this partner.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the trading partner record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the partner configuration record.']

  Note: 'EDI trading partner configuration for B2B customers — EDI partner ID, EDI standard (ANSI X12, EDIFACT), supported transaction sets (850 PO, 855 PO Ack, 856 ASN, 810 Invoice), communication protocol (AS2, SFTP, VAN), test vs production flag, EDI contact, and onboarding status. Manages electronic commerce connectivity for retail and distributor accounts.'
}

Table customer.consent_record {
  consent_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the consent record.']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the consumer to whom the consent applies.']
  consent_given_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the consent was granted.']
  consent_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the consent capture (e.g., web form, mobile app, in‑store, call center).']
  consent_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the consent record.']
  consent_type varchar [note: 'Category of consent (e.g., marketing email, SMS, push notification, data sharing, profiling).']
  consent_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the consent text or policy at the time of capture.']
  consent_withdrawn_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the consent was withdrawn, if applicable.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the consent record was first created in the system.']
  data_processing_purpose varchar [note: 'Intended purpose for processing the consumer\'s data under this consent.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the consent becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the consent expires or is no longer valid (nullable).']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Legal jurisdiction governing the consent (e.g., GDPR, CCPA, CASL).']
  legal_basis varchar [note: 'Legal basis for processing under the consent (typically \'consent\').']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments related to the consent record.']
  privacy_policy_version varchar [note: 'Version of the overall privacy policy referenced by this consent.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the consent record.']

  Note: 'Data privacy and marketing consent records for DTC and B2C consumers — consent type (marketing email, SMS, push notification, data sharing, profiling), consent status (granted, withdrawn, pending), consent date, withdrawal date, consent source (web form, mobile app, in-store), regulatory jurisdiction (GDPR, CCPA, CASL), and consent version. Supports FDA, FTC, and global privacy compliance obligations.'
}

Table customer.recall_notification {
  recall_notification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the RecallNotification association']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the customer account']
  recall_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the recall event']
  acknowledgment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the account\'s acknowledgment of the recall notification']
  notification_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time the recall notification was sent to the account']

  Note: 'Represents the notification sent from the company to a customer account about a specific recall event, capturing when the notification was sent and whether the account acknowledged it.. Existence Justification: Each recall event can affect many customer accounts, and a single account can be impacted by multiple recall events over time. The business actively tracks the date a notification was sent to an account and whether the account has acknowledged the recall. This information is managed as a distinct record linking an account to a recall event.'
}

Table customer.sponsorship {
  sponsorship_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the Sponsorship association']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the sponsoring account']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the sponsored R&D project']
  amount decimal [note: 'Monetary contribution of the account to the project']

  Note: 'Represents the contractual sponsorship relationship between a customer account and an R&D project. Each record captures the amount contributed, the period of sponsorship, and the role of the sponsor.. Existence Justification: An account (customer) can sponsor multiple R&D projects and a single R&D project can receive sponsorship from multiple accounts. The sponsorship relationship is actively managed, with amounts, dates, and roles recorded for each sponsorship, and stakeholders track these records for revenue sharing and strategic reporting.'
}

Table marketing.brand {
  brand_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each brand record in the Food Beverage portfolio. Primary key for the brand master data product. Serves as the enterprise-wide SSOT reference for brand identity across PLM, ERP, CRM, and marketing systems.']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Brand manager assignment is tracked for brand strategy decisions; linking to employee enables Brand Manager performance reports.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.plant. Business justification: Capacity planning and brand‑level supply chain reporting need a primary plant reference for each brand.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Brand performance is aggregated in profit centers for P&L reporting; FK enables profit‑center level analysis.']
  target_consumer_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_segment. Business justification: Brand strategy defines a target consumer segment; FK replaces the denormalized segment name, enabling segment‑based budgeting and compliance reporting.']
  allergen_free_claims varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of allergen-free claims associated with the brand (e.g., \'gluten-free, nut-free, dairy-free\'). Derived from the allergen matrix managed in TraceGains. Must comply with FDA FALCPA (Food Allergen Labeling and Consumer Protection Act) and FSMA requirements. Used in consumer marketing and retail planogram placement.']
  annual_marketing_budget decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total approved annual marketing investment budget allocated to the brand, expressed in the functional currency. Includes above-the-line (ATL) advertising, below-the-line (BTL) shopper marketing, digital, and trade promotion spend. Confidential financial data managed in Oracle Cloud ERP Financials and SAP CO cost centers.']
  architecture_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the brand within the corporate brand architecture hierarchy. \'masterbrand\' = umbrella brand covering multiple product lines; \'sub_brand\' = brand operating under a masterbrand; \'endorsed\' = independent brand with parent brand endorsement; \'standalone\' = fully independent brand; \'private_label\' = retailer-owned brand manufactured by Food Beverage under co-packing or toll manufacturing arrangements.']
  awareness_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Unaided or total brand awareness percentage among the target consumer segment, expressed as a value between 0.00 and 100.00. Sourced from Nielsen/IRI consumer panel studies or commissioned brand health tracking research. Key input to brand equity score calculation and marketing investment justification.']
  brand_category varchar [note: 'Primary consumer product category to which the brand belongs (e.g., \'Snacks\', \'Beverages\', \'Dairy\', \'Packaged Foods\', \'RTD Coffee\', \'RTE Cereals\'). Aligns with Nielsen/IRI/SPINS syndicated data category taxonomy for market tracking and ACV/TDP reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: snacks|beverages|dairy|packaged_foods|rte_cereals|rtd_beverages|condiments|frozen_foods|confectionery|other — promote to reference product]']
  brand_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the brand, used across SAP S/4HANA, Oracle ERP, and trade systems. Serves as the natural key for cross-system integration and EDI transactions. Example: \'SNKBR-001\', \'BEV-RTD-02\'.']
  brand_name varchar [note: 'Official commercial name of the brand as registered and used in consumer-facing marketing, packaging, and trade communications. This is the primary human-readable identifier for the brand (e.g., \'SunCrisp\', \'HydraFlow\'). Must align with FTC-approved marketing claims and FDA labeling requirements.']
  budget_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the annual marketing budget (e.g., \'USD\', \'EUR\', \'GBP\'). Ensures correct financial reporting and multi-currency consolidation in Oracle Cloud ERP and SAP S/4HANA FI.']
  clean_label_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the brand carries a clean label certification or commitment (no artificial colors, flavors, preservatives, or GMO ingredients). Drives consumer marketing claims, shelf placement in natural/organic sections, and SPINS natural channel data eligibility. Must be substantiated per FTC claim guidelines.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the brand record was first created in the enterprise data platform (Databricks Silver Layer). Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for data lineage, audit trail, and record lifecycle management.']
  crm_brand_code varchar [note: 'Brand identifier as recorded in Salesforce CRM (Sales Cloud / Marketing Cloud). Used for cross-system reconciliation between the lakehouse brand master and Salesforce campaign and consumer engagement records. Enables brand-level marketing performance attribution.']
  digital_presence_url varchar [note: 'Primary website URL for the brand\'s consumer-facing digital presence (e.g., \'https://www.suncrisp.com\'). Used in Salesforce Marketing Cloud digital campaign management, SEO tracking, and consumer engagement analytics.']
  discontinuation_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the brand was officially discontinued or withdrawn from the market. Null if the brand is currently active. Used for portfolio rationalization analysis, regulatory deregistration, and historical reporting in Nielsen/IRI market tracking.']
  distribution_channel varchar [note: 'Primary route-to-market channel through which the brand\'s products are sold. \'retail\' = traditional grocery/mass/drug; \'foodservice\' = restaurants/institutions; \'dsd\' = Direct Store Delivery; \'dtc\' = Direct-to-Consumer; \'ecommerce\' = online retail; \'club\' = club/warehouse channel. Drives trade promotion strategy (TPM/TPO) and supply chain configuration.']
  equity_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite brand equity score derived from consumer research inputs including awareness, consideration, preference, and loyalty metrics. Scored on a 0-100 scale. Sourced from Nielsen/IRI brand health tracking studies and internal NPS panel data. Confidential strategic metric used in portfolio investment decisions and brand valuation.']
  erp_brand_code varchar [note: 'Brand identifier as recorded in the source ERP system (SAP S/4HANA or Oracle Cloud ERP). Used for cross-system reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer brand master and operational ERP brand records. Supports data lineage and audit trail requirements.']
  gfsi_certification varchar [note: 'GFSI-benchmarked food safety certification scheme applicable to the brand\'s manufacturing operations. \'sqf\' = Safe Quality Food (SQF); \'brc\' = British Retail Consortium Global Standard; \'fssc_22000\' = Food Safety System Certification 22000; \'ifs\' = International Featured Standards; \'global_gap\' = GlobalG.A.P.; \'none\' = no GFSI certification. Tracked in Veeva Vault and TraceGains supplier compliance.']
  haccp_plan_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the HACCP plan document associated with the brand\'s manufacturing process, stored in Veeva Vault Document Control. HACCP (Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points) is a mandatory food safety management system under FDA FSMA and USDA FSIS regulations. Used for audit traceability and regulatory compliance reporting.']
  launch_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the brand was first commercially launched and made available to consumers in its primary market. Used for brand age calculations, anniversary marketing campaigns, and PLM lifecycle tracking. Aligns with SAP PLM product launch milestones.']
  legal_name varchar [note: 'Legally registered name of the brand as filed with trademark authorities (USPTO, EUIPO, etc.). May differ from the commercial brand name. Used for regulatory filings, contract management, and IP protection tracking.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the brand within its commercial lifecycle. \'active\' = currently marketed and sold; \'discontinued\' = no longer produced or sold; \'pipeline\' = in NPD/R&D phase, not yet launched; \'divested\' = sold or transferred to another entity; \'archived\' = historical record retained for compliance. Drives PLM alignment and portfolio management decisions.']
  logo_asset_url varchar [note: 'URL pointing to the master brand logo asset stored in the digital asset management (DAM) system. Used by Salesforce Marketing Cloud, packaging design teams, and agency partners for consistent brand identity application across all consumer touchpoints.']
  nps_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Net Promoter Score (NPS) for the brand, measuring consumer likelihood to recommend on a -100 to +100 scale. Sourced from consumer panel surveys and Salesforce Marketing Cloud feedback programs. Used as a leading indicator of brand health and consumer loyalty in brand equity tracking.']
  organic_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the brand holds USDA National Organic Program (NOP) certification or equivalent international organic certification (e.g., EU Organic). Enables organic marketing claims on packaging and in trade communications. Tracked in TraceGains supplier compliance and Veeva Vault document control.']
  owner_division varchar [note: 'Internal business division or operating unit that owns and manages the brand (e.g., \'North America Snacks\', \'Global Beverages\', \'International Dairy\'). Used for P&L attribution, marketing budget allocation, and organizational reporting in SAP CO and Oracle Financials.']
  plm_product_line_code varchar [note: 'Reference code linking the brand to its associated product line in the PLM system (SAP PLM or equivalent). Enables traceability between brand master data and the SKU/product hierarchy managed in PLM. Used for NPD pipeline tracking and BOM management.']
  positioning_statement varchar [note: 'Internal strategic statement articulating the brand\'s unique value proposition, competitive differentiation, and reason-to-believe for the target consumer segment. Confidential business strategy document used in brand planning, NPD briefs, and agency briefings. Not for external distribution.']
  primary_color_hex varchar [note: 'Hexadecimal color code for the brand\'s primary brand color as defined in the brand identity guidelines (e.g., \'#FF6B35\'). Used for consistent visual identity application across digital marketing, packaging, and trade materials in Salesforce Marketing Cloud and design systems.']
  primary_market varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the brand\'s primary commercial market (e.g., \'USA\', \'GBR\', \'DEU\'). Determines applicable regulatory frameworks (FDA, EFSA, USDA), labeling requirements, and market data sourcing (Nielsen/IRI for USA, SPINS for natural channel).']
  promise varchar [note: 'Consumer-facing articulation of the core benefit or commitment the brand makes to its consumers (e.g., \'Real ingredients, real taste\'). Used in marketing communications, packaging copy, and Salesforce Marketing Cloud campaign messaging. Must comply with FTC advertising claim substantiation requirements.']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of regulatory approval for the brand\'s product claims and labeling across applicable jurisdictions. \'approved\' = all required regulatory approvals obtained; \'pending\' = applications submitted, awaiting decision; \'under_review\' = regulatory authority review in progress; \'rejected\' = approval denied, remediation required; \'not_required\' = no regulatory approval needed for this brand type. Managed in Veeva Vault Quality Management.']
  segment varchar [note: 'Sub-category or segment within the broader product category (e.g., \'Salty Snacks\', \'Energy Drinks\', \'Greek Yogurt\', \'Plant-Based\'). Used for granular market share analysis via IRI/SPINS syndicated data and for internal portfolio segmentation reporting.']
  social_media_handle varchar [note: 'Primary social media handle or username for the brand\'s official social media presence (e.g., \'@SunCrispSnacks\'). Used for social media monitoring, influencer partnership management, and Salesforce Marketing Cloud social engagement tracking.']
  sustainability_claim varchar [note: 'Sustainability or environmental claim associated with the brand (e.g., \'Carbon Neutral\', \'Rainforest Alliance Certified\', \'B Corp Certified\', \'Recyclable Packaging\'). Must be substantiated per FTC Green Guides (16 CFR Part 260) and EPA environmental compliance standards. Supports ESG reporting and consumer engagement.']
  tier varchar [note: 'Commercial positioning tier of the brand within the portfolio pricing and consumer value ladder. \'super_premium\' = ultra-high-end, limited distribution; \'premium\' = above-mainstream RSP positioning; \'mainstream\' = mass-market EDLP positioning; \'value\' = price-entry or economy positioning. Drives RSP strategy, trade promotion investment, and retail planogram placement.']
  trademark_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the brand\'s trademark registration expires and must be renewed to maintain legal protection. Used for IP lifecycle management and legal compliance tracking. Triggers renewal workflows in legal management systems.']
  trademark_registration_number varchar [note: 'Official trademark registration number issued by the relevant intellectual property authority (e.g., USPTO, EUIPO, WIPO). Used for IP management, legal protection, and brand licensing agreements. Confidential business identifier managed in legal systems.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the brand record in the enterprise data platform. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change data capture (CDC), data freshness monitoring, and audit compliance.']

  Note: 'Master record for each brand in the F&B portfolio — captures brand identity, positioning, tier (premium, mainstream, value), brand architecture (masterbrand, sub-brand, endorsed), target consumer segment, brand equity score inputs, launch date, and lifecycle status. SSOT for brand identity across the enterprise. Supports brand equity tracking and PLM alignment.'
}

Table marketing.campaign {
  campaign_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each marketing campaign record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key.']
  agency_id varchar [note: 'FK to marketing.agency']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the owning brand under which this campaign is executed. Links to the brand master record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Campaign budgets are charged to cost centers; linking enables budget vs. actual financial analysis.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Campaign financial transactions need a GL account for posting; creates traceable accounting entries.']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Campaign manager ownership is required for campaign budgeting and ROI analysis; FK supports Campaign Manager accountability reports.']
  primary_sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Campaign planning uses a primary SKU for messaging and budgeting; the Primary SKU field drives the Campaign Brief and media allocation.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: Campaign planning aligns with a specific R&D project to synchronize messaging with product availability.']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_segment. Business justification: Campaign planning uses defined customer segments for targeting; linking enables segment‑level ROI reporting and compliance with marketing segmentation policies.']
  target_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.sustainability_target. Business justification: Marketing campaigns are aligned to sustainability targets (e.g., low‑carbon product launch); linking enables target‑aligned spend tracking.']
  actual_spend_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total actual expenditure incurred for the campaign in US dollars as of the latest financial close. Compared against planned_budget_usd to track budget utilization and variance.']
  acv_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target percentage of All Commodity Volume (ACV) distribution coverage for the campaign\'s featured products. ACV % is a standard food and beverage industry metric for measuring retail distribution breadth.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Workflow approval state of the campaign record: pending (awaiting review), approved (cleared for execution), rejected (not approved), or under_review (in active review). Supports governance and compliance workflows.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date on which the campaign received final business approval to proceed. Used for governance audit trails and time-to-market measurement.']
  campaign_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric business identifier for the campaign used in ERP cost tracking, trade promotion management (TPM), and cross-system reconciliation. Follows internal naming convention (e.g., \'CAMP-2024-BEV-001\').']
  campaign_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the campaign, including creative concept, key messages, promotional mechanics, and strategic rationale. Used for stakeholder briefings and campaign documentation.']
  campaign_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the marketing campaign (e.g., \'Summer Refresh 2024 — National TV Burst\'). Used in reporting, dashboards, and stakeholder communications.']
  campaign_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the campaign: draft (in development), planned (approved, not yet live), active (currently running), paused (temporarily halted), completed (finished), or cancelled (terminated before completion).']
  campaign_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the campaign by its primary marketing objective: awareness (brand building), trial (new consumer acquisition), loyalty (retention), trade (retailer/channel incentive), shopper (in-store activation), or influencer (creator-led). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: awareness|trial|loyalty|trade|shopper|influencer|sponsorship|sampling|digital|event — promote to reference product]']
  channel varchar [note: 'Primary media or activation channel through which the campaign is executed (e.g., tv, digital, shopper, social, influencer, in_store). Drives channel-level spend and performance reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tv|digital|shopper|social|influencer|in_store|radio|print|out_of_home|email|event — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the campaign record was first created in the system of record. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and governance requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this campaign record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency reporting for global campaigns.']
  end_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the campaign officially concludes and all media/activation ceases. Nullable for always-on campaigns. Used for budget reconciliation and post-campaign analysis.']
  fda_claims_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether all product claims featured in the campaign (e.g., health claims, nutrient content claims, clean label statements) comply with FDA regulations. True = compliant; False = non-compliant or under review.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period (month number within the fiscal year, 1–13) in which the campaign is primarily budgeted. Used for period-level financial reporting and budget pacing in SAP S/4HANA CO.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year in which the campaign budget is allocated and financial performance is reported (e.g., 2024). Aligns with SAP S/4HANA CO fiscal year for cost center and internal order reporting.']
  ftc_claims_reviewed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether all marketing claims in this campaign have been reviewed for compliance with FTC guidelines on advertising and marketing claims (e.g., substantiation of health/nutrition claims). True = reviewed and cleared; False = not yet reviewed.']
  influencer_partnership boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this campaign includes a paid influencer or creator partnership component. True = influencer partnership present; False = no influencer component. Supports FTC disclosure compliance tracking.']
  internal_order_number varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA CO internal order number used to collect and settle campaign costs. Provides granular cost tracking at the campaign level within the ERP system.']
  media_impressions_target varchar [note: 'Planned number of media impressions (total ad exposures) targeted for the campaign across all channels. Used as a key performance indicator (KPI) for reach planning and post-campaign evaluation against Nielsen/IRI data.']
  objective varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the primary business objective for the campaign (e.g., \'Drive 10% household penetration increase for RTD line in Q3\'). Supports brand marketing and consumer engagement planning.']
  planned_budget_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total approved budget allocated to the campaign in US dollars at the time of planning. Used for financial planning, trade promotion management (TPM), and return on investment (ROI) analysis.']
  private_label_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this campaign supports a private label product program rather than a branded product. True = private label campaign; False = branded campaign. Relevant for retailer co-marketing and private label program data.']
  product_category varchar [note: 'The product category or portfolio segment being promoted by this campaign (e.g., \'RTD Beverages\', \'Salty Snacks\', \'Dairy\'). Aligns with Nielsen/IRI category definitions for syndicated data benchmarking.']
  reach_target_households varchar [note: 'Planned number of unique households targeted for campaign reach. Aligns with Nielsen household panel measurement methodology for consumer packaged goods (CPG) reach reporting.']
