  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this supplier risk record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and compliance reporting requirements.']
  estimated_financial_exposure decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated monetary value of the business impact if the risk event materializes, expressed in the reporting currency. Includes potential supply disruption costs, expediting fees, reformulation costs, and lost revenue. Used in risk prioritization and EBITDA impact analysis.']
  exposure_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the estimated_financial_exposure amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency risk reporting.']
  financial_stability_rating varchar [note: 'External or internally-assessed financial stability rating for the supplier (e.g., Dun & Bradstreet PAYDEX, credit rating agency score, or internal procurement rating). Indicates risk of supplier insolvency or financial distress impacting supply continuity.']
  food_safety_risk_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this risk record involves a food safety dimension, including HACCP critical control point failures, allergen cross-contamination risk, microbiological contamination (CFU exceedance), or GMP non-compliance. Triggers mandatory escalation per FSMA and GFSI protocols.']
  fsma_verification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this risk triggers a mandatory FSMA Foreign Supplier Verification Program (FSVP) or domestic supplier verification activity under 21 CFR Part 117. True = verification activity required.']
  geopolitical_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region or country (ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 code) where the supplier operates, used to assess geopolitical, trade, and climate-related risk exposure for agricultural commodities and raw material sourcing.']
  identified_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the supplier risk was first formally identified and logged in the risk register. Used for aging analysis and risk trend reporting.']
  impact_score varchar [note: 'Numeric score (1–5) representing the assessed severity of business impact if the risk event materializes. 1 = negligible, 5 = catastrophic. Considers supply disruption, financial loss, food safety, and regulatory exposure.']
  is_single_source boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this supplier is the sole approved source for the associated material or ingredient, creating a single-source dependency risk. True = single source; False = multiple qualified sources exist. Critical for business continuity planning.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent formal review of this supplier risk record, including reassessment of likelihood, impact, and mitigation effectiveness.']
  likelihood_score varchar [note: 'Numeric score (1–5) representing the assessed probability that the identified risk event will materialize within the review horizon. 1 = rare, 5 = almost certain. Used in risk matrix calculations.']
  mitigation_completed_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the mitigation strategy was fully executed and verified. Compared against mitigation_target_date to measure timeliness of risk treatment.']
  mitigation_status varchar [note: 'Current execution status of the mitigation strategy for this risk. Tracks whether corrective or preventive actions have been initiated, are in progress, completed, or placed on hold.']
  mitigation_strategy varchar [note: 'Documented strategy to reduce or eliminate the identified risk, including actions such as dual-sourcing, safety stock build, supplier development programs, contractual protections, or regulatory corrective action plans.']
  mitigation_target_date date [note: 'Target completion date for the mitigation strategy actions. Used to track on-time execution of risk treatment plans and escalate overdue mitigations.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next formal review of this supplier risk record. Drives review calendar workflows and ensures timely reassessment of high and critical risks.']
  regulatory_compliance_risk_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this risk involves a regulatory compliance dimension, such as FDA labeling violations, USDA inspection failures, EFSA authorization issues, or FSSC 22000 certification lapses. Triggers compliance review workflows.']
  residual_risk_rating varchar [note: 'Risk rating remaining after mitigation actions have been applied. Represents the accepted level of risk exposure post-treatment. Distinct from risk_rating which reflects the inherent (pre-mitigation) risk level.']
  risk_category varchar [note: 'Broader business category grouping for the risk, used for portfolio-level risk reporting and supply chain resilience dashboards. Distinct from risk_type which captures the specific risk driver.']
  risk_closed_date date [note: 'Date on which the risk was formally closed, either through successful mitigation, risk acceptance, or supplier disqualification. Null if the risk remains open.']
  risk_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the identified risk, including the specific risk event, root cause drivers, and potential business consequences. Authored by the procurement risk analyst.']
  risk_open_date date [note: 'Date on which the risk record was formally opened and entered into the active risk register. May differ from identified_date if there is a lag between identification and formal registration.']
  risk_owner varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the procurement professional or category manager accountable for monitoring and managing this supplier risk. Aligns with risk ownership principles in ISO 31000.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Overall risk rating assigned to this supplier risk record following assessment. Drives escalation workflows, mitigation prioritization, and business continuity planning. Values: critical, high, medium, low.']
  risk_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier for the risk record, used in cross-functional communications, audit trails, and regulatory submissions. Format: SR-{YYYY}-{NNNNNN}.']
  risk_reviewer varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the individual who conducted the most recent risk review or assessment. Distinct from risk_owner who holds ongoing accountability.']
  risk_score varchar [note: 'Composite risk score derived from the product of likelihood_score and impact_score (range 1–25). Stored as a business-assessed value at the time of review; not dynamically recalculated. Supports risk prioritization and heat map reporting.']
  risk_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the supplier risk record. Tracks progression from initial identification through mitigation, acceptance, or closure. Drives workflow routing in procurement risk management processes.']
  risk_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the risk category associated with the supplier. Covers geopolitical instability, financial stability of the supplier, food safety and HACCP compliance failures, single-source dependency exposure, climate or weather-driven agricultural commodity risk, and regulatory compliance risk (FDA, FSMA, EFSA). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: geopolitical|financial_stability|food_safety|single_source_dependency|climate_weather|regulatory_compliance — promote to reference product]']
  supply_category varchar [note: 'Procurement spend category or commodity group associated with this risk (e.g., raw ingredients, packaging materials, indirect services, agricultural commodities). Aligns with the procurement category hierarchy in SAP S/4HANA MM.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this supplier risk record. Used for change tracking, incremental data loads in the Databricks Silver Layer, and audit compliance.']

  Note: 'Supplier risk assessment record capturing identified risks associated with specific suppliers or supply categories — including geopolitical risk, single-source dependency, financial stability risk, food safety risk, climate/weather risk for agricultural commodities, and regulatory compliance risk. Tracks risk type, risk rating (high/medium/low), likelihood, impact score, mitigation strategy, contingency supplier identified, and risk review date. Supports supply chain resilience and business continuity planning.'
}

Table procurement.supplier_approval {
  supplier_approval_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the SupplierApproval association']
  raw_material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the raw material']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the supplier']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the supplier was approved for the raw material']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the supplier for the raw material (e.g., Approved, Pending, Revoked)']

  Note: 'Represents the approval relationship between a raw material and a supplier. Each record tracks which supplier is approved for which raw material, including approval status and date.. Existence Justification: In procurement, each raw material can be approved for multiple suppliers, and each supplier can be approved for multiple raw materials. The approved‑supplier list is actively managed, with status and approval dates recorded for each raw material‑supplier pairing.'
}

Table procurement.sustainability_partnership {
  sustainability_partnership_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the sustainability_partnership association']
  initiative_id varchar [note: 'Links to the sustainability initiative']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Links to the supplier participating in the initiative']
  partner_contact_name varchar [note: 'Contact person responsible for the partnership on the supplier side']
  partner_organization varchar [note: 'Name of the supplier\'s organization as recorded for the partnership']
  partner_type varchar [note: 'Role of the supplier in the initiative (e.g., primary supplier, co‑partner)']

  Note: 'Represents the participation of a supplier in a sustainability initiative, capturing partnership details that are not intrinsic to either the supplier or the initiative.. Existence Justification: Suppliers are actively enrolled in multiple sustainability initiatives, and each initiative aggregates many suppliers. The partnership is managed as a distinct business entity that records the supplier\'s role, organization, and contact details for each initiative.'
}

Table procurement.supply_agreement {
  supply_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the SupplyAgreement association']
  spare_part_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the spare part']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the supplier']
  contract_terms varchar [note: 'Textual description of contractual terms (payment, warranty, etc.) for this supplier‑part pair']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Lead time in days specific to this supplier‑part contract']
  unit_price decimal [note: 'Agreed price per unit for this supplier‑part combination']

  Note: 'Represents the contractual relationship between a procurement supplier and a maintenance spare part. Each record captures the specific terms under which a supplier provides a particular spare part.. Existence Justification: A supplier can provide many spare parts, and a spare part can be sourced from multiple suppliers. Each supplier‑part pairing is actively managed with its own unit price, lead time, and contract terms, forming a distinct business entity that users create, update, and delete.'
}

Table inventory.stock_position {
  stock_position_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the stock position record. Primary key for the stock position entity.']
  lot_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production or procurement lot/batch number for traceability and FEFO/FIFO management.']
  packaging_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.packaging_profile. Business justification: Packaging Sustainability reports require linking inventory items to their packaging profile to calculate recyclability and carbon impact per SKU.']
  po_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.po_line. Business justification: REQUIRED: Stock aging and cost allocation reports must trace each inventory lot back to its originating purchase order line for financial and quality audits.']
  price_list_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list_line. Business justification: Inventory valuation report uses the price list line applied at receipt to calculate valuation per SKU, a standard accounting process.']
  product_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.product_registration. Business justification: Regulatory compliance reports require each stock position to be linked to its product registration for traceability and recall eligibility.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: R&D material reservation: stock positions are allocated to specific R&D projects to track raw material usage for experiments.']
  responsible_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Required for Stock Audit Responsibility Report; assigns a custodian employee to each stock position for accountability.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material master record (finished goods, WIP, or raw material SKU) for which stock is held.']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical storage location (plant, DC, 3PL warehouse, or specific bin/zone) where the stock is held.']
  abc_classification varchar [note: 'Inventory classification based on value and velocity. A items are high-value/high-velocity requiring frequent monitoring; B items are moderate; C items are low-value/low-velocity; X items are obsolete or non-moving.']
  allergen_segregation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the stock contains major allergens and requires physical segregation to prevent cross-contamination.']
  best_before_date date [note: 'Date until which the product maintains optimal quality characteristics. May differ from expiration date for shelf-stable products.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this stock position record was first created in the system.']
  cycle_count_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent physical cycle count verification for this stock position. Used to track count frequency and accuracy.']
  cycle_count_variance decimal(18,3) [note: 'Difference between system quantity and physical count quantity from the most recent cycle count. Positive indicates overage; negative indicates shortage.']
  expiry_status varchar [note: 'Classification of the stock based on remaining shelf life. Valid indicates sufficient shelf life; near-expiry indicates approaching expiration threshold; expired indicates past SLED; no-expiry indicates shelf-stable products without expiration date.']
  gtin varchar [note: 'GS1 global trade item number (UPC, EAN, or GTIN-14) for the material at this packaging level. Used for EDI and retail integration.']
  hazmat_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material is classified as hazardous and requires special handling, storage, and transportation procedures.']
  last_movement_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent goods movement transaction affecting this stock position (receipt, issue, transfer, or adjustment).']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this stock position record. Updated by every goods movement posting or inventory adjustment.']
  lot_traceability_code varchar [note: 'External lot or batch code used for supply chain traceability and recall management. May differ from internal lot ID.']
  organic_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the stock is certified organic and must be segregated from non-organic inventory to maintain certification integrity.']
  production_date date [note: 'Date when the lot was manufactured or packaged. Used for traceability and shelf life calculation.']
  quantity_available decimal(18,3) [note: 'Unrestricted quantity available for consumption, sale, or production use. Excludes reserved, blocked, and quality-inspection stock.']
  quantity_blocked decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity restricted from use due to quality issues, regulatory holds, or pending disposition (rework, scrap, return to vendor).']
  quantity_in_transit decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity currently in movement between locations (plant-to-DC, DC-to-DC, or inbound from supplier) but not yet received at destination.']
  quantity_on_hand decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total physical quantity of the material/lot currently present at the storage location, regardless of availability status.']
  quantity_quality_inspection decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity under quality inspection hold pending QA release. Cannot be used until inspection results are approved.']
  quantity_reserved decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity allocated to specific sales orders, production orders, or transfer orders but not yet physically moved.']
  quarantine_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the stock is under quarantine hold pending quality inspection, regulatory clearance, or investigation.']
  recall_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the stock is subject to an active product recall and must be segregated and disposed per recall procedures.']
  receipt_date date [note: 'Date when the stock was received into the storage location. Used for FIFO aging analysis and inventory turnover metrics.']
  remaining_shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Number of days remaining until shelf life expiration date. Used for FEFO prioritization and aged inventory alerts.']
  safety_stock_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this stock position is designated as safety stock buffer to protect against demand variability and supply disruptions.']
  shelf_life_expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the product should not be sold or consumed. Critical for FEFO lot management and food safety compliance.']
  special_stock_indicator varchar [note: 'Classification for non-standard stock ownership or purpose. Consignment is vendor-owned stock at customer location; customer-owned is stock held on behalf of customer; project is allocated to specific project; sample is for testing; promotional is for marketing programs. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: consignment|customer_owned|vendor_owned|project|sample|promotional|standard — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  stock_age_days varchar [note: 'Number of days since the stock was received at the storage location. Used for slow-moving and obsolete inventory analysis.']
  stock_category varchar [note: 'Classification of stock status indicating availability and restrictions. On-hand is total physical stock; available is unrestricted and not reserved; reserved is allocated to orders; in-transit is goods in movement; quality-inspection is under QA hold; blocked is restricted due to quality or regulatory issues.']
  temperature_zone varchar [note: 'Required storage temperature condition for the stock. Ambient is room temperature; refrigerated is 0-8°C; frozen is below 0°C; controlled is specific temperature range per product specification.']
  unit_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Cost per unit of measure for this stock position. Used to calculate total valuation amount and COGS.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit of measure for the stock quantity (e.g., EA for each, CS for case, KG for kilogram, LB for pound, LT for liter).']
  valuation_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the stock position calculated using the applicable valuation method (standard cost, moving average, FIFO). Used for inventory accounting and financial reporting.']
  valuation_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the valuation amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  vendor_lot_number varchar [note: 'Supplier-provided lot or batch number for raw materials and co-packed products. Used for upstream traceability.']

  Note: 'Core master record representing the current stock balance for each material/SKU and lot at each storage location. Tracks stock categories (on-hand, available, reserved, in-transit, quality-inspection, blocked) and shelf life attributes (remaining days, SLED, BBD, expiry status). Serves as the single source of truth for real-time inventory balances across finished goods, WIP, and raw materials. Updated by every goods movement posting.'
}

Table inventory.storage_location {
  storage_location_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the storage location. Primary key.']
  center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the distribution center if this storage location is part of a DC network node.']
  establishment_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.establishment_registration. Business justification: Facility inspection and audit processes map every storage location to the registered establishment for inspection scheduling and compliance tracking.']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Needed for Location Management & Safety Inspection process; links each storage location to its overseeing manager employee.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing plant or facility that owns or operates this storage location.']
  price_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_zone. Business justification: Location‑based pricing zones determine which price list applies to inventory stored at each location, used in regional pricing compliance.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the 3PL provider if this storage location is operated by an external logistics partner.']
  supplier_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier_site. Business justification: REQUIRED: Supplier‑managed warehouse locations need to be linked to the specific supplier site for inbound receipt traceability and regulatory compliance.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the storage location. Business-confidential organizational contact data.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information (suite, building, floor). Business-confidential organizational contact data.']
  allergen_segregation_class varchar [note: 'Allergen segregation classification for the storage location. Ensures physical separation of allergen-containing products to prevent cross-contamination. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: none|peanut|tree_nut|dairy|gluten|soy|shellfish|mixed — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  capacity_pallet_positions varchar [note: 'Number of standard pallet positions available in this storage location. Key metric for warehouse slotting and inventory planning.']
  capacity_volume_m3 decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total storage capacity of the location measured in cubic meters. Used for space utilization analytics and capacity planning.']
  capacity_weight_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum weight capacity of the storage location in kilograms. Critical for structural safety and load planning.']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the storage location is physically situated. Business-confidential organizational contact data.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code where the storage location is located (e.g., USA, CAN, MEX).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this storage location record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this storage location was decommissioned or ceased operations. Null if currently active.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this storage location became operational and available for inventory placement.']
  erp_storage_location varchar [note: 'Storage location code as defined in the ERP system (e.g., SAP S/4HANA storage location). Used for financial valuation and inventory accounting.']
  fefo_enforcement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether FEFO lot rotation based on expiration dates is enforced at this storage location. Critical for perishable goods management.']
  fifo_enforcement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether FIFO lot rotation is enforced at this storage location. True if FIFO rules are active.']
  gmp_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the storage location meets GMP standards for food safety and quality. True if GMP compliant.']
  halal_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the storage location is certified for halal product storage. Required for halal certification maintenance.']
  hazmat_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the storage location is certified to store hazardous materials. True if HAZMAT certification is active.']
  kosher_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the storage location is certified for kosher product storage. Required for kosher certification maintenance.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent quality or safety inspection of the storage location. Used for compliance tracking and audit readiness.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this storage location record was last updated. Supports change tracking and audit compliance.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the storage location. Used for logistics routing and geospatial analytics.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the storage location used in warehouse management systems and operational documents. Typically follows plant-site-aisle-bin hierarchy (e.g., PLT01-DC01-A12-B05).']
  location_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or description of the storage location (e.g., \'Chicago DC Frozen Zone A\', \'Plant 3 Raw Material Staging\').']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the storage location by its primary operational purpose within the supply chain network. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: plant|distribution_center|3pl_warehouse|cold_storage|staging_area|quarantine_zone|finished_goods|raw_material — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the storage location. Used for logistics routing and geospatial analytics.']
  next_inspection_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next required inspection of the storage location. Supports proactive compliance management.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational notes, special handling instructions, or location-specific comments.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the storage location. Determines whether the location can accept new inventory.']
  organic_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the storage location is certified for organic product storage. Required for USDA Organic compliance.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the storage location. Business-confidential organizational contact data.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province of the storage location. Business-confidential organizational contact data.']
  temperature_max decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable temperature in Celsius for this storage location. Exceeding this threshold triggers quality alerts.']
  temperature_min decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum allowable temperature in Celsius for this storage location. Used for cold chain monitoring and compliance.']
  temperature_zone varchar [note: 'Temperature control classification for the storage location. Critical for food safety compliance and FEFO/FIFO enforcement.']
  wms_location_code varchar [note: 'External location identifier used by the warehouse management system. Enables integration with WMS for real-time inventory tracking.']

  Note: 'Master reference for all physical and logical storage locations in the inventory network — plants, distribution centers, 3PL warehouses, cold storage facilities, staging areas, quarantine zones, and allergen-segregated zones. Captures location type, temperature zone (ambient, chilled, frozen), capacity, WMS location code, allergen segregation classification, and operational status. Enables location-level stock visibility and FEFO/FIFO enforcement.'
}

Table inventory.goods_movement {
  goods_movement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the goods movement transaction. Primary key for this entity. Serves as the immutable reference for all inventory state changes across the enterprise.']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'The user ID of the person who approved the movement. Nullable if approval is not required or still pending.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: COST ACCOUNTING: Material movements must be allocated to a cost center for internal cost of goods sold reporting; finance cost_center is the natural target.']
  order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.order. Business justification: Goods movement records shipments that satisfy a sales order; the FK ties the movement to the originating order for shipping and financial reporting.']
  price_list_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list_line. Business justification: Goods movement posting requires the price list line for material valuation and cost of goods sold, required by finance reporting.']
  primary_goods_employee_id varchar [note: 'The system user ID of the person who posted the goods movement. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  promotion_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trade.promotion_event. Business justification: Movement of promotional goods must be tied to the promotion event for traceability and cost allocation.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'The unique product identifier for the material being moved. References the master product catalog. Also known as material number in SAP.']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Goods movement records affect a specific stock position (location + SKU + lot). Adding stock_position_id creates a clear parent‑child link and removes the need for separate location/SKU columns if des']
  amount_lc decimal(18,2) [note: 'The financial value of the movement in the local currency of the plant. Calculated as quantity × standard cost or moving average price. Positive for receipts, negative for issues. Used for COGS posting and inventory valuation.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Workflow approval status for movements requiring authorization (e.g., write-offs above threshold, manual adjustments). Pending indicates awaiting approval, approved indicates cleared for posting, rejected indicates denied.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the movement was approved. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Nullable if approval is not required or still pending.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'The lot or batch identifier for the material being moved. Critical for traceability, FEFO/FIFO lot management, shelf life monitoring, and recall execution. Nullable for non-batch-managed materials.']
  cost_center varchar [note: 'The cost center to which the movement is charged (for issues to cost centers) or credited (for receipts from cost centers). Used for internal cost allocation.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the amount_lc field. Typically the local currency of the plant\'s company code. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USD|EUR|GBP|JPY|CNY|INR|BRL|MXN|CAD|AUD — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  customer_code varchar [note: 'Customer identifier for goods issue movements to external customers or for customer returns. Nullable for internal movements.']
  destination_plant_code varchar [note: 'For transfer movements, the plant to which the material is being moved. Nullable for non-transfer movements.']
  destination_storage_location varchar [note: 'For transfer movements, the storage location to which the material is being moved. Nullable for non-transfer movements.']
  document_date date [note: 'The date on the originating business document (e.g., delivery note date, production order completion date, cycle count date). May differ from posting_date. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  entry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The system timestamp when the goods movement was posted in the ERP system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Distinct from posting_date which is the financial effective date.']
  gl_account varchar [note: 'The GL account to which the financial impact is posted. Determined by movement type and account determination configuration. Examples: inventory asset account, COGS account, scrap expense account.']
  material_description varchar [note: 'Short text description of the material for human readability and reporting. Denormalized from product master for query performance.']
  material_document_item varchar [note: 'Line item number within the material document. Allows multiple movements to be grouped under a single document header.']
  material_document_number varchar [note: 'The SAP MM material document number generated upon posting. Unique identifier for the goods movement in the source ERP system. Ten-digit numeric string.']
  material_document_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year in which the material document was created. Part of the compound key in SAP MM (material_document_number + material_document_year).']
  movement_indicator varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the movement direction and purpose. Receipt increases stock, issue decreases stock, transfer moves between locations, adjustment corrects quantity or value, reversal cancels a prior posting, reclassification changes stock type without location change.']
  movement_type varchar [note: 'SAP MM movement type code defining the nature of the inventory transaction. Examples: 101 (GR for PO), 201 (GI for cost center), 261 (GI for production order), 301 (plant-to-plant transfer), 311 (storage location transfer), 551 (scrapping without PO), 561 (initial entry of stock balance), 701 (GR from blocked stock), 702 (GI to blocked stock). Determines debit/credit logic and account determination. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 101|102|201|202|261|262|301|302|311|312|321|322|343|344|411|412|551|552|561|562|701|702|711|712 — 24 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments or explanations about the movement. Used for manual adjustments, special circumstances, or audit documentation.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'The manufacturing plant or distribution center where the movement occurred. Maps to the organizational unit in SAP.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The financial effective date of the goods movement. Determines the fiscal period for COGS recognition and inventory valuation. Must fall within an open fiscal period. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  profit_center varchar [note: 'The profit center responsible for the movement. Used for segment reporting and profitability analysis.']
  quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The quantity of material moved in the base unit of measure. Positive for receipts, negative for issues. Three decimal places to accommodate fractional units (e.g., 123.456 kg).']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Business reason for the movement. Examples: production consumption, customer return, quality rejection, cycle count adjustment, obsolescence, damage, theft, donation, sample. Used for root cause analysis and variance reporting.']
  reference_document_item varchar [note: 'Line item number within the reference document. Nullable for document types without line items.']
  reference_document_number varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of the originating business document (e.g., PO number, production order number, sales order number, cycle count ID). Nullable for manual postings.']
  reference_document_type varchar [note: 'The type of originating business document that triggered this movement. Enables drill-back to source transaction. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: purchase_order|production_order|sales_order|transfer_order|cycle_count|physical_inventory|quality_inspection|manual_adjustment — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this movement is a reversal of a prior posting. True if this is a cancellation transaction, false otherwise.']
  reversed_document_number varchar [note: 'The material document number of the original posting that this movement reverses. Nullable if reversal_indicator is false.']
  reversed_document_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year of the original posting that this movement reverses. Nullable if reversal_indicator is false.']
  source_plant_code varchar [note: 'For transfer movements, the plant from which the material is being moved. Nullable for non-transfer movements.']
  source_storage_location varchar [note: 'For transfer movements, the storage location from which the material is being moved. Nullable for non-transfer movements.']
  stock_type varchar [note: 'The inventory status category of the material. Unrestricted is available for use, quality_inspection is awaiting QA clearance, blocked is not usable, restricted has limited use, returns is customer-returned stock.']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'The storage location within the plant where the material is physically located. Sub-division of plant for inventory management.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit in which the quantity is expressed. Examples: EA (each), CS (case), KG (kilogram), LB (pound), L (liter), GAL (gallon), OZ (ounce), G (gram), ML (milliliter), TON (ton), PAL (pallet). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|CS|KG|LB|L|GAL|OZ|G|ML|TON|PAL — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  valuation_type varchar [note: 'Split valuation category for materials with multiple valuation classes (e.g., domestic vs imported, organic vs conventional). Nullable if not split-valuated.']
  vendor_code varchar [note: 'Supplier identifier for goods receipt movements from external vendors. Nullable for internal movements.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of every inventory state change — goods receipts, goods issues, stock transfers, returns postings, scrap disposals, write-offs, write-ups, reclassifications, cycle count adjustments, reconciliation corrections, and all other inventory adjustments. Each posting captures movement type, quantity, UoM, source/destination locations, lot reference, posting date, reference document, reason code, adjustment reason (for variance and correction postings), financial impact (debit/credit to inventory and COGS accounts), approver where required, and originating event reference (cycle count, quality inspection, production order, or manual correction). Serves as the single, authoritative audit trail for ALL inventory quantity and value changes — no other entity in this domain records stock state mutations. Foundation for inventory reconciliation, COGS postings, adjustment approval workflows, and regulatory traceability. Aligned with SAP MM movement types (101, 201, 301, 311, 551, 561, 701/702).'
}

Table inventory.stock_transfer_order {
  stock_transfer_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the stock transfer order. Primary key for this entity.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the transportation carrier responsible for moving the stock between locations.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Cost center to which transfer costs are allocated. Used for internal cost accounting and supply chain cost analysis.']
  destination_location_storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the receiving location to which stock is being transferred. May reference plant, distribution center, or third-party logistics (3PL) facility.']
  order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.order. Business justification: Transfer orders are often generated to replenish stock for a specific sales order; linking provides traceability from order demand to internal transfer.']
  primary_stock_employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person or system that created this transfer order. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  primary_stock_storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the originating location from which stock is being transferred. May reference plant, distribution center, or third-party logistics (3PL) facility.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: Transfer orders moving materials to R&D labs are tied to a project; this FK supports the \'Material Transfer to R&D\' operational workflow.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the material, finished good, work-in-progress (WIP), or raw material being transferred. Links to product master data.']
  tertiary_stock_approved_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who approved this transfer order for execution. Required for high-value or cross-region transfers.']
  transfer_price_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.transfer_price. Business justification: Inter‑plant transfer orders are priced using a transfer price record for cost allocation and profitability analysis.']
  actual_receipt_date date [note: 'Actual date when stock was received at the destination location. Captured at goods receipt posting.']
  actual_shipment_date date [note: 'Actual date when stock was shipped from the source location. Captured at goods issue posting.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this transfer order was approved for execution. Marks transition from planned to released status.']
  cancelled_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation for why this transfer order was cancelled. Used for root cause analysis and process improvement.']
  confirmed_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Actual quantity confirmed for transfer after picking and verification. May differ from requested quantity due to availability or quality issues.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this transfer order record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and lifecycle tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this transfer order.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Product expiration or best-by date for the lot being transferred. Used for shelf life monitoring and FEFO lot selection.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing lot or batch number for traceability. Critical for FEFO/FIFO lot management, recall management, and food safety compliance.']
  maximum_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum acceptable temperature in Celsius for this transfer. Used for cold chain monitoring and compliance verification.']
  minimum_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum acceptable temperature in Celsius for this transfer. Used for cold chain monitoring and compliance verification.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this transfer order record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes or instructions related to this transfer order. May include special handling instructions, quality concerns, or coordination details.']
  planned_transfer_date date [note: 'Target date for initiating the stock transfer. Used for planning warehouse resources, transportation capacity, and inventory positioning.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level for processing and execution of the transfer order. Determines sequencing in warehouse operations and transportation planning.']
  received_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Actual quantity received at destination location. May differ from confirmed quantity due to damage, loss, or discrepancies during transit.']
  requested_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Planned quantity to be transferred as specified in the transfer order. Represents the target amount for the transfer.']
  shelf_life_remaining_days varchar [note: 'Number of days of shelf life remaining at the time of transfer initiation. Critical for FEFO (First Expired First Out) inventory management.']
  shipment_number varchar [note: 'Reference number for the physical shipment associated with this transfer order. Links to transportation management and carrier tracking.']
  sku varchar [note: 'Stock Keeping Unit code identifying the specific product variant being transferred. Used for inventory tracking and warehouse operations.']
  special_handling_code varchar [note: 'Special handling requirements for this transfer. Determines warehouse handling procedures, packaging requirements, and transportation mode.']
  stock_type varchar [note: 'Classification of stock status at source location. Determines whether stock is available for transfer and use.']
  temperature_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether temperature requirements were maintained throughout the transfer. False value triggers quality review and potential product hold.']
  temperature_controlled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this transfer requires temperature-controlled transportation and handling. Critical for perishable food and beverage products.']
  transfer_cost_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total cost associated with this stock transfer including transportation, handling, and administrative costs. Used for supply chain cost analysis.']
  transfer_order_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the stock transfer order. Used for tracking and reference across systems and documents.']
  transfer_reason_code varchar [note: 'Business reason or trigger for the stock transfer. Used for analytics, cost allocation, and supply chain optimization.']
  transfer_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the stock transfer order. Tracks progression from planning through execution to completion or cancellation.']
  transfer_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the transfer based on source and destination location types. Determines routing, handling requirements, and approval workflows.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for all quantity fields. Standard units include each (EA), case (CS), pallet (PL), kilogram (KG), pound (LB), gallon (GAL), liter (LTR). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|CS|PL|KG|LB|GAL|LTR — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Transactional record for planned and executed inter-location stock transfers — plant-to-plant, DC-to-DC, plant-to-DC, and 3PL replenishment transfers. Captures transfer order number, source and destination locations, material/SKU, lot/batch, requested quantity, confirmed quantity, transfer reason, priority, planned transfer date, actual shipment and receipt dates, temperature compliance flag, and transfer status (planned, released, in-transit, received, cancelled). Distinct from goods_movement: this entity represents the planning/execution document (the \'order\'), while goods_movement records the actual stock postings (issue at source, receipt at destination). Aligned with SAP WM Transfer Order (LT0A) and EWM Warehouse Task.'
}

Table inventory.cycle_count {
  cycle_count_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the cycle count event. Primary key for the cycle count record.']
  center_id varchar [note: 'Distribution center where the cycle count is performed. Null if count is at plant or 3PL location.']
  counter_assigned_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Cycle Count Counter Assignment report requires identifying the employee performing the count; creates clear accountability.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Manufacturing plant or production facility where the cycle count is performed.']
  accuracy_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated inventory accuracy percentage for this cycle count, representing the ratio of items counted correctly to total items counted.']
  actual_count_date date [note: 'Actual date when the physical count was performed. May differ from scheduled date due to operational constraints.']
  audit_completion_date date [note: 'Date when audit review of the cycle count was completed and findings documented. Null if no audit required or not yet completed.']
  audit_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this cycle count is subject to internal or external audit review due to materiality or compliance requirements. True if audit required, False otherwise.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text comments or notes related to the cycle count event, including special instructions, issues encountered, or operational observations.']
  count_completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the cycle count activity was completed and all items were counted.']
  count_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total duration in minutes from count start to count completion. Used for labor efficiency analysis.']
  count_method varchar [note: 'Method used to perform the physical count: manual (paper-based), barcode scan (handheld scanner), RFID (radio frequency identification), automated system (WMS-driven), blind count (no system quantity shown), or guided count (system quantity visible).']
  count_plan_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the master cycle count plan or schedule that triggered this count event. Used for planned cycle counts.']
  count_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the cycle count activity was initiated. Used to track count duration and labor efficiency.']
  count_type varchar [note: 'Type of inventory count being performed: full physical inventory (complete stock take), cycle count (scheduled rotation), spot check (ad-hoc verification), wall-to-wall (complete facility count), ABC cycle (frequency based on value), or perpetual inventory (continuous counting).']
  counter_assigned varchar [note: 'Name or employee ID of the warehouse worker or team assigned to perform the physical count.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the cycle count record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for variance value amounts.']
  cycle_count_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cycle count: planned (scheduled but not started), in-progress (counting underway), completed (count finished, awaiting reconciliation), reconciled (variances resolved and posted), cancelled (count aborted), or on-hold (temporarily suspended).']
  freeze_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when inventory freeze was lifted and normal operations resumed. Null if no freeze applied.']
  freeze_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when inventory movements were frozen for the count. Null if no freeze applied.']
  inventory_freeze_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether inventory movements were frozen during the count to ensure accuracy. True if frozen, False otherwise.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the cycle count record was last updated or modified.']
  lot_traceability_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether lot-level traceability is required for items in this cycle count due to FSMA or food safety regulations. True if lot tracking required, False otherwise.']
  physical_inventory_document_number varchar [note: 'SAP physical inventory document number generated for this cycle count event. Maps to SAP MI01/MI04 document.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the cycle count based on item criticality, value, or operational urgency: critical (immediate attention), high (expedited), medium (standard), or low (routine).']
  reconciliation_date date [note: 'Date when inventory variances were reconciled and adjustments posted to the general ledger and inventory records.']
  reconciliation_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting the reconciliation process, root cause analysis of variances, and corrective actions taken.']
  recount_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the recount was completed, if a recount was required. Null if no recount performed.']
  recount_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a recount is required due to variances exceeding threshold or data quality concerns. True if recount needed, False otherwise.']
  scheduled_date date [note: 'Planned date for the cycle count to be performed. Set during count planning phase.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that originated this cycle count record (e.g., SAP S/4HANA WM, Oracle WMS, Blue Yonder WMS).']
  storage_location_code varchar [note: 'Specific storage location code within the plant or DC where inventory is counted. Aligns with SAP WM storage location.']
  supervisor_assigned varchar [note: 'Name or employee ID of the supervisor responsible for overseeing and validating the cycle count.']
  total_items_counted varchar [note: 'Total number of distinct SKUs or line items included in this cycle count event.']
  total_variance_items varchar [note: 'Number of items where physical count differed from book inventory, exceeding the variance threshold.']
  total_variance_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total financial value of inventory variances identified during the cycle count, calculated using standard cost or moving average price.']
  variance_threshold_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Acceptable variance threshold as a percentage of book quantity before a recount or investigation is triggered.']
  variance_threshold_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Acceptable variance threshold in quantity units before a recount or investigation is triggered. Set based on item value and criticality.']

  Note: 'Master record for scheduled and ad-hoc physical inventory cycle count events at a storage location. Captures count plan reference, count type (full physical inventory, cycle count, spot check), scheduled date, actual count date, counter assigned, count status (planned, in-progress, completed, reconciled), and variance threshold. Aligned with SAP MI01/MI04 Physical Inventory Document. Supports continuous inventory accuracy programs and audit compliance.'
}

Table inventory.cycle_count_line {
  cycle_count_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the line record within a cycle count event.']
  cycle_count_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent cycle count header to which this line belongs.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee or user who performed the count.']
  adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation for the adjustment made.']
  adjustment_type varchar [note: 'Type of inventory adjustment applied to resolve the variance.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the lot complies with quality and regulatory standards.']
  count_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the physical count was performed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the line record was created in the system.']
  cycle_count_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the cycle count line.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the lot expires or reaches the end of its shelf life.']
  final_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity after adjustments and reconciliation.']
  inventory_valuation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to value inventory for this line (e.g., FIFO, FEFO).']
  is_critical_item boolean [note: 'True if the item is high‑value, regulated, or otherwise critical to business operations.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the line within the cycle count document.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot or batch identifier for traceability (FEFO/FIFO management).']
  material_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the material or SKU.']
  material_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier of the stocked material or SKU being counted.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Optional free‑text comments or observations captured by the counter.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant or production site.']
  quantity_book decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity recorded in the system (booked) for the material/lot.']
  quantity_counted decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity physically counted during the cycle count.']
  quantity_variance decimal(15,3) [note: 'Difference between counted quantity and book quantity (counted – book).']
  recount_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a recount is required for this line (true = recount needed).']
  regulatory_lot_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory state of the lot (e.g., released for sale, quarantined).']
  storage_bin varchar [note: 'Code of the storage bin or location where the material is stored.']
  unit_cost decimal(12,4) [note: 'Standard cost per unit of the material for valuation purposes.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the quantity (e.g., each, kilogram).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the line record.']
  variance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage variance calculated as (quantity_variance / quantity_book) * 100.']
  variance_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standard code indicating why a variance occurred.']
  warehouse_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution center, DC, or 3PL location.']

  Note: 'Line-level detail for each SKU/lot counted within a cycle count event. Captures the material/SKU, storage bin, lot number, system book quantity, physically counted quantity, variance quantity, variance percentage, variance reason code, recount flag, and final reconciled quantity. Supports root-cause analysis of inventory discrepancies and drives adjustment postings. Aligned with SAP MI04/MI07 Physical Inventory Count and Difference.'
}

Table inventory.adjustment {
  adjustment_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the inventory adjustment record.']
  adjustment_created_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created the adjustment record.']
  adjustment_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who approved the adjustment.']
  adjustment_reclass_target_location_storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Destination location identifier for inventory reclassification adjustments.']
  adjustment_reclass_target_material_sku_id varchar [note: 'Destination material (SKU) identifier for reclassification adjustments.']
  adjustment_sku_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the material (SKU) being adjusted.']
  adjustment_storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Warehouse, distribution center, or 3PL location where the adjustment took place.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Cost center associated with the financial impact of the adjustment.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: ACCOUNTING ENTRY: Adjustments (e.g., write‑offs, reclass) require a GL account for journal posting; finance.gl_account provides the required account.']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list. Business justification: Adjustment entries reference the price list used for re‑valuation of adjusted inventory, required for audit trails and financial statements.']
  product_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.product_registration. Business justification: Inventory adjustments affect quantities of registered products; regulators require linkage for accurate reporting of stock changes.']
  promotion_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trade.promotion_event. Business justification: Promotional events often trigger inventory adjustments; linking provides traceability for promo‑driven inventory changes.']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Inventory adjustments modify quantities of a particular stock position; linking directly removes redundant location/SKU data.']
  accounting_document_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the financial document generated by the adjustment.']
  adjusted_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity change resulting from the adjustment (positive for increase, negative for decrease).']
  adjusted_value_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the quantity adjustment before accounting postings.']
  adjustment_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the adjustment (e.g., posting number).']
  adjustment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the adjustment record.']
  adjustment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the adjustment event occurred in the business process.']
  adjustment_type varchar [note: 'Category of the inventory adjustment indicating the nature of the change.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the adjustment was approved.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the adjustment record was first created in the system.']
  credit_account varchar [note: 'General ledger account credited by the adjustment.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary amounts.']
  debit_account varchar [note: 'General ledger account debited by the adjustment.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Calendar date when the adjusted inventory item expires.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'Identifier linking to an external system record (e.g., EDI message).']
  financial_impact_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net monetary impact of the adjustment after accounting entries.']
  inventory_valuation_method varchar [note: 'Valuation method applied to the inventory at the time of adjustment.']
  is_manual_adjustment boolean [note: 'True if the adjustment was entered manually rather than generated by an automated process.']
  is_quality_hold boolean [note: 'True if the inventory is placed on quality hold as part of the adjustment.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments or remarks about the adjustment.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Accounting posting date for the adjustment.']
  quality_hold_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the inventory was placed on quality hold.']
  reason_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category grouping of adjustment reasons.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing why the adjustment was made.']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description providing additional context for the adjustment reason.']
  shelf_life_remaining_days varchar [note: 'Number of days remaining before the product expires at the time of adjustment.']
  source_document_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the originating event (e.g., cycle count document).']
  source_document_type varchar [note: 'Type of the originating document that triggered the adjustment.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Source ERP or system that originated the adjustment (e.g., SAP WM).']
  tax_implication_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the adjustment has tax consequences.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the adjusted quantity.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the adjustment record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of inventory quantity and value adjustments resulting from cycle counts, quality holds, write-offs, scrap, and reconciliation corrections. Captures adjustment type (write-off, write-up, scrap, quality block, reclass), adjusted quantity, adjustment reason, financial impact (debit/credit to COGS or inventory account), approver, and reference to originating event (cycle count, quality inspection, or manual correction). Aligned with SAP MM inventory adjustment postings.'
}

Table inventory.valuation {
  valuation_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each inventory valuation record.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: FINANCIAL REPORTING: Inventory valuation adjustments are posted to a GL account; linking to finance.gl_account enables accurate balance‑sheet entries.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant or distribution center where the inventory is located.']
  prepared_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Valuation Preparation Report mandates the employee who performed the valuation for compliance and audit purposes.']
  product_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.product_registration. Business justification: Valuation statements submitted to regulators must identify the product registration associated with each valuation record.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the material (SKU) being valued.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary adjustments applied during valuation (e.g., price variance, revaluation).']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch identifier used for traceability of the material.']
  class_code varchar [note: 'Code that groups materials for accounting purposes (e.g., raw material, finished goods).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the valuation record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the monetary amounts.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the material expires, if applicable.']
  gross_valuation_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total value before any adjustments (quantity_on_hand * unit_cost).']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Identifier for the production lot associated with the inventory.']
  material_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the material.']
  material_number varchar [note: 'Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) or Global Trade Item Number (GTIN) of the material.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the valuation record.']
  net_valuation_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final valuation amount after adjustments (gross - adjustments).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or remarks related to the valuation.']
  price_variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Difference between standard cost and actual cost for the material.']
  quantity_on_hand decimal(18,3) [note: 'Current physical quantity of the material in stock at the valuation date.']
  revaluation_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount added or subtracted to align inventory value with current market or cost changes.']
  shelf_life_remaining_days varchar [note: 'Number of days remaining before the material reaches its shelf life limit.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating ERP or warehouse management system that supplied the valuation data.']
  unit_cost decimal(18,6) [note: 'Cost per unit of material as determined by the selected valuation method.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the material quantity (kilograms, liters, each, case).']
  valuation_method varchar [note: 'Costing method used for the valuation (Standard Cost, Moving Average, FIFO, LIFO).']
  valuation_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the valuation transaction for audit and traceability.']
  valuation_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the valuation record.']
  valuation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the inventory valuation was performed.']

  Note: 'Financial valuation record for inventory by material, plant, and valuation class. Captures valuation method (standard cost, moving average, FIFO), unit cost, total inventory value, price variance, and revaluation amounts. Supports COGS calculation, balance sheet inventory reporting, month-end close, and cost variance analysis.'
}

Table inventory.lot_trace {
  lot_trace_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each lot traceability record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'System user that initially created the lot traceability record.']
  inspection_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_lot. Business justification: Traceability reports attach inspection outcomes to each lot; the link enables downstream quality status calculations.']
  product_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.product_registration. Business justification: Lot traceability reports must reference the product registration to ensure labeling, safety, and regulatory compliance of each lot.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: Lot traceability records which R&D project used a specific lot, required for compliance and audit of experimental batches.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'SKU or GTIN of the product contained in the lot.']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Lot traceability is tied to a specific stock position; the FK consolidates location and lot information.']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the storage location (warehouse, DC, or 3PL site) holding the lot.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the lot meets all applicable FDA/USDA/GFSI requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency for lot cost.']
  downstream_lot_refs varchar [note: 'Identifiers of finished‑goods or shipment lots derived from this lot.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the lot is considered expired for sale or consumption.']
  fsma_one_up_one_down_flag boolean [note: 'True when the lot must satisfy FSMA one‑up/one‑down traceability.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the latest modification to the lot traceability record.']
  lot_cost_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary cost attributed to the lot (e.g., material cost).']
  lot_creation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the lot traceability record was first created.']
  lot_owner varchar [note: 'Name of the party that owns or supplied the lot.']
  lot_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the lot within inventory.']
  lot_type varchar [note: 'Category indicating whether the lot is raw material, intermediate, finished good, or packaging.']
  max_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Upper bound of the temperature range for the lot.']
  min_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Lower bound of the temperature range for the lot.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the manufacturing plant where the lot was produced.']
  product_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the product associated with the lot.']
  production_date date [note: 'Date the lot was created in the manufacturing process.']
  quality_status varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent quality check performed on the lot.']
  quantity varchar [note: 'Total count or weight of units contained in the lot.']
  recall_flag boolean [note: 'True if the lot has been placed under a product recall.']
  receipt_date date [note: 'Date the lot entered inventory from inbound receipt.']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the lot remains usable from production date.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system of the lot data (e.g., SAP WM, Aveva MES).']
  temperature_controlled_flag boolean [note: 'True when the lot must be stored within a defined temperature range.']
  traceability_status varchar [note: 'Indicates the one‑up/one‑down traceability configuration for the lot.']
  transformation_event_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing or co‑packing event that transformed upstream lots into this lot.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the lot quantity (e.g., kilograms, liters).']
  upstream_lot_refs varchar [note: 'Identifiers of ingredient or packaging lots that were combined to create this lot.']

  Note: 'Traceability record linking each lot/batch through its complete supply chain journey — from raw material receipt (supplier lot), through production (manufacturing batch), to finished goods lot and outbound shipment. Captures upstream lot references (ingredient lots, packaging lots), downstream lot references (finished goods lots, customer shipment lots), transformation events (production order, co-packing run), and traceability status. Supports FSMA one-up/one-down traceability requirements and recall execution.'
}

Table inventory.quarantine_hold {
  quarantine_hold_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each quarantine hold record.']
  center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution center (DC) where the inventory is stored.']
  material_id varchar [note: 'Internal identifier of the material (SKU/GTIN) that is on hold.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant owning the inventory.']
  primary_quarantine_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the quality manager who authorized or reviewed the hold.']
  recall_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.recall_event. Business justification: Quarantine holds are often triggered by recall events; linking enables the recall management process to track affected inventory.']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Quarantine hold is applied to a specific stock position; linking provides the exact inventory record under hold.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier associated with the material, used when the hold originates from supplier deviation.']
  tertiary_quarantine_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who last modified the hold record.']
  allergen_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the hold is due to allergen cross‑contact risk.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes from quality personnel.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall compliance state of the held inventory.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the quarantine hold record was created in the system.']
  disposition_decision varchar [note: 'Final decision taken after review of the hold.']
  expected_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time for the hold to be reviewed or resolved.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the lot/batch that is on hold.']
  hold_initiation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the quarantine hold was placed on inventory.']
  hold_number varchar [note: 'External reference number used by quality teams to track the hold.']
  hold_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text description providing detailed justification for the hold.']
  hold_release_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the hold was officially released (nullable if not yet released).']
  hold_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that originated the hold record (e.g., SAP QM).']
  hold_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the hold.']
  hold_type varchar [note: 'Category of the hold indicating the underlying reason such as quality inspection, regulatory, allergen cross‑contact, recall, or supplier deviation.']
  is_critical_item boolean [note: 'True if the held inventory is classified as a critical item for production or supply.']
  material_number varchar [note: 'Business code of the material (e.g., SKU, GTIN).']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the quarantine hold record.']
  quantity_held decimal(18,3) [note: 'Amount of inventory (in the unit defined by unit_of_measure) placed under quarantine.']
  recall_flag boolean [note: 'True when the hold is associated with a product recall.']
  regulatory_lot_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory classification of the lot (e.g., approved, restricted, under review).']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the regulatory notice or compliance document (e.g., FDA recall notice).']
  shelf_life_remaining_days varchar [note: 'Number of days remaining before the product reaches its shelf life limit.']
  storage_location_code varchar [note: 'Code of the warehouse or storage bin where the held inventory resides.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for quantity_held (e.g., kg, L, units).']

  Note: 'Operational record for inventory placed under quality hold or quarantine status — pending quality inspection, regulatory hold, allergen cross-contact risk, or recall investigation. Captures hold type (quality inspection, regulatory, allergen, recall, supplier deviation), hold reason, quantity held, lot/batch reference, storage location, hold initiation date, expected review date, hold release date, disposition decision (release, rework, destroy, return to supplier), and authorizing quality manager. Aligned with SAP QM usage decision. Drives stock blocking in stock_position and triggers quality domain workflows.'
}

Table inventory.wip_stock {
  wip_stock_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each WIP stock record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Cost center responsible for the WIP cost allocation.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the WIP record.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant where the WIP is located.']
  product_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.product_registration. Business justification: Work‑in‑process tracking must reference product registration to ensure safety, labeling, and regulatory compliance during production.']
  production_line_work_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific production line within the work center.']
  production_shift_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the work shift during which the WIP was recorded.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the work center or production line handling the WIP.']
  actual_completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the WIP was actually completed.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch number associated with the WIP material.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance status of the WIP (e.g., FDA, GFSI).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the WIP record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the valuation amount.']
  estimated_completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned timestamp when the WIP is expected to be completed.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Calendar date when the WIP material must be used or discarded.']
  is_fefo boolean [note: 'True if the WIP is managed using First‑Expired‑First‑Out (FEFO) logic.']
  is_fifo boolean [note: 'True if the WIP is managed using First‑In‑First‑Out (FIFO) logic.']
  is_lot_traceable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the WIP lot is subject to traceability requirements (true/false).']
  is_quality_checked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the WIP has passed quality inspection (true/false).']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot identifier for traceability of the WIP batch.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the WIP record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations about the WIP record.']
  production_order_number varchar [note: 'External production order number associated with the WIP inventory.']
  quality_status varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent quality inspection for the WIP.']
  quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Amount of material in the WIP stage, expressed in the unit of measure.']
  shelf_life_remaining_days varchar [note: 'Number of days remaining before the WIP expires based on shelf‑life policy.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that supplied the WIP data.']
  stage_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the WIP stage (e.g., raw mix, in‑process).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the quantity (e.g., kilograms, pounds, units).']
  wip_stock_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the WIP record.']
  wip_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the WIP inventory type.']
