  reporting_date date [note: 'Date when the outcome was formally reported to stakeholders.']
  sdg_contribution varchar [note: 'UN SDG(s) that the outcome supports. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SDG1|SDG2|SDG3|SDG4|SDG5|SDG6|SDG7|SDG8|SDG9|SDG10|SDG11|SDG12|SDG13|SDG14|SDG15|SDG16|SDG17 — promote to reference product]']
  sdg_target varchar [note: 'Specific SDG target identifier (e.g., 1.2, 5.a) linked to the outcome.']
  third_party_verification_status varchar [note: 'Status of external verification of the reported outcome.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the outcome record.']
  verification_body varchar [note: 'Name of the third‑party organization that performed verification.']
  waste_reduction_kg decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of waste reduced as a result of the program, measured in kilograms.']

  Note: 'Measured outcomes and beneficiary impact records for social responsibility programs, capturing measurement period, outcome type (farmers trained, income uplift, meals donated, women empowered, children reached), number of beneficiaries, geographic reach, measurement methodology, third-party verification status, and SDG contribution evidence. Supports GRI social disclosures and ESG investor reporting.'
}

Table sustainability.esg_rating {
  esg_rating_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each ESG rating record.']
  esg_disclosure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.esg_disclosure. Business justification: Each ESG rating is produced for a specific ESG disclosure; linking rating to its disclosure enables traceability.']
  assessment_date date [note: 'Date on which the ESG rating was issued.']
  carbon_emission_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total reported carbon emissions in kilograms for the reporting period.']
  cdp_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score assigned by CDP for climate change disclosure.']
  data_gaps_flagged boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agency identified missing or incomplete data in the ESG submission.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the rating is considered effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date until which the rating remains effective; null if open‑ended.']
  environment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score for the environmental pillar of the ESG assessment.']
  governance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score for the governance pillar of the ESG assessment.']
  gri_report_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the GRI sustainability report linked to this rating.']
  index_inclusion_status varchar [note: 'Current status of inclusion in a sustainability index (e.g., FTSE4Good, DJSI).']
  index_name varchar [note: 'Name of the sustainability index where the rating is (or may be) included.']
  investor_inquiry_count varchar [note: 'Number of investor inquiries received regarding this ESG rating period.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text field for additional comments or observations about the rating.']
  overall_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite ESG score representing the overall performance across environmental, social, and governance pillars.']
  packaging_recyclability_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Proportion of packaging material that is recyclable or made from recycled content.']
  prior_period_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overall ESG score from the previous reporting period for trend analysis.']
  rating_agency varchar [note: 'Name of the third‑party ESG rating agency (e.g., MSCI ESG, Sustainalytics, ISS ESG, CDP, FTSE4Good, DJSI).']
  rating_category varchar [note: 'Qualitative tier assigned by the agency (e.g., Leader, Average, Laggard).']
  rating_methodology_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the rating methodology used by the agency for this assessment.']
  rating_scope varchar [note: 'Scope of emissions covered by the rating (Scope 1, Scope 2, Scope 3, or All).']
  rating_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the rating record.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the ESG rating record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the ESG rating record.']
  renewable_energy_usage_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total energy consumption derived from renewable sources.']
  responsible_sourcing_certifications varchar [note: 'List of sustainability certifications held by suppliers (e.g., Rainforest Alliance, Fair Trade).']
  score_change decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric difference between the current overall score and the prior period score.']
  score_detractors varchar [note: 'Key weaknesses or issues identified by the agency that negatively impacted the rating.']
  score_drivers varchar [note: 'Key factors or initiatives highlighted by the agency that positively influenced the rating.']
  social_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score for the social pillar of the ESG assessment.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record that supplied the ESG rating data (e.g., ESG Data Lake, third‑party API).']
  sustainability_target_alignment varchar [note: 'Alignment of the rating with external sustainability targets (e.g., UN SDGs).']
  waste_reduction_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage reduction in waste generated compared to the baseline period.']
  water_usage_intensity decimal(10,2) [note: 'Water consumption per unit of production (e.g., cubic meters per tonne).']

  Note: 'Records of external ESG ratings, scores, and index memberships assigned to Food Beverage by third-party rating agencies and sustainability indices. Captures rating agency (MSCI ESG, Sustainalytics, ISS ESG, CDP score, FTSE4Good, DJSI), rating methodology version, overall score and pillar sub-scores (E/S/G), rating category or tier (Leader/Average/Laggard), assessment date, prior period comparison, score drivers and detractors cited by the agency, data gaps flagged, index inclusion/exclusion status, and investor inquiry tracking. Supports investor relations, peer benchmarking, and targeted improvement of weak-scoring areas.'
}

Table sustainability.initiative {
  initiative_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the sustainability initiative.']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Brand-level ESG reporting requires linking each sustainability initiative to its owning brand for the Brand Sustainability Scorecard.']
  owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Each sustainability initiative has an owner employee responsible for execution; the FK supports initiative dashboards and resource allocation.']
  actual_co2e_tonnes_avoided decimal(12,3) [note: 'Measured CO₂‑equivalent emissions avoided (tonnes) to date.']
  actual_end_date date [note: 'Date when the initiative was actually completed.']
  actual_waste_kg_diverted decimal(12,3) [note: 'Measured amount of waste diverted from landfill (kilograms) to date.']
  actual_water_m3_saved decimal(12,3) [note: 'Measured volume of water saved (cubic metres) to date.']
  capital_investment_required_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total capital expenditure required to implement the initiative (USD).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  data_quality_status varchar [note: 'Current data quality validation status for the record.']
  esg_pillar varchar [note: 'Top‑level ESG pillar to which the initiative belongs.']
  implementation_status varchar [note: 'Progress status of implementation activities.']
  initiative_category varchar [note: 'Specific sustainability category (e.g., energy efficiency, water reduction, waste elimination, sustainable sourcing, fleet electrification, circular economy). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: energy_efficiency|water_reduction|waste_elimination|sustainable_sourcing|fleet_electrification|circular_economy|other — promote to reference product]']
  initiative_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code used to reference the initiative in external systems.']
  initiative_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the initiative, objectives, and scope.']
  initiative_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the sustainability initiative.']
  initiative_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the initiative.']
  linked_sustainability_target varchar [note: 'Reference code of the corporate sustainability target that this initiative supports.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information or comments.']
  operating_cost_savings_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Annual operating cost savings realized (USD).']
  owning_business_unit varchar [note: 'Business unit or department responsible for the initiative.']
  packaging_recyclability_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Share of packaging material that is recyclable or recycled.']
  payback_period_years decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated number of years to recoup the capital investment.']
  phase_gate varchar [note: 'Current phase gate of the initiative lifecycle.']
  projected_co2e_tonnes_avoided decimal(12,3) [note: 'Estimated CO₂‑equivalent emissions avoided (tonnes) if the initiative meets its target.']
  projected_end_date date [note: 'Planned completion date for the initiative.']
  projected_waste_kg_diverted decimal(12,3) [note: 'Estimated amount of waste diverted from landfill (kilograms).']
  projected_water_m3_saved decimal(12,3) [note: 'Estimated volume of water saved (cubic metres) by the initiative.']
  renewable_energy_usage_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total energy consumption sourced from renewable resources.']
  reporting_period_end date [note: 'End date of the reporting period for initiative metrics.']
  reporting_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the reporting period for initiative metrics.']
  responsible_sourcing_certifications varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of certifications (e.g., Rainforest Alliance, Fair Trade) associated with the initiative.']
  roi_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Projected return on investment expressed as a percentage.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record where the initiative originated (e.g., SAP S/4HANA, Oracle Cloud ERP).']
  stakeholder_engagement_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score (0‑100) reflecting stakeholder engagement and satisfaction.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the initiative officially started.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']

  Note: 'Master data for internal sustainability improvement projects and capital investments across manufacturing, sourcing, packaging, logistics, and corporate operations. Captures initiative name, ESG pillar (environmental, social, governance), category (energy efficiency, water reduction, waste elimination, sustainable sourcing, fleet electrification), owning business unit, implementation status and phase gates, projected environmental impact (CO2e tonnes avoided, water m3 saved, waste kg diverted), actual impact realized to date, capital investment required, operating cost savings, payback period, and linkage to corporate sustainability targets. Enables portfolio management of ESG investments and ROI tracking.'
}

Table sustainability.carbon_offset {
  carbon_offset_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each carbon offset credit record.']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Brands purchase carbon offsets to achieve carbon‑neutral claims; linking offsets to brand supports brand‑level carbon accounting.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the offset project supplier or provider.']
  allocation_category varchar [note: 'Business category or product line to which the offset credit is assigned (e.g., specific SKU, product family).']
  allocation_scope varchar [note: 'GHG accounting scope to which the offset is allocated (Scope 1, 2, or 3).']
  carbon_offset_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the offset credit (e.g., purchased, retired, pending, cancelled).']
  cost_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total monetary cost paid for the offset credit in US dollars.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the carbon offset record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for the transaction (e.g., USD, EUR).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or additional information about the offset transaction.']
  offset_reference_number varchar [note: 'External reference number or code assigned to the carbon offset transaction by the registry or internal system.']
  offset_type varchar [note: 'Category of the carbon offset project (e.g., reforestation, soil carbon, methane capture, cookstoves).']
  price_per_tco2e decimal(12,4) [note: 'Unit price paid per metric tonne of CO₂e.']
  project_location varchar [note: 'Geographic location (city, region, country) where the offset project is implemented.']
  project_name varchar [note: 'Official name of the carbon offset project.']
  quantity_tco2e decimal(15,6) [note: 'Amount of carbon dioxide equivalent (in metric tonnes) represented by the offset credit.']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the record in the data lake (active, inactive, archived).']
  registry_serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number assigned by the carbon registry for tracking the credit.']
  retirement_date date [note: 'Date on which the offset credit was retired or applied to a scope.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source operational system where the record originated (e.g., SAP S/4HANA, TraceGains).']
  standard varchar [note: 'Certification standard under which the offset credit is issued (e.g., Gold Standard, Verified Carbon Standard, American Carbon Registry).']
  transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the carbon offset transaction was executed or recorded.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit used for the quantity field; fixed as metric tonnes of CO₂e.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the carbon offset record.']
  verification_body varchar [note: 'Organization that performed the verification of the offset project.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Current verification status of the offset credit.']
  vintage_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year in which the carbon offset was generated.']

  Note: 'Records of carbon offset credits purchased or retired by Food Beverage to compensate for residual GHG emissions, capturing offset project type (reforestation, soil carbon, methane capture, cookstoves), project location, standard (Gold Standard, VCS/Verra, ACR), vintage year, quantity (tCO2e), registry serial numbers, retirement date, and allocation to specific Scope 3 categories or product lines. Supports net-zero claims and CDP disclosure.'
}

Table sustainability.audit {
  audit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the sustainability audit record.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.asset. Business justification: Audits often assess specific machines; FK allows audit findings and corrective actions to be tied to the audited asset.']
  auditor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Internal sustainability audits are performed by auditors who are employees; linking audit records to employee enables audit trail and compliance reporting.']
  initiative_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.sustainability_initiative. Business justification: Sustainability audits assess specific initiatives; linking audit to initiative enables drill‑down.']
  assurance_level varchar [note: 'Level of assurance provided (e.g., limited, reasonable, high).']
  assurance_provider varchar [note: 'Entity that performed the assurance engagement.']
  audit_date date [note: 'Date on which the audit was performed.']
  audit_number varchar [note: 'Business-assigned audit number used for external reference and tracking.']
  audit_scope varchar [note: 'Scope of the audit covering facilities, suppliers, distribution centers, or corporate operations.']
  audit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the audit.']
  audit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the audit based on the standard or focus area.']
  audited_entity_name varchar [note: 'Name of the organization, facility, or supplier being audited.']
  auditor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual who performed the audit.']
  auditor_organization varchar [note: 'Organization or firm providing the audit service.']
  carbon_footprint_kg decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total carbon emissions measured for the audited entity, expressed in kilograms CO₂e.']
  cdp_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score assigned by CDP for climate-related performance.']
  certification varchar [note: 'Certification result issued after the audit.']
  certification_date date [note: 'Date the certification was granted.']
  certification_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the certification document.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Target date for completion of corrective actions.']
  corrective_action_plan varchar [note: 'Detailed plan to address audit findings.']
  corrective_action_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the corrective action plan.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Source system or tool from which audit data was imported.']
  duration_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days the audit spanned.']
  emission_scope varchar [note: 'Greenhouse gas emission scope covered by the audit.']
  findings_severity varchar [note: 'Highest severity level among findings identified.']
  gri_disclosure_topic varchar [note: 'GRI reporting topic(s) addressed by the audit.']
  location varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary facility or site audited.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text observations captured by the auditor.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the audit after evaluation.']
  packaging_recyclability_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Share of packaging material that is recyclable or recycled.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the audit record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the audit record.']
  region varchar [note: 'Three-letter country code where the audit took place.']
  renewable_energy_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total energy consumption sourced from renewable resources.']
  report_url varchar [note: 'Link to the digital audit report document.']
  responsible_sourcing_certifications varchar [note: 'List of certifications (e.g., Rainforest Alliance, Fair Trade) held by the audited entity.']
  scope_boundary varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the geographic and operational boundaries covered.']
  standard varchar [note: 'Specific standard applied during the audit (e.g., ISO 14001, GFSI, ISAE 3000).']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date the verification was completed.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Status of third‑party verification of audit results.']
  waste_generated_kg decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total waste generated, measured in kilograms.']
  water_usage_m3 decimal(15,3) [note: 'Volume of water withdrawn/consumed during the reporting period.']

  Note: 'Records of internal and third-party sustainability audits and assurance engagements conducted at manufacturing plants, supplier facilities, distribution centers, and corporate offices. Captures audit type (ISO 14001 surveillance, social compliance, limited/reasonable assurance for ESG data, packaging recyclability verification, deforestation-free supply chain), audit standard applied, audit date and duration, auditing body or assurance provider, scope boundaries, findings by severity (critical, major, minor, observation), corrective action plans with due dates, closure status, and certification or assurance statement outcome. Supports ISAE 3000 assurance, GFSI benchmarked schemes, and responsible sourcing verification.'
}

Table sustainability.materiality_assessment {
  materiality_assessment_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for the materiality assessment record. _canonical_skip_reason: Entity does not fit standard role; treated as OTHER.']
  lead_assessor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Materiality assessments are led by a specific employee; linking captures who authored the assessment for governance and audit.']
  previous_materiality_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on materiality_assessment (previous_materiality_assessment_id)']
  target_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.sustainability_target. Business justification: Materiality assessments evaluate particular sustainability targets; FK creates the needed relationship.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name of the board member or executive who approved the assessment.']
  assessment_conducted_date date [note: 'Date on which the materiality assessment was completed.']
  assessment_methodology varchar [note: 'Methodology used to conduct the materiality assessment, aligned with recognized frameworks.']
  assessment_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name given to the assessment (e.g., FY2025 Double Materiality Review).']
  assessment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the materiality assessment.']
  assessment_type varchar [note: 'Specifies whether the assessment follows a double‑materiality or single‑materiality approach.']
  board_approval_date date [note: 'Date the board formally approved the materiality assessment results.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the assessment record was first created in the system.']
  data_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overall data quality rating for the assessment record (0‑100).']
  disclosure_scope varchar [note: 'Scope of ESG disclosures considered (e.g., Scope 1‑3 emissions).']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the assessment results are considered effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date until which the assessment results remain valid (null if ongoing).']
  environmental_impact_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative score reflecting the environmental significance of material topics.']
  esg_pillar varchar [note: 'Primary ESG pillar(s) addressed by the assessment.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'Identifier linking to external ESG disclosure or regulatory filing.']
  final_material_topics varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of ESG topics deemed material after scoring.']
  financial_impact_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative score reflecting the financial significance of material topics.']
  governance_impact_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative score reflecting the governance significance of material topics.']
  materiality_score_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric threshold value that separates material from non‑material topics.']
  materiality_threshold_criteria varchar [note: 'Criteria and numeric thresholds used to determine materiality (e.g., score > 0.6).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations related to the assessment.']
  overall_materiality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aggregated score representing the overall materiality of the assessment.']
  reassessment_frequency_months varchar [note: 'Number of months between required reassessments of materiality.']
  regulatory_framework varchar [note: 'Regulatory or reporting framework governing the assessment.']
  reporting_period_end date [note: 'End date of the reporting period covered by the assessment.']
  reporting_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the reporting period covered by the assessment.']
  responsible_business_unit varchar [note: 'Organizational unit accountable for the assessment execution and outcomes.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system of record for the assessment data.']
  stakeholder_engagement_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score measuring the level of stakeholder concern for each material topic.']
  stakeholder_groups_consulted varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of stakeholder groups (e.g., investors, employees, NGOs) consulted during the assessment.']
  topic_universe_evaluated varchar [note: 'Full set of ESG topics considered for materiality scoring.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the assessment record.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Status of internal or external verification of the assessment.']
  verified_by varchar [note: 'Name of the individual or entity that performed verification.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version identifier for the assessment record.']

  Note: 'Records of double materiality assessments conducted to identify and prioritize ESG topics that are material to Food Beverage from both impact and financial perspectives. Captures assessment methodology (GRI, ESRS/CSRD double materiality), stakeholder groups consulted, topic universe evaluated, materiality threshold criteria, scoring results, final material topics list, board approval date, and reassessment frequency. Required by GRI Universal Standards 2021 and EU CSRD for determining ESG disclosure scope.'
}

Table workforce.employee {
  employee_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the employee record.']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee ID of the direct manager.']
  network_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary work site or plant where the employee is assigned.']
  badge_number varchar [note: 'Physical access badge identifier assigned to the employee.']
  cba_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the applicable collective bargaining agreement.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Nature of the employment contract.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Cost center to which the employee\'s labor costs are charged.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Employee\'s birth date for age verification and benefits eligibility.']
  eeo_category varchar [note: 'Equal Employment Opportunity classification for reporting.']
  email varchar [note: 'Personal email address of the employee.']
  employee_status varchar [note: 'Current employment status of the employee.']
  employment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the employment relationship.']
  flsa_status varchar [note: 'Fair Labor Standards Act classification for overtime eligibility.']
  food_handler_certification_status varchar [note: 'Status of required food safety handling certification.']
  gmp_training_completion_date date [note: 'Date when Good Manufacturing Practice training was completed.']
  haccp_training_completion_date date [note: 'Date when HACCP training was completed.']
  hire_date date [note: 'Date the employee was initially hired.']
  i9_verification_status varchar [note: 'Status of the employee\'s I-9 employment eligibility verification.']
  job_classification varchar [note: 'Standard classification of the employee\'s role within the organization.']
  job_code varchar [note: 'Internal code representing the specific job title.']
  last_performance_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent performance evaluation.']
  legal_name varchar [note: 'Employee\'s legal full name as on official documents.']
  notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Required notice period for termination in days.']
  ooo_status varchar [note: 'Current out-of-office or leave status.']
  overtime_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates if the employee is eligible for overtime pay.']
  pay_basis varchar [note: 'Basis on which the employee is compensated.']
  performance_rating varchar [note: 'Overall rating from the latest performance review.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Personal contact phone number.']
  preferred_name varchar [note: 'Employee\'s preferred name used for internal communications.']
  probation_period_days varchar [note: 'Length of the probationary period in days.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the employee record was first created.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the employee record.']
  rehire_eligibility boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee is eligible for rehire after termination.']
  renewal_term_months varchar [note: 'Contract renewal term length in months, if applicable.']
  safety_training_completion_date date [note: 'Date of completion for general safety training.']
  shift_schedule varchar [note: 'Assigned work shift schedule (e.g., 1st Shift, 2nd Shift).']
  standard_hours_per_week decimal(5,2) [note: 'Typical weekly work hours for the employee.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date the employee\'s employment ended, if applicable.']
  union_local varchar [note: 'Union local identifier for unionized employees.']
  work_email varchar [note: 'Corporate email address used for business communications.']
  work_phone varchar [note: 'Corporate phone number assigned to the employee.']

  Note: 'Core master record and single source of truth for all Food Beverage workforce identity, employment agreements, and lifecycle history. Covers full-time, part-time, seasonal, and contract workers across manufacturing plants, DCs, corporate offices, and field sales. Stores Workday HCM employee ID, legal name, preferred name, employment type, hire date, termination date, rehire eligibility, job classification, FLSA status, EEO category, I-9 verification status, badge number, work location, cost center assignment, manager, HR business partner, and full employment contract details (contract type, CBA reference, union local, pay basis, standard hours, probation/notice periods, renewal terms). Captures embedded lifecycle event history (hire, transfer, promotion, termination, LOA). SSOT for workforce identity, employment agreements, and lifecycle audit — no separate contract product exists.'
}

Table workforce.position {
  position_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the authorized headcount position.']
  job_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.job_profile. Business justification: Position references a Job Profile; adding position.job_profile_id FK creates parent-child relationship and removes redundant string column.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the department that owns the position.']
  reporting_manager_position_id varchar [note: 'Position ID of the manager to whom this role reports.']
  budgeted_headcount varchar [note: 'Number of employees the organization plans to staff in this position for the budgeting period.']
  compensation_grade varchar [note: 'Internal grade used for salary banding and compensation planning.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Financial cost center to which labor costs for the position are charged.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the position record was first created in the system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the position is retired or no longer authorized (nullable).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the position becomes active for staffing.']
  employee_group varchar [note: 'Group classification such as "Hourly", "Salaried", "Contingent".']
  filled boolean [note: 'True if the authorized headcount has been assigned to an employee.']
  filled_date date [note: 'Date when the position was filled by an employee.']
  food_handler_cert_required boolean [note: 'True if the position requires a valid food‑handler certification.']
  fte_allocation decimal(5,2) [note: 'Proportion of a full‑time workload assigned to the position (e.g., 1.00 for full‑time, 0.50 for half‑time).']
  gmp_zone_required boolean [note: 'True if the role must work in a GMP‑controlled area.']
  is_contract boolean [note: 'True if the role is filled by a contract worker.']
  is_exempt boolean [note: 'True if the position is exempt from overtime regulations.']
  is_temp boolean [note: 'True if the position is intended for temporary staffing.']
  job_family varchar [note: 'Broad classification grouping similar jobs, such as "Manufacturing" or "Quality Assurance".']
  management_level varchar [note: 'Hierarchy level of the position, e.g., "Supervisor", "Manager", "Director".']
  overtime_eligible boolean [note: 'True if employees in this position may accrue overtime.']
  position_category varchar [note: 'High‑level functional category of the position.']
  position_code varchar [note: 'External business identifier for the position, used in HR and budgeting systems.']
  position_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of duties, responsibilities, and scope of the role.']
  position_level varchar [note: 'Indicates seniority or pay scale level, e.g., "Entry", "Mid", "Senior".']
  position_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the position.']
  position_type varchar [note: 'Classification of employment relationship for the position.']
  remote_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the position can be performed remotely.']
  required_certifications varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of mandatory certifications (e.g., "GMP, HACCP").']
  required_training_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum training hours a new hire must complete before assuming the role.']
  safety_sensitive boolean [note: 'Indicates if the role is classified as safety‑sensitive under OSHA regulations.']
  salary_range_max decimal(10,2) [note: 'Upper bound of the salary band for the position.']
  salary_range_min decimal(10,2) [note: 'Lower bound of the salary band for the position.']
  shift_type varchar [note: 'Scheduled work shift pattern for the position.']
  title varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the role, e.g., "Production Line Supervisor".']
  travel_required boolean [note: 'True if any travel is required for the position.']
  travel_required_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Expected proportion of time spent traveling for the role.']
  union_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the position is eligible for union representation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the position record.']
  vacancy_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the position is currently vacant.']
  work_location varchar [note: 'Physical site or plant where the position is based, identified by plant code or address.']

  Note: 'Authorized headcount positions within the organizational structure, representing approved roles that may or may not be filled. Captures position code, job profile, job family, management level, FTE allocation, budgeted headcount, work location, shift type (day/night/rotating), union eligibility, GMP zone requirement, food handler certification requirement, and whether the position is safety-sensitive under OSHA. Sourced from Workday HCM Position Management.'
}

Table workforce.org_unit {
  org_unit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the organizational unit.']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the manager responsible for the unit.']
  network_node_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the physical location (plant, DC, office) associated with the unit.']
  parent_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the immediate parent org unit in the hierarchy.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval state of the headcount plan for the unit.']
  attrition_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Projected attrition percentage for the unit during the planning horizon.']
  business_unit varchar [note: 'Higher‑level business unit to which the org unit belongs.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Finance cost‑center code linked to the org unit.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the org unit record was first created.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the org unit ceased to be effective (null if still active).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the org unit became effective.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'Primary GL account associated with the org unit for budgeting.']
  headcount_actual_fte decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual FTE count recorded for the fiscal period.']
  headcount_planned_fte decimal(10,2) [note: 'Approved planned FTE count for the fiscal period.']
  headcount_target_fte decimal(10,2) [note: 'Target FTE headcount set for strategic planning.']
  last_headcount_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent headcount planning review for the unit.']
  open_requisitions varchar [note: 'Number of open hiring requisitions for the unit.']
  org_unit_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the org unit (e.g., DEPT001, PLANT12).']
  org_unit_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the unit\'s purpose and scope.']
  org_unit_level varchar [note: 'Depth level of the unit within the org hierarchy (root = 0).']
  org_unit_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the organizational unit.']
  org_unit_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the org unit.']
  org_unit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the org unit within the hierarchy.']
  plan_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the headcount planning scenario.']
  region varchar [note: 'Geographic region (e.g., North America, EMEA) where the unit operates.']
  seasonal_headcount_addition varchar [note: 'Additional headcount planned for seasonal peaks (e.g., holiday, harvest).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the org unit record.']

  Note: 'Organizational hierarchy nodes and headcount planning records representing departments, cost centers, business units, plants, and functional groups within Food Beverage. Captures org unit code, name, type (department, division, plant, DC, region), parent org unit, effective date, org level, responsible manager, associated GL cost center, active status, and headcount planning details by fiscal period (approved FTE count, actual FTE count, open requisitions, attrition assumptions, seasonal headcount additions for harvest/holiday peaks, plan version, and approval status). Supports SAP CO cost center hierarchy, Workday HCM supervisory organization structure, and workforce planning integration.'
}

Table workforce.job_profile {
  job_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the job profile record.']
  compensation_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for the salary amounts.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the job profile record was first created.']
  education_requirements varchar [note: 'Minimum education level or specific degrees/certifications needed.']
  eeo_category varchar [note: 'EEO reporting category for the role.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the job profile is retired or superseded; null if still active.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the job profile becomes active.']
  experience_years_required varchar [note: 'Minimum years of relevant experience required.']
  flsa_classification varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the role is exempt or non‑exempt under FLSA.']
  gmp_training_required boolean [note: 'Indicates if GMP/HACCP training is mandatory for the role.']
  hazard_pay_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates if the role qualifies for hazard pay.']
  health_and_safety_cert_required boolean [note: 'Indicates if a health and safety certification is mandatory.']
  job_family varchar [note: 'Broad functional grouping of the role (e.g., Production, Quality, Finance).']
  job_level varchar [note: 'Numerical level indicating seniority within the job family.']
  job_profile_code varchar [note: 'Standardized alphanumeric code used to reference the job profile across systems.']
  job_profile_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the responsibilities and scope of the role.']
  job_profile_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the job profile.']
  job_summary varchar [note: 'Brief summary of the role for use in listings and internal portals.']
  overtime_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates if the role is eligible for overtime pay.']
  pay_grade varchar [note: 'Compensation grade associated with the job profile.']
  required_certifications varchar [note: 'Pipe‑separated list of mandatory certifications (e.g., food_handler|forklift|haccp).']
  role_type varchar [note: 'Specifies whether the role is production‑floor, office, or both.']
  salary_max decimal(10,2) [note: 'Upper bound of the salary range for the role.']
  salary_min decimal(10,2) [note: 'Lower bound of the salary range for the role.']
  shift_type varchar [note: 'Typical shift pattern for the role.']
  skill_requirements varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of core skills required for the role.']
  title varchar [note: 'Official title of the role as displayed to employees and managers.']
  travel_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the role requires regular travel.']
  union_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the labor union representing the role, if applicable.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the job profile record.']

  Note: 'Standardized job definitions used across Food Beverage to classify roles by function, skill requirements, and compensation grade. Includes job profile code, job title, job family, job level, FLSA classification (exempt/non-exempt), EEO-1 category, pay grade range, required certifications (food handler, forklift, HACCP), GMP training requirement flag, union code, and whether the role is production-floor vs. office. Sourced from Workday HCM Job Profile catalog.'
}

Table workforce.compensation_plan {
  compensation_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the compensation plan.']
  job_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.job_profile. Business justification: Compensation Plan is defined per Job Profile; adding compensation_plan.job_profile_id FK links plan to its job definition.']
  applicable_region varchar [note: 'ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code where the plan is valid; null if global.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person who approved the compensation plan.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the compensation plan was approved.']
  bonus_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Fixed monetary bonus amount awarded under the plan.']
  bonus_eligibility boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an employee qualifies for the bonus component.']
  commission_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Commission percentage applied to sales revenue for eligible employees.']
  compensation_plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the plan.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data classification indicating the sensitivity of the plan details.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the compensation plan record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three‑letter currency code for monetary amounts in the plan.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the compensation plan expires or is superseded; null for open‑ended plans.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the compensation plan becomes effective.']
  eligibility_criteria varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the business rules that determine employee eligibility for the plan.']
  eligibility_rule varchar [note: 'Standardized rule code defining the employment type required for eligibility.']
  grade_max varchar [note: 'Highest pay grade that can be assigned under this plan.']
  grade_midpoint varchar [note: 'Midpoint pay grade for the plan, often used for market benchmarking.']
  grade_min varchar [note: 'Lowest pay grade that can be assigned under this plan.']
  incentive_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Rate used to calculate performance‑based incentives, expressed as a decimal multiplier.']
  is_global boolean [note: 'True if the plan applies across all legal entities and regions.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent formal review of the compensation plan.']
  last_reviewed_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person who performed the last plan review.']
  merit_increase_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard merit increase percentage applied to eligible employees each review cycle.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the compensation plan.']
  overtime_multiplier decimal(5,2) [note: 'Multiplier applied to base hourly rate for overtime hours.']
  pay_frequency varchar [note: 'How often compensation under this plan is paid.']
  plan_category varchar [note: 'High‑level grouping of the plan for reporting purposes.']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'External business identifier for the compensation plan, used in payroll and HR systems.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the compensation plan.']
  plan_owner varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the HR or finance owner responsible for the plan.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Category of the plan indicating its primary purpose (e.g., base salary, merit increase, incentive, commission, bonus).']
  plan_version varchar [note: 'Incremental version number of the plan for change management.']
  probation_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days an employee must complete before becoming eligible for the plan.']
  shift_differential_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the plan includes a shift differential component.']
  shift_differential_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Percentage rate applied to base pay for eligible shift differentials (e.g., night, weekend).']
  target_achievement_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the target amount actually achieved, used for payout calculations.']
  target_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary target that must be achieved to earn incentive or bonus payouts.']
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether compensation under this plan is exempt from payroll taxes.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the compensation plan record.']
  vesting_schedule varchar [note: 'Description of the vesting timeline for deferred compensation components.']

  Note: 'Compensation structures and pay programs applicable to Food Beverage workforce segments, including base pay grades, merit increase matrices, shift differential rates (night, weekend, holiday), production incentive plans, sales commission structures, and executive compensation tiers. Captures plan code, plan type, effective date, pay frequency, currency, grade minimum/midpoint/maximum, and eligibility criteria. Sourced from Workday HCM Compensation.'
}

Table workforce.payroll_record {
  payroll_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the payroll record.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.org_unit. Business justification: Payroll records store cost_center_code; linking to org unit aligns payroll cost center with organizational hierarchy.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the work location (plant, warehouse, etc.) where the employee is assigned.']
  primary_payroll_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee receiving the payroll.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the payroll record.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the payroll record was approved.']
  bank_account_number varchar [note: 'Employee\'s bank account number for direct deposit.']
  bank_routing_number varchar [note: 'Routing number of the employee\'s financial institution.']
  benefit_deductions_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total amount deducted for employee benefits (e.g., health, retirement).']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for the payroll amounts.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'Organizational department responsible for the employee.']
  employee_ssn varchar [note: 'US Social Security Number for the employee, required for tax reporting.']
  employee_tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax ID (e.g., SSN) used for payroll tax reporting.']
  error_code varchar [note: 'System error code if the payroll run failed.']
  error_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the payroll processing error.']
  federal_tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Amount withheld for federal income tax.']
  garnishment_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Amount deducted for court-ordered garnishments.']
  gross_pay_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total earnings before any deductions or taxes.']
  is_manual_adjustment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the payroll record includes a manual adjustment.']
  job_code varchar [note: 'Job classification code for the employee\'s role.']
  local_tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Amount withheld for local (city/county) taxes.']
  net_pay_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final amount paid to the employee after all deductions.']
  overtime_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total overtime hours worked in the pay period, subject to FLSA rules.']
  overtime_rate decimal(8,4) [note: 'Compensation rate applied to overtime hours.']
  pay_method varchar [note: 'Method used to deliver net pay to the employee.']
  pay_period_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date of the payroll period.']
  pay_period_start_date date [note: 'First calendar date of the payroll period.']
  payroll_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes entered by payroll administrators.']
  payroll_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the payroll run, used for tracking and reconciliation.']
  payroll_record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payroll record.']
  payroll_run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the payroll was processed.']
  payroll_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the payroll run (e.g., regular salary, bonus, commission).']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payroll record was first created in the system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the payroll record.']
  regular_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total regular (non-overtime) hours worked in the pay period.']
  regular_rate decimal(8,4) [note: 'Base hourly compensation rate for regular hours.']
  shift_differential_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Hours worked under a shift differential (e.g., night shift) that attract a premium rate.']
  shift_differential_rate decimal(8,4) [note: 'Premium rate applied to shift differential hours.']
  state_tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Amount withheld for state income tax.']
  tax_withholdings_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Aggregate of all tax withholdings (federal, state, local).']

  Note: 'Periodic payroll processing records capturing gross-to-net pay calculations for each employee pay period at Food Beverage. Includes pay period start/end dates, regular hours, overtime hours (FLSA), shift differential hours, gross pay, federal/state/local tax withholdings, FICA, benefit deductions, garnishments, net pay, pay method (direct deposit/check), and payroll run status. Integrates Workday HCM Payroll with SAP FI for COGS labor allocation.'
}

Table workforce.total_rewards {
  total_rewards_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for total_rewards']
  benefit_plan_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the benefit plan.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee associated with the enrollment.']
  open_enrollment_event_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the open enrollment window.']
  beneficiary_name varchar [note: 'Name of the designated beneficiary.']
  beneficiary_relationship varchar [note: 'Relationship of beneficiary to employee.']
  contribution_frequency varchar [note: 'How often contributions are made.']
  contribution_type varchar [note: 'Tax treatment of the contribution.']
  coverage_tier varchar [note: 'Level of coverage selected.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the enrollment record was created.']
  dependent_count varchar [note: 'Number of dependents covered under the plan.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date the benefits become effective.']
  eligibility_status varchar [note: 'Eligibility of employee for the benefit.']
  employee_address varchar [note: 'Home address of the employee.']
  employee_age varchar [note: 'Age of the employee at enrollment.']
  employee_contribution_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Amount contributed by the employee per pay period.']
  employer_contribution_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Amount contributed by the employer per pay period.']
  employer_match_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of employee contribution matched by employer.']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'Date the employee submitted the enrollment.']
  enrollment_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the enrollment transaction.']
  enrollment_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the enrollment entry.']
  enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the enrollment.']
  is_401k_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates if employee is eligible for 401k.']
  is_eap_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates if employee is eligible for Employee Assistance Program.']
  is_fsa_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates if employee is eligible for FSA.']
  is_hsa_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates if employee is eligible for HSA.']
  is_life_insurance_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates if employee is eligible for life insurance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes regarding the enrollment.']
  payroll_deduction_code varchar [note: 'Code used in payroll to deduct contributions.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Category of the benefit plan. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: medical|dental|vision|401k|fsa|hsa|life_insurance|eap — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  plan_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year of the benefit plan.']
  tax_status varchar [note: 'Employee\'s tax filing status for benefit tax calculations.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date the enrollment was terminated, if applicable.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the enrollment record.']
  vesting_schedule varchar [note: 'Vesting schedule for employer contributions.']

  Note: 'Employee benefit plan enrollment records tracking active and historical benefit elections at Food Beverage. Captures benefit plan type (medical, dental, vision, 401k, FSA, HSA, life insurance, EAP), coverage tier (employee only, employee+spouse, family), enrollment date, effective date, termination date, employee contribution amount, employer contribution amount, and open enrollment event reference. Sourced from Workday HCM Benefits.'
}

Table workforce.time_entry {
  time_entry_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the time entry record.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.asset. Business justification: Labor tracking per asset is needed for cost allocation, overtime analysis, and regulatory reporting on equipment usage.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.org_unit. Business justification: Time entry records capture cost_center_code redundantly; linking to org unit centralizes cost center information.']
  primary_time_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who performed the work.']
  shift_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the scheduled shift template applied to this time entry.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the time entry (e.g., pending manager review, approved, or rejected).']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the time entry was approved.']
  clock_in_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the employee clocked in.']
  clock_out_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the employee clocked out.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the time entry record was first created in the system.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'Organizational department responsible for the work.']
  double_time_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of double‑time hours worked, usually for holidays or special circumstances.']
  hours_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the hours worked (e.g., regular, overtime, holiday, PTO, FMLA).']
  job_code varchar [note: 'Job or position code defining the employee\'s role for the shift.']
  labor_cost_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary value of labor incurred for the recorded hours before any adjustments.']
  labor_cost_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the labor cost amount (e.g., USD, EUR).']
  meal_break_minutes varchar [note: 'Length of the unpaid or paid meal break taken during the shift, in minutes.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for any additional comments or explanations related to the time entry.']
  overtime_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of overtime hours worked, typically paid at a premium rate.']
  overtime_multiplier decimal(3,2) [note: 'Factor applied to the base hourly rate for overtime hours (e.g., 1.5).']
  pto_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Hours recorded as paid time off (vacation, personal leave).']
  regular_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of regular (non‑overtime) hours worked in the shift.']
  shift_date date [note: 'Calendar date of the work shift.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the time entry record.']
  work_location_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the plant, distribution center, or office where the work was performed.']

  Note: 'Individual time and attendance records for Food Beverage hourly and salaried non-exempt employees, capturing clock-in/clock-out timestamps, shift date, work location (plant, DC, office), department, job code, hours type (regular, overtime, double-time, holiday, PTO, FMLA), meal break duration, and approval status. Critical for FLSA compliance, COGS labor costing in SAP CO, and production shift OEE calculations in Aveva MES.'
}

Table workforce.production_shift {
  production_shift_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the production shift record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee supervising the shift.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant or distribution center where the shift occurs.']
  production_line_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the production line assigned to the shift.']
  actual_end timestamp [note: 'Recorded actual end timestamp when workers completed the shift.']
  actual_headcount varchar [note: 'Number of workers who actually worked the shift.']
  actual_start timestamp [note: 'Recorded actual start timestamp when workers began the shift.']
  labor_cost_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total labor cost incurred for the shift (currency‑agnostic).']
  labor_cost_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the labor cost (e.g., USD, EUR).']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code for the specific site or floor within the plant.']
  overtime_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether overtime was incurred during the shift.']
  overtime_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total overtime hours logged for the shift.']
  planned_headcount varchar [note: 'Number of workers scheduled for the shift.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the shift record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the shift record.']
  scheduled_end timestamp [note: 'Planned end timestamp for the shift.']
  scheduled_start timestamp [note: 'Planned start timestamp for the shift.']
  shift_code varchar [note: 'Human‑readable code identifying the shift (e.g., SFT‑2023‑001).']
  shift_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for any additional information or observations about the shift.']
  shift_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the shift.']
  shift_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the shift schedule pattern.']
  swap_approved_flag boolean [note: 'True if a shift‑swap request was approved.']
  swap_requested_flag boolean [note: 'True if a shift‑swap request was submitted for this shift.']

  Note: 'Scheduled and actual production shift records for manufacturing plant and DC floor workers at Food Beverage, including per-employee crew assignment details. Captures shift code, shift type (day/afternoon/night/rotating), plant or DC location, production line assignment, scheduled start/end time, actual start/end time, shift supervisor, headcount planned vs. actual, shift status, and shift notes. Per-employee assignments include: role on shift (operator, line lead, QA technician, sanitation), assigned line, scheduled vs. actual hours, attendance status, overtime flags, and swap/coverage records. Links workforce scheduling to Aveva MES production orders and OEE tracking. Enables labor scheduling optimization, OTIF workforce coverage analysis, and shift-swap management.'
}

Table workforce.leave_request {
  leave_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each leave request record.']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manager or HR representative who approved or rejected the request.']
  primary_leave_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee submitting the leave request.']
  accrual_balance_at_request decimal(10,2) [note: 'Employee\'s leave balance (in days) at the time the request was submitted.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the leave request.']
  attachment_flag boolean [note: 'True if supporting documents (e.g., medical certificates) are attached to the request.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional comments or notes added by the employee or approver.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the leave request record was first created in the system.']
  end_date date [note: 'Last calendar day the leave is scheduled to end.']
  fmla_eligible boolean [note: 'True if the employee meets eligibility criteria for Family and Medical Leave Act coverage.']
  intermittent_flag boolean [note: 'True if the leave is to be taken on an intermittent (non‑consecutive) basis.']
  leave_balance_remaining decimal(10,2) [note: 'Projected leave balance remaining after the requested leave is deducted.']
  leave_request_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle status of the request as it moves through the approval process.']
  leave_type varchar [note: 'Category of leave being requested (e.g., FMLA, ADA accommodation, personal, military, parental, other).']
  medical_certification_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for any medical documentation supporting the leave request.']
  paid_leave_flag boolean [note: 'True if the requested leave is paid under the employee\'s leave accrual plan.']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation provided by the employee for the leave request.']
  request_date date [note: 'Date the employee submitted the leave request.']
  request_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable business identifier for the leave request, often used in communications and reporting.']
  return_to_work_date date [note: 'Date the employee is expected to resume regular duties after the leave period.']
  start_date date [note: 'First calendar day the leave is scheduled to begin.']
  total_leave_days decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total number of leave days (or equivalent hours) requested, including fractions.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the leave request record.']

  Note: 'Employee leave of absence requests and approvals at Food Beverage, covering FMLA, ADA accommodations, personal leave, military leave (USERRA), parental leave, bereavement, and jury duty. Captures leave type, request date, start date, end date, intermittent leave flag, FMLA eligibility determination, approval status, approver, return-to-work date, and any medical certification reference. Supports OSHA and DOL compliance reporting.'
}

Table workforce.training_record {
  training_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for training_record']
  primary_training_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee who completed the training or holds the certification.']
  training_course_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.training_course']
  assessment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score achieved on the training assessment (e.g., 85.5).']
  certification_number varchar [note: 'Unique number assigned by the issuing body to the certification.']
  completion_date date [note: 'Date the employee completed the training or passed the assessment.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the training satisfies the specific regulatory requirement (True = compliant).']
  credit_hours decimal(4,2) [note: 'Number of credit hours or units awarded for completing the training.']
  delivery_method varchar [note: 'Mode used to deliver the training (e.g., classroom, e‑learning, on‑the‑job).']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date the certification or training expires and must be renewed.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date the certification or training was officially issued.']
  issuing_body varchar [note: 'Organization that issued the certification or conducted the training (e.g., FDA, SQF Institute, State Health Dept.).']
  location varchar [note: 'Physical or virtual location where the training was delivered (e.g., Plant A, Online Portal).']
  next_due_date date [note: 'Calculated date when the next renewal or recertification is required.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations about the training event.']
  pass_fail_status varchar [note: 'Result of the assessment indicating whether the employee passed or failed.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the training record was first created in the system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the training record.']
  training_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the training record (e.g., active, expired).']
  training_type varchar [note: 'Category of training or certification (e.g., GMP, HACCP, ServSafe).']

  Note: 'Single source of truth for ALL training completions, certifications, and food safety credentials for Food Beverage plant, DC, QA, and foodservice employees. Covers GMP/HACCP training events AND all food safety certifications including ServSafe, PCQI (Preventive Controls Qualified Individual under FSMA), SQF Practitioner, state food handler cards, allergen awareness, and HACCP certification. Captures training course or certification type, issuing body, completion/issue date, expiration date, certification number, delivery method (classroom, e-learning, OJT), assessment score, pass/fail status, trainer or LMS reference, and next due date. No separate food handler certification product exists — all certification data is consolidated here. Sourced from Veeva Vault LMS and Workday HCM Learning. Critical for FDA FSMA compliance, GFSI audit readiness, OSHA training requirements, and state food handler card renewals.'
}

Table workforce.training_course {
  training_course_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the training course.']
  applicable_job_profiles varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of job profile identifiers to which the course applies.']
  certification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether successful completion grants a formal certification.']
  cost_usd decimal(10,2) [note: 'Monetary cost associated with delivering the course (e.g., external vendor fees).']
  course_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the course by functional area (e.g., Safety, Leadership).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the course record was first created in the system.']
  credit_hours decimal(4,2) [note: 'Number of credit hours awarded upon successful completion.']
  delivery_method varchar [note: 'Mode through which the training is delivered to employees.']
  duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total instructional time of the course expressed in minutes.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which the course is no longer offered (nullable).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the course becomes available for enrollment.']
  instructor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary instructor or facilitator for the course.']
  language varchar [note: 'Primary language in which the course content is delivered.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the course content was last reviewed for relevance and compliance.']
  mandatory_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether completion of the course is mandatory for the assigned employee group.']
  passing_score_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum score percentage required for a learner to pass the course.']
  recertification_interval_months varchar [note: 'Number of months after which the course must be retaken to maintain certification.']
  regulatory_requirement_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the regulatory framework(s) the course satisfies (e.g., FSMA, OSHA, GFSI).']
  review_cycle_months varchar [note: 'Planned interval in months between mandatory content reviews.']
  title varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the training course.']
  training_course_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or catalog code assigned to the course (e.g., GMP001).']
  training_course_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative of the course content, objectives, and scope.']
  training_course_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the course.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the course record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version identifier for the course definition.']

  Note: 'Master catalog of all training courses available to Food Beverage employees, including GMP, HACCP, food safety, allergen awareness, forklift certification, OSHA 10/30, leadership development, and compliance training. Captures course code, title, description, course type, delivery method, duration, passing score threshold, recertification interval (months), mandatory flag, applicable job profiles, and regulatory requirement reference (FSMA, OSHA, GFSI). Managed in Veeva Vault and Workday HCM Learning.'
}

  review_method varchar [note: 'Method used to conduct the review.']
  review_notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes captured during the review.']
  review_start_date date [note: 'Date when the review period started.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow status of the review.']
  review_type varchar [note: 'Type of performance review (annual, mid-year, probationary, performance improvement plan).']

  Note: 'Annual and mid-year performance review records for Food Beverage employees, capturing review cycle, review type (annual, mid-year, probationary, PIP), overall rating, individual goal ratings, competency ratings, manager comments, employee self-assessment, calibration status, and final approved rating. Sourced from Workday HCM Performance. Feeds merit increase and promotion decisions in compensation planning.'
}

  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the succession plan for change tracking.']

  Note: 'Succession planning records identifying ready-now and future successors for critical positions at Food Beverage, including plant manager, R&D director, supply chain VP, and other key roles. Captures target position, incumbent employee, successor candidate, readiness level (ready now, 1-2 years, 3-5 years), development actions, risk of loss rating, and plan review date. Sourced from Workday HCM Succession.'
}

Table workforce.osha_incident {
  osha_incident_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the OSHA incident record.']
  equipment_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of equipment involved, if any.']
  primary_osha_investigator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who performed the incident investigation.']
  body_part varchar [note: 'Body part(s) impacted by the injury. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hand|arm|leg|head|torso|face|eye|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  case_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the incident case within the investigation workflow.']
  corrective_action varchar [note: 'Planned or implemented actions to prevent recurrence.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the incident record was first created in the system.']
  days_away varchar [note: 'Number of workdays the employee was unable to work due to the incident.']
  department varchar [note: 'Business department or functional area of the employee at time of incident.']
  equipment_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the equipment involved.']
  exposure_level varchar [note: 'Level of employee exposure to the hazard.']
  hazard_type varchar [note: 'Primary hazard category associated with the incident.']
  incident_datetime timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the incident occurred.']
  incident_number varchar [note: 'Business-assigned alphanumeric identifier for the incident, used for tracking and reporting.']
  incident_type varchar [note: 'Category of the incident as defined by OSHA reporting requirements.']
  injury_type varchar [note: 'Specific type of injury sustained, if applicable.']
  investigation_complete boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the incident investigation has been completed.']
  location varchar [note: 'Physical location of the incident (plant, production line, distribution center zone, etc.).']
  lost_time_indicator boolean [note: 'True if days_away > 0, indicating lost work time.']
  medical_provider varchar [note: 'Name of the medical provider or facility that treated the employee.']
  medical_treatment_provided boolean [note: 'Indicates whether medical treatment was administered on-site or off-site.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for any supplemental information or observations.']
  recordable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the incident meets OSHA recordable criteria (true) or not (false).']
  reporting_date date [note: 'Date the incident was formally reported to OSHA or internal safety team.']
  restricted_duty_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the employee performed restricted or light duty work.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the root cause (e.g., equipment, human error, process).']
  root_cause_detail varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the underlying cause of the incident.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Severity rating of the incident based on impact and risk.']
  shift varchar [note: 'Work shift during which the incident occurred.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the incident record.']

  Note: 'OSHA recordable and first-aid incident records for Food Beverage manufacturing plants, DCs, and field operations. Captures incident date/time, location (plant, line, DC zone), incident type (injury, illness, near-miss, property damage), injury type, body part affected, OSHA recordability determination (300 log entry), days away from work, restricted duty days, root cause classification, corrective actions, and case status. Mandatory for OSHA 300/300A annual reporting and workers compensation.'
}

Table workforce.labor_grievance {

  grievance_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the grievance by the labor relations system.']
  grievance_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the grievance record.']
  grievance_step varchar [note: 'Current step in the grievance resolution process.']
  grievance_type varchar [note: 'Category of the grievance as defined in the collective bargaining agreement.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text notes captured by labor relations staff.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'Final outcome of the grievance after all steps.']
  plant_location_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant or distribution center where the grievance originated.']
