  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the grievance record.']

  Note: 'Union labor grievance and arbitration case records for Food Beverage unionized manufacturing and DC facilities. Captures grievance number, filing date, grievance type (discipline, scheduling, pay, safety, seniority), CBA article cited, plant or DC location, union local, grievance step (Step 1 through arbitration), resolution date, resolution outcome, and financial settlement amount if applicable. Supports labor relations management and CBA compliance tracking.'
}

Table workforce.headcount_plan {
  headcount_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the headcount planning record.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.org_unit']
  plan_owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the person responsible for the plan.']
  actual_fte decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual FTE count recorded during the planning period.']
  approved_fte decimal(10,2) [note: 'Number of FTEs approved in the plan for the org unit and job family.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the plan was approved by the governing authority.']
  attrition_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Assumed attrition percentage (e.g., 0.0500 for 5%).']
  budget_fte decimal(10,2) [note: 'FTE amount budgeted for the planning period, used for cost allocation.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Financial cost‑center linked to the headcount plan for budgeting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the headcount plan record was created.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the headcount plan becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the headcount plan expires or is superseded; null if open‑ended.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Four‑digit fiscal year to which the plan applies.']
  headcount_plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the headcount plan.']
  headcount_target_fte decimal(10,2) [note: 'Target FTE count the organization aims to achieve, may differ from approved.']
  job_family_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the job family or role category for the headcount plan.']
  job_family_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the job family.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or rationale associated with the plan.']
  open_requisitions varchar [note: 'Number of open hiring requisitions associated with the plan.']
  plan_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier for the headcount plan, used in reporting and approvals.']
  plan_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent formal review of the headcount plan.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the plan indicating its purpose, such as annual, seasonal, or projected.']
  plan_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the plan (e.g., v1.0, v2.1).']
  planning_period varchar [note: 'Period within the fiscal year covered by the plan (quarter or full year).']
  seasonal_headcount_addition varchar [note: 'Planned additional headcount for seasonal peaks (e.g., holiday, harvest).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that supplied the plan data (e.g., Workday HCM).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the headcount plan.']

  Note: 'Approved headcount planning records by org unit, job family, and fiscal period for Food Beverage. Captures plan version, fiscal year, planning period, org unit, job family, approved FTE count, actual FTE count, open requisitions, attrition assumption, seasonal headcount additions (harvest season, holiday peak), and plan approval status. Integrates with SAP CO cost center budgeting and Workday HCM workforce planning.'
}

Table workforce.requisition {
  requisition_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the job requisition record.']
  network_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical location (plant, warehouse, office, etc.) for the role.']
  requisition_approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the executive or finance officer who approved the requisition.']
  requisition_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manager responsible for filling the position.']
  requisition_recruiter_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the recruiter or recruiting team handling the requisition.']
  approved_fte varchar [note: 'Number of FTEs approved by finance or leadership.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the requisition received formal approval.']
  background_check_status varchar [note: 'Progress status of the required background verification.']
  budgeted_salary_max decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum salary range approved for the position.']
  budgeted_salary_min decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum salary range approved for the position.']
  closing_date date [note: 'Date when the requisition was closed to new applications.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the requisition record was first created in the system.']
  department varchar [note: 'Organizational department or business unit where the position resides.']
  diversity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the requisition is part of a diversity hiring initiative.']
  drug_screen_status varchar [note: 'Status of the drug screening process for food‑safety‑sensitive roles.']
  employment_type varchar [note: 'Nature of the employment relationship.']
  external_candidate_allowed boolean [note: 'True if external candidates may apply for the position.']
  internal_candidate_allowed boolean [note: 'True if internal employees may apply for the position.']
  interview_stage varchar [note: 'Current stage of the interview process for the requisition. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: screen|phone|onsite|final|offer|hired|rejected — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  job_family varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the role (e.g., Production, Quality, Finance).']
  job_level varchar [note: 'Organizational level of the role (e.g., IC1, M2).']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Category of the work location for the role.']
  offer_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the employment offer presented to the candidate.']
  offer_status varchar [note: 'State of the employment offer for the position.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Date when the requisition was first posted to candidates.']
  posting_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the job posting on internal/external channels.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the hiring request.']
  reason varchar [note: 'Business driver for the hiring request.']
  required_certifications varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of mandatory certifications (e.g., Food Handler, GMP).']
  required_education varchar [note: 'Minimum education level required for the position.']
  required_experience_years varchar [note: 'Minimum years of relevant experience needed for the role.']
  requisition_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative of duties, responsibilities, and expectations for the role.']
  requisition_number varchar [note: 'Business-visible identifier assigned to the requisition (e.g., RQ-2024-001).']
  requisition_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the requisition.']
  requisition_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the hiring need (e.g., new hire, replacement, temporary).']
  shift_schedule varchar [note: 'Typical shift pattern required (e.g., day, night, flex).']
  source_channel varchar [note: 'Primary channel through which candidates are sourced.']
  target_fte varchar [note: 'Number of full-time equivalents requested for the role.']
  target_start_date date [note: 'Planned date for the new hire to begin work.']
  time_to_fill_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days from opening to filling the requisition.']
  title varchar [note: 'Official title of the position being requested.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the requisition record.']

  Note: 'Job requisition and full candidate pipeline records for approved headcount at Food Beverage. Captures requisition details (requisition number, position, job profile, hiring manager, recruiter, target start date, requisition type, sourcing channels, posting status) and all associated candidate applications (applicant name, source channel, application date, application stage, interview scores, background check status, drug screen status for food safety roles, offer details, disposition reason, hire decision). Incorporates workforce planning context including approved FTE targets, seasonal headcount needs, and time-to-fill metrics. SSOT for all recruiting activity from requisition opening through candidate hire/rejection. Sourced from Workday HCM Recruiting.'
}

  related_policy_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the related policy document.']
  reviewer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who performed the latest review.']
  superseded_review_document_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on review_document (superseded_review_document_id)']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the document was approved.']
  approved_by_name varchar [note: 'Name of employee who approved the document.']
  audit_trail varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the audit trail log associated with this document.']
  author_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the document author.']
  business_unit varchar [note: 'Business unit owning the document.']

  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Confidentiality classification of the document.']

  document_url varchar [note: 'Link to the stored location of the document.']

  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the document expires or is superseded.']

  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates if the document is mandatory for compliance.']
  language varchar [note: 'Language of the document content.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent review of the document.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the document record.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next review.']
  retention_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the document must be disposed per retention policy.']

  review_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent review.']
  reviewer_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the reviewer.']
  revision_history varchar [note: 'Summary of revisions made to the document.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the document.']
  tags varchar [note: 'Comma-separated tags for categorization.']
  title varchar [note: 'Title of the review document.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the document.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for review_document. Referenced by review_document_id.'
}

Table workforce.shift_schedule {
  shift_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for shift_schedule']

  certification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether employees must hold a food‑handler or GMP/HACCP certification to work this shift.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the shift schedule record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed free‑text description of the schedule purpose and usage.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the shift schedule becomes active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the shift schedule is retired or superseded (null if open‑ended).']
  labor_rule_code varchar [note: 'Reference code to the labor rule or collective bargaining agreement governing this shift.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle state of the schedule definition.']
  max_consecutive_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of consecutive days an employee may be assigned this shift before a mandatory rest period.']
  overtime_allowed boolean [note: 'True if overtime may be scheduled for this shift; false otherwise.']
  schedule_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code used to reference the schedule in operational systems.']
  schedule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the shift schedule (e.g., \"Morning Production Shift\").']
  schedule_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the schedule pattern (regular, flex, on‑call, seasonal).']
  shift_duration_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated duration of the shift in decimal hours.']
  shift_end_time varchar [note: 'Planned end time of the shift (HH:mm, 24‑hour clock).']
  shift_group varchar [note: 'Logical grouping of shifts (e.g., morning, afternoon, night, graveyard).']
  shift_start_time varchar [note: 'Planned start time of the shift (HH:mm, 24‑hour clock).']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the schedule.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the shift schedule record.']
  weekly_pattern varchar [note: 'Pattern of days the shift recurs within a week (e.g., \"Mon‑Wed‑Fri\").']

  Note: 'Master reference table for shift_schedule. Referenced by shift_schedule_id.'
}

Table workforce.open_enrollment_event {
  open_enrollment_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for open_enrollment_event']

  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the enrollment event becomes legally effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the enrollment event expires; null if open‑ended.']

  enrollment_closed_flag boolean [note: 'True if the enrollment window has ended or the event is cancelled.']
  enrollment_end_date date [note: 'Final date for employees to submit enrollment selections.']

  event_type varchar [note: 'Category of the enrollment event indicating the type of benefits or programs offered.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments related to the enrollment event.']
  participant_actual_count varchar [note: 'Actual number of employees who completed enrollment.']
  participant_estimated_count varchar [note: 'Projected number of employees expected to enroll.']
  regulatory_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the event triggers mandatory regulatory reporting.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the enrollment event.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system process that last updated the record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the enrollment event record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system process that created the record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for open_enrollment_event. Referenced by open_enrollment_event_id.'
}
