  Note: 'Master reference table for policy. Referenced by related_policy_id.'
}

Table maintenance.asset {
  asset_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each maintainable asset.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Asset Ownership Register requires linking each asset to the owning customer account for warranty and service SLA tracking.']
  asset_group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.asset_group. Business justification: Assets are organized into groups; adding asset_group_id links each asset to its group.']
  asset_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.asset_hierarchy. Business justification: Each asset belongs to a hierarchy node; adding asset_hierarchy_id creates the required parent relationship.']
  assigned_responsible_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Asset Maintenance Responsibility Report requires linking each asset to the employee accountable for its upkeep, enabling compliance tracking and cost allocation.']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: REQUIRED: Asset‑to‑Brand Production Traceability report needs brand_id on asset to link equipment producing a specific brand for recall and compliance.']
  center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.distribution_center. Business justification: Regulatory equipment location reporting requires each asset to be tied to its distribution center for maintenance scheduling and compliance.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Asset accounting needs company code for ledger posting and statutory financial statements.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Required for capital asset depreciation accounting; assets are charged to a cost center for financial reporting.']
  establishment_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.establishment_registration. Business justification: Audit: Asset compliance tracking requires linking each asset to its facility\'s registration for regulatory audit and reporting.']
  network_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.network_node. Business justification: Required for Maintenance Scheduling report: links each asset to its network node location to calculate OTIF and logistics impact.']
  order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.order. Business justification: Links installed equipment to the originating sales order, supporting traceability for warranty claims and post‑sale service.']
  parent_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a higher‑level asset for hierarchical equipment (e.g., a line containing multiple machines).']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the plant/facility where the asset resides.']
  production_line_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the production line to which the asset is assigned.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: Required for R&D equipment cost allocation and compliance reporting linking each asset to its R&D project.']
  sales_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.sales_contract. Business justification: Needed for equipment lease tracking; each asset is provisioned under a sales contract, enabling contract compliance and revenue recognition reports.']
  ship_to_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.ship_to_location. Business justification: Service Scheduling uses the customer\'s ship‑to location to plan on‑site maintenance visits for assets.']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Asset location tracking links equipment to its storage location, supporting the Asset Utilization and Allocation report.']
  asset_category varchar [note: 'Specific functional category of the equipment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mixer|boiler|conveyor|refrigeration|cip_system|compressor|pump|valve — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  asset_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the asset used in reports and work orders.']
  asset_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the asset.']
  asset_type varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the asset within the plant.']
  barcode varchar [note: 'Machine‑readable barcode associated with the asset tag.']
  book_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'Net book value of the asset after depreciation.']
  calibration_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the asset must be recalibrated to maintain measurement accuracy.']
  calibration_interval_days varchar [note: 'Standard period between required calibrations.']
  capacity_units varchar [note: 'Production capacity expressed in appropriate units (e.g., liters_per_hour).']
  class_iso14224 varchar [note: 'Classification code defined by ISO 14224 for equipment type.']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date the asset became operational after testing.']
  compliance_certifications varchar [note: 'List of certifications (e.g., GMP, HACCP, ISO 22000) held by the asset.']
  criticality_rating varchar [note: 'Business impact rating used for risk‑based maintenance planning.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'Date the asset was retired or removed from service.']
  dimensions varchar [note: 'Length x Width x Height of the asset (e.g., 2.5m x 1.2m x 1.8m).']
  health_score varchar [note: 'Composite score indicating overall equipment health.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date the asset was physically installed at the plant.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent regulatory or internal inspection.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date the most recent maintenance work order was completed.']
  maintenance_interval_days varchar [note: 'Standard interval between preventive maintenance events.']
  maintenance_strategy varchar [note: 'Chosen maintenance approach for the asset.']
  manufacturer varchar [note: 'Company that built the asset.']
  model_number varchar [note: 'Model identifier assigned by the manufacturer.']
  mtbf_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average operating time between two consecutive failures.']
  mttr_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average time required to restore the asset to service after a failure.']
  next_inspection_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next required inspection.']
  next_maintenance_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next preventive maintenance activity.']
  operating_status varchar [note: 'Current operational condition of the asset.']
  power_rating_kw decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum electrical power consumption of the asset.']
  regulatory_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the asset with respect to regulatory compliance.']
  replacement_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated cost to replace the asset with a new equivalent unit.']
  risk_score varchar [note: 'Risk rating used for reliability and safety planning.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number stamped on the asset.']
  tag varchar [note: 'Barcode or RFID tag printed on the asset for physical identification.']
  useful_life_years varchar [note: 'Planned economic life of the asset expressed in years.']
  warranty_expiry_date date [note: 'Date the OEM warranty coverage ends.']
  weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Mass of the asset in kilograms.']

  Note: 'Master record for all maintainable physical assets across F&B production facilities — filling lines, mixers, pasteurizers, CIP systems, boilers, compressors, conveyors, refrigeration units, and utilities. Captures asset ID, asset name, asset type (production equipment, utility, facility, instrumentation), asset category, manufacturer, model number, serial number, installation date, commissioning date, decommission date, asset status (active, inactive, decommissioned, scrapped), criticality rating (critical, major, minor), plant/facility assignment, production line assignment, cost center, replacement value, current book value, expected useful life (years), maintenance strategy (preventive, predictive, run-to-failure), OEM warranty expiry date, asset tag/barcode, parent asset reference for hierarchy, and asset class per ISO 14224. SSOT for all maintainable equipment identity in the maintenance domain — distinct from manufacturing.equipment_master which focuses on production execution context.'
}

Table maintenance.asset_hierarchy {
  asset_hierarchy_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each node in the asset hierarchy.']
  parent_asset_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on asset_hierarchy (parent_asset_hierarchy_id)']
  parent_node_asset_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the immediate parent node in the hierarchy.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant to which the node belongs.']
  asset_category varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the asset type.']
  asset_hierarchy_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the asset or location.']
  asset_hierarchy_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the node.']
  asset_subcategory varchar [note: 'More specific classification within the asset category.']
  asset_tag varchar [note: 'Internal tag or barcode used for tracking the asset.']
  availability_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of time the asset is available for production.']
  calibration_interval_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between required calibrations.']
  calibration_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the asset requires periodic calibration.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance state with food safety regulations.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Financial cost center responsible for the asset.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the hierarchy record was created.']
  criticality_level varchar [note: 'Business criticality of the asset for production.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'Date the asset was retired or removed from service.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the node ceases to be effective (null if still active).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the node becomes effective in the hierarchy.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Depth of the node in the hierarchy (root = 0).']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date the asset was installed and placed into service.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent maintenance activity.']
  location_latitude double [note: 'Geographic latitude of the asset\'s physical location.']
  location_longitude double [note: 'Geographic longitude of the asset\'s physical location.']
  maintenance_group varchar [note: 'Team or group responsible for maintaining the asset.']
  maintenance_strategy varchar [note: 'Planned maintenance approach for the asset.']
  manufacturer varchar [note: 'Name of the company that manufactured the asset.']
  model_number varchar [note: 'Model identifier assigned by the manufacturer.']
  mtbf_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average operating time between failures for the asset.']
  mttr_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average time required to restore the asset after a failure.']
  next_maintenance_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next preventive maintenance.']
  node_code varchar [note: 'Standard SAP functional location code identifying the node (e.g., 1000‑2000‑3000).']
  node_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the asset or functional location.']
  node_type varchar [note: 'Category of the node within the hierarchy.']
  oee_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Combined metric of availability, performance, and quality.']
  otif_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target percentage for delivering orders on time and in full for the asset\'s output.']
  responsible_department varchar [note: 'Organizational department that owns the asset.']
  safety_rating varchar [note: 'Safety risk rating assigned to the asset.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number stamped on the equipment.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who last modified the record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the asset\'s manufacturer warranty ends.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who created the record.']

  Note: 'Defines the parent-child functional location and asset hierarchy used in CMMS for F&B facilities — from plant level down to functional location, system, sub-system, and individual equipment unit. Captures hierarchy node ID, node name, node type (plant, area, system, equipment), parent node reference, functional location code (SAP FL format), hierarchy level, plant assignment, description, and effective dates. Enables roll-up of maintenance KPIs (MTBF, MTTR, availability) across the asset tree and supports work order routing to the correct functional location.'
}

Table maintenance.work_order {
  work_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the work order record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Work Order Reporting aggregates orders per customer account to monitor downtime and service costs.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.asset. Business justification: Work orders are executed on a specific asset; adding asset_id links the order to its target asset.']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: REQUIRED: Work Order Impact on Brand Production Schedule analysis requires brand_id on work_order to assess maintenance effects on brand output.']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: Site coordination requires linking each work order to the primary customer contact responsible for the location.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Charges from work orders are allocated to cost centers for internal cost performance analysis.']
  equipment_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the equipment or asset to be maintained.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Work order costs are posted to a GL account; essential for maintenance expense reporting.']
  order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.order. Business justification: Associates after‑sale service work orders with the original sales order for warranty validation and service cost allocation.']
  parent_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on work_order (parent_work_order_id)']
  plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_plan. Business justification: Integrated Production Schedule process ties work orders to supply plans to adjust production quantities when equipment is down.']
  primary_work_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who requested the work order.']
  product_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.product_registration. Business justification: Traceability: Work orders for equipment producing a regulated product must reference the product registration to ensure compliance.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Maintenance expenses affect profit‑center P&L; linking enables profit‑center profitability reporting.']
  promotion_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trade.promotion_event. Business justification: Work orders for setting up promotional displays are created per promotion event; linking enables scheduling, labor costing, and audit of promotion‑related maintenance.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: Needed to track maintenance work orders affecting R&D project schedules and downtime impact for project performance reports.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Production Planning creates work orders for each SKU batch; linking work_order to sku enables the Production Schedule report and cost tracking per SKU.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'FK to manufacturing.work_center.work_center_id — Production-critical: maintenance work orders must join to manufacturing work centers to identify which production line is affected during equipment failure. Without this FK, maintenance-to-production ']
  work_order_template_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order_template. Business justification: Work orders are created from a template; adding work_order_template_id captures this relationship.']
  actual_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final total cost incurred for the work order.']
  actual_end_date date [note: 'Date when the maintenance work was completed.']
  actual_labor_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Recorded labor effort in hours actually spent.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'Date when the maintenance work actually began.']
  completion_notes varchar [note: 'Final remarks and observations recorded upon work order closure.']
  corrective_action_taken varchar [note: 'Description of the corrective measures applied to resolve the issue.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the work order record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for cost values.']
  downtime_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total production downtime caused by the maintenance activity.']
  estimated_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Projected total cost for the work order before execution.']
  estimated_labor_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Planned labor effort in hours for the work order.']
  failure_description varchar [note: 'Narrative of the observed failure or issue prompting the work order.']
  failure_mode_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the mode of failure observed.']
  functional_location varchar [note: 'Hierarchical location code where the asset resides.']
  order_issued_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the work order was officially issued to the maintenance team.']
  permit_to_work_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the issued permit to work for the activity.']
  planned_end_date date [note: 'Scheduled completion date for the maintenance activity.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Scheduled start date for the maintenance activity.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Urgency level assigned to the work order.']
  production_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the work order impacted production output.']
  root_cause_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code describing the underlying cause of the failure.']
  safety_permit_required_flag boolean [note: 'True if a safety permit is required before work can commence.']
  sap_pm_order_number varchar [note: 'Corresponding SAP Plant Maintenance order number for ERP integration.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the work order record.']
  work_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the maintenance tasks to be performed.']
  work_order_number varchar [note: 'External work order number used in CMMS and ERP integration.']
  work_order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the work order. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: created|planned|released|in_progress|completed|closed|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  work_order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the maintenance activity type. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: preventive|corrective|predictive|emergency|shutdown|inspection|calibration — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Core CMMS work order record representing every maintenance activity executed across F&B production facilities — the primary transactional entity of the maintenance domain. Captures work order number, work order type (preventive, corrective, predictive, emergency, shutdown, inspection, calibration), work order status (created, planned, released, in-progress, completed, closed, cancelled), priority (emergency, urgent, normal, low), asset reference, functional location, failure description, work description, requested by, assigned to (crew/technician), planned start date, planned end date, actual start date, actual end date, estimated labor hours, actual labor hours, estimated cost, actual cost, downtime hours caused, production impact flag, safety permit required flag, permit-to-work number, root cause code, failure mode code, corrective action taken, completion notes, and SAP PM order number for ERP integration.'
}

Table maintenance.work_order_task {
  work_order_task_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the work order task line item.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the technician or mechanic assigned to execute the task.']
  predecessor_work_order_task_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on work_order_task (predecessor_work_order_task_id)']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Task execution may require parts from a specific location; the Work Order Task Allocation report depends on this FK.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent work order that this task belongs to.']
  actual_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Actual time in minutes spent on the task.']
  completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the task was marked completed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the task record was initially created in the system.']
  equipment_tag varchar [note: 'Asset tag or identifier of the equipment on which the task is performed.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'True if the task is classified as critical to production continuity.']
  labor_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary cost of labor for the task, based on labor hours and labor rate.']
  labor_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total labor hours charged to the task (derived from actual duration).']
  maintenance_category varchar [note: 'Category of maintenance activity (preventive, corrective, or predictive).']
  part_cost_total decimal(12,2) [note: 'Aggregate cost of all parts used in the task.']
  part_quantity_used varchar [note: 'Total quantity of parts consumed for the task.']
  parts_used varchar [note: 'List of part numbers or SKUs consumed during the task.']
  planned_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Estimated time in minutes required to complete the task as planned.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the task.']
  quality_check_passed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the task passed post‑completion quality inspection.']
  quality_notes varchar [note: 'Comments or observations from the quality inspection.']
  safety_instructions varchar [note: 'Safety precautions and instructions that must be followed while performing the task.']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of the task within the work order workflow.']
  skill_required varchar [note: 'Skill or trade required to perform the task (e.g., electrician, mechanic).']
  task_code varchar [note: 'External reference code for the task, used in work order documentation.']
  task_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the maintenance activity to be performed.']
  task_type varchar [note: 'Category of the maintenance task (e.g., inspection, lubrication, replacement, adjustment, cleaning, testing).']
  tools_required varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of tools, equipment, or fixtures needed for the task.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the task record.']
  work_order_task_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the task.']

  Note: 'Individual task or operation line within a CMMS work order, representing a discrete step in the maintenance activity sequence. Captures task number, task description, task type (inspection, lubrication, replacement, adjustment, cleaning, testing), assigned technician, planned duration, actual duration, task status (pending, in-progress, completed, skipped), sequence number, required skill/trade, safety instructions, tools required, task completion notes, and actual completion timestamp. Enables granular tracking of multi-step maintenance procedures and supports labor time reporting at the task level.'
}

Table maintenance.pm_plan {
  pm_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the preventive maintenance plan.']
  asset_group_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the asset group (e.g., production line) the plan applies to when not asset-specific.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific equipment asset this plan applies to.']
  parent_pm_plan_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on pm_plan (parent_pm_plan_id)']
  responsible_crew_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the maintenance crew responsible for executing the plan.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the external vendor responsible for the maintenance, if applicable.']
  work_order_template_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the work order template used when generating work orders from this plan.']
  budgeted_annual_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Annual budget allocated for this maintenance plan.']
  calibration_required boolean [note: 'Indicates if calibration of the equipment is required as part of this plan.']
  compliance_requirement varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance requirement(s) applicable to the maintenance activity (e.g., FDA|USDA|ISO22000). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FDA|USDA|ISO22000|GFSI|EFSA|EPA|FTC|ISO9001|FSSC22000 — promote to reference product]']
  condition_monitoring_parameter varchar [note: 'Name of the condition monitoring parameter (e.g., vibration, temperature) used for condition-based plans.']
  condition_threshold_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of the condition threshold value (e.g., mm/s, °C).']
  condition_threshold_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'Threshold value for the condition monitoring parameter that triggers maintenance.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Cost center associated with the maintenance expenses.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the maintenance plan record was created.']
  cycle_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measurement for the maintenance_cycle.']
  documentation_reference varchar [note: 'Link or identifier to the detailed maintenance procedure documentation.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the plan expires or is decommissioned (nullable if open-ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the plan becomes effective.']
  estimated_labor_hours_per_cycle decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated labor hours required to execute the maintenance task each cycle.']
  estimated_material_cost_per_cycle decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated material cost associated with each maintenance cycle.']
  is_external_vendor boolean [note: 'Indicates whether external vendor services are required for this plan.']
  last_execution_date date [note: 'Date when the plan was last executed.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who last modified the plan record.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days required to schedule resources before the maintenance date.']
  maintenance_cycle varchar [note: 'Number of cycles between scheduled maintenance events (e.g., 30 days, 500 operating hours).']
  maintenance_strategy varchar [note: 'Strategic approach for the maintenance activities defined by the plan.']
  next_due_date date [note: 'Date when the next scheduled maintenance is due.']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'External reference code for the maintenance plan (e.g., PM-2023-001).']
  plan_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the maintenance plan, including scope and objectives.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the preventive maintenance plan.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the maintenance plan.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the plan is triggered by time, equipment usage counter, or condition monitoring.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority of the maintenance plan for scheduling purposes.']
  required_spare_parts varchar [note: 'List or reference to spare parts required for the maintenance task.']
  safety_risk_level varchar [note: 'Indicates the safety risk associated with the maintenance activity.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the maintenance plan record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who created the plan record.']

  Note: 'Preventive maintenance plan master record defining the scheduled maintenance strategy for an asset or group of assets in F&B facilities. Captures PM plan ID, plan name, plan type (time-based, counter-based, condition-based), maintenance strategy, asset or asset group reference, maintenance cycle (daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, annual, runtime hours, production cycles), cycle unit, plan description, work order template reference, estimated labor hours per cycle, estimated material cost per cycle, plan status (active, inactive, suspended), next due date, last execution date, lead time for scheduling, and responsible maintenance crew. Drives automatic work order generation in CMMS/SAP PM.'
}

Table maintenance.pm_schedule {
  pm_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the preventive maintenance schedule record.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the equipment or asset to be maintained.']
  compliance_document_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the compliance document associated with this maintenance schedule.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary technician or crew assigned to perform the maintenance.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the plant or facility where the maintenance will occur.']
  pm_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the preventive maintenance plan that generated this schedule.']
  rescheduled_pm_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on pm_schedule (rescheduled_pm_schedule_id)']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the work order generated from this maintenance schedule.']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'Date on which the maintenance work was actually completed.']
  actual_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Actual time taken to complete the maintenance activity.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the maintenance schedule.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the schedule was approved or rejected.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the maintenance activity met regulatory or internal compliance requirements.']
  cost_actual decimal(12,2) [note: 'Actual cost incurred for the maintenance activity.']
  cost_estimate decimal(12,2) [note: 'Projected cost for performing the maintenance activity.']
  counter_reading decimal(12,2) [note: 'Equipment counter (e.g., runtime hours) recorded at the time the schedule was created.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the schedule record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter currency code for cost fields.']
  downtime_minutes varchar [note: 'Total production downtime incurred due to the maintenance activity.']
  equipment_condition varchar [note: 'Condition of the equipment at the time of scheduling.']
  estimated_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Planned duration of the maintenance activity in minutes.']
  is_out_of_service boolean [note: 'True if the equipment is taken out of service pending maintenance.']
  is_recurring boolean [note: 'True if the schedule repeats on a regular interval.']
  last_counter_reading decimal(12,2) [note: 'Most recent equipment counter value prior to this schedule.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent inspection prior to this schedule.']
  last_updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who last modified the schedule record.']
  maintenance_category varchar [note: 'Broad category describing the nature of the maintenance.']
  maintenance_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the maintenance work (preventive, corrective, or predictive).']
  next_due_counter decimal(12,2) [note: 'Counter value at which the next preventive maintenance is due.']
  next_inspection_due_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next required inspection after this maintenance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional comments or instructions related to the maintenance activity.']
  overdue_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the schedule is past its planned date without completion.']
  pm_schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the maintenance schedule.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the maintenance activity.']
  recurrence_interval_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between recurring maintenance occurrences.']
  required_spare_parts varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of spare parts needed for the maintenance.']
  safety_risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk classification for safety associated with the maintenance activity.']
  schedule_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable business identifier for the maintenance schedule.']
  schedule_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the schedule creation – either generated automatically by the system or entered manually.']
  scheduled_date date [note: 'Planned calendar date for the preventive maintenance activity.']
  scheduling_basis varchar [note: 'Basis used to trigger the schedule (calendar date, runtime hours, or production count).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the schedule record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who initially created the schedule record.']

  Note: 'Individual scheduled maintenance call generated from a PM plan, representing a specific upcoming or completed preventive maintenance event for an asset. Captures schedule ID, PM plan reference, asset reference, scheduled date, actual completion date, work order generated reference, schedule status (open, completed, overdue, skipped, cancelled), counter reading at scheduling, scheduling basis (calendar, runtime, production count), and overdue days. Provides the operational calendar view of all upcoming PM activities across the facility and enables compliance tracking against the PM plan.'
}

Table maintenance.failure_record {
  failure_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the failure record.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the equipment or asset that experienced the failure.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who reported the failure.']
  root_cause_failure_record_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on failure_record (root_cause_failure_record_id)']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Failure records capture the exact storage location of the affected asset; needed for the Failure Impact Analysis.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the maintenance work order created to address the failure.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or observations related to the failure.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of the failure remediation with regulatory requirements.']
  corrective_action varchar [note: 'Description of the corrective action taken to resolve the failure.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the failure record was first created in the system.']
  detection_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the failure was identified.']
  downtime_end timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the equipment was returned to service.']
  downtime_start timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the equipment was taken out of service.']
  failure_cause varchar [note: 'Root cause that triggered the failure.']
  failure_effect varchar [note: 'Impact of the failure on production output.']
  failure_mode varchar [note: 'Technical classification of how the failure manifested.']
  failure_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable sequential number assigned to the failure event.']
  failure_record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the failure record.']
  failure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the failure was first detected.']
  fmea_reference_code varchar [note: 'Code linking to the associated Failure Mode and Effects Analysis record.']
  is_safety_incident boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the failure resulted in a safety incident.']
  maintenance_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the maintenance action associated with the failure.']
  mtbf_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average operating time between successive failures for the asset.']
  mttr_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average time required to repair the asset after a failure.']
  preventive_action varchar [note: 'Planned preventive measure to avoid recurrence of the failure.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Operational priority for addressing the failure.']
  production_loss_quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Quantity of product not produced due to the failure.']
  regulatory_report_flag boolean [note: 'True if the failure must be reported to regulatory bodies (e.g., FDA).']
  root_cause_analysis_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the root‑cause investigation.']
  sensor_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sensor that generated an alarm, if applicable.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Business‑impact severity assigned to the failure.']
  shift varchar [note: 'Work shift during which the failure occurred.']
  total_downtime_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Calculated total downtime duration in hours.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the failure record.']

  Note: 'Structured failure event record capturing the complete details of an asset breakdown or functional failure at an F&B production facility. Captures failure record ID, asset reference, failure date and time, failure detection method (operator report, sensor alarm, inspection, predictive alert), failure mode (mechanical, electrical, instrumentation, process, human error), failure cause (wear, fatigue, corrosion, contamination, misalignment, operator error), failure effect on production (no impact, minor slowdown, line stoppage, plant shutdown), downtime start, downtime end, total downtime hours, production loss quantity, associated work order reference, root cause analysis status, and FMEA reference code. Supports MTBF/MTTR calculation and reliability engineering analysis.'
}

Table maintenance.spare_part {
  spare_part_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the spare part record.']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Inventory of spare parts must record exact warehouse location for maintenance crews; the Spare Part Location Report relies on this FK.']
  superseded_spare_part_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on spare_part (superseded_spare_part_id)']
  cmms_material_master_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier linking the spare part to the CMMS material master record.']
  compatible_asset_references varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of asset identifiers that can use this spare part.']
  compliance_certifications varchar [note: 'List of regulatory or industry certifications applicable to the part (e.g., FDA, GFSI).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the spare part record was first created.']
  criticality varchar [note: 'Indicates the importance of the part to production continuity.']
  dimensions varchar [note: 'Physical dimensions of the part expressed as Length x Width x Height.']
  disposal_instructions varchar [note: 'Guidance on environmentally compliant disposal of the part.']
  hazardous_material_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the part is classified as hazardous under GHS/OSHA regulations.']
  is_serialized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether each unit of the part is tracked with a unique serial number.']
  last_inventory_check_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent physical inventory verification for this part.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Average procurement lead time in calendar days.']
  manufacturer_part_number varchar [note: 'Part number assigned by the original equipment manufacturer.']
  maximum_stock_level varchar [note: 'Maximum quantity allowed in storage to control inventory cost.']
  minimum_stock_level varchar [note: 'Minimum quantity to be kept on hand to avoid stock‑outs.']
  oem_part_number varchar [note: 'Original Equipment Manufacturer part number, if different from the internal part number.']
  part_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the spare part by functional type.']
  part_number varchar [note: 'Primary part number used internally to identify the spare part.']
  reorder_point varchar [note: 'Inventory level that triggers a replenishment order.']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the part remains usable from receipt, if applicable.']
  spare_part_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the spare part, including function and key characteristics.']
  spare_part_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the spare part.']
  spare_part_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the spare part.']
  standard_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Baseline cost of the spare part used for valuation and budgeting.']
  supplier_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the preferred supplier for this spare part.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit of measure for inventory transactions (e.g., each, kg, liter).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the spare part record.']
  warranty_period_months varchar [note: 'Manufacturer warranty period expressed in months.']
  weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Physical weight of the part, used for logistics and handling.']

  Note: 'Master record for all spare parts, replacement components, and maintenance materials stocked for F&B production equipment. Captures spare part ID, part number, part name, part description, manufacturer part number, OEM part number, part category (mechanical, electrical, instrumentation, consumable, lubricant, seal/gasket), unit of measure, criticality (critical, non-critical, insurance spare), lead time (days), minimum stock level, maximum stock level, reorder point, standard cost, storage location, compatible asset references, shelf life (if applicable), hazardous material flag, and CMMS material master reference. SSOT for spare parts identity in the maintenance domain — distinct from inventory.stock_position which tracks quantities.'
}

Table maintenance.parts_consumption {
  parts_consumption_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the parts consumption record.']
  primary_parts_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the technician who issued the parts.']
  reversal_parts_consumption_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on parts_consumption (reversal_parts_consumption_id)']
  spare_part_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the spare part or material consumed.']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Parts Consumption Log tracks which storage location parts were issued from; required for cost allocation and audit.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the external supplier that provided the spare part.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the maintenance work order to which the parts consumption belongs.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch identifier of the part, if applicable, for traceability.']
  consumption_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the part was issued/consumed.']
  consumption_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the consumption event (preventive maintenance, corrective repair, or emergency).']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Financial cost center associated with the consumption for internal accounting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the consumption record was first created in the system.']
  goods_issue_doc_number varchar [note: 'SAP MIGO document number that records the goods issue transaction.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the consumption line within the work order.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot number for the part, used for quality and regulatory traceability.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text field for additional remarks or observations about the consumption.']
  parts_consumption_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the consumption line.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant or facility where the consumption occurred.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'Purchase order number linked to the acquisition of the spare part.']
  quantity decimal(12,3) [note: 'Amount of the part consumed, expressed in the selected unit of measure.']
  total_cost decimal(14,4) [note: 'Total cost for the line (quantity * unit cost).']
  unit_cost decimal(12,4) [note: 'Cost per unit of the part at the time of consumption, in the functional currency.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit of measure for the quantity (e.g., each, kilogram, liter, meter, box).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the consumption record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of spare parts and materials consumed against a maintenance work order at an F&B facility. Captures consumption ID, work order reference, spare part reference, quantity consumed, unit of measure, consumption date, storage location issued from, actual unit cost at time of consumption, total line cost, technician who issued the parts, and goods issue document number (SAP MIGO reference). Enables actual maintenance material cost tracking, spare parts usage history per asset, and drives replenishment signals to the MRO inventory process.'
}

Table maintenance.calibration_order {
  calibration_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the calibration work order.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the technician or laboratory that performed the calibration.']
  instrument_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the calibrated instrument or measurement device asset.']
  previous_calibration_order_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on calibration_order (previous_calibration_order_id)']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the plant or site where calibration was performed.']
  actual_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the calibration was actually performed.']
  as_found_reading decimal(12,4) [note: 'Instrument reading measured before any adjustment.']
  as_left_reading decimal(12,4) [note: 'Instrument reading measured after adjustment (if any).']
  calibration_method varchar [note: 'Method used for calibration (e.g., laboratory, field, on‑line).']
  calibration_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes captured by the technician or lab.']
  calibration_order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the calibration order.']
  calibration_source varchar [note: 'Whether calibration was performed by internal staff or an external provider.']
  calibration_standard varchar [note: 'Reference standard or traceable artifact used for the calibration.']
  calibration_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the calibration activity.']
  certificate_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the issued calibration certificate.']
  corrective_action varchar [note: 'Action taken if calibration failed or was out of tolerance.']
  cost_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary cost incurred for the calibration activity.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the calibration order record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the calibration cost.']
  instrument_serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-assigned serial number of the instrument.']
  instrument_type varchar [note: 'Category of the instrument (e.g., flow meter, temperature sensor, pH meter).']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the calibration readings (e.g., °C, psi, %).']
  next_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next required calibration.']
  procedure_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the documented calibration procedure applied.']
  regulatory_requirement_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the regulatory clause (e.g., FDA 21 CFR, NIST) governing the calibration.']
  result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the calibration activity.']
  scheduled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time when the calibration is to be performed.']
  tolerance_lower decimal(12,4) [note: 'Minimum acceptable value for the calibration reading.']
  tolerance_upper decimal(12,4) [note: 'Maximum acceptable value for the calibration reading.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the calibration order record.']

  Note: 'Calibration work order and results record for instrumentation and measurement equipment at F&B production facilities — covering flow meters, temperature sensors, pressure gauges, pH meters, checkweighers, and metal detectors. Captures calibration order ID, instrument asset reference, calibration type (routine, as-found/as-left, post-repair, regulatory), calibration standard used, calibration procedure reference, scheduled date, actual calibration date, technician/lab performing calibration, as-found reading, as-left reading, tolerance limits (upper/lower), calibration result (pass, fail, adjusted, out-of-tolerance), next calibration due date, calibration certificate number, regulatory requirement reference (FDA 21 CFR, NIST traceability), and corrective action if failed. Critical for HACCP CCP instrument compliance.'
}

Table maintenance.calibration_schedule {
  calibration_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'System generated unique identifier for the calibration schedule record.']
  parent_calibration_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on calibration_schedule (parent_calibration_schedule_id)']
  primary_calibration_asset_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the instrument or measurement device that requires calibration.']
  calibration_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the certificate issued after successful calibration.']
  calibration_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary cost incurred for the calibration activity.']
  calibration_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the calibration cost.']
  calibration_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Estimated or actual time required to complete the calibration.']
  calibration_frequency varchar [note: 'Planned interval at which the instrument must be calibrated.']
  calibration_location varchar [note: 'Plant, line, or area where the calibration will be performed.']
  calibration_method varchar [note: 'Technique used to perform the calibration.']
  calibration_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations recorded during calibration.']
  calibration_responsible_contact varchar [note: 'Email address of the person or lab responsible for the calibration.']
  calibration_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the most recent calibration activity.']
  calibration_standard varchar [note: 'Reference standard or protocol applied during calibration.']
  calibration_standard_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the calibration standard used.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the calibration schedule record was created.']
  instrument_type varchar [note: 'Category of the instrument based on measurement principle.']
  last_calibration_date date [note: 'Date when the instrument was most recently calibrated.']
  next_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next required calibration.']
  overdue_alert_threshold_days varchar [note: 'Number of days past the due date after which an overdue alert is generated.']
  regulatory_basis varchar [note: 'Regulatory or audit requirement that drives the calibration.']
  responsible_party varchar [note: 'Entity responsible for executing the calibration.']
  schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the calibration schedule.']
  tolerance_specification varchar [note: 'Acceptable deviation limits for the instrument measurement.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the calibration schedule record.']

  Note: 'Master calibration schedule defining the required calibration frequency and parameters for each instrument or measurement device at F&B facilities. Captures schedule ID, instrument asset reference, calibration frequency (daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, annual), calibration method, responsible party (internal lab, external accredited lab), calibration standard required, tolerance specification, regulatory basis (HACCP CCP, FDA, USDA, customer audit requirement), schedule status (active, suspended, decommissioned), last calibration date, next due date, and overdue alert threshold (days). Ensures all HACCP-critical instruments maintain current calibration status.'
}

Table maintenance.crew {
  crew_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the maintenance crew.']
  home_plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the plant/facility where the crew is based.']
  lead_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the crew lead or supervisor.']
  parent_crew_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on crew (parent_crew_id)']
  availability_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of scheduled time the crew is available (e.g., 92.5).']
  average_mttr_minutes decimal(6,2) [note: 'Mean Time To Repair for tasks completed by the crew, expressed in minutes.']
  capacity_hours_per_shift decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard number of labor hours the crew can deliver in a single shift.']
  certifications varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of certifications held by the crew (e.g., "Electrical Safety, Confined Space").']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Financial cost‑center to which crew labor costs are charged.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the crew record was first created in the system.']
  crew_code varchar [note: 'Business‑assigned code used for scheduling and reporting (e.g., "MC‑001").']
  crew_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the crew (e.g., "North Plant Mechanical Team").']
  crew_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the crew.']
  crew_type varchar [note: 'Category of work the crew primarily performs.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the crew was retired or de‑activated (null if still active).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the crew became operationally active.']
  last_assigned_date date [note: 'Most recent date the crew was assigned to a work order.']
  next_available_date date [note: 'Projected date when the crew will be free for new assignments.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form remarks or operational notes about the crew.']
  shift_assignment varchar [note: 'Typical shift pattern the crew works.']
  skill_specializations varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of primary skill areas (e.g., "HVAC, PLC, Welding").']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the crew record.']

  Note: 'Master record for maintenance crews, teams, and work groups organized within F&B production facilities. Captures crew ID, crew name, crew type (mechanical, electrical, instrumentation, utilities, general maintenance, contractor crew), home plant/facility, crew lead employee reference, crew capacity (standard hours per shift), shift assignment, skill specializations, certifications held by crew (electrical safety, confined space, pressure vessel), active status, and cost center assignment. Used for work order assignment, capacity planning, and maintenance labor scheduling. References workforce.employee for individual technician identity.'
}

Table maintenance.permit_to_work {
  permit_to_work_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the permit to work record.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the equipment or location where the work will be performed.']
  parent_permit_to_work_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on permit_to_work (parent_permit_to_work_id)']
  primary_permit_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee or contractor who requested the permit.']
  quaternary_permit_updated_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'System user who last updated the permit record.']
  quinary_permit_approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person who approved the permit.']
  tertiary_permit_created_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'System user who created the permit record.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the maintenance work order associated with the permit.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the permit was approved.']
  closure_signoff_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person who signed off the permit closure.']
  closure_signoff_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the closure sign‑off.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the permit record was first created in the system.']
  emergency_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person to contact in case of an emergency.']
  emergency_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number for the emergency contact.']
  gas_test_result varchar [note: 'Result of atmospheric gas testing performed before work begins.']
  gas_test_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the gas test was performed.']
  gas_test_value_ppm decimal(6,2) [note: 'Measured concentration of hazardous gas in parts per million.']
  hazard_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of hazards identified for the work.']
  isolation_confirmed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether all required isolation points have been verified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments related to the permit.']
  permit_closure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the permit was formally closed.']
  permit_duration_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated duration between permit start and expiry, expressed in hours.']
  permit_expiry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the permit expires.']
  permit_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the permit, used in operational documentation.']
  permit_revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential revision number for the permit record.']
  permit_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the permit becomes active.']
  permit_to_work_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the permit.']
  permit_type varchar [note: 'Category of high‑risk activity covered by the permit.']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the permit request was submitted.']
  required_ppe varchar [note: 'List of PPE items that must be used during the activity.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall risk rating assigned to the permit activity.']
  temperature_monitoring_required boolean [note: 'Indicates if temperature monitoring is required for the work.']
  temperature_reading_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Recorded temperature in Celsius at the work site, if monitored.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the permit record.']

  Note: 'Safety permit-to-work (PTW) record authorizing high-risk maintenance activities at F&B production facilities — covering hot work, confined space entry, electrical isolation (LOTO), working at height, and chemical handling. Captures permit ID, permit type (hot work, confined space, LOTO/electrical, cold work, chemical, height), associated work order reference, asset/location reference, permit requestor, permit issuer (authorized person), permit start date/time, permit expiry date/time, permit status (requested, approved, active, suspended, closed, cancelled), hazard identification, required PPE, isolation points confirmed flag, gas test results (if applicable), emergency contact, and permit closure sign-off. Mandatory for OSHA compliance and insurance requirements.'
}

Table maintenance.reliability_event {
  reliability_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the reliability engineering event.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the equipment or asset associated with the event.']
  follow_up_reliability_event_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on reliability_event (follow_up_reliability_event_id)']
  actual_cost_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Actual cost incurred for the maintenance changes, in US dollars.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of the asset after the event.']
  cost_currency varchar [note: 'Currency code of the cost values.']
  estimated_cost_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Projected cost to implement the recommended maintenance changes, in US dollars.']
  event_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the reliability activity occurred.']
  event_trigger varchar [note: 'Reason that initiated the reliability event.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Category of reliability activity performed (e.g., RCM analysis, FMEA, etc.).']
  expected_mtbf_improvement_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Projected increase in mean time between failures expressed in hours.']
  facilitator_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person who facilitated the reliability event.']
  findings_summary varchar [note: 'Brief summary of key findings from the reliability analysis.']
  follow_up_action_refs varchar [note: 'Identifiers of follow‑up actions or work orders linked to this event.']
  implementation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of implementing the recommended strategy.']
  maintenance_strategy_change varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the maintenance strategy alteration.']
  mtbf_after decimal(10,2) [note: 'Mean time between failures measured after the event.']
  mtbf_before decimal(10,2) [note: 'Mean time between failures measured before the event.']
  mtbf_improvement_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage improvement in MTBF resulting from the event.']
  participants varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of participants involved in the event.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority of the reliability event for execution planning.']
  recommended_strategy varchar [note: 'Suggested maintenance strategy change based on the event findings.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the reliability event record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the reliability event record.']
  regulatory_requirement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the event was driven by a regulatory requirement.']
  risk_assessment varchar [note: 'Risk level assigned to the asset based on the event analysis.']

  Note: 'Asset reliability program event record capturing planned and executed reliability engineering activities — RCM (Reliability Centered Maintenance) analyses, FMEA reviews, bad actor investigations, and reliability improvement initiatives for F&B production assets. Captures event ID, event type (RCM analysis, FMEA, bad actor review, reliability improvement project, root cause analysis), asset or asset group reference, event trigger (recurring failure, new asset, regulatory requirement, cost threshold breach), event date, facilitator, participants, findings summary, recommended maintenance strategy changes, implementation status, expected MTBF improvement, and follow-up action references. Drives continuous improvement of the PM program.'
}

Table maintenance.asset_condition {
  asset_condition_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each asset condition assessment entry.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the manufacturing equipment or asset whose condition is being recorded.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person (e.g., technician or engineer) who performed the assessment.']
  previous_asset_condition_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on asset_condition (previous_asset_condition_id)']
  assessment_notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments or contextual information captured by the assessor.']
  assessment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the condition assessment was performed.']
  condition_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score representing overall asset health; can be a 1‑5 rating or a percentage.']
  condition_status varchar [note: 'Categorical health status derived from the condition score.']
  findings_text varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of any notable observations or anomalies identified during the assessment.']
  method varchar [note: 'Technique used to evaluate the asset condition, such as visual inspection or vibration analysis.']
  next_assessment_due_date date [note: 'Planned date for the subsequent condition assessment.']
  oil_contamination_level varchar [note: 'Qualitative or numeric indicator of oil cleanliness from oil analysis.']
  recommended_action varchar [note: 'Prescribed next step based on the assessment outcome.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the condition record was initially created in the system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the condition record.']
  temperature_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Temperature reading in degrees Celsius from thermography or sensor.']
  vibration_level_mm_s decimal(8,2) [note: 'Measured vibration amplitude in millimeters per second, captured during vibration analysis.']

  Note: 'Periodic asset condition assessment record capturing the physical health and condition state of F&B production equipment through inspections, condition monitoring, and predictive maintenance technologies. Captures condition record ID, asset reference, assessment date, assessment method (visual inspection, vibration analysis, thermography, oil analysis, ultrasound, motor current analysis), condition score (1-5 scale or percentage), condition status (good, fair, poor, critical), specific findings (vibration level mm/s, temperature reading °C, oil contamination level), recommended action (monitor, schedule PM, immediate repair, plan replacement), next assessment due date, and assessor reference. Feeds predictive maintenance triggers and asset replacement planning.'
}

Table maintenance.maintenance_contract {
  maintenance_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the maintenance contract record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Contract Billing Report links each maintenance contract to the customer account for invoicing and SLA compliance.']
  bill_to_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.bill_to_location. Business justification: Billing address for a maintenance contract must reference the customer\'s bill‑to location for accurate invoicing.']
  primary_maintenance_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the external service provider (references procurement.supplier).']
  purchase_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_contract. Business justification: Maintenance service contracts are often tied to the underlying procurement contract; linking enables consolidated contract compliance reporting.']
  renewed_maintenance_contract_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on maintenance_contract (renewed_maintenance_contract_id)']
  retailer_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trade.retailer_agreement. Business justification: Retailer agreements often include equipment‑maintenance clauses; linking contracts to agreements supports compliance reporting and joint budgeting.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the contract was formally approved by authorized signatories.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract automatically renews at expiry.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'Regulatory or industry compliance clauses attached to the contract (e.g., FDA, GFSI).']
  confidentiality_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract contains confidential information.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Classification level of the contract content.']
  contract_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the maintenance contract.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'External reference number or code assigned to the contract by the organization.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Category of the maintenance agreement (e.g., Annual Maintenance Contract, OEM Service Agreement, Time‑and‑Material, Fixed‑Price, SLA‑Based).']
  contract_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the contract as agreed with the service provider.']
  covered_asset_category varchar [note: 'Category or classification of equipment/assets covered (e.g., Production Line, HVAC, Elevator).']
  covered_asset_ids varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of asset identifiers that the contract applies to.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the contract record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the contract value (e.g., USD, EUR).']
  document_url varchar [note: 'Link to the stored contract document (PDF, scanned copy, etc.).']
  end_date date [note: 'Date on which the contract expires or terminates (null for open‑ended).']
  exclusions varchar [note: 'Specific services or items not covered by the contract.']
  maintenance_contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the contract.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms for the contract (e.g., Net 30, 2% 10 Net 30).']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of required regulatory approvals for the contract.']
  renewal_notice_date date [note: 'Date by which a renewal notice must be issued to avoid lapse.']
  resolution_time_sla_minutes varchar [note: 'Maximum time in minutes to resolve a reported issue after response.']
  response_time_sla_minutes varchar [note: 'Maximum time in minutes for the provider to respond to an emergency request.']
  scope_of_services varchar [note: 'Detailed description of services covered under the contract.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date on which the contract becomes binding.']
  termination_notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days notice required for early termination.']
  termination_notice_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal notice is required to terminate the contract early.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the contract record.']

  Note: 'Master record for external maintenance service contracts and OEM service agreements covering F&B production equipment — including annual maintenance contracts (AMC), OEM service agreements, elevator/HVAC service contracts, and specialist contractor agreements. Captures contract ID, contract name, contract type (AMC, OEM service, time-and-material, fixed-price, SLA-based), service provider (references procurement.supplier), covered assets or asset categories, contract start date, contract end date, contract value, payment terms, response time SLA (emergency, urgent, normal), resolution time SLA, scope of services, exclusions, auto-renewal flag, contract status (active, expired, terminated, pending renewal), and contract document reference. Distinct from procurement.purchase_contract which covers raw material sourcing.'
}

Table maintenance.contractor_visit {
  contractor_visit_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each contractor visit record.']

  contractor_visit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the visit.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the visit record was first created in the system.']

  departure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the contractor left the site.']
  findings varchar [note: 'Observations, issues, or anomalies identified during the visit.']
  gross_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total amount billed before taxes, discounts, or adjustments.']

  net_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final amount payable after taxes and adjustments.']
  parts_replaced varchar [note: 'List of parts or components replaced, including part numbers and quantities.']

  recommendations varchar [note: 'Suggested actions or follow‑up activities based on the findings.']
  site_induction_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contractor completed the required site safety induction.']
