  technician_certification varchar [note: 'Certification or qualification held by the technician relevant to the work performed.']
  technician_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the external technician performing the work.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the visit record.']
  visit_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the contractor visit, used in communications and reporting.']
  visit_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the contractor visit officially started.']
  work_acceptance_signoff boolean [note: 'Flag indicating that the site owner accepted the completed work.']
  work_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the work performed during the visit.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for external contractor and OEM service technician visits to F&B production facilities for maintenance, repair, and inspection activities. Captures visit ID, contractor/supplier reference, maintenance contract reference, associated work order reference, visit purpose (scheduled PM, emergency repair, inspection, commissioning, warranty repair), visit date, arrival time, departure time, technician name and certification, work performed description, parts replaced, findings and recommendations, site induction completed flag, PPE compliance confirmed flag, work acceptance sign-off, and invoice reference. Supports contractor management, site safety compliance, and service cost tracking.'
}

Table maintenance.shutdown_plan {
  shutdown_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the shutdown plan.']
  facility_plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the facility/plant where the shutdown will occur.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific plant within the facility.']
  previous_shutdown_plan_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on shutdown_plan (previous_shutdown_plan_id)']
  actual_end_date date [note: 'Real end date when the shutdown completed.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'Real start date when the shutdown began.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the shutdown plan.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance status of the shutdown plan.']
  coordinator_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person responsible for coordinating the shutdown.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the shutdown plan record was created.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or comments captured by planners.']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the shutdown plan in external systems.']
  planned_end_date date [note: 'Scheduled end date for the shutdown (date only).']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Scheduled start date for the shutdown (date only).']
  regulatory_inspection_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a regulatory inspection is required during the shutdown.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite risk rating (0‑100) for the shutdown based on safety, cost, and schedule impact.']
  safety_review_completed boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if a safety review has been completed prior to shutdown execution.']
  shutdown_plan_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description providing context or details about the shutdown.']
  shutdown_plan_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the shutdown plan.']
  shutdown_plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the shutdown plan.']
  shutdown_plan_type varchar [note: 'Category of the shutdown (e.g., annual overhaul, seasonal changeover).']
  total_actual_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Actual cost incurred for the shutdown.']
  total_actual_downtime_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual downtime hours incurred during the shutdown.']
  total_planned_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Budgeted cost for the shutdown (currency assumed local).']
  total_planned_downtime_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total number of downtime hours planned for the shutdown.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the shutdown plan record.']
  work_order_count varchar [note: 'Number of work orders associated with the shutdown.']

  Note: 'Master record for planned plant shutdowns, turnarounds, and major overhaul events at F&B production facilities — annual shutdowns, seasonal changeovers, and major capital maintenance windows. Captures shutdown plan ID, shutdown name, shutdown type (annual overhaul, seasonal changeover, capital project, regulatory inspection, deep clean), facility/plant reference, planned start date, planned end date, actual start date, actual end date, shutdown status (planning, approved, in-progress, completed, cancelled), total planned downtime hours, total actual downtime hours, total planned cost, total actual cost, number of work orders included, shutdown coordinator, and approval status. Enables integrated planning of all maintenance activities during production downtime windows.'
}

Table maintenance.lubrication_route {
  lubrication_route_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the lubrication route record.']
  parent_lubrication_route_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on lubrication_route (parent_lubrication_route_id)']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant where the route is executed.']
  responsible_crew_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the maintenance crew responsible for executing the route.']
  area_code varchar [note: 'Code of the specific plant area or production line assigned to the route.']
  average_mechanical_efficiency_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target mechanical efficiency percentage for equipment after lubrication.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the lubrication route record was created.']
  documentation_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to SOP, work instruction, or external document governing the route.']
  estimated_duration_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated total time required to complete the route, expressed in hours.']
  food_grade_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the route uses NSF H1/H2 food‑grade lubricants; otherwise false.']
  frequency varchar [note: 'Planned recurrence interval for the route.']
  gmp_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the route complies with GMP requirements.']
  haccp_control_point varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the route is a critical control point under HACCP.']
  historical_success_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of past executions that met all quality and safety criteria.']
  last_audit_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent internal audit of the route.']
  last_execution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the route was most recently executed.']
  lubricant_quantity_per_point_ml decimal(6,2) [note: 'Standard volume of lubricant applied at each point, measured in milliliters.']
  lubricant_type varchar [note: 'Standard classification of the lubricant used (e.g., synthetic, mineral, semi‑synthetic).']
  maintenance_strategy varchar [note: 'Strategy applied to the route (e.g., time‑based, condition‑based).']
  mean_time_between_failures_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Average operating time between failures for equipment covered by the route.']
  mean_time_to_repair_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Average time required to repair equipment after a failure.']
  next_scheduled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time for the next execution of the route.']
  nsf_h1_h2_certification varchar [note: 'NSF certification level of the lubricant used on this route.']
  number_of_points varchar [note: 'Count of individual lubrication points (e.g., bearings, gears) covered by the route.']
  required_tools varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of tools needed to complete the route.']
  route_code varchar [note: 'Business-assigned alphanumeric code used to reference the lubrication route in work orders and schedules.']
  route_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the purpose, scope, and steps of the lubrication route.']
  route_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the lubrication route.']
  route_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the route.']
  route_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the route is preventive, corrective, or both.']
  safety_instructions varchar [note: 'Safety precautions and PPE requirements for performing the route.']
  updated_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the lubrication route record.']

  Note: 'Master record for lubrication routes and rounds defining the structured lubrication program for F&B production equipment — a critical PM sub-program for food-grade lubrication compliance. Captures route ID, route name, route description, plant/area assignment, responsible crew, route frequency (daily, weekly, monthly), estimated duration (hours), number of lube points on route, food-grade lubricant compliance flag (NSF H1/H2 classification), route status (active, suspended), and last execution date. Food-grade lubrication management is a distinct regulatory requirement in F&B manufacturing (NSF H1 lubricants for incidental food contact) that warrants its own master entity.'
}

Table maintenance.lubrication_event {
  lubrication_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the lubrication event record.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the equipment or asset receiving lubrication.']
  lube_point_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific lubrication point on the asset.']
  lubrication_route_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the predefined lubrication route applied to the asset.']
  previous_lubrication_event_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on lubrication_event (previous_lubrication_event_id)']
  primary_lubrication_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the technician who performed the lubrication.']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Lubrication events consume lubricants stored in defined locations; linking enables the Lubricant Usage Dashboard.']
  tertiary_lubrication_updated_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the lubrication event record.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the maintenance work order that includes this lubrication event.']
  application_method varchar [note: 'Method used to apply the lubricant.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch identifier of the lubricant for traceability.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lubrication event met food‑grade compliance requirements.']
  corrective_action_taken varchar [note: 'Description of any corrective action performed if the lube point condition was abnormal.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the lubrication event record was first created in the system.']
  event_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable business number or code assigned to the lubrication event for tracking in maintenance systems.']
  execution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the lubrication task was performed.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the lubricant batch used.']
  humidity_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Relative humidity percentage at the time of lubrication.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lubrication task is classified as critical for production continuity.']
  lube_point_condition varchar [note: 'Observed condition of the lubrication point at the time of service.']
  lubricant_nsf_classification varchar [note: 'NSF food‑grade classification of the lubricant (H1, H2, 3H, or none).']
  lubricant_product_name varchar [note: 'Commercial name of the lubricant product used.']
  lubricant_type varchar [note: 'Category of lubricant applied (e.g., grease, oil, synthetic, food‑grade).']
  lubrication_event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the lubrication event.']
  maintenance_interval_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between scheduled lubrication events for this asset.']
  next_scheduled_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next lubrication event.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form observations or comments captured by the technician.']
  quantity_applied decimal(10,2) [note: 'Amount of lubricant applied during the event.']
  safety_data_sheet_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the safety data sheet associated with the lubricant.']
  temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Ambient temperature in degrees Celsius at the time of lubrication.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the quantity applied (milliliters, grams, ounces).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the lubrication event record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the execution of a lubrication route or individual lubrication task at an F&B production facility. Captures event ID, lubrication route reference, asset/lube point reference, execution date, technician reference, lubricant type used (product name, NSF classification H1/H2/3H), quantity applied (ml/g), application method (grease gun, oil can, automatic lubricator), lube point condition observed (normal, dry, contaminated, over-lubricated), corrective action taken, and completion status. Provides the audit trail for food-grade lubrication compliance and supports lubricant consumption tracking.'
}

Table maintenance.asset_replacement_plan {
  asset_replacement_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each asset replacement plan record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee responsible for overseeing the replacement project.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant where the asset resides.']
  primary_asset_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the equipment or asset that the replacement plan targets.']
  production_line_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific production line that uses the asset.']
  superseded_asset_replacement_plan_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on asset_replacement_plan (superseded_asset_replacement_plan_id)']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the plan received formal approval.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the plan within the capital budgeting process.']
  asset_replacement_plan_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle stage of the replacement plan.']
  budgeted_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total budget allocated for the replacement project.']
  capex_project_reference varchar [note: 'External reference code linking the plan to a capital expenditure project.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes or remarks about the plan.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Cost‑center to which the replacement expense will be charged.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the replacement plan record was first created.']
  current_asset_age_years varchar [note: 'Age of the existing asset measured in years at the time of plan creation.']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Accounting depreciation method to be applied to the new asset.']
  depreciation_period_years varchar [note: 'Number of years over which the asset will be depreciated.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date on which the plan becomes effective for budgeting and execution.']
  effective_until_date date [note: 'Optional end date after which the plan is no longer valid.']
  estimated_refurbishment_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected cost for refurbishing the existing asset if a refurbishment path is chosen.']
  estimated_replacement_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected capital cost required to acquire and install the new asset.']
  expected_savings_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected cost savings from the replacement over the asset\'s lifecycle.']
  funding_source varchar [note: 'Source of capital funding for the replacement.']
  justification_narrative varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation of why the replacement is required, including business and operational drivers.']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'Human‑readable code assigned to the replacement plan for tracking and reference.']
  planned_replacement_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year in which the replacement is scheduled to occur.']
  priority_ranking varchar [note: 'Business‑assigned priority (1 = highest) for the replacement plan.']
  regulatory_requirement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the replacement is driven by a regulatory mandate.']
  remaining_useful_life_years varchar [note: 'Estimated remaining service life of the asset, expressed in years.']
  replacement_trigger varchar [note: 'Reason that initiates the replacement plan, such as age, condition score threshold, reliability failure, obsolescence, or regulatory requirement.']
  replacement_type varchar [note: 'Category of replacement: like‑for‑like, upgrade, or technology change.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative risk rating associated with delaying the replacement.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the plan record.']

  Note: 'Capital asset replacement planning record for F&B production equipment reaching end-of-life or requiring major capital refurbishment. Captures plan ID, asset reference, replacement trigger (age, condition score threshold, reliability failure, obsolescence, regulatory requirement), current asset age (years), remaining useful life estimate, replacement type (like-for-like, upgrade, technology change), estimated replacement cost, estimated refurbishment cost (if applicable), planned replacement year, priority ranking, justification narrative, approval status (proposed, approved, deferred, cancelled), capex project reference, and finance fixed asset reference. Feeds the annual capital expenditure planning process and links to finance.fixed_asset for capitalization.'
}

Table maintenance.maintenance_budget {
  maintenance_budget_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each maintenance budget record.']
  maintenance_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.maintenance_contract. Business justification: Budgets are often scoped to a maintenance contract; adding maintenance_contract_id captures this association.']
  parent_maintenance_budget_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on maintenance_budget (parent_maintenance_budget_id)']
  actual_contractor_cost_ytd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative external service spend to date.']
  actual_labor_cost_ytd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative labor spend incurred to date.']
  actual_material_cost_ytd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative spend on parts and consumables to date.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the budget was formally approved.']
  budget_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the budget record.']
  budget_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the record is an annual plan, a revised forecast, or a rolling 12‑month budget.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Internal cost‑center code responsible for the budget.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the budget record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency identifier for all monetary amounts.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar day of the budget period.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'First calendar day of the budget period.']
  maintenance_category varchar [note: 'Classification of maintenance activity covered by the budget.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Optional free‑text comments or remarks about the budget.']
  planned_contractor_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected expense for external service contracts.']
  planned_labor_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected labor expense for the budget period.']
  planned_material_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected cost of spare parts and consumables.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the manufacturing plant or facility.']
  quarter varchar [note: 'Quarter of the fiscal year for the budget (Q1‑Q4).']
  total_actual_cost_ytd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of all actual cost components incurred to date.']
  total_planned_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of all planned cost components for the budget period.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the budget record.']
  variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Difference between total planned cost and total actual cost YTD.']
  variance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Variance amount expressed as a percentage of total planned cost.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version used for budget revisions and audit tracking.']
  year varchar [note: 'Four‑digit calendar year to which the budget applies.']

  Note: 'Annual and rolling maintenance budget record tracking planned versus actual maintenance expenditure by plant, asset category, maintenance type, and cost center for F&B facilities. Captures budget ID, budget period (fiscal year, quarter), plant/facility reference, cost center reference, maintenance category (preventive, corrective, predictive, shutdown, contractor, spare parts), budget type (annual plan, revised forecast, rolling 12-month), planned labor cost, planned material cost, planned contractor cost, total planned cost, actual labor cost YTD, actual material cost YTD, actual contractor cost YTD, total actual cost YTD, variance amount, variance percentage, and budget status (draft, approved, closed). Distinct from finance.budget which covers enterprise-wide financial budgeting.'
}

Table maintenance.inspection_round {
  inspection_round_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the inspection round record.']
  crew_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the crew or operator assigned to execute the inspection round.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant where the inspection round takes place.']
  previous_inspection_round_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on inspection_round (previous_inspection_round_id)']
  area_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the specific area or zone within the plant for the inspection round.']
  completion_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the inspection round completed (0‑100).']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall compliance outcome of the inspection round.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the inspection round record was first created in the system.']
  execution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the inspection round was performed.']
  inspection_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total time spent executing the inspection round, measured in minutes.']
  inspection_round_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the inspection round.']
  is_autonomous boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the round is part of an autonomous maintenance (AM) program.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations captured by the inspector.']
  number_of_defects varchar [note: 'Count of defects identified during the inspection round.']
  number_of_inspection_points varchar [note: 'Total count of distinct inspection points included in the round.']
  number_of_work_orders varchar [note: 'Count of maintenance work orders generated from defects discovered in the round.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business‑defined priority of the inspection round.']
  round_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the inspection round (e.g., "Morning Equipment Care").']
  round_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable code or number assigned to the inspection round.']
  round_type varchar [note: 'Category of the inspection round indicating its purpose.']
  route_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the inspection route or path taken during the round.']
  scheduled_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the inspection round is scheduled to recur.']
  scheduled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time when the inspection round should start.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the inspection round record.']

  Note: 'Operator and maintenance inspection round record capturing structured walk-around inspections of F&B production equipment and facilities. Captures round ID, round name, round type (operator care round, maintenance inspection, safety inspection, hygiene inspection, pre-startup check), plant/area reference, assigned crew or operator, scheduled frequency (hourly, per shift, daily, weekly), route description, number of inspection points, round status (scheduled, in-progress, completed, overdue), execution date and time, completion percentage, number of defects found, and number of work orders raised from findings. Supports autonomous maintenance (AM) programs and operator-driven defect detection.'
}

Table maintenance.inspection_finding {
  inspection_finding_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the inspection finding record.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the equipment or asset associated with the finding.']
  crew_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the team that performed the inspection.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee or technician who resolved the finding.']
  inspection_round_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the inspection round during which the finding was recorded.']
  related_inspection_finding_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on inspection_finding (related_inspection_finding_id)']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Inspection findings are tied to the physical location inspected; required for the Inspection Traceability Report.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the corrective work order generated from the finding, if any.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch identifier of the product associated with the finding, if applicable.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments or notes related to the finding.']
  compliance_standard varchar [note: 'Regulatory or industry standard applicable to the finding.']
  corrective_action_description varchar [note: 'Description of the corrective action planned or taken to address the finding.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which the corrective action should be completed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the finding record was first created in the system.']
  equipment_tag varchar [note: 'Tag or barcode associated with the equipment where the finding was recorded.']
  finding_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the finding was observed.']
  finding_type varchar [note: 'Category of the finding recorded during inspection.']
  follow_up_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the follow‑up inspection should occur.']
  follow_up_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a follow‑up inspection is required after corrective action.']
  inspection_finding_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the observation or defect.']
  inspection_finding_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the finding.']
  inspection_method varchar [note: 'Method used to conduct the inspection.']
  is_repeat_finding boolean [note: 'True if this finding has been recorded previously for the same asset or location.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot identifier of the raw material or product linked to the finding.']
  photo_evidence_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether photographic evidence is attached to the finding.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority assigned to the finding for remediation scheduling.']
  product_sku varchar [note: 'Stock Keeping Unit of the product related to the finding.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the finding is subject to regulatory compliance requirements.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific regulatory clause or guideline applicable to the finding.']
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the finding was resolved or closed.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative risk score assigned to the finding based on severity and impact.']
  root_cause_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the root cause of the finding.']
  safety_permit_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if a safety permit is required to address the finding.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Severity level indicating the impact of the finding.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Source system where the finding originated.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the finding record.']

  Note: 'Individual defect or observation recorded during an inspection round at an F&B production facility. Captures finding ID, inspection round reference, asset reference, finding date and time, finding type (defect, abnormality, safety hazard, hygiene issue, near-miss, improvement opportunity), finding description, severity (critical, major, minor, observation), finding status (open, work order raised, resolved, accepted risk), photo evidence flag, work order generated reference, resolution date, and resolved by reference. Provides the granular defect log that drives corrective work order generation and supports TPM defect elimination programs.'
}

Table maintenance.lube_point {
  lube_point_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for lube_point']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.asset. Business justification: Lube points are attached to a specific asset; replace generic equipment_id with asset_id FK.']
  parent_lube_point_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on lube_point (parent_lube_point_id)']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the point complies with applicable safety and environmental regulations.']
  criticality_level varchar [note: 'Importance of the point to equipment reliability; drives maintenance priority.']
  last_lubricated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the point was last lubricated.']
  location_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the physical location of the lubrication point on the equipment (e.g., bearing housing, gear shaft).']
  lubrication_interval_days varchar [note: 'Recommended number of days between routine lubrications.']
  lubrication_interval_hours varchar [note: 'Recommended number of operating hours between routine lubrications.']
  lubrication_type varchar [note: 'Category of lubricant applied at this point.']
  lubrication_volume_ml decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard volume of lubricant applied per service (milliliters).']
  maintenance_strategy varchar [note: 'Strategy governing how this point is maintained.']
  next_due_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time for the next lubrication activity.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form remarks or special instructions for the lubrication point.']
  point_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code used to reference the lubrication point in work orders.']
  point_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the lubrication point on equipment.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the lubrication point record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the lubrication point record.']
  safety_rating varchar [note: 'Safety classification of the lubricant at this point.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the lubrication point.']
  viscosity_cst decimal(6,2) [note: 'Viscosity rating of the recommended lubricant in centistokes.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for lube_point. Referenced by lube_point_id.'
}

  total_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'Aggregate financial value of all assets in the group.']
  type varchar [note: 'Category that classifies the purpose or function of the asset group.']
  updated_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier who performed the most recent update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the asset group record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version number for optimistic concurrency control.']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the collective warranty for the asset group expires.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for asset_group. Referenced by asset_group_id.'
}

Table maintenance.work_order_template {
  work_order_template_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for work_order_template']

  default_priority varchar [note: 'Default priority assigned to work orders generated from this template.']

  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the purpose and scope of the template.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the template becomes valid for use.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the template is no longer valid (null if open‑ended).']

  frequency_interval_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between scheduled occurrences for preventive templates.']
  is_template_shared boolean [note: 'True if the template is available across multiple plants or business units.']
  labor_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard labor rate applied when calculating labor cost.']
  material_cost_estimate decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated cost of materials required for the work.']
  plant_location varchar [note: 'Code of the plant or facility where the template is primarily used.']

  required_equipment varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of equipment identifiers required for the work.']

  safety_procedure_code varchar [note: 'Reference code to the safety procedure applicable to the work.']
  safety_risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk classification for safety considerations.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the template.']
  template_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the template in systems and documentation.']
  template_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the work order template.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the template record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for change management of the template.']
  work_center varchar [note: 'Identifier of the work center associated with the template.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for work_order_template. Referenced by work_order_template_id.'
}

Table pricing.price_list {
  price_list_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the price list record.']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Brand‑level price lists are created to enforce brand pricing strategy; marketing brand managers need this link for campaign budgeting.']
  establishment_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.establishment_registration. Business justification: Price‑list creation/approval requires verification of the establishment\'s regulatory registration; compliance report ties price list to establishment_registration.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Revenue posting requires mapping each price list to a GL account for sales revenue recognition per legal entity.']
  ibp_scenario_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.ibp_scenario. Business justification: SCENARIO‑BASED PRICING: price list is tied to an IBP scenario to reflect scenario‑specific cost and margin targets.']
  owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Price list ownership is assigned to a pricing manager; required for Price List Ownership report.']
  purchase_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_contract. Business justification: Contract‑based pricing: price lists are derived from supplier purchase contracts to ensure pricing reflects contract terms.']
  superseded_price_list_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on price_list (superseded_price_list_id)']
  target_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.sustainability_target. Business justification: Corporate Sustainability‑Aligned Pricing Strategy links each price list to a specific carbon‑reduction target.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Governance status of the price list approval workflow.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name of the person or role that approved the price list.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the price list was approved.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Business justification for the most recent change to the price list.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Commercial channel(s) to which the price list applies.']
  confidentiality_flag varchar [note: 'Data classification level for the price list.']
  cost_plus_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Markup percentage applied to the standard cost to compute the list price.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the price list record was created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the price list amounts.']
  customer_segment varchar [note: 'Target customer segment for the price list.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the price list becomes valid.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the price list expires; null for open‑ended.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the price list was last reviewed for relevance.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the price list.']
  margin_floor_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum gross margin percentage required for the price list.']
  owner varchar [note: 'Business owner or responsible team for the price list.']
  price_calculation_method varchar [note: 'Algorithm or rule used to derive the list price.']
  price_ceiling_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Absolute maximum price allowed for any SKU in the list.']
  price_floor_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Absolute minimum price that may be set for any SKU in the list.']
  price_list_category varchar [note: 'Higher‑level grouping such as \'core\', \'promotions\', or \'clearance\'.']
  price_list_code varchar [note: 'Business‑level unique code assigned to the price list (e.g., PL‑2024‑Q1).']
  price_list_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description providing context or notes about the price list.']
  price_list_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the price list used for display and reporting.']
  price_list_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the price list.']
  price_list_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the price list purpose.']
  region varchar [note: 'Region to which the price list is scoped.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating ERP or pricing system (e.g., SAP S/4HANA, Oracle ERP).']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the price list for change tracking.']

  Note: 'Master record defining authorized base selling prices for SKUs by customer segment, channel (retail, foodservice, DTC), and effective date range. Serves as the SSOT for list price governance across all commercial channels. Captures price list name, version, currency, channel applicability, approval status, and effective/expiry dates. Distinct from sales.price_list which is the commercial-facing view — this domain owns the pricing master governance record including cost-plus build-up, margin floors, and pricing authority approvals.'
}

Table pricing.price_list_line {
  price_list_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the price list line record.']
  carbon_footprint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.carbon_footprint. Business justification: Required for Sustainable Pricing Adjustment report that adjusts list price based on SKU carbon intensity.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Cost allocation for each priced SKU line uses a cost center to allocate overhead and production costs in financial reporting.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who approved the price.']
  packaging_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.packaging_profile. Business justification: Used in Packaging Cost Impact analysis to reflect recyclability and material cost in price list decisions.']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent price list that groups this line.']
  product_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.product_registration. Business justification: Each price‑list line must be validated against the product\'s registration status; pricing compliance checks use this FK.']
  purchase_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_contract. Business justification: Traceability: price list lines are often tied to a specific purchase contract governing price, lead time, and MOQ.']
  quality_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.specification. Business justification: Pricing team references product specification to apply spec‑based price premiums or discounts in price list lines, a standard process in food & beverage pricing.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: Pricing of new SKU lines must reference the originating R&D project for launch coordination and price‑list approval.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the SKU (product) to which this price applies.']
  superseded_price_list_line_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on price_list_line (superseded_price_list_line_id)']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Supplier‑specific pricing: each price list line must reference the supplier that provides the SKU under negotiated terms.']
  supply_plan_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.plan_version. Business justification: COST‑PLUS PRICING: each price line references the supply plan version that provides the latest cost base for markup calculations.']
  ceiling_price decimal(15,4) [note: 'Maximum allowable price for the SKU under this price list.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Sales channel for which this price is valid.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the price line record was created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the price.']
  discount_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Discount percentage applied to the list price.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this price expires; null if open‑ended.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this price becomes effective.']
  floor_price decimal(15,4) [note: 'Minimum allowable price for the SKU under this price list.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequence order of the line within the price list.']
  list_price decimal(15,4) [note: 'Standard list price before any discounts or promotions.']
  min_order_qty decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum quantity that must be ordered to qualify for this price.']
  price_approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval status of the price line.']
  price_change_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for the price change (e.g., cost increase, promotion).']
  price_change_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent price change.']
  price_condition_code varchar [note: 'Code representing any conditional pricing rule (e.g., volume break).']
  price_currency_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Exchange rate to the base currency at the effective date.']
  price_effective_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the price became effective (if time‑sensitive).']
  price_expiration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the price expires.']
  price_margin_amount decimal(15,4) [note: 'Calculated margin amount for this price.']
  price_margin_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Margin percentage derived from the price.']
  price_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text notes regarding the price line.']
  price_override_flag boolean [note: 'True if this price overrides default pricing rules.']
  price_source_system varchar [note: 'Source system that originated the price record.']
  price_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the price line.']
  price_tier varchar [note: 'Identifier for tiered pricing structures.']
  price_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the price (e.g., list, promotional, cost‑plus).']
  price_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the price line for audit tracking.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Geographic region code where the price applies.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the price complies with applicable regulatory constraints.']
  tax_included_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether tax is included in the price.']
  uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the price (e.g., each, kilogram).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the price line.']

  Note: 'Individual SKU-level price record within a price list, capturing the authorized selling price for a specific SKU, unit of measure, and minimum order quantity. Stores list price, floor price, ceiling price, currency, UoM, and effective date range. Enables granular price governance at the SKU level across all active price lists.'
}

Table pricing.price_condition {
  price_condition_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the pricing condition record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer or business partner the condition applies to.']
  demand_balance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_demand_balance. Business justification: DYNAMIC DISCOUNT RULE: price condition evaluates supply‑demand balance gaps to trigger volume or shortage discounts.']
  distribution_channel_id varchar [note: 'Distribution channel (e.g., retail, foodservice, DTC) for the condition.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Discounts and surcharges defined in price conditions are posted to specific GL accounts for accurate financial impact tracking.']
  price_group_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the price group used in the condition.']
  procedure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.pricing_procedure. Business justification: price_condition belongs to a pricing procedure; linking enables hierarchy and lookup of procedure details.']
  purchase_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_contract. Business justification: Contract‑driven conditions: discounts, surcharges, and other price conditions are negotiated in purchase contracts.']
  raw_material_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the material/product the condition applies to.']
  sales_org_id varchar [note: 'Sales organization context for the condition.']
  superseded_price_condition_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on price_condition (superseded_price_condition_id)']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code for the most recent change to the condition.']
  condition_category varchar [note: 'Higher-level category grouping of condition (e.g., price, discount, rebate).']
  condition_key varchar [note: 'Composite key identifying the condition record (e.g., combination of customer, material, price group).']
  condition_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the condition.']
  condition_type varchar [note: 'Type of pricing condition defining the pricing logic (e.g., base price, discount, surcharge).']
  condition_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the condition record for change tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the condition record was created.']
  currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the rate.']
  discount_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Discount percentage if condition is a discount.']
  is_exclusive boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the condition is exclusive (cannot be combined with others).']
  is_manual boolean [note: 'Indicates if the condition was entered manually versus system generated.']
  last_changed_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who last modified the condition.']
  max_quantity decimal(15,5) [note: 'Maximum quantity threshold for the condition to apply.']
  min_quantity decimal(15,5) [note: 'Minimum quantity threshold for the condition to apply.']
  price_condition_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the condition purpose or notes.']
  price_condition_group varchar [note: 'Group identifier for grouping related conditions.']
  price_condition_source varchar [note: 'Source system or origin of the condition record.']
  pricing_procedure varchar [note: 'Pricing procedure name that includes this condition.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority order for condition determination when multiple conditions apply.']
  rate decimal(15,5) [note: 'Monetary rate or amount defined by the condition.']
  scale_amount decimal(15,5) [note: 'Amount used for scaling calculations.']
  scale_basis varchar [note: 'Basis for scaling the condition (e.g., quantity thresholds).']
  surcharge_amount decimal(15,5) [note: 'Surcharge amount if condition adds a fee.']
  tax_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicator whether the condition amount is taxable.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the rate (e.g., per unit, per kg).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the last update to the condition record.']
  validity_end_date date [note: 'Date until which the condition is valid (null if open-ended).']
  validity_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the condition is valid.']

  Note: 'SAP SD-aligned pricing condition record (Konditionssatz) defining the pricing logic applied during order pricing determination. Captures condition type (PR00, K004, K005, RA01), condition table key combination (customer/material, price group/material, channel/material), rate, currency, UoM, scale basis, and validity period. The atomic unit of price determination in the pricing engine.'
}

Table pricing.procedure {
  procedure_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the pricing procedure record.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Pricing procedures are defined per legal entity; linking to company code enables entity‑specific pricing rules and compliance.']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Pricing procedures are authored by a pricing analyst; tracked for Procedure Creation log.']
  superseded_procedure_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on procedure (superseded_procedure_id)']
  allow_discount boolean [note: 'Specifies if discount conditions may be applied.']
  allow_surcharge boolean [note: 'Specifies if surcharge conditions may be applied.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the pricing procedure.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who approved the current version.']
  calculation_method varchar [note: 'Core algorithm used to compute the net price.']
  condition_type_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct condition types defined in the sequence.']
  condition_type_sequence varchar [note: 'Ordered list of condition types (steps) applied during price determination, stored as a delimited string.']
  cost_plus_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage added to standard cost when using a cost‑plus calculation method.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the pricing procedure record was created.']
  distribution_channel varchar [note: 'Channel (retail, foodservice, direct‑to‑consumer, online) for which the procedure is valid.']
  division varchar [note: 'Business division (e.g., Snacks, Beverages) linked to the procedure.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the pricing procedure becomes active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the pricing procedure expires (null for open‑ended).']
  include_freight boolean [note: 'Indicates whether freight costs are added to the net price.']
  include_tax boolean [note: 'Indicates whether tax is included in the net price calculation.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this procedure is the default for its sales org/channel.']
  max_condition_steps varchar [note: 'Maximum number of steps allowed in the condition sequence.']
  max_margin_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowed profit margin expressed as a percentage.']
  min_margin_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum allowed profit margin expressed as a percentage.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or additional information about the pricing procedure.']
  price_change_approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates if a price change must go through an approval workflow.']
  price_change_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the price change approval workflow.']
  price_change_approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the price change was approved.']
  price_change_approver varchar [note: 'User identifier of the approver for the price change.']
  price_change_effective_date date [note: 'Date on which a scheduled price change takes effect.']
  price_change_history_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether historical price changes are retained for audit.']
  price_change_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days required to notify customers of a price change.']
  price_change_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason for the price change (e.g., cost increase, promotion).']
  price_override_allowed boolean [note: 'Specifies whether sales users may manually override the calculated price.']
  pricing_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code used for the pricing calculations.']
  procedure_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the pricing procedure in transactions and master data.']
  procedure_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the pricing logic and business purpose.']
  procedure_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the pricing procedure.']
  procedure_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the pricing procedure.']
  procedure_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the procedure (e.g., standard list price, promotional, custom calculation).']
  rounding_rule varchar [note: 'Rounding rule applied to the final price.']
  sales_organization varchar [note: 'Sales organization to which the pricing procedure applies.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'System user who performed the last update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version of the pricing procedure for change management.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'System user who initially created the record.']

  Note: 'Master record defining the pricing procedure (calculation schema) used to determine the net selling price for a sales transaction. Captures procedure name, description, applicable sales organization, distribution channel, division, and the ordered sequence of condition types (steps) applied during price determination. Governs how list price, discounts, surcharges, freight, and taxes are combined to arrive at net price.'
}

Table pricing.procedure_step {
  procedure_step_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each step within a pricing procedure.']
  capacity_constraint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.capacity_constraint. Business justification: SURCHARGE CALCULATION: procedure step uses capacity constraint data to apply surcharges when lane capacity is limited.']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Each step in a pricing procedure is created by a specific employee; needed for Step Ownership reporting.']
  procedure_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent pricing procedure to which this step belongs.']
  reference_procedure_step_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on procedure_step (reference_procedure_step_id)']
  account_key varchar [note: 'Key that determines which GL account the pricing result posts to.']
  active_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the step.']
  calculation_type varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate the price impact (e.g., percentage, fixed amount).']
  calculation_value decimal(15,4) [note: 'Numeric value used by the calculation type (e.g., 5.0 for 5%).']
  condition_operator varchar [note: 'Logical operator used to evaluate the condition (e.g., =, >, <).']
  condition_type varchar [note: 'Type of pricing condition applied in this step (e.g., discount, surcharge).']
  condition_value decimal(15,4) [note: 'Numeric value against which the condition is evaluated.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the step record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code applicable to monetary values in the step.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the step becomes valid.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the step is no longer valid (null for open‑ended).']
  mandatory_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the step must be executed for the pricing procedure to be valid.']
  printed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the step should be displayed on pricing condition printouts.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority used when multiple steps have the same sequence; lower numbers run first.']
  procedure_step_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the step purpose and behavior.']
  requirement_routine varchar [note: 'Name of the ABAP routine that evaluates additional business logic for the step.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of origin for the step record (e.g., SAP, Oracle).']
  statistical_flag boolean [note: 'True if the step is used only for statistical reporting and does not affect the price.']
  step_category varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the step (e.g., condition, calculation, output).']
  step_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or label for the pricing step.']
  step_sequence varchar [note: 'Numeric order of the step within the pricing procedure.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the step record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for change management of the step definition.']

  Note: 'Individual step within a pricing procedure defining the sequence, condition type, from/to reference steps, requirement routine, calculation type, account key, and whether the step is mandatory, statistical, or printed. Enables full reconstruction of the pricing calculation schema for audit and troubleshooting.'
}

Table pricing.channel_price {
  channel_price_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the channel price record.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Unique product identifier used for inventory and sales tracking.']
  superseded_channel_price_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on channel_price (superseded_channel_price_id)']
  channel_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the commercial channel (e.g., retail, foodservice, direct-to-consumer).']
  channel_price_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the price record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the price record was initially created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the net price.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date on which the price is valid; null indicates an open‑ended price.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'First calendar date on which the price becomes valid.']
  minimum_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Minimum quantity that must be ordered to qualify for the channel price.']
  net_price decimal(12,4) [note: 'Authorized selling price for the SKU in the specified channel, expressed in the transaction currency.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments or business rules associated with the price.']
  price_change_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation for why the price was changed (e.g., cost increase, promotion launch).']
  price_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the price calculation method (e.g., cost‑plus, everyday low price, promotional, negotiated).']
  price_tier varchar [note: 'Classification of the price level (e.g., standard, premium, discount, promotional).']
  price_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the amount is a net price, list price, or promotional price.']
  price_version varchar [note: 'Sequential version number for the price record, incremented on each change.']
  uom varchar [note: 'Unit in which the net price is expressed (e.g., each, case, kilogram).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the price record.']

  Note: 'Channel-specific net price record capturing the authorized selling price for a SKU within a specific commercial channel (retail, foodservice, DTC, club, convenience, vending). Stores channel code, SKU, net price, currency, UoM, effective date range, and channel pricing tier. Enables channel-differentiated pricing strategies including EDLP (Everyday Low Price) for retail vs. cost-plus for foodservice vs. DTC direct pricing.'
}

Table pricing.cost_plus_model {
  cost_plus_model_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the cost-plus pricing model record.']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Cost‑plus models require approval by a pricing manager; needed for Model Approval compliance report.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Cost‑plus models allocate costs to a cost center to reflect internal cost allocation for margin calculations.']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list. Business justification: cost_plus_model is defined per price list; linking provides context and removes duplicated channel attribute.']
  superseded_cost_plus_model_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on cost_plus_model (superseded_cost_plus_model_id)']
  applicable_product_category varchar [note: 'Product category or segment to which the model is applicable.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Governance status indicating whether the model has been approved for use.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who approved the model.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the model received approval.']
  cost_plus_model_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the model purpose, assumptions, and scope.']
  cost_plus_model_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the pricing model.']
  cost_source_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the standard cost record used as the base cost for this model.']
  cost_type varchar [note: 'Type of cost used as the base for markup calculations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the model record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for all monetary values. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USD|EUR|GBP|CAD|MXN|JPY|CNY|AUD|CHF|SEK|NZD — promote to reference product]']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the cost-plus model becomes active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the model expires or is superseded; null if open‑ended.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this model is the default for its channel and category.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the model was last reviewed for relevance and accuracy.']
  markup_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage markup applied to the base cost to derive the target price.']
  maximum_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Ceiling price that the model will not exceed.']
  minimum_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Floor price enforced by the model to prevent under‑pricing.']
  model_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code assigned to the pricing model for reference in ERP and pricing systems.']
  model_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the cost-plus pricing model used by pricing analysts.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes or comments from pricing analysts.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the model has been validated against FDA/USDA pricing regulations.']
  review_cycle_months varchar [note: 'Number of months between mandatory model reviews.']
  target_contribution_margin_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Desired contribution margin percentage for the SKU or product category.']
  target_gross_margin_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Desired gross margin percentage that the model aims to achieve.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'System user identifier that performed the latest update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the model record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the pricing model for change management.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'System user identifier that created the model record.']

  Note: 'Cost-plus pricing model master record defining the markup methodology applied to COGS to derive target selling prices for a SKU or product category. Captures model name, applicable channel, base cost type (standard cost, moving average, contract cost), markup percentage, target gross margin %, target contribution margin %, and approval status. Links to finance standard cost for COGS input and drives floor price governance.'
}

Table pricing.promotional_price {
  promotional_price_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each promotional price record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: REQUIRED: Link promotional pricing to the specific customer account it targets; essential for account‑specific promotion execution and reporting.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Promotional prices are defined per marketing campaign to ensure compliance with FTC/FDA claims and budget tracking.']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Promotions are created by marketing employees; tracked for Promotion Creation audit.']
  label_approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.label_approval. Business justification: Promotions involving marketing claims need label approval verification; regulatory label approval is linked to promotional_price.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Unique product identifier (SKU, UPC, GTIN) to which the promotional price applies.']
  superseded_promotional_price_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on promotional_price (superseded_promotional_price_id)']
  channel varchar [note: 'Channel where the promotion is offered: retail stores, food‑service, direct‑to‑consumer, or online.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier who created the promotional price record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the promotional price record was created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the promotional price.']
  customer_segment varchar [note: 'Targeted customer segment for the promotion.']
  discount_amount decimal(12,4) [note: 'Absolute monetary discount applied to the base price.']
  discount_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied to the base price.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the promotional price expires.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the promotional price becomes active.']
  minimum_quantity varchar [note: 'Minimum quantity a customer must buy to qualify for the promotion.']
  price_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the promotional price is net, list, or gross.']
  price_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the promotional price (per unit, per weight, per volume).']
  promotion_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category of the promotion for reporting and analytics.']
  promotion_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code assigned to the promotion for tracking and reference.']
  promotion_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the promotion, including marketing copy and conditions.']
  promotion_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name describing the promotion.']
  promotion_source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that created the promotional price record.']
  promotion_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the promotion.']
  promotion_type varchar [note: 'Category of promotion such as Temporary Price Reduction (TPR), Buy‑One‑Get‑One (BOGO), multi‑buy, scan‑down, or display pricing.']
  promotional_price decimal(12,4) [note: 'Discounted price offered during the promotion, expressed in the transaction currency.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the promotion complies with FDA/USDA labeling and pricing regulations.']
  updated_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier who last updated the promotional price record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the promotional price record.']

  Note: 'Promotional pricing record capturing time-limited price reductions, TPR (Temporary Price Reductions), feature/display pricing, and deal pricing applied to SKUs for specific customers or channels. Captures promotion type (TPR, BOGO, multi-buy, scan-down), promotional price, discount amount/percentage, minimum purchase quantity, effective start/end dates, and redemption channel. Distinct from trade.promotion_event which tracks the trade promotion execution — this record owns the promotional price point itself.'
}

Table pricing.price_scale {
  price_scale_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the price scale record.']
  parent_price_scale_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on price_scale (parent_price_scale_id)']
  price_list_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list_line. Business justification: price_scale applies to specific price list lines; linking eliminates redundant channel and segment data.']
  base_price decimal(12,4) [note: 'Price applied to the lowest tier or default when no tier matches.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the price scale record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the price values (e.g., USD, EUR).']
  discount_type varchar [note: 'Method of discount applied in tiers – percentage or fixed amount.']
  discount_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Numeric value of the discount (percentage or amount) applied at tier level.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the price scale becomes valid for transactions.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the price scale expires; null for open‑ended.']
  is_promotional boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the price scale is part of a promotional pricing program.']
  price_change_approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the price scale change was approved.']
  price_change_approved_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manager who approved the price scale change.']
  price_change_reason varchar [note: 'Reason documented for creating or modifying the price scale (e.g., cost‑plus, market response).']
  price_scale_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description providing context or business rules for the price scale.']
  price_scale_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the price scale.']
  scale_basis varchar [note: 'Metric on which the tiering is based: quantity of units, weight, or monetary value.']
  scale_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the price scale in ERP and pricing systems.']
  scale_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name describing the price scale (e.g., "Volume Discount Tier 1").']
  scale_type varchar [note: 'Method of tier calculation – graduated (incremental) or from‑value (single break).']
  tier_count varchar [note: 'Total number of tier lines defined for this price scale.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit used for the scale basis (e.g., each, kg, liter).']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the price scale.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the price scale record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the price scale.']

  Note: 'Volume-based price scale (tiered pricing) record defining quantity break thresholds and corresponding unit prices or discount rates for a SKU or product group. Captures scale basis (quantity, weight, value), scale type (graduated, from-value), scale lines with lower bound quantities and corresponding prices/discounts, and applicable customer segment or channel. Enables volume incentive pricing and quantity discount structures.'
}

Table pricing.price_change_request {
  price_change_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the price change request.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: REQUIRED: Price change requests are initiated for a particular customer account; linking enables request tracking and impact analysis.']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who approved or rejected the request.']
  non_conformance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.non_conformance. Business justification: When a quality non‑conformance occurs, a price change request may be issued to reflect discounts or compensation, linking the request to the non‑conformance record.']
  primary_price_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who originated the price change request.']
  product_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.product_registration. Business justification: Price change requests are evaluated for regulatory compliance against the product\'s registration record.']
  purchase_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_contract. Business justification: Price change requests originate from contract renegotiations; linking ensures request is tied to the governing contract.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: Price change requests for new/reformulated products stem from R&D updates; linking ensures traceability for finance and compliance.']
  superseded_price_change_request_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on price_change_request (superseded_price_change_request_id)']
  channel varchar [note: 'Distribution channel(s) to which the price change applies.']
  competitive_rationale varchar [note: 'Explanation of competitive pressures or market conditions driving the price change.']
  cost_plus_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage markup over cost used to calculate the proposed price, if applicable.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the request record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency for the price values.']
  current_price decimal(12,4) [note: 'Existing price before the change is applied.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the approved price change becomes active in the market.']
  forecasted_margin_impact decimal(12,2) [note: 'Projected change in gross margin dollars resulting from the price change.']
  forecasted_sales_impact decimal(12,2) [note: 'Projected change in sales volume attributable to the price change.']
  justification_text varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation of the business reason for the price change.']
  margin_impact_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated impact on gross margin expressed as a percentage of sales.']
  market_segment varchar [note: 'Target consumer segment for the price change.']
  price_ceiling decimal(12,4) [note: 'Maximum allowable price for the SKU(s) based on market or regulatory limits.']
  price_change_amount decimal(12,4) [note: 'Numeric difference between proposed and current price (positive for increase, negative for decrease).']
  price_change_request_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the price change request.']
  price_floor decimal(12,4) [note: 'Minimum allowable price for the SKU(s) based on policy or cost constraints.']
  proposed_price decimal(12,4) [note: 'New price being requested for the affected SKU(s).']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter code representing the geographic region for the price change.']
  request_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable business identifier for the request, often used in communications and approvals.']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the price change request was initially submitted.']
  request_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the price change (e.g., new price, increase, decrease, promotional).']
  sku_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct SKUs impacted by this request.']
  total_sku_quantity varchar [note: 'Aggregate quantity of all affected SKUs (e.g., total units) referenced in the request.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the request record.']

  Note: 'Price change governance record capturing the full lifecycle of a proposed price change from initiation through approval and activation. Captures requestor, change type (new price, price increase, price decrease, promotional), affected SKUs, affected channels/customers, proposed price, current price, effective date, business justification, margin impact, competitive rationale, and approval workflow status. Enforces pricing governance and change control across the organization.'
}

Table pricing.price_approval {
  price_approval_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the price approval record.']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who made the approval decision.']
  escalated_from_price_approval_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on price_approval (escalated_from_price_approval_id)']
  price_change_request_id varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the price change request.']
  primary_price_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who submitted the price change request.']
  product_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.product_registration. Business justification: Price approval workflow checks product registration compliance before finalizing a price change.']
  purchase_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_contract. Business justification: Approval workflow must reference the purchase contract that defines the price being approved.']
  approval_deadline date [note: 'Date by which the approval must be completed.']
  approver_role varchar [note: 'Organizational role of the approver within the pricing governance hierarchy.']
  audit_trail_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the detailed audit log entry for this approval.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Channel to which the price change applies.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑text comments provided by the approver.']
  compliance_check_passed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the price change passed internal compliance validation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the approval record was created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the price values.']
  decision varchar [note: 'Final decision taken for the price change request.']
  decision_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the approval decision was recorded.']
  delegation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the approval was delegated to another approver.']
