  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the new price becomes effective.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the request was escalated beyond the current approver.']
  escalation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided for escalating the price change request.']
  new_price decimal(12,4) [note: 'Price amount after the change.']
  previous_price decimal(12,4) [note: 'Price amount before the change.']
  price_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the approval workflow.']
  price_change_amount decimal(12,4) [note: 'Monetary difference between new and previous price.']
  price_change_category varchar [note: 'Higher‑level categorization of the price change.']
  price_change_impact_currency varchar [note: 'Currency code for the impact estimate.']
  price_change_impact_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the estimated impact.']
  price_change_impact_estimate decimal(12,4) [note: 'Projected financial impact (e.g., revenue effect) of the price change.']
  price_change_justification varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explaining the business need for the price adjustment.']
  price_change_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage change between new and previous price.']
  price_change_reason varchar [note: 'Business justification for the price adjustment.']
  price_change_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the price change rationale.']
  request_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the price change request.']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the price change request was submitted.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the approval record.']

  Note: 'Approval workflow record for price change requests, capturing each approval step, approver identity, approval level (manager, director, VP, CFO), decision (approved, rejected, escalated), decision date, comments, and delegation details. Provides full audit trail for pricing governance and SOX compliance on price change authorization.'
}

Table pricing.pricing_price_history {
  pricing_price_history_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each price change history record.']
  previous_pricing_price_history_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on pricing_price_history (previous_pricing_price_history_id)']
  price_change_request_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the originating price change request.']
  product_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.product_registration. Business justification: Historical price changes are audited for compliance; linking to product_registration enables traceability.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the SKU whose price changed.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the price change was approved.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the approver.']
  change_amount decimal(12,4) [note: 'Absolute difference between new price and previous price.']
  change_percent decimal(6,2) [note: 'Percentage change between new price and previous price.']
  change_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code describing why the price was changed.']
  channel_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the sales channel where the price is applied.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the price history record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the price values.']
  customer_segment_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the customer segment or market segment for the price.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the new price becomes effective.']
  event_source varchar [note: 'Source system that generated the price change event.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the price change event was recorded in the system.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the event; always \'price_change\' for this table.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the price ceases to be valid; null if open‑ended.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this price record is currently active (true) or superseded (false).']
  new_price decimal(12,4) [note: 'Price of the SKU after the change, expressed in the base currency.']
  previous_price decimal(12,4) [note: 'Price of the SKU before the change, expressed in the base currency.']
  price_change_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of the price change request.']
  price_change_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category describing the pricing strategy applied.']
  price_change_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the price change was applied.']
  price_change_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes explaining context or details of the price change.']
  price_change_origin varchar [note: 'Origin of the price change request (internal business decision or external mandate).']
  price_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the price (e.g., regular, promotional, clearance).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the price history record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person or process that created the record.']

  Note: 'Historical price change audit record capturing every price change event for a SKU across all channels and customer segments. Stores previous price, new price, change amount, change percentage, effective date, expiry date, change reason code, and the originating price change request reference. Enables price trend analysis, regulatory audit support, and competitive pricing benchmarking over time.'
}

Table pricing.customer_price_agreement {
  customer_price_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the customer price agreement record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer party to which the agreement applies.']
  price_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_zone. Business justification: customer_price_agreement is scoped to a price zone; linking removes duplicated region and channel fields.']
  renewed_customer_price_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on customer_price_agreement (renewed_customer_price_agreement_id)']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product or SKU covered by the agreement.']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'External reference number or code assigned to the pricing agreement.']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the agreement (e.g., contract, blanket, spot, volume).']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the agreement.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the user who approved the agreement.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the agreement was approved.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the agreement record was created.']
  customer_price_agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the pricing agreement.']
  discount_type varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate the discount (percentage, fixed amount, or none).']
  discount_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Value of the discount expressed as a percentage or fixed amount, based on discount_type.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the agreement becomes binding.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the agreement expires or is terminated (null for open‑ended).']
  min_order_quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum quantity that can be ordered per transaction under the agreement.']
  net_price decimal(12,4) [note: 'Negotiated net price per unit after discounts.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments or remarks related to the agreement.']
  price_agreement_effective_quantity_max decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum quantity threshold for the price to apply.']
  price_agreement_effective_quantity_min decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum quantity threshold for the price to apply.']
  price_agreement_revision varchar [note: 'Revision sequence within a version, used for minor updates.']
  price_agreement_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic scope of the agreement (global, regional, or local).']
  price_agreement_target_customer_type varchar [note: 'Category of the customer party (retailer, foodservice, distributor, etc.).']
  price_agreement_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the agreement record for change tracking.']
  price_basis varchar [note: 'Underlying methodology used to calculate the price (cost‑plus, market, list, or custom).']
  price_change_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation for any price adjustments made during the agreement lifecycle.']
  price_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the net price.']
  price_margin_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target margin percentage applied to the base cost to derive the net price.']
  price_source_system varchar [note: 'Source system where the price agreement originated.']
  price_tax_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether tax is already included in the net price.']
  price_tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax rate (percentage) for the agreement.']
  price_tier_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the price tier.']
  price_tier_level varchar [note: 'Numeric tier identifier for tiered pricing structures.']
  price_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the net price (e.g., per case, per kilogram, per liter).']
  price_validity_status varchar [note: 'Current validity state of the price (e.g., current, expired, upcoming).']
  renewal_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for agreement renewal, if applicable.']
  renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement is set for automatic renewal.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided for early termination or non‑renewal of the agreement.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the agreement.']
  volume_commitment decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum purchase volume the customer commits to under the agreement.']

  Note: 'Customer-specific pricing agreement record capturing negotiated net prices, special pricing arrangements, and contract pricing for key accounts (retailers, foodservice chains, distributors). Captures customer account, SKU or product group, agreed net price, discount structure, volume commitment, effective date range, agreement type (contract, blanket, spot), and approval status. Distinct from sales.contract (commercial agreement) — this record owns the specific price terms within that agreement.'
}

Table pricing.price_exception {
  price_exception_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the pricing exception record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee or user who approved the pricing exception.']
  order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales order associated with the pricing exception.']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the authorized price list or contract governing the transaction.']
  related_price_exception_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on price_exception (related_price_exception_id)']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product or SKU affected by the pricing exception.']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the underlying transaction (e.g., invoice) where the exception applied.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the exception was formally approved.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the transaction occurred (retail, foodservice, direct‑to‑consumer).']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the exception complies with internal pricing governance policies.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the exception record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the exception amount.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the approved exception becomes effective.']
  exception_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Monetary deviation amount from the authorized price.']
  exception_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the exception for external reference and tracking.']
  exception_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage deviation of the transaction price from the authorized price.']
  exception_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the underlying reason for the exception.']
  exception_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the pricing exception was recorded.']
  exception_type varchar [note: 'Category of the pricing deviation (e.g., below floor price, above ceiling price, outside agreement).']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the exception is no longer valid, if applicable.']
  is_manual_override boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the exception required a manual price override.']
  justification varchar [note: 'Business reason provided for granting the pricing exception.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or additional information about the exception.']
  price_exception_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the pricing exception.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the exception record.']

  Note: 'Record of approved pricing exceptions where a transaction price deviates from the authorized price list or customer price agreement. Captures exception type (below floor price, above ceiling, outside agreement), exception amount, exception percentage, business justification, approver, approval date, and the specific order or transaction reference. Enables exception tracking, leakage identification, and pricing discipline enforcement.'
}

Table pricing.competitor_price {
  competitor_price_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each competitor price observation record.']
  previous_competitor_price_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on competitor_price (previous_competitor_price_id)']
  price_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_zone. Business justification: competitor_price observations are tied to a geographic price zone; linking enables zone‑based analysis.']
  store_id varchar [note: 'Internal identifier of the specific store or location where the price was captured.']
  competitor_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the competitor brand or company offering the observed product.']
  competitor_sku_code varchar [note: 'Identifier used by the competitor for the product (e.g., UPC, GTIN, SKU).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the price record was first loaded into the data lake.']
  data_capture_method varchar [note: 'Method used to capture the price observation.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the observed price ceased to be effective (if known).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the observed price became effective for the competitor.']
  geography_region varchar [note: 'Broad geographic region (e.g., North America, EMEA) for analytical grouping.']
  is_promotion boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the observed price is a promotional price (true) or regular price (false).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations about the price record.']
  observation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the competitor price was captured.']
  observed_price decimal(12,4) [note: 'Base retail price observed for the competitor product (excluding promotions).']
  price_change_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason for the price change (e.g., seasonal, cost‑plus, competitor action).']
  price_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code of the observed price.']
  price_per_unit decimal(12,4) [note: 'Derived price per standard unit (e.g., per ounce, per liter).']
  price_per_volume decimal(12,4) [note: 'Derived price per volume measure where applicable.']
  price_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the price data (e.g., Nielsen, IRI, field observation, web scrape).']
  price_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the observed price record.']
  price_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the price (e.g., per unit, per ounce, per liter).']
  product_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category or segment to which the observed product belongs.']
  product_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the product observed in the competitor\'s offering.']
  promotion_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the promotion applied to the price.']
  promotional_price decimal(12,4) [note: 'Discounted price observed when the product was on promotion.']
  retailer_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the retailer sells (e.g., retail store, foodservice, direct‑to‑consumer, online).']
  retailer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the retail outlet or chain where the price was observed.']
  shelf_price decimal(12,4) [note: 'Price displayed on the shelf label, may include taxes or fees.']
  store_city varchar [note: 'City of the store location.']
  store_country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the store location.']
  store_state varchar [note: 'State or province of the store location.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the price record.']

  Note: 'Competitive price intelligence record capturing observed market prices for competitor products at specific retail accounts, channels, or geographies. Captures competitor brand, competitor SKU description, observed retail price, observed promotional price, shelf price, price per unit/oz/liter, data source (syndicated Nielsen/IRI, field observation, web scrape), observation date, and retailer/channel. Feeds competitive pricing strategy and price gap analysis.'
}

Table pricing.retail_shelf_price {
  retail_shelf_price_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each retail shelf price record.']
  previous_retail_shelf_price_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on retail_shelf_price (previous_retail_shelf_price_id)']
  primary_retail_promotional_price_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the promotion linked to this price, if applicable.']
  retailer_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the retailer account (e.g., chain or banner) responsible for the store.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Internal product identifier used for inventory and sales tracking.']
  store_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the retail store where the price was observed.']
  actual_price decimal(12,4) [note: 'Price observed on the shelf at the time of capture.']
  competitor_price decimal(12,4) [note: 'Average competitor price used for gap calculation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the price record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the price values.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which the price is no longer valid; null if open‑ended.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the price is considered effective.']
  gtin varchar [note: 'Global identifier for trade items, may be 14‑digit.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations about the price record.']
  price_change_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for a price adjustment (e.g., promotion, cost increase, competitor match).']
  price_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the shelf price was captured.']
  price_elasticity_flag boolean [note: 'True if the price observation is used in elasticity modeling.']
  price_gap_to_competition decimal(12,4) [note: 'Difference between actual shelf price and the average competitor price for the same SKU.']
  price_per_oz decimal(12,4) [note: 'Price expressed per fluid ounce or ounce, used for volume‑based comparison.']
  price_per_unit decimal(12,4) [note: 'Price expressed per standard unit of measure (e.g., per pack, per case).']
  price_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the price data (e.g., internal audit, third‑party data feed, POS system, manual entry).']
  price_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the price record.']
  price_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the price is a recommended retail price (RRP) or the actual observed shelf price.']
  promotion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the price is associated with an active promotion.']
  recommended_price decimal(12,4) [note: 'Manufacturer‑recommended price for the SKU at the store.']
  uom varchar [note: 'Standard unit of measure for the price (e.g., each, pack, case).']
  upc varchar [note: '12‑digit barcode identifier for the product.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the price record.']

  Note: 'Retail shelf price (RSP) record capturing the actual consumer-facing shelf price observed or recommended for Food Beverage SKUs at retail accounts. Captures retailer account, store banner, SKU, recommended retail price (RRP), actual observed shelf price, price per unit, price per oz/liter, shelf price date, and data source. Enables RSP compliance monitoring, price gap to competition, and consumer price elasticity inputs.'
}

Table pricing.price_elasticity {
  price_elasticity_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the price elasticity record.']
  previous_price_elasticity_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on price_elasticity (previous_price_elasticity_id)']
  price_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_zone. Business justification: price_elasticity estimates are zone‑specific; linking removes duplicated channel and market region fields.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Unique product identifier (SKU) to which the elasticity applies.']
  confidence_interval_high decimal(10,4) [note: 'Upper bound of the statistical confidence interval for the elasticity estimate.']
  confidence_interval_low decimal(10,4) [note: 'Lower bound of the statistical confidence interval for the elasticity estimate.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the elasticity record was first created in the system.']
  cross_competitor_sku varchar [note: 'SKU of a key competitor product used for cross‑price elasticity calculation.']
  cross_price_elasticity decimal(10,4) [note: 'Estimated demand sensitivity to price changes of the competitor product (cross‑elasticity).']
  elasticity_model_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the elasticity estimation model used.']
  estimation_method varchar [note: 'Methodology used to estimate elasticity (econometric, machine learning, or hybrid).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or business notes about the elasticity record.']
  own_price_elasticity decimal(10,4) [note: 'Estimated demand sensitivity to changes in the product\'s own price (percentage change in quantity per percentage change in price).']
  price_elasticity_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the elasticity record.']
  price_threshold decimal(10,2) [note: 'Psychological price point at which demand behavior changes sharply.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that generated the elasticity estimate.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the elasticity record.']
  validity_end_date date [note: 'Date after which the elasticity estimate expires (null if open‑ended).']
  validity_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the elasticity estimate is considered valid.']

  Note: 'Price elasticity parameter record capturing the estimated demand sensitivity to price changes for a SKU within a specific channel and market. Stores own-price elasticity coefficient, cross-price elasticity with key competitors, price threshold (psychological price points), elasticity model version, estimation method (econometric, ML), confidence interval, and validity period. Used as input to pricing optimization and revenue management decisions — this is a business parameter record, not an analytics output.'
}

Table pricing.surcharge_rule {
  surcharge_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each surcharge rule.']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Surcharge rules are created by pricing staff; required for Surcharge Rule Creation tracking.']
  parent_surcharge_rule_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on surcharge_rule (parent_surcharge_rule_id)']
  price_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_zone. Business justification: surcharge_rule definitions are scoped to price zones; linking removes duplicated scope attributes.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the surcharge rule.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who approved the rule.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the rule was approved.']
  calculation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate the surcharge amount.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the surcharge complies with relevant regulations (e.g., FDA, GFSI).']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Internal cost center responsible for the surcharge.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the rule record was created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary fields.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the surcharge rule expires (null for open‑ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the surcharge rule becomes active.']
  flat_fee_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Fixed monetary amount applied when calculation_method is flat_fee.']
  is_stackable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this surcharge can be combined with other surcharges.']
  minimum_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Minimum quantity required for the surcharge to apply.']
  minimum_order_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for minimum_order_quantity (e.g., case, pallet).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional remarks or operational notes.']
  percent_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage applied to the base price when calculation_method is percentage.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Integer indicating the order in which rules are evaluated (lower = higher priority).']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Status of regulatory review for the surcharge rule.']
  responsible_owner varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the owner of the surcharge rule.']
  rule_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the surcharge rule in pricing systems.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name describing the surcharge rule.']
  surcharge_rule_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the surcharge rule purpose and logic.']
  surcharge_rule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the surcharge rule.']
  surcharge_type varchar [note: 'Category of surcharge applied (e.g., fuel, small order, cold chain handling).']
  tax_applicable boolean [note: 'True if the surcharge is subject to tax.']
  tax_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Tax rate applied to the surcharge amount when tax_applicable is true.']
  threshold_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary threshold that must be met for the surcharge to trigger (e.g., minimum order value).']
  threshold_unit varchar [note: 'Currency of the threshold_amount.']
  tier_structure varchar [note: 'JSON‑encoded definition of tier thresholds and corresponding fees for tiered calculation_method.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the rule.']

  Note: 'Master record defining surcharges, fees, and uplifts applied on top of base prices — including fuel surcharges, small order surcharges, cold chain handling fees, minimum order value fees, and DTC fulfillment fees. Captures surcharge type, calculation method (flat fee, percentage, tiered), applicable channel, applicable customer segment, threshold conditions, effective date range, and approval status.'
}

Table pricing.discount_rule {
  discount_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the discount rule record.']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Discount rules are authored by pricing analysts; needed for Discount Rule Ownership report.']
  parent_discount_rule_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on discount_rule (parent_discount_rule_id)']
  price_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_zone. Business justification: discount_rule applicability is defined per price zone; linking consolidates channel and region data.']
  applicable_customer_segment varchar [note: 'Customer segment (e.g., retail, foodservice, DTC) to which the discount rule may be applied.']
  applicable_product_category varchar [note: 'Product category or family for which the discount is valid.']
  applicable_product_sku varchar [note: 'Specific SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) that the discount can be applied to; blank means all SKUs in the category.']
  approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the discount rule must be approved before activation.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the discount rule received approval.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who approved the discount rule.']
  calculation_basis varchar [note: 'Base price element on which the discount is applied (e.g., net price, list price, cost‑plus).']
  compliance_flag varchar [note: 'Regulatory or certification requirement associated with the discount rule.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the discount rule record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for monetary values in the rule.']
  discount_rule_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the discount rule purpose and application.']
  discount_rule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the discount rule.']
  discount_type varchar [note: 'Category of discount calculation method (e.g., percentage off, fixed amount, per case, tiered).']
  discount_unit varchar [note: 'Unit that qualifies discount_value (percent, currency amount, or per‑case amount).']
  discount_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Numeric value of the discount; interpreted according to discount_unit (percentage, currency amount, or per‑case amount).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which the discount rule is no longer valid; null indicates open‑ended.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the discount rule becomes valid.']
  is_stackable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this discount can be combined with other discounts.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the discount rule was last reviewed for relevance or compliance.']
  maximum_discount_cap decimal(12,2) [note: 'Upper limit on the total discount amount that can be granted per transaction.']
  minimum_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Minimum number of units that must be ordered for the discount to be eligible.']
  minimum_order_value decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum monetary value of the order required for the discount to apply.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional remarks, exceptions, or operational notes.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Relative priority of the rule when multiple discounts are applicable; lower numbers indicate higher priority.']
  regulatory_approval_required boolean [note: 'True if the discount rule must be reviewed by regulatory compliance before activation.']
  rule_code varchar [note: 'Human‑readable code used to reference the discount rule in pricing systems.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the discount rule for reporting and UI display.']
  stack_order varchar [note: 'Sequence order for applying stackable discounts; lower numbers apply first.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who performed the latest update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the discount rule record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the discount rule for change management.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who initially created the discount rule.']

  Note: 'Master record defining authorized discount structures applicable to sales transactions — including off-invoice discounts, cash discounts, early payment discounts, new distribution discounts, and loyalty discounts. Captures discount type, calculation basis (percentage, fixed amount, per-case), applicable customer group, applicable product group, minimum order quantity/value, maximum discount cap, effective date range, and approval authority.'
}

Table pricing.transfer_price {
  transfer_price_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the intercompany transfer price record.']
  cost_plus_model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.cost_plus_model. Business justification: transfer_price uses cost‑plus percentages defined in cost_plus_model; linking removes redundant percentage field.']
  material_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the material or SKU being transferred.']
  primary_transfer_company_code_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the legal entity (company code) that supplies the goods or materials.']
  receiving_entity_company_code_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the legal entity (company code) that receives the goods or materials.']
  superseded_transfer_price_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on transfer_price (superseded_transfer_price_id)']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who approved the transfer price.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the transfer price was approved.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the transfer price record was initially created in the system.']
  cup_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the comparable uncontrolled price source or transaction used for the CUP method.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency in which the transfer price is expressed.']
  documentation_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier (e.g., document number or URL) to the supporting tax authority documentation.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the transfer price expires or is superseded; null if open‑ended.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the transfer price becomes effective for intercompany transactions.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional comments or special conditions related to the transfer price.']
  price_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Authorized transfer price amount expressed in the transaction currency.']
  price_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code assigned to the transfer price agreement for reference in contracts and reporting.']
  price_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the price (intercompany, internal transfer, external market).']
  price_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the transfer price record to track revisions over time.']
  pricing_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate the transfer price (e.g., Cost‑Plus, Resale‑Minus, Comparable Uncontrolled Price, Transactional Net Margin Method).']
  reason varchar [note: 'Business rationale for establishing or changing the transfer price (e.g., cost alignment, market parity).']
  resale_minus_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Fixed amount subtracted from the resale price when the Resale‑Minus method is used.']
  sku_code varchar [note: 'Stock Keeping Unit code that uniquely identifies the product for inventory and pricing.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source ERP or planning system where the record originated (e.g., SAP S/4HANA, Oracle ERP).']
  tax_authority_code varchar [note: 'Two‑letter code of the tax authority that governs the transfer pricing documentation.']
  tnmm_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the data set or benchmark used for the Transactional Net Margin Method.']
  transfer_price_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the transfer price (e.g., active, inactive, pending approval, expired, draft).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the transfer price record.']

  Note: 'Intercompany transfer pricing record defining the authorized prices at which finished goods, raw materials, and semi-finished products are transferred between Food Beverage legal entities (company codes) across geographies. Captures sending entity, receiving entity, material/SKU, transfer price, currency, pricing method (cost-plus, resale minus, CUP, TNMM), effective date range, and tax authority documentation reference. Critical for OECD transfer pricing compliance and intercompany P&L integrity.'
}

Table pricing.price_simulation {
  price_simulation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the price simulation record.']
  baseline_price_simulation_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on price_simulation (baseline_price_simulation_id)']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Price simulations are run to forecast revenue impact of a specific marketing campaign; linking ties simulation results to the campaign.']
  formulation_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.formulation_version. Business justification: Simulations are run on a specific formulation version to forecast margin, revenue, and competitive response.']
  price_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_zone. Business justification: price_simulation scenarios are scoped to price zones; linking removes duplicated channel, segment, and category attributes.']
  primary_price_employee_id varchar [note: 'Internal user responsible for the simulation.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Price simulations impact profit center forecasts; linking to profit center aligns simulated margins with financial planning.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'SKU of the product being simulated.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the simulation.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User who approved the simulation.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the simulation was approved.']
  competitive_response_assumption varchar [note: 'Assumed competitive reaction (e.g., price cuts, promotions) used in the simulation.']
  cost_plus_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Cost‑plus markup percentage applied in the scenario, if applicable.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the simulation record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three‑letter currency code for the proposed price.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Source system that originated the simulation data (e.g., SAP, Oracle).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the proposed price would cease to be effective.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the proposed price would become effective.']
  estimated_margin decimal(12,4) [note: 'Projected gross margin amount (currency) for the scenario.']
  estimated_revenue decimal(14,2) [note: 'Projected revenue generated by the scenario.']
  estimated_volume_units decimal(12,2) [note: 'Projected sales volume under the scenario (in the specified unit).']
  forecasted_impact varchar [note: 'Narrative of expected market impact from the simulation.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent review of the simulation.']
  margin_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Projected margin expressed as a percentage of revenue.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the simulation.']
  price_change_reason varchar [note: 'Business rationale for the proposed price change.']
  price_simulation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the simulation.']
  proposed_price decimal(12,4) [note: 'Proposed selling price per unit in the scenario.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the simulation complies with relevant pricing regulations.']
  scenario_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the pricing scenario purpose and assumptions.']
  scenario_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the pricing scenario.']
  scenario_status_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for the current status of the simulation.']
  sensitivity_analysis_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if a sensitivity analysis was conducted.']
  sensitivity_range_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage range used in sensitivity analysis.']
  simulation_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the simulation, used for external reference.']
  simulation_date date [note: 'Date on which the simulation was executed.']
  simulation_scenario_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the scenario type.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User who performed the most recent update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the simulation record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version of the simulation record for change tracking.']
  volume_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the estimated volume.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User who created the simulation record.']

  Note: 'Price simulation and what-if scenario record capturing proposed pricing scenarios evaluated before a price change is submitted for approval. Captures scenario name, simulation date, proposed price points, estimated volume impact, estimated revenue impact, estimated margin impact, competitive response assumptions, and simulation status (draft, reviewed, submitted). Enables data-driven pricing decisions and supports the price change governance workflow.'
}

Table pricing.foodservice_price {
  foodservice_price_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the foodservice price record.']
  price_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_zone. Business justification: foodservice_price varies by price zone and segment; linking consolidates these attributes.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Unique product identifier for which the price is defined.']
  superseded_foodservice_price_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on foodservice_price (superseded_foodservice_price_id)']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval state of the price record.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User or system that approved the price.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the price was approved.']
  broadline_price decimal(12,4) [note: 'Price set by a broadline distributor for the SKU.']
  broadline_price_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the broadline price.']
  case_price decimal(12,4) [note: 'Authorized net price for a full case of the SKU.']
  case_price_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the case price (typically \'case\').']
  contract_price decimal(12,4) [note: 'Price agreed under a specific contract with the operator or distributor.']
  contract_price_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the contract price.']
  cost_plus_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Markup percentage applied over cost to derive the price.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the price record was created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the price.']
  distributor varchar [note: 'Name or code of the distributor providing the product to the foodservice channel.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the price expires or is superseded.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the price becomes effective.']
  foodservice_price_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the price record.']
  margin_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target gross margin percentage for the price.']
  minimum_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Minimum quantity required to qualify for this price.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-form comments about the price.']
  operator_segment varchar [note: 'Segment of the foodservice operator (e.g., Quick Service Restaurant, Food Service Restaurant).']
  portion_price decimal(12,4) [note: 'Authorized net price for a single portion of the SKU.']
  portion_price_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the portion price.']
  price_calculation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate the price.']
  price_category varchar [note: 'Business category of the price.']
  price_ceiling_amount decimal(12,4) [note: 'Maximum allowable price for the SKU.']
  price_change_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for the most recent price change.']
  price_floor_amount decimal(12,4) [note: 'Minimum allowable price for the SKU.']
  price_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the price data.']
  price_source_system varchar [note: 'System of record that supplied the price data.']
  price_tier varchar [note: 'Tier classification for volume or contract based pricing.']
  price_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the price (e.g., net, gross, contract).']
  price_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the price record for change tracking.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the price complies with applicable regulations (e.g., FDA labeling).']
  split_case_price decimal(12,4) [note: 'Authorized net price for a split case (half case) of the SKU.']
  split_case_price_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the split-case price.']
  uom varchar [note: 'Standard unit of measure for price values.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the price record.']

  Note: 'Foodservice-specific pricing record capturing authorized net prices for SKUs sold through foodservice channels (QSR, FSR, healthcare, education, lodging, vending). Captures operator segment, distributor, SKU, case price, split-case price, portion price, contract price, broadline distributor price, and effective date range. Foodservice pricing is structurally distinct from retail — governed by distributor cost-plus models, operator contracts, and broadline pricing agreements.'
}

Table pricing.dtc_price {
  dtc_price_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for the DTC price record.']
  price_rule_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the pricing rule or algorithm that generated this price.']
  price_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_zone. Business justification: dtc_price is channel‑specific; linking to price_zone eliminates duplicated channel attribute.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Unique product code for which the DTC price applies.']
  superseded_dtc_price_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on dtc_price (superseded_dtc_price_id)']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person who approved the price.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the price received approval.']
  bundle_price decimal(12,4) [note: 'Price for a pre‑defined product bundle sold through DTC.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the price record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the price values.']
  dtc_price_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the price record purpose or scope.']
  dynamic_pricing_algorithm varchar [note: 'Algorithm type used for dynamic pricing (rule‑based, machine‑learning, or manual).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the price ceases to be valid (null for open‑ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the price becomes valid for the DTC channel.']
  is_dynamic boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the price is subject to dynamic adjustment.']
  margin_floor decimal(12,4) [note: 'Minimum gross margin that must be achieved for the price.']
  member_price decimal(12,4) [note: 'Discounted price offered to loyalty‑program members.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments or business context for the price record.']
  price_approval_status varchar [note: 'Governance status indicating whether the price has been approved.']
  price_category varchar [note: 'Business classification of the price (standard, promotional, clearance).']
  price_ceiling_amount decimal(12,4) [note: 'Maximum allowable price for the SKU in the DTC channel.']
  price_change_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation for why the price was changed.']
  price_code varchar [note: 'Business‑level code used to reference the price record in external processes.']
  price_effective_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the price is scheduled to be active (derived from start/end dates).']
  price_effective_flag boolean [note: 'True if the price is currently active based on effective dates.']
  price_floor_amount decimal(12,4) [note: 'Minimum allowable price for the SKU in the DTC channel.']
  price_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next price review.']
  price_source varchar [note: 'Originating system or process that supplied the price.']
  price_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the price record.']
  price_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the price (regular, member, subscriber, or bundle).']
  price_uom varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the price (e.g., per unit, per kilogram).']
  price_version varchar [note: 'Sequential version number for change tracking.']
  regular_price decimal(12,4) [note: 'Standard price charged to non‑member consumers.']
  subscriber_price decimal(12,4) [note: 'Price applied to customers enrolled in a subscription service.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the price record.']

  Note: 'Direct-to-consumer (DTC) pricing record capturing authorized prices for SKUs sold through owned e-commerce, subscription, and DTC channels. Captures SKU, DTC channel (website, app, subscription box, marketplace), regular price, member price, subscriber price, bundle price, currency, effective date range, and margin floor. DTC pricing is distinct from retail and foodservice — governed by consumer psychology, dynamic pricing rules, and subscription economics.'
}

Table pricing.price_zone {
  price_zone_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the price zone master record.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Price zones are often defined per company code to reflect regional pricing policies within each legal entity.']
  parent_price_zone_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on price_zone (parent_price_zone_id)']
  parent_zone_price_zone_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent price zone in the hierarchical structure, if any.']
  applicable_channels varchar [note: 'Pipe‑separated list of sales channels where the zone pricing is valid.']
  confidentiality_flag varchar [note: 'Data classification level for the price zone record.']
  country_codes varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of ISO‑3 country codes included in the zone.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the price zone record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code used for pricing in the zone.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the price zone ceases to be active (null if open‑ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the price zone becomes active for pricing calculations.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Level of geographic granularity the zone applies to.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level indicating depth in the zone hierarchy (1 = top level).']
  is_default boolean [note: 'True if this zone is the default fallback for pricing calculations.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent governance review of the zone.']
  maximum_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Ceiling price that cannot be exceeded for any SKU in this zone.']
  metro_area varchar [note: 'Metro area name (e.g., New York City) covered by the zone.']
  minimum_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Floor price that cannot be undercut for any SKU in this zone.']
  nielsen_market varchar [note: 'Nielsen market identifier used for analytics and pricing alignment.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or business rationale for the zone.']
  price_change_approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a price change for this zone must be approved.']
  price_change_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of the pending price change.']
  price_change_approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the price change was approved or rejected.']
  price_change_approver varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who approved the price change.']
  price_change_effective_date date [note: 'Date on which a scheduled price change becomes effective.']
  price_change_history_flag boolean [note: 'True if historical price change records are retained for this zone.']
  price_change_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days required to notify stakeholders before a price change takes effect.']
  price_change_reason varchar [note: 'Business rationale for the price change (e.g., cost increase, market competition).']
  price_multiplier decimal(10,4) [note: 'Factor applied to base price when pricing within this zone (e.g., 1.10 for 10% uplift).']
  price_zone_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the price zone in pricing rules and systems.']
  price_zone_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the zone\'s purpose and characteristics.']
  price_zone_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the geographic price zone.']
  price_zone_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the price zone.']
  price_zone_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the zone based on its primary differentiation driver.']
  pricing_authority varchar [note: 'Organizational unit or role responsible for approving pricing within the zone.']
  region varchar [note: 'Name of the region (e.g., Midwest, South‑East) covered by the zone.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the zone complies with all applicable pricing regulations.']
  review_cycle_months varchar [note: 'Number of months between mandatory reviews of the price zone.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that created or maintains the price zone record (e.g., SAP S/4HANA).']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province name included in the zone.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the price zone record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the price zone definition.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system account that created the price zone record.']

  Note: 'Geographic price zone master record defining regional pricing territories where differentiated pricing is authorized. Captures zone name, zone code, geographic scope (country, region, state/province, metro area, Nielsen market), applicable channels, pricing authority, and zone hierarchy. Enables geographic price differentiation strategies — e.g., premium pricing in high-cost metros vs. value pricing in price-sensitive rural markets.'
}

Table pricing.price_waterfall {
  price_waterfall_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each price waterfall step record.']
  price_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_zone. Business justification: price_waterfall steps are tied to a price zone; linking eliminates redundant channel, segment, and region data.']
  reference_price_waterfall_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on price_waterfall (reference_price_waterfall_id)']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent transaction (order, invoice, or sales event) to which this waterfall step belongs.']
  amount_allowance decimal(18,4) [note: 'Off‑invoice or trade allowance value applied at this step.']
  amount_discount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total discount value applied at this step.']
  amount_gross decimal(18,4) [note: 'Monetary amount before any discounts, allowances, or taxes are applied.']
  amount_net decimal(18,4) [note: 'Resulting amount after this step\'s adjustments; the net‑to‑net price at the bottom of the waterfall.']
  amount_tax decimal(18,4) [note: 'Tax component (e.g., sales tax, VAT) applied at this step.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the price step.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who approved the price step.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the price step received approval.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the price waterfall step record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for all monetary amounts in this record.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this price step ceases to be effective (null if open‑ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this price step becomes effective for the transaction.']
  is_manual_adjustment boolean [note: 'True if the step was entered or modified manually rather than by automated logic.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or business rationale for the price step.']
  price_change_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative description of why the price amount was changed.']
  price_change_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent price change affecting this step.']
  price_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the price value (system‑generated, manually entered, or external feed).']
  price_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the price (e.g., list price, net price, promotional price, contract price).']
  price_waterfall_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the price step record.']
  quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Quantity of the SKU or service to which the price step applies.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating why this price step was applied.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the price step complies with applicable regulations (e.g., FDA pricing rules).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that supplied the price step data.']
  step_category varchar [note: 'Broad category of the step used for analytics and reporting.']
  step_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the waterfall step (e.g., Gross List Price, Trade Promotion Discount).']
  step_sequence varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of the step within the price waterfall, starting at 1 for the gross list price.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the price waterfall step record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version of the price step record for audit and change tracking.']

  Note: 'Price waterfall record capturing the step-by-step decomposition of gross-to-net price for a specific transaction, customer, or period. Captures gross list price, invoice discounts, off-invoice allowances, trade promotion accruals, cash discounts, freight allowances, and net-net price at each waterfall step. Enables pocket price analysis, net revenue management (NRM), and identification of price leakage across the commercial value chain.'
}

Table pricing.revenue_realization {
  revenue_realization_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the revenue realization record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Internal identifier of the customer or account associated with the transaction.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Revenue realization records need the GL account used for posting net revenue to the general ledger.']
  price_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_zone. Business justification: revenue_realization records are associated with a price zone for regional revenue reporting.']
  prior_period_revenue_realization_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on revenue_realization (prior_period_revenue_realization_id)']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Internal identifier of the SKU for which net revenue is recorded.']
  allowance_flag boolean [note: 'True if allowance amounts are applied.']
  case_size_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of individual units contained in one case.']
  cost_plus_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a cost‑plus pricing model was used.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values.']
  discount_flag boolean [note: 'True if discount amounts are applied.']
  gross_revenue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total gross revenue before any deductions for the SKU in the period.']
  net_revenue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual net revenue after all deductions for the SKU.']
  net_revenue_per_case decimal(12,4) [note: 'Net revenue earned per case (standard packaging) of the SKU.']
  net_revenue_per_unit decimal(12,4) [note: 'Net revenue earned per individual unit of the SKU.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or remarks about the revenue record.']
  period_date date [note: 'The fiscal period (date) to which the revenue realization applies.']
  price_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the price basis used for the revenue calculation.']
  pricing_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the pricing logic applied.']
  processing_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the revenue realization was processed.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the revenue realization record was first created.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  return_flag boolean [note: 'True if return amounts are deducted.']
  revenue_realization_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the revenue record.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating source system for the revenue data.']
  total_allowance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total allowance amount (e.g., rebates, volume allowances) applied.']
  total_discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of all discount amounts applied to the SKU.']
  total_return_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Value of product returns deducted from revenue.']
  total_trade_spend_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate trade spend (promotions, slotting fees, etc.) applied to the SKU.']
  trade_spend_flag boolean [note: 'True if trade spend deductions are present.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for quantity (e.g., each, case).']

  Note: 'Net revenue realization record capturing the actual realized net revenue per unit for a SKU after all deductions, allowances, and trade spend are applied. Captures gross revenue, total deductions (trade spend, discounts, allowances, returns), net revenue, net revenue per case, net revenue per unit, and period. Serves as the SSOT for net revenue management (NRM) reporting and pricing effectiveness measurement at the SKU/channel/customer level.'
}

Table pricing.price_list_assignment {
  price_list_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the price_list_assignment association']
  center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution_center']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to price_list']
  channel varchar [note: 'Commercial channel (e.g., retail, foodservice, DTC) for which the price list applies at the center']
  effective_from date [note: 'Start date when the price list becomes active for the distribution center']
  effective_until date [note: 'End date when the price list ceases to be active for the distribution center']
  region varchar [note: 'Geographic region scope of the price list at the distribution center']

  Note: 'Association linking distribution centers to price lists, capturing which price list is active for a center by channel, region and effective date range.. Existence Justification: A distribution center can be linked to multiple price lists simultaneously, based on channel, region and effective date ranges, and a single price list can be applied to many distribution centers. The business actively manages these assignments, tracking effective dates, channel and region for each link.'
}

Table pricing.price_rule {
  price_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for price_rule']
  parent_price_rule_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on price_rule (parent_price_rule_id)']
  applicability_scope varchar [note: 'Scope of entities the rule applies to (e.g., channel, product, customer, region, store).']
  condition_expression varchar [note: 'Logical expression defining conditions under which the rule is triggered.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the rule logic and purpose.']
  discount_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Principal numeric value of the rule (e.g., percentage or fixed amount).']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the rule becomes active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the rule expires or is superseded (null if open‑ended).']
  is_exclusive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the rule excludes other rules when applied (true = exclusive).']
  last_applied_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the rule was last applied to a transaction.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Integer indicating rule precedence when multiple rules apply (lower number = higher priority).']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the price rule record was first created.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the price rule record.']
  rule_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the pricing rule in external systems.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the pricing rule.']
  rule_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the rule defining its calculation method.']
  rule_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the rule definition (e.g., v1.0, v2.1).']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the pricing rule.']
  usage_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of how many times the rule has been applied.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for price_rule. Referenced by price_rule_id.'
}

Table pricing.price_group {
  price_group_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for price_group']
  parent_price_group_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on price_group (parent_price_group_id)']
  applicable_channels varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of sales channels where this price group is valid.']
  code varchar [note: 'Business code that uniquely identifies the price group across systems.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the price group record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary values in this price group.']
  default_discount_percent decimal(6,4) [note: 'Standard discount percentage applied to the price group in promotional scenarios.']
  default_margin_percent decimal(6,4) [note: 'Standard margin percentage applied to products assigned to this group when no specific margin is defined.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed free‑text description of the price group purpose and rules.']
  effective_from date [note: 'First date on which the price group becomes active for pricing calculations.']
  effective_to date [note: 'Date on which the price group ceases to be active; null if open‑ended.']
  is_exclusive boolean [note: 'True if the price group is mutually exclusive with other groups for a given product.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the price group was last reviewed for relevance and compliance.']
  max_quantity varchar [note: 'Maximum purchase quantity for which the price group pricing is valid.']
  min_quantity varchar [note: 'Minimum purchase quantity required for the price group pricing to apply.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the price group used in pricing rules and reports.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes for internal use, such as special handling instructions.']
  price_factor decimal(8,4) [note: 'Multiplicative factor used to adjust base prices for this group (e.g., 1.05 for a 5% uplift).']
  price_override_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether manual price overrides are permitted for this group.']
  region_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic region or market segment to which the price group applies (e.g., NA, EU, APAC).']
  review_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the price group is evaluated and potentially updated.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the price group.']
  type varchar [note: 'Classification of the price group indicating its pricing strategy.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the price group record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for price_group. Referenced by price_group_id.'
}

Table research.rd_project {
  rd_project_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the research and development project.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Legal entity (company code) is required for consolidation of R&D expenses in financial statements.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: R&D projects are charged to cost centers for internal cost allocation; required for cost allocation reports.']
  finance_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: Project budget aligns with corporate budget record; required for budget‑vs‑actual variance analysis.']
  forecast_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.forecast. Business justification: R&D expense forecasts feed financial forecasting process; needed for forward‑looking expense planning.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: R&D expense postings use a specific GL account; needed for accurate journal entries.']
  innovation_pipeline_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.innovation_pipeline. Business justification: R&D project belongs to a single innovation pipeline; child side gets FK to parent.']
  parent_rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on rd_project (parent_rd_project_id)']
  primary_rd_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the business sponsor responsible for funding and championing the project.']
  product_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.product_registration. Business justification: Regulatory Submission Management: each R&D project must record the product registration it supports for filing.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Projects need profit‑center assignment to assess profitability; used in profit‑center performance reports.']
  rd_employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee']
  rd_sku_id varchar [note: 'FK to product.sku.sku_id — Innovation-to-commercialization traceability: must trace which R&D project produced which commercialized SKU. Required for R&D ROI analysis and NPD pipeline reporting.']
  target_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.sustainability_target. Business justification: R&D projects define specific sustainability targets; linking enables target alignment tracking in project governance.']
  target_sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: An R&D project aims to develop or reformulate a specific SKU; linking tracks project‑to‑product ownership.']
  tertiary_rd_sponsor_employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee']
  actual_end_date date [note: 'Date when the project was actually closed or delivered.']
  actual_spent decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cumulative amount actually spent to date on the project.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the project received final approval to proceed to launch.']
  budget_allocation decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total budget approved for the project, expressed in USD.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall compliance standing of the project against internal and regulatory standards.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the project record was first created in the system.']
  expected_cogs decimal(15,2) [note: 'Projected cost of goods sold for the product at launch.']
  expected_margin_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Projected gross margin percentage for the product.']
  ip_patent_filed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a patent application has been filed for technology developed in the project.']
  ip_patent_number varchar [note: 'Official patent number assigned after filing, if applicable.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Overall status of the project in its lifecycle.']
  market_segment varchar [note: 'Target market segment for the resulting product (e.g., snacks, beverages, dairy).']
  objectives varchar [note: 'Key measurable objectives that the project seeks to achieve.']
  planned_end_date date [note: 'Projected completion date for the project.']
  project_code varchar [note: 'Internal alphanumeric code used to reference the project across systems.']
  project_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the R&D project.']
  project_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the R&D effort: new product development, product renovation, cost optimization, or technology scouting.']
  rd_project_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the project\'s purpose, scope, and expected outcomes.']
  regulatory_approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the project requires formal regulatory approval before launch.']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of any required regulatory approvals.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall risk rating assigned to the project.']
  risk_status varchar [note: 'Current status of risk mitigation activities.']
  stage_gate_phase varchar [note: 'Current stage‑gate phase of the project within the product development lifecycle.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the project officially commenced.']
  strategic_goal varchar [note: 'Specific strategic objective the project aims to achieve.']
  strategic_pillar varchar [note: 'High‑level strategic pillar (e.g., Growth, Innovation, Sustainability) the project aligns to.']
  target_customer_profile varchar [note: 'Key consumer demographics and psychographics the product is designed for.']
  target_launch_date date [note: 'Planned commercial launch date for the product resulting from the project.']
  target_market varchar [note: 'Geographic market(s) where the product is intended to be launched.']
  technology_area varchar [note: 'Primary technology focus of the project (e.g., plant‑based, low‑sugar, functional ingredients).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the project record.']

  Note: 'Master record for each R&D and innovation project in the Food Beverage pipeline — captures project ID, name, type (new product development, renovation, cost optimization, technology scouting), stage-gate phase (ideation, concept, feasibility, development, scale-up, launch), project sponsor, lead scientist, target launch date, budget allocation, strategic pillar alignment, and current status. This is the backbone entity for the entire research domain, linking all formulation, trial, sensory, and IP activities.'
}

Table research.innovation_pipeline {
  innovation_pipeline_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the innovation pipeline record.']
  owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Innovation pipeline ownership requires assigning a senior manager; the HR system tracks this manager via employee_id for approval and accountability.']
  parent_innovation_pipeline_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on innovation_pipeline (parent_innovation_pipeline_id)']
  product_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.product_registration. Business justification: Innovation Pipeline Tracking: pipeline entries need product_registration_id to map concepts to registered products.']
  actual_spend_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative spend to date.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the project received formal approval to proceed.']
  budget_amount_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Approved budget for the project.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance standing with relevant regulations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the pipeline record was first created.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Planned or actual completion date; nullable if open‑ended.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the project officially commenced.']
  estimated_cost_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected total cost to develop and bring the product to market.']
  estimated_revenue_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected revenue potential of the product if successfully launched.']
  health_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite score reflecting overall health of the project.']
  innovation_horizon varchar [note: 'Strategic horizon indicating core, adjacent, or transformational focus.']
  innovation_pipeline_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle status of the project.']
  market_opportunity varchar [note: 'Qualitative assessment of market size and growth potential.']
  milestone_count varchar [note: 'Number of defined milestones for the project.']
  next_milestone_date date [note: 'Planned date for the upcoming milestone.']
  patent_filed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a patent has been filed for the innovation.']
  patent_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the granted or pending patent.']
  pipeline_stage varchar [note: 'Current stage of the project within the stage‑gate process.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the project.']
  project_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code assigned to the project (e.g., internal SKU‑style code).']
  project_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the project\'s objectives and scope.']
  project_lead varchar [note: 'Name of the internal employee responsible for the project.']
  project_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the R&D project.']
  project_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the innovation type.']
  projected_roi_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated return on investment expressed as a percentage.']
  regulatory_impact varchar [note: 'Summary of regulatory considerations affecting the project.']
  resource_allocation_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total R&D capacity assigned to this project.']
  resource_capacity_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total labor hours allocated to the project.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall risk rating for the project.']
  strategic_category varchar [note: 'High‑level business category the innovation aligns to.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the record.']

  Note: 'Portfolio-level master record tracking the aggregate innovation pipeline for Food Beverage — captures pipeline stage distribution, estimated revenue potential by project, strategic category alignment (snacks, beverages, dairy, packaged foods), innovation horizon (H1 core, H2 adjacent, H3 transformational), resource capacity allocation, and pipeline health metrics. Serves as the SSOT for R&D portfolio governance and stage-gate review meetings.'
}

Table research.experimental_formula {
  experimental_formula_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each experimental formula record.']
  derived_from_experimental_formula_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on experimental_formula (derived_from_experimental_formula_id)']
  formulation_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.formulation_version. Business justification: Experimental formula is a concrete instance of a formulation version; child side gets FK to parent.']
  owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee']
  product_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.product_registration. Business justification: Formulation Approval: experimental formula must be linked to the product registration it will be marketed under.']
