  raw_material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ingredient.raw_material. Business justification: Regulatory traceability requires each experimental formula to reference the exact raw material used for safety and label compliance.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: Experimental formulas are created within an R&D project; linking provides project context and eliminates silo.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: R&D experimental formulas are created for a specific SKU; linking enables traceability from formula to product for PLM and regulatory filings.']
  allergen_statement varchar [note: 'Declared allergen information for the experimental formula.']
  batch_size_kg decimal(8,2) [note: 'Target batch size in kilograms for pilot production runs.']
  cost_estimate_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated cost in US dollars to develop the experimental formula.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the experimental formula record was first created.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the experimental formula becomes effective for testing.']
  experimental_formula_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the formulation purpose and characteristics.']
  experimental_formula_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the experimental formula.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the experimental formula is no longer valid for testing.']
  formula_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code assigned to the experimental formula.']
  formula_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name describing the experimental formulation.']
  formula_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the formula lifecycle stage.']
  formulation_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes captured by R&D during formulation development.']
  ingredient_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct ingredients included in the experimental formulation.']
  intellectual_property_status varchar [note: 'Current status of intellectual property protection for the formula.']
  intended_market varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the market where the formula will be launched.']
  is_confidential boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the experimental formula is marked as confidential within the organization.']
  nutritional_info_summary varchar [note: 'Brief summary of key nutritional values (e.g., calories, protein, sugar).']
  patent_pending boolean [note: 'True if a patent application is pending for the formulation.']
  processing_temperature_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Target processing temperature in degrees Celsius for the formulation.']
  processing_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Planned processing time in minutes for the experimental batch.']
  regulatory_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory approval status of the experimental formula.']
  risk_assessment_level varchar [note: 'Overall risk level associated with the experimental formula.']
  scale_up_feasibility varchar [note: 'Assessment of feasibility to scale the formulation to commercial production.']
  sensory_panel_date date [note: 'Date when the sensory panel evaluation was conducted.']
  sensory_panel_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average score from sensory panel evaluation (0‑100 scale).']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Projected shelf life in days for the experimental product.']
  target_product_category varchar [note: 'Primary product category for which the experimental formula is intended.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the experimental formula record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of the formula within its code.']

  Note: 'R&D working formulation record representing each experimental or approved recipe version developed during product innovation — captures formulation code, version number, associated R&D project, target product category, ingredient composition (linked to ingredient domain), processing conditions, intended market/jurisdiction, regulatory status, and formulation owner. Distinct from product.formulation which is the approved commercial formulation; this entity tracks all R&D-stage working versions including experimental and rejected iterations.'
}

Table research.formulation_version {
  formulation_version_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each formulation version record.']
  packaging_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.packaging_profile. Business justification: Each formulation version selects a packaging profile for lifecycle impact assessment; link supports packaging sustainability scoring.']
  patent_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the associated patent or patent application.']
  previous_formulation_version_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on formulation_version (previous_formulation_version_id)']
  primary_formulation_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system user who created the version record.']
  product_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.product_registration. Business justification: Label Compliance: each formulation version is tied to the product registration for accurate labeling.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Each formulation version represents the formulation of a SKU; needed for version control, cost, and compliance reporting.']
  allergen_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates presence or risk of allergens in the formulation.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory or internal approval state of the version.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the version received its approval decision.']
  batch_size_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard batch size for production runs of this version.']
  carbon_footprint_kg_co2e decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated greenhouse‑gas emissions per kilogram of product.']
  change_rationale varchar [note: 'Reason for the change (e.g., cost reduction, taste improvement, allergen removal).']
  changed_ingredients varchar [note: 'JSON‑encoded list of ingredient modifications introduced in this version.']
  changed_processing_parameters varchar [note: 'JSON‑encoded processing parameter changes (e.g., temperature, time, pH).']
  cost_estimate decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated cost per unit for the formulation version.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the formulation version record was created in the system.']
  delta_summary varchar [note: 'Narrative description of differences compared to the prior version.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the formulation version becomes operationally effective.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Optional date after which the version is no longer valid.']
  formulation_category varchar [note: 'High‑level product family to which the formulation belongs.']
  formulation_version_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the formulation version.']
  gras_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates if a GRAS (Generally Recognized As Safe) submission is needed.']
  gras_notification_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the GRAS submission process.']
  is_approved_for_release boolean [note: 'True when the version has passed all release gates.']
  is_current_version boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this version is the latest active version.']
  nutritional_profile varchar [note: 'JSON‑encoded nutrition facts (calories, fat, protein, etc.) for the version.']
  packaging_material varchar [note: 'Primary material used for packaging the product.']
  patent_related_flag boolean [note: 'True if the formulation is covered by a pending or granted patent.']
  processing_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Temperature used during manufacturing of the formulation.']
  processing_time_min varchar [note: 'Total time required for the processing step.']
  regulatory_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall compliance state with FDA/USDA/EFSA regulations.']
  regulatory_review_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text notes from regulatory reviewers.']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Maximum storage duration before product quality degrades.']
  storage_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Recommended storage temperature for the formulation.']
  sustainability_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite score reflecting environmental impact of the formulation.']
  target_market varchar [note: 'Geographic market or channel for which the formulation is intended (e.g., NA, EU, APAC).']
  taste_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Sensory panel rating (0‑10 scale) for the formulation version.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the formulation version record.']
  version_author varchar [note: 'Name of the R&D staff who authored the version.']
  version_code varchar [note: 'External identifier (e.g., PLM code) assigned to the formulation version.']
  version_comment varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments supplied by the author.']
  version_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name for the formulation version used by R&D staff.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential integer indicating the version order of the formulation.']
  version_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the version lifecycle stage.']

  Note: 'Versioned iteration record for each R&D working formulation — captures version number, change rationale (cost reduction, taste improvement, allergen removal, regulatory compliance), changed ingredients, changed processing parameters, version author, approval status, and comparison delta versus prior version. Enables full formulation change history and supports regulatory traceability for novel food submissions and GRAS notifications.'
}

Table research.rd_sensory_panel {
  rd_sensory_panel_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the sensory evaluation panel.']
  follow_up_rd_sensory_panel_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on rd_sensory_panel (follow_up_rd_sensory_panel_id)']
  panel_leader_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Sensory panel execution is overseen by a panel leader (employee); HR records this role for scheduling and compliance reporting.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the research and development project linked to this panel.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Sensory panels evaluate a concrete SKU; linking ties sensory data to the product for launch decisions.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the panel results were formally approved.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the panel record was first created in the system.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Source system where the panel data originated (e.g., Veeva Vault, MES).']
  evaluation_protocol varchar [note: 'Description of the standardized protocol used for the panel.']
  facility_location varchar [note: 'Name or code of the facility where the panel took place.']
  formulation_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the product formulation evaluated in the panel.']
  is_blind boolean [note: 'True if the panel was conducted blind to product identity.']
  is_controlled_environment boolean [note: 'True if the testing environment was controlled for variables such as lighting and temperature.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form observations and comments captured during the panel.']
  overall_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the panel after analysis (e.g., pass, fail, inconclusive).']
  overall_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aggregated numeric score representing overall panel rating.']
  panel_category varchar [note: 'Primary sensory attribute focus of the panel.']
  panel_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code assigned to the panel for tracking and reference.']
  panel_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the sensory evaluation was conducted.']
  panel_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total duration of the panel session in minutes.']
  panel_leader varchar [note: 'Name of the individual leading the sensory evaluation.']
  panel_location_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the physical location of the panel.']
  panel_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the sensory panel.']
  panel_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the panel methodology (e.g., trained descriptive, consumer hedonic, discrimination, QDA).']
  panelist_count varchar [note: 'Number of participants who took part in the sensory panel.']
  rd_sensory_panel_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the panel.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the panel complied with relevant FDA/USDA regulations.']
  reviewer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual who reviewed and approved the panel outcomes.']
  sample_preparation_details varchar [note: 'Notes on how product samples were prepared for evaluation.']
  score_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measurement for the overall score (e.g., points, scale, rating).']
  sensory_methodology varchar [note: 'Specific sensory testing methodology employed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the panel record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version number for audit tracking.']

  Note: 'Master record for sensory evaluation panels conducted during product development — captures panel ID, panel type (trained descriptive, consumer hedonic, discrimination, QDA), associated R&D project and formulation version, panel date, facility location, panelist count, panel leader, evaluation protocol, and overall panel outcome. Central entity for sensory science program management at Food Beverage.'
}

Table research.sensory_result {
  sensory_result_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each sensory evaluation result record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Internal identifier of the consumer panelist who provided the evaluation.']
  formulation_version_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product formulation version evaluated in this test.']
  raw_material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ingredient.raw_material. Business justification: Sensory testing is performed on a particular raw material; linking records the ingredient for product development decisions.']
  rd_lab_sample_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the physical product sample presented to the panelist.']
  replicate_sensory_result_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on sensory_result (replicate_sensory_result_id)']
  session_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sensory testing session to which this result belongs.']
  aftertaste_score varchar [note: 'Numeric rating (e.g., 1‑10) of the lingering aftertaste.']
  appearance_score varchar [note: 'Numeric rating (e.g., 1‑10) of the visual appearance of the sample.']
  aroma_score varchar [note: 'Numeric rating (e.g., 1‑10) of the aroma perceived by the panelist.']
  blind_branded_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the sample was presented blind (unbranded) or with branding cues.']
  comment_text varchar [note: 'Free‑text field for optional qualitative feedback from the panelist.']
  device_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the hardware device (e.g., tablet, kiosk) used to capture the response.']
  evaluation_location varchar [note: 'Code of the facility or lab where the sensory test was conducted.']
  evaluation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the panelist completed the sensory evaluation.']
  flavor_score varchar [note: 'Numeric rating (e.g., 1‑10) of the overall flavor profile.']
  hedonic_scale_rating varchar [note: 'Rating on the standard 9‑point hedonic scale (1 = dislike extremely, 9 = like extremely).']
  humidity_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Relative humidity of the testing environment during evaluation, expressed as a percentage.']
  internal_notes varchar [note: 'Optional free‑text field for analysts to capture observations or data quality comments.']
  overall_liking_score varchar [note: 'Overall acceptance rating (e.g., 1‑10) reflecting the panelist\'s liking.']
  rating_scale_type varchar [note: 'Indicates which hedonic scale (9‑point, 7‑point, or 5‑point) was used for this evaluation.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this sensory result record was first created in the data lake.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this sensory result record.']
  sensory_result_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the sensory result record.']
  temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured temperature of the sample at the time of evaluation, in degrees Celsius.']
  texture_score varchar [note: 'Numeric rating (e.g., 1‑10) of the mouthfeel and texture.']

  Note: 'Individual panelist response record from a sensory evaluation session — captures panelist ID, panel session reference, formulation version evaluated, attribute scores (appearance, aroma, flavor, texture, aftertaste, overall liking), hedonic scale ratings, free-text comments, and blind vs. branded condition flag. Provides the granular data foundation for statistical sensory analysis and consumer preference modeling.'
}

Table research.pilot_trial {
  pilot_trial_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each pilot trial record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Pilot trials are often performed for a key customer; the link supports trial agreements and cost allocation.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Pilot trial costs are charged to cost centers; required for trial cost accounting.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the scientist or analyst responsible for the trial.']
  finance_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: Trial budget is tracked against corporate budget; enables budget‑vs‑actual for pilot trials.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Trial expenses need a GL account for journal posting; supports financial reporting of trial spend.']
  lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ingredient.lot. Business justification: Pilot production batches are executed with a defined ingredient lot; linking enables traceability and quality investigation.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing facility where the trial took place.']
  previous_pilot_trial_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on pilot_trial (previous_pilot_trial_id)']
  production_line_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific production line or equipment used.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the research & development project linked to this trial.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Pilot production trials are run on a specific SKU to validate scale‑up; linking supports trial tracking and cost analysis.']
  actual_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Actual expenditure incurred for the trial, in US dollars.']
  batch_size decimal(12,2) [note: 'Planned or actual batch size for the trial in kilograms.']
  cost_estimate decimal(12,2) [note: 'Projected cost for conducting the trial, in US dollars.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the trial record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values.']
  formulation_version varchar [note: 'Version label of the product formulation used in the trial.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form observations, comments, or deviations recorded by the trial team.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the trial: pass, fail, or conditional.']
  pilot_trial_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the trial.']
  processing_parameters varchar [note: 'Key processing settings (e.g., temperature, time, speed) captured as a structured string.']
  quality_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the trial met predefined quality criteria (true = met).']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of regulatory review for the trial outcome.']
  scale_up_readiness varchar [note: 'Readiness level for moving the formulation to larger scale production.']
  trial_code varchar [note: 'Human‑readable code assigned to the trial (e.g., PT‑2024‑001).']
  trial_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the trial was executed or recorded.']
  trial_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the trial scale: bench, pilot, semi‑commercial, or full plant.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the trial record.']
  yield_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of usable product obtained from the trial batch.']

  Note: 'Pilot plant and bench-scale trial record capturing each production trial conducted during R&D scale-up — captures trial ID, associated R&D project and formulation version, trial type (bench, pilot, semi-commercial, plant trial), trial date, facility and line used, batch size, processing parameters applied, yield achieved, trial outcome (pass/fail/conditional), and scale-up readiness assessment. Links R&D formulation work to manufacturing feasibility.'
}

Table research.trial_observation {
  trial_observation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each trial observation record.']
  batch_record_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the production batch associated with the trial.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person who recorded the observation.']
  equipment_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the equipment or machine involved in the observation.']
  pilot_trial_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the pilot plant trial to which this observation belongs.']
  related_trial_observation_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on trial_observation (related_trial_observation_id)']
  corrective_action varchar [note: 'Action taken to address the observation, including steps performed and responsible party.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the observation record was first created in the system.']
  location varchar [note: 'Plant or facility location identifier where the observation was made.']
  measured_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Actual value recorded for the parameter during the observation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments providing additional context or details about the observation.']
  observation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the observation was recorded in the pilot plant trial.']
  observation_type varchar [note: 'Category of the observation indicating the nature of the event.']
  observer_role varchar [note: 'Job role of the observer at the time of recording.']
  parameter_name varchar [note: 'Name of the measured parameter (e.g., temperature, pH, viscosity).']
  process_step varchar [note: 'Specific step or operation within the pilot trial where the observation occurred.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Impact level of the observation on product quality, safety, or production.']
  target_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Planned or acceptable target value for the parameter.']
  trial_observation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the observation record.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit in which the parameter is measured (e.g., kg, °C, %). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: kg|g|ml|l|°C|%|ppm — promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the observation record.']

  Note: 'Granular observation record captured during a pilot plant trial — captures observation type (process deviation, equipment issue, quality defect, yield loss, safety concern), observation timestamp, process step, parameter measured versus target, severity, corrective action taken, and observer identity. Provides the detailed trial log that supports root cause analysis and formulation/process optimization decisions.'
}

Table research.patent {
  patent_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the patent record.']
  continuation_patent_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on patent (continuation_patent_id)']
  abstract varchar [note: 'Brief summary of the invention as provided in the application.']
  annuity_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount required for each annuity payment.']
  annuity_frequency varchar [note: 'How often annuity payments are due.']
  annuity_next_due_date date [note: 'Date of the next scheduled annuity payment.']
  application_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the patent application.']
  assignee_name varchar [note: 'Legal entity to which the patent rights are assigned.']
  claims_summary varchar [note: 'Concise description of the patent claims.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the patent record was first created in the system.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date the patent rights expire.']
  filing_date date [note: 'Date the patent application was filed with the granting authority.']
  grant_date date [note: 'Date the patent was officially granted.']
  inventor_names varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of inventor full names.']
  is_patent_family boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this patent is part of a broader patent family.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Country or region where the patent is granted (ISO 3‑letter code).']
  legal_status varchar [note: 'Current legal standing of the patent with the granting authority.']
  licensing_status varchar [note: 'Current licensing state of the patent.']
  maintenance_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the upcoming maintenance fee.']
  maintenance_fee_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the next maintenance fee must be paid to keep the patent in force.']
  patent_family_code varchar [note: 'Identifier linking all patents belonging to the same family.']
  patent_number varchar [note: 'Official patent number assigned by the granting authority.']
  patent_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle status of the patent record.']
  patent_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the patent (utility, design, plant, provisional).']
  priority_country varchar [note: 'Country of the priority filing (ISO 3‑letter code).']
  priority_date date [note: 'Date of the earliest filing that the patent claims priority from.']
  rnd_project_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the R&D project associated with the invention.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system where the patent data originated (e.g., Veeva Vault, internal IP tracker).']
  technology_domain varchar [note: 'Primary technology area the patent covers (e.g., formulation, process, packaging, equipment).']
  title varchar [note: 'Full title of the patent as recorded in the application.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the patent record.']

  Note: 'Intellectual property patent master record for Food Beverage — captures patent number, title, patent type (utility, design, plant, provisional), filing date, grant date, expiration date, jurisdiction(s), inventors, assignee entity, technology domain (formulation, process, packaging, equipment), associated R&D project, licensing status, and annuity payment schedule. SSOT for IP portfolio management and freedom-to-operate assessments.'
}

Table research.ip_asset {
  ip_asset_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the IP asset.']
  parent_ip_asset_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on ip_asset (parent_ip_asset_id)']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: IP assets (patents, trade secrets) are owned by R&D projects; linking enables traceability.']
  asset_type varchar [note: 'Category of IP asset indicating whether it is a patent, trade secret, know‑how, trademark, or copyright.']
  commercialization_status varchar [note: 'Stage of commercialization for the IP asset.']
  compliance_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance review for the IP asset.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall compliance status with regulatory and internal IP policies.']
  confidentiality_classification varchar [note: 'Classification indicating the sensitivity level of the IP asset (restricted, confidential, internal, public).']
  conflict_description varchar [note: 'Narrative describing the nature of any conflict or infringement risk.']
  conflict_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if there is a known conflict or infringement risk.']
  disclosure_level varchar [note: 'Level of public disclosure permitted for the IP asset.']
  document_reference varchar [note: 'URL or path to the primary document that defines the IP asset.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date the IP asset became effective or enforceable.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date the IP asset expires or loses protection.']
  filing_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Cost incurred to file the IP asset with the relevant authority.']
  ip_asset_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the IP asset, its scope and purpose.']
  ip_asset_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the IP asset used for identification.']
  ip_asset_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the IP asset.']
  ip_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the IP asset record was first created.']
  ip_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the IP asset record.']
  ip_manager varchar [note: 'Internal manager responsible for the IP asset.']
  ip_value_estimate decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary estimate of the IP asset\'s worth.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code(s) where the IP protection is sought. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USA|CAN|MEX|GBR|DEU|FRA|JPN|CHN|IND|BRA|... — promote to reference product]']
  legal_counsel varchar [note: 'External or internal legal counsel overseeing the IP asset.']
  legal_status varchar [note: 'Current legal standing of the IP asset.']
  licensee varchar [note: 'External party that holds a license to the IP asset.']
  licensing_end_date date [note: 'Date when the licensing agreement for the IP asset ends.']
  licensing_start_date date [note: 'Date when the licensing agreement for the IP asset begins.']
  licensing_status varchar [note: 'Current licensing condition of the IP asset.']
  maintenance_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Ongoing cost to maintain the IP asset (e.g., renewal fees).']
  maintenance_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the next maintenance fee must be paid.']
  owner_department varchar [note: 'Business department that owns or is responsible for the IP asset.']
  protection_strategy varchar [note: 'Strategic approach for protecting the IP asset.']
  publication_date date [note: 'Date the IP asset was publicly published.']
  publication_status varchar [note: 'Whether the IP asset has been published in a public registry.']
  renewal_date date [note: 'Date the IP asset was last renewed.']
  renewal_status varchar [note: 'Status of the most recent renewal action.']
  risk_assessment varchar [note: 'Qualitative risk rating for the IP asset.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric risk score (0‑100) derived from risk assessment.']
  royalty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of royalty payable per period.']
  royalty_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for royalty payments.']
  royalty_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Royalty rate expressed as a percentage for licensed IP.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Source system where the IP asset originated.']
  creation_date date [note: 'Date the IP asset record was initially created in the system.']

  Note: 'Broader intellectual property asset master covering all IP types beyond patents — captures trade secrets, know-how records, proprietary processes, copyrights, trademarks relevant to R&D, and confidential formulation disclosures. Captures asset type, description, creation date, owner, confidentiality classification, associated R&D project, and commercialization status. Complements the patent entity to provide complete IP portfolio visibility.'
}

Table research.technology_scout {
  technology_scout_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each technology scouting record.']
  analyst_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Each technology scouting effort is assigned to a specific analyst employee; linking supports workload tracking and performance metrics.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: Technology scouting records are tied to specific R&D projects; linking captures ownership.']
  related_technology_scout_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on technology_scout (related_technology_scout_id)']
  analyst_name varchar [note: 'Name of the analyst who performed the scouting assessment.']
  cost_estimate decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated monetary cost to acquire or implement the technology (in the specified currency).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the scouting record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code for the cost estimate currency.']
  department_responsible varchar [note: 'Business unit or department that will own the technology evaluation.']
  evaluation_status varchar [note: 'Current stage of the technology evaluation workflow.']
  expected_implementation_timeline_months varchar [note: 'Projected time required to integrate the technology into production, expressed in months.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'Identifier assigned by the source organization (e.g., startup catalog number, university project code).']
  external_reference_url varchar [note: 'Web link to the source document, website, or repository containing detailed technology information.']
  intellectual_property_status varchar [note: 'Current IP protection status of the technology.']
  last_updated date [note: 'Date of the most recent modification to the scouting record.']
  market_opportunity_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (0‑100) estimating market size and growth potential.']
  next_action varchar [note: 'Suggested follow‑up activity for the technology.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional observations, comments, or qualitative insights recorded by the analyst.']
  potential_impact varchar [note: 'Qualitative description of expected impact on revenue, cost, or market position.']
  regulatory_implications varchar [note: 'Regulatory bodies or standards that may affect adoption of the technology.']
  related_product_codes varchar [note: 'Pipe‑separated list of internal product SKUs or GTINs that could benefit from the technology.']
  relevance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (0‑100) reflecting alignment with Food Beverage strategic priorities.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Assessment of technical, regulatory, or market risk associated with the technology.']
  scouting_date date [note: 'Date when the technology was first identified or logged.']
  source_name varchar [note: 'Name of the organization, institution, or event that originated the technology.']
  source_type varchar [note: 'Category of the entity that provided the technology information.']
  stakeholder_contact varchar [note: 'Name or email of the internal stakeholder overseeing the technology.']
  strategic_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned by the innovation team for this technology.']
  technology_readiness_level varchar [note: 'Standardized TRL rating (1‑9) indicating maturity of the technology.']
  technology_scout_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form textual description of the technology, its function, and key features.']
  technology_scout_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the external technology or innovation being scouted.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the scouting record.']

  Note: 'Technology scouting record capturing external technology opportunities identified for potential adoption or partnership — captures technology name, source (startup, university, supplier, competitor, conference), technology readiness level (TRL 1-9), relevance to Food Beverage strategic priorities, scouting analyst, evaluation status (identified, assessed, shortlisted, rejected, in-licensing), and recommended next action. Feeds the innovation pipeline with external technology inputs.'
}

Table research.stage_gate_review {
  stage_gate_review_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each stage gate review record.']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Gate review records must capture which employee created the review entry for audit trails and regulatory compliance.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the research & development project linked to this gate review.']
  recycled_stage_gate_review_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on stage_gate_review (recycled_stage_gate_review_id)']
  budget_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Approved budget for the project at this gate (currency‑agnostic).']
  commercial_viability_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric assessment (0‑100) of market and commercial potential.']
  committee_members varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of individuals participating in the gate review.']
  conditions_attached varchar [note: 'Specific conditions required for a conditional go decision.']
  cost_estimate decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated cost to bring the product to market as of this gate.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the review record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three‑letter currency code for monetary values.']
  decision varchar [note: 'Outcome of the gate review (go, kill, hold, recycle).']
  decision_rationale varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation for the gate decision.']
  documentation_link varchar [note: 'Link to the digital folder or document containing review artifacts.']
  expected_launch_date date [note: 'Projected commercial launch date if the decision is go.']
  financial_return_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric assessment (0‑100) of projected financial return for the product.']
  gate_number varchar [note: 'Sequential gate identifier (0 through 5) in the NPD stage‑gate process.']
  is_conditional_go boolean [note: 'True if the decision is a conditional go; otherwise false.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any extra comments or observations.']
  overall_gate_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aggregated score summarising all gate criteria.']
  projected_roi decimal(5,2) [note: 'Projected ROI percentage based on financial return score and cost estimate.']
  regulatory_compliance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric assessment (0‑100) of compliance with FDA, USDA, GFSI, etc.']
  review_date date [note: 'Date on which the gate review was conducted.']
  review_location varchar [note: 'Physical or virtual location where the gate review took place.']
  review_method varchar [note: 'Mode of the gate review (in‑person, virtual, or hybrid).']
  review_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable reference number for the review (e.g., RG-2023-001).']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall risk classification assigned during the review.']
  stage_gate_review_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the review record.']
  technical_feasibility_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric assessment (0‑100) of technical feasibility at this gate.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last updated the review record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the review record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the review record.']

  Note: 'Stage-gate decision review record for each formal gate review conducted on an R&D project — captures gate number (Gate 0 through Gate 5), review date, decision (go, kill, hold, recycle), decision rationale, gate criteria scores (technical feasibility, commercial viability, regulatory compliance, financial return), review committee members, and conditions attached to a conditional go decision. Governs the NPD stage-gate process at Food Beverage.'
}

Table research.concept {
  concept_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the product concept.']
  concept_employee_id varchar [note: 'System user who created the concept record.']
  innovation_pipeline_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the innovation pipeline entry linked to this concept.']
  owner_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'System user responsible for the concept.']
  parent_concept_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on concept (parent_concept_id)']
  product_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.product_registration. Business justification: Concept to Market: final product registration is linked to the originating concept for traceability.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: Concept originates from a specific R&D project; child side gets FK to parent.']
  benefit_proposition varchar [note: 'Core consumer benefit the concept promises (e.g., low‑calorie, high‑protein).']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text notes on compliance considerations.']
  concept_category varchar [note: 'High‑level product category (e.g., snack, beverage, dairy).']
  concept_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the concept, its purpose and key attributes.']
  concept_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the product concept.']
  concept_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the concept idea.']
  concept_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the concept.']
  consumer_segment varchar [note: 'Primary consumer segment the concept is aimed at (e.g., health‑conscious, premium, family).']
  cost_estimate decimal(12,2) [note: 'Preliminary estimate of total cost to develop the concept.']
  creation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the concept record was created.']
  flavor_hypothesis varchar [note: 'Proposed flavor profile for the concept.']
  format_hypothesis varchar [note: 'Proposed product format (e.g., ready‑to‑drink, powder, bar).']
  intellectual_property_filed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any IP (patent, trademark) has been filed for the concept.']
  last_test_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent concept testing.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the concept record.']
  launch_window_estimate date [note: 'Estimated calendar window for product launch.']
  market_trend_alignment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score (0‑100) indicating how well the concept aligns with current market trends.']
  origin_date date [note: 'Date the concept was first created or logged.']
  owner_role varchar [note: 'Job role of the concept owner (e.g., R&D Manager, Brand Manager).']
  pack_size_hypothesis varchar [note: 'Proposed packaging size or unit (e.g., 250 ml, 12‑oz).']
  packaging_material varchar [note: 'Material used for packaging (e.g., PET, aluminum, paper).']
  packaging_type varchar [note: 'Primary packaging format (e.g., bottle, pouch, can).']
  patent_status varchar [note: 'Current patent status for the concept.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority ranking (e.g., 1 = high, 5 = low).']
  projected_cogs decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated COGS per unit if the concept proceeds to production.']
  projected_margin_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated gross margin as a percentage of sales price.']
  projected_revenue decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated annual revenue (currency‑agnostic) for the concept.']
  projected_sales_volume varchar [note: 'Estimated annual unit sales if the concept is launched.']
  projected_shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Estimated shelf life in days for the finished product.']
  purchase_intent_rating decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average consumer purchase intent rating (0‑100).']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of regulatory review for the concept.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Risk assessment score (0‑100) based on technical, market, and regulatory factors.']
  sustainability_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Internal sustainability rating (0‑100) for the concept.']
  target_market varchar [note: 'Geographic market(s) the concept is intended for.']
  test_comments varchar [note: 'Free‑text observations from concept testing.']
  test_method varchar [note: 'Method used to obtain the concept test score.']
  test_sample_size varchar [note: 'Number of respondents in the latest concept test.']
  test_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aggregated score from concept testing (0‑100 scale).']

  Note: 'Product concept master record representing a consumer-validated or internally generated product idea prior to full formulation development — captures concept ID, concept name, category, target consumer segment, key benefit proposition, flavor/format/pack size hypothesis, concept test score (CLT or online), purchase intent rating, associated innovation pipeline entry, and concept status (active, advanced, parked, killed). Bridges consumer insight and formulation development.'
}

Table research.consumer_test {
  consumer_test_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the consumer test record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Consumer test participation must be linked to the customer account for incentive tracking and compliance reporting.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the R&D analyst responsible for the test.']
  rd_sensory_panel_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the consumer panel used for recruitment.']
  retest_consumer_test_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on consumer_test (retest_consumer_test_id)']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Consumer tests are conducted on a SKU to gauge market acceptance; linking feeds results into product launch planning.']
  concept_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the product concept or formulation being evaluated.']
  consumer_test_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the consumer test.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the consumer test record was created in the system.']
  end_date date [note: 'Planned end date of the consumer test.']
  execution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the test execution was completed.']
  geography varchar [note: 'Geographic region or city where the test participants are located, if more granular than market.']
  key_driver_attributes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of attributes identified as key drivers of liking (e.g., sweetness, texture).']
  overall_liking_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average overall liking rating on a 1-10 scale across all participants.']
  purchase_intent_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average purchase intent rating on a 0-100 scale.']
  recommendation varchar [note: 'Recommended action based on test results.']
  recruitment_criteria varchar [note: 'Criteria used to select participants (e.g., age range, consumption frequency, dietary restrictions).']
  sample_size varchar [note: 'Number of participants recruited for the consumer test.']
  start_date date [note: 'Planned start date of the consumer test.']
  target_demographic varchar [note: 'Target consumer demographic segment for the test (e.g., Millennials, Parents).']
  test_category varchar [note: 'Broad category of the test focus.']
  test_code varchar [note: 'External reference code for the consumer test, used in reporting and tracking.']
  test_duration_days varchar [note: 'Planned duration of the test in days.']
  test_market varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code representing the primary market where the test is conducted.']
  test_method varchar [note: 'Blinding method applied during the test.']
  test_protocol varchar [note: 'Detailed protocol description outlining test procedures, product usage instructions, and data collection methods.']
  test_type varchar [note: 'Methodology used for the test: in-home use test (IHUT), central location test (CLT), or online concept test.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the consumer test record.']

  Note: 'Consumer product test record capturing in-home use tests (IHUT), central location tests (CLT), and online concept/product tests conducted during R&D — captures test ID, test type, associated concept or formulation version, test market/geography, sample size, recruitment criteria, test protocol, overall liking score, purchase intent, key driver attributes, and test outcome recommendation. Distinct from marketing.consumer_insight which covers broader market research; this entity is R&D-specific product testing.'
}

Table research.rd_lab_sample {
  rd_lab_sample_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the laboratory sample record.']
  batch_record_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the production batch from which the sample originated.']
  derived_from_rd_lab_sample_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on rd_lab_sample (derived_from_rd_lab_sample_id)']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the analyst responsible for the sample.']
  lab_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the laboratory where the sample is stored or processed.']
  lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ingredient.lot. Business justification: Lab samples are taken from a specific ingredient lot; the link supports chain‑of‑custody and regulatory documentation.']
  pilot_trial_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the pilot trial linked to the sample.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Lab samples are taken from a SKU for analytical testing; linking ensures sample data is tied to the correct product.']
  abv_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Alcohol content of the sample expressed as percent ABV.']
  allergen_info varchar [note: 'Allergen content or related declarations for the sample.']
  brix_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Sugar content expressed as degrees Brix.']
  chain_of_custody varchar [note: 'Narrative record of custody transfers for the sample.']
  collection_date date [note: 'Date on which the sample was collected.']
  collection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp of sample collection.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the sample record was first created.']
  disposal_date date [note: 'Date on which the sample was disposed.']
  disposal_method varchar [note: 'Method used to dispose of the sample.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the sample is considered expired.']
  formulation_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the formulation associated with the sample.']
  hazard_status varchar [note: 'Risk classification of the sample based on safety considerations.']
  microbial_count_cfu varchar [note: 'Colony forming units measured in the sample.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or observations about the sample.']
  ph decimal(4,2) [note: 'Acidity/alkalinity measurement of the sample.']
  quality_flag varchar [note: 'Result of quality assessment for the sample.']
  quantity decimal(12,3) [note: 'Amount of material in the sample.']
  rd_lab_sample_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the sample.']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory review outcome for the sample.']
  sample_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code assigned to the sample (e.g., SMP-2023-001).']
  sample_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or description of the sample.']
  sample_type varchar [note: 'Category of the sample indicating its role in R&D.']
  storage_humidity_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Relative humidity level of the storage environment.']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'Identifier of the storage location (e.g., freezer, shelf, cabinet).']
  storage_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Temperature at which the sample is stored, expressed in degrees Celsius.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit used for the sample quantity.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the sample record.']

  Note: 'Laboratory sample master record for all physical samples managed within R&D labs — captures sample ID, sample type (raw material, intermediate, prototype, competitor benchmark, reference standard), associated formulation version or pilot trial, collection date, storage conditions, quantity, chain of custody, expiry date, and disposal record. Enables sample traceability and lab inventory management across R&D facilities.'
}

Table research.rd_test_request {
  rd_test_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the laboratory test request record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Custom R&D test requests are initiated by a specific customer account; linking enables request tracking and billing.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Test request costs are allocated to cost centers; required for cost tracking of lab work.']
  finance_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: Test request budget is managed against corporate budget; needed for budget approval workflow.']
  primary_rd_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the R&D scientist who submitted the test request.']
  rd_lab_sample_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the physical sample submitted for testing.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the research and development project to which the test request belongs.']
  retest_rd_test_request_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on rd_test_request (retest_rd_test_request_id)']
  completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the test was completed and results were recorded.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the test request record was first created in the data lake.']
  estimated_turnaround_hours varchar [note: 'System‑calculated estimate of how long the test will take based on method and lab load.']
  formulation_version varchar [note: 'Version label of the product formulation being tested (e.g., v1.2, revA).']
  hazardous_material_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the sample contains hazardous substances requiring special handling.']
  lab_location varchar [note: 'Physical location or facility where the test will be performed.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or special instructions provided by the scientist.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the request, influencing lab scheduling.']
  rd_test_request_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the test request as it moves through the R&D lab workflow.']
  regulatory_compliance_required boolean [note: 'True if the test must meet FDA/USDA or other regulatory specifications.']
  request_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable identifier assigned to the test request, used in lab tracking and reporting.']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the test request was initially submitted by the scientist.']
  required_turnaround_hours varchar [note: 'Maximum number of hours the scientist expects the test results to be returned.']
  result_available_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the test results became accessible to the requesting scientist.']
  sample_quantity decimal(12,3) [note: 'Amount of sample material submitted, expressed in grams or milliliters as appropriate.']
  test_method varchar [note: 'Analytical or physical test method requested (e.g., HPLC, GC‑MS, texture analysis).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit for the sample quantity (e.g., g, ml, mg).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of the most recent modification to the test request record.']

  Note: 'Laboratory test request record submitted by R&D scientists for analytical, microbiological, or physical testing of R&D samples — captures request ID, requesting scientist, associated project and formulation version, sample reference, test methods requested (HPLC, GC-MS, texture analysis, viscosity, pH, water activity), priority, required turnaround time, and request status. Manages the R&D lab testing workflow distinct from quality.inspection_lot which covers commercial production QC.'
}

Table research.rd_test_result {
  rd_test_result_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the test result record.']
  batch_record_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the production batch associated with the sample.']
  calibration_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the instrument calibration record used.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the analyst who performed or reviewed the test.']
  rd_lab_sample_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sample material tested.']
  rd_test_request_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the associated test request.']
  retest_rd_test_result_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on rd_test_result (retest_rd_test_result_id)']
  analyst_comments varchar [note: 'Additional comments provided by the analyst.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory or internal approval status of the result.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the result complies with regulatory requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the test result record was created.']
  data_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score representing overall data quality assessment.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Source system that supplied the test result (e.g., LIMS, MES).']
  detection_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'Lowest concentration that can be reliably measured.']
  formulation_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the product formulation tested.']
  instrument_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the analytical instrument used.']
  lab_location_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the laboratory where testing was performed.']
  measured_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'Numeric value obtained from the measurement.']
  measurement_repeat_flag boolean [note: 'True if the measurement was repeated for verification.']
  measurement_type varchar [note: 'Category of measurement performed.']
  measurement_uncertainty decimal(18,6) [note: 'Estimated uncertainty associated with the measured value.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text comments or observations related to the test result.']
  oos_flag boolean [note: 'True if the result is out of specification.']
  parameter_name varchar [note: 'Name of the measured parameter (e.g., pH, Brix).']
  pass_fail_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the measured value meets specification limits.']
  quality_control_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the result is part of a QC sample.']
  rd_test_result_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the test result.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific regulatory clause or standard applicable.']
  result_date date [note: 'Date component of the result timestamp.']
  result_quality_flag varchar [note: 'Quality assessment of the result.']
  result_reviewed_by varchar [note: 'Analyst identifier who reviewed the result.']
  result_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequence number for multiple parameters within the same test request.']
  result_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the test result was recorded.']
  review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the result was reviewed.']
  specification_limit_high decimal(18,6) [note: 'Upper acceptable limit for the parameter.']
  specification_limit_low decimal(18,6) [note: 'Lower acceptable limit for the parameter.']
  test_method varchar [note: 'Method used to perform the analytical test.']
  test_result_category varchar [note: 'Broad category of the test result.']
  test_result_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the test result data.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit associated with the measured value (e.g., mg/L, %).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the test result record.']

  Note: 'Analytical test result record for R&D laboratory tests — captures result ID, associated test request, test method applied, instrument used, analyst, result values per parameter (with units), specification limits for comparison, pass/fail determination, result date, and any out-of-specification (OOS) flags. Provides the scientific data foundation for formulation optimization decisions. Distinct from ingredient.test_result (raw material CoA testing) and quality.micro_test_result (production QC).'
}

Table research.competitor_benchmark {
  competitor_benchmark_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each competitor benchmark record.']
  concept_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.concept. Business justification: Competitor benchmarks are evaluated against product concepts; linking provides context for analysis.']
  previous_competitor_benchmark_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on competitor_benchmark (previous_competitor_benchmark_id)']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Benchmarking competitor products against our SKU requires associating benchmark data with the SKU for pricing and positioning analysis.']
  allergen_info varchar [note: 'Allergen declarations (e.g., contains milk, soy).']
  benchmark_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code used to reference the benchmark in external systems.']
  benchmark_date date [note: 'Date when the competitor product was evaluated.']
  competitor_benchmark_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the benchmark record.']
  competitor_brand varchar [note: 'Name of the competitor\'s brand.']
  competitor_product_name varchar [note: 'Name of the competitor\'s product being benchmarked.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the product complies with relevant regulatory requirements (e.g., FDA, USDA).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the benchmark record was first created.']
  evaluation_method varchar [note: 'Methodology used to conduct the benchmark evaluation.']
  ingredient_list varchar [note: 'Full list of ingredients as declared on the product label.']
  label_claims varchar [note: 'Other label claims (e.g., "Organic", "Non‑GMO").']
  market_region varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code representing the geographic market where the product was purchased.']
  nutrient_claims varchar [note: 'Marketing claims related to nutrients (e.g., "Low Sugar", "High Protein").']
  nutritional_calories_kcal varchar [note: 'Energy content per serving expressed in kilocalories.']
  nutritional_carbohydrate_g decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total carbohydrate grams per serving.']
  nutritional_fat_g decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total fat grams per serving.']
  nutritional_protein_g decimal(5,2) [note: 'Protein grams per serving.']
  nutritional_saturated_fat_g decimal(5,2) [note: 'Saturated fat grams per serving.']
  nutritional_sodium_mg varchar [note: 'Sodium milligrams per serving.']
  nutritional_sugar_g decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total sugar grams per serving.']
  packaging_format varchar [note: 'Description of the packaging type and size (e.g., Bottle 500 ml, Can 330 ml).']
  packaging_material varchar [note: 'Primary material of the packaging (e.g., PET, Glass, Aluminum).']
  price_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the retail price.']
  product_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category of the product (e.g., Beverage, Snack).']
  product_subcategory varchar [note: 'More specific sub‑category (e.g., Carbonated Soft Drink, Energy Bar).']
  product_type varchar [note: 'Broad type classification (e.g., Beverage, Snack, Dairy).']
  purchase_market varchar [note: 'Channel through which the benchmark product was purchased.']
  retail_price decimal(10,2) [note: 'Retail price of the competitor product in the specified currency.']
  sample_size varchar [note: 'Number of panelists or units evaluated for the benchmark.']
  sensory_aftertaste_score varchar [note: 'Score (0‑10) for aftertaste from the sensory panel.']
  sensory_appearance_score varchar [note: 'Score (0‑10) for visual appearance from the sensory panel.']
  sensory_aroma_score varchar [note: 'Score (0‑10) for aroma from the sensory panel.']
  sensory_flavor_score varchar [note: 'Score (0‑10) for flavor from the sensory panel.']
  sensory_overall_liking_score varchar [note: 'Overall liking score (0‑10) from the sensory panel.']
  sensory_texture_score varchar [note: 'Score (0‑10) for mouthfeel/texture from the sensory panel.']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the product remains fit for consumption under recommended storage.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System or process that supplied the benchmark data (e.g., TraceGains, Manual).']
  storage_temperature_c decimal(4,1) [note: 'Optimal storage temperature in degrees Celsius.']
  strategic_implications varchar [note: 'Analyst commentary on how the benchmark influences product strategy.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the benchmark record.']
  volume_ml varchar [note: 'Net liquid volume of the product in milliliters.']

  Note: 'Competitive product benchmarking record capturing the systematic evaluation of competitor and market reference products — captures benchmark ID, competitor brand and product name, category, purchase market, evaluation date, sensory profile scores, nutritional composition, ingredient list analysis, packaging format, retail price point, and strategic implications. Informs formulation targets and positioning decisions during NPD.'
}

Table research.rd_budget {
  rd_budget_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the R&D budget record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'System identifier of the user who approved the budget.']
  finance_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: R&D‑specific budget must map to the master finance budget record for consolidation and reporting.']
  parent_rd_budget_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on rd_budget (parent_rd_budget_id)']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the R&D project to which this budget belongs.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the budget was formally approved.']
  budget_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the budget, often used in reporting and external communication.']
  budget_external_testing_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Approved budget for third‑party testing and validation services.']
  budget_licensing_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Approved budget for licensing fees, patents, and IP costs.']
  budget_materials_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Approved budget for raw material and ingredient costs.']
  budget_owner varchar [note: 'Name of the individual responsible for the budget.']
  budget_personnel_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Approved budget for personnel costs within the R&D project.']
  budget_pilot_plant_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Approved budget for pilot‑scale production runs.']
  budget_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the budget.']
  budget_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the budget purpose: operational, capital, or research.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Internal cost center to which the budget is charged.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the budget record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for all monetary amounts.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'Department responsible for the R&D effort.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the budget becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the budget expires or is superseded.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Four‑digit fiscal year for which the budget is planned.']
  is_confidential boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the budget details are confidential.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional remarks, comments, or audit notes.']
  rd_budget_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the budget purpose and scope.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter code representing the geographic region of the budget.']
  total_actual_spend decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative actual spend to date across all categories.']
  total_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Approved total budget amount across all categories.']
  total_committed_spend decimal(18,2) [note: 'Spend that has been committed but not yet incurred.']
  total_forecast_to_complete decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected additional spend required to complete the budget.']
  total_variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Difference between approved budget and actual spend (budget - actual).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the budget record.']

  Note: 'R&D project-level budget and spend tracking record — captures budget ID, associated R&D project, fiscal year, approved budget by category (personnel, materials, external testing, pilot plant, licensing), actual spend to date, committed spend, forecast to complete, variance, and budget owner. Provides R&D financial governance at the project level. Distinct from finance.budget which covers enterprise-wide financial budgeting.'
}

Table research.external_collaboration {
  external_collaboration_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the external collaboration record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: External collaborations may involve a strategic customer partner; linking captures the account for governance and revenue sharing.']
  finance_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: Collaboration funding is recorded in corporate budget; required for financial oversight of external R&D.']
  formulation_version_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the formulation version that is the focus of the collaboration.']
  parent_external_collaboration_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on external_collaboration (parent_external_collaboration_id)']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal R&D project associated with this collaboration.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the collaboration agreement was formally approved.']
  collaboration_code varchar [note: 'Human‑readable code assigned to the collaboration for external reference.']
  collaboration_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the external R&D collaboration.']
  collaboration_scope varchar [note: 'Brief description of the functional and technical scope of the collaboration.']
  collaboration_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the collaboration.']
  collaboration_type varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the collaboration (e.g., joint development, technology scouting).']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data sensitivity classification for the collaboration record.']
  contract_document_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the stored contract document in the document management system.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the collaboration record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three‑letter currency code for the funding amount.']
  deliverables varchar [note: 'Expected tangible outputs (e.g., prototypes, reports, patents).']
  end_date date [note: 'Date the collaboration agreement terminates or expires (nullable for open‑ended).']
  funding_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary funding allocated to the collaboration.']
  funding_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the funding (e.g., internal R&D budget, external grant).']
  ip_ownership_terms varchar [note: 'Key terms defining ownership and licensing of IP generated through the collaboration.']
  location_city varchar [note: 'City of the partner\'s primary location.']
  location_country varchar [note: 'ISO‑3 country code of the partner\'s primary location.']
  milestones varchar [note: 'Structured list of major milestones and target dates for the collaboration.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations about the collaboration.']
  partner_contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the external partner contact.']
  partner_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the external partner contact.']
  partner_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the external partner organization.']
  partner_type varchar [note: 'Category of the external partner.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the collaboration complies with all applicable food‑safety and regulatory requirements.']
  renewal_option varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the collaboration may be renewed after the end date.']
  risk_assessment_level varchar [note: 'Overall risk rating assigned to the collaboration.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date the collaboration agreement becomes effective.']
  status_change_date date [note: 'Date when the collaboration status last changed.']
  sustainability_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Internal sustainability rating for the collaboration (0‑100 scale).']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for termination if the collaboration ends before the planned end date.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the collaboration record.']

  Note: 'External R&D collaboration and partnership record capturing joint development agreements, university research partnerships, startup co-development arrangements, and contract research organization (CRO) engagements — captures collaboration ID, partner name, partner type (university, startup, CRO, supplier, industry consortium), collaboration scope, start and end dates, IP ownership terms, milestones, deliverables, and collaboration status. Manages the open innovation ecosystem at Food Beverage.'
}

Table research.regulatory_submission_dossier {
  regulatory_submission_dossier_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the regulatory submission dossier record.']
  amended_regulatory_submission_dossier_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on regulatory_submission_dossier (amended_regulatory_submission_dossier_id)']
  primary_regulatory_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created the dossier record.']
  product_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.product_registration. Business justification: Regulatory Dossier Traceability: dossier must reference the product registration to ensure correct filing.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the R&D project linked to the submission.']
  target_formulation_version_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the formulation version associated with this submission.']
  agency_name varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory agency handling the submission.']
  agency_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number assigned by the agency to the submission.']
  approval_outcome varchar [note: 'Final outcome of the regulatory review.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the submission meets all regulatory compliance requirements.']
  confidentiality_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the dossier contains confidential information.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the dossier record was created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Currency of the submission fee.']
  docket_url varchar [note: 'Web URL linking to the electronic dossier.']
  dossier_number varchar [note: 'Agency-assigned identifier for the dossier.']
  dossier_title varchar [note: 'Human‑readable title describing the dossier.']
  expected_decision_date date [note: 'Projected date for agency decision on the submission.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the dossier is no longer active.']
  formulation_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the formulation associated with the submission.']
  ingredient_list varchar [note: 'List of ingredient identifiers included in the submission.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Geographic jurisdiction for the submission.']
  regulatory_agency_contact varchar [note: 'Contact details of the agency representative handling the dossier.']
  regulatory_review_notes varchar [note: 'Notes and comments from the regulatory agency during review.']
  regulatory_submission_dossier_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the dossier.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the agency\'s review process.']
  risk_assessment_level varchar [note: 'Risk level assigned to the submission based on product and ingredient profile.']
  scientific_evidence_summary varchar [note: 'Summary of scientific evidence supporting the submission.']
  submission_category varchar [note: 'Category indicating whether the submission is pre‑market or post‑market.']
  submission_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the submission was transmitted.']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date the dossier was submitted to the regulatory agency.']
  submission_fee_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Fee charged by the agency for processing the submission.']
  submission_method varchar [note: 'Method used to submit the dossier to the agency.']
  submission_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the submission for processing.']
  submission_source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that created the dossier record.']
  submission_type varchar [note: 'Category of the regulatory submission.']
  supporting_documents_count varchar [note: 'Number of supporting documents attached to the dossier.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the dossier record.']

  Note: 'R&D-originated regulatory submission dossier record for novel food ingredients, GRAS notifications, health claims substantiation, and new product market authorizations — captures dossier ID, submission type (GRAS, novel food, health claim, food additive petition), target jurisdiction, associated formulation and R&D project, submission date, agency reference number, review status, and approval outcome. Distinct from regulatory.agency_submission which tracks post-commercialization regulatory filings; this entity covers R&D-stage pre-market submissions.'
}

Table research.ingredient_feasibility {
  ingredient_feasibility_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each ingredient feasibility assessment record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Ingredient feasibility studies can be requested by customers; linking tracks customer‑driven feasibility work.']
  primary_ingredient_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created the assessment record.']
  raw_material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ingredient.raw_material. Business justification: Feasibility studies assess a specific raw material\'s technical, regulatory and supply attributes, so they must link to that raw_material record.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the R&D project associated with this feasibility assessment.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Feasibility assessments of ingredients are performed for a particular SKU; linking enables ingredient selection per product.']
  superseded_ingredient_feasibility_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on ingredient_feasibility (superseded_ingredient_feasibility_id)']
  allergen_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ingredient is a known allergen.']
