  assessment_code varchar [note: 'Business-friendly alphanumeric code assigned to the feasibility assessment for external reference.']
  assessment_date date [note: 'Date on which the feasibility assessment was performed.']
  assessment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the feasibility assessment.']
  brix_value decimal(5,2) [note: 'Sugar content expressed in degrees Brix.']
  cfus_per_gram varchar [note: 'Microbial count indicating safety or probiotic potency.']
  cost_per_unit_usd decimal(12,4) [note: 'Unit cost of the ingredient expressed in US dollars.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the assessment record was first created.']
  feasibility_cost_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score (0‑100) representing cost competitiveness of the ingredient.']
  feasibility_regulatory_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory clearance status for the ingredient in target markets.']
  feasibility_supply_availability_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score (0‑100) indicating reliability and volume of supply from the vendor.']
  feasibility_sustainability_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score (0‑100) reflecting environmental and social sustainability attributes.']
  feasibility_technical_functionality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score (0‑100) reflecting how well the ingredient meets technical formulation requirements.']
  functional_properties varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the ingredient\'s functional benefits (e.g., emulsification, gelling).']
  gluten_free boolean [note: 'True if the ingredient is certified gluten‑free.']
  halal_certified boolean [note: 'True if the ingredient is certified halal.']
  ingredient_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the ingredient being evaluated.']
  ingredient_origin_country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the ingredient\'s primary source.']
  ingredient_type varchar [note: 'Category of the ingredient based on its functional role in formulations.']
  kosher_certified boolean [note: 'True if the ingredient is certified kosher.']
  non_gmo boolean [note: 'True if the ingredient is verified non‑genetically modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional observations, comments, or rationale captured by the assessor.']
  organic_certified boolean [note: 'True if the ingredient holds an organic certification.']
  overall_feasibility_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aggregated weighted score summarizing all feasibility dimensions.']
  ph_value decimal(4,2) [note: 'Acidity/alkalinity measurement of the ingredient.']
  recommendation varchar [note: 'Decision outcome based on the overall feasibility score.']
  regulatory_comments varchar [note: 'Narrative notes on regulatory considerations, exemptions, or pending actions.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall risk classification derived from feasibility findings.']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Recommended shelf life of the ingredient under standard storage conditions.']
  storage_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Optimal storage temperature for the ingredient in degrees Celsius.']
  supplier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the ingredient supplier providing the material.']
  supply_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Estimated number of days required for the supplier to deliver the ingredient.']
  sustainability_metric varchar [note: 'Key sustainability indicator (e.g., carbon_footprint_kg_co2e).']
  sustainability_metric_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Numeric value associated with the selected sustainability metric.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the assessment record.']

  Note: 'Ingredient feasibility assessment record evaluating new or novel ingredients for potential use in Food Beverage formulations — captures assessment ID, ingredient name, supplier, ingredient type (novel protein, functional fiber, natural color, flavor, bioactive), feasibility dimensions assessed (technical functionality, regulatory status, supply availability, cost, sustainability), overall feasibility score, recommendation, and associated R&D project. Bridges technology scouting and formulation development.'
}

Table research.scale_up_plan {
  scale_up_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the scale-up plan.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Scale‑up activities are charged to cost centers for cost tracking; required for cost‑center reporting.']
  finance_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: Scale‑up plan budget aligns with corporate budget; supports budget control and variance analysis.']
  formulation_version_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the formulation version being scaled up.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Scale‑up expenses post to GL accounts; needed for accurate financial statements.']
  primary_scale_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who performed the readiness sign‑off.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the R&D project linked to this scale‑up plan.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Scale‑up plans are drafted for a SKU to transition from pilot to full production; linking provides schedule and cost alignment.']
  superseded_scale_up_plan_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on scale_up_plan (superseded_scale_up_plan_id)']
  target_plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the plant where commercial production will occur.']
  target_production_line_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific production line within the target plant.']
  tertiary_scale_last_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who last modified the scale‑up plan record.']
  actual_cost_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total actual cost incurred to date for the scale‑up plan, in US dollars.']
  budget_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Approved budget for the scale‑up effort, expressed in US dollars.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of the scale‑up plan with internal and external regulations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the scale‑up plan record was created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for budget and cost fields. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USD|EUR|GBP|JPY|CAD|AUD|CHF|CNY|INR|BRL|MXN|ZAR — promote to reference product]']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the scale‑up plan ends or is superseded (nullable for open‑ended plans).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the scale‑up plan becomes effective.']
  equipment_qualification_requirements varchar [note: 'Requirements and criteria for qualifying equipment for scale‑up.']
  milestone_count varchar [note: 'Total count of defined milestones in the scale‑up plan.']
  next_milestone_date date [note: 'Planned date of the upcoming milestone.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments related to the scale‑up plan.']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'Human‑readable code identifying the scale‑up plan.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the scale‑up plan.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Category of the scale‑up plan indicating the nature of the transition.']
  process_parameter_transfer_specifications varchar [note: 'Detailed specifications for transferring process parameters from pilot to commercial scale.']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Status of regulatory approvals required for the scaled‑up product.']
  risk_assessment_details varchar [note: 'Narrative description of identified risks and mitigation actions.']
  risk_assessment_level varchar [note: 'Overall risk level assigned to the scale‑up plan.']
  scale_up_plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the scale‑up plan.']
  scale_up_readiness_signoff boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the plan has been formally signed off as ready for execution.']
  signoff_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the readiness sign‑off was recorded.']
  trial_batch_schedule varchar [note: 'Schedule and sequencing of trial batches during scale‑up.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the scale‑up plan record.']

  Note: 'Manufacturing scale-up plan record defining the transition pathway from pilot plant to commercial production for an approved R&D formulation — captures plan ID, associated R&D project and formulation version, target manufacturing plant and line, scale-up milestones, process parameter transfer specifications, equipment qualification requirements, trial batch schedule, risk assessment, and scale-up readiness sign-off. Bridges R&D and manufacturing domains.'
}

Table research.rd_milestone {
  rd_milestone_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the research and development milestone record.']

  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the milestone received its final approval decision.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the milestone meets all applicable regulatory and internal compliance requirements.']
  cost_actual_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Actual cost incurred to complete the milestone, expressed in US dollars.']
  cost_target_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Planned cost budget for achieving the milestone, expressed in US dollars.']

  expected_duration_days varchar [note: 'Planned number of calendar days allocated to complete the milestone.']
  gate_decision varchar [note: 'Result of the gate review for the milestone.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'True if the milestone is a critical path item for product launch.']
  is_gate_milestone boolean [note: 'True if the milestone serves as a stage‑gate decision point.']
  milestone_code varchar [note: 'Human‑readable code assigned to the milestone for tracking and reference.']
  milestone_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the milestone (e.g., "Formulation Freeze").']
  milestone_sequence varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of the milestone within the R&D project timeline.']
  milestone_type varchar [note: 'Category of the milestone indicating the gate or decision point in the R&D project.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments or observations related to the milestone.']
  owner_department varchar [note: 'Organizational department responsible for the milestone owner.']
  owner_role varchar [note: 'Job role or function of the milestone owner (e.g., Project Lead, Scientist).']
  planned_date date [note: 'Target date on which the milestone is scheduled to be achieved.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the milestone.']
  rd_milestone_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the milestone.']
  regulatory_clearance_status varchar [note: 'Status of any required regulatory approval for the milestone.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk assessment rating assigned to the milestone.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the milestone record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the milestone record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the milestone record.']

  Note: 'Project milestone record tracking key deliverables and decision points within an R&D project — captures milestone ID, associated R&D project, milestone name, milestone type (formulation freeze, sensory approval, pilot trial completion, regulatory clearance, cost target confirmation, launch readiness), planned date, actual completion date, milestone owner, completion status, and any delay rationale. Enables project timeline management and stage-gate readiness tracking.'
}

  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance audit.']

  longitude double [note: 'Geographic longitude of the laboratory (decimal degrees).']

  max_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable temperature for controlled zones.']
  min_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum allowable temperature for controlled zones.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the laboratory.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information about the laboratory.']
  operating_hours varchar [note: 'Standard operating hours for the laboratory (e.g., 08:00‑18:00).']
  owner_department varchar [note: 'Internal department responsible for the laboratory.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal/ZIP code for the laboratory address.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the laboratory\'s primary contact.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the main point‑of‑contact for the laboratory.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the laboratory\'s primary contact.']
  region varchar [note: 'Broad geographic region (e.g., North America, EMEA) where the laboratory is located.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall risk classification based on safety and compliance assessments.']
  safety_certification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the laboratory\'s safety certifications.']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province of the laboratory location.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the laboratory.']
  temperature_control_capability boolean [note: 'Indicates if the laboratory can maintain controlled temperature environments.']
  type varchar [note: 'Category of laboratory based on its primary function.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the laboratory record.']
  waste_disposal_method varchar [note: 'Primary method used for hazardous waste disposal at the laboratory.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for lab. Referenced by lab_id.'
}

Table research.sensory_session {
  sensory_session_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for sensory_session']
  follow_up_sensory_session_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on sensory_session (follow_up_sensory_session_id)']

  conducted_by varchar [note: 'Name of the researcher or lead who oversaw the session.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the sensory session record was first created.']
  data_collection_device varchar [note: 'Tool used to capture panelist responses (e.g., tablet, paper, mobile app).']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the sensory session concluded.']
  is_blinded boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the session was conducted blind to product identity.']
  location_name varchar [note: 'Name of the facility or site where the session was conducted.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional observations or comments.']
  number_of_panelists varchar [note: 'Total count of panelists participating in the session.']
  protocol_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the sensory testing protocol applied.']
  randomization_scheme varchar [note: 'Approach used to randomize sample presentation order.']
  sampling_method varchar [note: 'Methodology for selecting panelists for the session.']
  scale_type varchar [note: 'Scale used by panelists to record their responses.']
  sensory_attribute varchar [note: 'Specific sensory characteristic being measured.']
  session_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code used internally to reference the session.']
  session_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name given to the sensory session for easy identification.']
  session_type varchar [note: 'Category of the sensory session based on its purpose.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the sensory session began.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the session.']
  test_method varchar [note: 'Methodology used to conduct the sensory evaluation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the session record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for sensory_session. Referenced by session_id.'
}

Table research.panelist_group {
  panelist_group_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for panelist_group']
  parent_panelist_group_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on panelist_group (parent_panelist_group_id)']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the panelist group for use in studies.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data sensitivity classification for the panelist group.']
  consent_obtained_date date [note: 'Date when data privacy consent was recorded for the group.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the panelist group record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the incentive amount.']
  data_privacy_consent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether all panelists have provided required data privacy consent.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the group\'s purpose, composition, and usage.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the panelist group is retired or expires; null if open-ended.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the panelist group becomes active for research projects.']
  eligibility_criteria varchar [note: 'Business rules defining which individuals qualify for inclusion in the panelist group.']
  group_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code used to reference the panelist group in external systems.']
  group_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the panelist group based on participant characteristics.']
  incentive_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Monetary incentive offered per panelist for participation.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent review of the panelist group\'s composition and relevance.']
  name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the panelist group.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text field for any supplemental information about the panelist group.']
  panelist_count varchar [note: 'Number of individual panelists assigned to the group.']
  panelist_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that originally sourced the panelist data.']
  recruitment_method varchar [note: 'Primary method used to recruit panelists into the group.']
  research_focus varchar [note: 'Primary research domain(s) the panelist group supports.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the panelist group.']
  target_market varchar [note: 'Geographic market(s) the panelist group is intended to represent.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the panelist group record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for panelist_group. Referenced by panelist_group_id.'
}
